title: Iran minister heads to Russia as talks remain stalled
body: Iran's foreign minister headed to Russia on Sunday as peace efforts between Tehran and Washington hung in the balance, following a flurry of regional diplomacy and the collapse of planned talks in Pakistan. Abbas Araghchi visited Oman between trips to Pakistani capital Islamabad and is expected to meet President Vladimir Putin on Monday, according to the Iranian ambassador. On Saturday, US President Donald Trump scrapped a planned trip to Islamabad by his envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner. In a sign that efforts were ongoing, the Fars news agency said Iran had sent 'written messages' to the Americans via mediator Pakistan outlining red lines, including nuclear issues and the Strait of Hormuz. Fars said the messages were not part of formal negotiations, however. A ceasefire in the US-Israeli war with Iran has so far held, but its economic shockwaves continue to reverberate globally. Iran has sealed off the strait, cutting flows of oil, gas and fertilizer and sending prices soaring , raising fears of food insecurity in developing countries. Hopes for talks had centred on a planned visit by Witkoff and Kushner, but Trump cancelled the trip, dismissing it as 'sitting around talking about nothing'. On Sunday, Trump told Fox News that if Iran wanted the talks, 'they can come to us, or they can call us.' Trump faces domestic pressure as fuel prices rise following Iran's closure of Hormuz, with midterm elections due in November. Polls show the war is unpopular among Americans. Asked whether cancelling signalled renewed fighting, Trump said: 'No, it doesn't mean that.' On Saturday, Araghchi met Pakistan's military chief Asim Munir, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar, before travelling to Oman and returning to Islamabad. He later left for Russia for talks with senior officials, his ministry said. Russia confirmed the visit but did not say whether he would meet Putin. Iranian state television quoted Iran's envoy to Moscow, Kazem Jalali, saying Araghchi and Putin would meet in St Petersburg, Russia's second city. Araghchi himslef posted on X that the talks in Oman had focused on ensuring safe transit through Hormuz, 'to benefit of all dear neighbors and the world.' 'Our neighbors are our priority,' he added. Pressure to end the war has intensified as the Strait of Hormuz remains closed. Iran's Revolutionary Guards said they had no intention of lifting their blockade, which has roiled energy markets. 'Controlling the Strait of Hormuz and maintaining the shadow of its deterrent effects over America and the White House's supporters in the region is the definitive strategy of Islamic Iran,' the Guards said on their official Telegram channel. The United States has imposed a blockade of Iranian ports in retaliation. In a statement carried by state media, Iran's military warned that continued US 'blockading, banditry and piracy' would draw a response. Israel and Hezbollah traded blame over violations of the fragile ceasefire in Lebanon, with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu saying the military was 'vigorously' targeting the Iran-backed militia as both sides claimed new attacks. Hezbollah drew Lebanon into the Middle East war on March 2 by firing rockets at Israel to avenge the death of Iran's supreme leader Ali Khamenei, with Israel responding with strikes and a ground invasion. But claims that both sides have breached a 10-day ceasefire agreed earlier this month have continued. Netanyahu told Sunday's weekly cabinet meeting that Hezbollah's actions were 'dismantling the ceasefire' while Hezbollah said it would respond to Israeli violations and its 'continued occupation'. Lebanon's health ministry said Israeli strikes on the country's south on Sunday killed 14 people, including two women and two children, and wounded 37. The state-run National News Agency reported that Israeli warplanes had struck after evacuation warnings in Kfar Tibnit. An Israeli strike on Zawtar al-Sharqiyah, another of the flagged villages, destroyed a mosque and another religious building, the news agency said. Israel, which reported a soldier killed in combat in south Lebanon, says it can act against 'planned, imminent or ongoing attacks'. 'This means freedom of action not only to respond to attacks...but also to pre-empt immediate threats and even emerging threats,' Netanyahu said. Mourners chant slogans during a memorial rally held by supporters of Lebanon’s Shiite Muslim Hezbollah movement a day after Iran’s supreme leader was assassinated during a US and Israeli offensive on Tehran, in Beirut’s southern suburbs on March 1, 2026. Anwar Amro, AFP

title: Dizon orders immediate declogging of SLEX drainage systems after weekend flooding
body: Dizon said the DPWH will work together with SLEX operator San Miguel Corporation on it as early as this week. 'Tumawag tayo immediately sa San Miguel Corporation… Baka merong mga naiwan doon na barado, may mga semento from construction na medyo na-clog nang konti. 'Yun ang suspetya namin,' he said. But he noted that compared to previous flooding incidents, the water subsided a lot faster this time around. 'Nag-flash flood kasi sobra yung buhos sa South, pero napansin namin parang 30 minutos, bumaba na. So good sign yun kasi nung nakaraang mga baha, oras yung binibilang bago bumaba e,' added Dizon. The DPWH chief also inspected a couple of flood-prone areas in Parañaque City. He attributed the flooding problem in Barangay Moonwalk and nearby villages to the heavy siltation and large volume of trash that can be found in the Dungalo Creek, which serves as the outfall area. Dizon has instructed his district engineer to implement dredging operations. 'Alam niyo naman limitado yung pondo ng 2026 para sa flood mitigation, pero hindi naman ibig sabihin nun wala na lang tayong gagawin… Magde-dredge tayo dito kasi yung tubig halos hindi na umaagos e. Ibig sabihin nun, barado— dahil sa basura, dahil sa siltation. Ang babaw,' he said. The same issue plagues the Villanueva Creek inside the Manila Memorial Park. Parañaque 2nd District Rep. Brian Yamsuan said this is why flooding occurs along Dr. Santos Avenue. 'Pag nagbaha dyan di na kumikilos lahat ng sasakyan mula Skyway pababa, at hindi na makatawid ng Sucat papunta sa kanilang mga bahay,' he said. Apart from dredging operations, Dizon plans to allocate P50 million from the agency’s savings to build a flood interceptor for Dr. Santos Avenue. For 2027, the DPWH also wants to put up detention basins underneath basketball courts and gymnasiums, similar to what is being done in Quezon City. The local government unit volunteered to help identify possible locations, but it also urged private subdivisions to cooperate. 'Pagbigyan kaming magawa yung mga proyekto sa loob ng mga lugar ninyo para makatulong doon sa pangkalahatan na problema ng ating lungsod,' said Yamsuan. According to Dizon, the DPWH has also seen improvements in some areas of Metro Manila where drainage systems have already been rehabilitated and expanded such as in G. Araneta Avenue in Quezon City, Taft Avenue and along Roxas Boulevard in Manila. 'Nung umulan nung Sabado, Linggo, nakita na natin may tulong e. Hindi mawawala ang baha pero nabawasan at importante din, mabilis humupa yung tubig,' he said. Dizon vowed to fast track other flood control measures in the coming weeks before the official start of the rainy season. MANILA -- The P49.5 million drainage expansion project along Bonifacio Drive in Manila is set to be finished by Friday, according to the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH). During an inspection with Manila Mayor Isko Moreno on Wednesday, DPWH Secretary Vince Dizon said the agency is three weeks ahead of schedule and well ahead of the rainy season. READ: Dizon vows continued push for accountability in flood control scandal amid Senate shake-up 'Itong intersection ng Bonifacio at Roxas Boulevard, laging binabaha 'to. At ang main source ng baha ay yung maliliit na drainage na after the war pa yata hindi pa nababago. So nakita niyo naman, yung mga malalaki at modernong drainage na ang ginamit natin,' he said, noting that they were able to triple the capacity of the old canals. 'Happy to report kami na ito na yung last portion. Three-days concrete na yung pinalagay natin dito para mabilis ang pagtuyo,' added Dizon. Moreno welcomed the development, saying motorists will soon experience lighter traffic along the busy road. 'By Friday, dadaloy na ulit ang ginhawa sa kalsada and hopefully by June dadaloy na rin yung ginhawa ng tubig sa imburnal sa ilalim ng kalsada,' he said. Dizon likewise vowed to make sure that nearby water pumping stations are operational. Dizon and Moreno also visited the 200-meter drainage rehabilitation project along Taft Avenue northbound, which was completed on May 13, 2026 after years of delays. It doubles the capacity of the existing drainage network to mitigate flooding along Taft Avenue, specifically from TM Kalaw to Ayala Boulevard, where a lot of schools are situated. Dizon said the DPWH has been able to accelerate project implementation after enforcing stricter guidelines and carrying out an internal cleansing of district and regional officials. This is in the wake of the massive flood control corruption scandal exposed last year. 'Alam mo pag hindi tinututukan ng DE (District Engineer) at DWPH yung kontratista, ganun talaga mangyayari, mabagal. Petiks-petiks lang. Pero pag nakatutok at alam na may consequence, ibig sabihin pag hindi mo na-meet deadline mo, may financial consequence sa kontratista, may liquidated damages, edi masakit yun,' he said. 'Ang problema dati, siguro may mga padulas yung mga contractor, hindi iniimpose yung penalty. Hindi natatapos nang mabilis ang proyekto. Pero ngayon wala nang ganun,' he added. READ: DPWH starts demolition of ‘substandard’ Bulacan dike, vows stronger flood control project For its part, the local government of Manila said it is also working to improve the drainage systems in other areas of the city. 'To complement yung action ng national government, dinevelop naman namin yung paligid ng Lawton hanggang Basa at Arroceros. Doon naman natin nilagay yung mga box culvert na single barrel, double barrel and triple barrel. Para yung ginawa dito, sasaluhin doon. Tapos yung pinakadulo nito, going north, is an MMDA (Metropolitan Manila Development Authority) pumping station,' said Moreno. Through the coordination between the local government unit and national agencies, Moreno is optimistic that flooding problems in the areas can be solved 'slowly but surely.' 'Makakatikim pa rin tayo ng ilang pagbaha dahil malaki yung kinocover ng flooding sa Maynila. But ang binabantayan namin ngayon, kung saan nagkaroon ng new installation, new development, maging effective,' he added. The P49.5 million drainage expansion project along Bonifacio Drive in Manila is set to be finished by Friday, according to the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH). The DPWH starts the demolition of a 'substandard' dike in Bulacan, May 12, 2026. Handout/DPWH MANILA -- The Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) on Tuesday began demolishing the allegedly substandard dike in Brgy. Calizon in Calumpit, Bulacan, to make way for a stronger and higher river control project. DPWH Secretary Vince Dizon led the inspection together with Calumpit Mayor Lem Faustino and oversaw the installation of 15-meter sheet piles into the flood control structure along the Bagbag River. 'Ang gagawin natin dito, yung dati na ginawa na useless naman, gigibain natin yan, at gagawa tayo ng panibago. Gagawa tayo nang tama… Ampaw yung mga semento e. Ito yung mga nakita ni Presidente (Bongbong Marcos) na nadudurog sa kamay lang niya. Talagang substandard,' he said. The project, which was recently bid out for P600 million using the DPWH’s contingent funds for 2026, is expected to be completed by March 2027. Officials said the new flood control structure aims to protect around 3,000 residents in the barangay from severe flooding, with water levels sometimes reaching up to the ceilings of houses. Dizon added that criminal cases have already been filed against former engineers of the Bulacan 1st District Engineering Office, including Henry Alcantara and Brice Hernandez, as well as private contractors involved in the original project. 'Iba-iba (yung contractors) e, may Discaya, may Wawao, may Syms— yung usual na mga nambaboy sa proyekto rito over the past years. May mga iba dito nakakulong na pero kailangan ngayon, solusyon na tayo… Hindi naman pwedeng forever na lang binabaha yung mga kababayan natin,' he added. The DPWH has also started dredging operations along the river as part of preparations for the rainy season. Faustino welcomed the agency’s initiatives, saying it’s a big departure from the previous DPWH leadership’s lack of coordination with the local government unit. 'Kampante ako na this time magagawa na nang tama at mailalagay na yung pondo ng bayan sa tamang proyekto,' she said. Some residents who may be displaced by the new dike approached Dizon during the inspection to ask whether the project’s design could still be adjusted. Around 54 households are expected to be affected by the reconstruction. 'Sana lang kung gagawa kayo, wala kayong masisira. Walang magagalit kung sa ilog kayo kakain… Kung kayo ang nakatira papayag ba kayo na masira? Pinag-ipunan namin yun,' said Irene Lanto. But Dizon explained that, based on assessments by experts including Project NOAH, clearing obstructions along the waterway is necessary to expand the river’s carrying capacity and reduce flooding risks. Still, the DPWH chief assured affected residents that the government would provide compensation and relocation assistance. 'Nakita naman natin yung disgrasya sa Cebu. Yung mga bahay nasa loob ng no-build zone, katabi ng ilog, nung rumagasa ang Bagyong Tino, andaming nadisgrasya, na-wipe out lahat ng mga bahay. Hindi na natin pwedeng payagan yun kasi wala tayong kontrol sa klima,' stressed Dizon. 'Bibigyan natin ng tamang compensation yung mga maaapektuhan, at ang NHA (National Housing Authority) dadalhin ko rin dito para mabigyan ng alternatibong pabahay,' he added. Local officials also vowed to help facilitate more dialogues with the community. Dizon vows continued push for accountability in flood control scandal amid Senate shake-up The Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) on Tuesday has began demolishing the allegedly substandard dike in Brgy. Calizon in Calumpit, Bulacan, to make way for a stronger and higher river control project. MMDA Chairman Don Artes, joined by DPWH Secretary Vince Dizon and Las Piñas LGU officials, leads an on-site inspection of the 'Bayanihan sa Estero' clean-up at Tartar Creek in Barangay Pamplona Tres and Talon Dos, Las Piñas City on Wednesday, December 10, 2025. Maria Tan, ABS-CBN News/File MANILA – Public Works Secretary Vince Dizon assured the public on Monday that the Executive branch, together with the Office of the Ombudsman, will continue pursuing accountability in the alleged flood control scandal despite the ongoing leadership shake-up in the Senate. The assurance came after changes in the Senate leadership led not only to the election of a new Senate president, but also to the declaration of all Senate committee chairmanships as vacant. Cayetano is new Senate president, replacing Sotto The development prompted concerns online that the Senate Blue Ribbon Committee’s partial report on the flood control probe could be sidelined, particularly if Sen. Panfilo Lacson, who spearheaded the hearings, would no longer chair the panel. Questions were also raised over the possible release of controversial contractor Curlee Discaya from Senate detention. But Dizon said the government remains firm in holding those involved accountable, stressing that Discaya would not evade prosecution. Curlee Discaya back in Senate after surgery '‘Yung kay Curlee hindi mangyayari ‘yun. We’ll make sure na si Curlee, soon makakasama na niya ‘yung asawa niya sa kulungan. Hindi tayo papayag doon,' Dizon said. 'Ikalawa, ‘yung sa Blue Ribbon, hindi ako makakapag-comment diyan. Kailangan desisyunan ‘yan ng Senado. Kami sa executive, tuloy-tuloy ang pagpo-provide namin ng documentation. Nakikipagtulungan kami hindi lang sa Blue Ribbon kundi sa Ombudsman, at may tiwala tayo na aaksyon ang Ombudsman. Regardless kung anong mangyari, tuloy-tuloy ang magiging aksyon ng Ombudsman,' he added. Dizon was in Calumpit, Bulacan early this morning to inspect the reconstruction of a revetment structure that is supposed to help address flooding in the area. According to Dizon, the existing flood control project, which stretches for several kilometers, is substandard and is now the subject of a criminal case involving the Discayas, along with contractors Wawao Builders and Syms. The existing structure is noticeably low and provides little protection against the massive flooding that residents experience, with water levels that can rise up to rooftops Some portions of the structure have also already been damaged, allowing water from the river to continue seeping into nearby communities. Dizon says the DPWH now plans to rebuild the revetment properly and targets to complete the project by March 2027. Public Works Secretary Vince Dizon assured the public on Monday that the Executive branch, together with the Office of the Ombudsman, will continue pursuing accountability in the alleged flood control scam despite the ongoing leadership shake-up in the Senate. DPWH Secretary Vince Dizon on Monday ordered the immediate declogging of drainage pipes along SLEX after heavy rains triggered flooding in parts of Sucat and Magallanes over the weekend. MANILA -- Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) Secretary Vince Dizon on Monday ordered the immediate declogging of drainage pipes along the South Luzon Expressway (SLEX), after heavy rains triggered flooding in parts of Sucat and Magallanes over the weekend.

title: Marcos to tackle China's gray zone tactics with Japan PM during Tokyo meeting
body: Marcos said he wants to find out more about 'the differences now between the approach in Japan when it comes to security and defense, especially in the region' and 'try to get more clarity' on Takaichi’s proposed power system for Asia. 'Japan and the Philippines have experienced the same difficulties in terms of coercive acts, in terms of different gray zone tactics that are being exercised in the South China Sea and in all of China so that is something that we will certainly be discussing,' Marcos told reporters in Malacañang. 'We would like to hear more about how, what exactly does Japan intend to do and what they are willing to do,' he said. 'That new posture of Japan, we have to define it now in terms, and this is something that I would seek to clarify… how we're going to work for Japan and the Philippines bilaterally, and together – multilaterally, together with our other friends and allies in the region,' he said. Since Marcos ascended to power, the Philippines has actively pushed back against Chinese incursions in the West Philippine Sea, with Manila repeatedly underscoring a 2022 arbitral ruling that invalidated China’s sweeping claims in the South China Sea, where trillions-worth of seaborne goods pass through annually. Takaichi, on the other hand, drew the ire of China after Japan’s first female prime minister criticized Beijing's illegal maneuvers in the South China Sea, and said that she could deploy Japan’s Self-Defense Forces should there be an attack on Taiwan. Months after Takaichi made these statements, Japan — for the first time — actively joined the Balikatan Exercises , the largest annual military drills involving the Philippines, the United States and other nations that uphold the freedom of navigation and rule of law in the South China Sea. 'Japan moved this year from being an observer to a participant, and that is a significant development in terms of our continuing cooperation and training our personnel for further interoperability,' Marcos said. 'This is all in the context of Japan's new posture in terms of defense and security, and that Japan is now allowing itself to participate in those – in such exercises,' he noted. 'That is important because that changes the playing field, as it were. It changes it significantly.' Marcos described the relations between the Philippines and Japan as a 'necessary partnership' as Manila continues to depend on its allies for funding to modernize its military and maritime assets. 'It is becoming very, very clear to all stakeholders around the Indo-Pacific region that we will not be able to secure the region and maintain peace in the region if we do not cooperate and closely ally with one another and coordinate with one another,' Marcos said. 'I'm not talking necessarily only about large military exercises, but also about the sharing of information, the sharing of technology, the training that the Japanese military can have here in the Philippines and in Japan so there are many, many aspects to it,' he said. While the Philippines has clashed with China’s expansionist agenda in the South China Sea, Manila also upholds its policy of seeking 'the peaceful resolution of any conflict,' Marcos said. 'Peace is always an important part of aspiration in terms of foreign policy,' he said. 'I have always simply defined our foreign policy as the national interest and peace, which are actually conjoined because peace is in our national interest,' he said. 'That has been our standard position, and I think it is the right one.' President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. on Monday said that he would discuss defense and energy issues with Japanese Prime Minister Takaichi Sanae during their meeting in Tokyo later this month. MANILA — President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. on Monday said that he would discuss defense and energy issues with Japanese Prime Minister Takaichi Sanae during their meeting in Tokyo later this month.

title: 11 of 19 Toboso fatalities tested positive for gunpowder residue, police say
body: Police Colonel Reynaldo Calaoa, chief of the Police Regional Forensic Unit in the Negros Island Region, said forensic examinations showed that 11 of the fatalities tested positive for gunpowder residue. 'Out of the 19 bodies, 11 were found to be positive for gunpowder. Although there is a follow-up test, it’s still an indication,' Calaoa told reporters in a press briefing at Camp Aguinaldo, Quezon City. He said investigators also recovered spent cartridge cases from multiple areas identified as encounter sites. 'Hindi lang po isang site. May ibang sites din na pinagcollect-an ng cartridge cases. Kung saan-saan ang sinasabing encounter sites. Consistent naman na nakakahanap kami ng cartridge cases,' he noted. The findings were raised amid allegations questioning whether some fatalities had been executed rather than killed in combat. Calaoa, however, stressed that a negative gunpowder result does not automatically mean a person had not fired a weapon, citing possible causes of false negatives. 'It also has to be understood na hindi po ibig sabihin na kapag nag-negative ay hindi na nagpaputok. Meron din kasing tinatawag na false negative. Ibig sabihin, may factors bakit nag-negative iyan—primarily ay naimmerse sa tubig ang bodies,' he said. 'Most of them were immersed sa tubig, basa yung mga kamay.' He went on, 'Another one to consider is yung distance ng muzzle, yung layo. Rifle iyan, malayo. Ang gunpowder residue niyan, mas malayo sa kamay.' Philippine Army 3rd Infantry Division (3ID) Commander Major General Michael Samson maintained that troops encountered armed individuals and defended the legitimacy of the military operation. 'Ang nakasagupa namin were armed. It was a legitimate encounter. Ang identities nila—bakit may mga foreigner, may ganito—ang makakasagot nun ay sila [communist rebels],' Samson said. He emphasized that the military had no intention of involving civilians, minors or foreigners in the conflict situation. 'Sa AFP [Armed Forces of the Philippines], on our side, how we wish na wala sila doon. Sana. How we wish na wala tayong pag-uusapang minors, students and foreigners. Hindi naman namin mapili kung engkwentro yun,' the official said. 'Hindi nila narealize ang danger until the event na maengkwentro sila... It is very difficult na to distinguish during the time ng encounter ang identity,' he added. For his part, National Task Force to End Local Communist Armed Conflict (NTF-ELCAC) Executive Director Undersecretary Ernesto Torres Jr. said the communist insurgency movement in the country has significantly weakened in recent years. 'We have to understand that the CPP-NPA-NDF [Communist Party of the Philippines - New People’s Army – National Democratic Front] has recognized na dwindling na ang kanilang capability in terms of manpower, influenced barangays, even the number of firearms,' Torres said. 'They only have 800 NPA [members] all over the country.' According to Torres, the remaining insurgents have shifted focus to selected areas where issues still persist. 'With the limited manpower they have, they have refocused on areas… na mayroon pang issues na kailangang iaddress ng whole-of-government approach natin. So, they need to consolidate their forces in areas like Mindoro, some parts of Bicol Region, Samar, Leyte, and Negros,' he said. The official also noted that peace and security efforts continue through community-based systems established in formerly insurgency-influenced areas. 'Historically, there were more than 9,500 barangays that were influenced by the CPP-NPA-NDF. That’s about 20% of the total number of barangays all over the country, and it was painstakingly cleared by our security forces with the support of local chief executives and other agencies,' Torres explained. 'That is our way in preserving and sustaining our peace gains,' he added. Authorities on Thursday cited the gunpowder residue found on 11 of the 19 fatalities in the Toboso, Negros Occidental encounter as evidence supporting claims that an exchange of gunfire took place. MANILA — Authorities on Thursday cited the gunpowder residue found on 11 of the 19 fatalities in the Toboso, Negros Occidental encounter as evidence supporting claims that an exchange of gunfire took place, amid allegations questioning the legitimacy of the operation.

title: Leviste unhappy in Congress, open to other positions
body: Speaking at the Kapihan ng Samahang Plaridel in Manila, Leviste said was unhappy with his first term as representative due to the current system in Congress. 'Nalulungkot kapag pupunta ako sa Kongreso at kailangan mong sumunod sa script at pag lumilihis ka sa script, pinapatay ang mikropono,' he said. He said it was still too early to discuss the elections and he was still studying all his options. But based on his current feelings, he said he might not seek reelection. 'Ang mga posibilidad ay huwag akong tumakbo muli sa Kongreso at tumakbo na lang ako sa ibang posisyon, pero wala naman po akong calculations para sa ano mang plano sa politika,' he said. 'Kaya ko ngang gusto kong sulitin ang panahon ko sa Kongreso,' he added. Leviste also slammed the alleged lack of transparency in the distribution and audit of the Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) funds, which he said he opted not to receive. At the same time, he announced that he was giving one million sacks of rice in Batangas province to help families who were having difficulties buying food. The first term congressman has been at odds with various government officials, including Executive Secretary Ralph Recto. Leviste also faced a complaint by Energy Secretary Sharon Garin over an idle power franchise. But Leviste said accusations against him were fake news, which he claimed were triggered by his criticism of the government. 'Lahat ng mga isyu na binabato sa akin ay mga gawang Recto,' he said. Batangas First District Rep. Leandro Leviste said Friday he was open to running for other political posts in the 2028 elections. MANILA — Batangas First District Rep. Leandro Leviste said Friday he was open to running for other political posts in the 2028 elections.

title: Tiny shifts seen in divided ASEAN's approach to Myanmar
body: Myanmar is still a member of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations bloc but has been formally excluded from summits such as the one held in the central Philippines this week since the military junta snatched power. The junta's 2021 coup toppled Suu Kyi's democratically elected government, triggering a bloody civil war and a crackdown on dissent. Philippines President Ferdinand Marcos said on Friday members were 'frustrated' by the years-long failure to successfully implement the bloc's five-point peace plan, suggesting a 'fine-tuning' was in order. 'One of the arguments that was made is that the world has turned how many times since we started this discussion... and perhaps the context has changed a little bit,' he said. 'Unfortunately, we have not seen... any progress in Myanmar,' he added, describing an occasionally 'emotional' meeting in Cebu. A Thai diplomat confirmed that Foreign Minister Sihasak Phuangketkeow had suggested ASEAN invite his Myanmar counterpart for a meeting. 'We need to change the way that we are engaging with Myanmar,' the diplomat told reporters on Friday. Maratee Nalita Andamo, deputy spokeswoman for Thailand's foreign affairs department, said the 'proposal was for engagement with Myanmar at the political level... and it was the start of a discussion'. She said it had been received 'in a positive light'. However, a potential softening of the 11-member bloc's stance is not palatable to every member. Malaysian Foreign Minister Mohamad Hasan told reporters on Thursday his country did not believe the junta had done enough to reclaim a seat at the table. 'We still feel uncomfortable, because oppression is still taking place, atrocities towards their own citizens are still occurring,' he said of Myanmar's junta. 'We want the new government formed after the election in Myanmar to truly follow what we set out in our (peace plan),' he said. 'ASEAN consensus on this matter is fraying,' said Dr Siew Mun Tang, a senior fellow at Singapore's ISEAS-Yusof Ishak Institute There were member states ready to 'let bygones be bygones', given the bloc's longstanding lack of progress. 'There's no way you're going to turn back the clock, right? So let's move forward,' he said in describing the view of those states. He said Thailand's porous border with Myanmar made it an 'urgent national security' issue. Sharon Seah, a principal fellow at the same university, said the bloc had limited leverage. 'The only real leverage ASEAN has is letting (Myanmar) play with the big boys at these summits,' she said, noting it still attends smaller meetings. While it is widely believed Indonesia and Singapore are aligned with Malaysia, countries in the middle could tip the scales at some point, Seah said. 'I would say there's a fairly sizable group of middle-group countries,' she said, identifying Vietnam, Laos, Cambodia, and possibly Brunei as potential 'fence-sitters'. 'They can go either way if they see that there's actually some strategic sense in getting Myanmar to come back into the fold.' Before heading to a gala dinner that ended the summit, Marcos said ASEAN members had left their Friday afternoon session asking 'what more can we do?' Even a small shift in policy could potentially be of benefit, he said. 'Sometimes, when you move the parameters one millimeter, it makes all the difference,' he said, insisting that would not mean abandoning human rights. 'We're asking all the member states, all the leaders and their ministries, what that millimeter shift could possibly be,' he said. 'There are no definitive answers as yet, but we are certainly going to work on it very, very hard.' MACTAN, Cebu — Beyond the global uncertainty brought about by the conflict in the Middle East, the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) continues to grapple with major geopolitical challenges within the region. Among the most pressing issues facing the regional bloc are the political crisis in Myanmar following the military takeover that led to the detention of former leader Aung San Suu Kyi, the ongoing border dispute between Cambodia and Thailand, and continued tensions in the South China Sea. Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. acknowledged these challenges during the plenary session of the 48th ASEAN Leaders’ Summit on Friday, emphasizing the need for stronger collective action among member-states. 'We meet at a defining moment for our region and the global community,' Marcos said. 'The international geopolitical landscape continues to be marked by uncertainty and heightened geopolitical tensions, particularly, by the grave situation in the Middle East,' the President told the Southeast Asian leaders. ASEAN has yet to reach a consensus on recognizing the elections in Myanmar, which saw military leader Min Aung Hlaing assume the presidency and formalize the military’s hold on power. The military takeover in February 2021 led to the detention of Aung San Suu Kyi, who was sentenced to a total of 33 years in prison on multiple charges. She was later transferred to house arrest, with her sentence reportedly reduced to 27 years. As ASEAN Chair, the Philippines earlier urged Myanmar 'to provide greater transparency regarding this transfer by allowing Daw Aung San Suu Kyi to communicate with her family, which would demonstrate a genuine commitment to national reconciliation.' Foreign Affairs Secretary Ma. Theresa Lazaro, serving as the ASEAN Chair’s Special Envoy on Myanmar, is expected to brief leaders on the latest developments in the country. Lazaro had earlier requested limited access to the detained leader. Myanmar is being represented at the summit only by the permanent secretary of its Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Another major concern confronting ASEAN is the long-running border dispute between Cambodia and Thailand. The territorial conflict centers on areas surrounding the 11th-century Preah Vihear Temple and escalated into armed clashes in 2025, leaving more than a hundred people dead and displacing thousands of civilians. Despite a ceasefire reached in October 2025, tensions remain high. Ahead of the leaders’ summit, Marcos facilitated a trilateral meeting with Thai Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul and Cambodian Prime Minister Hun Manet in a bid to create 'a meaningful space for dialogue' on the border issue. Following the meeting, both countries agreed to direct their respective foreign ministries to continue efforts aimed at rebuilding trust and easing tensions. 'The Prime Ministers have agreed to task their respective foreign ministers to sustain this open and candid dialogue and to discuss more directly with one another in detail constructive approaches to prevent escalation, foster the conditions for peace and stability, and to safeguard the well-being of their peoples,' Marcos said. 'We express our confidence in the ability of both Cambodia and Thailand to resolve the matter amicably and to manage their border differences peacefully in the spirit of ASEAN solidarity and unity,' he added. Thailand's Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul committed to the deescalation by walking the path of peace. 'Thailand and Cambodia are two neighboring countries. It is best that we avoid conflict — it only brings losses and suffering,' he said. Now is the time for us to look forward and walk this path together toward peace. This requires sincerity, good faith, and determination,' the Thai leader added. Meanwhile, Cambodian Prime Minister Hun Manet commended the Philippines’ constructive leadership in promoting regional peace, stability, and unity. 'Cambodia remains firmly committed to the peaceful settlement of disputes based on international law and existing agreements. Cambodia stands ready to move forward, constructively, swiftly, and in good faith,' Manet said. Aggressive actions and competing claims in the South China Sea remain another major challenge for Southeast Asia. While negotiations continue on a proposed Code of Conduct between ASEAN and China, the parties have yet to adopt a legally binding agreement despite years of discussions. Department of Foreign Affairs Assistant Secretary Dominic Xavier Imperial said the COC would still form part of summit discussions, although no finalized document is expected since negotiations are being handled separately. Marcos, however, has so far avoided directly discussing the South China Sea in his speeches during the summit, with this year’s ASEAN agenda focusing heavily on energy security. As ASEAN Chair for 2026, Marcos said this year’s theme, 'Navigating the Future Together,' reflects the urgent need for unity and cooperation amid growing uncertainty. 'It is a stark reminder that, in an increasingly uncertain world, our collective action has never been more urgent,' Marcos said. 'We must continue to uphold the principles of ASEAN centrality and solidarity and pursue practical cooperation,' he added. 'It is in this spirit that we gather today to pursue concrete outcomes and forge a collective path, with particular focus on three urgent and interrelated priorities.' Marcos also underscored the importance of implementing the ASEAN Community Vision 2045. 'As we push forward the first year of the implementation of the ASEAN Community Vision 2045, we are reminded that our region is strongest when we turn shared challenges, however daunting they may be, into shared purpose,' he said. ASEAN looks inwards as Mideast crisis hits home Beyond the global uncertainty brought about by the conflict in the Middle East, the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) continues to grapple with major geopolitical challenges within the region. This handout photo taken and released by Agence Kampuchea Press (AKP) on May 7, 2026 shows Philippines' President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. (L) speaking as Cambodia's Prime Minister Hun Manet (2nd R) and Thailands Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul (C) listen during a trilateral meeting ahead of the 48th Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) summit in Cebu, central Philippines. Agence Kampuchea Press (AKP)/AFP ASEAN leaders are expected to call for strengthened regional energy security and 'secure and open sea lanes', according to a draft document seen by AFP on Thursday. The meeting of the 11-member Association of Southeast Asian Nations in the central Philippines has focused largely on solutions to the crisis in the Middle East that has sent fuel and food costs soaring in the region. While the draft statement does not specifically mention the Strait of Hormuz , which has been largely bottled up since US-Israeli strikes on Iran began on February 28, it will call for 'the safe, unimpeded and continuous transit passage of vessels and aircraft in straits used for international navigation'. The document also includes a range of proposals aimed at promoting greater energy independence, including an expansion of the ASEAN power grid, a shift to renewable energy, and even the exploration of nuclear power. Closer to home, the civil war in Myanmar remained centre-stage on Thursday, with ASEAN Secretary-General Kao Kim Hourn telling reporters the possibility of a meeting with the country's foreign minister was on the table. Myanmar has been formally excluded from summits such as the one in Cebu since its military junta snatched power in a 2021 coup that triggered a bloody civil war and crackdown on dissent. While Myanmar's junta earned praise from the Philippines this week for its decision to transfer democracy figurehead Aung San Suu Kyi to house arrest, the bloc has yet to achieve a consensus on a return to the fold for the country. 'The foreign ministers had an exchange of views, including a proposal to have a meeting with the foreign minister of Myanmar,' Hourn told reporters on the summit sidelines on Thursday. 'There will be something, maybe it will be an online meeting.' Thursday also saw the late addition of previously unannounced talks between the leaders of Thailand and Cambodia. The Southeast Asian neighbours have maintained an uneasy truce since signing a ceasefire agreement in late December after a border dispute triggered clashes that killed dozens of people and displaced more than a million others. Thai Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul and Cambodian counterpart Hun Manet both pledged on Thursday to task their foreign ministers with continuing talks aimed at achieving a lasting peace. Both sides have traded accusations of truce violations since the ceasefire was signed. Charnvirakul told a news briefing that they had agreed to work closely on measures aimed at 'restoring trust and gradually rebuilding our bilateral relations'. First Lady Liza Marcos hands on in ASEAN Summit preps Establishment of ASEAN Maritime Center in PH proposed in ASEAN Summit ASEAN 2026 to focus on energy security, disaster resilience ASEAN leaders are expected to call for strengthened regional energy security and 'secure and open sea lanes', according to a draft document seen by AFP on Thursday. CEBU — The establishment of an ASEAN Maritime Center in the Philippines is one of the key discussion points in this year's edition of the summit. In the Political Security Community meeting on Thursday, Department of Foreign Affairs Secretary Theresa Lazaro underscored the importance of maritime cooperation in the region. 'Equally vital is the need to advance maritime security and cooperation. As a region linked by our seas, the maritime domain remains central to our pursuit of stability and prosperity,' Lazaro said. 'It is in this context that the Philippines underscores the importance of the ASEAN Leaders’ Declaration on Maritime Cooperation, recognising the multi-dimensional nature of maritime issues and underscoring the importance of enhancing coordination among AMS,' the Foreign Affairs Secretary added. 'An important aspect of this cooperation is the proposed establishment of an ASEAN Maritime Centre here in the Philippines.' The proposal comes as the Philippines continued its call for a legally binding South China Sea Code of Conduct , which seeks to prevent conflict in the waters. In an earlier briefing, DFA Spokesperson Dominic Xavier Imperial said the ASEAN Leaders’ Declaration on Maritime Cooperation was among three key outcome documents to be considered by the regional leaders. '[This] underscores the region’s commitment to advancing maritime cooperation through concrete initiatives such as the inclusion of the ASEAN Coast Guard Forum as an ASEAN sectoral body and the proposed establishment of the ASEAN Maritime Center in the Philippines,' Imperial said. Lazaro also noted that threats on cybersecurity remained a pressing concern for the region. 'In the realm of cybersecurity, we remained concerned of emerging threats, including the use of artificial intelligence for criminal purposes, necessitating a robust and coordinated approach in this area, such as through capacity-building and information-sharing initiatives,' Lazaro said. 'It is also within the political-security pillar that we strengthen regional efforts to effectively address transnational crime,' she added. The Political Security Community of ASEAN met on Thursday ahead of the Leaders' Summit. 'Our vision is crystal clear: a peaceful, stable and secure region based on the fundamental principles as enshrined in the ASEAN Charter, the UN Charter and principles of international law,' Lazaro said. ASEAN 2026 to focus on energy security, disaster resilience All heads of state, except for Myanmar, expected to attend ASEAN Leader's Summit Wars in Middle East, backyard loom over ASEAN summit The establishment of an ASEAN Maritime Center in the Philippines is one of the key discussion points in this year's edition of the summit. Leaders of the ASEAN states are descending on the central Philippines for what President Ferdinand Marcos has pledged will be a 'bare bones' summit focused on economic issues tied to the Middle East war. While supply chain woes sparked by the US-Israeli attack on Iran may dominate talks, the Association of Southeast Asian Nations, now 11 strong with the addition of Timor-Leste, has a still-raging civil war in its own backyard. Here are a few of the topics the region's leaders may discuss this week. Dwindling fuel supplies, soaring food costs and the safety of migrant workers near the Middle East conflict zone will be the most pressing issues at the summit, Marcos said in March, days after declaring a national energy emergency. 'What we really need at this time is for leaders to talk about... how can we help each other,' he said, batting down rumors the summit could be called off entirely amid the crisis. But while ASEAN has issued broad statements about energy cooperation , it has no existing mechanisms that mandate action. The bloc's decades-old Petroleum Security Agreement, for instance, a fuel-sharing scheme aimed at ensuring energy stability, has never been invoked and is purely voluntary. The Philippines said Wednesday it had endorsed a group 'response to the crisis' in the Middle East it hoped to issue this week, without providing details. That document would likely include a call to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and ensure freedom of movement in other critical sea lanes, according to an ASEAN diplomat who had seen an early draft and spoke to AFP on condition of anonymity. ASEAN member state Myanmar has been formally excluded from summits like the one in Cebu since its military junta snatched power in a 2021 coup that triggered a bloody civil war and crackdown on dissent. Whether or not a recent election that installed junta leader Min Aung Hlaing as president -- or the decision to move democracy leader Aung San Suu Kyi from prison to house arrest -- will inch it closer to a return to the fold is unclear. Min Aung Hlaing said his government would 'work to restore normal relations' with ASEAN after he was sworn in as president last month, but the bloc -– which has worked fruitlessly to restore peace for years -- appears far from a consensus. Thai Foreign Minister Sihasak Phuangketkeow told AFP in January that Bangkok had proposed a 'calibrated engagement' with the new government, saying he hoped the election could be the 'start of the transition'. 'A good number' of the bloc's members hold similarly 'pragmatic' views, Philippines Foreign Minister Theresa Lazaro said at that month's ASEAN summit. But at least a few countries are believed to be setting the bar higher. The ASEAN diplomat who spoke to AFP said the bloc had 'only one card' to play with Myanmar: full diplomatic recognition. On Wednesday, the Philippines praised Myanmar's decision to move Suu Kyi to house arrest while asking that a special envoy be granted 'brief access' to the 80-year-old. A declaration on 'maritime cooperation' is also expected to emerge from this week's summit, but not the long-sought-after Code of Conduct for the South China Sea. ASEAN and China have been negotiating a code of conduct to regulate behavior in the contested maritime area for more than two decades since the idea was first proposed. Brunei, Malaysia, the Philippines and Vietnam all have claims in the critical waterway, which China claims in nearly its entirety despite an international ruling that its stance has no legal basis. The Philippines, which has seen its ships engage in repeated clashes with Chinese vessels, said in February it hoped to bring long‑running talks to a conclusion this year while serving as ASEAN chair. However, talks have stalled repeatedly over disagreements on the code's scope, enforcement and legal status. Manila-based geopolitical analyst Don McLain Gill told AFP this year that while the Philippines could be expected to stress maritime security, any pact China would agree to would lack teeth. The Southeast Asian diplomat, meanwhile, said there was 'no way' Beijing would sign anything legally binding but would instead seek a 'political declaration'. Leaders of the ASEAN states are descending on the central Philippines for what President Ferdinand Marcos has pledged will be a 'bare bones' summit focused on economic issues tied to the Middle East war. MACTAN, Cebu– All heads of state of Southeast Asian nations, except that of Myanmar, are expected to attend the 48th Leader's Summit on Friday. Myanmar will be represented by their Permanent Secretary of their Ministry of Foreign Affairs. 'We are guided by the leader’s decision. Noong mayroon pong lumabas na desisyon iyong mga leaders noon na because of the situation in Myanmar, so, ang decision po noon for the summits dahil hindi naman po nabago iyon at wala pa naman pong bagong desisyon iyong mga leaders, so, ganoon pa rin po. The representation of Myanmar stands,' said Asec. Dominic Xavier Imperial, DFA Spokesperson for the ASEAN. Imperial says the ASEAN is yet to reach a consensus on recognizing the Myanmar elections, which saw military leader Min Aung Hlaing assume the presidency several years after military ousted the civilian government led by Aung San Suu Kyi. Marcos: May 6-8, 2026 special non-working holiday in Lapu-Lapu City and Cordova, Cebu due to ASEAN Summit 'As you know at the moment, ASEAN has not reached any consensus with regard to the recognition of the results of the election in Myanmar,' Imperial said. 'However, there is continued engagement within the region, within ASEAN, as regards the engagement of not just the Philippines as chair, but also the individual AMS or ASEAN Members countries with regard to their relations with Myanmar,' he added. Imperial said ASEAN continues to be guided by the Five Point Consensus in addressing the crisis in Myanmar. 'The 5-PC remains to be the framework on how we deal, how the region deals with Myanmar at the moment and that has not changed and including the leaders decision with regard to the 5-PC that is still the main framework that we are guided by,' Imperial said. The 5-PC calls for cessation of violence and delivery of humanitarian assistance to affected population. ASEAN 2026 to focus on energy security, disaster resilience 'And so, for that reason, we will continue dealing with Myanmar in terms of the situation there with regard to how the progress is being done regarding the five PC,' he added. All heads of state of Southeast Asian nations, except for Myanmar, are expected to attend the 48th Leader's Summit on Friday. Myanmar's junta moved deposed leader Aung San Suu Kyi to house arrest last week but that was not enough to ease deep divisions among ASEAN leaders over how best to engage with the pariah state. CEBU, Philippines -- Myanmar's junta moved deposed leader Aung San Suu Kyi to house arrest last week but that was not enough to ease deep divisions among ASEAN leaders over how best to engage with the pariah state.

title: Thousands ordered to evacuate around leaking California chemical tank
body: The tank contained 7,000 gallons (26,000 liters) of methyl methacrylate, a volatile and flammable liquid used to make plastics, with firefighters warning the situation was serious. 'There are literally two options left,' Incident Commander Craig Covey said. 'The tank fails and spills a total of about 6-7,000 gallons of very bad chemicals into the parking lot in that area or, two, the tank goes into a thermal runaway and blows up, affecting the tanks around them that have fuel or chemicals in them as well. 'We are setting up these evacuations in preparation for these two options: it fails, or it blows up,' he said. The incident unfolded in the Garden Grove area of Orange County, southeast of Los Angeles. Garden Grove Police Chief Amir El-Farra said about 40,000 people were affected by the evacuation order, with several thousand refusing to leave their homes. Aerial footage filmed by local TV stations showed jets of water being sprayed at the tank, which has a capacity of 34,000 gallons. Covey said later Friday that efforts to cool the tank had been successful. 'It's down to a temperature around 61 degrees, with 50 being its happy place so those efforts are succeeding,' Covey said in a video update. 'Our group is going to do everything they can to come up with a third, a fourth, a fifth option,' he added. Orange County health officer Regina Chinsio Kwong said the large exclusion zone around the tank was a necessary precaution. 'If it does explode and there is a vapor, you are all safe as long as you are out of the zone that was determined to be an evacuation zone,' she said. She appealed for anyone who might notice 'a fruity and heavy smell' to alert authorities. 'Smelling it doesn't mean you've reached a level that causes symptoms. But we don't want you to smell that. So we need to know if you're smelling it.' No injuries had been reported by Friday evening, and there was no immediate indication as to what caused the leak, which was initially reported on Thursday. Covey said crews were preparing for a chemical spill, which he described as a 'best-case scenario' and far preferable to an explosion and toxic plume. Responders were working to put containment barriers in place to prevent any spilled material from reaching storm drains or river channels that funnel into the ocean. The US Environmental Protection Agency says methyl methacrylate is irritating to the skin, eyes, and mucous membranes in humans. 'Respiratory effects have been reported in humans following acute (short-term) and chronic (long-term) inhalation exposures,' a fact sheet on the agency's website says. 'Neurological symptoms have also been reported in humans following acute exposure.' Tens of thousands of people were ordered to leave their homes in California Friday after a huge chemical tank began leaking, with warnings it might blow up, sending toxic fumes over a heavily populated area. LOS ANGELES (UPDATED) -- Tens of thousands of people were ordered to leave their homes in California on Friday after a huge chemical tank began to leak, sending toxic fumes over a heavily populated area and posing the risk of an explosion.

title: DILG chief: 'All evidence points no attack on Senate'
body: On Wednesday, Senate security personnel and a National Bureau of Investigation employee allegedly fired into the air during a tense confrontation at the Senate complex, forcing legislators to take cover inside their offices. PNP chief Jose Melencio Nartatez Jr. presented the following timeline of the incident. Nartatez noted in the afternoon of May 13, NBI agents arrived at the adjacent GSIS office 'to fortify the security of the GSIS.' 'None of the NBI agents had tactical gear with them... They did not have a battering ram, they did not have provisions...to breach any door,' Remulla said. Nartatez said drilling sounds were heard at the Senate before the gunfire because a steel door at the ground floor of the GSIS building was being 'fortified.' 'All entrances from Senate to GSIS since early in the morning were being fortified by the GSIS maintenance. They had duly informed the maintenance of the Senate that there was gonna be drill work going on in all the doors of the GSIS connecting to the Senate,' Remulla said. 'Hindi gulatan ito... They were duly informed,' he said. The GSIS secured its entrances 'because Senator Bato had called for a people power movement and had there been anarchy in the building, the GSIS contains billions of pesos worth of artworks that are displayed on their walls,' Remulla said. 'Had there been an overflow of people going to GSIS, the losses would have been incredible,' he said. Remulla said that at 7:44 p.m., then Acting Senate Sergeant-at-Arms Mao Aplasca assembled the Senate security personnel 'and gave the order to lock and load.' 'In his order, you can see the Marines were cocking their guns... after which he proceeded to the hallway leading to the doorway where the NBI were previously,' Remulla said. 'General Aplasca did it [in] full view of the press... and he allowed them to follow up to a certain point. In normal situations, if there is a perceived confrontation, you will clear the building of all personnel because of possible stray fire. However, he allowed them to follow,' he said. Remulla said CCTV footage taken from 7:46 to 7:49 p.m. showed 'clouds of smoke and possible muzzle fire' during the Senate security personnel's confrontation with NBI agents. 'The NBI agent withdrew behind the air-conditioning duct units and because he was being fired upon, he discharged a cover fire,' Remulla said. 'At this point, the officer-in-charge instructed agents, 'Do not engage, do not engage.' So they retreated,' he said. Remulla said Aplasca 'refused to appear' before the Criminal Investigation and Detection Group and turn over his firearm on Tuesday. Meanwhile, Dela Rosa left the Senate at around 2:30 a.m. of May 14. Dela Rosa had sought refuge at the Senate to escape arrest over alleged crimes against humanity charges before the International Criminal Court. He was the national police chief from 2016-2018 during the first two years of Duterte's anti-narcotics crackdown that killed thousands. Asked whether investigators were prepared to conclude that the chaos was staged, Remulla said, 'No.' 'The only conclusion is that there was no attack to the Senate,' he said. The Department of Justice said it would validate the report. 'The DOJ will not take this as is. We will check on the timelines, the source of the videos,' Justice Secretary Fredderick Vida said. 'As to kung sino ang culprit, ayoko muna i-preempt. As to the type of cases that will be filed, again, I do not want to preempt my panel,' he said. -- With a report from Agence France-Presse Interior Secretary Jonvic Remulla said Tuesday that 'all evidence points that there was no attack on the Senate' during the May 13 gunfire incident. Here's what happened at the Senate, based on the investigation of the Pasay City Police Station and the Criminal Investigation and Detection Group (CIDG) on the shooting incident at the Senate on May 13, Wednesday. 5:23 p.m. Sen. Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa posted appeal to the public for support. 6:14 p.m. Supreme Court did not issue a Temporary Restraining Order (TRO) regarding Dela Rosa's petition. 6:57 p.m. OSAA MGen Mao Aplasca announced somebody will be arrested. Aplasca said they will enforce a total lockdown of the Senate in 5 minutes. 7:05 p.m. The Senate was placed on lockdown. 7:11 p.m. Secretary of Congress transmitted the Articles of Impeachment. 7:22 p.m. House Secretary General Cheloy Garafil along with House Sergeant-at-Arms Melchor Dela Cruz completed the transfer of the Articles of Impeachment against Vice Pres Sara Duterte to the Senate. 7:45 p.m. Gunshots were heard inside the Senate premises. 8:14 p.m. Senate Sec. Mark Llandro Mendoza said that 'perceived NBI agents' entered the Senate. 8:39 p.m. NBI Dir. Melvin Matibag told the media that his agents were not involved. 9:35 p.m. DILG Sec Jonvic Remulla and Senate President Alan Peter Cayetano had a press conference outside the Senate building. May 14, 2026 2:30 a.m. Sen. Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa escaped from the Senate protective custody. Meanwhile, the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) also released a timeline of its movements inside the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) complex. 10 a.m. Security coordination meeting was conducted at the OSAA attended by GSIS Security Officer and the Senate detailed personnel from the PNP, BFP and AFP 2:21 p.m. GSIS President Jose Arnulfo Veloso directed the implementation of red alert status 3:13-4:07 p.m. NBI Agent Aubren Cosidon together with 3 NBI Agents inspected the identified 9 vulnerable points within the GSIS compound 6:17 p.m. Arrival of 29 NBI agents at NBI Main Building escorted by GSIS Security 6:51 p.m. Start of security survey and inspection by NBI agents on 9 vulnerable areas identified by GSIS security Site inspection at medical hallway level 2 6:52-7:01 p.m. Site inspection at identified vulnerable area 5 (Outdoor canteen hallway leading to Kalayaan Garden) 7:17 p.m. 2 NBI agents were detailed to secure vulnerable area 5 (Outdoor canteen hallway leading to Kalayaan Garden) 7:39 p.m. Coordination on the on-going repair works at door fronting Senate lower ground floor lobby (GSIS carpentry area) 7:44 p.m. PMGen Mao Aplasca assembled the Marines Security PNP-PSPG and OSAA security personnel to arrest and gave a 'lock and load' order 7:45 p.m. PMGen Aplasca and company enroute to target area (exit door leading to outdoor canteen hallway and Kalayaan Garden) 7:46-7:49 p.m. Confrontation with the 2 NBI Agents posted at outdoor canteen hallway leading to Kalayaan Garden 7:50 p.m. PMGen Aplasca together with the OSAA personnel abandoning the crime scene or place of incident 7:53 p.m. Evasive maneuver of targeted NBI agents Here's what happened at the Senate, based on the investigation of the Pasay City Police Station and the police Criminal Investigation and Detection Group (CIDG) on the shooting incident at the Senate on May 13, Wednesday. Senator Alan Peter Cayetano answers questions during a press briefing where he confirmed that Sen. Bato Dela Rosa had left the Senate premises in Pasay City on Thursday, May 14, 2026. Maria Tan. ABS-CBN News MANILA -- Senate President Alan Peter Cayetano is ready to take responsibility for how he reacted during the gunfire at the Senate last May 13. This was his assurance on Monday, May 18, after the Senate was criticized for the shooting and departure of Senator Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa. READ: Cayetano holds on to Senate presidency 'Doon sa nagsasabing take responsibility, kayo pumasok dito, kayo namaril eh. Tapos kami pagre-resignin ninyo. Dapat kayo mag-resign. Di ba? Pati yung mga ibang nagsasabing take responsibility for the shooting. I take responsibility for my reaction… How I handled everything,' Cayetano said in a press conference. 'But I cannot take responsibility for them shooting at us. Confirmed na. May gunpowder yung hinuli. Sa loob ng Senado hinuli yun. Until this time may nagsasabi sa Pangulo na ang NBI daw nasa GSIS wala daw dito sa loob at hindi raw nagpaputok. So makikita mo there's lying all over going around,' he added. He insisted that he does not have control over the NBI, so there is no basis to say that the gunfire was scripted. 'Many of those who are calling for my resignation are political operators,' Cayetano said. 'Ang message ko sa NBI, hindi niyo natibag ang Senado. Under Alan Cayetano, hindi niyo natibag ang Senado,' he added. On Monday morning, the Office of the Senate Secretary received the order of preventive suspension against Acting Sergeant-At-Arms Mao Aplasca, as well as the subpoena for the CCTV footage of the gunfire. READ: Senate has received 6-month suspension order vs Aplasca -- Ombudsman Cayetano clarified that they did not refuse to accept the documents last Friday, May 15. 'Kasi wala naman tayong trabaho ng Friday, Saturday, Sunday. Wala naman official na pwedeng tumanggap,' Cayetano said. 'It's not true na walang CCTV,' he added. '[Interior] Secretary Jonvic [Remulla] called me, yun ang una niyang hiningi, secure the CCTVs. When he got here, inulit ko sa harap, two copies… So yung isa nasa PNP.' According to Cayetano, senators will still discuss if they will replace Acting Sergeant-at-Arms Mao Aplasca. For Cayetano, the NBI and Department of Justice (DOJ) should be the ones investigated. Asked if there was a lack of communication between the Senate and Government Service Insurance System (GSIS), which requested security from the NBI, Cayetano said, 'Hindi kulang. Sinadya nila. Sinadya nila, ang GSIS never nagpapasok ng NBI kung may problema sa peace and order. Pulis parati yun.' 'Number two, ibabarena mo yung pinto na lalabas yung mga senador. Isasara mo yung gate sa likod kung kailan lalabas yung mga senador. Tapos nung tinanong namin, wala daw NBI sa loob. Bakit yung ngayon yung may pulbura sa kamay at dito sa loob ng Senate nahuli?' he added. He said it would have been simpler if the GSIS informed them why there was drilling but alleged that the plan would have been destroyed. 'At this point in time, the burden is on them to prove to us that it's a series of coincidences. Kasi pag ni-reverse engineer namin, pinlano talaga nila na pakita sa Senate na ganyan ha, sige may numero kayo, nandiyan si Bato ganyan ha, papaligiran naman namin kayo ng mga armado,' Cayetano said. 'Gusto ni [Acting Justice] Secretary [Fredderick] Vida mag-imbestiga ang NBI, DOJ pero sila ang dapat imbestigahan eh. Kaya nga ang message ko sa kanya, hindi ka pwedeng Vida kung kontra-Vida ka. So kung gusto mo mag-imbestiga, kailangan neutral ka,' he added. Senate President Alan Peter Cayetano is ready to take responsibility for how he reacted during the gunfire at the Senate last May 13. Ronald Dela Rosa speaks to the media during an ambush interview at the Philippine Senate on Wednesday, May 13, 2026, to address ongoing issues pertaining to the ICC arrest warrant. Dela Rosa appeals for understanding and sympathy from his fellow uniformed service comrades. Maria Tan, ABS-CBN News MANILA — National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) director Melvin Matibag on Tuesday said authorities have leads on the whereabouts of Senator Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa, but declined to provide details due to the ongoing investigation. Did Robin Padilla help Bato leave Senate? Matibag added that if there is anyone who may know Dela Rosa’s location, it would be Senator Robinhood Padilla. Padilla on Monday said Dela Rosa was supposedly hiding from an alleged kidnapper, which is why he left the Senate last May 14. Padilla refused to answer if he helped Dela Rosa leave the Senate premises last Thursday amid claims that they left together. 'Mahirap naman po mag-announce na nandiririto siya kasi people try to speculate,' Matibag said in a press briefing. 'Marami pong lead na binigay, but we have a lead kung nasaan talaga pero in the meantime we will suspend ‘yong announcement kung nasaan talaga siya.' 'If there’s somebody or person that should be asked, ang unang-unang dapat tanungin si Senator Robin Padilla dahil siya ‘yong huling kasama,' he added. Dela Rosa, a former national police chief who oversaw the previous administration’s war on drugs, has an outstanding warrant from the International Criminal Court (ICC). Matibag urged Dela Rosa to face the legal process and respond to the ICC warrant. 'Galing siya sa law enforcement… it's better na harapin niya ‘yung warrant niya, mag-submit siya. Harapin niya ‘yung kanyang pananagutan sa batas,' Matibag said. The NBI chief stressed that the ICC warrant remains active and that Philippine law enforcement agencies are obligated to monitor developments and coordinate under existing commitments to the international tribunal. 'Nasa wanted list siya ng ICC. So, we have a commitment to the International Criminal Court. As a law enforcement, naka-monitor tayo,' Matibag said, adding that operational details on monitoring efforts are being withheld to avoid compromising law enforcement activities. Matibag also noted that Dela Rosa’s camp has filed a petition before the Supreme Court, saying authorities will await the court’s ruling while continuing coordination among agencies. Meanwhile, the NBI has released new body-worn camera footage showing that the arrested NBI driver linked to the recent Senate gunfire incident was not carrying any firearm and was in a different area when the shooting occurred. Authorities earlier said no firearm was recovered from the suspect, though ammunition was allegedly found in his sling bag. 'Wala akong pinaputok': NBI driver says he was unarmed, denies firing gun inside Senate The NBI volunteer also tested positive for gunpowder nitrates , but officials cautioned that this alone does not conclusively prove involvement in firing a weapon. Matibag said it is possible that the residue came from the second floor of the Senate building, where the gunfire incident occurred and where the suspect was brought after he was arrested from the GSIS building. 'In the chain of custody, dapat nakukuha mo ‘yong baril tapos ipa-paraffin mo siya. That is the possibility na nagpaputok ng baril but pwede ka ring nakakuha ng nitrate, no’ng gunpowder in other means. Halimbawa ‘yong humawak sa’yo kung meron gunpowder sa kamay,' Matibag added. 'Nakita ko when he was arrested from the GSIS, dinala siya sa Senate lounge sa second floor, idinapa siya kung saan mismo nagpaputok nang nagpaputok ng baril ‘yong mga OSAA.' The NBI driver has since been temporarily released after posting bail on Monday. He remains one of the subjects of an ongoing investigation by the Criminal Investigation and Detection Group (CIDG). The NBI director also insisted that their agents were at the GSIS Building solely to secure the area upon the request of GSIS General Manager John Arnulfo Veloso on May 13. He clarified that they were not there to arrest Senator Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa, but only to 'monitor the situation without entering the Senate premises.' 'Kung narinig n’yo ‘yong video, ang sinasabi no’ng mga agent, ‘Do not engage!’ Do not engage!’ Kaya kami nag-retreat… Walang assault. Walang service [of arrest warrant],' Matibag said. President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. previously said that he had given no instruction to arrest Dela Rosa. Matibag also responded to the appeal of Senate President Alan Peter Cayetano asking the Ombudsman to suspend him following the gunfire incident. According to Matibag, if command responsibility is the basis of Cayetano’s argument, then the senator should also be suspended because the Office of the Senate Sergeant-at-Arms is under his supervision. 'Unang-una po hindi naman ako nandoon na nagpaputok ng baril. Siguro ang sinasabi because I’m the head of the NBI kaya dapat suspendihin ako dahil command responsibility ko ‘yong mga tao, e kung gano’n po ‘yong magiging basehan, ang unang-una pong sususpendihin si Senator Alan Peter Cayetano dahil command responsibility niya [ang Office of the] Sergeant-at-Arms, e. In fact, inaamin ni Sergeant-at-Arms sila ang nag-uusap doon sa pangyayari,' he said. On Monday, the Senate received the preventive suspension order against Acting OSAA chief Mao Aplasca and the subpoena for a copy of the CCTV footage related to the gunfire incident. The Department of Justice, Office of the Ombudsman, and the PNP-Criminal Investigation and Detection Group have formed an investigating panel to probe the incident, Matibag added. The NBI added that if there is anyone who may know Sen. Dela Rosa’s location, it would be Senator Robinhood Padilla. MANILA -- Interior Secretary Jonvic Remulla said Tuesday that 'all evidence points that there was no attack on the Senate' during the May 13 gunfire incident that preceded Sen. Ronald Dela Rosa's departure from the building. Interior Secretary Jonvic Remulla said Tuesday that 'all evidence points that there was no attack on the Senate' during the May 13 gunfire incident.

title: DND, AFP reject China’s claims, assert sovereignty over West Philippine Sea features
body: In a statement, the DND said Pag-asa Island and Lawak Island are part of the Kalayaan Island Group (KIG), which has long been administered by the Philippines under the municipality of Kalayaan in Palawan. The department said development and improvement activities in these areas are intended to support the welfare, safety, and livelihood of Filipinos living and working there. The DND also rejected China’s assertion of 'indisputable sovereignty' over nearly the entire South China Sea through its so-called nine-dash line, saying such claims lacked legal basis under international law. It pointed to the 2016 arbitral ruling under the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS), which found no legal basis for China’s historical claims in the South China Sea. The DND added that the arbitral tribunal found China’s large-scale reclamation and construction activities in the area caused severe damage to the marine environment and unlawfully interfered with Philippine sovereign rights within its exclusive economic zone (EEZ). 'The Department of National Defense remains steadfast in safeguarding Philippine territory, sovereign rights, and jurisdiction in the West Philippine Sea, in accordance with international law and in pursuit of a peaceful, stable, and rules-based maritime domain,' the DND said. The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP), meanwhile, maintained that the country has full authority over its occupied features in the West Philippine Sea and will continue enhancing facilities there. AFP spokesperson for the West Philippine Sea Rear Admiral Roy Vincent Trinidad (Reserve) stressed that the Philippines does not need permission from any country to carry out development projects within areas under its jurisdiction. 'The Philippines has absolute sovereignty and complete jurisdiction over our nine features in the West Philippine Sea,' Trinidad told reporters at Camp Aguinaldo, Quezon City on Tuesday. 'Any repair, construction, enhancement, or development that we do on these features are all within our legal rights. We do not need to seek the permission of any country,' he added. Trinidad said infrastructure work continues on Pag-asa Island and other Philippine-held features to support both military personnel and civilian communities in the area. 'We have been doing improvements, enhancements, development, repair, and fortification to ensure that these islands will be able to support any deployed troops and the local community,' he said. The AFP reported a lower number of monitored Chinese vessels in the West Philippine Sea from May 12 to 18 compared to the previous week, although Chinese presence remained persistent in key areas. AFP data showed 27 Chinese vessels were monitored during the period, consisting of 18 China Coast Guard (CCG) vessels and nine People’s Liberation Army Navy (PLA-N) ships. Bajo de Masinloc recorded the highest concentration with eight CCG vessels and six PLA-N ships. Ayungin Shoal followed with seven CCG vessels and one PLA-N vessel. Two CCG and two PLA-N vessels were monitored near Pag-asa Island, while one CCG vessel was observed in Escoda Shoal. The Department of National Defense (DND) on Wednesday rejected China’s latest statements on Pag-asa Island and Lawak Island, reiterating that the Philippines has the legal right to develop and improve areas under its jurisdiction in the West Philippine Sea. The PCG's new station in Pag-asa Island in the West Philippine Sea opened on Friday. Bianca Dava, ABS-CBN News

title: Marcos Jr. says ‘birthday wish’: Accomplish all goals or begin some before term ends
body: 'Simple lang naman ang aking mga ano eh, mga hangarin sa – lalo na dito para sa administrasyon na ito, matapos namin lahat ng aming ginagawa or syempre marami diyan, hindi talaga matatapos sa anim na taon,' he said in his latest podcast released Tuesday, September 9. 'Kaya sana masimulan lang para kahit papaano, sa susunod na administrasyon ay matutuloy dahil sayang naman at maganda naman ang nagiging resulta,' he noted. Marcos Jr. also touted the economy’s state and the praises of other nations to Filipinos. 'Maganda ang takbo ng ekonomiya, maganda ang – mataas ang tingin sa atin ng ating mga – ng ating mga kaibigan sa buong mundo, mataas ang tingin sa Pilipinas at lalo pang mas mataas ang tingin sa Pilipino,' he said. lahat, unang-una sasabihin: Ang gagaling ng mga Pilipino doon sa amin,' the Chief Executive added. 'Ang mga hari, mga presidente, mga prime minister, lagi: Sana magpadala pa kayo. Magpadala pa kayo ng Pilipino para tutulong sa amin,' he noted. 'And ‘yan ang talagang – that's where the – perfect ‘yung expression na nakakataba ng puso,' the President concluded. As his birthday approaches, President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. revealed his wish is for his administration to accomplish all of its goals. MANILA — As his birthday approaches, President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. revealed his wish is for his administration to accomplish all of its goals.

title: 25 countries suspend postal services to US over tariffs: UN
body: The Trump administration said late last month that it will abolish a tax exemption on small packages entering the United States from August 29. The move has sparked a flurry of announcements from postal services, including in France, Britain, Germany, Italy, India, Australia and Japan, that most US-bound packages would no longer be accepted. The United Nations' Universal Postal Union said it had already been advised by 25 member countries that their postal operators 'have suspended their outbound postal services to the US, citing uncertainties specifically related to transit services'. It said the suspensions will remain in place until there is more clarity on how US authorities plan to implement the announced measures. The UPU did not provide a list of postal services it had heard from. The organization, which was established in 1874 and counts 192 member states, warned that the new US measures would 'entail considerable operational changes for postal operators around the world'. From Friday, the UPU said the measures would require postal carriers delivering packages to the United States 'to collect customs duties from senders in advance', on behalf of the US Customs and Border Protection agency. India's communications ministry said at the weekend that Trump's executive order issued last month requires transport carriers or other 'qualified parties' approved by US authorities to collect and remit the tariff duties. But 'several critical processes relating to the designation of 'qualified parties' and mechanisms for duty collection and remittance remain undefined,' it said in a statement. Under the new US measures, individual customers will still be able to send documents and items worth up to $100 as presents to the country without being taxed. But anything above that value is expected to be hit with the same tariff rates applied to other imports from the sending country. That means 15 percent for countries in the European Union and 50 percent for India. And Germany's postal service DHL warned last week that even exempted items would be subject to extra checks to prevent the service being used for commercial goods. The UPU stressed that it was 'taking all possible measures to prepare its member countries for the impacts (the new measures) may have on their postal flows'. UPU chief Masahiko Metoki had sent a letter to US Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Monday to convey member countries' concern regarding the operational disruptions, it said. Pointing to the short implementation timeline and voicing particular concern over the impact on the delivery of e-commerce items, the union said it was 'working with the relevant US authorities to ensure that information on the operational requirements of the measures is communicated effectively to other member countries'. In parallel, it said it was working with 'relevant postal stakeholders' to help find sustainable solutions, including one initiative aimed at developing a system to facilitate duty collection and remittance across its network. Former US President and Republican presidential candidate Donald Trump speaks during an election night event at the West Palm Beach Convention Center in West Palm Beach, Florida, on November 6, 2024. Jim Watson, AFP WASHINGTON -- President Donald Trump on Monday threatened further tariffs and export restrictions against countries that he says are targeting US tech firms.
