Duterte in August said he is in favor of postponing the synchronized barangay and Sangguniang Kabataan, amid fears that drug money will be used in the elections. Members of the two houses of congress also voiced their proposals on the postponement of the October polls, saying there are various compelling reasons for the postponement. But House Speaker Pantaleon Alvarez would rather have the positions of the barangay kagawad and SK officials removed from the polls altogether. Senator Leila De Lima meanwhile cited the proposed shift to a federalism and the anti-drugs campaign of the Duterte administration as reasons that would necessitate the poll postponement. Senator Joel Villanueva, for his part, said delaying the elections will give Comelec ample time to improve voter turn-out. The Commission on Elections (Comelec) subsequently scaled down its own preparations for this October's election once it became clear that Congress intends to reschedule the election and that the president also backs its position. Senator Antonio Trillanes on Wednesday said he believes the current administration could delay the 2022 national elections as was done with the barangay elections originally scheduled for October 2016.

title: DOJ defends probe on Sara Duterte's possible anti-terror law violation
body: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) is probing Duterte for possible acts of inciting to sedition, grave threats, and violation of Republic Act 11479 or the Anti-Terrorism Law when she claimed that she hired someone to kill President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. if an alleged plot against her succeeds. In a press conference on Nov. 27 in Zamboanga, Duterte questioned the investigation, claiming that the government is using a 'playbook' of what it allegedly do to expelled Negros Oriental Rep. Arnolfo 'Arnie' Teves, Jr. in applying the Anti-Terrorism Law against her. The law was signed in 2020 by her father, former President Rodrigo Duterte. DOJ Undersecretary Jesse Andres clarified there is a different process in designating a person as 'terrorist'. 'Marami po akong naririnig, na sinasabing bakit gagawing terorista si Vice President, hindi po. Iba pong proseso ang tinatawag nating designation as terrorist. Iba pong ebidensya ang kailangan doon. Ngunit hindi naman kailangan na ma-designate as terrorist, bago ka lumapag o posibleng lumapag sa probisyon ng Anti-Terror Law,' he said. Andres said they are looking at section 4 of the law which defines acts of terrorism as basis for Duterte’s possible violation for the law. 'Dahil titignan po natin, Section 4 of the ATL anybody who engages or attempts to commit death or serious bodily injury to any person in the context of causing destabilization, or sowing fear, yan ang tinatawag nating Acts of Terrorism. And any attempt, or proposal to commit these acts is already punishable,' the DOJ official added. He added that Duterte could possibly be held liable for 'conspiring' to execute the said acts. 'Kaya nililinaw natin, hindi natin bina-brand as terrorist si Vice President pero posible syang maging liable under the Anti-Terror Law simply by conspiring,' Andres noted. The Department of Justice (DOJ) has clarified that the government does not intend to brand Vice President Sara Duterte as terrorist. MANILA — The Department of Justice (DOJ) has clarified that the government does not intend to brand Vice President Sara Duterte as terrorist.

title: Manila gov't confirms donation of motorbikes from alleged Chinese spies, denies further encounters with donor
body: However, the LGU denied any further interactions between Manila Mayor Honey Lacuna and the donor, Wang Yongyi, of the Qiaoxing Volunteer Group, following the public turnover of the motorbikes. This clarification follows a Reuters report on February 28, 2025, which raised concerns about donations from individuals linked to Chinese espionage activities. The Manila LGU said that the donation process, which involved a total of 10 motorcycles, was conducted transparently and in accordance with established procedures. 'Consistent with current and established practice, the City Legal Officer and the City General Services Office reviewed the deed of donation per se and checked the documents about the motorcycles,' the statement read. 'The deed of donation and motorcycles from Sinski Motorcycle Philippines were found to be in order. The motorcycles were registered with the Land Transportation Office,' it added. The donated motorcycles included one 2015 model and nine 2016 model gasoline-fueled vehicles, which were handed over for use by the Manila Police District (MPD). The LGU also clarified that the only public encounter between Lacuna and Wang took place during the turnover ceremony of the motorcycles. 'There were no other subsequent nor prior encounters between them,' the Manila LGU said. 'There were no cash donations or any other donations, projects, or discussion with Wang about any of the things he said during his remarks at the turnover of the motorcycles for the Manila Police District.' The statement emphasized that the donation was limited to the motorcycles, with no further commitments made by either party. 'We remain open to any further inquiries from you,' the Manila LGU concluded in its statement, reiterating its commitment to transparency and cooperation. The Manila Local Government Unit (LGU) confirmed that motorcycles, reportedly donated by alleged Chinese spies, were received by the city’s police force. MANILA — The Manila Local Government Unit confirmed that motorcycles, reportedly donated by alleged Chinese spies, were received by the city’s police force.

title: Instead of total ban, PAGCOR wants stricter regulation on online gambling
body: In an interview over DZMM Teleradyo on Tuesday, Tengco acknowledged the problems arising from online gambling, but emphasized that these are caused by illegal sites -- not the ones licensed by PAGCOR. 'Napakadami pong ilegal na hanggang ngayon ay nag-ooperate pa sa pangkasalukuyan. At yan po ay masasabi kong dahilan kung bakit ang ating industriya ay gumugulo, yun pong proliferation kasi ng online illegal operators na unfortunately hindi po nagmumula dito sa ating bansa kung hindi nagmumula sa ibang bansa na ang tinatarget ay customer na Filipino,' Tengco explained. 'Regulation is the key and the answer, not a total ban. Sapagkat kapag tinotal ban mo yan, mas mabibigatan at magkakaproblema ang ating mga kababayan sapagkat wala nang kontrol lalong walang regulasyon, lalong ma-a-agrabyado ang ating mga kapwa Pilipino,' he added. He said the government stands to lose billions if online gambling is banned completely. 'Sa PAGCOR alone, for 2024, humigit kumulang po nasa P50 billion ang licensed fees na kinita. Bukod po ito sa ibinabayad nung mga operators sa Bureau of Internal Revenue, ano po, sa BIR. Kasi ang totoo, isa sa pinakamalaking online gaming company na may lisensya sa PAGCOR ang balita ko ay mahigit tatlumpu o apatnapung bilyong piso ang ibinayad sa taxes,' Tengco said. He added: 'Daang bilyon po ang kinikita sa pangkasalukuyan ng gobyerno directly at indirectly.' He also pointed out the jobs created by online gambling. 'Huwag po nating isasantabi meron na pong 32,000 more or less, directly employed by the industry. Pangalawa, meron pong mga ancillary businesses ano po. Nandiyan po ang mga security guard, drivers, messengers, ang mga restaurants kung saan kumakain ang mga to at iba’t ibang transporation companies na ginagamit itong mga involved sa online gaming industry. So yun po ang indirect,' he noted. 'Kaya ang epekto po nito talaga ay daang bilyon sa ating bansa ang kita na posibleng mawala kung maisasabatas yung total ban.' Sen. Juan Miguel Zubiri on Monday called for a total ban on all forms of online gambling , even those authorized by PAGCOR, as he highlighted the problems caused by what he called a 'silent epidemic.' Meanwhile, the Anakbayan party-list filed House Bill No. 1351, or Kontra E-Sugal Bill , which seeks to limits access to online gambling platforms to individuals aged 21 and above, and mandates platforms to require government-issued identification for registration. The Catholic Bishops' Conference of the Philippines (CBCP) has also joined calls from the public and lawmakers to tighten regulations surrounding online gambling, calling it a new plague that destroys individuals and families . Tengco acknowledged the societal ills caused by illegal online gambling but stressed that they already have several regulations in place and are looking to employ even more safeguards. Among these are: ensuring that those who gamble are aged 21 up and requiring a proof identification before one can access an online gambling site. Meanwhile, in the process of procurement is an Artificial Intelligence (AI) tool that imposes a cooling off period. 'Mayroon pong AI-powered monitoring system kung saan pag nakita na mas malaki ang talo mo, halimbawa, at mas maraming talo ka kaysa sa panalo, ikaw din ay magkakaroon ng cooling off period kung saan hindi ka na rin makapaglalaro hanggang maiayos mo yung pamamaraan mo or yung habit mo ng pagsusugal,' Tengco said. PAGCOR is also set to sign a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the Ad Standards Council to regulate billboards advertising online gambling. Tengco said they have talked to licensees about this who did not object. PAGCOR has also proposed a regulation on ads during prime time. 'Sa prime time media, minungkahi po ng PAGCOR sa Ad Standards Council na tayo po ay hindi na dapat mag-advertise, ang online gaming licensees ng PAGCOR, during prime time. So may window na po, yung parang 5:30 to 8:30, yun ang prime time, bawal na po. Ilalabas na natin ang reglamento na hindi na po puwedeng mag-advertise,' Tengco said. PAGCOR is also coordinating with a foundation that can help gambling addicts recover, and will put up a 24/7 hotline in the next few months. Tengco said he agrees with imposing a minimum bet, but will still need to determine the amount. He appealed to the public to use online gambling platforms which are accredited by PAGCOR. Kalookan Bishop Pablo Virgilio David leads a requiem Mass for the late Pope Francis at the San Roque Cathedral on April 22, 2025. Maria Tan, ABS-CBN News/File MANILA — The Catholic Bishops' Conference of the Philippines (CBCP) has joined calls from the public and lawmakers to tighten regulations surrounding online gambling, calling it a new plague that destroys individuals and families. In a statement posted by CBCP News on Facebook, Caloocan Bishop and CBCP President Pablo Virgilio David reflected on the adverse impacts of online gambling, saying they were breaking their silence because of the stories they had heard about how betting addiction destroyed lives. The Catholic bishops expressed 'deep concern' about how many Filipinos, including the youth, were being kept in bondage by internet gaming, which they called the 'new face of gambling' after the government outlawed Philippine offshore gaming operators and e-sabong. They said taking advantage of another person's weakness for profit is a sin. The CBCP urged the government to put controls on online payment systems to make it harder for people, especially young people, to access online gambling sites. They also called on other sectors of society to recognize that gambling addiction is a public health issue that should be addressed through proper education, legislation, and treatment. 'Malinaw na ang online gambling ay hindi na simpleng libangan lamang. Isa na itong malalim at malawak na suliraning moral, na nakakubli sa anyo ng libangan at teknolohiya,' CBCP said. (It’s clear that online gambling is no longer just a simple form of entertainment. It has become a deep and widespread moral problem, hidden behind the guise of leisure and technology.) 'At kahit ito'y inaangkin na 'karapatang pantao,' hindi pa rin nangangahulugang tama ito, lalo na kung ito naman ay nagbubunga ng kapahamakan sa mismong tao. Ang pagkasugapa sa online gambling ay isang anyo ng pagkaalipin,' they said. (And even if they claim it to be a 'human right,' that does not make it right, especially when it brings harm to the person themselves. Addiction to online gambling is a form of slavery.) Several lawmakers in recent days proposed varying degrees of regulation on online gambling, including banning the youth from participating in it and restricting e-wallets from accommodating online gaming sites. Sen. Juan Miguel Zubiri, meanwhile, is pushing for a total ban on all forms of online gambling . On Monday, Communications Undersecretary Claire Castro said President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. would not oppose any measure that would curb gambling addiction among Filipinos. The CBCP clarified that it was not outright against forms of entertainment but said that when these begin to destroy lives and families, it becomes necessary to make an appeal and issue a warning. CBCP TO PEOPLE WITH GAMBLING ADDICTION: YOU'RE NOT ALONE The group said it was praying for people enslaved by gambling addiction, telling them that they are not alone. 'Ang inyong pinagdadaanan ay hindi kahinaan lang ng loob, kundi madalas ay nag-uugat sa malalim na sugat sa iyong buhay na maaaring lumikha ng lamat sa ating mga pag-iisip o damdamin,' CBCP said. 'Hindi na nila alam kung paano sila nalubog sa ganitong kalagayan — kaya't sila ay namumuhay sa hiya, takot, at kawalan ng pag-asa.' (What you are going through is not merely a weakness of will but often stems from deep wounds in your life that may have caused cracks in your thoughts or emotions. They no longer know how they ended up in such a state, which is why they live in shame, fear, and hopelessness.) Gambling 'alters the mind, disrupts sound judgment, and steals the peace within us,' CBCP said. They added that gambling addiction victims need compassion, support, and guidance, not shame or judgment. 'Hindi ito simpleng problema ng indibidwal. Isa na itong pampublikong krisis ng kalusugan sa lipunan, katulad na rin ng droga at iba pang bisyo,' they said. (This is not merely an individual problem. It has become a public health crisis in society, much like drugs and other vices.) 'Uunlad ba tayo kung nalulusaw ang mga moral values natin? Magiging matatag ba tayo, maiaahon ba natin sa kahirapan ang bansa o ang pamilya natin, kung nagiging manhid tayo sa mali at masama? Hindi ba natin nakikita ang panganib sa kalusugan, mga krimen, karahasan, at banta sa kaligtasan na dulot ng talamak na sugal? Sugal ba ang sagot sa kahirapan?' (Can we truly progress if our moral values are eroding? Can we build a strong nation or lift our families out of poverty if we become numb to what is wrong and harmful? Do we not see the dangers to health, the crimes, violence, and threats to safety brought about by rampant gambling? Is gambling really the answer to poverty?) — With reports from Victoria Tulad and Katrina Domingo, ABS-CBN News The Catholic Bishops' Conference of the Philippines has joined calls from the public and lawmakers to tighten regulations surrounding online gambling, calling it a new plague that destroys individuals and families. Citizens use their phones in Quezon City on August 16, 2020. Mark Demayo, ABS-CBN News/File MANILA — A bill seeking to regulate online gambling platforms in the country 'to address reports of worsening gambling addiction particularly among the youth,' has been filed in the House. The House Bill No. 1351, or Kontra E-Sugal Bill filed by Anakbayan party-list, limits access to online gambling platforms to individuals aged 21 and above, and mandates platforms to require government-issued identification for registration, said the party-list. 'We cannot gamble away our youth's future. Our children cannot become collateral in the jackpot dreams of gambling tycoons,' said Anakbayan Rep. Jose Manuel Tadeo 'Chel' Diokno. 'We need a whole-of-government approach if we want to combat the ill effects of online gambling. 'Wag nating hayaang isugal ng ating mga kababayan, hindi lang ang kanilang pera kundi pati buhay at pangarap,' he added. The bill bans e-wallets and super apps from advertising and linking to online gambling. It also prohibits online gambling ads in public spaces. Under the bill, the Philippine Amusement and Gaming Corporation (PAGCOR) is mandated to set daily, weekly, and monthly betting and loss limits. Breaching the limits entails auto-suspension. The bill also seeks to impose a 10 percent tax on online gambling, which the party-list said will fund addiction rehabilitation, education, enforcement, and research. There will also be a National Online Gambling Self-Exclusion Registry to be maintained and administered by PAGCOR. Once the bill is passed into a law, the government is also required to 'adopt a comprehensive, evidence-based, and multi-sectoral approach to prevent and mitigate gambling-related harms.' Aside from the Akbayan-filed bill, House Bill 721 or the proposed Anti-Online Online Gambling Promotions in E-Wallets Act has also been filed, seeking to restrict e-wallets’ promotion of online gambling platforms. E-wallets to be restricted from linking with online gambling under House bill Manila 2nd District Rep. Rolando Valeriano also filed on Monday a bill to 'shut down online gambling operators' in the country. According to Valeriano, he has seen a proliferation of online gambling among the youth in Manila. ''Yung district ko ng Maynila— nakikita ko na, mga kapitbahay ko na, mga bata may hawak na cellphone, nagsusugal na. ‘Di ba? So dapat na ‘tong mahinto. Huwag na nating antayin na gumrabe pa, na maging malala pa, bago natin gawan ng paraan,' Valeriano said. Meanwhile, Sen. Pia and Alan Peter Cayetano also filed a bill in Senate, which aside from users, will also penalize online gambling operators and enablers. Proposed ban on online gambling also penalizes bettors, endorsers The Senate bill also seeks to establish an Online Gambling Control Task Force under the Office of the President. PAGCOR, for its part, vowed to follow any regulations to be set by the law to curb Filipinos’ engagement with online gambling. It also assured the public that it is intensifying its crackdown against illegal online gaming activities. -- with a report from Agatha Gregorio, ABS-CBN News PAGCOR says to follow regulation vs online gambling The Akbayan Party-list has filed a bill regulating online gambling platforms in the country 'to address reports of worsening gambling addiction particularly among the youth.' An e-sabong app remains shut on May 4, 2022. Jonathan Cellona, ABS-CBN News/File MANILA — President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. will not oppose any measure that will curb gambling addiction among Filipinos, a Malacañang official said on Monday, when asked about plans to hike taxes or impose an outright ban on online gambling operations in the Philippines. The Department of Finance (DOF) earlier said it plans to impose a new tax on online gaming, while the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas is set to issue a circular mandating banks and electronic wallets to come up with stricter measures against online gambling addiction, possibly including 'various limits to gaming access.' 'Batid po ng Pangulo ang maaaring mangyari sa mga gumon sa sugal at hindi niya naman po tututulan basta po mayroon pong sapat na pag-aaral para po—tungkol sa buwis na ipapataw man,' Communications Undersecretary Claire Castro said in a press conference. 'Gusto nating limitahan ang ganitong klaseng mga pagsusugal at mabawasan ang mga gumon sa sugal at ito po ay para sa taumbayan,' she said. 'Ang lahat ng puwedeng suggestion at maaaring batas para ito ay masawata, hindi naman po iyan tututulan ng ating Pangulo,' she said. Meanwhile, Castro said the President will have to look into several legislative bills seeking for a total ban on online gambling in the Philippines. On Monday, the Akbayan Party-list filed a bill seeking the regulation of online gambling platforms in the country 'to address reports of worsening gambling addiction particularly among the youth.' Sen. Juan Miguel Zubiri, for his part, has pushed for a total ban on all forms of online gambling, even those authorized by the Philippine Amusement and Gaming Corporation (PAGCOR). READ: Zubiri pushes for total online gambling ban, appeals to Marcos Jr. to address issue READ: House bill seeks to ban youth from online gambling 'Ang Pangulo po ay nakikinig kung anong nangyayari sa ating mga kababayan at kung anuman po ang bill na ipapasa nila, ito'y pag-aaralang mabuti kung ito'y makakabuti sa ating ekonomiya and at the same time makakabuti para sa taumbayan,' the Communications official said. 'Pero paiigtingin pa po maliban po dito sa mga online gaming sites na licensed or lehitimo naman, paiigtingin po lalo ang paglaban sa mga illegitimate o iyong hindi tama or hindi rehistradong mga online gaming sites,' she said. Last year, Marcos Jr. banned all Philippine Offshore Gaming Operations here after months of investigations showed that illegal activities have been happening in these gambling hubs. Castro said she is not privy if the President will include his stance on online gaming operations in his state of the nation address later this month. President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. will not oppose any measure that will curb gambling addiction among Filipinos, a Malacañang official said on Monday. Senator Juan Miguel Zubiri during a press conference in Pasay City on September 26, 2024. Mark Demayo, ABS-CBN News/File MANILA — Sen. Juan Miguel Zubiri is pushing for a total ban on all forms of online gambling, even those authorized by the Philippine Amusement and Gaming Corporation (PAGCOR), as he highlighted the problems caused by what he called a 'silent epidemic.' In line with this, Zubiri filed his Senate Bill 142 or 'Anti-Online Gambling Act of 2025' which 'prohibits all forms of online gambling in the country.' 'Maglalagay po tayo ng blanket ban sa lahat ng uri ng online gambling,' Zubiri said in a press conference on Monday. 'Hindi na po ito basta regulation, total ban na po ito para tuluyan na nating matigil ang online gambling na umuubos ng pera ng ating mga kababayan mula sa iba't ibang sektor. And similar to how we banned e-sabong, similar how we banned POGO (Philippine Offshore Gaming Operators), ito ay magandang hakbang para matanggal na po itong adiksyon sa ating mga kababayan.' 'Hindi lang oras ang nauubos dito kundi life savings ng ating mga kababayan. Kaya dapat na po natin ipagbawal ito kahit ang mga authorized online games ng PAGCOR,' he added. Zubiri lamented what happened to a 16-year-old in Bukidnon who supposedly lost P100 to online gambling and committed suicide out of guilt for spending his parents’ savings. He also shared how some farm workers lose an average of P500 to P1,000 to online gambling when their disposable income is not that big to begin with. 'The taxes earned are not worth the social cost. Nasisira na po ang buhay ng ating mga kababayan. Nag-aaway-away na po ang ating mga pamilya. Tumataas ang kaso ng krimen. Nababaon na sa gabundok na utang ang manggagawang Pilipino which then severely affects their mental health,' Zubiri stated. He believes the government can let go of the revenue from online gambling, which, he said, reached more than P47 billion in the first quarter of 2025. 'The 47 billion is not enough to justify that. As a matter of fact, napakaliit niyan. Proper tax collection ng BIR, proper tax collection ng Bureau of Customs ay makukuha mo na yan,' Zubiri said. He appealed to President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. to address the problem of online gambling and include this in the next State of the Nation Address (SONA). 'It will take the President a lot of political will because Filipino-operated ito. Marami rin siyang kaibigan na nagmamay-ari nitong mga kumpanya na ito. And I think he will show great leadership, he will show great leadership if he puts an end to this online gaming and gambling for the Filipino people,' Zubiri said. 'Na makikita talaga na without fear or favor for the good of the country, we can earn this P47 billion elsewhere, let’s stop online gaming. I would love to hear that from the President even in his State of the Nation Address or some sort of statement saying that let us allow Congress to study the ban on online gaming,' he added. 'So Mr. President, let's do something about this. We can't just take this sitting down. That income is not enough justification for the social ills it will cause our country. And before you leave in 2028 at hindi po natin aksyonan ito, our country might be in a worse off position than what you entered in 2022,' Zubiri said. He also called on celebrities not to use their influence to encourage online gambling, and for advertising companies to take down billboards and posters about this. For Zubiri, a total ban is more effective than regulation because there will always be those who will circumvent the law. His bill enumerates prohibited acts, such as: - establishment, operation, conduct of online gaming in the Philippines, which shall cover those offshore or foreign-operated digital gambling platforms or applications; - acceptance of any form of betting or wager by digital gambling platforms or applications through online; - facilitating the use of electronic wallet or e-wallet and other digital payment systems for online gambling; - advertising through traditional media online and social media, including posts of content creators, vloggers, influencers, which promote online gambling and specific digital gambling platforms. The bill also proposes a fine of not less than P20 million and suspension of license for six months on the first offense, a fine of at least P50 million or one year suspension of license on the second offense, and a fine of not less than P100 million and permanent revocation of license or permit on the third and succeeding offenses. Offenders may also be subject to criminal prosecution with imprisonment of not more than six years. Zubiri also shared the concern of some malls about their sales going down, which they think might be linked to online gambling. 'I spoke to manufacturers, I spoke to mall operators doon sa probinsya… bumagsak ang sales,' he said. 'They also told me their suspicion is online gaming. Kasi nauubos na yung pera mo.' 'We cannot deny that manufacturing is down, consumption is down, consumer spending is down. Would it be because of this? If you ask me, my opinion, yes,' he added. Zubiri said they will call representatives of malls to the hearings the Senate will conduct regarding his bill. He said rich and powerful people own online gambling sites and it will be tough fight to have it banned, but said the situation will only worsen if this is not acted on. 'If we continue this in the next two years, by the end of the term of the President, we'll be a nation of online gamblers, a nation of addicted gamblers, and a drop, and you'll see all across the board, in all sectors, there will be a drop in productivity, drop in consumer spending, there will be a drop even in energy utilization kasi wala tayong pampayad ng kuryente,' he said. He is also worried this will lead to an increase crimes. An e-sabong app remains shut on May 4, 2022. Jonathan Cellona, ABS-CBN News/File Meanwhile, Lanao del Sur 1st District Rep. Zia Adiong and TGP Party-list Rep. Jose 'Bong' Teves expressed their support for the bill filed by Senator Juan Miguel Zubiri. Adiong believes that if the country is able to put a stop POGOs, it should also ban online gambling. 'Marami po sa mga ibang countries na talagang bina-ban po ‘yung gambling. In fact, na-ban nga po natin ang POGO dito. If we ban the Pogo in real and physical form, why can't we do that virtually? Kasi sa totoo lang, itong gambling na ito, may nakakasira hindi lang sa mental health ng ating mga individual, but ‘yung moral fiber ng society kasi it actually reinforces ‘yung addiction ng tao,' Adiong said on Monday, July 7. According to Teves, children often participate in online gambling, something which often goes unmonitored. 'Sa totoo lang, hindi naman po natin ito nababantayan. ‘Pag ang isang kabataan, online gambling, mayroon sariling internet, mayroon sariling cellphone. Wala namang nagbabawal na pati ang kabataan ay hindi bawal gumamit ng cellphone. ‘Yung below 18 years old. Kaya ‘pag papasok doon, makakapanood sila, makakapaglaro sila sa online gambling,' Teves told reporters. 'Kaya sumusuporta ako sa ibang congressman, lalong lalo na ‘yung panukalang batas na ginawa ngayon ni Senator Zubiri na pagbawal na ang online gambling dahil marami pong experience tayo ng mga kabataan ‘di na nakakapag-aral dahil nalululong sila sa online gambling,' Teves added. PAGCOR earlier said in a statement it will follow regulations against online gambling once passed into law. WRAP: Flurry of allegations revives dormant ‘missing sabungeros’ case Involved cops in missing sabungeros case may rise to over 15: PNP Sen. Juan Miguel Zubiri is pushing for a total ban on all forms of online gambling, even those authorized by the Philippine Amusement and Gaming Corporation (PAGCOR), as he highlighted the problems caused by what he called a 'silent epidemic.' MANILA — Amid a string of proposed bills aimed at curbing online gambling in the Philippines, the Philippine Amusement and Gaming Corporation (PAGCOR) on Friday said it will follow any regulation once they are passed into law. Some lawmakers are pushing for an outright ban, while others want to prohibit betting games using electronic wallets. 'It is the prerogative of our lawmakers to propose laws which they think will be beneficial to the public. PAGCOR is duty-bound to follow any and all relevant regulations once they are passed by Congress and signed into law by the President,' it said in a statement. In the meantime, it assured the public that it is intensifying its crackdown against illegal online gaming activities, together with like-minded agencies and organizations and with the support of all gaming industry stakeholders. The Cybercrime Investigation and Coordinating Center (CICC) said it is working with both PAGCOR and the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) to take down illegal gambling sites. 'It’s part of our threat monitoring center. Pag nakakita tayo, binabato natin sa kanila for verification… Marami-rami na rin tayong napablock na sites. I have 150 more sites, pending certification from PAGCOR para i-block,' said CICC Deputy Executive Director Renato Paraiso. He also said that the government will also go after influencers who promote illegal gambling platforms. 'From a legal perspective, they’re part of a conspiracy to promote illegal gambling so they are part of that criminal organization. So sa lahat po ng mga influencers na nagpopromote ng illegal gambling, pinagtitibay na namin yung mga batas na gagamitin namin para habulin po kayo so kung ako sa inyo, tigilan niyo na,' added Paraiso. To deter minors from participating in online gambling activities, Paraiso said the CICC also helps PAGCOR and PCSO in strengthening their registration and Know Your Customer guidelines. But he admits that some are still able to cheat the system such as by lying about their age. 'Ang suggestion namin is idikit na ‘to sa national ID as the single source of truth. Kung wala kang national ID, hindi ka registered, ibig sabihin kaduda-duda yung mga ipi-present mong credentials to prove your age… Pag national ID kasi hindi na mapepeke yan e,' said Paraiso. Amid a string of proposed bills aimed at curbing online gambling in the Philippines, the Philippine Amusement and Gaming Corporation (PAGCOR) on Friday said it will follow any regulation once they are passed into law. Amid calls for a total ban on online gambling, Philippine Amusement and Gaming Corporation (PAGCOR) Chairman and Chief Executive Officer (CEO) Alejandro Tengco believes stricter regulation is the key. MANILA -- Amid calls for a total ban on online gambling, Philippine Amusement and Gaming Corporation (PAGCOR) Chairman and Chief Executive Officer (CEO) Alejandro Tengco believes stricter regulation is the key.

title: Northern Alliance bloc supports Duterte, Alvarez
body: MANILA - Members of the Northern Alliance bloc at the House of Representatives have also joined the Speakership bid of incoming Davao del Norte Rep. Pantaleon Alvarez and declared their support for incoming president Rodrigo Duterte. Some, however, had initial reservations on joining the administration bloc due to Duterte's stand on the death penalty, according to Ilocos Sur Rep. Eric Singson. 'That question was raised kanina. Cong. Alvarez said it will be debated upon. It will be the choice of members,' said Singson. Alvarez said congressmen who are jumping ship are not 'balimbings' but lawmakers who love their country. He said it was not difficult to convince lawmakers to support the incoming Duterte administration. 'Mukhang madali dahil siyempre with a president na may malaking mandato,' he said. Alvarez said he has the support of 'no less than 180' congressmen. Earlier, incoming 1-Pacman Rep. Mikee Romero said in a gathering of party-list representatives that a total of 260 congressmen support Alvarez. Rep. Alvarez administers oath-taking of new PDP-Laban members from 'Northern Alliance' pic.twitter.com/Uye22mgcQb

title: Driver who sideswiped Angkas rider faces attempted murder charge
body: An MPV driver who sideswiped an Angkas rider and his passenger may have had intent to injure, the chief of the Land Transportation Office said Thursday. Viral video of the incident showed the MPV driver and Angkas driver allegedly having an altercation before the MPV sideswiped the motor taxi along EDSA Santolan. Both the Angkas driver and passenger sustained injuries in the incident. The MPV driver, 53-year-old Pedro Magalit, is currently under police custody. 'The video speaks for itself. Medyo may pagka-reckless driving. Ang charge sa kanya nga attempted murder. It’s not just a simple accident kasi parang may intent. Even police operatives, by filing attempted murder charges 'yun ang kanilang profile, has also seen that. Parang may intent to kill or intent to injure at the very least,' LTO chief Asec. Vigor Mendoza said in a TeleRadyo Serbisyo interview. In the interview, Mendoza said the agency will issue a show cause order to the driver, noting that a local bank is listed as the owner of the vehicle. 'Papadalhan din natin 'yun. Gusto nating malaman kung authorized driver ito o paano niya nakuha 'yung kotse. Gusto nating malaman kung ito ba ay company car, naka-loan,' he said. He said the driver will be meted preventive suspension upon the issuance of the show cause order. Angkas CEO George Royeca, meanwhile, said the rider involved in the incident admitted he was surprised when the MPV driver started berating him in the middle of the highway. 'Nagulat na lamang siya na may nagpunta sa tabi niya at binaba 'yung bintana at pinagmumura siya. Iniisip niya baka naunahan niya o nasingitan niya kaya nagalit. Tapos po nun umiwas daw siya sa kaliwa tapos talagang tinuluyan siya. Nahulog 'yung motor pati 'yung pasahero at 'yung biker,' he said in a separate interview. Royeca said both rider and passenger are out of danger. 'Naospital 'yung pasahero, may mga cast, injury sa neck, paa. Pero nadischarge na kahapon. In fact, pumunta rin siya presinto...Nagpapagaling' yung rider, mayroon siyang injury sa balikat,' he said, adding that Angkas is paying for the medical bills. Royeca said Angkas will support moves to pursue legal action against the driver, saying the crash cannot be considered an accident. He said the SUV driver claimed he only lectured the Angkas rider but did not say anything about the collision, saying he will consult a lawyer first. The Angkas CEO urged all motorists to accord mutual respect to all vehicles on the road, regardless of size. 'Our bikers go through extensive training, professional sila, hindi naman yung singit-singit na bara-bara. Meron talagang course 'yan na dinadaanan at may safety mindset para aware sila sa surroundings nila,' he said. He added: 'We have one of the highest safety records in the region, 99.997 percent safety record. And this is after millions of rides.' (UPDATED) A multi-purpose vehicle (MPV) driver who sideswiped an Angkas rider and his passenger may have had intent to injure, the chief of the Land Transportation Office said Thursday.

title: Customs turns over P100-million auction proceeds from Discaya vehicles
body: Nine out of 13 luxury cars seized from the controversial flood control contractors Sarah and Curlee Discaya had been sold. Investigations earlier showed that the luxury vehicles were unlawfully imported into the Philippines as some of the documents were processed from the Port of Davao, but the actual units were shipped via the Batangas Port. These include the 2023 Rolls-Royce Cullinan made infamous by the Discaya matriarch’s statement that she only bought it because she liked the free umbrella that came with it, a 2022 Mercedes Benz G63 AMG, a 2019 Mercedes Benz G500 Brabus, a 2021 Lincoln Navigator L, a 2023 Toyota Sequoia, a 2022 Cadillac Escalade ESV, and a 2022 Bentley Bentayga. The four remaining Discaya-linked cars, along with 20 other vehicles seized from previous BOC operations against Philippine Offshore Gaming Operators (POGO), are set to be auctioned off in the coming weeks. 'Ang gagawin namin, every 2 weeks yung aming target, para maubos na rin itong mga sasakyan. Baka makalikom pa tayo ng mahigit P700 million pa, malaki yun, makakatulong yun sa ating bansa,' said Customs Commissioner Ariel Nepomuceno. Treasury said the symbolic turnover marks more than a transfer of funds. 'It represents a victory for the rule of law. Through the disposition of these forfeited assets, resources once tied to illegal disputes are now converted into public funds that will directly support national priorities,' said Treasury Complaints and Investigation Division OIC Jerekko Cadorna. Nepomuceno said the continued public auction of smuggled luxury vehicles forfeited from those involved in the flood control corruption scandal is one way the government can raise funds instead of suspending the excise taxes and VAT on fuel. The Bureau of Customs turned over Tuesday some P100 million in auction proceeds from the Discaya-owned vehicles to the Bureau of the Treasury. MANILA (UPDATE) — The Bureau of Customs turned over Tuesday some P100 million in auction proceeds from the Discaya-owned vehicles to the Bureau of the Treasury.

title: Duterte team seeks time to get ‘complete’ info for ICC interim release bid
body: The request, submitted on July 18, highlights difficulties in getting vital materials and approvals that the defense said it needs. Lawyer Nicholas Kaufman and the defense team said they have been attempting to acquire certain information from an undisclosed source and get permission for an independent assessment related to the case. They said their efforts have been 'slow-walked and stymied' despite numerous communications and that the Pre-Trial Chamber should have all the information before making its initial and 'crucial determination' on interim release. A key point raised by the defense is a report that still needs to be completed after their expert was 'temporarily' prevented from conducting its own assessment. They said this leaves them unable to properly challenge a prosecution claim that they describe as incomplete and prejudicial. The defense is also urging the Chamber to order, or strongly encourage, the urgent performance of these assessments. They noted that chambers are 'typically encouraged' to decide on such applications within 30 days. Duterte's was filed on June 12. Duterte is in ICC custody for the alleged crime against humanity of murder for his administration's war on drugs, where the government has acknowledged the deaths of more than 7,000 supposed drug personalities. Authorities during his presidency had routinely said those killed in operations had violently resisted arrest, giving law enforcement no option but to kill them. The prosecution has formally requested the Pre-Trial Chamber to reject the plea, saying the defense chose to file its urgent application for interim release fully aware that certain submitted information was incomplete. 'The Defense should not be permitted to suspend issuance of the decision on the basis that that it may, at some unknown point in the future, attempt to supplement its submission,' it said. The prosecution also refutes the defense's claim that it 'mischaracterized' the existing information as incomplete, saying this was stated in the document itself and had been confirmed by the Chamber. 'The fact that the Defense finds itself in the situation where it is unable to substantiate assertions made in its own, urgent motion, is entirely due to the Defense's choice to file prematurely, The Defense has not been 'prejudiced',' it also said. Prosecution also said it was 'speculative' to say that the missing information would have 'vital' details relevant to the interim release request. They also said that Duterte will still have the opportunity to renew his application in the future, should new circumstances or information arise. Vice President Sara Duterte, who is in The Hague in the Netherlands, said she is hopeful her father will be granted interim release and that his ICC case will be dismissed. She made the statement when asked whether the patriarch of the Duterte clan is expected to return to the Philippines any time soon 'Paano ko ba sagutin yan ha? Siguro let's take it one day at a time. Sa ngayon, ang inaantay natin ay sana ma-dismiss ang kaso based on the filings sa jurisdiction at in the meantime, mabigyan din siya ng interim release habang nagtatalo yung mga abogado at prosecutor diyan sa loob ng korte.' (How do I answer that? Let's just take it one day at a time. For now, we are waiting for the case to be dismissed based on the filings on jurisdiction and that he be granted interim release while the defense and prosecutors argue the case in court) She said neither she nor her father's lawyers can give a 'definitive timeline' on his release or what the chances are of him going back to Davao City, where he was elected mayor in May. Vice President Duterte said he has also asked Kaufman to make himself available to Philippine media to answer questions. — with Harlene Delgado, ABS-CBN News Former President Rodrigo Duterte's lawyers have filed an urgent request with the Pre-Trial Chamber to hold off on deciding on his bid for temporary release, saying it is still waiting for documents and information to support the application. MANILA — Former President Rodrigo Duterte's lawyers have filed an urgent request with the Pre-Trial Chamber to hold off on deciding on his bid for temporary release, saying it is still waiting for documents and information to support the application.

title: An A-Z of players in the Philippine flood control scandal
body: Despite the reported completion of some 5,500 flood control projects since 2022, numerous media reports have revealed many substandard and 'ghost' flood control projects that were exposed following massive floods in parts of the country this year. But who are the personalities and entities involved in this scandal? Here’s a quick look: Dismissed from service for signing off on ghost projects in his district, Alcantara was recently implicated by his former deputy, Brice Hernandez, as a 'chief implementor' in the kickback scheme of the flood control controversy. He was among the first officials dismissed and charged in connection with the scandal. One of the lawmakers accused of getting kickbacks from government flood control projects, Ang has denied the allegations by the Discaya couple. One of 15 top government contractors who cornered P100 billion in flood control projects, Wawao Builders president Mark Allan Arevalo affirmed in a Senate hearing that his company’s license was forcibly used for some flood control projects across the country, suggesting that insiders in the DPWH were behind the scheme. The company was blacklisted by DPWH, which would permanently bar them from participating in any government projects. One of the lawmakers accused of getting kickbacks from government flood control projects, Arogancia has denied the allegations by the Discaya couple and said he will fill charges. The incumbent Representative for the 3rd District of Caloocan City, Asistio is one of the lawmakers accused by the Discaya couple of getting kickbacks from government flood control projects. He has denied the claims, saying there are no ghost projects in his district. An actor who achieved fame in the hit TV series 'FPJ’s Ang Probinsiyano', Atayde has denied accusations by the Discaya couple that he pocketed kickbacks from government flood control projects in his capacity as congressman of Quezon City. Atayde is married to actress Maine Mendoza This group, named by Senator Panfilo Lacson, includes engineers from the Bulacan 1st District Engineering Office who allegedly facilitated ghost projects in the district and lost P950 million in casinos while using fake IDs. The members of the BGC Boys are Henry C. Alcantara, Brice Ericson Hernandez, Jaypee Mendoza, Arjay Domasig and Edrick San Diego. A civil engineer who served in the Department of Public Works and Highways for decades until his retirement in 2010, Bonoan was tapped to lead the DPWH in 2022 until his resignation on September 1, 2025 amid the flood control scandal. Sen. Panfilo Lacson has revealed Bonoan’s daughter is an executive of MBB Global Properties Corporation and a business partner of Globalcrate Builders, which secured at least P2 billion in flood control projects from 2018 to 2024. One of the lawmakers accused of getting kickbacks from government flood control projects, Briones was recently embroiled in controversy when he was caught watching a cockfight video on his cellphone during a House session. Another DPWH official under probe for allegedly lying about her role in budget insertions, Cabral reportedly reached out to Sen. Tito Sotto's staff in a private message, offering to ‘insert’ priority projects in the 2026 National Expenditure Program. She supervises DPWH infrastructure planning and programming. Calatrava is one of the officials accused by the Discaya family of pocketing kickbacks from government flood control projects. He has denied the charges, saying he has never met the Discayas. Ako Bicol party-list Rep. Zaldy Co, former chairperson of the House Committee on Appropriations, has been linked to SunWest Inc. and Hi-Tone Construction and Development Corp. – two of the top 15 government contractors that bagged a fifth of all flood control projects from 2022 to 2025. He is accused of being the proponent of P13.8 billion in budgetary insertions including funds for flood control and other infrastructure projects in the bicameral conference committee version of the 2025 budget. Named by the Philippine Center for Investigative Journalism as the king and queen of flood control projects, the Discayas own 9 construction firms including St. Timothy Construction Corporation and Alpha & Omega General Contractor, which are included in the top 15 contractors that bagged P100 billion in flood control projects in the last 3 years. A PCIJ report said six companies founded by the Discayas plus 3 other joint ventures bagged 421 of 9,855 flood-control projects in the last 3 years, totaling P31.035 billion. Before the flood control controversy, the couple gained attention after lifestyle interviews showed they owned at least 40 luxury cars. Sarah Discaya also ran for mayor of Pasig in the 2025 elections but lost to Vico Sotto. In their testimony before the Senate Blue Ribbon Committee, the two claimed they were coerced by officials to participate in anomalous bidding and pay kickbacks to secure flood control project contracts. The Discayas are among the individuals now facing criminal complaints. The newly appointed DPWH Secretary has led a crackdown on the flood control project anomalies, ordering the dismissal of dozens of officials and the filing of charges against those involved. One of several officials who exposed substandard flood control projects under his jurisdiction, Dolor said total of P17.8 billion worth of flood-control projects were inserted in the province's budget from 2022 to 2025, with no consultation or coordination with his office. He said some of the projects were substandard and overpriced, with one 'ghost' project thrice in 2024 but never built. Former Senate President Chiz Escudero has confirmed receiving a P30 million campaign donation from Lawrence Lubiano of Centerways Construction, one of 15 government contractors that bagged P100 billion in flood control projects from 2022-2025. From 2021 to 2024, Centerways secured over P5.16 billion in flood control projects, with a significant portion located in Sorsogon. Escudero was recently ousted as the Senate President of the Philippines and replaced by veteran senator Vicente 'Tito' Sotto III. The former Senate President Pro Tempore, Estrada was recently tagged by ex-DPWH district engineer Brice Hernandez as being the alleged proponent of a P350 million budget insertion in Bulacan, with 30 percent going to SOP or kickbacks. Estrada has denied the allegations and threatened to sue Hernandez, daring the latter to take a lie detector test. A former Assistant District Engineer for the Bulacan 1st District Engineering Office, Hernandez implicated Senators Jinggoy Estrada and Joel Villanueva in the flood control projects controversy, alleging they received kickbacks from projects. Accused of owning luxury cars and expensive watches, Hernandez is one of the so-called BGC Boys or Bulacan Group of Contractors – a group of engineers from Bulacan 1st District Engineering Office who allegedly facilitated ghost projects in the district. Hernandez was dismissed from his position and is currently detained for his connection to the flood control project irregularities. Senate President Pro Tempore and now chairman of the Senate Blue Ribbon Committee, Lacson was the first to reveal that 67 lawmakers in 2022 also doubled as contractors in their respective districts, giving them control over project funds. He also exposed the link between the daughter of former DPWH Secretary Manuel Bonoan to a construction firm that bagged government flood control projects in Bulacan. A close friend of ex-Senate President Francis Escudero and the president of Centerways Construction and Development Inc., Lubiano’s firm was one of the top 15 contractors that secured 84 projects worth over 5 billion, with 56 of these based in Sorsogon. He earlier testified that he gave P30 million to Escudero as a donation for the latter’s campaign in 2022. The former chairman of the Blue Ribbon Committee investigating the flood control controversy, he lost the chairmanship to Sen. Panfilo Lacson following a change in the Senate leadership. He recently moved to have the Discaya couple admitted as state witnesses, which was denied by Senate President Tito Sotto. The outspoken mayor of Baguio City has claimed he has information on corrupt congressmen who received up to 40% in kickbacks from flood control projects. He has stated his willingness to join an independent commission that will investigate the flood control controversy. The chairman of the House infrastructure committee, Ridon has led the House investigation on the flood control controversy. He was recently accused by Sen. Jinggoy Estrada of favoring ex-DPWH Engineer Brice Hernandez - a batchmate of Ridon in elementary school - after the latter accused Estrada of pocketing millions of pesos in kickbacks. Ridon has denied Estrada's allegations. Named by the Discayas as one of the lawmakers allegedly tied to the scandal, Romulo has denied the allegations, saying he is willing to open his SALN and sign a bank secrecy waiver to prove his innocence. Marjorie Samidan, owner of the singe proprietorship MG Samidan Construction, bagged 5.3 billion in flood control projects mostly in the Cordillera region, particularly in Abra province. Investigation has shown that some of the company's flood control projects were located in areas with little to no flood risk. During her testimony, Samidan admitted she had P1 billion total assets in 2024. Santos was invited to the House InfraComm hearing after her company was tagged in a P55-million reinforced concrete river wall project in Barangay Piel, Baliuag, Bulacan province. In her testimony, Santos revealed she delivered P245 million in cash to a Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) office in one day. One of the lawmakers being linked by the Discaya couple to the flood control controversy, Teodoro has denied the allegations, saying no Discaya firm has been awarded a project in his city. He said the allegations are a demolition job after he exposed fresh allocations in the 2026 National Expenditures Program for projects already completed. Current representative of the Lone District of Navotas, Tiangco has accused former House Appropriations Chairman Zaldy Co of overseeing P13.8 billion in insertions in the 2025 national budget. Severe floods in Barangay Navotas East, Navotas City, on July 24, 2025. Jonathan Cellona, ABS-CBN News/File MANILA -- In the last six weeks, the Philippines has been rocked by a controversy over anomalous flood control projects, which first came to light during President Ferdinand Marcos Jr’s State of the Nation Address last July 28. The scandal has led to the firing of several public works officials, online shaming of government contractors and their families, and allegations that dozens of lawmakers pocketed billions of pesos from the scam. Here’s a timeline of events related to the controversy: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. orders an investigation into possible corruption in flood control projects, some of which faltered in July when monsoon rains driven by three cyclones spawned floods in various parts of the country. Sen. Panfilo Lacson reveals that 67 lawmakers in 2022 doubled as contractors for their own projects. President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr. names 15 contractors who bagged P100 billion worth of flood control projects since 2022. He noted most of the flood control projects were located in areas that were not among the most flood-prone places in the Philippines. Senate President Chiz Escudero admits receiving a P30 million donation from a government contractor in 2022, but denies helping the donor’s construction firm bag several flood-control projects after the elections. Senate Majority Leader Joel Villanueva claims no senator has been linked to the 15 contractors involved in questionable flood control projects. Public Works Secretary Manny Bonoan says the DPWH will audit infrastructure projects built by the 15 top government contractors. President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. visits two substandard anti-flood infrastructure in Calumpit, Bulacan, which were passed off as complete -- despite failure to deliver some parts of the contract. The contractors for the two projects are St. Timothy Construction Corporation and Wawao Builders. Oriental Mindoro Governor Humerlito Dolor discovers that a flood dike in Barangay Mulawin, Naujan town, which collapsed due to massive flooding, was filled mostly with sand , thin concrete, and supported by small steel bars. MG Samidan Construction and Development Corp., identified as the government’s 13th top contractor on flood-mitigation projects, had only P250,000 paid-up capital in 2019 , according to a document obtained by ABS-CBN News The Blue Ribbon Committee chaired by Sen. Rodante Marcoleta starts its inquiry into the flood control anomalies. Eight out of 15 government contractors invited to the hearing fail to attend . Public Works Secretary Manny Bonoan admits there are ghost projects , saying he had learned about the scam just recently. A P77 million flood mitigation structure in Barangay San Agustin in Hagonoy, Bulacan that should have been completed in July 2023 is discovered to be a ghost project . Oriental Mindoro Governor Dolor names contractors behind 9 flood control projects that collapsed last July. President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. discovers that a P55-million river wall project in Bgy. Piel, Baliuag, Bulacan that had already been paid for by the government has yet to be constructed . Sen. Panfilo Lacson delivers a privilege speech exposing how contractors, government officials and DPWH insiders divide up funds and build projects using substandard materials. A construction company linked to COA Commissioner Mario Lipana’s wife bagged nearly P200 million worth of flood control projects , according to government data. Oriental Mindoro Governor Dolor says a flood control project in Barangay Apitong, Naujan, Oriental Mindoro, which was funded three times in 2024, was never built. More flood control projects built by the DPWH Mimaropa regional office collapse. Pasig City Mayor Vico Sotto urges media to think twice about interviewing the Discaya couple who are allegedly willing to pay millions of pesos for a lifestyle interview. A DPWH district engineer is arrested for allegedly trying to bribe Batangas 1st District Rep. Leandro Leviste. Frustration over corruption turns into online ‘lifestyle checks’ of contractors and their ‘nepo babies’ The President orders a lifestyle check on all government officials amid the ongoing probe into flood projects Sen. Panfilo Lacson reveals a racket at the Philippine Contractors Accreditation Board (PCAB) to let unqualified contractors buy accreditation to operate and bag government contracts. Pasig City Mayor Vico Sotto urges the public to shun displays of wealth from government officials and those linked to tax-funded projects Secretary Manuel Bonoan resigns as Public Works Secretary , and erstwhile Transportation Secretary Vince Dizon takes over the DPWH. Sarah Discaya appears before the Senate Blue Ribbon Committee and confirms she owns 28 luxury cars . She also admits that she and her husband are owners of nine construction firms who sometimes bid against each other for government projects. New DPWH chief Vince Dizon orders the courtesy resignations of DPWH officials. Finance Secretary Ralph Recto says the economy lost over P100 billion to 'ghost' flood control projects from 2023 to 2025. The House TriComm kicks off its own probe on flood control anomalies. House Infra Comm members are ordered to disclose ties to flood control projects. Centerways Construction and Development Inc. President Lawrence Lubiano confirms giving a P30 million donation to the campaign of Senate President Chiz Escudero. Vince Dizon, the new DPWH chief scraps an internal committee formed to probe the flood control mess. The Bureau of Customs raids the Discaya compound and seizes their luxury vehicles. The Philippine Contractors Accreditation Board revokes the contractors' licenses of nine companies owned or controlled by flood control projects contractor Sarah Discaya. The DOJ issues an immigration lookout bulletin order (ILBO) against contractors and DPWH officials tagged in the flood control scam. Various groups protest outside St. Gerrard Construction in Pasig City, one of the companies owned by contractors Sarah and Curlee Discaya. DPWH finds ex-District Engineer Henry Alcantara guilty of grave misconduct over the Bulacan flood control mess A lawmaker urges Ako Bicol party-list Rep. Zaldy Co , former chair of the House appropriations panel, to answer allegations linking him to budget insertions and clarify his ties with Sunwest Inc. Pacifico and Sarah Discaya name lawmakers and officials linked to the flood control mess. These officials deny the allegations . Arjo Atayde says 'never dealt' with Discayas, viral photo not from a planned meeting Navotas City Rep. Toby Tiangco says Ako Bicol party-list Rep. Zaldy Co, the former chairperson of the House Committee on Appropriations, proposed billions of pesos in insertions in the 2025 budget. Contractors accuse Bulacan 1st District officials of involvement in a 'hiram lisensiya' scheme. Senate President Chiz Escudero, who had admitted to receiving a P30 million donation from a government contractor, is ousted, with former Senate President Tito Sotto taking his place . A new Senate majority is formed. Ex-DPWH engineer Brice Hernandez tags Senators Jinggoy Estrada and Joel Villanueva in flood control anomalies ; senators deny allegations. He also reveals photos of stacks of cash , which were allegedly distributed as kickbacks to various officials. Curlee Discaya testifies in the House InfraComm hearing, says he will no longer add names in alleged flood control kickbacks Sen. Panfilo Lacson says ex-DPWH Secretary Manny Bonoan’s daughter is a business partner of Globalcrete Builders, which bagged P2.195 billion in flood control projects from 2018 to 2024 in Bulacan. Massive corruption, luxury cars, and billions of pesos in budget insertions. The flood control scandal has become the biggest controversy to affect Philippine government in recent years. Sen. Joel Villanueva on Tuesday denied allegations that he received kickbacks from flood control projects in his province Bulacan, following a bombshell from a DPWH engineer at the House of Representatives. Voltaire F. Domingo/Senate Social Media Unit/Maria Tan, ABS-CBN News MANILA — Sen. Joel Villanueva on Tuesday denied allegations that he received kickbacks from flood control projects in his province Bulacan, following a bombshell from a DPWH engineer at the House of Representatives. 'Wala po ako kailanman naging flood control project. Hindi ko po sasabihin na 'I categorically deny this accusation' dahil po may resibo po tayo. Meron pong pwedeng iberipika bakit po ito nangyayari,' Villanueva said during a Senate session Tuesday. 'I will never betray my principle, I will never ever destroy the name that was given to me by my parents,' he added. Former Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) engineer Brice Hernandez, during the House hearing, accused Villanueva and Sen. Jinggoy Estrada of receiving kickbacks from the flood control projects. Former Bulacan DPWH engineers Brice Hernandez and JP Mendoza presented before the House InfraComm pictures allegedly showing the closeness between ex-DPWH Bulacan 1st District Engineer Henry Alcantara and Sen. Jinggoy Estrada, piles of cash supposedly 'for delivery' to politicians, and photos of disappearing messages between Alcantara and Sen. Joel Villanueva. Maria Tan, ABS-CBN News Hernandez and his fellow DPWH engineer Jaypee Mendoza showed photos of allegedly disappearing messages between their 'boss,' former DPWH district engineer Henry Alcantara, and a 'Sen. Joel,' who Mendoza said was Villanueva. Mendoza noted that he captured the photos in October 2023, wherein Villanueva was allegedly requesting flood control projects from Alcantara. 'Sa summary po na yan, sinasabi po ni Sen. Joel na mababa lang yung kaniyang pondo subalit majority floor leader siya that time and member siya ng Commission on Appointment,' he said, explaining then supposed text messages. Villanueva, in his defense, showed a video of him questioning the failed flood control projects in Bulacan two years ago, which President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. also visited this year. He claimed that his name surfaced in the engineer's statement because he possesses affidavits of DPWH Bulacan personnel. He said he was supposed to present the affidavit in Monday's Senate hearing, but he failed to attend. 'Alam ko po kung bakit lumabas ang pangalan ko. Kahapon po, may hawak po akong mga affidavits ng mga taga-DPWH sa Bulacan. Pinakita ko po sa kanila at sabi ko ibi-biring up ko po sa committee ni Sen. Marcoleta,' he said. The senator underscored he is willing to cooperate in the investigations. 'Sa mga susunod na pagdinig, ibibigay ko po lahat ng aking nalalaman, wala rin po akong sasantuhin.' The two engineers also revealed photos of Estrada posing with Alcantara, and stacks of money in DPWH office and in their 'tambayan.' Estrada denied the allegations, daring Hernandez to take a lie detector test. Villanueva questioned if investigations are still after the mastermind, saying that former Senate President Francis Escudero has mentioned 'a name that cannot be mentioned,' whom Villanueva called 'Dracula.' 'Hinahanap pa ba natin ang mastermind? Dito rin po sa bulwagang ito unang nasabi yung binabanggit kahapon ni Senate President Escudero, ‘a name that cannot be mentioned.’ Walang iba kundi si Dracula na ginawang tagapagbantay ng Kamara doon po sa national blood bank natin,' he said. Discaya couple says lawmakers, DPWH execs 'used' them in flood control anomalies 'Ngayon, Mr. President, nasaan po si Dracula? Bumaba daw po ang dugo. Bakit? Kulang na ba ang supply ng dugo kaya sa iba humahanap ng dugo si Dracula?' he added. Escudero, in his speech yesterday, said 'the name that cannot be mentioned,' that was exposed under his Senate leadership, pertains to House Speaker Martin Romualdez and Ako Bicol Party-list Rep. Zaldy Co. Romualdez, lawmakers deny 'false, malicious' Discaya corruption claims Villanueva said: 'Klarong-klaro po kung nasaan na si Dracula, kung bakit bumaba ang dugo kasi hindi na po malaman kung saan itatago ang bilyun-bilyon o trilyong salapi kaya bumaba po ang dugo.' 'At mismong si Dracula sinasabing hindi naman nya gagawin ito kung walang basbas ng amo nya. Sino ba ang amo nya, Mr. President? Sino ba ang naglagay sa kanya bilang tagapagbantay ng national budget?' he said. In the 19th Congress, Co was the chairperson of the House Committee on Appropriations while Romualdez was the House Speaker. Zaldy Co proposed P13.8 billion insertions for flood control, other infra in 2025 budget: Tiangco Villanueva said the accusation against him was of the same scheme as when the people’s initiative issue surfaced January last year, saying: 'Scripted lahat kahit walang ebidensya i-contempt mo. Kahit walang ebidensya ipako mo sa krus.' 'Hindi po tayo natatakot sa mga ganitong uri ng demolition job. Handa rin po tayong madungisan ang ating pangalan. Mainsulto o batikusin kung ito naman po ang magliligtas sa ating mga kababayan at tuldok sa unli-baha,' he said. Sen. Joel Villanueva denied allegations that he received kickbacks from the flood control projects, saying he never engaged with such a project and that there were receipts to prove his innocence. MANILA — Davao City 1st District Rep. Paolo Duterte on Tuesday said lawmakers are free to investigate the P51 billion budget allocation his congressional district received from 2020 to 2022. He claimed that lawmakers' questions about the budget allocation are a 'diversion' from flood control anomalies and involved public officials. Marcos Jr. says no more budget for flood control projects in 2026 'Let me say this straight, Davao has nothing to hide. Kung gusto n’yo talagang maghanap ng ghost projects under that ₱51B, sige lang, investigate it all. Ipakita ang records, tingnan ang aktwal. Nandoon ang katotohanan—mga proyektong nakikita, naitayo, at nagagamit ng mga tao sa Davao,' he said in a statement. 'Kaya huwag ninyo kaming gawing panakip-butas. Focus on the real issues -- flood control anomalies, payola, ug ang mga opisyal nga nisulti mismo nga nanuhol sila. (at ang mga opisyal na mismong umamin na nanuhol sila.)' he added. Duterte said that 'he is not like the others,' saying he once removed a Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) regional director due to a right-of-way corruption issues. The congressman, brother of Vice President Sara Duterte and son of former President Rodrigo Duterte, alleged that the questions on Davao City’s P51 billion budget were intended to attack his family and protect lawmakers’ allies. 'Klaro na klaro na ang layunin ng diversion na ito ay saksakin ang pamilya namin at depensahan ang inyong mga alyado,' he said. Duterte challenged lawmakers to discuss the issues surrounding House Speaker Martin Romualdez and Ako Bicol Party-list Rep. Zaldy Co, who Curlee Discaya named as one of the lawmakers who benefited 25% from flood control projects' funds. Curlee Discaya denies direct ties to Romualdez, Co in alleged corruption scheme Talk of House leadership change swirling, says Tiangco 'Huwag n’yong gamitin ang Davao at ang pamilya ko para lang ma-divert ang isyu at mailihis ang tunay na korapsyon,' he said. Duterte also called on lawmakers to answer issues surrounding the 'anomalies in DSWD (Department of Social Works and Development),' DOLE (Department of Labor and Employment) insertions sa mga congressmen,' as well as the PhilHealth budget and the Maharlika Fund. 'May nagbabantay': Why the Maharlika Fund is investing in NGCP 'Huwag ninyong baliktarin lagi ang istorya at ipahid sa Duterte para lang kayo maligtas.' The representative further dared his fellow congressmen to declare their budgets and show the public the projects in their districts. 'Huwag puro salita, ipakita ninyo ang ebidensya ng trabaho ninyo. Para makita gyud kinsa ang tinuod nga nagserbisyo ug kinsa ang nangurakot. (Para makita talaga kung sino ang totoong nagseserbisyo at sino ang nagnanakaw.)' 'Davao’s projects are built on solid ground. Makasulti ba mo ug pareho ana sa inyong mga distrito? (Maaari ba ninyong sabihin ang ganyan tungkol sa mga distrito ninyo?)' he added. DPWH's 'BGC Boys' lost P950 million in casinos: Lacson Davao City 1st District Rep. Paolo Duterte said lawmakers are free to investigate the P51 billion budget allocation his congressional district recieved from 2020 to 2022. MANILA — Government contractor Curlee Discaya on Tuesday denied that he had direct dealings with House Speaker Martin Romualdez and Ako Bicol party-list Rep. Zaldy Co. Discaya during a Senate hearing Monday made a bombshell allegation that Romualdez and other congressmen were engaged in an alleged corruption scheme involving government infrastructure projects to get kickbacks. Romualdez and his colleagues immediately rejected the accusation. In Tuesday's hearing in the House of Representatives, Discaya said: 'Wala po akong direktang transaksyon kina Zaldy Co at Speaker.' 'Lahat ng sinabi ko… kaya nabanggit ang mga pangalan na yun dahil yun ang sinasabi ng mga kausap ko… madalas nila gamitin,' he said. 'Baka po nagagamit lang po ng mga pulitiko ang kanilang pangalan… pero wala po akong direct na unawa o capacity na masabi na kay Speaker talaga napupunta yun o kay Zaldy Co. Pero yun ang binabanggit ng mga pulitikong kausap ko,' he added. Government contractor Curlee Discaya on Tuesday denied that he had direct ties to House Speaker Martin Romualdez and Ako Bicol party-list Rep. Zaldy Co. Rep. Elizaldy Co chairs the House Committee on Appropriations on September 14, 2022. Press and Public Affairs Bureau handout/File MANILA (UPDATED) — Ako Bicol party-list Rep. Zaldy Co, the former chairperson of the House Committee on Appropriations, had proposed billions of pesos in insertions in the 2025 budget, a lawmaker alleged Monday. Navotas City Rep. Toby Tiangco, during the third Senate hearing on the anomalous flood mitigation programs, accused Co of inserting P13.8 billion that included funds for flood control and other infrastructure projects in the bicameral conference committee version of this year's budget. Zaldy Co should clarify ties to SunWest, alleged budget insertions: lawmaker Co is being tied to Sunwest Inc., the conglomerate that he founded and which has landed billions in government contracts. His colleagues in the House of Representatives, which is also conducting a separate inquiry into the flood control projects, had said it would be better for Co to appear and address the allegations. Tiangco claimed Co inserted projects totaling P3.46 billion in Abra, P3.2 billion in Occidental Mindoro, and P3.1 billion in Oriental Mindoro. Co also inserted projects in Romblon, Bukidnon, Camarines Norte, Batangas, and Sarangani, he said. Tiangco explained that the House has created a 'small committee' to make insertions and other amendments after the 2nd reading is approved, which supposedly ends the period of amendment. He said the small committee was composed of Co, former Senior Vice Chairman and Marikina 2nd District Rep. Stella Quimbo, 19th Congress Majority Floor Leader Mannix Delipe, and Minority Floor Leader Marcelino Libanan. He said this committee, unlike the bicameral conference committee, has not left a record in the House archives about the changes it made to the national budget. 'Yung problema po dun sa apat na tao na yun, at least yung bicam po, may bicam report po yun eh. Makikita sa bicam report kung ano yung original NEP (National Expenditure Program), tapos makikita kung ano yung dinagdag or binawas, at makikita yung natirang pondo,' said Tiangco. Tiangco said he requested the small committee’s records during the House’s first day of budget deliberation, but it has yet to provide them. In a separate statement, Libanan clarified that the small committee has a limited, temporary role in the budget process, contrary to what Tiangco mentioned. ''Pero kailangan natin ilagay sa tamang perspective ang ating paggawa ng batas … Ang function ng small committee ay tumanggap ng individual amendments para sa GAB I, at ‘yung individual amendments ipo-proseso po ng small committee at ito po ilalagay sa GAB II na aprubahan po ng plenaryo,' Libanan said. Libanan also said the small committee was practiced in the 18th Congress and even during the Duterte administration to streamline the flood of individual amendments coming from the members of the House of Representatives. He also emphasized that the finished product of the process is always debated, amended and approved in full view of the public. According to Libanan, his own role in the small committee is limited, and does not give him or the minority bloc control over allocations. 'Ang role ng minority leader sa small committee ay i-collate ang mga kahilingan ng mga minority members. Sa akin po, ipina-received ko yung ating kahilingan, yung kahilingan ng mga committee members at iprinisinta ko ito kay Chairman Zaldy Co,' he said. Libanan likewise expressed concern that misconceptions about the small committee have already distorted public opinion. 'Sana hindi magkaroon ng masamang alintana tungkol sa small committee na yan dahil nakikita naman natin transparent naman ang ginagawa dito sa Kongreso,' he said. 'Pinagbobotohan po ito sa floor. Imino-motion po ito pinagbobotohan sa floor. Lahat po ito pinagbobotohan sa floor,' Libanan said. Tiangco said his research showed that the budgets allocated for the insertions came from the funds supposedly allotted for the flagship projects of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. 'Kala natin nung una PhilHealth lang ang binawasan, DepEd (Department of Education) lang. Later on, natuklasan ko po na yung mga flagship projects ng administration ng pangulong BBM, ay tinanggalan po nila ng pondo, para maisingit yung pondo na gusto nilang isingit doon sa bicam,' he said. Tiangco said he also saw how the budget for 2 party-list groups grew. 'Tapos po, nagulat din ako kasi, siyempre makikita ko doon sa proponents tsaka doon sa district congressmen. Nagulat po ako na for example, alam ko po kayo dating party-list your honor. Pero may dalawang party-list po doon na sa bicam lang, hindi ko alam sa small committee kung nadagdagan sila 2 billion.' 'Ako-Bicol at BHW po. Ang alam ko po your honor ang party-list congressman ang allocation po yata kung hindi ako nagkakamali but I’m sure you know better is 100 – 150 million [pesos] lang.' Tiangco explained that the insertion of flood-control projects came with the 'parking' and 'sagasa' fees. Parking fees, or parada, he said, is when the district congressman allowed the favor of insertion. 'Nakiusap ka sa district congressman, pumayag yung district congressman,' he said. Sagasa, meanwhile, is when the district congressman does not have a choice about the insertion. 'Madidinig mo sa congressman eh, ‘Nasagasaan kami.’ ‘Paano naman kayo nasagasaan?’ ‘Eh binatuhan kami ng pondo na hindi naman namin alam,’' he said. Tiangco said he was unaware of how much these fees are, saying he does not engage with the scheme. During the same hearing, Oriental Mindoro Gov. Humerlito 'Bonz' Dolor said each project entails a 'parking' fee of 5 percent of its funds. In a statement, Co denied 'the baseless and irresponsible accusations made against me during the Senate hearing.' 'These unsubstantiated claims, which attempt to implicate me are not only hearsay but are also politically motivated designed to mislead the public and deflect accountability,' he said. He noted that the 2025 GAA was certified and approved by both Houses of Congress and signed by the President, who even vetoed some provisions and withheld the release of funds for projects. 'However, the validity of the 2025 GAA is currently the subject of a case before the Supreme Court and the Ombudsman. For this reason, I am unable to comment on the matter to the public but reserve my right to comment on this matter, if and when, required by the courts where this matter is pending,' he said. Co is in the United States for medical treatment, House Spokesperson Princess Abante said last week. Co's trip is covered by a travel authority, which government officials need when leaving the country. Ako Bicol party-list Rep. Zaldy Co, the former chairperson of the House Committee on Appropriations, had proposed billions of pesos in insertions in the 2025 budget, a lawmaker alleged Monday. The flood control corruption scandal centers on the alleged loss of P118 billion in government funds, which went to a web of favored contractors, lawmakers and other officials involved in flood control projects. The flood control corruption scandal, which first came to light during President Ferdinand Marcos Jnr’s 2025 State of the Nation Address, centers on the alleged loss of P118 billion in government funds, which went to a web of favored contractors, lawmakers and other officials involved in the construction of government flood projects in the last 3 years.

title: Marcos orders 'overhaul' of government's performance-based incentive system
body: READ: Revilla lauds Marcos for signing law hiking teachers' allowance Both systems '[lack] a review mechanism leading to the accumulation of rules, regulations, and issuances from the [Inter-Agency Task Force] thereby making compliance therewith burdensome, bureaucratic, laborious and time consuming for government agencies,' it read. 'It is imperative to streamline, align and harmonize the RBPMS and PBI System with Ease of Doing Business initiatives, and reform the government performance evaluation process and incentives system towards a more responsive, efficient, agile and competent bureaucracy,' the President said. The panel that will review the government’s incentive system will consist of representatives from the Office of the Executive Secretary, Department of Budget and Management, Department of Finance, the National Economic and Development Authority and the Anti-Red Tape Authority. The technical working group’s primary task is to 'integrate, streamline and align the new government performance management system with the government internal audit program and activities,' and is expected to submit their recommendations to the President within 6 months. RELATED: Gov't workers to receive midyear bonus beginning May 15, 2024 'The TWG shall study and review the RBPMS and PBI System with the goal of streamlining, overhauling, improving and simplifying government performance management and incentives systems.' The panel may also 'involve the private sector, through the National Competitiveness Council, for the purpose of soliciting inputs and recommendations.'
