MANILA — The Philippines tallied 1,206 new COVID-19 cases in the past week, the Department of Health said Monday.

title: WikiLeaks says Julian Assange is 'free' after US plea deal
body: 'Julian Assange is free,' WikiLeaks wrote on X of its founder, who had been detained in Britain for five years as he fought extradition to the United States which sought to prosecute him for revealing military secrets. He has agreed to plead guilty to a single count of conspiracy to obtain and disseminate national defence information, according to a document filed in court in the Northern Mariana Islands in the Pacific. A charter plane flew Assange, 52, from London to Bangkok, where it had been scheduled to stop for refuelling. AFP journalists saw it touch down at Bangkok's Don Mueang airport at around 12:30 pm (0530 GMT). From there it is scheduled to fly to Saipan, capital of the US territory where Assange will appear in court on Wednesday morning. Assange is expected to be sentenced to five years and two months in prison, with credit for the same amount of time spent behind bars in Britain. This means he could return to his native Australia, where the government said his case had 'dragged on for too long' and there was 'nothing to be gained by his continued incarceration.' The publisher was wanted by Washington for publishing hundreds of thousands of secret US documents from 2010 as head of the whistleblowing website WikiLeaks. Since then Assange became a hero to free speech campaigners and a villain to those who thought he endangered US security and intelligence sources. US authorities wanted to put Assange on trial for divulging military secrets about the wars in Iraq and Afghanistan. Assange was indicted by a US federal grand jury in 2019 on 18 counts stemming from WikiLeaks' publication of a trove of national security documents. Former vice president Mike Pence slammed the plea deal on X as a 'miscarriage of justice' that 'dishonors the service and sacrifice of the men and women of our Armed Forces.' WikiLeaks released a short video of Assange speaking with people in what appeared to be an office and then boarding a plane. Assange's family expressed deep gratitude for his freedom, including his mother Christine Assange who said in a statement carried by Australian media that she was 'grateful that my son's ordeal is finally coming to an end.' His wife Stella meanwhile thanked campaigners, writing on X that 'words cannot express our immense gratitude.' Assange met his wife while he was holed up in Ecuador's London embassy, then married her in a ceremony in prison. They now have two young children. Announcement of the deal came two weeks before Assange was scheduled to appear in court in Britain to appeal a ruling approving his extradition to the United States. Assange had been detained in the high-security Belmarsh prison in London since April 2019. He was arrested after spending seven years in Ecuador's London embassy to avoid extradition to Sweden, where he faced accusations of sexual assault that were eventually dropped. The material he released included video showing civilians being killed by fire from a US helicopter gunship in Iraq in 2007. The victims included two Reuters journalists. The United States has accused Assange under the 1917 Espionage Act. Supporters have warned this means he could be sentenced to 175 years in prison. The British government approved his extradition in June 2022. This plea bargain agreement will presumably end Assange's nearly 14-year legal drama. BANGKOK, Thailand ( UPDATED ) -- Julian Assange has been released from prison and left Britain, WikiLeaks said, as he reached a landmark plea deal with US authorities that brought an end to his years-long legal drama.

title: Japanese researchers test pioneering drug to regrow teeth
body: Unlike reptiles and fish, which usually replace their fangs on a regular basis, it is widely accepted that humans and most other mammals only grow two sets of teeth. But hidden underneath our gums are the dormant buds of a third generation, according to Katsu Takahashi, head of oral surgery at the Medical Research Institute Kitano Hospital in Osaka. His team launched clinical trials at Kyoto University Hospital in October, administering an experimental medicine to adult test subjects that they say has the potential to jumpstart the growth of these concealed teeth. It's a technology 'completely new' to the world, Takahashi told AFP. Prosthetic treatments used for teeth lost to decay, disease or injury are often seen as costly and invasive. So 'restoring natural teeth definitely has its advantages', said Takahashi, the project's lead researcher. Tests on mice and ferrets suggest that blocking a protein called USAG-1 can awaken the third set, and the researchers have published lab photographs of regrown animal teeth. In a study published last year, the team said their 'antibody treatment in mice is effective for tooth regeneration and can be a breakthrough in treating tooth anomalies in humans'. For now, the dentists are prioritizing the 'dire' needs of patients with six or more permanent teeth missing from birth. The hereditary condition is said to affect around 0.1 percent of people, who can have severe trouble chewing, and in Japan often spend most of their adolescence wearing a face mask to hide the wide gaps in their mouth, Takahashi said. 'This drug could be a game-changer for them,' he added. The drug is therefore aimed primarily at children, and the researchers want to make it available as early as 2030. Angray Kang, a dentistry professor at Queen Mary University of London, only knows of one other team pursuing a similar objective of using antibodies to regrow or repair teeth. 'I would say that the Takahashi group is leading the way,' the immunotechnology expert, who is not connected to the Japanese research, told AFP. Takahashi's work is 'exciting and worth pursuing', Kang said, in part because an antibody drug that targets a protein nearly identical to USAG-1 is already being used to treat osteoporosis. 'The race to regenerate human teeth is not a short sprint, but by analogy a set of back-to-back consecutive ultra-marathons,' he said. Chengfei Zhang, a clinical professor in endodontics at the University of Hong Kong, said Takahashi's method is 'innovative and holds potential'. 'The assertion that humans possess latent tooth buds capable of producing a third set of teeth is both revolutionary and controversial,' he told AFP. He also cautioned that 'outcomes observed in animals do not always directly translate to humans'. The results of the animal experiments raise 'questions about whether regenerated teeth could functionally and aesthetically replace missing teeth', Zhang added. A confident Takahashi argues that the location of a new tooth in a mouth can be controlled, if not pinpointed, by the drug injection site. And if it grows in the wrong place, it can be moved through orthodontics or transplantation, he said. No young patients with the congenital disorder are taking part in the first clinical trial, as the main objective is to test the drug's safety, rather than its effectiveness. So for now, the participants are healthy adults who have lost at least one existing tooth. And while tooth regeneration is not the express goal of the trial this time around, there is a slim chance that it could happen to subjects anyway, Takahashi said. If so, the researchers will have confirmed that the drug can be effective for those with acquired toothlessness -- which would be a medical triumph. 'I would be over the moon if that happens,' Takahashi said. This could be particularly welcome news in Japan, which has the second-oldest population in the world. Health ministry data shows more than 90 percent of people aged 75 or older in Japan have at least one tooth missing. 'Expectations are high that our technology can directly extend their healthy life expectancy,' Takahashi said. TOKYO, Japan — People with missing teeth may be able to grow new ones, say Japanese dentists testing a pioneering drug they hope will offer an alternative to dentures and implants.

title: Mar to Binay: Explain 'overpriced' Makati building
body: Roxas said the Binay family's explanation on the allegedly overpriced building is long overdue, and that the public has long been clamoring for VP Binay to answer the allegation in detail. WATCH: COA: Makati City Hall parking building, overpriced ng halos P2-B 'Ito ang panawagan ng ating mga kababayan - na ipaliwanag lahat ng ito,' Roxas said. ''Paanong ang isang project na higit lang sa P200 million ay biglang lumobo sa P2.3 billion?' Roxas said the Makati building issue should serve as a red flag to those thinking of voting for Binay in the May 9 elections. 'Nais ba nila ang ganitong uri ng pamumuno, ganito ang ginawa nila sa Makati, tapos sasabihin ni (VP) Binay gagawin niya ito sa buong bansa? Huwag naman,' Roxas told reporters in the middle of his campaign and visit to Annie Candy Manufacturing production plant in Imus, Cavite. In a statement, the United Nationalist Alliance (UNA) questioned the timing of the release of the COA special audit, which found irregularities in the project's budgeting, procurement and execution. ''The commission could be held liable for violating COA Resolution No. 2015-033 dated September 29, 2015, prohibiting the conduct of special audit against candidates of the 2016 elections,'' UNA president Toby Tiangco said. ''As UNA president, I wrote COA to request them to abide by their own rules. I asked that they should apply the resolution equally to all candidates even those from the opposition.' Tiangco also accused the Office of the Ombudsman and the COA of partiality in dealing with the case of the Binay, his son former Makati Mayor Junjun Binay, and several Makati officials. ''It's obvious that both agencies want to hasten the release of the report in hopes that it would tarnish the image of VP Binay and affect his candidacy,'' he said. ''Basta si VP Binay, fast-forward ang mga audit kahit pa sila mismo ang nagbawal nito. Pero pag kaalyado ng administrasyon, uupuan ang kaso hanggang makalimutan.'' The younger Binay also questioned the timing of the release of the special audit. ''Tingnan ninyo, timing is suspect again. They are running the surveys now. In a few weeks, malapit na ‘yong presidential debate and most likely this will come out again next week, probably on a Friday or Thursday,'' he said. The next presidential debate is on March 20. ''Ti-tiyempo ‘yan na malapit na ‘yong debate… isa sa magiging topic doon is corruption. Again, makikita natin ito. In a few days, lalabas ‘yan. Perfect timing na naman ang gagawin nila dito.' CAVITE – In the wake of the release of a Commission on Audit (COA) special report on the Makati City Hall Building II, presidential candidate Mar Roxas again urged Vice President Jejomar Binay to explain the alleged overpricing of the building.

title: Trust ratings of Marcos, Duterte drop in January: SWS
body: Results from the survey, 'conducted in partnership with the Stratbase Group,' showed that Marcos and Duterte garnered 50 percent and 49 percent trust rating respectively. The survey found that Filipinos who indicated 'much trust' in Marcos continue to go down, from 54 percent in December 2024 to 50 percent in January. The number of people who have 'little trust' in Marcos barely moved from 25 percent in December to 26 percent in January, while those who are 'undecided' went up from 19 percent to 22 percent in the same period. Strongest support toward Marcos in January still came from Balance Luzon at 60 percent, but it was 6 percent lower than December. Highest distrust in Marcos was recorded amongst Mindanaoans with 36 percent saying they have 'little trust' in the President. Meanwhile, Duterte’s trust rating went down from 52 percent in December 2024 to 49 percent in January 2025. But Duterte was still most trusted in Mindanao at 78 percent, although this number is 5 percent lower than her 'much trust' rating in the region last month. The highest number of people with 'little trust' in Duterte was recorded in Balance Luzon at 41 percent in January 2025, a 4 percent increase since December 2024. The latest SWS survey was conducted from January 17 to 20, 2025, and asked 1,800 respondents how much they trust the President and Vice President. The SWS survey found that Filipinos who indicated 'much trust' in Marcos continues to go down, from 54 percent in December 2024 to 50 percent in January. MANILA — The Filipinos' trust in both President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr. and Vice President Sara Duterte continue to decline as of January 2025, according to the latest survey by the Social Weather Stations (SWS).

title: Talks with Reds still possible, Bello says
body: Asked in an interview with ANC's Headstart if the meeting, scheduled for February 22 in Europe will not push through, Secretary Silvestro Bello III said it is 'not exactly accurate.' Duterte suspended peace talks earlier this month following the termination of the two sides' unilateral ceasefires. The collapse of the truce stemmed from the government’s refusal to heed the demands of communist rebels to release about 400 political prisoners detained on various charges and attacks launched by the Reds' armed wing, the New People's Army. Bello noted that Duterte said he would consider resuming the talks for 'compelling reason.' Bello believes that among the things that would make the chief executive change his mind is for the rebels to agree to a bilateral ceasefire. 'Sa akin, the compelling reason that the President mentioned—ito’y speculation na—it’s possibly for them to agree to a bilateral ceasefire agreement,' he said. 'Kasi dito, maliwanag na ang ceasefire. Hindi gaya nung sa unilateral ceasefire, walang definition of terms, walang parameters. Hindi mo malaman sinong magiging referee in terms of violation,' he added. The next round of talks with communist rebels may still push through despite President Rodrigo Duterte's suspension of peace negotiations, the chair of the negotiating panel for the government said. MANILA - The next round of talks with communist rebels may still push through despite President Rodrigo Duterte's suspension of peace negotiations, the chair of the negotiating panel for the government said.

title: Human remains, belongings found from EgyptAir crash at sea
body: Unconfirmed reports about flight data from the Airbus plane that disappeared while flying from Paris to Cairo in the early hours of Thursday local time pointed to several problems that its veteran pilot may have struggled with minutes before the crash. 'The Egyptian navy was able to retrieve more debris from the plane, some of the passengers' belongings, human remains, and plane seats,' the Civil Aviation Ministry said in a statement. President Abdel Fattah al-Sisi offered condolences for those on board. The navy was searching an area about 290 kilometers (180 miles) north of Alexandria, just south of where the signal from the plane was lost early on Thursday. There was no sign of the bulk of the wreckage, or of a location signal from the 'black box' flight recorders that are likely to provide the best clues to the cause of the crash. EgyptAir Chairman Safwat Moslem told state television that the radius of the search zone was 40 miles, giving an area of 5,000 square miles, but said it may be expanded. A European satellite spotted a 2 km-long oil slick in the Mediterranean, about 40 km southeast of the aircraft's last known position, the European Space Agency said. Egyptian Prime Minister Sherif Ismail said it was too early to rule out any cause for the crash. The aviation minister said a terrorist attack was more likely than a technical failure, but offered no evidence. Although early suspicion centered on Islamist militants who blew up another airliner over Egypt seven months ago, no group had claimed responsibility more than 36 hours after the disappearance of flight MS804, an Airbus A320. CNN reported on Friday that flight data, from an automatic system called the Aircraft Communications Addressing and Reporting System (ACARS), said smoke alerts were triggered aboard the EgyptAir jet shortly before it crashed. ACARS routinely downloads flight data to the airline operating the aircraft. Two U.S. officials told Reuters they could not confirm CNN's report. But they said an electronic sensor system had detected some kind of disturbance outside the jet around the time investigators believe it began falling from cruising altitude. One of the officials said the disturbance outside the aircraft may have been caused by its sudden and rapid breakup, but it also could have been generated by some kind of mechanical fault or accident or a possible explosion or attack. The officials asked for anonymity when speaking about the still-evolving investigation. A screen grab of the flight data transmitted by ACARS to operators on the ground, published on the website of the aviation journal AVHerald.com, indicated failures in the jet's flight control system and alerts related to smoke in a lavatory and the avionics system, minutes before the crash. The screen grab provided on the website showed only very terse messages sent from the aircraft, such as 'SMOKE LAVATORY SMOKE,' 'AVIONICS SMOKE' and 'F/CTRL SEC 3 FAULT.' The U.S. officials said they could not confirm the authenticity the data, however, and EgyptAir officials could not be reached for immediate comment. Jihadists have been fighting Egypt's government since Sisi toppled an elected Islamist leader in 2013. In October, the Islamic State group claimed responsibility for blowing up a Russian airliner that exploded after taking off from an Egyptian tourist resort. Russian investigators blamed a bomb smuggled on board. That crash devastated Egypt's tourist industry, one of the main sources of foreign exchange for a country of 80 million people, and another similar attack would crush hopes of it recovering. The plane vanished just as it was moving from Greek to Egyptian airspace control. Greek Defence Minister Panos Kammenos said it had swerved radically and plunged from 37,000 feet to 15,000 before vanishing from Greek radar screens. Officials from a number of U.S. agencies told Reuters that a U.S. review of satellite imagery so far had not produced any signs of an explosion. They said the United States had not ruled out any possible causes for the crash, including mechanical failure, terrorism or a deliberate act by the pilot or crew. Three French investigators and a technical expert from Airbus arrived in Cairo early on Friday, airport sources said. Adam Schiff, the top Democrat on the U.S. House of Representatives Intelligence Committee, played down comments from U.S. figures including likely presidential nominees Donald Trump and Hillary Clinton that terrorism was the most likely cause. 'At this point, we still can't corroborate the theory that terrorism brought it down or there was some structural problem with the plane,' Schiff told CNN. 'Certainly, the backdrop is suggestive of terrorism in the sense that we have the Russian plane in Sharm el-Sheikh and we have the aspiration we've seen time and time again, not only of ISIL (Islamic State) now but of AQAP (al Qaeda), still very potent and still very determined to bring down aircraft. 'But the reality is, we don't have hard evidence that this was terrorism yet.' Hardline Islamists have targeted airports, airliners and tourist sites in Europe, Egypt, Tunisia and other Middle Eastern countries over the past few years. Khaled al-Gameel, head of crew at EgyptAir, said the pilot, Mahamed Saeed Ali Shouqair, had 15 years' experience and was in charge of training and mentoring younger pilots. 'He comes from a pilot family; his uncle was a high-ranking pilot at EgyptAir and his cousin is also a pilot,' Gameel said. 'He was very popular and was known for taking it upon himself to settle disputes any two colleagues were having.' A Facebook page that appeared to be Shouqair's included criticism of the Muslim Brotherhood, repostings of articles supporting President Sisi and pictures of Shouqair wearing aviator sunglasses. Two former senior crash investigators said the list of possible causes remained wide open and noted there had been cases where deliberate action had been suspected wrongly. In 1996, a terrorism probe was launched after a TWA jumbo jet crashed off Long Island, New York, on the east coast of the United States, but investigators later found it had probably been brought down by a fuel tank explosion. The EgyptAir plane that crashed Thursday was carrying 56 passengers, including one child and two infants, and 10 crew. They included 30 Egyptian and 15 French nationals, along with citizens of 10 other countries. The aircraft had made scheduled flights to Tunisia and Eritrea on Wednesday before arriving in Paris from Cairo. CAIRO - Egypt said on Friday its navy had found human remains, wreckage and the personal belongings of passengers floating in the Mediterranean, the first confirmation that an EgyptAir jet with 66 people on board had plunged into the sea.

title: China reiterates claim to Ayungin amid outcry over dangerous maneuvers
body: In a statement , China Coast Guard spokesman Gan Yu claimed that two supply ships and 2 coast guard vessels from the Philippines 'intruded into the adjacent waters of Ren'ai Reef in China's Nansha Islands without the permission of the Chinese government.' 'The Chinese Coast Guard strictly warned the Philippine vessels in accordance with the law, tracked and monitored the vessels all the way, and effectively regulated them,' he added. He reiterated China’s 'indisputable sovereignty' over the waters of Nansha Island (part of the Spratly Islands), and its opposition to the 'the illegal delivery of construction materials by the Philippines to the illegally grounded warship.' 'The Chinese Coast Guard will continue to carry out law enforcement activities in the sea areas under China's jurisdiction according to law,' Gan also said. The office of Senate President Juan Miguel Zubiri earlier released a video clip showing a Chinese ship cutting through the path of a Philippine Coast Guard vessel in Ayungin Shoal. Ayungin Shoal lies 105 nautical miles (194 kilometers) west of the Palawan province in the Philippines, and is within the country's 200-nautical-mile exclusive economic zone. Maritime security expert Ray Powell said 2 Chinese militia ships, the Qiong Sansha Yu 00008 and 00231, passed within a few meters of the BRP Sindangan and the BRP Cabra, both Parola-class vessels of the PCG. China claims sovereignty over almost the entire South China Sea, despite a 2016 international court ruling that its stance has no legal basis. MANILA - China has reiterated its claims over Ayungin Shoal, as its expressed opposition to the latest resupply mission by Philippine vessels to BRP Sierra Madre.

title: Man arrested for sending explicit videos, indecent online proposals
body: The harassment began in According to PNP-ACG Acting Director Brig. Gen. Wilson Asueta, the harassment began in August 2022 when the suspect sent messages to the victim through a social media platform. The victim was only 17-years-old at the time. 'The victim was subjected to cyber-stalking since 2022 until now. So through messaging, the suspect usually sent indecent messages, mga bastos na message, then offering sexual offer,' Asueta said. Fearing for her safety, the victim immediately blocked the suspect’s account. Despite this, the suspect continued contacting her months later, sending another explicit video and offering her P5,000. On February 26, 2026, the suspect sent another message offering P10,000 in exchange for sexual activity. 'Kung ano-ano ang sinasabi. That’s why ang ating biktima ay lumapit sa ating himpilan. So nag-complain, then pinag-aralan natin,' Asueta said. 'So itong gender harassment natin ay hindi lang dahil through public o sa kalsada. Nangyayari ito sa online,' he added. The suspect was arrested through an entrapment operation and is now in police custody in Pasig City. A case has already been filed against him for violating the Safe Spaces Act on gender-based online sexual harassment and grave coercion, in relation to the Cybercrime Prevention Act. 'This is in relation to the Women's Month, kailangan natin ipakita na hindi dapat binabastos ang mga babae kahit sa online platform,' Asueta said. MANILA — A 41-year-old man was arrested in Pasig City for allegedly sending explicit videos and offering sexual favors in exchange for money to a woman online, the Philippine National Police Anti-Cybercrime Group (PNP-ACG) reported.

title: Lalaking nanloob sa bahay ng komedyante, arestado
body: Ang isa rito ay pagmamay-ari pa ng isang sikat na aktor, komedyante at TV host. Araw ng Pasko, napasugod sa Batasan Police Station sa Quezon City ang aktor-host at komedyante matapos umanong pasukin ng isang lalaki ang kanilang bahay. 'Naghahanda na para sa noche buena, kagagaling lang namin magsimba. Pinasok kami ng isang nakaposas na individual. Nakpasok sya nang walang pahintulot, luckily na-restrain namin at nakatawag kami ng security at ng police,' ayon sa komedyante. Sa imbestigasyon ng Quezon City Police District Station 6, lumabas na bago pasukin ang bahay ng komedyante, pinasok umano ng suspek ang isang condo unit na tinangka umanong pagnakawan. 'May lalaki na pumasok sa bahay paglingon ko, nandoon na siya sa likod nakatayo na. Nagmamakaawa siya, nanghihijngi siya ng tulong dahil papatayin daw siya ng guards. Nakatakas siya sa amin, nagpunta sa ibang lugar,' ayon sa unang biktima. Nahuli ng security ng condo ang suspek pero nakatakas ito at nakatawid pa sa katabing village sa bahay ng artista. Doon na siya muling naaresto. Pero habang nasa security office, nagpumiglas at natanggal ng suspek ang kanyang posas. Hinablot niya ang 9-mm na baril ng isang security guard. Nagpambuno ang guwardya at suspek. Dito tinangka pa umano ng suspek na barilin ang guwardya. 'Nagpambuno na kami, sabi ko patingin nga ng posas mo, gumanon bigla. Babalik posas isang kamay kumapit sa baril ko nagpaputok. Paskong-Pasko, yayariin mo buhay ko,' ayon sa security guard. Maswerteng walang natamaan. Pero dito na tuluyang naaresto ang kawatan. 'Hihingi lang po ako ng pagkain, Patawarin na lang po sana nila ako,' paliwanag ng suspek. Bagama’t wala naman na nanakaw sa mga biktima, sinampahan ng aktor, isa pang biktima at ng security guard ng tatlong magkakahiwalay na reklamo ang naarestong suspek. 'Bale ang ikakaso natin two counts trespass to dwelling, robbery; dahil sa pag-agaw ng baril, attempted homicide; dahil tangkang pagbaril sa security guard,' ayon kay PCapt. Ronnie Ereno ng QCPD Station 6. Magdadagdag naman ng seguridad ang biktimang komedyante. 'Siguraduhing enough security siguro yung CCTV kahit di mamahalin makakatulong din at maganda yung may mga sensors,' sabi niya. Payo din ng mga pulis, palaging siguruhing saradong mabuti ang mga bahay lalo na ngayong holiday season. Arestado ang isang suspek na nanloob umano sa dalawang bahay sa Quezon City. MANILA — Arestado ang isang suspek na nanloob umano sa dalawang bahay sa Quezon City.

title: The ANC Brief: Circus coming to town
body: There they go! And they are off! The campaign season for May’s midterm elections has started. The opposition slate Otso-Diretso (Straight Eight) which is four short of a complete slate is up against the juggernaut administration Hugpong ng Pagbabago (Faction for Change). The midterm elections are widely seen as a referendum on the Duterte administration. It should be noted that the President himself enjoys high survey ratings. The Comelec warned candidate to remove campaign posters. PNP Chief Dir. Gen. Oscar Albayalde is due to hold a press conference in Camp Crame on peace and order efforts during the elections. Shot in the arm The measles outbreak has affected 4,300 with 70 dead. Will the issues be lost in the campaign for the May elections? Presidential Communications Sec. Martin Andanar said President Duterte will campaign for immunization. With the President’s popularity, this should give the government’s vaccination efforts a shot in the arm. What’s in a name? In a speech in Maguindanao on Monday, President Duterte said he might one day change the country’s name from Philippines to 'Maharlika.' Meanwhile, the Palace defends the President’s endorsement of Jinggoy Estrada who is accused of plunder. Budget blues Sen. Ping Lacson said some of his colleagues may have been hurt by his pork expose. Will the President take Lacson’s advice and veto the 'pork' in the budget? Superpowers in the Pacific The UK sends an aircraft carrier to the Pacific. And two US warships sail near Mischief Reef. Two superpowers intent to assert their right to sail in the disputed waters. What will be Beijing’s response and will this stabilize or disturb the delicate situation the region? The campaign for the May midterm elections has started. These are the stories ANC will be covering today. The election circus is coming to town. The campaign for the May midterm elections has started. These are the stories ANC will be covering today:

title: Finding the departed: Some Manila North Cemetery visitors struggle to locate graves of loved ones
body: Yolanda Cuaresma approached the Puntod Finder Assistance Desk at the Manila North Cemetery, hoping to locate her grandmother’s grave. It had been more than ten years since her last visit, and she could no longer remember the exact location. She came alone, so cemetery staff accompanied her through narrow walkways and crowded paths in search of the grave. Despite their efforts, the search ended in vain. 'Ipagdarasal at ipagtitirik na lang ng kandila sa bahay, papamisahan, pero nagpapasalamat po ako sa matiyagang pagtulong nila sa paghahanap,' she said. Another visitor, Zenaida Ramido, came early to avoid the expected surge of visitors, as she was already a senior citizen. She has not visited her brother’s grave in over five years and needed help finding it. After a long search and several wrong turns, Zenaida finally found the grave—covered in moss and seemingly long neglected. Cemetery personnel were deployed in full force to maintain order. Tents and assistance booths were set up outside, and free shuttle services were made available since private vehicles are no longer allowed inside. At the Manila South Cemetery, early visitors noticed several changes. Manila City officials have already conducted inspections to ensure crowd safety and proper traffic flow. Although the South Cemetery is located in Makati, it remains under the jurisdiction of the Manila city government. Some visitors of Manila North Cemetery struggled to find the graves of their loved ones, despite the help of a digital finder. For some, it has been years—or even decades—since their last visit, making it nearly impossible to recall where exactly their family members are buried.

title: Suspected death from Ebola in Sierra Leone: official
body: The female student was taken ill in the northern village of Bamoi Luma near the Guinean border and died soon after, with an initial swab testing positive for Ebola, a senior health ministry official told AFP. 'Complete findings will be made known to the public by tomorrow (Friday),' he said, adding that further tests were under way. Earlier Thursday the WHO said a two-year Ebola epidemic that killed 11,000 people and triggered a global health alert was over, with Liberia the last country to get the all-clear. The deadliest outbreak in the history of the feared tropical virus wrecked the economies and health systems of the three worst-hit west African nations after it emerged in southern Guinea in December 2013. At its peak, it devastated Guinea, Liberia and Sierra Leone, with bodies piling up in the streets and overwhelmed hospitals recording hundreds of new cases a week. 'A high-level team of ministry of health officials and key partners including the World Health Organization and the Atlanta-based Centre for Disease Control are in the area from the capital to undertake intensive investigations,' Sierra Leone government spokesman Abdulai Bayrayta told AFP following the suspected new case. Rick Brennan, the WHO's chief of emergency risk management, hailed the declaration of the end of the epidemic as an important milestone but said in Geneva that 'the job is still not done' due to the persistence of the virus in survivors. UN chief Ban Ki-moon also warned that the region can expect sporadic cases in the coming year but that 'we also expect the potential and frequency of those flare-ups to decrease over time'. FREETOWN, Sierra Leone - A woman in Sierra Leone is thought to have died from Ebola, officials said Thursday, just hours after the World Health Organization (WHO) declared an end to an epidemic of the disease.

title: IBP president says SC en banc has no jurisdiction over integrity issue vs Sereno
body: Atty. Abdiel Dan Fajardo echoed the assertion of Sereno's camp that the correct way to remove a sitting chief justice is through the process of impeachment. #ANCRecap : IBP national president Atty. Abdiel Dan Fajardo says the SC en banc has no jurisdiction over the integrity issue of a chief justice. pic.twitter.com/Jmq26G8SUj 'It would appear that the Supreme Court has directed the chief justice to file a comment but without giving due cause to the petition,' he said. 'The Supreme Court en banc has no jurisdiction over an integrity issue against the chief justice and the correct way of proceeding on this is for the impeachment trial to unfold so that the chief justice would have opportunity to present in public and for the public to benefit her answer and how she would present her evidence,' Fajardo added. The Supreme Court on Tuesday ordered Chief Justice Maria Lourdes Sereno to answer the Office of the Solicitor General's (OSG) petition seeking to void her appointment as it junked another petition that also sought to remove her from office. Sereno was given 10 days to comment on the OSG's petition for 'quo warranto,' which alleged that she is 'unlawfully holding' her post due to her supposed failure to fully disclose her wealth. The president of the Integrated Bar of the Philippines stands firm that the Supreme Court en banc has no jurisdiction over the integrity issue of a chief justice.

title: Duterte gov't eyes truce with Reds before SONA
body: The truce will be declared after formal peace negotiations resume in the third week of July, one week before Duterte's first ever State of the Nation Address, said incoming labor secretary Silvestre Bello, who is part of the negotiations. The nearly half-century long Maosit campaign of the New People's Army (NPA), one of the world's longest running insurgencies, has claimed over 30,000 lives according to military estimates. 'After the formal resumption of the talks, both parties will declare a unilateral ceasefire,' Bello said. Informal talks resumed in Norway this month between the leftists' political wing, the National Democratic Front, and Duterte's emissaries, breaking a decade-long deadlock. The rebels pulled out of negotiations under President Gloria Arroyo's government to protest the inclusion of their leaders in the United States and the European Union's lists of terrorist organizations. DAVAO CITY - The Philippines and leftist rebels plan to declare a ceasefire before President-elect Rodrigo Duterte’s first policy speech to Congress next month, a government negotiator said Tuesday.

title: Caritas Philippines names Fr. Tito Caluag as new executive director
body: Caluag succeeds Fr. Antonio Labiao Jr, who served the post from July 2020 until his resignation in March 2024. Caritas Philippines is the humanitarian, development, and advocacy arm of the Catholic Church. 'I am honored to be entrusted with this leadership role at Caritas Philippines,' Fr. Caluag said. 'I look forward to working alongside our dedicated staff and the entire social action network to strengthen our programs and advocate for the most vulnerable in our society.' Fr. Caluag has been with Caritas Philippines since 2020, helping forge the organization’s partnership with ABS-CBN for the Kapamilya Daily Masses. Currently, Caluag is leading the Alay Kapwa Expanded Fund Campaign, aimed at increasing local resources to support various programs. He is also involved in the AK Community Schooling and the Caritas Philippines Academy, reflecting his commitment to the organization’s educational and developmental initiatives. In a press conference, Fr. Caluag emphasized the importance of sustainability for the organization’s programs. 'Ang isang mahalaga sa Caritas Philippines ngayon is yung sustainability ng kani-kanilang programs. Kasi parang nakasalalay na yung key programs to address poverty [and] other basic social needs,' he said. He also elaborated on his vision for Caritas Philippines, focusing on sustainability, organizational capacity building, and the scalability of programs across various parishes and dioceses. He stressed the need for a foundational approach to tackle issues such as education, malnutrition, food security, and environmental concerns. 'Karamihan ng na-address na suliranin ng Caritas Philippines, ng Social Action networks is ‘yung foundation approach ang kailangan eh. Andyan yung education, malnutrition, food security, environment, all of these require a foundation approach kaya mahalaga yung sustainability, replicability, and scalability,' he said. Caritas Philippines president Bishop Jose Colin Bagaforo said that Fr. Caluag's 'impressive background and proven leadership skills, along with his extensive experience with Caritas Philippines initiatives, make him a perfect fit.' 'We are confident that he will guide our organization through this next chapter and help us further our mission of serving those in need,' Bagaforo said. -- with a report from Addie Cuadra, ABS-CBN News Caritas Philippines on Sunday named Fr. Carmelo Tito Caluag of the Diocese of Novaliches as its new executive director. MANILA ( UPDATED ) -- Caritas Philippines on Sunday named Fr. Carmelo 'Tito' Caluag of the Diocese of Novaliches as its new executive director.

title: ‘Go beyond confidential funds’: Analyst weighs in on plunder raps vs VP Sara
body: Duterte, with 15 others, is facing plunder, graft, bribery, and malversation complaints over an alleged 'deliberate system' to mishandle P612.5-million in confidential funds. The complaint also included Office of the Vice President (OVP) chief-of-staff Zuleika Lopez and former Department of Education undersecretary Michael Poa. But if groups want a better case against Duterte or establish a stronger one, they 'need to go beyond confidential funds,' said Prof. Froilan Calilung, who teaches at the University of Santo Tomas (UST) Political Science Department. 'Parang nag-file ka ng plunder case but you don’t really have strong evidence to back it up. Dito lamang sa course ng pag-file mo sa Ombudsman, it is like you are asking the Ombudsman to do the preliminary evidence-gathering for you but you are the one who filed it,' Calilung said in an interview on DZMM Teleradyo. 'Dapat mayroon ka nang solidong evidence to begin with. I don’t know if the piles of paper there na dala-dala nila, I really don’t know if yun ay sapat na. Kasi kung titingnan mo mayroong shock factor kung titingnan mo… pero ano ba ang laman nun?' he added. Calilung said this could be used later on for a possibility of filing a 'stronger impeachment case' against Duterte. 'Kumbaga you are already paving or clearing the path towards that or leaning towards the possibility. Pero yung Ombudsman kasi, you need to understand, ang gagawin lang naman niya basically is preponderance at pagtingin dito sa mga preliminary evidence,' the analyst said. 'If the Ombudsman feels there is a strong case, then tsaka siya mag-elevate ng kaso either to the Sandiganbayan, or if it is an impeachment, pwede niyang i-transmit sa Congress so they would be able to have a stronger case buildup against the Vice President,' he added. The Office of the Ombudsman, he added, would look into these complaints thoroughly. 'This will be supplementary evidence for the possibility of filing an impeachment complaint against the Vice President mainly. Ang nakita ko dito ay yung optics and the narrative that they would want to create is very obvious,' he said. Bagong Alyansang Makabayan (BAYAN) chair Teddy Casiño, for his part, said they are already preparing for Duterte’s next impeachment complaint. In an interview, Casiño said it is 'interesting' what would happen to Duterte’s plunder complaint at the Office of the Ombudsman, noting that this should be acted on first. 'Noong nakaraan, ang iniisip na papel ng Ombudsman, puwede siya mag-imbestiga pero ang kaso mapupunta din sa Kongreso through impeachment,' said Casiño 'Sa bahagi ng group namin, we are preparing for [the] next impeachment fight by February,' he said. Earlier on Saturday, Duterte said the plunder complaint filed against her is a 'fishing expedition' weaponized 'to create a semblance of procedural legitimacy for an investigation.' Casiño said they would monitor the developments regarding this. 'Ito yung paraan sa Konstitusyon paano panagutin ang impeachable official. So aantabayanan natin kung paano iha-handle ito ng Ombudsman kasi mayroong prinsipyo na immune from suit ang mga impeachable officials. At kailangan muna sila matanggal sa puwesto bago sila makasuhan,' Casiño said. A political analyst urged groups to look into other issues against Vice President Sara Duterte when filing complaints and ensure these have 'stronger legal merit.' MANILA — A political analyst on Sunday urged groups and stakeholders to look into other issues against Vice President Sara Duterte when filing complaints and ensure these have 'stronger legal merit,' noting this could lay the groundwork for a fresh impeachment complaint against the official. A political analyst urged groups to look into other issues against Vice President Sara Duterte when filing complaints and ensure these have 'stronger legal merit.'

title: DILG urges LGUs to audit public, private buildings to prepare for strong quakes
body: In a statement, the DILG said assessing the structural integrity of buildings is vital to ensure public safety and to lessen the risk of casualties and property damage during major seismic events. Aside from inspecting key public facilities such as hospitals, schools, government offices, and emergency response centers, the agency also wants privately owned structures to be covered by the audits. 'With the increasing frequency and intensity of earthquakes, proactive preparedness is our best defense... Through harmonized audits, stronger partnerships, and evidence-based planning, we can build safer, more resilient communities across the country ,' the DILG said. The DILG in 2024 partnered with various government agencies and professional groups, including the Association of Structural Engineers of the Philippines and the Philippine Institute of Civil Engineers, to develop the Harmonized Infrastructure Audit Tool (HIAT). The HIAT serves as a standardized framework for evaluating building resilience and identifying potential vulnerabilities before disasters strike. According to the DILG, 18 high-risk LGUs in Metro Manila, CALABARZON, and Central Luzon, areas traversed by the West Valley Fault, have already been trained to carry out infrastructure audits. Another 12 LGUs in the National Capital Region and 20 in Cavite, located outside the fault corridor, have also completed the training, while 10 more LGUs in Central Luzon are expected to follow suit by the end of the month. As part of the nationwide rollout, the department has also built a corps of trained coaches composed of technical experts from professional organizations, academic institutions, and local governments representing 74 LGUs nationwide. These coaches will mentor local audit teams and guide them in using the HIAT effectively. The DILG is also encouraging private sector participation through the establishment of Local Infrastructure Learning Hubs in its regional offices. These hubs will serve as centers of collaboration among LGUs, engineers, and schools to support audit teams led by local chief executives and composed of building officials and disaster management officers. 'By institutionalizing innovative safety mechanisms and strengthening intersectoral collaboration, the DILG reaffirms its commitment to building disaster-ready localities and ensuring a safer infrastructure sector for all Filipinos.' MANILA— The Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) is urging all local government units (LGUs) across the country to conduct comprehensive infrastructure audits on both public and private buildings to help communities prepare for the threat of strong earthquakes.

title: Beijing says US, Japan 'smeared and attacked' China
body: 'Ignoring China's grave concerns, the US and Japan have smeared and attacked China on Taiwan and maritime issues, grossly interfered in China's internal affairs, and seriously violated the basic norms governing international relations,' foreign ministry spokeswoman Mao Ning told a regular press conference. Biden and Kishida unveiled plans to restructure the US military command in Japan, the biggest such change since the 1960s. The move is aimed at making US and Japanese forces more nimble in the event of threats, such as a Chinese invasion of Taiwan. The US president insisted that the military upgrade in China's backyard was 'purely defensive' but has made no bones about his desire to create alliances to counter Beijing. In response to a question on Kishida's visit and the upgrade in ties, Mao said China was 'strongly dissatisfied with and firmly opposed to this and has lodged solemn representations with relevant parties'. 'The US-Japan relationship should not target or harm other countries' interests, and should not undermine regional peace and stability,' she said. Beijing on Thursday said the United States and Japan had 'smeared and attacked' China during a Washington summit, where US President Joe Biden and Japanese Prime Minister Fumio Kishida unveiled their countries' biggest-ever upgrade in defense ties. title: EU allocates P54M in humanitarian aid for typhoon-hit areas of PH
body: In a statement Friday, the EU said the funding will provide emergency assistance to the hardest-hit communities, focusing on shelter, health, and water and sanitation needs. The latest aid comes on top of the €6.5 million in assistance the EU has already allocated this year to strengthen the Philippines’ disaster preparedness. Last Tuesday, the death toll from Severe Tropical Storm Opong in the province of Masbate has risen to 18, the provincial government said. The Office of Civil Defense (OCD) said last week over 2 million Filipinos were displaced or affected by the back-to-back onslaught of Mirasol, Nando, and Opong, along with the southwest monsoon. After them, cyclone Paolo came and submerged some areas in flood waters. Aside from these weather disturbances, the EU said it is also closely monitoring the situation in Cebu, where a magnitude 6.9 earthquake struck Bogo City on September 30 — which left at least P3 billion worth of infrastructure damage in the province. 'The EU expresses its condolences to the families of the victims and stand ready to provide additional support pending on-going needs assessment,' the EU said. Seeking to help address the needs of Filipinos affected by recent typhoons, the European Union has released on Friday €800 000 or P54 million in humanitariad aid. MANILA — Seeking to help address the needs of Filipinos affected by recent typhoons, the European Union has released on Friday €800 000 or P54 million in humanitarian aid.

title: PGH records lowest number of COVID patients in a year
body: The Philippine General Hospital will to non-COVID patients as it recorded its lowest number of virus patients in more than a year, its spokesman said Friday. The country's largest COVID-19 referral center now only has 54 confirmed virus patients, according to Dr. Jonas del Rosario. It was able to close 3 of its 4 COVID-19 wards and open these to non-virus patients, he said. 'The one good news I was the told, for the last 2 days we have not admitted anybody with COVID. Our numbers are continuously going down,' he told ANC's Headstart. 'Double digit numbers are really a rare occurrence in PGH and who knows maybe in the next few days or weeks we will be down to the 20s, and that’s good news because we can now open our hospital to non-COVID patients.' Del Rosario attributed the decline in virus cases to herd immunity achieved in Metro Manila. At least 50 percent of the capital region's eligible population has been fully vaccinated, authorities earlier said. 'I think that’s a big part of it, a lot of people got vaccinated. I’m sure there are also a lot of people who got COVID...they developed natural immunity. I would say it’s really the herd immunity we achieved for the last 3-4 months,' he said. Government is urged to increase health workers' salary and invest in medical facility infrastructures as preparation, according to Del Rosario. 'We might have all the equipment, all the rooms, if we do not have the people to man the hospital then we’re still limited to the number of patients we can take care of,' he said. 'Maybe now that there’s this lull, maybe we can look into dedicating a big hospital maybe like what Wuhan did. Try to identify or make a hospital for this crisis, for a pandemic, so even if it happens you already have a hospital.' The Philippines has fully vaccinated 36.9 individuals, while 51.8 million others have received an initial dose as of Wednesday, according to data gathered by the ABS-CBN Investigative and Research Group. It inoculated some 7.6 million during the 3-day national vaccination drive, prompting local governments to extend until Friday and another run from Dec. 15 to 17. MANILA - The Philippine General Hospital has recorded its lowest number of virus patients in more than a year, its spokesman said Friday.

title: Higit 300 pamilya nasunugan sa Port Area, Maynila
body: Ayon sa Bureau of Fire Protection - Manila, dalawampung minuto lang ang lumipas o dakong 12:24 a.m. ay agad na itinaas sa Task Force Alpha ang sunog. Higit 25 fire trucks mula BFP ang rumesponde at mahigit 100 fire volunteers ang tumulong sa pag-apula ng apoy. Mabilis na kumalat ang apoy dahil pawang gawa sa mga light materials ang magkakadikit na bahay. Naging hamon din sa mga bumbero ang masikip na eskinita. Kaya bukod sa hirap na makapasok ang mga fire trucks, naging mabagal ang suplay ng tubig. 'This is considered fire-prone area po kasi gawa po sa light materials halos lahat ng kabahayan po rito. Ang naging struggle namin, sobrang daming tao. At the same time, iyong mga allies natin, iyong daanan ng hose is sobrang sisikip po. And iyong supply ng ating tubig medyo kinukulang dahil malayo po ang location ng ating fire hydrant,' ani Fire Senior Inspector Leslie Ramos, Station 4 Commander ng Manila Fire District. Dagdag pa ng BFP-Manila, nagsimula umano ang sunog sa ikaapat na palapag ng isang bahay. Ligtas ang pamilya ni Deedang Abdurahman pero natupok ang kanilang bahay at mga gamit. Kuwento niya, katatapos niya lang magtinda sa mga oras na nangyari ang sunog. 'Wala kaming nakuhang gamit. Wala talaga. Ito lang mga suot namin. Wala na kaming ibang nakuha. Problema ko nga iyong birth certificates ng mga anak ko, nandoon hindi ko nakuha,' saad ni Deedang Abdurahman, isa sa mga daan-daang nasunugan. Hindi pa tukoy kung ano ang naging mitsa ng sunog at halaga ng pinsalang iniwan nito. Idineklara ang Fire Under Control bago mag alas-6 ng umaga. Nagpapatuloy rin ang imbestigasyon ng BFP-Manila kung may naitalang sugatan o nasawi sa insidente. Summertime signals a break from school and moments spent with friends. For those who are working, long-awaited vacation leaves provide the chance for spontaneous trips and memorable adventures. Many turn to social media for travel suggestions. However, disappointment often follows due to misleading reviews and inflated ratings. Traveling to these destinations also presents its own set of challenges. Grab Philippines Country Head Ronald Roda and Department of Tourism Assistant Secretary Sherlene Batin lead the launch of the #GrabIbaAngSummer campaign, spotlighting under-the-radar adventure and culinary spots across the Philippines. Photo source: Grab Philippines This summer, Grab Philippines aims to help users go beyond the typical. In a launch event at the BGC Arts Center in Taguig, Grab introduced two new features on the app that promise to enrich travel experiences. Through its latest campaign titled: Grab Iba ang Summer, the brand invites users to uncover hidden culinary gems and explore lesser-known destinations in the country. Two new in-app tools are central to this campaign: GrabCar Travel Guide and GrabFood 5-Star Eats. 'So, this summer, what I wish from you is not to be stuck with the usual. Grab Iba ang Summer is a campaign that embodies what we do best—unlocking fresh and new experiences,' said Ronald Roda, Grab Philippines Country Head. 'As an online platform, we are enhancing the visibility and accessibility of hidden gems, adventure spots, and culinary treasures tucked away in various corners of the country,' Roda continues. GrabCar Travel Guide provides a curated list of destinations that users may easily access through the app. A single tap sets the course for your next adventure. Consumers continuously search for exciting experiences, from the local restaurants and cozy cafes to unique staycations and scenic escapes. Authenticity, aesthetic appeal, and comfort remain top priorities. Grab simplifies the discovery process by highlighting must-visit locations informed by real-time data such as social media trends and ride activity. Categories such as Thrill Trips, Staycation Hotspots, Chow Zones, and Caffeine Corners allow users to browse destinations that suit their interests. 'Typically, GrabCar is used for getting to work, and GrabFood is just for when you are hungry. What we want to do is show people the many other ways they can use the app—such as discovering experiences. That is why we chose 'experiences' as the theme of our summer campaign,' Roda explained. 1. Open the Grab app and tap on the GrabCar icon. 2. Scroll down to locate the GrabCar Travel Guide section. 3. Swipe through curated lists such as Thrill Trips, Staycation Hotspots, Chow Zones, and Caffeine Corners. 4. Select your destination, and it will automatically become your drop-off point. Grab 5-Star Eats highlights highly rated food spots on GrabFood. This curated list features community-based restaurants known for quality food, excellent service, and customer satisfaction. The platform emphasizes authenticity by focusing on eateries that consistently receive 5-star ratings and glowing user reviews. 'Grab is more than just rides and food. Through GrabCar Travel Guide and Grab 5-Star Eats, we are enabling users to discover meaningful experiences while uplifting local businesses,' Roda shared. During the Grab Iba ang Summer launch, three partner food businesses introduced their signature dishes to guests: Ceci Korean – Operating since 2018, this Korean grocery and street food business has earned a loyal customer base. Co-owner Ada Rojo said, 'It is an honor to be supported by Grab. Seeing our business grow through this platform confirms that we offer real value to our customers.' Kind Cookie – Launched during the pandemic and officially opened in Makati in 2024, the bakery offers 18 unique flavors and donates a portion of sales to charity. 'Our goal is to make customers happy, and the 5-star ratings feel like a crown. Grab has been a big help, especially for young entrepreneurs. It sustained our business during difficult times and continues to support us,' said owner Jamie Icasas. Sugarbee – A neighborhood favorite for 22 years, Sugar Bee is known for its Salted Caramel Chocolate Cake and Alfajores. Owner Jenny Silayan noted, 'Being part of Grab 5-Star Eats helps us reach more customers and provides a platform for local businesses to grow.' To qualify for the Grab 5-Star Eats list, merchants must meet the following criteria: - Maintain a customer rating of 4.5 or higher. - Deliver consistent and reliable service. 1. Open the Grab app and tap on the GrabFood tile. 2. Scroll and tap GrabFood 5-Star Eats on the homepage. 3. Browse top-rated restaurants and begin your order. Through GrabCar Travel Guide and GrabFood 5-Star Eats, Grab supports MSMEs by helping them reach broad audiences. This initiative not only brings authentic experiences to users, but also uplifts local economies. 'We are proud to support MSMEs and offer them a platform to showcase their best. Whether it is a family-owned bakery or a hidden tourist destination, Grab ensures these treasures become more accessible,' said Roda. Collaboration with the Department of Tourism (DOT) Grab has partnered with the Department of Tourism (DOT) for the Grab Iba ang Summer campaign. This nationwide initiative aims to encourage exploration beyond major urban centers while showcasing the rich culture, flavors, and destinations of the Philippines. Photo source: Mark Demayo, ABS-CBN News 'This partnership celebrates the vibrant culinary scene of the Philippines and provides MSMEs in the tourism sector with the support they need to thrive in the digital age,' said Sharlene Batin, Assistant Secretary for Tourism Digitalization and Resource Generation. Roda added, 'Since this campaign revolves around gastronomy and adventure, we collaborated with the DOT to highlight key experiences. We want people to explore more and, in doing so, support local spots and small businesses.' The launch event at the BGC Arts Center featured summer-themed décor, a vibrant atmosphere, and interactive booths. Media members, content creators, and influencers enjoyed photo opportunities, tote bag customizations, and hands-on demos of the app’s new features. Introducing the Grab Domestic Travel Pack To make summer travel memorable, Grab has unveiled the Grab Travel Pack, a limited-time offer designed for domestic travelers. For only P99, users can access 15-day exclusive discounts: - P200 off for advanced airport ride bookings - P50 off on GrabFood orders with a minimum spend of P400 - Special deals from Agoda, Klook, Smart, 2Go, and Havaianas Grab currently services eight major airports across the Philippines, ensuring smooth, convenient travel for summer escapades. This summer, experience more. Discover more. Support local. Summer hits differently with the new GrabCar Travel Guide and GrabFood 5-Star Eats. Phone brand nubia unveils their Neo 3 series in an event launch in Manila after the success of the Neo 2 series. Mark Demayo, ABS-CBN News MANILA — Chinese smartphone brand nubia released the third iteration of their gaming-focused Neo Series, featuring the Neo 3, Neo 3 5G and the Neo 3 GT. The budget gaming phone series was launched in a mall event and showcased in a Mobile Legends: Bang Bang community match between selected attendees and M6 Champions ONIC Philippines. Fans play a Mobile Legends: Bang Bang series with M6 Champions ONIC Philippines during the launch of the nubia Neo 3 series. Mark Demayo, ABS-CBN News The Neo 3 series comes after the success of the Neo 2 series last year according to Gavin Lian, ZTE Philippines' General Manager for Terminals. 'Because once we shipped it here in the Philippines, just one week or two weeks it goes out of stock. So that's why we have the confidence for this year we launched the Neo3 series,' Lian said on the sideline of the product launch. With high demand for their flagship Red Magic gaming phones, the Neo series is an alternative for those who want to enjoy some of the flagship's features on a budget such as the air triggers and cooling system, Lian adds. 'Actually, there are a lot of customers who want to enjoy the game. But they want to have the shooter trigger, have the cooling system like the Red Magic. But sometimes they don't have enough budget to buy the Red Magic. That's why we have the Neo series to meet the other requirements of the customers.' Aside from the mentioned gaming shoulder triggers, the series features AI optimizations such as their NeoTurbo AI Engine and AI Frame Rate Stabilization for better gaming. Other hardware shared between the series is a 6000mAh battery with bypass charging, which draws direct power from the charger, an 6.8-inch display with a 120Hz refresh rate, and a triple AI camera system. The nubia Neo 3 4G is priced at P7,999, the nubia Neo 3 5G at P9,999, and the nubia Neo 3 GT at P12,999. Higit 300 pamilya o 200 kabahayan ang apektado ng sumiklab na sunog sa Barangay 650, Port Area, Manila pasado alas-12 ng madaling araw ng Miyerkules.

title: AFP chief visits Pag-asa Island, inspects other facilities in Palawan
body: AFP chief Gen. Andres Centino visited Pag-asa Island in the West Philippine Sea and inspected other government facilities in Palawan. During his visit last Friday, Centino also led the donning of three-star rank on Vice Admiral Alberto Carlos, the new commander of the AFP Western Command. Carlos became chief of the AFP Wescom after Vice Admiral Ramil Roberto Enriquez retired last Jan. 21. 'This is very significant because we are all here in the most remote deployment of our troops, the valiant defenders of the Philippines' first frontier,' Centino said of the donning ceremony, according to a statement from the AFP Public Affairs Office. Pag-asa (international name: Thitu Island) is part of the Spratlys, which is being claimed by several Asian countries including the Philippines and China, as well as Taiwan. It is among nine features in the Kalayaan Islands Group occupied by the Philippines, and is part of Palawan's Kalayaan municipality. A day earlier, the Philippine military chief also inspected the headquarters of Joint Task Force (JTF) Malampaya in El Nido, which is responsible for safeguarding the Malampaya gas field, the AFP said. He also went to the former Matinloc and Nido gas platforms, which are being manned by JTF Malampaya. Centino inspected as well the Philippine Navy's BRP Jose Rizal, which conducts routine patrols in the West Philippine Sea. During his talk to the troops, Centino assured them that the AFP would support the continued modernization of the Navy. China is the most aggressive among claimants in the South China Sea, within which is the West Philippine Sea, building artificial islands on some features, fortifying those with military installations, and driving away officials and fishermen from other countries. It continues to defy a landmark ruling of the Permanent Court of Arbitration in 2016, declaring its nine-dash line claim over almost the entire South China Sea as having no legal basis. MANILA — Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) chief Gen. Andres Centino visited Pag-asa Island in the West Philippine Sea and inspected other government facilities in Palawan, the organization said Sunday.

title: Lalaki pinagbabaril ang pamangkin dahil 'pinakulam' umano ang kaniyang apo
body: Ayon kay Col. Randy Glenn Silvio, Acting District Director ng Quezon City Police District, nangyari ang pamamaril sa kahabaan ng Mindanao Avenue sa Barangay Talipapa pasado alas 5:00 ng hapon noong Linggo. Nakasakay sa motor ang biktima nang pagbabarilin siya ng suspek na nakasakay din sa motorsiklo. 'Alam namin papasok sa trabaho at dun nga po naka-receive tayo ng call sa 122 yung station 3 at nirespondehan naman agad natin at yun nga, during the investigation nagtanong-tanong dun sa tapat, may narinig po silang tatlong putok ng baril,' kwento ni Silvio. Agad tumakas ang suspek sakay ang kanyang motorsiklo dala ang ginamit na baril. Naaresto ang suspek sa kanyang bahay sa Barangay Culiat pasado alas 11:00 ng gabi noong Lunes, November 3. Nakuha sa kanya ang kalibre .38 na baril na may lamang mga bala at ang motorsiklong ginamit sa krimen. Ayon sa suspek, pinakulam ng biktima ang kanyang apo kaya niya nagawa ang pamamaril. 'Pinagsususpetsahan niya itong victim na nagpakulam sa apo niya kaya niya po nagawa yon. Ginantihan niya,' sabi ni Silvio. Aminado naman ang suspek sa nagawang krimen. Depensa niya, inunahan niya lamang ang biktima dahil plano din siyang patayin nito. 'Nung pagbantaan niya kami patayin lahat, kasi napatay niya yung dalawa kong apo dun sa pagbabanta niya dun sa nanay ng bata… Mailap na siya eh, kasi isusunod na niya ako… Hindi ko intensyon na patayin siya.' Nakakulong na ang suspek sa Criminal Investugation and Detection Unit sa Camp Karingal. Nasampahan na siya ng mga reklamong murder, paglabag sa Comprehensive Firearms and Ammunition Regulation Act, at Motorcycle Crime Prevention Act. MAYNILA — Arestado ang 44-anyos na lalaki sa pamamaril sa kanyang 32-anyos na pamangkin sa Quezon City noong Linggo ng hapon, November 2.

title: Philippines to 'seek help' securing release of Houthi-held sailors
body: The Iran-backed Houthis released footage on Monday of crew members missing after attacks on the Eternity C and Magic Seas cargo ships, claiming in an accompanying statement to have 'rescued' the mariners. Last week, Human Rights Watch said the rebels were unlawfully detaining the crew and that their attacks on shipping amounted to war crimes. The United States has accused the Houthis of kidnapping. Philippine Foreign Undersecretary Eduardo De Vega confirmed the Houthis were holding nine Filipino seafarers. 'I do not want to use the term hostage. At least we know they are alive,' he told AFP. 'We're not going to talk directly with the Houthis. We're going to seek help from friendly countries,' he added. The European Union's Operation Aspides naval task force told AFP that 15 of the 25 people onboard the Eternity C were still missing -- with four presumed dead. The Philippines Department of Migrant Workers, which has overseen efforts to bring the survivors home, did not immediately respond to a request for comment. The Houthis sank the Magic Seas and Eternity C in separate Red Sea attacks this month, after a temporary hiatus in their campaign against maritime traffic. The Houthis launched attacks on ships in the trade route soon after the start of the Gaza war, claiming solidarity with Palestinians. The sinking of the Magic Seas was their first attack since late last year, with the Eternity C facing a similar fate soon after. In its statement, the Houthis said they rescued 11 crew members, including two injured, and also recovered a body from aboard the ship before it sank. The video appeared to show the moment the mostly Filipino crew were pulled from the sea wearing life jackets. A man the Houthis said was an electrician was shown lying in bed and speaking in English. Aspides had said a Russian electrician onboard the ship had lost his leg. De Vega said one of the nine Filipinos had suffered an unspecified injury, and that one of the non-Filipino personnel was also injured. Two weeks ago, eight other Filipino crew members who survived the Eternity C attack were flown back to the Philippines. All 17 Filipino seafarers from the Magic Seas have likewise been flown home. Previously, the rebels held the mostly Filipino crew of the Galaxy Leader merchant ship for more than a year, before releasing them in January. Filipino sailors make up as much as 30 percent of the world's commercial shipping force. The nearly $7 billion they sent home in 2023 accounted for about a fifth of the remittances to the archipelago nation. The Philippines said Tuesday it would ask 'friendly countries' to help secure the release of nine Filipino sailors being held by Yemen's Houthi rebels. The Philippines said Tuesday it would ask 'friendly countries' to help secure the release of nine Filipino sailors being held by Yemen's Houthi rebels.

title: DOJ prosecutors indict 6 alleged rebels for murder, terrorism
body: Prosecutors have indicted 6 alleged rebels for murder and terrorism over an encounter with soldiers in Batangas in July last year. Those who will be charged before the Regional Trial Court of Batangas include Isagani Isita, Junalice Arante-Isita, Mariano Bico and Gilbert Orr who all go by different aliases and 2 more persons only known by their aliases, according to a a press briefer from the DOJ. They were supposedly identified as members of the Sub-Regional Military Area-4C, Southern Tagalog Regional Party Committee of the Communist Party of the Philippines-New People’s Army (CPP-NPA) who figured in an encounter with members of the Philippine Army on July 18, 2022 in Sito Amatong, Barangay Ginhawa in Taysan, Batangas. The 6 allegedly carried shotguns, M16 rifles and long high-powered firearms, bullets and improvised blasting machine and fired at the soldiers who were conducting a focused military operation in the area. As a result, a soldier was injured and a 9-year-old civilian bystander died. 'After evaluation of the evidence, the Panel of Prosecutors found sufficient evidence to hold respondents for trial. While it may appear that the murder victim was not the direct target of herein respondents and was just caught in the line of fire when the respondents fired upon the members of the Philippine Army, her death clearly was the result of the unlawful acts perpetrated by the respondents,' the press briefer said, citing a Dec. 27, 2022 resolution recommending prosecution for murder. The panel of prosecutors also found probable cause to charge the alleged rebels with terrorism for violating section 4(a) and 4(d) of the Anti-Terrorism Act of 2020. Section 4(a) punishes engaging in acts intended to cause death or serious bodily injury or to endanger a person’s life, while section 4(d) considers as terrorism the possession, supply or use of weapons, explosives, or biological, nuclear, radiological or chemical weapons if the purpose is to intimidate the public, create an atmosphere of fear, seriously destabilize the fundamental political, economic or social structures of the country, or create a public emergency or undermine public safety. 'Clearly, respondents’ use of high-powered firearms seriously endangered the lives of the members of the Philippine Army and consummated in injury and death,' the DOJ said. 'Further, the said weapons were intended to cause a disproportionate amount of damage or harm of sufficient magnitude. The acts of respondents were calculated to seriously undermine public safety and to create an atmosphere of fear,' it added. The DOJ has yet to release a full copy of the resolution. MANILA — Department of Justice (DOJ) prosecutors have indicted 6 alleged rebels for murder and terrorism over an encounter with soldiers in Batangas in July last year which led to the death of a 9-year-old civilian and the injury of a soldier, the agency said Wednesday.

title: Bongbong: Up to people to define Marcos legacy
body: 'Alam ng tao ang nangyari, kaya siguro, self-serving kung ako ang magsasabi. Ang lagi kong sinasabi, tingnan niyo ang kasaysayan. Tingnan ninyo kung ano ba talaga ang nangyari. Kung gusto ninyo, tingnan niyo hindi 'yung kwento, kung hindi talaga ang mga nangyari, anong pagbabago ang dumating doon. Ang tao na ang maghusga,' Marcos said in an interview on DZMM. He also added that politicians should not be asked about his father's legacy, since all of them have their own political agenda. 'Ang paghusga sa aking ama, huwag nating iwan sa mga pulitiko, dahil may agenda lahat,' Marcos said. During the 'Ikaw Na Ba: The DZMM Vice-Presidential Candidates' Interview,' Marcos also gave his opinion on different issues such as the welfare of persons with disability (PWD), teachers, the implementation of the K-12 program, and other political issues. Marcos expressed his approval of the Social Security System (SSS) pension hike, the anti-political dynasty bill, the reopening of the investigation on the Mamasapano incident, lowering of income tax, the Freedom of Information (FOI) bill, and charter change. He is also in favor of federalism, but added that the government should first decide whether it is ready to make changes in the Constitution. 'Approve, in the sense na kailangan nating pag-aralan ng mabuti. Pero kailangan nating pag-usapan na at kailangan na natin i-decide kung bubuksan talaga natin ang Saligang Batas,' Marcos explained. Marcos, likewise, expressed his approval of the use of death penalty for criminals involved in illegal drugs, as well as allowing former president Gloria Macapagal Arroyo and Senators Ramon 'Bong' Revilla Jr. and Jinggoy Estrada to be placed under house arrest. On divorce, Marcos believes that having a divorce law in the country would only destroy marriages. 'Dahil parang hindi bagay sa Pilipino eh. So, palagay ko, para maging mas matibay ang pamilya. Kasi kung masyadong madaling magdivorce, talagang magdidivorce. Mag-away lang ng konti, magdidivorce eh. Sa Amerika, 50 percent ang divorce rate. Siguro kung walang divorce, at least sasabihin natin na ayusin,' he said. He also expressed his disapproval of the Bangsamoro Basic Law (BBL), adding that the Senate is working on a better version of the law. ''Pag sinasabing BBL, ako ang pagkaunawa ko, 'yung original draft ng BBL. Ang batas na tinatalakay ngayon sa Senado ay Basic Law on the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region. Hindi BBL, BLBAR [Basic Law for the Bangsamoro Area of Responsibility] ang tawag namin. So kung tatanungin mo sa akin, BBL, disapprove. Kung BLBAR, approve.' Marcos believes the government needs to provide PWDs and other vulnerable people the means to provide for themselves so as to empower them. 'Dapat ang pamahalaan ay gagawin, susuportahan, lalo na 'yung persons with disabilities (PWDs) na sila ay magkakaroon ng silang hanapbuhay, na bibigyan sila ng pagkakataon na may training, na meron silang, bibigyan sila ng pagkakataon na makapaghanapbuhay ng disente, para naman hindi lang sila umaasa sa tulong ng gobyerno. Hindi lang 'yung handout na binibigay, dahil ang tao, ang dignidad ng tao, ay nanggagaling doon sa kanilang kakayahan na buhayin ang kanilang sarili, buhayin ang kanilang pamilya at ipag-aral ang kanilang mga anak. 'Yan ay ibinibigay natin sa lahat ng mamamayang Pilipino, ay dapat ay lahat, lalo na ang mga persons with disabilities, na mas malaki ang pangangailangan, siguro dapat mas mabigat ang suporta natin niyan,' he said. 'Hindi lamang PWD, 'yung mga vulnerable, 'yung mga bata, 'yung mga matatanda, 'yung may sakit, 'yung may disabilities, lahat 'yan ay madaling makalimutan at madisenfranchise. Huwag nating pabayaan na mangyari yun,' Marcos added. On the issue of the implementation of the K-12 program, Marcos said the government needs to reassess the status of the country's educational system so as to ensure that all sectors are ready for its full implementation. ''Yung problema, hindi sa prinsipyo ng K-12, ang problema, doon sa implementasyon. Hindi pa natin masyadong nagagawa, kaya 'yung 60 percent, hindi siguro...magiging magulo 'yan eh kasi kung dadalhin mo sa private schools, magkakagulo 'yan. Sinong pupunta sa private, sinong pupunta sa public? Sinong magiging scholar sa private, sinong magiging scholar sa public?' he said. He added that the government should also review the timeline of the implementation of K-12, and push it back if necessary. 'Siguro dapat tingnan natin ng mabuti ang schedule ng full implementation ng K-12, at isipin nating mabuti, gaano pa katagal ang kailangan natin? Gaano pa karami ang kailangan nating gawin tungkol sa pagtayo ng school room at pagkuha ng bagong teacher, at gawin natin 'yung tunay na schedule para naman 'pag sinabi nating full implementation, talagang full implementation na,' Marcos explained. For Marcos, it is up to the courts to decide whether Senator Grace Poe should be disqualified from the presidential race. 'Wala akong ginugusto na madisqualify, pero kailangan natin sundan ang batas. Hindi ko naman sinasabi na i-disqualify siya, pero kung hindi natin susundan ang Saligang Batas, nasaan na tayo? For me, it's still a legal question. Kailangan natin sagutin ang legal question.' He also joked that he is willing to give a DNA sample if it can help Poe's case, adding that it was only in 2013 when he heard of rumors that Poe is his sister. 'Ang katotohanan, narinig ko lang 'yun nung 2013 elections. Natatawa ako. Paulit ulit ang media na nagtatanong, handa ba kayong magpaDNA? Sinabi ko o sige, kung makakatulong sa kanya, wala namang mawawala sa akin,' Marcos said. Should Nur Misuari be held responsible for Zamboanga siege? Marcos believes that aside from looking into those who are responsible for the 2013 Zamboanga siege, the government should also look into the root cause of the siege. 'Kung siya talaga ang naging inspirasyon noong uprising sa Zamboanga, at may namatay, may nasaktan, labag sa batas ang nangyari, kailangan niyang sagutin 'yun. Pero tingnan din natin kung ano 'yung nagiging dahilan kung bakit nangyari 'yun. Naging dahilan 'yun dahil ginawa 'yung BBL na nga, na binalewala 'yung mga treaty, yung Tripoli Agreement, 'yung Jakarta Agreement, na pinirmahan na nga sa gitna ng gobyerno at ng MNLF. Nung lumabas ang BBL, walang role ang MNLF. Ang tanong nga nila, anong nangyari doon sa pinaglabanan namin, pinagsakripisyuhan namin,' he explained. Marcos said he wants to work in the Department of Labor and Employment (DOLE) should he be elected as vice president. 'Isa sa pinakamalaking problema ang trabaho dito sa atin. At nakikita ko naman, ayusin lang natin, konti lang ang kailangan nating gawin, ayusin lang ang training program sa gobyerno para sa private sector, ayusin natin ang infrastructure para palakihin at pagandahin 'yung ekonomiya, at hindi lamang 'yun, yung tinatawag na business driven infrastructure, para lumaki 'yung ekonomiya,' he explained. He also said that despite the common thinking that vice presidents only wait for the chance to be president, Marcos believes that it is up to the person who will be elected if he wants to work on his advocacies while serving as vice president. 'Pero dito sa atin, hiwalay ang pagboto sa presidente, hiwalay ang pagboto sa bise, kaya may sariling posisyon ang vice president dahil may mandate sa tao na sarili niya. Ngayon, dahil hindi maliwanag sa Constitution kung ano pa, bukod sa pag-aantay na kailangan na mag-presidente, kung ano pa ang trabaho ng isang vice president, nasa tao na 'yun, kung sinong uupo bilang vice president,' Marcos said. MANILA - Vice-presidential candidate Senator Ferdinand ''Bongbong'' Marcos Jr. on Thursday said it is up to the people to decide what his father, the late president Ferdinand Marcos, has left as his legacy.

title: 'Devotees can secure traslacion sans cops'
body: In an interview on ANC’s 'Talkback,' Albayalde said he believes devotees have enough discipline to manage the annual traslacion on their own. 'They can do it without security forces,' he said, noting that lay marshalls of the procession take it upon themselves to discipline unruly devotees. Albayalde said policemen do not have problems managing the devotees since they know exactly what to do. 'Basically, these devotees are very manageable because they are very disciplined. They know exactly what to do before and after the mass. They have these things that they have to do,' he said. 'Everything they do it’s like step by step they know what to do.' A total of 5,762 law enforcers were deployed to secure the Black Nazarene procession this year.
