Casiple also believes the elite may also present some roadblocks in Duterte's path to peace with the left. 'It's not only the peace process but the entire political position of the elite has been undermined. And any problem in the peace process can be used to capitalize in the campaign against him,' he said. But Casiple maintained it would be unfair to characterize Duterte as socialist, communist, leftist in his pursuance of peace talks with the Communist Party of the Philippines (CPP). 'It’s not an ideological-based political thinking that’s governing Duterte. He’s more the street-smart; he’s willing to go out of the box in many issues,' he said. According to Casiple, the key to success of the peace talks is to build more trust between the government and the CPP-NPA, and that began when Duterte sent to Oslo, Norway a delegation that CPP Founder Jose Maria Sison has confidence in. 'Joma Sison is confident with the people who went to Oslo, because he had been talking to them throughout the years, more than a decade in fact, and there had been, I don’t think a problem with this relations,' he said. The United States and the elite may hinder the peace process President-elect Rodrigo Duterte wants to pursue, political analyst Ramon Casiple said.

title: Grandpa who died in NLEX Marilao incident saved 2-year-old
body: According to Marilao police chief PltCol. Eulogio Lamqui III, the 54-year-old man who was killed when a trailer truck hit the gantry of the Marilao Interchange was able to secure the safety of his granddaughter. 'Bale nung nangyari po yung aksidente, niyakap po niya yung bata, nung bumaligtad po yung sasakyan yakap-yakap po nung matanda yung bata kaya naligtas at hindi siya gaanong nasugatan,' Lamqui told ABS-CBN News. Lamqui said seven members of the family from Parañaque were onboard their AUV going to Baguio City. 'Galing po ng Parañaque ito ay pupunta po sila ng Baguio…pasyal sa mga relatives po,' Lamqui said. According to Lamqui, four out of six survivors remain in the hospital for treatment. Meantime, the 31-year-old truck driver who remains in detention at the Marilao police station asked for forgiveness from the family of the victims. He said he is willing to face the consequence of what happened. 'Pasensiya na ho, hindi ko naman ginusto yun, pagbayaran na lang kung may kasalanan yun,' he said. He is facing multiple charges, according to Lamqui including reckless imprudence resulting in homicide. The two-year old survivor from the tragic incident along the North Luzon Expressway (NLEX) in Marilao, Bulacan on Wednesday was saved by his grandfather who later died that day. MANILA — The two-year old survivor from the tragic incident along the North Luzon Expressway (NLEX) in Marilao, Bulacan on Wednesday was saved by his grandfather who later died that day.

title: Several lanes in EDSA-Pasay closed for EDSA rehab
body: Early today, December 24, workers from the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) and its contractors widened the closed parts of EDSA in Pasay City area near Roxas Boulevard. Concrete barriers are now placed at the middle of northbound and southbound lanes of EDSA-Pasay, closing about 3 lanes on both sides. As a result, motorists experienced early and heavier traffic jam in the area. 'Mabagal-bagal po ang daloy ng biyahe namin - wala po, dito lang talaga po kami - sa EDSA lang talaga po,' bus driver Roderick Bardon told ABS-CBN News. 'Talagang apektado talaga biyahe namin, wala na kong kita,' said Alipio, jeepney driver in the area. Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) said in its advisory, the EDSA repair works will be on a 24-7 schedule from December 24 at 11:00 p.m., until 4:00 a.m., of January 5, 2026. The EDSA rehabilitation includes road re-blocking in some select parts and there will also be asphalt overlay along busway lanes. Meanwhile, backhoe operator Rafael Jumawan told ABS-CBN News that he informed his family that he will not be with them in tonight’s noche buena, as needs to work. 'Wala tayo magagawa eh. Utos ng amo namin kailangan magtrabaho eh. Hindi po, okay lang, nagpaalaam naman ako sa mga anak ko na trabaho muna, kailangan eh,' said Jumawan. The DPWH is expected to issue and updated schedule of the next repair works starting December 28 to January 5, 2026. By January 5, some construction works will only be done at night to minimize impact on traffic. It is all systems go for the scheduled rehabilitation of Epifanio delos Santos Avenue (EDSA) that will commence on Wednesday night at 11:00 p.m. MANILA — It is all systems go for the scheduled rehabilitation of Epifanio delos Santos Avenue (EDSA) that will commence on Wednesday night at 11:00 p.m.

title: 'Threat to sovereignty': Marcos says gov't will 'not lift a finger' to help ICC probe
body: The Philippines 'will not lift a finger to help any investigation' of the International Criminal Court, President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. said on Tuesday. 'Let me say this for the 100th time. I do not recognize the jurisdiction of ICC in the Philippines. I consider this as a threat to our sovereignty,' Marcos Jr. told reporters in a chance interview in Quezon City. 'Therefore, the Philippine government will not lift a finger to help any investigation that the ICC conducts,' he said. Sen. Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa, national police chief during the Duterte administration, on Monday had urged Marcos to 'be man enough' and say whether ICC investigators were already in the Philippines. Marcos Jr. said Tuesday that while ICC officials 'can come and visit the Philippines,' authorities would ensure that 'they do not come into contact with any agency of government.' 'If they are contacting agencies of government, na sasabihin ng whatever, pulis, local government, ‘Wag nyong sasagutin.’ Iyon ang sagot natin,' he said. 'We do not recognize your jurisdiction, therefore, we will not assist in any way, shape, or form, in any investigation that the ICC is doing in the Philippines,' he said. Justice Secretary Jesus Crispin Remulla said last week that the government would not stop ICC personnel from taking statements or gathering evidence as long as they do not break the law. 'But we have to clarify many issues, especially about procedure,' he also said. Dela Rosa said Monday that the President should be frank about whether the ICC's investigators had already been let into the country. 'Sabihan na lang kami, hindi yung patago. Ang mangyari dyan, para tayong sinaksak sa likod niyan pagka ganun, di ba? I-totoo nyo kami kung pumayag kayo. Hindi na yung patago na magugulat na lang tayo di ba? Please be man enough to tell us,' he said. Dela Rosa, a former chief of the Philippine National Police, was the implementer of former President Rodrigo Duterte’s war on drugs, which is the subject of ICC investigations due to possible crimes against humanity. MANILA — The Philippines 'will not lift a finger to help any investigation' of the International Criminal Court (ICC), President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. said on Tuesday, following speculation that ICC probers had already finished their investigation in the country over the war on drugs.

title: 46% in Japan have issues interacting with foreign co-workers — survey
body: The figures underscore that Japanese companies may need to make more efforts in creating better environments to integrate foreign staff, as increasing numbers are employed amid labor shortages triggered by an aging population and falling birthrates at home. The number of foreign workers in Japan surpassed 2 million for the first time as of the end of October 2023, according to data released in January by the Ministry of Health, Labor and Welfare. The survey conducted in March by human resources company Staff Service Holdings Co. targeted adults aged 20 to 69 and received responses from 1,467 people who currently work with foreign nationals or have experience doing so. Language issues were chosen by 57.3 percent of respondents in a multiple choice section as a cause of difficulties in communicating. Misunderstandings or mistakes arising from insufficient Japanese comprehension were cited by 32.3 percent. Despite the hurdles presented by language difficulties, 53.4 percent of managers, executives and senior employees surveyed said their firms do not offer programs or provide opportunities for foreign staff to learn Japanese. Labor shortages emerged as the top motivation for hiring foreigners in the survey, with some companies hiring people regardless of nationality, while others have job roles that specifically require foreign language skills. TOKYO — Some 45.8 percent of Japanese who have worked with foreign colleagues experienced communication difficulties, a private firm's survey results showed Sunday, with language issues blamed in the majority of cases.

title: Protesters demand release of minors arrested after Manila rally
body: Salinlahi Alliance for Children’s Concerns said many of those apprehended were only bystanders and should not have been detained. Police had arrested more than 200 in the wake of violence in parts of Manila that erupted when the rallies ended. 'They were left inside without proper medical care, sleeping on cold floors, with injuries unattended and food and water inadequate,' said Candice Sering of Salinlahi. 'Even if they joined, it’s their democratic right,' she added. Among those who joined the indignation protest was Marianne Karingal, mother of 'Fishball Boy' Alvin Karingal, who she said has a mental condition and should not have been locked up. 'Wala namang ginagawa ang anak ko. Kita ko sa video, hinatak niyo siya, nasira ang damit at sinikmuraan pa. Masakit para sa akin bilang ina,' Karingal said. (My son didn't do anything. You can see in the video that police grabbed him. His clothes were ripped and he was hit in the stomach. That hurt me as a mother to see it) She said she wanted to file complaints against the police but cannot identify them because their faces were covered. 'Sana tulungan kami ni Yorme para makalabas ang mga bata. PWD ang anak ko at may pangarap pa rin sila,' she said. (I hope Manila Mayor Francisco 'Isko Moreno' Domagoso helps us get the youths released. My son is a PWD and the youths also have dreams) Karingal said she has been visiting her son in detention and delivering his prescribed medicine. Rights group Karapatan called on the Commission on Human Rights to investigate what it described as police brutality. 'There are photos, videos and eyewitness accounts proving police used teargas and live ammunition,' said Atty. Maria Sol Taule of Karapatan in a statement. 'Reports even say two were killed during the dispersal,' she adds. The CHR on Monday said it had sent teams to monitor the rallies and was collating documentation and testimonies. It also said it would continue monitoring the treatment of those detained. It said that while protests have a 'rightful place' in a democracy, these should not endanger others or compromise public safety. It also said state regulation of protests and assemblies 'must always comply with human rights standards, guided by the principles of necessity, proportionality and accountability.' CHR said it will also conduct impartial inquiries into potential violations of police procedure and 'determine whether grave human rights violations were committed.' Lawyers from the National Union of People's Lawyers also reported detainees, including minors and PWDs, bore bruises, head injuries and signs of torture. The MPD, however, denied the allegations. 'Unang-una, ang magsasabi niyan kung may illegal arrest ay ’yung piskalya. Na-inquest sila, ibig sabihin legal ang pag-aresto,' said PMaj. Philipp Ines, MPD spokesperson. (In the first place, the prosecutor would be the one to determine if arrest was illegal. Those detained went through inquest proceedings, so that means the arrests were legal) He added the minors were not detained in cells but were placed in the custody of the Women and Children Protection Section. He said they were provided a medical team, lawyers, access to a human rights observer as well as 'food and care'. Ines said the MPD is continuing its probe, including a review of social media and of CCTV footages, into those behind the September 21 disturbance. The MPD earlier reported that 244 individuals were arrested during the protest, all processed through inquest. Salinlahi Alliance for Children’s Concerns said many of those apprehended were only bystanders and should not have been detained. MANILA — Children’s rights advocates and the families of minors detained after the September 21 rallies staged a protest Thursday, September 25, in front of the Manila Police District.

title: More than 8,200 vaccinated so far vs COVID-19 in San Juan, says Mayor Zamora
body: The city government has administered the anti-coronavirus jabs to 4,170 medical frontliners, 2,443 persons with comorbidities and 1,609 senior citizens, Mayor Francis Zamora told ANC. 'Every single day, we vaccinate around 600 to 800 San Juaneños. Definitely, we can increase these numbers as supply increase as well,' Zamora said. San Juan on Monday began inoculating older people with the CoronaVac vaccine developed by China's Sinovac Biotech. The city aims to vaccinate more than 85,000 of its residents or two-thirds of its population to achieve herd immunity. In the interview, Zamora said San Juan had 1,125 coronavirus active cases. 'If we hadn’t gone to ECQ (enhanced community quarantine), I would assume that by now, we would be around the 2,000-level given the daily growth we are experiencing before,' he said. To decongest its hospitals from COVID-19 admissions, the city is set to open container vans as quarantine facilities, Zamora said. It will have 26 rooms with air conditioning and toilet. The city mayor said they were also collaborating with the Philippine Red Cross to open 2 community-based isolation facilities, which could cater around 200 patients. 'I'm optimistic that by end of April, all of these will be fully functional,' Zamora said. San Juan City has so far vaccinated more than 8,200 people against COVID-19 since it began its inoculation program on March 6, its mayor said Tuesday. MANILA - San Juan City has so far vaccinated more than 8,200 people against COVID-19 since it began its inoculation program on March 6, its mayor said Tuesday.

title: Special election set on December 9 to replace Teves
body: This, after Commission on Elections (Comelec) Chairman George Garcia received the Certificate of Permanent Vacancy and Resolution Calling for a Special Election from the House of Representatives. In the meantime, the House of Representatives designated Speaker Martin Romualdez as the legislative caretaker of Teves' former district. In a press statement, the poll body said aspirants may file their Certificate of Candidacy (COC) from November 6 to 8. The Comelec en banc also approved the dates of other related activities. The election period will start from November 9 to December 24, which coincides with the implementation of a gun ban. Campaign period is from November 9 to December 7. A liquor ban will be in effect from December 8 to 9. Included in prohibited acts from November 9 to the eve of election day are: the release, disbursement, or expenditure of public funds; the construction of public works, delivery of materials for public works, etc.; and the appointment or hiring of new employees, creation of new positions, filling of new positions, promotion or giving of salary increases, remuneration or privileges. t The House of Representatives expelled Teves earlier this month in a historic 265-0-3 vote, citing his political asylum bid in Timor Leste, continued absences, and indecent behavior on social media when he danced in his undergarments in a social media post. Teves had over 22 months left before he was expelled on his 3rd and final term as lawmaker. Teves is accused of masterminding the assassination of his political nemesis former governor Roel Degamo. His expulsion was the climax of a long disciplinary process which began when he failed to physically report for work at the House after his travel clearance expired even if he was ordered to do so by the House leadership. The lower chamber previously sanctioned Teves with 2 60-day suspensions after he failed to report to work last March, as his travel authority expired that month. Romualdez was also the caretaker of the third district of Negros Oriental during the two times that Teves was suspended from the House before his eventual expulsion. MANILA (UPDATED) -- A special election will be held on December 9 in the Third Legislative District of Negros Oriental in order to fill the vacancy caused by the expulsion of former Rep. Arnolfo 'Arnie' Teves Jr. the appointment or hiring of new employees, creation of new positions, filling of new positions, promotion or giving of salary increases, remuneration or privileges. t

title: 'Pure and simple kidnapping,' Medialdea tells ICC on Duterte's arrest
body: Duterte - who arrived in the Netherlands on a flight from Manila on Wednesday after being arrested on an ICC warrant on charges of crimes against humanity - sounded frail as he spoke via video link from a detention unit, confirming his name and date of birth for the court. Defense attorney and former Palace executive secretary Salvador Medialdea said the former leader was too ill to say more, that Duterte's arrest and transfer to the Netherlands was 'pure and simple kidnapping' and that his client was suffering from 'debilitating' medical issues. Presiding Judge Iulia Antoanella Motoc responded that the court doctor, who examined Duterte upon arrival, was of the opinion that he was 'fully mentally aware and fit.' Prosecutors have accused the 79-year-old of carrying out a systematic attack on the civilian population during his time in office. Thousands of purported drug dealers and users were killed during a crackdown, when death squads he allegedly created and armed carried out widespread extrajudicial killings. Duterte arrived at Rotterdam airport on a chartered plane Wednesday and was transferred to a detention unit on the Dutch coast up the road from the ICC building. In a video message on social media, he took responsibility for his actions. During the initial appearance, the judge summarized the allegations against Duterte, who was not asked to enter a plea. Duterte, who led the Philippines from 2016 to 2022, is set to be the first Asian former head of state to go on trial at the ICC, a court of last resort created more than two decades ago to prosecute individuals for war crimes, crimes against humanity, aggression and genocide. Duterte has said that he suffers from a series of ailments including a chronic neuromuscular disorder, back problems, migraines and a condition that can cause blockages in the blood vessels. Duterte's surrender to the court marks a big victory for Chief Prosecutor Karim Khan, who faces U.S. sanctions over his arrest warrant for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu. Former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte was 'abducted' before being taken to The Hague to face murder allegations and is too ill to give evidence, his lawyer told the International Criminal Court at his initial appearance on Friday. Former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte was 'abducted' before being taken to The Hague to face murder allegations and is too ill to give evidence, his lawyer told the International Criminal Court at his initial appearance on Friday.

title: DOH rejects two-tier tax system for cigarettes
body: Under the proposed measure, a pack of cigarettes worth below P11.50 will be taxed P32 while those above P11.50 will be taxed P36. Health Secretary Paulyn Ubial said the two-tier system will only make smokers shift from expensive brands to cheaper brands, and will not lessen the number of smokers in the country. She said that when the sin tax law was signed in 2012, the DOH expected it to be implemented without changes for the next 10 years just like in other countries. 'Changing the mode of taxation like in the middle of the game, 5 years from its implementation, we don’t know what to expect, how this will actually influence the smoking prevalence or smoking rates in certain population groups that we believe are at high risk,' she said on ANC’s 'Early Edition.' Ubial said that under the current system, the DOH will be able to collect the expected revenues and fund its programs. The sin tax law has been credited by Ubial for the reducing the number of smokers in the country by 1 million as seen in a Global Adult Tobacco Survey. Ubial also suggests increasing the tax rates for cigarettes since prices of cigarettes in the Philippines ($1-$2) are still way cheaper than in other countries such as Hong Kong ($7-$8) and Australia ($19). 'We hope to eventually increase that so that 75 percent of the cost of cigarette pack is tax collected,' she said. 'Making it (cigarettes) less affordable will force the lower socioeconomic classes and the young to stop smoking,' she said. The Department of Health (DOH) rejects the proposed two-tier excise structure for cigarettes, saying this will result in lower revenues and hamper efforts to reduce the number of smokers. MANILA- The Department of Health (DOH) rejects the proposed two-tier excise structure for cigarettes, saying this will result in lower revenues and hamper efforts to reduce the number of smokers.

title: Frenchman given conditional release after years on Indonesia death row
body: Serge Atlaoui, a 61-year-old welder from Metz, was flown back to France in February after being on death row in Indonesia since 2007. The father of four, currently incarcerated near Paris, had his sentence adapted by the French courts to 30 years imprisonment. Atlaoui has been approved for conditional release on July 18, the prosecutor's office in Meaux said in a statement, adding that it is subject to follow-up obligations. 'It has been a very long battle, there was no question of me giving up at any moment. This is a very great moment for me today, and it will be for him as soon as he is released,' his lawyer Richard Sedillot told AFP. Atlaoui was arrested in 2005 at a factory in a Jakarta suburb where dozens of kilos (pounds) of drugs were discovered and accused of being a 'chemist' by the authorities. He has always denied being a drug trafficker, saying that he was installing machinery in what he thought was an acrylic factory. Initially sentenced to life in prison, his sentence was reviewed by the Indonesia's supreme court and changed to death on appeal. He was due to be executed alongside eight others in 2015, but was granted a reprieve after Paris applied pressure and the Indonesian authorities allowed an outstanding appeal to proceed. Indonesia, which has some of the world's toughest drug laws, has recently released several high-profile detainees, including a Filipina mother on death row and the last five members of the so-called 'Bali Nine' drug ring. The 61-year-old was flown back to France in February after being on death row in Indonesia since 2007. O n July 18, the government approved his conditional release. PARIS, France — A Frenchman who spent almost two decades on death row in Indonesia over drug offenses before being returned to France, has been granted a conditional release, prosecutors said Tuesday.

title: Sandiganbayan denies Bong's plea to attend Kuya Germs' wake
body: The anti-graft court sided with the argument of the Office of the Special Prosecutor that the wake is not considered an 'extraordinary circumstance' that would merit a furlough for Revilla. Both Revilla and detained Senator Jinggoy Estrada filed motions before the Sandiganbayan, requesting for a pass to pay their final respects to the late showbiz icon. Revilla asked for a five-hour leave from his detention cell, while Estrada sought for a three-hour pass. The two senators, who had also been active in the entertainment industry, expressed closeness to Moreno in their motions. Revilla and Estrada are currently detained for plunder after allegedly pocketing over P100-million worth of kickbacks from their pork barrel. The Sandiganbayan Fifth Division has yet to rule on the request of Estrada. The Office of the Special Prosecutor vigorously opposed Revilla's motion during a hearing at the Sandiganbayan First Division on Tuesday, saying it did not see Revilla's immediate need to attend Moreno's wake. Being detained for plunder, the prosecutors reiterated that a person under a non-bailable offense may only be allowed for furlough under extraordinary or exceptional events. The state lawyers also argued that Revilla's appearance on Moreno's funeral services, which is open to the public, may post problem to the Philippine National Police (PNP) who is in-charge of his security. But Revilla's lawyer, Atty. Keith Pio Quinto, reasoned that the police would have no problem in his safety because the necrological mass will be held in a confined space. Quinto added that Revilla's detention was only to ensure his attendance to the hearings, and the senator has religiously abided by the conditions set by the court in his previous approved furloughs. The defense lawyer also stated that the senator wants to pay final respects to Moreno, who acted as his second father. The prosecution, meanwhile, also argued that Estrada may find an opportunity to escape during the furlough. 'No-less important, safety and security considerations dictate that accused Estrada's request should be denied. Accordingly, to grant accused Estrada his sought-for-furlough will make it possible for him to hold the people therein, including his PNP escorts, hostage to effect escape, considering the power and influence he wields,' the prosecution stated in their opposition. The opposition added that Estrada's movement should be restricted since he is currently detained for plunder, a non-bailable offense. It added that Estrada should not be given a special treatment. 'Further, to grant his request would unduly create an impression to the public that accused Estrada, being a high-ranking public official, is favored detainee over and above other similarly situated detainees,' it added. MANILA - The Sandiganbayan First Division denied the request of detained Senator Ramon 'Bong' Revilla Jr. to visit the wake of German 'Kuya Germs' Moreno.

title: Magalong: Albayalde lawsuit threat a 'smokescreen' to cover deception
body: Magalong had claimed that Albayalde, then Pampanga police chief, sought to defer the dismissal order against his former subordinates when they allegedly made off with some P648 million worth of shabu in 2013. Albayalde said he was innocent and that he was studying possible legal actions against his accusers. 'It’s deception to portray we are the ones lying and not him. If I were in his shoes, it's the best course of action I will have to take,' he told ANC's Early Edition. 'At the end of the day, it's all about the truth. When everything is laid out and spelled out, the truth will come out that we are the ones who are actually telling the truth.' Albayalde was head of the Pampanga police when 13 of its officers were accused of pilfering some 160 kilos of shabu from a 2013 operation against a suspected Chinese drug lord. Magalong, in a recent Senate hearing, claimed Albayalde sought to defer the dismissal order against his former subordinates. Another retired police official, Rudy Lacadin, later claimed that Albayalde called him up to inquire about the case of the so-called 'ninja cops' and allegedly admitted that he got some of the drug loot. Albayalde on Monday said he was going on non-duty status starting Monday. He said last week that he was leaving the PNP top post on Oct. 29. He will retire on Nov. 8, when he turns 56. Magalong said the PNP chief had 'no choice' but to step down as fellow police officers were already 'avoiding' him amid the 'ninja cops' issue. 'It only shows he does not have the moral high ground. His only choice was to leave,' he said. He urged the Pampanga 'ninja cops,' or policemen who recycle illegal drugs, to cooperate with authorities. 'I hope that those involved in the 2013 drug bust in Mexico, Pampanga will finally realize they’re on their own and I hope some of them will start talking. Unless they end up dead like the other civilian agents who were also involved in that drug operation,' he said. 'If you look at the civilian agents involved, all of them are all already dead, they were killed. Anything can happen.' Former police official Benjamin Magalong on Tuesday said resigned PNP chief Oscar Albayalde's threat to file charges against him was only a 'smokescreen' to cover his 'deception.' MANILA - Former police official Benjamin Magalong on Tuesday said resigned PNP chief Oscar Albayalde's threat to file charges against him is a 'smokescreen' to cover his 'deception.'
