'Hindi po kasalanan namin 'yan na ganoon ang kinalalabasan. Syempre, noong tinignan na 'yung sa AMLC records and then sa SALN, because, you know, these are the joint SALNs of the husband and wife, and then hindi namin kasalanan 'yun na ganoon ang lumabas sa P6.7 billion, P4-point-something billion of inflows ng pera sa mga accounts nila,' the lawmaker said. 'Nakita nyo naman na naging very comprehensive din and very fair 'yung naging proceedings before the Committee on Justice at lumabas mga dokumento, lumabas mga official findings,' De Lima added. Caloocan 2nd District Representative Edgar Erice, meanwhile, spoke up during plenary session to defend his integrity on what he described to be criticisms regarding his 'stand not to participate' in the impeachment process. Erice said he is against the notion that Congress has the 'moral ascendancy' to say the process is in pursuit of the mandate for accountability. The lawmaker alleged 'hundreds of billions in infrastructure allocations' and 'massive ayuda programs' were involved in proceedings. 'I cannot in conscience agree that this Congress has the moral ascendancy to claim that the impeachment process is genuinely in pursuit of our constitutional mandate to uphold accountability. Why? Number one, the process beginning the 19th Congress is already tainted. Ito po ay pagpapatuloy lamang ng proseso na naganap noong 19th Congress. It is marked by hundreds of billions in infrastructure allocations and massive ayuda programs to cooperating legislator in the 19th Congress,' Erice said. 'Even my opponent in the last elections– the 2025 elections, publicly admitted that she signed the impeachment of VP Sara Duterte in aid of allocation. Natatakot siya na hindi siya mabibigyan ng pondo kung hindi siya pipirma sa impeachment,' Erice added. The House Committee on Justice formally adopt its report and the corresponding Articles of Impeachment against Vice President Sara Duterte during a committee hearing at the House of Representatives, Quezon City, on May 4, 2026 . Jonathan Cellona, ABS-CBN News MANILA — House members’ political parties should not affect their decision on Vice President Sara Duterte’s impeachment case, Committee on Justice Chair Gerville Luistro said Tuesday. The House Committee on Justice on Tuesday formally adopted its report and the corresponding Articles of Impeachment against Duterte. Palace defends Marcos Jr.’s pivot from ‘hands-off’ policy on Duterte’s impeachment 'Even if the presidential son is the majority leader of the 20th Congress, the individual members have actually the privilege, the prerogative to make their own stand, notwithstanding the position of the majority block,' Luistro told ANC’s Headstart. Presidential Son and Ilocos Norte 1st District Rep. Sandro Marcos is the majority floor leader of the 20th Congress. President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. did not explicitly express his stand on his former ally's impeachment case, but said it 'should be treated like the investigations into flood control anomalies' where those involved in irregularities should be held liable. 'At the end of the day, conscience vote ito eh. It is really how you appreciate this impeachment process that should be the basis on voting during the plenary,' Luistro added. Luistro also acknowledged that aside from the presented evidence and political parties, the 2028 presidential elections, in which Duterte announced her intent to run for president, may affect lawmakers’ decision on the impeachment proceedings. But she stressed that the plenary should prioritize public interest in deciding whether or not Duterte’s case should reach an impeachment trial. 'If what matters to them is the best interest of the nation, siguro dapat mawala yung fear ng consequence ng kanilang vote,' Luistro said. NUP to House panel: Present 'stronger' impeachment case vs VP Duterte Meanwhile, the justice panel chair said the plenary will have a reasonable time to scrutinize the articles of impeachment and vote on them next week, May 11. The National Unity Party (NUP), the second largest political party in the House, said 'independence must be preserved' in the impeachment vote. 'If any form of sanction or punitive measure is imposed on Members for voting according to their judgment, the Party will act collectively and respond in a manner it deems appropriate,' NUP Chairman and Antipolo First District Rep. Ronaldo Puno said. At the same time, the party 'reaffirmed its continued support for the administration of President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr., regardless of the outcome of the impending vote on the impeachment matter before the House of Representatives.' In the same statement, the NUP said it is the 'In matters of this nature, independence must be preserved… If any form of sanction or punitive measure is imposed on Members for voting according to their judgment, the Party will act collectively and respond in a manner it deems appropriate,' Puno said. Five Manila representatives are now part of the NUP after Third District Rep. Joel Chua, Fifth District Rep. Irwin Tieng, and First District Rep. Ernesto Dionisio joined. Manila Sixth District Rep. Bienvenido Abante Jr. and Manila Second District Rep. Rolando Valeriano, who were already members beforehand, invited the three to join. '‘Yung Manila bloc halos lahat kami nag-NUP dahil matagal na naming talagang plano ‘yan na mag-solid kami sa Manila. Before kasi supposedly, mag-NUP kami but unfortunately wala namang slot,' Chua told reporters. He said allegations that some lawmakers were withdrawing their support for the impeachment of the Vice President were 'fake news.' 'Nakita naman natin yesterday na from dati, 53 ‘yung bumoto. Ngayon, biglang naging 55 pa without counting the chair pa, di ba? So technically that is 56. Kaya lahat ito puro pinapalabas lang, puro fake news. In fact, naglabas sila ng mga pangalan—those who are not gonna vote during the committee hearing, pero makikita mo halos karamihan doon bumoto ng yes,' Chua said. The committee on Monday approved its committee report finding probable cause in the impeachment complaints against Duterte, officially sending it to the plenary for voting. Senate President Tito Sotto earlier said the upper chamber is ready regardless of what happens to the impeachment complaint. He also said the trial may continue even after the Senate goes on a break on June 5. The complaints are grounded on various accusations against Duterte, including her alleged misuse of confidential funds, unexplained wealth and a supposed assassination plot against President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. Duterte has repeatedly denied the allegations. VP Sara Duterte unfazed by looming impeachment, visits dad in The Hague House members’ political parties should not affect their decision on Vice President Sara Duterte’s impeachment case, Committee on Justice Chair Gerville Luistro said Tuesday. Vice President Sara Duterte announces her intention to run for the presidency in the 2028 national elections during a press conference at the Office of the Vice President (OVP) headquarters in Mandaluyong City on Wednesday, February 18, 2026. Maria Tan, ABS-CBN News/File MANILA — The Committee Report on the House Resolution setting forth the articles of impeachment against Vice President Sara Duterte has been referred to the House Committee on Rules, bringing it a step closer to being tackled and voted on by the House of Representatives. The House plenary made the referral of Committee Report no. 261 on House Resolution No. 989 on Tuesday night. READ: House justice panel OKs committee report 'setting forth' VP Sara impeachment House members expected to vote in favor of the committee report finding probable cause to impeach Vice President Sara Duterte are at 'almost 200', according to House Committee on Justice Chairperson and Bicol Saro Party List Representative Terry Ridon. 'As we speak, we are looking at almost 200 already House members who are signifying their intent to support the impeachment,' he said.'All of the major political parties I think will be represented in that at least 200 to date that we are expecting to support the impeachment… From our conversations with colleagues, parang ganoon na ang mga nakikita naming bilang,' Ridon added. Some lawmakers did not give specific numbers, but also believe the needed vote of at least 106 or a third of house members to elevate to senate the articles of impeachment against duterte will be exceeded. They denied allegations of pressure to vote in favor of Duterte’s impeachment. 'I'm positive that because of the evidence presented before the Justice Committee, and considering the 53-0 (committee vote on probable cause finding) and the 55-0 (committee vote on the committee report), I am positive that this will be approved by the plenary,' House Committee on Justice Chairperson Gerville Luistro said. 'I think the numbers are encouraging from what I've been able to hear… Seems like more (than 106 congressmen),' House Assistant Majority Leader and Negros Occidental 3rd District Representative Javier Miguel Benitez, for his part, said. 'I think, overwhelmingly na, sobra na 120 ‘yung nagsabi na pipirma sila… 106 lang ang kailangan eh… Bulong-bulong sa halls of Congress, alam mo na. Estimation ng bawat party,' House Committee on Justice Vice Chairperson and Palawan 2nd District Representative Jose Alvarez said. On Vice President Duterte’s statement questioning the certainty of lawmakers, Ridon replied, 'Tiyak ho kami, kasi tiyak kami sa ebidensya. Tiyak kami sa P6.7 billion total transactions na pumasok at lumabas sa accounts ng mag-asawang Duterte-Carpio. Tiyak kami sa P448 million na ipinapasoli sa kanya. Tiyak kami sa pagbabanta niya sa Pangulo.' Members of the Partido Federal ng Pilipinas met monday night and agreed the party will have a stand on Duterte’s impeachment case. The president’s political party has over 50 members at the lower chamber. 'May party stand na ang PFP, and we're basing it on what we see as evidence for probable cause. So kung may justification for that, that is what we will stand for,' Benitez said, but refused to disclose the party stand on Tuesday. The National Unity Party and the Liberal Party, meanwhile, will allow members to vote on the impeachment based on their conscience. READ: Political party should not affect House members’ stand on VP Sara impeachment—Luistro Alvarez, who voted at the committee level in support of the committee report finding probable cause to impeach the Vice President, said he will abstain when the report is tackled at the House plenary, citing 'personal reasons'. But the lawmaker asserted he is not changing is earlier vote. 'Wala naman akong pinirmahan doon sa committee report. Bumoto lang ako. Taas lang ako ng kamay kasi ‘yung proceeding lang ‘yun para dalhin sa plenary. Hindi man ‘yun confirming na guilty na si Vice President Sara,' Alvarez said. 'Kung i-require ako sa boto sa plenary, abstain ako. Kasi nagsabi na ako na hindi ako boboto,' he added. Other leaders and members of the House Committee on Justice also denied claims on social media that some congressmen are withdrawing their signature from the committee report. 'I don't know any Cong who is withdrawing his vote or signature from the determination of probable cause and even from our approval of committee report yesterday. So from my end, I deny that allegation that there are Congs who are withdrawing their signatures,' Luistro said. 'Well, after pa lang noong voting, saka pa lang nagsimulang magpapirma. In fact, ngayong umaga lang ako nakapirma dahil ngayon lang ako napuntahan noong committee secretariat. So parang fake news yata yung lumabas na ‘yun. Even before the voting, narinig ko na ‘yan eh. So mukhang fake news lang ‘yon, nang-iintriga,' House Deputy Minority Leader and ACT Teachers Party List Representative Antonio Tinio added. The Committee Report on the House Resolution setting forth the articles of impeachment against Vice President Sara Duterte has been referred to the House Committee on Rules, bringing it a step closer to being tackled and voted on by the House of Representatives. MANILA — Senate President Pro Tempore Panfilo Lacson said Wednesday the Senate could decide not to convene the impeachment court even if the articles of impeachment against Vice President Sara Duterte were transmitted by the House of Representatives. 'That's a possibility kasi nga 24-member court ito na kung ano yung sabi ng majority, yun ang ruling,' Lacson told the Kapihan sa Senado. 'Divide the House lagi. Kung matalo sa botohan…paano magko-convene?' 'And kung ganoon naman mangyari, it's on them, na sila yung sumagot sa taong bayan kung bakit ayaw mag-convene. Yung minority na talo sa botohan, wala namang magagawa yun eh,' he said. Lacson cited the potential opposition from the Senate minority, which is composed of allies of the Vice President. 'Ina-expect na namin yun eh, na even yung, ah, yung convening of the Senate as an impeachment, i-objectan yun eh. Expected na yun.' House justice panel OKs committee report 'setting forth' VP Sara impeachment The senator noted that even if the court were convened, trial would not immediately start since there were processes for preparation, the issuance of summons, as well as pre-trial. 'Baka umabot ng July, first week of July, yung trial proper.' But he also noted the trial could proceed even during the sine die adjorunment. 'Maski naka-break, pwede mag-proceed- yung trial.Kasi independent naman ang Senate. Hindi saklaw ng sine die yung impeachment court. That's a different animal.Ang sakop ng sine die yung Congress. But not the impeachment court. Nagkataon lang na yung impeachment court is the Senate,' he said. Congress adjourns sine die on June 5, 2026. Senate may hold VP Sara impeachment trial during break Lacson said the investigation of the Blue Ribbon Committee on the flood control scandal could continue alongside the impeachment trial. 'Kaya nga sabi namin, impeachment trial at saka yung session, simulan natin sa hapon para yung umaga natin libre para yung committee hearings, not only yung Blue Ribbon kundi other legislative, yung mga bills pending, magkaroon ng, ah, space para sa committee hearings para ma-report out pag may session,' he said. Lacson added that because Senate President Vicente Sotto III is not a lawyer, they might need an impeachment manager to assist him as presiding officer.n'Kasi si Senator Zubiri, Majority Leader, hindi rin lawyer. So we're planning to appoint an impeachment manager. Ah, tingin namin si Senator Kiko para lawyer,' Lacson said. 'Halimbawa may mga question of law na pwedeng interpret kasi kailangan kabisado mo yung rules of court, 'di ba? So although ang mananaig dito yung rules of impeachment. Suppletory yung rules of court. So mas mainam na yung merong lawyer doon si SP hindi rin lawyer.' 'Pero mas mainam na yung lawyer na sanay sa trial. Para more or less masisi-signal-an niya man lang si SP na yung objection would be overruled or sustained.' House plenary may vote on Sara Duterte impeachment in second week of May — lawmaker Senate President Pro Tempore Panfilo Lacson said Wednesday the Senate could decide not to convene the impeachment court even if the articles of impeachment against Vice President Sara Duterte were transmitted by the House of Representatives. Vice President Sara Duterte believes it was unfair that her family members were implicated in the impeachment proceedings against her in the House of Representatives. OVP via Harlene Delgado, ABS-CBN News MANILA — Vice President Sara Duterte believes it was unfair that her family members were implicated in the impeachment proceedings against her in the House of Representatives. Speaking to supporters on May 5 in The Hague, Netherlands following her visit to her father, former President Rodrigo Duterte, the Vice President said her family has the right to privacy as 'ordinary individuals.' 'Well, I think it’s unfair dahil hindi naman sila vice president, and hindi naman sila ini-impeach. And tulad ng mga asawa at mga anak din ng mga congressman, they have the right to privacy being ordinary individuals and private citizens,' she said. During the April 22 House Justice panel impeachment hearing, the Anti-Money Laundering Council confirmed that it had received reports of 'suspicious' and 'covered' bank transactions amounting to P6.77 billion linked to Duterte and her husband, lawyer Manases Carpio, during a period of nearly two decades. In the same hearing, former senator Antonio Trillanes IV also alleged the Duterte family including the vice president, former president Duterte, Veronica 'Kitty' Duterte, Honeylet Avanceña, Rep. Paolo Duterte, and Davao City Mayor Sebastian Duterte received millions of pesos in manager’s checks from an alleged drug lord. The second-in-command also noted that she did not inform the Duterte patriarch of her potential second impeachment to prevent him from overthinking. 'Dapat ang alam lang niya ay ‘yung mga happy na mga bagay lalo pati nag-iisip tayo na baka nga diyan siya bawian ng buhay sa loob at saka mamatay sa loob ng detention,' she said. 'So dapat ‘yung maalala niya at naiisip niya ay ‘yung mga masasayang bagay hindi ‘yung mga kalokohan, korapsyon at abuso ng mga congressman at iba pang pulitiko, kasama na ang administrasyon,' the younger Duterte stated. The Vice President also expressed gratitude to her supporters including those who have been vocal against the impeachment proceedings against her that she claimed 'fabricated' by the House of Representatives. However, she said she accepts whatever her fate is, as it is all 'written by God.' 'Pero, base din sa discussion namin kanina ni Pangulong Duterte, lahat ng mga nangyayari sa buhay ng isang tao ay sinulat ‘yan ng Diyos. So kung ma-impeach ako, ‘yan ay sinulat na ng Diyos, hindi man ako ma-impeach, see you sa – see you tomorrow,' the younger Duterte said humorously, which also drew laughs from her supporters. On Tuesday night, the House Justice Committee report on the House Resolution setting forth the articles of impeachment against the Vice President was referred to the House Committee on Rules, bringing it a step closer to being tackled and voted on the plenary whether or not it will advance to the Senate sitting as an impeachment court for trial. Vice President Sara Duterte believes it was unfair that her family members were implicated in the impeachment proceedings against her in the House of Representatives. Vice President Sara Duterte on Monday dismissed the growing momentum of impeachment efforts against her, expressing a stoic indifference toward the political moves led by her ‘detractors’ in the House of Representatives. In an interview during her visit to the Netherlands, the Vice President addressed recent claims by Bicol Saro Party-list Representative Terry Ridon that the 106 votes required to endorse articles of impeachment to the Senate are already within reach. 'Well, hindi ako maka-comment sa kanyang mga nalalaman kasi hindi ko alam kung ano yung alam niya,' she said, referring to Ridon. Duterte maintained that while she respects the opinions of ordinary citizens, she views the involvement of elected officials differently. 'It is different siguro kapag congressman na yung nagsasabi or senator na yung nagsasabi. Kasi magtatanong ka, bakit sobrang sigurado ka na...' she added. The Vice President’s remarks come as the House Committee on Justice continues its formal proceedings. Notably, Duterte has been absent from the committee hearings since the first day of the inquiry, opting instead for official travel and personal visits abroad. Despite the 'persecution' she claims to face from critics, the Vice President insisted her disposition remains unchanged. 'I've always been like this. Even before I was Vice President, I don't see na magbabago 'yung disposition ko because of a few problems here and there,' she said. Duterte is currently in the Netherlands to visit her father, former President Rodrigo Duterte, who is facing a crimes against humanity case before the International Criminal Court (ICC). Palace confirms Sara Duterte’s 14-day rescheduled overseas trip While she admitted she wasn't 'happy' to be back given the circumstances of the elder Duterte, she shared that he appeared 'well and healthy.' Regarding the ICC’s recent decision to establish jurisdiction over her father's case, the Vice President said the move was expected. 'I accepted it as it is. Discussion na rin naman namin yun ng mga abogado. We don't see any way that the ICC will let go of the case of President Duterte since isa na lang ito sa dalawa na ICC cases,' she said. She said they are preparing for the possibility of a full trial, stating, 'This will really go to trial if the best way to move forward is to prepare for time.' Amid the looming legal and political battles, Duterte confirmed her long-term political plans, identifying herself as a 'presumptive candidate' for the 2028 presidential race. 'I've already declared my intention to run for the next presidential elections,' she said, adding a prayer for an 'honest, orderly, and peaceful' vote. When asked about her source of resilience, the Vice President credited her supporters rather than political allies. 'Yung lakas ko lang talaga galing talaga sa mga tao. Sa totoo lang, iniwan na siguro ako lahat ng mga kaibigan ko na politiko pero hindi ako iniwan ng mga tao,' she said. In a lighter moment, she shared a message from her father to their supporters who vowed never to give up, 'Pinaabot ko din sa kanya, sabi ko, nagsabi sila na hindi sila susuko at sabi niya na sumuko na daw kayo.' She quickly clarified that she took the former president's remark as a joke. Members of the House of Representatives were given copies on Wednesday of the committee report containing the House resolution setting forth the articles of impeachment against Vice President Sara Duterte. MANILA -- Members of the House of Representatives were given copies on Wednesday of the committee report containing the House resolution setting forth the articles of impeachment against Vice President Sara Duterte, according to Manila 1st District Representative Ernesto Dionisio Jr. Lacson added that because Senate President Vicente Sotto III is not a lawyer, they might need an impeachment manager to assist him as presiding officer.n'Kasi si Senator Zubiri, Majority Leader, hindi rin lawyer. So we're planning to appoint an impeachment manager. Ah, tingin namin si Senator Kiko para lawyer,' Lacson said.

title: Batangas Rep. Leviste fails to secure a copy of ICI final report
body: Leviste arrived at the ICI in the morning but was asked to come back in the afternoon to meet ICI chairman Andres Reyes Jr. 'The reason I’m here, is I was hoping that today being the last day of the ICI, they will release the report on the findings of their investigation, the final report. And at least make any press briefing or statement summarizing and concluding what came out of the resources that were spent for the ICI investigation,' Leviste told reporters. During the ambush interview, Leviste said he had a meeting with Reyes for more than an hour who repeatedly asked him not to quote him or the ICI to the media. 'Ang problema ng ICI, wala silang contempt powers at wala ding immunity ang ICI para sa kanilang paglalabas ng mga findings including allegations of corruption. At dahil dito, hindi nila nakuha ang mga sapat na ebidensiya para maitukoy (matukoy) conclusively sino ang may kasalanan at pangalawa, kung meron man silang mga findings, hindi din nila puwedeng gawing allegation dahil sa takot na baka, babalikan sila dahil wala silang immunity at ang short answer is - gusto nilang ipaubaya na lang sa gobyerno, dahil ang gobyerno ang may contempt powers at may immunity para isapubliko ang report at iba pang mga conclusions sa mga anomalous flood control projects,' Leviste said. According to Leviste, the ICI said the copy of its final report was already submitted to the Office of the President. 'Ang final report ng ICI at ibinigay kay President BBM at na kay President BBM na lang ang pagsasapubliko ng report na ito. Pati daw ang mga miyembro ng ICI ay walang kopya ng final report. Nakay president BBM lang ang final report ng ICI,' said Leviste. Leviste hopes that the transcripts of records during the hearing of the commission will be made available to the public. 'What will be more interesting for the public than the report is the transcript of the ICI questions in sessions with all members because we don’t know what was discussed in any of these and I hope that this transcripts are safely kept somewhere and that eventually they will be released,' said Leviste. 'Ang aking panawagan ay isapubliko hindi lang ng gobyerno ang final report ng ICI, kung hindi…isapubliko ng ICI ang transcript ng lahat ng mga sessions nila,' he added. Earlier, ICI chair retired Supreme Court Justice Andres Reyes Jr said that they have turned over to the Ombudsman all documents and evidence gathered in the course of its investigation. Leviste described employees of the ICI as demoralized, since starting Wednesday, April 1, they will be jobless. 'Hindi na-accomplish ng ICI ang kanilang mandato at hindi ito kasalanan ng ICI. Mula sa simula pa lang kasi, hindi sila binigyan ng contempt or immunity,' Leviste told reporters. 'Actually kung titingnan lang natin yung entrance ng ICI dito, medyo malungkot kasi nakasara ang mga ilaw, ang mga staff actually - mga 40 yung contract at until earlier this year, inaasahan pa kasi na ipapasa ng kongreso yung ICI bill para tuloy-tuloy ang mga imbestigasyon ng ICI,' he added. 'Pero dahil tigil na ang ICI, yung mahigit kumulang 40 na mga contractual hires ng ICI ay mawawalan na rin ng trabaho by tomorrow. 'I think ang buong ICI ngayon, base sa nakita natin dito na parang sarado ang opisina, patay ang ilaw, ayaw nilang humarap sa media - masasabi natin ang ICI ngayon ay demoralized dahil kaya sila pumasok dito, gusto nila may kalabasan sa mga imbestigasyon, pero wala namang nagsasabi to the contrary. Hindi na-accomplish ng ICI ang kanilang mission kaya demoralized ang ICI ngayong araw,' Leviste explained. Leviste believes there has been no closure into the anomalous flood control projects investigation of the commission. Also on Tuesday, former commissioners of the ICI, Rogelio 'Babes' Singson, and Rosanna Fajardo were seen entering the ICI building. Several personnel of the Bureau of Customs also inspected and started the engines of luxury vehicles parked at the ICI compound. Those vehicles are being linked to former Cong. Zaldy Co. On the last day of operation of the Independent Commission for Infrastructure (ICI) on Tuesday, March 31, Batangas 1st District Rep. Leandro Leviste tried to secure a copy of the final report of the commission tasked to investigate anomalous flood control projects. MANILA -- On the last day of operation of the Independent Commission for Infrastructure (ICI) on Tuesday, March 31, Batangas 1st District Rep. Leandro Leviste tried to secure a copy of the final report of the commission tasked to investigate anomalous flood control projects.

title: Pope Leo XIV leads Chrism Mass on Maundy Thursday
body: In his homily, the Pope also encouraged bishops and priests to go where they are sent with simplicity. The Basilica was filled with priests from the Diocese of Rome. 'There is no peace without setting out, nor awareness without detachment. No joy without risk. We are the body of Christ if we move forward,' the Holy Father said. 'In life we may face situations where everything seems to be over. We then ask our ourselves whether the mission has been in vain. While it is true that unlike Jesus we also experience failures that stem from our own shortcomings or those of others, often from a tangled web of responsibilities of light and shadow, we can make the hope of many witnesses our own,' he added. The Pope also reminded members of the church that no good can come from abuse of power, and invited them to walk together. 'Let us renew our yes to this mission that calls for unity and brings peace. Yes, we are here. Let us overcome the sense of powerlessness and fear. We proclaim Your death, Oh Lord, and we proclaim Your resurrection as we await Your coming,' he said. After his homily, the priests renewed their promises before the Pope. He also blessed three sacred oils — the oil of the sick, which is used for the sacrament of the anointing of sick, oil of the Catechumens, which strengthens those preparing for baptism, and the sacred Chrism, which signifies the gift of the Holy Spirit and is used for baptism, confirmation, ordinations, and dedication of churches and altars. The oils will be distributed to the clergy who will bring them to their parishes and communities. On Holy Wednesday, the Pope held his weekly general audience in Saint Peter’s Square, which was filled with devotees. He invited the public to be the church that Pope Francis spoke about, one that is 'always open to mission.' 'Brothers and sisters, may the Easter we are preparing to celebrate renew in us the grace to be like Mary Magdalene, like Peter and John, witnesses of the risen one,' Pope Leo said. Before the gathering ended, he greeted pilgrims and visitors from various countries, including the Philippines. Pope Leo XIV led the Chrism Mass on Maundy Thursday in Saint Peter’s Basilica, calling on priests to renew their mission that calls for unity and brings peace. MANILA — Pope Leo XIV led the Chrism Mass on Maundy Thursday in Saint Peter’s Basilica, calling on priests to renew their mission that calls for unity and brings peace.

title: 2 suspects in N. Ecija mayor's slay nabbed
body: Mayor Ferdinand Bote of General Tinio town, Nueva Ecija, and his driver were leaving a government office in the capital of Cabanatuan City when motorcycle-riding gunmen approached his vehicle and shot him repeatedly last July 3. The suspects were arrested at a checkpoint in Del Gallego, Camarines Sur. They were on board a Toyota Avanza bearing a license plate that was reported lost, police said in a spot report. A caliber .45 gun was recovered from the vehicle, police said. Philippine National Police spokesperson Sr. Supt. Benigno Durana on Wednesday afternoon told ANC that the suspects are currently detained at the Bicol Police Regional Office. He added that initial investigation showed that the motive for the killing is business-related. 'It appears that the motive of the killing was related to the business dealings of the late mayor,' he said. Authorities were hunting down two more suspected gunmen, ABS-CBN sources said. Bote was the second of three city officials killed last week. Tanauan Mayor Antonio Halili of Batangas, whom President Rodrigo Duterte linked to the narcotics trade, was shot dead during a flag-raising ceremony at his city hall last July 2. Trece Martires, Cavite Vice Mayor Alexander Lubigan, meanwhile, was on board his sports utility vehicle when assailants in another SUV opened fire last Saturday. The broad daylight attack outside a hospital instantly killed the vice mayor and a bodyguard. MANILA - (2nd UPDATE) Two suspects in the killing of a mayor in Nueva Ecija province have been arrested, police said Wednesday. MANILA - (3rd UPDATE) Two suspects in the killing of a mayor in Nueva Ecija province have been arrested, police said Wednesday.

title: Fighter jet maker expects US gov't to make F-16 sale offer to PH soon
body: Back in April, the US approved the possible sale of 20 F-16 jets and related equipment to its treaty ally, the Philippines, amid rising tensions in the West Philippine Sea. But in a recent interview with Defense Secretary Gilbert Teodoro, he said he has yet to receive a formal offer. In a press briefing in Taguig City, Lockheed Martin clarified that while it will be providing the F-16 Block 70 jets, discussions are being done on a government-to-government level. 'The F-16 offering is what’s called an FMS or Foreign Military Sale… So as far as very specific timing, that’s really up to the US government when they want to make that offer. But we do believe it’s going to be in the near future,' said Aimee Burnett, Lockheed Martin’s Vice President for Integrated Fighter Group Business Development. The company touted its offerings to be the total package, as it not only includes the F-16 jets, but also industrial development and tech transfer aspects such as in-country maintenance, repair and overhaul capacity which, it said, supports the Philippines’ self-reliant defense posture. Jess Koloini, Lockheed Martin’s International Business Development Director, also said they plan to establish an innovation center, similar to what they’ve done in the United Arab Emirates. 'That innovation center was a hub for a number of workforce development programs— aerospace engineering, space fundamentals training, internship programs, robotics competitions. So we’re looking to bring that exact concept here to the Philippines,' she said. 'These programs increase jobs, they upskill workforce in countries all around the world. And really what they’re about is developing indigenous talent that stays and work in host countries that we’re investing in,' she added. These perks are meant to sweeten the deal for the Philippine government, which Ambassador Jose Manuel Romualdez said is still looking for funds to buy the F-16 jets. An analyst believes negotiations might still be going on behind the scenes. 'Ni ne-negotiate pa siguro natin sa US government kung pwede nila siguro gawing mas soft 'yung deal in terms of credit, siguro may loan dyan,' said Richard Heydarian, a senior lecturer at the University of the Philippines Asian Center. Despite the hefty price tag, Heydarian believes there should be a sense of urgency for the Philippines to improve its defensive capability as China ups the ante in the West Philippine Sea with more hostile acts against Philippine forces, and even civilian missions and local fishermen. 'As they say, weakness invites aggression so we have to deal with the gaps we have in our defensive capability,' he said. 'Let’s be honest, tayo basically ang leader ng ASEAN because no one in ASEAN dares to stand up to China. Even Vietnam has been quiet in recent years… Whether we like it or not, there’s going to be more tensions here, there could be war in Taiwan, there could be war in the West Philippine Sea, there could be more conflicts down the road so this is the least we can do,' he said. Heydarian also acknowledged how this weapons sale could be a stepping stone for the Philippines to develop its own defense industry. 'We don’t want to rely on the Americans forever. We want to be able to defend our own territory and sovereign rights on our own terms and with minimal reliance on others,' he said. Meanwhile, former US Air Force F-16 fighter pilot Monessa 'Siren' Balzhiser, the first Filipino-American to achieve the feat, also attended the event and vouched for the jet’s wide range, which she said would greatly help the Philippines’ maritime patrols. 'The range that the F-16 can do with the conformal fuel tanks that it has, it provides almost 240 kilometers of extra combat radius… That aspect in itself, with the Block 70, is key to be able to support the entire area of the Philippine sovereign area without a tanker,' she said. A U.S. Air Force F-16 Fighting Falcon flies over Afghanistan, March 17, 2020. The F-16 Fighting Falcon is a compact, multi-role fighter aircraft that delivers war- winning airpower to the U.S. Central Command area of responsibility. U.S. Air Force photo by Tech. Sgt. Matthew Lotz WASHINGTON ( UPDATED ) -- The United States said Tuesday it has approved the possible sale of $5.58 billion in F-16 fighter jets to the Philippines, as Washington backs its ally in rising tensions over China. The State Department said it was green-lighting a sale that includes 20 F-16 jets and related equipment to the Philippines, a treaty-bound ally of the United States. The sale would 'improve the security of a strategic partner that continues to be an important force for political stability, peace and economic progress in Southeast Asia,' a State Department statement said. It would also boost 'the Philippine Air Force's ability to conduct maritime domain awareness' and 'enhance its suppression of enemy air defenses,' the statement said. The news follows months of increasing confrontations between the Philippines and China in the South China Sea, which Beijing claims almost in its entirety despite an international ruling that its assertion has no merit. A State Department spokesperson said Wednesday that the deal would be final only after 'a signed Letter of Offer and Acceptance' was received from the 'purchasing partner'. Philippine defense department spokesman Arsenio Andolong told AFP he had 'not received any official notice of such a decision.' But China warned Manila against the purchase, saying the Philippines was 'threatening' regional peace. US, Philippines must stand 'shoulder to shoulder' in face of China threat: Hegseth China's bomber deployment in West PH Sea 'expected' amid Hegseth's visit – analyst 'The Philippines' defense and security cooperation with other countries should not target any third party or harm the interests of a third party. Nor should it threaten regional peace and security or exacerbate regional tensions,' foreign ministry spokesman Guo Jiakun said. Manila and Washington have deepened their defense cooperation since President Ferdinand Marcos took office in 2022 and began pushing back on Beijing's sweeping South China Sea claims. In December, the Philippines angered China when it said it planned to acquire the US mid-range Typhon missile system in a push to secure its maritime interests. Beijing warned such a purchase could spark a regional 'arms race'. The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) welcomed the possible jet sale. 'Anything po that will modernize us and propel us to greater heights with our assets, we gratefully welcome that po for the Armed Forces,' AFP spokesperson Col. Francel Margareth Padilla told Teleradyo Serbisyo on Wednesday. But Padilla deferred details of the matter to the Department of National Defense (DND). 'Matters concerning ongoing acquisitions remain under the purview of the DND,' she said in a message sent to reporters. 'Nonetheless, we remain steadfast in our mission to defend the nation while anticipating future enhancements that will strengthen our ability to safeguard our territory,' she added. Meanwhile, PAF spokesperson Colonel Ma. Consuelo Castillo said the PAF could only comment on assets that had already been turned over to them. 'Nonetheless, we continue to perform our mandate as we also look forward to future developments that will further enhance our capabilities to protect our nation and our skies,' Castillo added. President Donald Trump's administration has sought to redirect US military efforts to Asia to face a rising China, especially as tensions rise over Taiwan, and to lessen involvement in Europe despite Russia's invasion of Ukraine. On Tuesday, as Chinese ships and warplanes surrounded Taiwan in a simulated blockade, Philippines military chief General Romeo Brawner said his country would 'inevitably' be involved should the self-ruled island be invaded. 'Start planning for actions in case there is an invasion of Taiwan,' he told troops in northern Luzon island, without naming the potential invader. 'Because if something happens to Taiwan, inevitably we will be involved.' He also said that the bulk of this month's joint military exercises would be conducted in northern Luzon, the part of the Philippines nearest Taiwan. 'These are the areas where we perceive the possibility of an attack. I do not want to sound alarmist, but we have to prepare,' he added. Asked about Brawner's comments, Beijing foreign ministry spokesman Guo said resolving 'the Taiwan issue is a matter for the Chinese people.' 'We advise certain individuals in the Philippines not to play with fire or make provocations on the Taiwan issue -- those who play with fire will only get burned,' he said. On a visit to Manila last week, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth vowed to 'reestablish deterrence in the Indo-Pacific region' in light of 'threats from the Communist Chinese.' Secretary of State Marco Rubio has also reiterated US defense commitments to the Philippines, a contrast to the Trump administration's frequent talk of 'freeloading' off the United States by allies in Europe. — With a report from Bianca Dava, ABS-CBN News The United States said Tuesday it has approved a sale of $5.58 billion in F-16 fighter-jets to the Philippines, as Washington backs its ally in rising tensions over China. MANILA — Malacanang on Thursday said the possible sale of $5.58 billion worth of US F-16 fighter jets to the Philippines is meant to beef up the country’s defense and is not 'for any specific target or state.' This comes after Beijing claimed that the Philippines is 'threatening 'regional peace' with its procurement of fighter jets. 'As far as the details are concerned, I have no awareness of that. But the principle of it, the Americans would give us the material for a defensive force, a defensive stance. That’s okay because the details would be worked out still. But that is not for any specific target or state. It is for our defensive posture,' Executive Secretary Lucas Bersamin said in a Palace interview. National Security Council Assistant Director General Jonathan Malaya meanwhile said that the proposed sale is part of the commitment of the Trump administration's 'ironclad commitment to the defense of the Philippines.' 'If you will remember, (Defense) Secretary Hegseth said in a joint statement with Secretary Teodoro that the United States will deploy additional advance military capabilities to the Philippines for joint training, enhanced interoperability and defense industrial cooperation,' Malaya said in a separate Palace briefing. 'So, we see this announcement by the US Defense Cooperation Agency as the implementation, the initial implementation of that joint statement between the two defense secretaries. We should note that the F-16 is the most advanced fourth generation fighter in the world. And if this sale pushes through, it will be a significant upgrade to our current arsenal,' he added. Malacanang on Thursday said the possible sale of $5.58 billion worth of US F-16 fighter jets to the Philippines is meant to beef up the country’s defense and is not 'for any specific target or state.' MANILA — The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) said Monday it was looking into 'all options' for its multi-role fighter aircraft acquisition. AFP chief General Romeo Brawner Jr. said that the military needs multi-role fighter jets to enhance the country’s defense capabilities. 'Pinag-aaralan natin lahat ng options natin. Definitely, gusto nating bumili ng multi-role fighters kasi kailangan natin sa pagdepensa ng ating bansa, whether these are the F-16, the Gripen or whatever multi-role fighters,' Brawner said in a chance interview at Camp General Mateo Capinpin in Tanay, Rizal on Monday. Earlier this month, the United States government approved the possible sale of $5.58 billion in F-16 fighter jets to the Philippines. 'We are happy that the US Congress approved the sale of 20 F-16s to us, pero wala pang final decision on this—decision ng AFP at ng ating pangulo,' Brawner said. He said there was no final decision yet on which type of multi-role fighter aircraft the Philippines would purchase, adding that budget was a big consideration. Meanwhile, the AFP chief noted that the acquisition of 12 more FA-50 lead-in trainer fighter jets was already in the pipeline. 'Sana po ay mapadali ang pag-acquire natin ng assets na ito dahil napakahalaga,' he said. 'But as to the MRFs, we’re still in the process of determining which one we can get according to our fiscal space or how we can afford these platforms.' The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) said Monday it was looking into 'all options' for its multi-role fighter aircraft acquisition. A U.S. Air Force F-16 Fighting Falcon flies over Afghanistan, March 17, 2020. The F-16 Fighting Falcon is a compact, multi-role fighter aircraft that delivers war-winning airpower to the U.S. Central Command area of responsibility. U.S. Air Force photo by Tech. Sgt. Matthew Lotz MANILA — The Philippines’ defense department said Wednesday it has yet to receive a formal offer and proposal on the potential sale of 20 F-16 fighter jets by Washington to Manila. 'Naghihintay pa kami ng formal offer and proposal. Wala pa kaming natatanggap na formal offer and proposal hanggang sa ngayon,' Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. told reporters in Mandaluyong City. US approves $5.58-B sale of F-16 fighter jets to Philippines Earlier this month, the United States government approved the possible sale of $5.58 billion in F-16 fighter jets and related equipment to the Philippines. In its statement, the US Defense Security Cooperation Agency (DSCA) said Manila had requested to buy 16 F-16s or single-seat, single-engine fighter jets and four F-16Ds or dual-seat jets that are usually used for training purposes. Manufacturer Lockheed Martin said F-16s are the world’s most advanced fourth-generation fighters. The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) earlier said it was keeping all options available for its multi-role fighter (MRF) aircraft acquisition. AFP chief General Romeo Brawner Jr. said the military needs multi-role fighter jets to enhance the country’s defense capabilities. 'Pinag-aaralan natin lahat ng options natin. Definitely, gusto nating bumili ng multi-role fighters kasi kailangan natin sa pagdepensa ng ating bansa, whether these are the F-16, the Gripen or whatever multi-role fighters. Pinag-aaralan po natin iyan,' Brawner said in a chance interview at Camp General Mateo Capinpin in Tanay, Rizal on April 14. The Philippines’ defense department has yet to receive a formal offer and proposal regarding the potential sale of 20 F-16 fighter jets by Washington to Manila. WASHINGTON D.C. - The Philippines is still looking for funds to buy at least 20 F-16 fighter jets from the United States, Philippine Ambassador Jose Manuel Romualdez said, adding that they are also looking to buy other defense items instead. Speaking to Filipino and Japanese journalists at the Philippine Embassy in Washington D.C., Romualdez said they are now finding ways to look for money for the fighter jets worth $5.58 billion. The US State Department announced in April it has approved the possible sale of fighter jets and related equipment to the Philippines. 'It's a question of how we would be able to finance it. And we're working with the United States and Lockheed to see whether we can find a way to be able to purchase this,' Romualdez said. He added that they may focus on other equipment or items instead like land-based defense equipment. US approves $5.58-B sale of F-16 fighter jets to Philippines 'We're really looking at other Items that are, I think in the view of our national defense strategy, more important, the priority for us rather than the F-16… our priority is more on perhaps land-based type of defence capabilities,' Romualdez added. In an earlier statement, The Philippine Department of Defense has said it has yet to receive a formal offer and proposal on the potential sale of the F-16 fighter jets. The US State Department also said last month that the deal would be final only after 'a signed Letter of Offer and Acceptance' was received from the ‘purchasing partner.’ The Philippines is still looking for funds to buy at least 20 F-16 fighter jets from the United States, Philippine Ambassador Jose Manuel Romualdez said, adding that they are also looking to buy other defense items instead. American defense contractor Lockheed Martin is optimistic that the U.S. government will soon move forward with a formal offer for the proposed $5.58-billion sale of F-16 fighter jets to the Philippines.

title: ICC unlikely to recognize Senate resolution for Duterte house arrest: lawyer
body: The bid for Duterte’s interim release from ICC detention is a matter that should be tackled by prosecutors, the defense, and the judges, said rights lawyer Kristina Conti. 'Hindi para sa Senado ang maghanap ng compromise,' she told DZMM. 'Una, mayroon bang kapasidad, may awtoridad ba, kikilalanin ba itong resolusyon na ito ng ICC? Sa puntong ito, hindi — actually sa kahit anong punto hindi kikilalanin ng ICC ang ganitong resolusyon na parang nangingialam sa kaniyang mga proseso,' Conti added. Cayetano’s resolution cited 'Duterte's deteriorating health, attributed to old age and prolonged social isolation, warning that continued detention may severely impact his emotional and physical well-being,' according to a press release from his office. But Conti noted that the resolution appeared to have no proof of Duterte’s medical status. 'Mahirap naman po na isang third party pa nga. Walang kinalaman si Senator Alan Peter Cayetano sa sitwason ni Duterte ngayon, walang pruweba na nakabisita siya at puro hearsay kumbaga ang kaniyang basehan,' Conti said. 'Sa appreciation ng ICC, okay po sana kung mayroong facts to appreciate. Pero dito, wala. Mas lumalabas pa nga na ito ay political posturing ng one branch of government,' she said. Meanwhile, Malacañang acknowledged Justice Secretary Jesus Crispin Boying Remulla's opposition to calls for Duterte's interim release when asked for comment on Cayetano's resolution. In an interview with ANC's Headstart last month, Remulla said Duterte's interim release was 'not appropriate' and could cause more harm than good. 'Nagsalita na po ang ating SOJ Boying Remulla patungkol po sa interim release. At kung may ganiyan pong mga suggestions mula kay Senator Alan Cayetano, noted,' Castro said. A leader of the 19th Congress House Quad Committee and a party list solon also expressed doubts on Friday that the International Criminal Court would accommodate a Senate Resolution seeking the house arrest of former President Rodrigo Duterte, as he awaits possible trial for alleged crimes against humanity. ACT Teachers Party List Repesentative Antonio Tinio said 'the ICC will only consider interim release to a member state'. He asked Sen. Alan Peter Cayetano, who filed the resolution, if he is expecting special treatment for Duterte. 'Yet another senator playing to the gallery, telling Duterte supporters what they want to hear to rally the faithful and keep them agitated… Too bad for him now, since Duterte himself caused the Philippines to withdraw from the ICC. Or does the senator expect special treatment for Duterte from the ICC? In which case it’s not going to happen,' Tinio said in a statement. Manila 6th District Representative Bienvenido Abante Jr. noted that the ICC has yet to rule on the application for Duterte’s interim release, which was formally opposed by an ICC prosecutor. 'The resolution does not have any bearing at all dahil ‘yung resolution naman is asking the Philippine government, ‘di ba? And the request should come from the Philippine government kung ipapagamit nila ‘yung ating embassy doon sa Netherlands para ‘yung ating dating Pangulo ay doon siya mag-stay. So, I believe that Sen. Alan would have the right to file any resolution. Yet sa akin, I don't think there is any bearing on that as far as the ICC is concerned,' he said. 'It remains to be seen kung ‘yan ay aprubahan ng ating pamahalaan. As far as I'm concerned, kahit na po siguro ang ating paamahalaan ay mag-request sa Netherlands about that. I do not know if the ICC would listen, sapagkat palagay ko mukhang hindi na yata pinakinggan ‘yung petition ng kampo ni dating Pangulo na siya ay ilagay sa isang o itangkilik ng ibang bansa. So kung ‘yan ang nangyari, I don't think that the ICC would also try to accommodate him on that,' Abante added. 'I do not have any problem with that, actually. If the government will be able to justify that resolution, baka maaari. Sa akin, medyo okay na rin kung halimbawa he'll be staying at the embassy ng Pilipinas for health consideration…I think the Philippine Embassy also has a perhaps a mandate to help a Filipino abroad, on that consideration. Palagay ko naman, titingnan din maigi ng ating embahada, ang health condition ng ating dating Pangulo, because that would be their concern,' he noted. Duterte was arrested in Manila on March 11, flown to the Netherlands that same night and has been held at the ICC's detention unit at Scheveningen Prison since. The International Criminal Court (ICC) is unlikely to recognize Sen. Alan Peter Cayetano’s resolution seeking the house arrest of former President Rodrigo Duterte, a lawyer representing victims of alleged killings in his drug war said. MANILA (UPDATED) — The International Criminal Court (ICC) is unlikely to recognize Sen. Alan Peter Cayetano’s resolution seeking the house arrest of former President Rodrigo Duterte as he awaited possible trial for alleged crimes against humanity, a lawyer representing victims of alleged killings in his drug war said Friday.

title: PNoy's Lenten message: Choose the right, just
body: In his final Lenten message as president, Aquino said he is hoping and praying that Filipinos will unite and continue to help one another in order to sustain the gains of his administration. 'Sa pag-ikot po natin sa bansa, harapan kong nakikita ang layo ng ating narating kumpara sa ating dinatnan. Napakasarap pong gunitain ang dami ng ating napagtagumpayan ng halos walang dagdag na pabigat sa bawat isang nakiambag,' Aquino said. 'Ang panalangin ko: Lalo pa nating paigtingin ang pagmamahal sa kapwa at lagi tayong pumanig sa tama at makatwiran.' The President also reminded Filipinos of the need to contemplate on God's redeeming love that led to the incarnation, passion, and death of Jesus Christ. 'Kung tutuusin, bilang bugtong na Anak ng Diyos, pwede namang iniwasan na lang niya ang lahat ng mga pasakit ngunit batid ni Hesukristo ang kanyang dakilang misyon. Bukal sa puso niyang inialay ang kanyang sarili para matupad ang kalooban ng Diyos. Ang pagmamahal na ipinamalas niya ang naging tulay sa ating kaligtasan,' Aquino said added. The President said the love and sacrifice of Christ became his government's guide in fulfilling its mandate of serving the people. 'Sa pag-ibig natin sa kapwa, alinsunod sa aral ng Panginoon, buong loob nating isinulong ang Daang Matuwid. Nilaban natin ang korupsyon at kahirapan upang maipamana ang mas maliwanag na bukas sa mga susunod na salinlahi,' Aquino said. MANILA - In time for the upcoming national and local elections in May, President Benigno Aquino III encouraged the Filipino people to choose the right and the just and to follow Christ's example of being faithful and obedient.

title: Vico Sotto supports anti-dynasty law: 'Ayaw natin 'yung concentration ng kapangyarihan sa iilan lamang'
body: 'Hindi po ito laban sa isang pamilya, hindi po ito laban sa isang indibidwal o sa isang politiko... Ang ayaw po natin dito ay yung concentration ng kapangyarihan sa kamay ng iilan lamang,' Sotto said. The Senate Committee on Electoral Reforms and People's Participation, chaired by Sen. Risa Hontiveros, on Monday held the first consultative meeting for the 20th Congress of the Senate Committee on Electoral Reforms and People’s Participation on anti-political dynasty bills. The event held in Rizal High School, Pasig City on Monday brought together students, civil society organizations, academics, and representatives from the local government and church. Panelists include Sotto and Francis Aquino Dee, co-convenor of the Anti-Dynasty Network (ADN). 'Pangalawa po ang nilalabanan at inaayawan po natin dito ay ang paggamit ng resources ng taumbayan. The use of state resources, the use of taxpayers’ money to perpetuate themselves in power. Ibig pong sabihin, ginagamit ang makinarya ng pamahalaan para sa pangangampanya nila, ginagamit ang pera ng taumbayan para hindi na sila maalis sa puwesto,' Sotto added. Sotto also comes from a political family with his uncle being Senate President Vicente 'Tito' Sotto III. According to the mayor, the Constitution guarantees equal access to opportunities for public service, and that those who are qualified to serve should be allowed to serve, no matter the surname and even if they are lacking in resources and machinery. For Dee, the ban on political dynasties should extend up to the fourth degree of consanguinity, which covers cousins, and not just the second degree, which covers spouses, descendants, and siblings. 'Ngayon at sa mga darating na taon feeling ko mas kailangan yun kasi mas tight-knit ang mga pamilya ngayon,' Dee said. Dee is a scion of the Aquino family, which has been in politics for decades. He is the grandchild of former President Corazon Aquino and former Senator Benigno 'Ninoy' Aquino Jr., and nephew of the late President Benigno 'Noynoy' Aquino III. The ADN is also of the position that political dynasties should not be allowed to hold government positions in a successive manner. 'Kung tuloy-tuloy po 'yung kapit ng isang pamilya dito sa isang distrito o sa isang office, sa pagka-mayor, sa pagka-gobernardor, parang nagiging kampante po 'yung mga politiko natin, mas hindi sila mag-e-effort para sa taumbayan dahil sigurado na hindi sila matatalo sa isang eleksiyon,' Dee explained. He also cautioned against allowing members of political dynasties from holding positions simultaneously. ''Yung kongresista ipagtatanggol niya 'yung kapatid niya o 'yung kamag-anak niyang mayor kapag kinu-kuwestiyon 'yung budget nito sa Kongreso,' Dee said. 'Yung malalang korupsiyon na nararanasan natin ngayon sa flood control, malinaw 'yung koneksyon nito dito sa issue ng nepo babies sa pagkakaroon ng mga kamag-anak na sila 'yung dapat nagtse-check sa isa’t isa pero pinapabayaan 'yung isa’t isa dahil magkakaanak nga sila,' he added. Sangguninang Kabataan Federation President Jonas Abadilla, meanwhile, cited the Sangguniang Kabataan Reform Act of 2015, which has a provision banning political dynasties up to the second civil degree of consanguinity or affinity. 'Napatunayan na po na kaya po natin sa Pilipinas na magpasa po ng ganitong klaseng batas at anti-political dynasty. Kaya po sa Sangguniang Kabataan, ang stance po ng ating mga kabataan ay talaga namang mag-oppose po tayo sa political dynasty,' Abadila stated. Senator Risa Hontiveros, chairman of the committee, said a true anti-political dynasty bill must be passed. 'Ang kailangan po natin ay isang batas kontra-dinastiya na praktikal at may pagkakasundo — hindi lang sa aming mga mambabatas, pati sa ating mga mamamayan. Isang panukalang of course, sumusunod sa Konstitusyon, dinig ang mga concern ng iba’t ibang sektor, inuuna ang kapakanan ng botante, at kayang aktwal na ipatupad sa totoong sitwasyon ng mga komunidad,' Hontiveros said. The committee will hold more consultative meetings in Laguna, Bacolod, Bulacan and Cagayan De Oro in the coming days. Pasig City Mayor Vico Sotto on Monday expressed support for the passage of an anti-political dynasty law, saying power should not be concentrated in the hands of a few individuals. MANILA (UPDATE) — Pasig City Mayor Vico Sotto on Monday expressed support for the passage of an anti-political dynasty law, saying power should not be concentrated in the hands of a few individuals.

title: Typhoon Egay's agri damage soars to near P2 billion
body: The NDRRMC said that at least 63 local government units have also declared a state of calamity. NDRRMC spokesperson Edgar Posadas said the damage was reported by regions that were badly-hit by the super typhoon. He added that at least 63 local government units have also declared a state of calamity. 'This is a mechanism that will more or less stabilize prices and important part of this is mobilizing the local DRRM fund for them to address the immediate need of their constituents,' he said. Over 137,000 hectares of crop areas are still seen to have chances of recovery. More than 10,000 hectares have been totally damaged. Because of the continuous flow of reports from the regions, Posadas said that figures are still expected to rise. Damage to infrastructure has also been seen to reach over P3.5 billion. While floods in some areas have receded, some regions particularly in Central Luzon continue to experience flooding. Egay has so far affected almost 2.4 million Filipinos across the country. Of the 23 deaths that were reported to the NDRRMC, 2 have so far been validated. MANILA – Agricultural damage caused by supertyphoon Egay has reached almost P2 billion, according to the latest report from the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC) on Monday.

title: Duterte's ex-spokesman insists ICC has no jurisdiction over Philippines
body: Duterte maintained that the ICC has no jurisdiction over the Philippines after the tribunal authorized the reopening of an inquiry into the drug war. According to lawyer Harry Roque, Duterte is ready to face complaints against him, should there be any, but only before Philippine courts. 'President Duterte has made it clear, 'Do what you want.' You will never have jurisdiction over his person and of course, we have always maintained that we will uphold Philippine sovereignty, that he will only face criminal prosecution before a domestic court,' he told ANC's 'Headstart'. Duterte pulled the Philippines out of the Hague-based tribunal in 2019, a year after the ICC began a preliminary probe into the crackdown. The ICC launched a formal inquiry in September 2021, only to suspend it 2 months later after Manila said it was re-examining several hundred cases of drug operations that led to deaths at the hands of police, hitmen and vigilantes. Officially, 6,181 people were killed in Duterte's 'war on drugs' but rights group say that up to 30,000 may have been killed, some innocent victims, and that corruption was rife among security forces that acted with impunity. ICC prosecutor Karim Khan asked to restart the inquiry in June 2022, saying the Philippine government under Duterte's successor, President Ferdinand Marcos Jr., had not provided enough evidence it was carrying out thorough inquiries. The ICC said that while the Philippines' withdrawal from the Statute took effect on March 17, 2019, the court 'retains jurisdiction with respect to alleged crimes that occurred on the territory of the Philippines while it was a State Party, from 1 November 2011 up to and including 16 March 2019. ' In a statement on Jan. 27, the ICC said its pre-trial chamber 'is not satisfied that the Philippines is undertaking relevant investigations that would warrant a deferral of the court's investigations'. 'The various domestic initiatives and proceedings, assessed collectively, do not amount to tangible, concrete and progressive investigative steps,' it added. Duterte's camp took exception to ICC's decision. 'As the President has said, we take offense because there's standing in judgement against the efficiency and effectiveness of our criminal justice system,' Roque said. 'The ICC was never intended to stand in judgement against domestic institutions. It is very clear. It is only when domestic institutions are unable and unwilling to exercise jurisdiction that the ICC should exercise jurisdiction,' he added. MANILA — Former President Rodrigo Duterte has maintained that the International Criminal Court has no jurisdiction over the Philippines after the tribunal authorized the reopening of an inquiry into the bloody war on drugs, his former spokesman said Thursday.

title: Marcos Jr orders DMW, OWWA to locate, account for Filipinos in Israel
body: Marcos, Jr., meanwhile, ordered the DMW and OWWA to locate and account for all migrant workers in Israel amid the ongoing armed conflict there. Malacañang said the directive covers overseas Filipino workers' (OFW) families in Israel. 'The government is closely coordinating with the Philippine Embassy in Tel Aviv and the Migrant Workers Office (MWO) in Israel to ensure the safety and welfare of Filipinos affected in the ongoing conflict,' Press Secretary Cheloy Garafil said. 'The DMW has opened a hotline and several Viber and WhatsApp hotline numbers that will accept calls and queries from our OFWs and the Filipino community who are in need of government assistance,' Garafil added. In a separate statement, Marcos, Jr. condemned the attacks on civilians in the reignited armed conflict between Israel forces and Hamas militants, which has left more than 200 dead so far . 'The Philippines conveys its deepest sympathies and condolences to those who have lost family members and loved ones in recent attacks,' said Marcos, Jr. 'The Philippines condemns the attacks, especially against civilian populations,' the statement added. Marcos Jr said it is the right of states to defend themselves in light of 'external aggression', which is embodied in the United Nations Charter. In a press conference over Zoom earlier Sunday, Israeli Ambassador Ilan Fluss said his country's military has responded to the attacks. 'Israel has the full right to respond and protect itself,' he also said. Gaza, where Hamas launched its attacks from, is a Palestinian enclave that has been militarized and has been under blockade by Israel and by Egypt. The 17-year-old conflict in Gaza is part of an older and larger conflict between Israel and Palestine . Migrant Workers Secretary Hans Leo Cacdac earlier said his agency is currently checking on overseas Filipino workers in Israel, most especially in areas near the Gaza Strip, a Palestinian area governed by Hamas and blockaded by Israel and Egypt. The DMW and the Overseas Workers Welfare Administration (OWWA) have formed a task force, Cacdac said, to assist Filipinos there. Israeli Ambassador Fluss, meanwhile, said the Israeli and Filipino embassies were still verifying reports that Filipinos were among those kidnapped by Hamas militants. He said at least 250 killed, 1,600 injured, and 100 Israeli nationals have reportedly been kidnapped. 'I heard about reports of Philippine nationals. We do not have, at this stage, any confirmation on Philippine nationals,' Fluss told reporters. 'We know there are Thai nationals involved but we are not aware of any Philippine nationals.' He also said that Israel would protect Filipinos working in the country as it would other members of Israeli society. Fluss said commercial flights to and from Israel would continue despite the armed conflict. International airports in Israel, including Ben Gurion airport in Tel Aviv are still operating, he said. 'Anyone who wants to fly in can fly in, there is no problem. Israeli Airlines continue with the flights as usual. As with the other airlines, at this stage, some of them have cancelled the flights,' he said. Fluss said there were already 'interruptions' in the flights. Meanwhile, the envoy said he does not expect the war in Israel to affect commodity prices as Israel is 'a relatively small country contributing to economy of technologies.' 'In my personal analysis, I don't see any global economic ramifications as to what is happening,' he said. MANILA (UPDATE) — President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr. has ordered the Department of Migrant Workers (DMW) and the Overseas Workers Welfare Administration (OWWA) to locate and account for all Filipinos in Israel, where attacks by the fundamentalist and militant Hamas have led to airstrikes on Gaza.

title: Duterte supporters hold prayer vigil at Supreme Court
body: They lit candles and prayed in front of the logo of the court as the magistrates tackle four petitions asking for the invalidation of the arrest and transfer of the former President to the International Criminal Court. 'Ibalik ho natin at hayaan natin harapin ni Presidente Duterte kung ano man ang kanilang sinampa na kaso dito sa Pilipinas,' said Ali Dizon. 'Wala naman problema eh, huwag lang natin ipagbili ang ating kapwa Pilipino na parang kidnapping ang nangyari sorry, it's my own opinion,' Dizon added. The 79-year-old Duterte faces a charge of 'the crime against humanity of murder', according to the ICC, for a crackdown that rights groups estimate killed tens of thousands of mostly poor men, often without proof they were linked to drugs. Duterte was arrested at Manila's international airport Tuesday after 'Interpol Manila received the official copy of the warrant of the arrest from the ICC', the presidential palace said. An ICC spokesman later confirmed the warrant and said an initial appearance hearing would be scheduled when Duterte was in court custody. A group of supporters of former President Rodrigo Duterte held a prayer vigil Thursday afternoon at the Supreme Court. MANILA — A group of supporters of former President Rodrigo Duterte held a prayer vigil Thursday afternoon at the Supreme Court.

title: Watchdog gives 5/10 rating to Marcos Jr.
body: The 'pasang awa' grade for Marcos, Jr., according to CENPEG Board chairman Dr. Temerio Rivera, is primarily due to the many unfinished or unaccomplished promises of Marcos, Jr. 'We will award President Marcos, Jr during his first year, a rating of 5, parang pasang awa. Mainly because we realize that there is a failure on his (President) part to address our most urgent issue,' Rivera explained. Topping Rivera's number one assessment of Marcos Jr. is the president's alleged fixation to rebrand the image of the Marcos family. 'President Marcos has been fixated, strongly fixated in rebranding the family image. Among the concrete examples of this, is his attempt, not just attempt, but he has actually done this. To revive many projects associated with his father, including failed projects such as Masagana '99, Kadiwa, and now, there is even an attempt to rebrand the old image, it is now referred to as 'Bagong Pilipinas,'' Rivera explained. With support given by the Duterte family in the last elections, Marcos Jr. is now in a 'very conflicted response' when it comes to addressing former president Rodrigo Duterte's human rights issue, he said. Rivera said pressing issues like agricultural woes are not being fully addressed by the President. The immediate passage and signing into law of the Maharlika Investment Fund also comes as a questionable to many sectors, he said. 'The record of Bongbong Marcos himself does not provide any confidence about their ability to address corruption issues,' Rivera said. Former University of the Philippines Vice President Maragtas Amante said Marcos Jr.'s effort in uplifting the country's economy remains pale. While taking note of the Marcos, Jr., administration's success in reducing the inflation rate, Amante pointed out the many other things that spell the country's economy, such as the Gross Domestic Product (GDP), Gross National Product (GNP), budget deficit, unemployment and underemployment rates and debt. 'May budget tayo, laging kapos yun eh... pero kakapos, at how much? Ang sabi nya (Marcos, Jr) 3 percent na lang daw. Pero may hindi siya sinabi dyan ha? Foreign debt. Meron tayong malaking problema sa debt ngayon, kailangan bigyang-pansin,' Amante stressed. Even the President's foreign policy is not well-defined, according to CENPEG Director for Policy Studies Professor Bobby Tuazon. The President's seeming leaning toward the United States, according to Tuazon, is not a 'pivot,' but a 'reaffirmation' of the Philippines' ties with the US. Tuazon warned claimed that this would only open the Philippines to a more serious conflict with China. 'The making of Philippine foreign policy, including under Marcos Jr., is inflexible. It is incapable (of) adjusting to new realities not only in the region but also throughout the world,' Tuazon said. Meantime, CENPEG Senior Fellow Hector Barrios underlined the Marcos Jr. administration's need to strengthen its campaign in addressing climate change, which he said remains weak despite an increased P453.1 billion budget for this year. 'No progress report showing by how much our risk and vulnerability has been reduced. Or by how much our resilience, well-being and capacity to anticipate and response to change has been hatched by spending so much or doing so much, Barrios said. But if there is one thing that must lauded, it's the President signing into law of the Farmers' Condonation Program which notably, came late, and needed to take Marcos Jr. to lead in order to be realized, Rivera said. 'At least he (President) has maintained the growth, number 2. Inflation, he stabilized. Jobs are being created,' Amante on the other hand, stated. MANILA - President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr., deserves a '5 out of 10' grade for his first year in office performance, according to Center for People Empowerment in Governance (CENPEG) officials.

title: Israel confirms ban on 37 NGOs in Gaza
body: The international NGOs, which had been ordered to disclose detailed information on their Palestinian staff, will now be required to cease operations by March 1. The United Nations has warned that this will exacerbate the humanitarian crisis in the war-ravaged Palestinian territory. 'Organizations that have failed to meet required security and transparency standards will have their licenses suspended,' Israel's Ministry of Diaspora Affairs and Combating Antisemitism said in a statement. Several NGOS have said the requirements contravene international humanitarian law or endanger their independence. Israel says the new regulation aims to prevent bodies it accuses of supporting terrorism from operating in the Palestinian territories. Prominent humanitarian organizations hit by the ban include Doctors Without Borders (MSF), the Norwegian Refugee Council (NRC), World Vision International and Oxfam, according to a ministry list. In MSF's case, Israel accused it of having two employees who were members of Palestinian militant groups Islamic Jihad and Hamas. MSF said this week the request to share a list of its staff 'may be in violation of Israel's obligations under international humanitarian law' and said it 'would never knowingly employ people engaging in military activity'. NRC spokesperson Shaina Low told AFP its local staff are 'exhausted' and international staff 'bring them an extra layer of help and security. Their presence is a protection.' Submitting the names of local staff is 'not negotiable', she said. 'We offered alternatives, they refused,' she said, of the Israeli regulators. The ministry said Thursday: 'The primary failure identified was the refusal to provide complete and verifiable information regarding their employees, a critical requirement designed to prevent the infiltration of terrorist operatives into humanitarian structures.' In March, Israel gave NGOs 10 months to comply with the new rules, which demand the 'full disclosure of personnel, funding sources, and operational structures'. The 37 NGOs 'were formally notified that their licenses would be revoked as of January 1, 2026, and that they must complete the cessation of their activities by March 1, 2026,' the ministry said Thursday. A ministry spokesperson told AFP that following the revocation of their licences, aid groups could no longer bring assistance into Gaza from Thursday. However, they could have their licenses reinstated if they submitted the required documents before March 1. Minister of Diaspora Affairs and Combating Antisemitism Amichai Chikli said 'the message is clear: humanitarian assistance is welcome -- the exploitation of humanitarian frameworks for terrorism is not'. On Thursday, 18 Israel-based left-wing NGOs denounced the decision to ban their international peers, saying 'the new registration framework violates core humanitarian principles of independence and neutrality'. 'This weaponization of bureaucracy institutionalizes barriers to aid and forces vital organizations to suspend operations,' they said. UN Palestinian refugee agency chief Philippe Lazzarini had said the move sets a 'dangerous precedent'. 'Failing to push back against attempts to control the work of aid organizations will further undermine the basic humanitarian principles of neutrality, independence, impartiality and humanity underpinning aid work across the world,' he said on X. On Tuesday, the foreign ministers of 10 countries, including France and Britain, urged Israel to 'guarantee access' to aid in the Gaza Strip, where they said the humanitarian situation remains 'catastrophic'. A fragile ceasefire has been in place since October, following a deadly war waged by Israel in response to Hamas's unprecedented October 7, 2023 attack on Israel. Nearly 80 percent of buildings in Gaza have been destroyed or damaged by the war, according to UN data. About 1.5 million of Gaza's more than two million residents have lost their homes, said Amjad Al-Shawa, director of the Palestinian NGO Network in Gaza. JERUSALEM, Undefined — Israel on Thursday said 37 humanitarian agencies supplying aid in Gaza had not met a deadline to meet 'security and transparency standards', and would be banned from the territory, despite an international outcry.

title: Women’s groups call for passage of SOGIE Equality bill anew
body: Despite progress in Congress, the bills have had to be refiled after failing to hurdle the legislative mill. 'It’s a bit questionable. Ilang years na,' Jhulia Datu of Bayi Inc said. 'Hindi rin namin maintindihan kung bakit hanggang ngayon hindi pa natin matapos,' Lilibeth Beltran of KAbabaihang MAabilidad at Organisado (KAMAO) stated. Bayi and KAMAO, together with the group Lunas Collective, called for the passage of the SOGIESC or the Sexual Orientation, Gender Identity, Gender Expression, or Sex Characteristics bill. Some opponents of the bill have raised questions on religious freedom while others have claimed that legislation isn't necessary because the LGBTQ+ community is 'accepted' in the Philippines. 'This landmark legislation stands as a beacon of progress, championing the rights of the LGBTQ+ community and fostering a society built on the principles of acceptance and inclusion,' read the Unity Statement of the different women’s groups. 'Hence, we fervently urge you to pass the SOGIE Equality Bill without delay, thereby affirming our shared commitment to building an equitable society,' it continued. Asked if there has been an improvement in the protection of rights of marginalized sectors, Wynkqui Cruz of Lunas Collective said, 'Masasabi lang natin that it improved if we really hear sa balita na yes, finally napasa na.' 'Wala pang improvement,' Datu said. 'Regardless of the administration, regardless of kung sino ang nakaupo sa Presidente, hangga’t hindi pa napapasa ang bill na ‘yan, walang improvement eh.' After the press conference, the groups held a 'Rainbow Pasavogue' where they splashed colored powder while waving their rainbow flags. MANILA — Various women’s groups are puzzled why a bill prohibiting discrimination and exclusion over sexual orientation and gender identity has been pending in Congress for 23 years.

title: DNA test results in missing beauty queen case out next week: PNP
body: The PNP’s forensics team earlier collected DNA samples from Camilon’s family to compare it with hair strands found inside the vehicle, said PNP spokesperson Col. Jean Fajardo. 'Hindi po nila minamadali itong pag-aanalyze ng mga DNA results. As of kahapon po ay may karagdagan pa silang kinuhanan ng mga specimen,' she said in a news forum in Quezon City. 'So they committed na by next week ay mailalabas na po nila yung resulta ng DNA examination,' she said. Two witnesses earlier claimed that they saw 3 men carrying a bloodied woman from a Nissan Juke vehicle to a Honda CRV.
