Authorities are still looking into the identification of the CRV’s owner, but the Juke is owned by Police Major Allan De Castro. De Castro has 'impliedly admitted to his relationship with Ms. Camilon but opted not to answer when asked if he was involved in her disappearance,' Fajardo said. 'Sinabi niya na may mga kaso na namang sinampa sa kaniya, sasagutin niya na lang sa korte itong mga kasong isinampa sa kaniya,' the spokesperson said. De Castro is currently under restrictive custody in the PNP’s headquarters in Calabarzon. Camilon was last seen by her family on October 12. MANILA — Results of the DNA test that will help confirm if missing beauty queen Catherine Camilon was stashed in an abandoned vehicle in Batangas City will be released next week, the Philippine National Police (PNP) said on Saturday.

title: UPLB's College of Forestry launches nationwide greening initiative on Earth Day 2024
body: At the heart of this initiative is an original musical film by I.Syoot Multimedia Production, designed to educate young generations about forests' crucial role in fighting the climate crisis. Dean Marlo Mendoza of UPLB's College of Forestry and Natural Resources unveiled the ambitious goal of establishing a thousand forests, nurtured through collective efforts from various stakeholders. By 2050, the initiative targets to have planted and maintained 2 million hectares of forests. He said the La Mesa Eco Park is proof that proper 'greening program' is doable and is planning to replicate that with the 'A Thousand Forests' program. 'We plan to expand that La Mesa is just one part it just a small but we have so much learning we think yung natutunan natin sa La Mesa we can replicate it but with different sectors, private, the academe, schools, NGO, LGU,' he said. The inauguration of the 'A Thousand Forests' (ATF) program took place at the Ramon Magsaysay Center on Sunday, gathering leaders from diverse sectors like government, business, academia, and the diplomatic community, all uniting for environmental conservation causes. The late Gina Lopez inspired the program, with her brother Ernie Lopez, director for advocacy of ABS-CBN Foundation Incorporated, showing support during the launch event. ATF, a collaborative effort led by UPLB-CFNR with support from the Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR), and co-founded by I.Syoot Multimedia, focuses on addressing the concerning lack of climate change awareness among Filipino youth, as highlighted by Jenie Chan, Executive Director of I.Syoot Multimedia. The Philippines, according to the United Nations, stands at the forefront of the climate crisis, disproportionately affecting children and leading to millions of displacements. Recognizing these challenges, the ATF program aims to bridge the awareness gap and inspire proactive climate actions among the youth. Dr. Jose Camacho, Jr., Chancellor of UP Los Baños, emphasized the university's dedication to addressing present and future environmental challenges, highlighting forestry's critical role in mitigating climate change impacts. 'A Thousand Forests: The Movie,' the flagship project under ATF, will premiere on Arbor Day, June 25, 2024, in selected Philippine cinemas. The film follows five teenagers exploring the Philippine Forest Camp, intertwining themes of self-discovery and environmental stewardship, brought to life through captivating music and cinematography. Directed by Hanz Florentino, former director of ABS-CBN Foundation's acclaimed ecotourism program, the movie aims to engage a wide audience, especially targeting school children from the fifth grade and above through nationwide block screenings. 'I have been very fortunate to work with Ma’am Gina Lopez, G Diaries. We travel around the Philippines and we have talked to real people who were victims of climate change. That's how we put them all together in this film,' Florentino said. The University of the Philippines Los Baños (UPLB) College of Forestry and Natural Resources took the lead in Earth Day 2024 celebrations with the launch of 'A Thousand Forests,' a nationwide initiative aimed at enhancing collaborative and impactful greening programs across the Philippines. MANILA -- The University of the Philippines Los Baños (UPLB) College of Forestry and Natural Resources took the lead in Earth Day 2024 celebrations with the launch of 'A Thousand Forests,' a nationwide initiative aimed at enhancing collaborative and impactful greening programs across the Philippines. The University of the Philippines Los Baños (UPLB) College of Forestry and Natural Resources took the lead in Earth Day 2024 celebrations with the launch of 'A Thousand Forests,' a nationwide initiative aimed at enhancing collaborative and impactful greening programs across the Philippines.

title: DepEd eyes hiring of displaced teachers through Bayanihan 2
body: Under the measure, some P300 million is allotted for teachers and non-teaching personnel displaced by the COVID-19 pandemic. Some 440 private schools would be temporarily ceasing operations in the coming academic year due to lack of enrollees, the agency earlier said. Education Undersecretary Nepomuceno Malaluan said some of the department's regional directors have reported that part of their distance learning delivery would involve the hiring of teacher facilitators. 'I believe there have been partnerships as reported with DOLE on the ground as part of the work assistance to the teachers... We are also looking to the Bayanihan 2 and we will actively engage the process of the implementation of the Bayanihan 2 programming to hopefully include this in its operationalization,' he told ANC. 'It will be on a need basis still and on the part of the private schools we will need also their assistance... for a clear listing of these displaced teachers. I think that will have to come from the private schools association themselves.' Malaluan, meantime, said the number of hours that a student will spend learning would depend on the class programming of the region or his school. This after Education Undersecretary Jesus Mateo earlier said students may spend up to 7 to 8 hours studying at home or attending online classes, as an adjustment to the new academic calendar. 'A big part of the distance learning delivery modality would involve self-directed learning. While there will be a definite time period, there will be a lot of self-based learning that will be happening at home,' he said. The agency is currently conducting dry runs in preparation for the school opening on October 5, he added, 'These dry runs we are doing are informing the regionwide how it is being done by example and of course there will be a lot of learning by doing in the first quarter, Malaluan said. The Department of Education said Wednesday it was eyeing to fund the hiring of displaced teachers as tutors through the Bayanihan 2, government's second coronavirus response measure. MANILA - The Department of Education said Wednesday it was eyeing to fund the hiring of displaced teachers as tutors through the Bayanihan 2, government's second coronavirus response measure.

title: DepEd probing 2 cases of child grooming, justifies confidential funds
body: Child grooming was one of the reasons the DepEd cited last year when it sought P500 million in confidential funds for 2023. Kabataan Rep. Raoul Manuel asked DepEd to supply data on the use of confidential funds against child grooming. DepEd Undersecretary Michael Wesley Poa said the agency had submitted its accomplishment reports. 'Sa ngayon po ang ongoing pa lang po na-handle natin na child grooming case ay yung nasa Cavite, yan po ay ongoing na po ang prosecution and meron rin po sa Zamboanga. So 2 cases ang officially na under ng DepEd,' he told lawmakers. (We are handling a child grooming case in Cavite, prosecution is ongoing for that, and there is also a case in Zamboanga. So 2 cases are officially under DepEd.) But Manuel claimed this low record meant that child grooming was only fabricated as an excuse for the confidential funds. 'Parang ang naging dating po sa akin, nag-iimbento tayo ng dahilan to justify confidential funds,' Manuel said. (For me, it seems that we are inventing a reason to justify confidential funds.) Vice President and Education Secretary Sara Duterte in the same briefing said, 'There is a purpose and a need for confidential funds in the DepEd, because basic education is intertwined with national security.' 'But of course, as we always say, we leave it to the discretion of the members of the House of Representatives and the Senate, the wisdom of granting confidential funds to DepEd,' she added. MANILA —The Department of Education (DepEd) is looking into 2 cases of child grooming, or when teachers or adults 'groom' minors to be their 'dates.'

title: DSWD eyes procuring mobile showers, expanding mobile kitchens in 2026
body: DSWD Spokesperson Irene Dumlao neither said how many additional pieces of equipment the agency hopes to procure nor how much has been earmarked for this project. 'Gusto natin dagdagan 'yong ating mga specialized disaster equipment tulad ng ating mga mobile command centers at ating mga mobile kitchens pati na yung ating mga water tanks and water filtration systems,' Dumlao told state television PTV. 'Nakita natin kung paano ito nakatulong sa ating mga kababayan so we want to have more of these,' she said. 'We hope that aside from that makapagprocure din kami ng pangangailan sa mga evacuation centers like mga mobile shower systems para magamit sa mga evacuation centers,' she said. Aside from increasing the number of its equipment, the DSWD is also looking to hike the number of beneficiaries for various social welfare and development programs in 2026, Dumlao said. 'We are targeting to cover more than 4 million indigent senior citizens,' she said. 'Doon naman po sa supplementary feeding program, kung nasa 1.5 million tayo na kabataan, ang target naman po natin for next year ay ma-serve natin ang around 1.8 million na children enrolled in child development centers,' she said. 'Hopefully we can cover 3.6 million individuals in crisis situations by 2026,' she said. The DSWD is also seeking to expand the coverage of its flagship 'Walang Gutom Program' to over 600,000 beneficiaries, as well as increase the number of vendors accredited to provide nutritious food in exchange for credits. The agency is also working to further digitalize its systems to ensure that beneficiaries are properly targeted and that its programs translate into improved living conditions for marginalized Filipinos, Dumlao said. The DSWD seeks to procure more specialized disaster equipment such as portable shower systems and additional mobile kitchens to enhance its calamity-response services next year. MANILA — The Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) said Thursday it seeks to procure more specialized disaster equipment, such as portable shower systems and additional mobile kitchens, to enhance its calamity-response services next year.

title: ‘Remembering Ninoy’: Filipinos urged to continue Ninoy’s fight for good governance, justice
body: A wreath-laying ceremony was held at Ninoy’s marker at the Terminal 1 of Ninoy Aquino International Airport, formerly known as Manila International Airport, where Ninoy was fatally shot at the tarmac. Senator Bam Aquino, Ninoy’s nephew, underscored the need for Filipinos to continue his uncle’s crusade for good governance. 'At least nakita naman po natin na may demokrasya na tayo ngayon pero yung ibang mga pinaglalaban niya, gaya ng good governance, mas patas na lipunan, alam ho nating hindi pa natin nakakamit,' he said. A Holy Mass was also celebrated at the tomb of Ninoy at the Manila Memorial Park in Parañaque City attended by family members, notable friends and supporters. One of the attendees is 69-year-old Ramon Balang, one of the witnesses in Ninoy’s assassination. Balang was at the tarmac working as an aircraft mechanic assigned to assist in the parking of the China Airlines aircraft where Ninoy was aboard. Over 40 years after the Aquino-Galman murders, he still remembers what happened vividly. 'I was doing my routine check of the aircraft, nandun ako sa ilalim nung bridge nakita ko may nagbabaan… Hindi ko pa alam nun na si Ninoy ‘yun, ang alam ko lang the man in white,' Balang recalled. 'Pagbaba 1 or 2 step, bigla na lang siyang bumagsak sa tarmac... During my testimony, in-identify ko yung sunud-sunod na nasa likod,' he recounted. The August Twenty-One Movement (ATOM) said Balang was the first witness to testify under oath with the Agrava Fact-finding Board that investigated Ninoy’s assassination and helped convict the AVSECOM soldiers later on. He vowed to continue commemorating Ninoy’s death and legacy and heralding the truth as he witnessed. 'Kasi ngayon puro fake news puro pambubudol ang ginagawa ng mga politiko. Sa konting nalalaman ko, naitutuwid yung mga pambubudol nila,' he said. Kiko Aquino-Dee, Ninoy’s grandson, said there would be no reconciliation between the Aquinos and Marcoses until the latter recognize the injustices of Martial Law. 'I think we’ve been very consistent that there’s no reconciliation without justice. So, for as long as there’s no recognition of the atrocities that were committed under Marcos dictatorship… I don’t think there’s much to say between our families,' he said. President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. earlier released a statement joining Filipinos in commemorating Ninoy Aquino Day by reflecting on the lessons of history, and emphasizing on the importance of leadership guided by 'conscience, sobriety, and foresight.' 'In honoring this day, the Republic signals its readiness to uphold leadership that strives towards wholeness and reconciliation,' the president stated. Aquino-Dee also welcomed the decision of the Marcos Jr. administration not to move the observance of Ninoy Aquino Day to a later date. 'I think Pres. Marcos has a lot on his plate right now, I think historical distortion thankfully wasn’t on it this year, but we will continue to be vigilant,' he noted. Last year, the Palace moved the observance of the Ninoy Aquino Day from August 21, 2024, Wednesday, to August 23, 2024, Friday, 'to promote domestic tourism in the country'. August Twenty One Movement (ATOM) President Volt Bohol also appreciated the move, noting that those who would attempt to make people forget history should be opposed. 'Puwede naman palang hindi gawing holiday economics ‘di ba? So it works even better. My advise for all those who continue to do initiatives to make people forget siguro tigilan na po nating yung ganun, kasi the more you do things like attempting to make people forget our history, the more our people will continue to remember and mas nagsasalita po ang karamihan,' he stated. Historian Professor Xiao Chua, meanwhile, underscored the importance of keeping Ninoy’s legacy alive up to this modern day especially among the youth. 'Kung ikaw ay nakakapag TikTok, sarap na sarap ka sa pagti-TikTok ngayon ano, at malaya mo itong nagagawa, yan ay bahagi ng demokrasyang ipinaglaban ng buong bayang Pilipino sa pangunguna ni Sen. Ninoy Aquino noong dekada ’80 na sana ‘wagv nating sasayangin,' he said. 'Kasi pag ginagamit natin ang mga kalayaan natin, nang hindi maganda, nang mali, ng hindi responsable eh parang sinampal na rin natin lahat ng mga martyr ng Martial Law,' he added. Several family members, friends, and supporters commemorated the 42nd death anniversary of former senator Benigno 'Ninoy’'Aquino on Thursday, August 21. MANILA -- Several family members, friends and supporters commemorated the 42nd death anniversary of former senator Benigno ‘Ninoy’ Aquino on Thursday, August 21. title: Cha-cha panel member says no reason to postpone 2019 polls
body: Political science professor Edmund Tayao said the committee is done with the draft federal constitution which they will submit to the President for review before his state of the nation address. 'The time frame still allows the midterm elections. Unless of course there's a clear enough reason why we should not [proceed with the elections],' he told ANC. The professor said the midterm polls in 2019 may also include a plebiscite for the new federal constitution if the schedule goes as planned, as Duterte's last three years will be a transitory period. Retired Supreme Court associate justice Antonio Nachura, also a committee member, earlier said some lawmakers are 'inclined' to call for the postponement of next year's polls for charter change. But Tayao said lawmakers they consulted have told them that Congress can fast-track the budget deliberations so they can discuss the proposed new charter. The committee headed by former Chief Justice Reynato Puno is set to hold regional consultations about the draft federal constitution before submitting its recommendations to Duterte. MANILA - A member of President Rodrigo Duterte's consultative committee on charter change sees no reason to postpone the 2019 midterm elections.

title: Thousands of riders flock to Quezon Circle for DSWD cash aid
body: The DSWD rollout was intended to provide a financial cushion for transport workers grappling with high fuel costs. While the agency targeted 10,561 beneficiaries for the day, the event was marred by scheduling confusion that left one rider frustrated and empty-handed. Rolando Taganas, a delivery rider who arrived before dawn, found his name excluded from the day's list despite following earlier announcements. 'Nagtanong ako kasi ang schedule niya ngayon is March 27 J-Z. Eh ngayon pagpunta namin dito, J-R na lang,' Taganas explained. 'Kasi wala silang record ng S-Z. Ang schedule na namin is Monday, March 30.' Taganas expressed disappointment over the lack of coordination, noting that the misinformation led to wasted time and lost income. 'Hindi natin alam kung sino may mali rito kaya ang inaano lang namin is correction kasi hindi dapat nagpopost ng ganito para walang naabalang tao,' he added. For those who successfully received their assistance, the funds are already intended for bills. Jerico Jamot, an MC taxi rider, noted that while the aid helps, he cannot afford to stop working. 'Malaking bagay kasi bale sa taas ngayon ng gasolina medyo makakabawas din kami… Dun na siguro kasama siguro sa mga expenses pero hahatiin ko din,' Jamot said. 'Kailangan sumagad ng byahe para kumita.' 'Sa school ng anak ko... okay masaya kaso kung tutuusin talaga kulang eh sa taas ng gasolina,' Jamora shared. 'Sa hirap ng byahe ngayon halos wala ka na kinikita eh sa taas ng gasolina buti nga merong ganito para pandagdag.' The remaining riders, specifically those with surnames starting with S through Z, are scheduled for processing on Monday, March 30. Meanwhile, the second day of distributing the P5,000 financial assistance for delivery service riders in Pasig City proceeded early. A total of 6,000 delivery service riders of Pasig are on the list of the LTFRB submitted to the DSWD for this Friday’s payout. However, some riders, such as George De Leon, experienced confusion and was unable to receive their cash relief assistance. 'Naging magulo ‘yung naging schedule. Bulacan ako tapos nakita ko ‘yung list ko sa Pasig. Ilang araw na ako naghahanap. Nandito ako kahapon ang sabi delivery rider lang ang makakakuha, so hindi ako pumila kasi MC taxi ako. Pagbalik ko rito alphabetical daw at dapat kahapon pa, at ang name ko raw nasa delivery rider so hindi ako makaka-claim. Ang problema namin, kailan ‘yung reschedule para roon sa unclaimed?' De Leon said. De Leon and other riders who had concerns were given the opportunity to speak directly with DSWD Secretary Rex Gatchalian. They were reassured by the agency that they would be accommodated in the special payout. The said payout is intended for drivers who have not yet received their assistance due to various reasons, including those who were unable to come on their scheduled claim date. 'Wag ho kayong mag-alala, ‘yung mga nakausap kasi natin nasa listahan sila pero hindi nila nakuha, may iba-ibang rason, mayroong hindi nila alam, mayroong nasa ospital. Huwag kayong mag-alala meron tayong special na araw para sa unclaimed at pati na rin doon sa mga hinabol pa na listahan ng inyong mga kumpanya.' The DSWD has not yet announced an exact date for the special payout, but the secretary assured that it will be scheduled as soon as possible. 'Sa mabilis na panahon ilalathala rin namin iyong mga araw na iyon,' Gatchalian added. The DSWD rollout was intended to provide a financial cushion for transport workers grappling with high fuel costs. Bea Cuadra, ABS-CBN News MANILA (UPDATE) — Thousands of delivery and motorcycle taxi riders filled the Quezon Memorial Circle as early as 4:30 a.m. Friday for the Department of Social Welfare and Development’s (DSWD) cash relief distribution.

title: Trump vows to cut off Cuba's oil after toppling Venezuelan ally Maduro
body: The threatening social media post drew an angry retort from Cuban President Miguel Diaz-Canel, who said 'no one' would tell his country what to do. Washington has imposed economy-crippling sanctions on its island neighbor for decades, but Trump has ramped up the pressure in recent days. US special forces seized Maduro and his wife last week in a lightning raid that left dozens of the ousted Venezuelan president's security personnel dead -- many of whom were Cuban. Though Maduro's allies have become interim leaders, Trump has claimed the United States now actually controls Venezuela, through a US naval blockade of its vital oil sector. 'THERE WILL BE NO MORE OIL OR MONEY GOING TO CUBA - ZERO!' Trump said Sunday morning on his Truth Social platform. 'I strongly suggest they make a deal, BEFORE IT IS TOO LATE.' Trump provided no details about what potential deal he referred to, or what such an arrangement would achieve. He said 'Cuba lived, for many years, on large amounts of OIL and MONEY from Venezuela. In return, Cuba provided 'Security Services' for the last two Venezuelan dictators, BUT NOT ANYMORE!' 'Most of those Cubans are DEAD from last week's U.S.A. attack, and Venezuela doesn't need protection anymore from the thugs and extortionists who held them hostage for so many years.' A week ago, Trump stated that 'Cuba is ready to fall,' noting that the island's economic crisis was worsening and it would be difficult for Havana to 'hold out' without receiving heavily subsidized Venezuelan oil. Earlier on Sunday, the president reposted a message that jokingly suggested US Secretary of State Marco Rubio -- a child of Cuban immigrants who concurrently holds the posts of national security advisor, acting head of the US archives, and acting international aid administrator -- could also become the president of Cuba. Trump shared that post with the comment: 'Sounds good to me!' Cuba's president rebuffed Trump's threatening language, saying the Caribbean island's residents were 'ready to defend the homeland to the last drop of blood.' 'Cuba is a free, independent and sovereign nation. No one tells us what to do,' Diaz-Canel wrote on X. Foreign Minister Bruno Rodriguez also weighed in to stress that Cuba is within its rights to import fuel from any willing exporter, 'without interference or subordination to the unilateral coercive measures of the United States.' A Cold War-era US trade embargo has cinched Cuba's economy beginning in 1962, and since 2000 Havana increasingly has relied on Venezuelan oil provided as part of a deal struck with Maduro's predecessor, the firebrand leftist Hugo Chavez. On Sunday in the streets of Havana, retiree Mercedes Simon seemed to dismiss the US leader's latest bluster. 'Trump is not going to touch Cuba,' the 65-year-old told AFP. 'All the presidents talk, talk, talk' about Cuba, for decades, 'but they don't act.' Marcos Sanchez, a 21-year-old working in the restaurant business, said the two countries should find common ground, 'without resorting to violence.' Trump's provocative language on Cuba comes as the emboldened American leader has hinted he has other countries in his sights after capturing Maduro. Trump, who had openly sought last year's Nobel Peace Prize, has recently threatened Colombia, Mexico, Iran and Greenland. Some Republican US lawmakers on Sunday lauded Trump for his aggressive comments on Cuba, including congressman Mario Diaz-Balart from Florida. 'The tyranny in Cuba will not survive the second term of President Trump,' Diaz-Balart posted in Spanish on X, 'and Cuba will finally be free after decades of misery, tragedy, and pain.' US President Donald Trump urged Cuba on Sunday to 'make a deal' soon, pledging to cut off all oil and money flowing to the communist-run island after the toppling of Havana's key ally, Venezuelan leader Nicolas Maduro. WASHINGTON, United States — US President Donald Trump urged Cuba on Sunday to 'make a deal' soon, pledging to cut off all oil and money flowing to the communist-run island after the toppling of Havana's key ally, Venezuelan leader Nicolas Maduro. US President Donald Trump urged Cuba on Sunday to 'make a deal' soon, pledging to cut off all oil and money flowing to the communist-run island after the toppling of Havana's key ally, Venezuelan leader Nicolas Maduro.

title: IS blasts kill more than 140 as US, Russia press Syria truce
body: The Islamic State group said it was behind the carnage. US Secretary of State John Kerry said a provisional deal had been reached on the terms of a truce in Syria's brutal five-year conflict, only for the bloodshed to intensify on the ground. Near Damascus, a car bombing followed by two consecutive suicide attacks ripped through the area of the Shiite shrine of Sayyida Zeinab and killed 83 people, Syria's official news agency SANA reported. SANA, quoting a police source, said 178 people, including children, were among the wounded. State television said the attacks came as pupils were leaving school in the area. The Syrian Observatory for Human Rights gave a lower toll of 68 dead but said many wounded were in critical condition. An AFP reporter said the blasts struck about 400 metres (yards) from the revered Shiite shrine containing the grave of a granddaughter of the Prophet Mohammed. At least 60 shops were damaged and cars reduced to mangled metal. A January attack in the same area -- also claimed by IS -- killed 70 people. The Observatory also reported that two car bombs killed at least 59 people and wounded dozens in the pro-regime district of Al-Zahraa in the central city of Homs. IS said online that two suicide bombers struck in Sayyida Zeinab and two others drove explosive-packed cars into crowds in Homs. State television footage from Homs showed emergency workers carrying a charred body on a stretcher past devastated shops and mangled cars and minibuses. Al-Zahraa -- whose residents are mostly from the same Alawite sect of Shia Islam as Syria's ruling clan -- has been regularly targeted. World powers, which have been pushing for a halt in Syria's nearly five-year war, had hoped to see a truce take effect on Friday but have struggled to agree on the terms. On Sunday, Kerry spoke with Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov at least three times to try to nail down a truce. 'We have reached a provisional agreement, in principle, on the terms of the cessation of hostilities that could begin in the coming days,' Kerry said in Amman after one round of talks. The Russian foreign ministry later said Lavrov and Kerry held two more telephone conversations and finalised the ceasefire terms to be submitted to their respective presidents. World powers proposed the truce just over a week ago as part of a plan that also included expanded humanitarian access, in a bid to pave the way for peace talks to resume. The talks, which collapsed earlier this month in Geneva, had been scheduled to resume on February 25, but the UN's Syria envoy has already acknowledged that that date is no longer realistic. Key opposition umbrella group the High Negotiations Committee said at the weekend it would agree a temporary truce only if regime backers halted fire. HNC chief Riad Hijab said any ceasefire must be reached 'with international mediation and with guarantees obliging Russia, Iran and their sectarian militias and mercenaries to stop fighting'. Syrian President Bashar al-Assad, meanwhile, told Spain's El Pais newspaper he was 'ready' for a ceasefire, but that it should not be exploited by 'terrorists'. Moscow is a key architect of the proposed ceasefire, but has shown little sign so far that it plans to rein in the air campaign it began in September in support of Assad's government. On Saturday, the Kremlin said it would continue 'to provide assistance and help to the armed forces of Syria in their offensive actions against terrorists'. Regime forces backed by Russian strikes were advancing on Sunday east of Aleppo city against IS, consolidating their control over a stretch of highway from the city to the Kweyris military base. The Observatory said at least 50 IS fighters had been killed in clashes and Russian strikes since Saturday morning. Tensions have been rising between Moscow and opposition-backer Ankara, alarmed by both the regime's Russian-backed advances and a major operation by Kurdish-led forces in Aleppo province. The Kurdish People's Protection Units (YPG) and their Arab partners have seized key territory from rebel forces in Aleppo province, prompting Turkey to shell their positions. Ankara considers the YPG to be an affiliate of the outlawed Kurdistan Workers' Party, which has waged a decades-long insurgency against Turkey. It fears the Kurdish advances are intended to link areas in north and northeast Syria to create a contiguous semi-autonomous Kurdish zone along the Syrian-Turkish border. On Sunday, Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan defended his country's fight against the YPG as 'legitimate defense' after international calls for Ankara to halt its military action in Syria. SAYYIDA ZEINAB - A string of suicide bombings near a Shiite shrine outside Syria's capital and in Homs claimed by jihadists killed at least 142 people Sunday, as Washington and Moscow worked to secure a ceasefire.

title: Duterte’s narco list has more than 5,000 gov’t officials
body: Duterte announced Saturday during the San Beda College Law alumni homecoming that he may submit his final narco list to the National Security Council (NSC) and the legislative branch before the end of the month. 'And I will give each of them a copy, and then the Senate President and the Speaker of the House, then let us formulate how to prevent disaster for the next generation,' Duterte said. In an interview with ANC, Panelo said he saw the folders of the list, which he estimates to be more than one foot-high and contains around 5,000 to 10,000 names of government officials from barangay officials and mayors to governors, members of the judiciary and prosecutors. He said the huge number of narco-politicians poses a threat to public safety. 'There are about 10,000 people, government officials involved. That means that the magnitude and depth of the drug menace is so much, so huge, that public safety is now in danger.' Asked on the possible next step of the President after submitting the list, Panelo said he heard the President is considering the suspension of the privilege of the writ of habeas corpus. Panelo also responded to critics saying the President can only suspend the writ on two grounds — rebellion and invasion — and argued that similar circumstances may also be considered in declaring a suspension. Earlier this month, Duterte said he may be forced to suspend the writ if lawlessness in Mindanao continues. The President, under the 1987 constitution, may suspend the privilege of the writ of habeas corpus only in cases of invasion or rebellion and when public safety requires it. 'Some are arguing that there is no invasion and neither is there rebellion but he (Duterte) is saying that drug money is being used to fund the rebellion in Mindanao therefore there is a rebellion,' Panelo explained, noting that critics are looking at the constitution 'literally.' Panelo argued that suspending the writ is done to prevent the effects of invasion or rebellion and therefore any circumstance similar to such can be considered in its suspension. 'You cannot limit the President on two grounds of invasion and rebellion. Any circumstance similar or akin to those grounds, I think the President and I think the Supreme Court will do so,' he said. President Rodrigo Duterte’s 'validated' list of alleged drug personalities contains more than 5,000 names of government officials, Presidential Chief Legal Counsel Salvador Panelo said Sunday. MANILA- President Rodrigo Duterte’s 'validated' list of alleged drug personalities contains more than 5,000 names of government officials, Presidential Chief Legal Counsel Salvador Panelo said Sunday.

title: New rules force some to sleep outside Manila North Cemetery
body: The Pasig City Cemetery and the Evergreen Memorial Garden meanwhile are both quiet and peaceful in the wee hours of Wednesday. Ma. Sheryl Laco of the Public Safety Division (PSD) office of the Pasig City Hall said that the situation was normal as there were not a lot of people who went to the cemetery. The cemetery is open to the public 24 hours on Wednesday as well as Thursday for All Souls' Day. A total of 19 PSD personnel, 17 Bantay Pasig personnel, and 10 members of the Pasig police are deployed to the Pasig City Cemetery to maintain peace and order. 'Sa ngayon wala pa gaano nag-o-overnight gawa ng bukas ng gabi magdamag po sila dito pagod pa kasi mga tao kasi di ba naglinis po, may naglilinis na po ng 30, 31 po so bukas po halos magdamag po ang tao dito,' Laco said. Alcoholic beverages, gambling, sharp objects, and loudspeakers are prohibited inside the Pasig City Cemetery. On the opposite side of C. Raymundo Avenue, some visitors to the Evergreen Memorial Garden are planning to stay overnight. One of them is Mary Joy Baeza whose father passed away last September 29 due to a heart attack. 'Actually dito naman kasi sa cemetery na ito may overnight stay din kada yearly dito kasi may iba din naman kaming relatives dito pero first time din namin father namin yung nandito siyempre ayun makasama din namin siya for this day,' Baeza said. Evergreen Memorial Garden has 7 PSD personnel, 14 Bantay Pasig, and 5 police officers from the Pasig City Police Station to ensure security. —Reports from Karen De Guzman and Champ de Lunas, ABS-CBN News MANILA — New guidelines forced some visitors to spend the night outside the Manila North Cemetery on the eve of All Saints' Day, November 1. Regie Vasquez and his family from Pandi, Bulacan were supposed to stay overnight at the cemetery to visit their departed and celebrate his birthday. However, they were unaware of the new guidelines set by the cemetery for Undas 2023. 'Dumating po kami dito mga 5 ng hapon. Nakita naman namin na nag-uuwian na ‘yung mga tao, pero siyempre hindi na namin sinubukan umuwi dahil sa layo nga ng lugar namin,' Vasquez said. 'Kasi kung alam namin, hindi na kami para makipagsapalaran pa. Dito na lang kami tapos mag-aantay na lang ako ng a-uno. Dito na lang ako mag birthday sa labas ng North,' he added. Angelina Marzol and her relatives from Laguna decided to sleep on cardboard boxes until the cemetery opened for visitors. They questioned why only residents could enter the cemetery's premises beyond operating hours. 'Talagang 31 kami pumupunta dito. Ngayon na lang namin nalaman sa mga guwardiya. Ang daming pinapasok, mga nakatira daw diyan, may mga ID daw,' Marzol said. 'Dapat hindi na nila ginanito. Hindi namin alam e. Ang layo pa namin. E kung babalik kami bukas, baka hindi na kami makapasok agad-agad. Marami nang tao, dagsa na,' she said. The management of Manila North Cemetery is reminding the public that their main gates are open from 5:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. only starting October 30 to November 2, 2023, to give enough time to clean the cemetery.

title: China turns over 15 M/V Devon Bay survivors to PH Coast Guard
body: According to the PCG, the rescued Filipino crew members were transferred using the rigid-hull inflatable boats of both vessels due to big waves prevailing in the area. Meanwhile, the two bodies that were transferred to the PCG will undergo proper disposition in coordination with concerned authorities and the bereaved families. PCG spokesperson Captain Noemie Cayabyab said the Coast Guard thanked the CCG for the humanitarian assistance and cooperation in ensuring safety of life at sea. 'BRP Teresa Magbanua (MRRV-9701) is scheduled to arrive at Pier 13, Port Area, Manila in the early morning of January 26, 2026,' she said. As of press time, BRP Cape San Agustin (MRRV-4408) and the PCG Islander aircraft continue search and rescue operations to locate the four remaining missing Filipino crew members in the reported distressed aarea. The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) received 15 survivors and two deceased crew members of M/V Devon Bay during a turnover operation conducted between the PCG and the China Coast Guard (CCG) at approximately 168 nautical miles west off Tambobong, Pangasinan on Sunday.

title: PHOTO: Ex-Peruvian president Alberto Fujimori laid to rest
body: Former Peruvian President Alberto Fujimori's children Kenji and Keiko mourn during his burial at the Campo Fe cemetery in Huachipa on September 14, 2024. The state funeral of Peru's divisive ex-president Alberto Fujimori, a major figure in Latin American politics, began Saturday in Lima, capping three days of national mourning. Relatives of former Peruvian president Alberto Fujimori mourn during his burial at the Campo Fe cemetery in Huachipa in eastern Lima on Saturday. The state funeral of Peru's divisive ex-president Alberto Fujimori, a major figure in Latin American politics, began Saturday in Lima, capping three days of national mourning. Fujimori was revered by many in Peru for crushing a bloody leftist insurgency and helping shore up the economy while in office from 1990 to 2000. However, others see Fujimori as a power-hungry autocrat after committing human rights abuses for crushing a brutal insurgency by Shining Path and Tupac Amaru leftist rebels in a conflict that left more than 69,000 people dead and 21,000 missing between 1980 and 2000. In 2009, he was sentenced to 25 years in prison over two massacres of civilians by an army death squad tasked with fighting the Shining Path - one at a house party, the other in a university dormitory. In all 25 people were killed. 5 things to know about Peru's political instability Ousted Peru president put in provisional detention

title: Persons deprived of liberty cast vote in barangay, SK elections
body: Persons deprived of liberty have also exercised their right to vote in Monday's barangay and Sangguniang Kabataan elections. At least 7 PDLs voted at the Taguig National High School before 8 a.m. Personnel of the Taguig police and of the Bureau of Jail Management and Penology stood guard while they voted. Interior Secretary Benhur Abalos earlier said a total of 31,125 PDLs would cast their votes in the scheduled Barangay and Sangguniang Kabataan Elections (BSKE). According to Comelec rules, inmates who are formally charged and are awaiting trial, and those serving prison sentences of less than a year are qualified to vote in the local polls. Of the PDL voters, 29,133 would vote inside the special polling precincts within BJMP jails, while 1,992 would be escorted by jail officers to their barangays. 'Makasaysayan ito dahil ngayon pa lang sila magpa-participate sa barangay elections. This is very historic. Ito’y dahil sa Supreme Court decision noong August 26, 2002 na sinasabing unless convicted by final judgment, all PDLs have a constitutional right to vote,' Abalos said. (This is historic because this is the first time for PDLs to participate in barangay elections.) Abalos directed BJMP Director Ruel Rivera to ensure orderly and peaceful voting by the PDLs in their respective designated precincts during election day. Meanwhile, a total of 303 PDLs at the Manila City Jail cast their votes for BSKE. Of this figure, 278 are males and 25 are females. 'Recka' and 'Mamu' were among the PDLs who exercised their right to vote. 'Masaya kasi bilang isang PDL na nasa loob ng piitan, kami ay binigyan ng karapatan upang piliin ang karapat-dapat na mamuno sa barangay namin na nasasakupan niya. Masaya kami kasi hindi kami hinigpitan para makapili kami ng barangay chairman at mga taong mamumuno sa amin,' Recka said. (I am happy because we were allowed our right to choose deserving leaders for our barangay. We are happy because they did not restrict us in choosing our barangay chairman and the people who will lead us.) 'Sa paglaya namin sa piitang ito, wala naman kaming ibang matatakbuhan kundi ang barangay na uuwian namin. Sila rin ang tatanggap sa amin,' she told reporters. (When we are released from here, we really only have our barangay to go home to. It will be them who will welcome us back.) The PDLs at the Manila City Jail are among the thousands of inmates nationwide who are eligible to vote inside special polling precincts within the jail. 'Ang boboto na ihahatid sa labas… wala pang six months sa kulungan o hindi umabot ng 50 ang kanilang presinto,' said Abalos. (Those who will brought to polling centers outside will be those who have been in detention for less than six months or those in special precincts with fewer than 50 voters.) Abalos and Rivera visited the Manila City Jail to ensure the seamless execution of the voting process there. As of 10 a.m., some 11,000 PDLs nationwide have cast their ballots, the BJMP said. Rivera said that only detainees win good medical condition are allowed out. They would be brought to the polling centers under escort and would have special lanes. 'Kasama po sila sa vulnerable sector. Pagdating, mabibigyan sila agad ng linya para doon,' said Rivera. (They will line up with those in the vulnerable sector. When they get there, they will be given the special line to use.) The BJMP chief noted that videos of the candidates’ campaign platforms were shown to the PDLs to help them choose their political leaders. The Manila City Jail also designated a common poster area for candidates. Abalos said that, aside from voting, the PDLs were allowed to run in the BSKE. 'Hindi lang ito karapatang bumoto ng ating kapatid na nasa piitan. Ang karapatan ding tumakbo ay atin ding ginagalang. In fact, walo sa ating PDLs ang tumatakbo ngayon: 6 for barangay kagawad at 2 for barangay chairman,' said Abalos. '’Yan talaga. Kung mananalo ka, ‘yan ang proseso ng demokrasya, pero you still have to wait for the judgment. Kung ‘yung judgment ay palalayain ka, okay, lalo kung dismissed ang kaso,' he said. According to the Comelec rules, inmates who are formally charged and awaiting trial and those serving prison sentences of less than a year are qualified to vote in the local polls. Earlier this month, the Comelec signed a deal with the BJMP and the Public Attorney’s Office (PAO) on the implementation of PDLs voting for the BSKE. 'Ang BJMP nagkaroon ng MOA with Comelec and PAO para mas maganda ang exercise ngayong umaga,' he added. Of the 31,000 PDL voters, 29,133 will vote inside the special polling precincts within BJMP jails, while 1,992 will be escorted by jail officers in their respective registered precincts in barangays. MANILA (UPDATE) — Some Persons Deprived of Liberty exercised their right to vote in Monday's barangay and Sangguniang Kabataan elections.

title: Broadcasters deny Vico Sotto claim they were paid 'P10M' to interview Discayas
body: Sotto called the two out on Thursday, saying in a Facebook pos t post that many 'good, honest people in media' are either disappointed or angered by unethical practices like that. 'Bago tanggapin ng mga kilalang journalists ang alok para mag-interview ng Contractor na Pumapasok sa Politika, hindi ba nila naisip na, 'Uy teka, ba’t kaya handa ’to magbigay ng 10 million para lang magpa-interview sa akin?'' he asked. (Before these two famous journalists accepted the invitation to interview a contractor joining politics, did they not ask themselves why they were willing to pay P10 million just to be interviewed?) He clarified in the same post that the P10 million is 'not an exact figure'. Sotto said that while the two can argue they were doing 'lifestyle' content, they 'rose to national prominence as broadcast journalists/news personalities' and were lending their credibility to the Discayas. 'In this case, maybe they didn’t do anything technically 'illegal', but at the very least it should be considered shameful and violative of the spirit of their code of ethics,' he said, referring to the Journalist's Code of Ethics that press groups in the Philippines adopted in 1988. Under that code, journalists should not 'accept or offer any present, gift or other consideration of a nature which may cast doubt on [their] professional integrity.' The management of the programs 'Rated Korina' and 'Korina Interviews' released an open letter on Friday, saying they chose to interview the Discaya couple because of 'public interest' and denying Sotto's claim they were paid to feature the couple. 'There is no such thing as P10 million placement for an interview. This is simply not true,' they said as they also hinted Sotto may have committed cyber libel. An earlier statement from the producers, since taken down and replaced had acknowledged payments but did not specify amounts. UP Journalism professor Danilo Arao, speaking on ANC's 'Dateline', said this would '[violate] the principle in journalism that there should be no paid segments or no paid items in pieces that are meant for information dissemination.' He said that 'sponsored posts or sponsored content have no room in trying to shape public opinion.' He acknowledged that platforms and formats of content creation have evolved, 'but let me be clear here: The standards remain the same.' The executive producers of the two programs said they decided to interview the couple because they are relevant to their audience. 'Their sudden prominence in the public eye makes for public interest.... Their rags-to-riches life story is their story to tell,' the producers said, saying they do not make presumptions about their subjects’ moral character and that their interviews are aired without cuts or additions. They also said they let guests tell their own story and that they prohibit slander of other personalities or businesses. They stressed they also reached out to Sotto about the Discayas' interview but that he declined to be interviewed himself. The producers said the interview with the Discayas was 'transparent and meets the strict standards of the airing stations'. Babao also denied being paid P10 million for the interview. He stressed that his interview with the couple is not a news report but a lifestyle feature for his YouTube channel for inspiring success stories. The broadcasters' camps said the two interviews happened long before the 2025 midterm elections. Sanchez' interview with the couple was aired on NET 25's 'Korina Interviews' on January 2, 2025, while Babao's content with them was posted on his YouTube channel 'Julius Babao UNPLUGGED' in September 2024. The Discayas have gained traction on social media recently after Sotto linked them to St. Timothy Construction, one of the top contractors involved in flood control projects that President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. mentioned in his State of the Nation Address last month. Sarah Discaya lost to Sotto in the May 2025 mayoral race in Pasig City. Broadcasters Korina Sanchez and Julius Babao have denied allegations from Pasig City Mayor Vico Sotto that they were paid 'P10 million' to interview Sarah and Curlee Discaya, the mayor's political rivals in the May elections. MANILA (UPDATED) — Broadcasters Korina Sanchez and Julius Babao have denied allegations from Pasig City Mayor Vico Sotto that they were paid 'P10 million' to interview Sarah and Curlee Discaya, the mayor's political rivals in the May elections. Broadcasters Korina Sanchez and Julius Babao have denied allegations from Pasig City Mayor Vico Sotto that they were paid 'P10 million' to interview Sarah and Curlee Discaya, the mayor's political rivals in the May elections.

title: Marcos assures support for PH Air Force as it marks 78th anniversary
body: In his speech during the PAF’s anniversary celebration at the Air Force Headquarters at Villamor Airbase in Pasay City, Marcos urged them to renew their commitment to excellence and unity, as he reaffirmed the organization’s critical role in securing the country’s skies. 'Thank you for keeping us safe. You do all your work in the sky so that all of us here on the ground may live in peace and security. Let our resolve be focused and unshaken as we serve the Philippines and fly ever higher together,' Marcos said. Marcos said his administration is committed to supporting the Philippine Air Force as it continues to address disasters and evolving threats in the country. 'The government will exert all efforts to provide the Philippine Air Force with the best possible equipment, training, and facilities,' the President assured. The President in his speech also highlighted the significant accomplishments of the Philippine Air Force in internal security operations, law enforcement support, humanitarian assistance and disaster response, and international defense cooperation. 'Hundreds of unidentified tracks have been monitored in our air defense zone and thousands of vessels near our waters have been kept under close watch by the Air Force... Insurgents have been tracked. Threats neutralized,' Marcos said. 'But even with the demands of duty, the Air Force has continued to grow, welcoming six new aircraft, enhancing skills through flight simulation and advanced training. It also completed many infrastructure projects and ensured our air assets are always well protected and strategically positioned,' he added. In a separate statement, Vice President Sara Duterte also expressed support for the Philippine Air Force. 'Ngayong araw na ito, ako ay sumasaludo sa inyong magiting na serbisyo sa bayan, na inyong patuloy na ipinapakita sa inyong katatagan at sakripisyo, anumang oras at panahon,' she said. In March, Vice President called out the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) for what she said was its silence on the arrest of her father former President Rodrigo Duterte, who was transported to The Hague, Netherlands from Villamor Air Base, the headquarters of the Philippine Air Force. PAF commanding general Lt. Gen. Arthur Cordova meanwhile affirmed the organization’s commitment to further strengthen the country’s air defense operations as the organization transitioned from internal security operations to territorial defense. 'Across our vast and porous archipelago, your Air Force has risen to that call, flying and defending our airspace and territorial integrity. More importantly, we transitioned mindset, posturing from internal security operations to territorial defense operations,' he said. Cordova said the PAF has made headway in advancing its capabilities in surveillance, precision strike, advanced systems, and integrated air defense, and is preparing to adopt space-related technologies to ensure readiness across all domains. 'We are also evolving our doctrines, force structure, and strategic partnerships to ensure that we remain relevant and decisive in times of peace and in times of crisis. But more than platforms and systems, it is our values that propel us forward: integrity, service above self, teamwork, excellence, and professionalism,' he said. Prior to the program, the PAF held a capability demonstration, which Cordova said provided a 'glimpse of what the Philippine Air Force can do' when all its program platforms and systems become available to complete its integrated air defense system. 'Together, we fly farther, not just to defend the skies, but to shape a future where our Air Force stands ready, relevant, and resilient,' he said. President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr. said his administration is committed to supporting the Philippine Air Force as it continues to address evolving threats in the country. MANILA — President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr. on Tuesday called on the Philippine Air Force (PAF) to remain swift and responsive in serving the Filipino people, as the organization marked its 78th founding anniversary.

title: Metro Manila tops list of metro areas with worst traffic: study
body: Based on the 2023 Tomtom Traffic Index, it takes an average of 25 minutes and 30 seconds to drive for 10 kilometers in Metro Manila. This is 50 seconds slower than its previous record. Manila topped the list of 387 metro centers, overtaking Lima, Peru; Bengaluru, India; Sapporo, Japan; and Bogota, Colombia. Tomtom defines a metro center as 'a circle covering the city and rural areas in close proximity.' The circles were defined in the early years of the Traffic Index and with input from locals where possible, it said. The study said that on average, a motorist in Manila spent 240 hours driving in a year, with 117 of those hours stuck in traffic. They said this time could have been used reading 48 books. Traffic in Metro Manila worsens on Friday evenings, between 5 and 6 p.m., when it takes an average of 35 minutes and 30 seconds to drive 10 kilometers. Some motorists agreed with the findings of the study. 'Pahirap talaga, wala kaming kinikita dahil sa traffic. Parang lumalala eh dahil number 1 lalo pag rush hour siya grabe ang sasakyan, grabe ang traffic lalo sa EDSA, kapag traffic, nagbabawas ang gasolina, malakas sa gas,' said Orlando Magallanes, a taxi driver. Transportation analyst and urban planner Peter Paredes said this ranking could be attributed to the state of public transportation. 'Traffic congestion is a systemic issue, we cannot solve it at a snap of a finger. Numbers don't lie. Mataas din ang motorization rate based on data and traffic congestion impacts our economy based on previous studies,' Paredes said. 'One of the reasons is the current quality of our mass transport system though we acknowledge that new mass transit lines like the MMSP (Metro Manila Subway Project) and NSCR (North–South Commuter Railway) are already underway. There had been improvements, too, in MRT-3,' he added. He said vehicular volume could be reduced by encouraging the public to use mass transport systems, promoting active mobility like biking, maximizing waterways and rivers for alternative transport. 'In the long run, urban land development should be efficiently controlled and planned. We cannot separate land use and transport, palaging magkasama yan. Develop more growth centers outside Metro Manila, too, to lessen trips,' Paredes said. The Department of Transportation said it has projects to address the traffic problems in the metro. 'Our ongoing road transport infrastructure projects are directed at improving commuter experience while addressing worsening traffic in highly urbanized areas,' the DOTr said in a statement. 'The top ranking of Metro Manila in world traffic poses a challenge not just for DOTr but other agencies as well to be creative at finding lasting solutions to metro traffic. We will fast track road projects while collaborating with appropriate agencies with the help of the private sector,' it added. Meanwhile, the Metropolitan Manila Development Authority (MMDA) said it would would seek clarification on the data presented by Tomtom. 'Unang-una, hindi namin alam anong methodology na inemploy ng Tomtom to say na number 1 ang Metro Manila. Do they use GPS? Hindi ko alam gaano karami subscribers nila kasi dito, mas sikat Waze at Google maps. Twenty-four hours ba sila or peak hours?' MMDA Chairman Romando Artes said in a briefing. He pointed out there were discrepancies with MMDA data and Tomtom's data. Tomtom identified Quezon Avenue as the busiest street in the metro. But based on MMDA data, EDSA remains the busiest thoroughfare, followed by Commonwealth, while Quezon Avenue is only third. However, Artes admitted that Metro Manila has exceeded its capacity. 'Ang volume ng sasakyan over na ang carrying capacity, kasi ang EDSA 400,000 na ang cars daily, ang carrying capacity ay 300,000 lang,' he said. 'Pinagkakasya natin 3.6 million vehicles in 5000 kilometers ng kalsada. Ang 2023, may record sales ng mga sasakyan, 429,800 nabentang sasakyan noong 2023,' the official added. Major causes of traffic jams include illegal parking, vehicles fetching students from schools, road accidents and nosey onlookers, the MMDA said. 'Decade-old problem na ito, hindi ko masabi kung due to neglect or mistakes, inaaral ito ng administrasyon, whole of government approach para mabawasan ang traffic,' Artes said. MANILA — Metro Manila topped the list of metro areas with the worst traffic in 2023, according to a transportation data specialist.

title: Vico Sotto, contractors bare ‘diploma system,’ illegal schemes in DPWH projects
body: The 'diploma system' is when local officials give away contracts to certain construction firms — similar to how diplomas are distributed in graduation ceremonies — without undergoing proper evaluation and bidding, Sotto told ABS-CBN News. 'Binibigay lang na ganun ‘yung kontrata. Basically, parang may listahan ng projects na, ‘O ito sa iyo ito. Ito, sa iyo ito,’' the Mayor said as he recalled a bribery attempt in 2019. 'Noong bagong upo ako, first 100 days definitely noong bagong mayor ako, may mga pumunta sa opisina so in-entertain ko. A group of maybe 10, 20, 30 people, kinausap ko sila,' he said. 'Sila daw yung mga matatagal nang contractor sa city hall. Sabi nila, ‘Ito yung ginagawa dati. Kapag may listahan, may budget. Tinatanong kami kung ano yung magandang gawing project o tinatanong kami kung ano yung gusto naming gawin na infra project, siyempre may SOP,’' he said. SOP, in this context, stands for 'standard operating procedure,' a clear indication that the practice has been common for years, the Mayor said. When asked who was giving away the so-called 'diplomas' in the past, Sotto said: 'Ayon sa kwento nila, yung mayor mismo.' In separate interviews, two contractors who have worked in public projects in and out of Pasig confirmed that several local officials and businesses have been colluding to illegally siphon taxpayers’ money. Aside from using substandard materials to cheat the cost of several projects, some governors and congressional members have also been 'borrowing' the licenses and receipts of contractors with triple A or quadruple A ratings, said alias 'Bobby,' a contractor who spoke on the condition of anonymity due to fear for the safety of his family. 'Bobby' has worked in several St. Gerrard Construction projects, and has discussed the details of several deals with its founder Pacifico 'Curlee' Discaya II. '‘Yung iba ang kailangan lang sa kaniya (Discaya) ay yung kaniyang construction firm. Babayaran lang yung royalty,' Bobby told ABS-CBN News. 'Mostly sa Visayas at Mindanao yung mga project. Hinihiram ng mga governor, mga congressman pero ang gagawa noon sila Gov, sila Gong, ginagamit lang yung mga companies niya,' he said. Local officials have to borrow a private contractor’s license as Republic Act No. 6713 or the Code of Conduct and Ethical Standards for Public Officials and Employees prohibit government workers from having 'any financial or material interest in any transaction requiring the approval of their office.' The same law also mandates public officials not to 'own, control, manage or accept employment as officer, employee, consultant, counsel, broker, agent, trustee or nominee in any private enterprise regulated, supervised or licensed by their office unless expressly allowed by law.' 'Malaking pera yung royalty. Sa kanila na din yung hardware, sa kanila na kumukuha. Wala nang bidding,' Bobby said. The contractor said he was not privy as to which governors and congressional members Discaya has been transacting with, as the St. Gerrard owner never mentioned names when discussing the scheme. Corrupt contractors usually prepare two 'bodegas' (warehouses) for inspections, Bobby said. One storage area contains the construction materials that will meet the project requirements, while the other holds cheaper, substandard materials, he said. Several projects have to make do with substandard materials — such as 3-meter sheet piles instead of the standard 6-meter metals — as the original budget for these programs have already been reduced by more than half due to corruption, he said. 'Doon na namin dadalhin yung inspektor sa tamang sukat sa tamang bodega,' Bobby said. 'Mayroon pa siyang (Discaya) sinabi sa amin na kapag nag-inspeksyon, tagalan namin yung pagbabaon ng sheet pile para mainip,' he said. 'Sabi niya ang mga DPWH, mainipin. Hindi magtatagal nang isang oras ‘yan. Basta ipakita lang namin na nakatayo ‘yung isang 6 meters tapos kaunting pukpok-pukpok tapos huwag na namin ipakita yung mga susunod na ilalagay namin hanggang umalis. At saka namin itutuloy yung pag lubog ng 3 meters,' he said. Several contractors have affirmed this practice to ABS-CBN News, but they refused to sit down for a formal interview due to fear for their safety. 'Joey' — another contractor — claimed that several parties have been emboldened to engage in this scheme as they are in cahoots with officials from the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH). St. Gerrard Construction staff have been holding briefings to discuss how subcontractors can skirt laws and inspections, Joey told ABS-CBN News in a separate interview. 'Sabi niya, ‘Sige yung panambak walang problema. Hindi na kailangan diyan. Kumuha na lang kayo ng lupa lupa kung saan pwede,' he said, recalling his discussions with one of SGC’s engineers. 'Sa sheet pile, walang problema. Mag picture lang kayo na dalawang ibaon nang 6 meters yung iba putulin na natin sa 3 [meters],' he said. 'Nagkakalagayan po. Lagi po silang may parte kasi kapag yung tao, kunyare engineer na mag inspeksyon, kapag wala siyang parte, hindi talaga pipirma yun,' he said. Former DPWH Secretary Rogelio Singson has been pushing to have an independent body review his former agency’s projects, underscoring that the current review and audit systems within the government may already have been compromised. 'Forget COA muna. Time out sa COA,' Singson said, referring to the Commission on Audit. 'They should have seen this early on. Ngayon lang nag iingay, atsaka sila lumalabas?' he said. 'Saan yung COA auditors ninyo in the field? Baka naman ginood time na lang yung COA auditors imbis na mag-inspect,' he said. Sen. Panfilo Lacson earlier alleged that COA officials have been receiving between 0.5 percent and 1 percent of project costs in exchange for audit approvals, even for questionable projects. The COA earlier launched a review of DPWH-funded flood control projects in Bulacan after President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. questioned why only 15 contractors cornered over P100-billion worth of these programs between 2022 and 2025. But several lawmakers have also questioned the COA’s investigation, noting that one of its commissioners, Mario Lipana, and his family won several flood control projects in the said province. 'It’s systemic, if not endemic and that’s really the problem,' Sotto said. 'The problem isn’t that there are a few bad eggs, the problem isn’t that you have one or two bad Filipinos. The problem is that the problem is systemic if not endemic already at kailangan talaga may drastic na mangyari dito,' he said. The Pasig City Government has filed requests for the DPWH to release documents on flood control programs in the city to help sift through the data and check if projects have been implemented according to proper standards, Sotto said. 'Gusto namin tumulong sa pamamagitan ng imbestigasyon within our area of responsibility,' he said. 'Sa totoo, wala naman kaming complete list ng mga proyekto ng flood control, hindi lang flood control pero kahit anong infrastructure project, wala kaming complete list,' he said. 'Minsan nagcocoordinate sa amin kapag kailangan ng tulong sa right of way or relocation ng informal settlers or other residents for that matter pero kapag hindi nila kailangan yun. Madalas hindi nagcocoordinate so wala kaming control,' he added. Sotto hopes that after the flood control projects saga, 'there will be better coordination between national government agencies and local government units.' 'As much as possible, we don’t want to add additional layers to the bureaucracy, but at the same time, when we talk about things for instance like roads, flood control programs, hindi naman puwede na hindi alam ng LGU,' he said. 'Halimbawa, simple drainage, gagawin ng isang grupo, tatamaan yung existing project or minsan may proposed project kami, naglaan na kami ng pondo, yun pala may project din ang nasyonal so masasayang yung pondo namin,' he said. 'It’s not necessarily about corruption. It’s also about efficiency. It’s also about proper manning. It’s about proper coordination in order to deliver services better,' he said. 'I guess with everything that is happening now, I think it’s all for the better. Napapaisip na tayo ngayon.' Severe floods in Barangay Navotas East, Navotas City, on July 24, 2025. Jonathan Cellona, ABS-CBN News/File MANILA -- In the last six weeks, the Philippines has been rocked by a controversy over anomalous flood control projects, which first came to light during President Ferdinand Marcos Jr’s State of the Nation Address last July 28. The scandal has led to the firing of several public works officials, online shaming of government contractors and their families, and allegations that dozens of lawmakers pocketed billions of pesos from the scam. Here’s a timeline of events related to the controversy: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. orders an investigation into possible corruption in flood control projects, some of which faltered in July when monsoon rains driven by three cyclones spawned floods in various parts of the country. Sen. Panfilo Lacson reveals that 67 lawmakers in 2022 doubled as contractors for their own projects. President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr. names 15 contractors who bagged P100 billion worth of flood control projects since 2022. He noted most of the flood control projects were located in areas that were not among the most flood-prone places in the Philippines. Senate President Chiz Escudero admits receiving a P30 million donation from a government contractor in 2022, but denies helping the donor’s construction firm bag several flood-control projects after the elections. Senate Majority Leader Joel Villanueva claims no senator has been linked to the 15 contractors involved in questionable flood control projects. Public Works Secretary Manny Bonoan says the DPWH will audit infrastructure projects built by the 15 top government contractors. President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. visits two substandard anti-flood infrastructure in Calumpit, Bulacan, which were passed off as complete -- despite failure to deliver some parts of the contract. The contractors for the two projects are St. Timothy Construction Corporation and Wawao Builders. Oriental Mindoro Governor Humerlito Dolor discovers that a flood dike in Barangay Mulawin, Naujan town, which collapsed due to massive flooding, was filled mostly with sand , thin concrete, and supported by small steel bars. MG Samidan Construction and Development Corp., identified as the government’s 13th top contractor on flood-mitigation projects, had only P250,000 paid-up capital in 2019 , according to a document obtained by ABS-CBN News The Blue Ribbon Committee chaired by Sen. Rodante Marcoleta starts its inquiry into the flood control anomalies. Eight out of 15 government contractors invited to the hearing fail to attend . Public Works Secretary Manny Bonoan admits there are ghost projects , saying he had learned about the scam just recently. A P77 million flood mitigation structure in Barangay San Agustin in Hagonoy, Bulacan that should have been completed in July 2023 is discovered to be a ghost project . Oriental Mindoro Governor Dolor names contractors behind 9 flood control projects that collapsed last July. President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. discovers that a P55-million river wall project in Bgy. Piel, Baliuag, Bulacan that had already been paid for by the government has yet to be constructed . Sen. Panfilo Lacson delivers a privilege speech exposing how contractors, government officials and DPWH insiders divide up funds and build projects using substandard materials. A construction company linked to COA Commissioner Mario Lipana’s wife bagged nearly P200 million worth of flood control projects , according to government data. Oriental Mindoro Governor Dolor says a flood control project in Barangay Apitong, Naujan, Oriental Mindoro, which was funded three times in 2024, was never built. More flood control projects built by the DPWH Mimaropa regional office collapse. Pasig City Mayor Vico Sotto urges media to think twice about interviewing the Discaya couple who are allegedly willing to pay millions of pesos for a lifestyle interview. A DPWH district engineer is arrested for allegedly trying to bribe Batangas 1st District Rep. Leandro Leviste. Frustration over corruption turns into online ‘lifestyle checks’ of contractors and their ‘nepo babies’ The President orders a lifestyle check on all government officials amid the ongoing probe into flood projects Sen. Panfilo Lacson reveals a racket at the Philippine Contractors Accreditation Board (PCAB) to let unqualified contractors buy accreditation to operate and bag government contracts. Pasig City Mayor Vico Sotto urges the public to shun displays of wealth from government officials and those linked to tax-funded projects Secretary Manuel Bonoan resigns as Public Works Secretary , and erstwhile Transportation Secretary Vince Dizon takes over the DPWH. Sarah Discaya appears before the Senate Blue Ribbon Committee and confirms she owns 28 luxury cars . She also admits that she and her husband are owners of nine construction firms who sometimes bid against each other for government projects. New DPWH chief Vince Dizon orders the courtesy resignations of DPWH officials. Finance Secretary Ralph Recto says the economy lost over P100 billion to 'ghost' flood control projects from 2023 to 2025. The House TriComm kicks off its own probe on flood control anomalies. House Infra Comm members are ordered to disclose ties to flood control projects. Centerways Construction and Development Inc. President Lawrence Lubiano confirms giving a P30 million donation to the campaign of Senate President Chiz Escudero. Vince Dizon, the new DPWH chief scraps an internal committee formed to probe the flood control mess. The Bureau of Customs raids the Discaya compound and seizes their luxury vehicles. The Philippine Contractors Accreditation Board revokes the contractors' licenses of nine companies owned or controlled by flood control projects contractor Sarah Discaya. The DOJ issues an immigration lookout bulletin order (ILBO) against contractors and DPWH officials tagged in the flood control scam. Various groups protest outside St. Gerrard Construction in Pasig City, one of the companies owned by contractors Sarah and Curlee Discaya. DPWH finds ex-District Engineer Henry Alcantara guilty of grave misconduct over the Bulacan flood control mess A lawmaker urges Ako Bicol party-list Rep. Zaldy Co , former chair of the House appropriations panel, to answer allegations linking him to budget insertions and clarify his ties with Sunwest Inc. Pacifico and Sarah Discaya name lawmakers and officials linked to the flood control mess. These officials deny the allegations . Arjo Atayde says 'never dealt' with Discayas, viral photo not from a planned meeting Navotas City Rep. Toby Tiangco says Ako Bicol party-list Rep. Zaldy Co, the former chairperson of the House Committee on Appropriations, proposed billions of pesos in insertions in the 2025 budget. Contractors accuse Bulacan 1st District officials of involvement in a 'hiram lisensiya' scheme. Senate President Chiz Escudero, who had admitted to receiving a P30 million donation from a government contractor, is ousted, with former Senate President Tito Sotto taking his place . A new Senate majority is formed. Ex-DPWH engineer Brice Hernandez tags Senators Jinggoy Estrada and Joel Villanueva in flood control anomalies ; senators deny allegations. He also reveals photos of stacks of cash , which were allegedly distributed as kickbacks to various officials. Curlee Discaya testifies in the House InfraComm hearing, says he will no longer add names in alleged flood control kickbacks Sen. Panfilo Lacson says ex-DPWH Secretary Manny Bonoan’s daughter is a business partner of Globalcrete Builders, which bagged P2.195 billion in flood control projects from 2018 to 2024 in Bulacan. Massive corruption, luxury cars, and billions of pesos in budget insertions. The flood control scandal has become the biggest controversy to affect Philippine government in recent years. The PDAF scam, allegedly led by businesswoman Janet Lim Napoles and some lawmakers, was said to have funneled some P10 billion in government funds through bogus non-governmental organizations. ABS-CBN News/File MANILA — The scale of corruption in the Philippines' flood control projects is much bigger than the Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) scam that gripped the nation over a decade ago, Public Works Secretary Vince Dizon said Thursday. The PDAF scam , allegedly led by businesswoman Janet Lim Napoles and some lawmakers, was said to have funneled some P10 billion in government funds through bogus non-governmental organizations. 'This [flood control scheme] is a corruption on a scale that we have not seen in the past,' Dizon said. 'The Napoles scandal is like petty change compared to this,' he told ANC. In August, President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. revealed that only 15 contractors bagged about P100 billion or 20 percent of about 10,000 flood control projects from July 2022 to May 2025. He said that more than half of these government-funded infrastructure projects did not provide details on the type of structure being built. The President's revelation triggered investigations and led to the discovery of 'ghost' or non-existent projects and an alleged pay-off scheme between contractors, politicians, and Department of Public Works and Highways officials. Dizon made the comparison as he spoke about the need to make every individual involved in the corruption 'syndicate' accountable. He said he was 'overwhelmed' by the gravity of the corruption he had seen inside the DPWH, adding that it would be 'difficult' and would 'take time' to cleanse the agency. Flood control scandal harms PH reputation among business community, foreign countries: PCCI 'I can't do this alone. I need people to help me… It's a deeply seated problem that has been there for decades,' he said. Dizon said the DPWH was set to file complaints Thursday at the Office of the Ombudsman against some of the agency's personnel and contractors allegedly involved in the flood control project anomalies, which he called 'massive theft of people's money.' A timeline of the Philippine flood control scandal 'The Napoles scandal is like petty change compared to this,' Public Works Secretary Vince Dizon said. The 'diploma system' is when local officials give away contracts to construction firms sans proper evaluation and bidding, Sotto said. MANILA — A so-called 'diploma system' between contractors and some local government officials is among the many corrupt schemes that plague several public projects, Pasig Mayor Vico Sotto said as he bared several irregularities in the government’s infrastructure program. 'The Napoles scandal is like petty change compared to this,' Public Works Secretary Vince Dizon said.

title: PCG 'refuses' to use water cannon as China may 'justify' next level of aggression
body: Tarriela explained this to reporters after the House West Philippine Sea (WPS) bloc’s screening of the documentary 'Food Delivery: Fresh from the Philippine Sea,' which tackles the lives of fisherfolk working affected by the conflict at the WPS. During a panel discussion at the screening, Tarriela was asked why the Philippines has not taken 'revenge' and employed the same tactics as the China Coast Guard (CCG). When the media followed up on this, Tarriela answered: 'Ang tanong kasi natin diyan is what is the strategic objective? Why are we going to do that? I’ve always highlighted kung ang ating mga kababayang Pilipino every time na nararamdaman natin ‘yung galit every time na wina-water cannon tayo, just imagine ‘yung pagtitimpi rin ng ating mga personnel na nandoon mismo sa loob ng barko.' Tarriela also stressed the importance of maintaining the 'moral high ground' to show other countries that China is 'bullying' the Philippines. 'We also have our own water cannon, but we refuse to use that kasi the moment you use that, you’re going to get an opportunity for the People’s Republic of China to justify the next level of aggression. So we don’t need to give such opportunity. We have to maintain the moral high ground to show the international community na China ang nambu-bully,' Tarriela said. On Monday, a collision occurred involving two Chinese vessels and a Filipino patrol boat near Bajo de Masinloc in the WPS. According to Tarriela, there is a possibility of injuries among CCG crew. 'We reported that there is a possibility of injured Chinese Coast Guard crew because based on the video that we released, mayroong apat na tao sa unahan, but right after the impact, hindi na natin sila nakita. That’s actually the reason why the Philippine Coast Guard immediately contacted them through radio offering assistance to conduct search and rescue and also to provide medical assistance,' Tarriela said. When asked about how Congress could help them in relation to the WPS, Tarriela answered 'more vessels' as fisherfolk said they appreciate the presence of the PCG. And following pleas from fisherfolk during the screening for the Philippine government to speak with China so they could be allowed to sail to Scarborough Shoal, Tarriela said Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) has been active in 'engaging China diplomatically.' 'Ang atin namang gobyerno never stopped engaging the People’s Republic of China. I think the Department of Foreign Affairs has been very active in engaging China diplomatically, so that is something na ginagawa pa rin ng ating pamahalaan,' Tarriela said. When asked if he was open to a constitutional convention, especially if this means including the WPS in the constitution, Akbayan Rep. Chel Diokno of the WPS bloc said there are more important matters that need attention at this time. 'Ang Akbayan matagal nang kasama sa plataporma natin ‘yung pagbabago ng konstitusyon. Ang tanong ngayon ay is now the right time to change the constitution? Sa tingin ko po ay mas marami tayong dapat tugunan na mga problema, problema sa pagtaas ng presyo ng bilihin. Kasama doon ‘yung food security natin, ‘yung problema ng ating mga manggagawa, problema ng edukasyon, problema ng access to affordable health care bago natin pag-usapan ‘yung constitutional amendment o ‘yung tinatawag na cha-cha. Pero darating din dapat tayo sa panahon na ‘yun,' Diokno said. Diokno added, with the documentary, he is hoping lawmakers are given a look at the lives of fisherfolk at the West Philippine Sea.
