The statement made no mention of the United States intervening in the conflict overnight when American planes bombed nuclear facilities in Iran, a move that Tehran said would have 'irreparable consequences.' The Islamic republic on Sunday threatened US bases in the Middle East and warned that US forces could be attacked in retaliation for the unprecedented air strikes that the Pentagon said devastated Iran's nuclear program. 'Any country in the region or elsewhere that is used by American forces to strike Iran will be considered a legitimate target for our armed forces,' Ali Akbar Velayati, an advisor to Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, said in a message carried by the official IRNA news agency. The United States on Saturday began evacuation flights from Israel for American citizens and permanent US residents living in Israel or the West Bank. It also ordered staff at its diplomatic missions in Iraq and Lebanon to leave those countries. The US State Department issued a 'worldwide caution' for Americans on Sunday, saying the conflict in the Middle East could put those traveling or living abroad at an increased security risk. A classified preliminary US intelligence report has concluded that American strikes on Iran set back Tehran's nuclear program by just a few months -- rather than destroying it as claimed by President Donald Trump. US media on Tuesday cited people familiar with the Defense Intelligence Agency (DIA) findings as saying the weekend strikes did not fully eliminate Iran's centrifuges or stockpile of enriched uranium. The US State Department issued a 'worldwide caution' for Americans on Sunday, saying the conflict in the Middle East could put those traveling or living abroad at an increased security risk.

title: DOH prioritizes 'most vulnerable' for pertussis vaccine
body: The Department of Health said it is prioritizing the 'most vulnerable' population for the vaccines that protect against the highly transmissible pertussis infection or whooping cough. Watch more News on iWantTFC.com . Watch hundreds of Pinoy shows, movies, live sports and news. The Department of Health said it is prioritizing the 'most vulnerable' population for the vaccines that protect against the highly transmissible pertussis infection or whooping cough.

title: Fire hits same QC area months after previous blaze; around 100 families affected
body: According to the Bureau of Fire Protection (BFP), the incident was first reported at around 5:47 a.m. and was quickly raised to first alarm by 5:50 a.m., before reaching second alarm minutes later. Around 40 to 50 houses, mostly made of light materials and occupied by informal settler families (ISFs), were destroyed by the blaze. The fire was declared under control at 6:26 a.m. and was fully extinguished by 6:35 a.m., the BFP said. No injuries or fatalities were reported despite the extent of the damage. Fire Senior Inspector Rodrigo Delos Santos, commander of BFP Quezon City Station 1, said the area had also been hit by a fire in November 2025. 'Ito rin ’yung nasunog noong nakaraang November 2025,' Delos Santos said. He added that the fire originated from a two-story residential structure made of light materials, though the cause is still under investigation. 'Ang origin ay nagmula sa two-story light materials na residential [area]. Under investigation pa ’yung cause ng fire,' he said. Authorities also cited crowd congestion as a challenge during the firefighting operations. 'Sabi ko nga, dati nasunog ’to. ’Yung mga challenges na naranasan namin [noong] nakaraan, nalampasan namin. Although talagang hindi maiiwasan ang crowd. Marami talagang tao,' Delos Santos said. The BFP reiterated its call for vigilance, especially amid the hot weather. 'Tuloy-tuloy ang panawagan sa publiko: sobrang init ng panahon. Hindi maiiwasan kung magkakaroon ng sunog… Sana ang publiko ay maging alerto, maging vigilant para sa pag-iwas sa sunog,' he said. 'Sa ngayon, wala naman pong reported na nawawala o nasaktan.' Resident Cristina Zuniega recounted how the fire broke out: 'May narinig po ako na nagsisigawan dito… akala ko may nag-aaway… binalewala ko lang po. Tapos maya-maya, biglang may nagtakbuhan, sabi, ‘Sunog, sunog!’' She immediately woke up her family so they could evacuate. 'Ginising ko na ’yung mag-aama ko kasi tulog pa po eh. Tapos paglabas ko, nakita ko ’yung apoy, nasa kapitbahay na namin. Kadikit lang naman ’yung bahay… Kaya wala kaming nailigtas. Ito lang, mga dokumento lang po,' she said. Zuniega said this was the third time their area had been hit by fire since 2021: 'Pangatlo na po… April 2021, tapos November 2025, tapos ngayon po.' She admitted they have yet to recover from the previous fire. 'Mahirap po. Lalo’t wala kang maililigtas na gamit. Sobrang hirap. Nakakapanginig,' she added. 'Hindi pa po nakakabangon. Sa totoo lang, hindi pa nakakabangon last year. Wala pa, hindi pa masyado. Ito na naman. Panibagong dagok na naman. Wala e. Wala ka na maiiyak. Naubos na lahat,' patuloy niya. Another resident, Danilo Faeldonia, said they were unable to save any belongings. 'Paggising namin, malaki na ’yung sunog. Tulog pa kami nang mangyari,' Faeldonia said. 'Wala, sir. Inuna ko ’yung mga bata e,' he said. Faeldonia appealed for assistance: 'Tulad ng dati, sana matulungan kami uli makabalik at makatayo uli.' Authorities are continuing their investigation to determine the cause of the fire and the total amount of damage. QUEZON CITY — An estimated 100 families were affected after a fire hit a residential area along Mindanao Avenue in Barangay Bagong Pag-asa early Saturday, May 9, 2026.

title: Trump says 'getting along well' with China, downplays hostile acts
body: President Donald Trump on Sunday dismissed the importance of aggressive actions linked to Beijing including recent arrests of two Chinese nationals accused of smuggling a dangerous pathogen into the United States, saying 'that's the way the world works.' Trump told Fox News that Washington behaves in a similar way. 'You don't think we do that to them? We do, we do a lot of things,' he said on the 'Sunday Morning Futures with Maria Bartiromo' show. 'That's the way the world works. It's a nasty world.' Trump was responding to a question about how he viewed Chinese intellectual property theft, hacking of the US telecoms system and controversy around the Covid-19 pandemic. He likewise dismissed concerns over a case in which two Chinese nationals were accused last month of smuggling in a toxic fungus. Yunqing Jian, 33, and Zunyong Liu, 34, are charged with conspiracy, smuggling, false statements, and visa fraud. The US Justice Department said the pair conspired to smuggle into the United States a fungus called Fusarium graminearum that causes 'head blight,' a disease of wheat, barley, maize, and rice. Trump downplayed this saying, 'you don't know where that came from, though. I mean, did that come from the country, or is that three wackos that happened to carry something?' Referring to the ongoing US-China trade war, Trump appeared to signal he was satisfied, saying 'We're getting along well with China.' He added: 'I think getting along with China is a very good thing, but they are paying substantial tariffs.' This past week the White House signaled trade progress with China, with an official saying both sides have reached an understanding on issues including expediting rare earth shipments to the United States. After talks in Geneva in May, Washington and Beijing agreed to temporarily lower steep tit-for-tat tariffs on each other's products. President Donald Trump on Sunday dismissed the importance of aggressive actions linked to Beijing including recent arrests of two Chinese nationals accused of smuggling a dangerous pathogen into the United States, saying 'that's the way the world works.' US President Donald Trump (left) shakes hand with China's President Xi Jinping at the end of a press conference at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing on November 9, 2017. Fred Dufour, AFP/File

title: Woman dies, children hurt in Bahrain bomb blast - police
body: Shrapnel hit the car the woman was in, the police said, and security forces were investigating the scene of the attack in the village of East Eker, south of Bahrain's capital, Manama, according to an Interior Ministry statement. 'A terrorist act on Thursday claimed the life of a woman and injured three children who were with her in a car that was hit by shrapnel after a bomb exploded,' the director general of Manama's police directorate was quoted as saying by state media. Sporadic violence and bomb attacks largely aimed at Bahraini security forces have become the norm since mass, Shi'ite-led pro-democracy protests were put down by the government in 2011. In July, 2015 two policemen in the Shi'ite village of Sitra were killed in a bomb blast that authorities said involved the use of explosives smuggled from Iran. Tehran denies interference in Bahrain but openly supports opposition groups seeking greater rights for the Shi'ite majority. The human rights record of Bahrain has been criticized by the United States, Britain and rights groups. On Thursday, seven U.S. senators said in a letter to Secretary of State John Kerry the United States should be prepared to reconsider arms sales, if a recent crackdown on opposition continues. In June, authorities closed down the main Shi'ite opposition society, detained a prominent rights campaigner Nabeel Rajab and stripped the spiritual leader of the island's Shi'ite Muslim majority of his citizenship. Bahrain, which hosts the United States' Fifth Fleet, is seen by Sunni-ruled Gulf kingdoms as a strategic bulwark against Iranian influence. DOHA - A Bahraini woman died and three children sustained minor injuries when their car was hit by a bomb blast on Thursday that police said was carried out by 'terrorists' in southern Bahrain.

title: New OWWA chief Arnell Ignacio tests positive for COVID-19
body: In an interview with ANC Thursday, Ignacio said he has been quarantining for 4 days. 'I don't know where I got it kasi everyday I'm in the airport welcoming our OFWs (overseas Filipino workers),' he said. Ignacio said he would announce his plans about the agency as soon as he ends his isolation period. 'As soon as I can work they can expect that they can feel that OWWA will be around, will always be around for them,' he said. Malacanang said Wednesday Ignacio was appointed by President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. to lead OWWA. The television personality first served as OWWA deputy administrator in 2018 before resigning for personal reasons. He was reappointed in September of last year to the same post. Before his stint in OWWA, Ignacio was appointed by former President Rodrigo Duterte as the Philippine Amusement and Gaming Corp's assistant vice president for community and services department. The Department of Health on Wednesday reported 3,181 new COVID-19 cases, bringing the country's total confirmed coronavirus infections to 3,813,952. MANILA ( UPDATE ) — Newly appointed Overseas Workers Welfare Administration administrator Arnell Ignacio has tested positive for COVID-19.

title: US Embassy flags Chinese firm in Manila Bay reclamation
body: The US Embassy said China Communications Construction Co. is on the US Department of Commerce’s Entity List for helping the Chinese military build and militarize artificial islands in the South China Sea. According to US Embassy spokesperson Kanishka Gangopadhyay, they are in regular discussions with the Philippine government about the matter. The US Embassy also expressed concern about the project’s ties to China Communications Construction Co. 'We are also concerned that the projects have ties to the China Communications Construction Co., which has been added to the U.S. Department of Commerce’s Entity List for its role in helping the Chinese military construct and militarize artificial islands in the South China Sea. The company has also been cited by the World Bank and the Asian Development Bank for engaging in fraudulent business practices,' Gangopadhyay said in a statement. The US Embassy said it will 'continue to support high quality, sustainable, and transparent investments to benefit the Filipino people and will continue to engage with the appropriate authorities on this matter.' China Communications Construction Co. has yet to issue a statement on the issue, as of posting. MANILA - The United States government has expressed concern over 'potential negative long-term and irreversible impacts to the environment, the resilience to natural hazards of Manila and nearby areas, and to commerce' of the reclamation projects in Manila Bay.

title: Duterte catches up, ties Poe for lead: ABS-CBN survey
body: This was the result of the latest ABS-CBN survey conducted by Pulse Asia—which was also the first poll taken after the Supreme Court upheld Poe's candidacy for president. READ: SC: Poe qualified to run for president The poll was conducted two months before the May 9 elections—from March 8 to 13, 2016 nationwide on a sample size of 4,000—yielding a margin of error of +/- 1.5%. The respondents were asked 'If the coming 2016 election were held today, whom would you vote for as President of the Philippines?' and then they were shown a sample ballot and list of candidates as of February 15, 2016. Respondents were adults (18 and above) who were registered voters with biometrics. A total of 26% picked Poe, 25% picked Duterte, 22% picked Vice President Jejomar Binay, 20% picked Mar Roxas, 3% picked Senator Miriam Defensor Santiago, and 0.1% picked the late Congressman Roy Seneres. This poll has a bigger sample compared to the March 1-6 ABS-CBN Pre-Electoral National Survey also by Pulse Asia which had 2,600 respondents. In that survey Poe had a slight lead over Duterte (28%-24%). In the latest poll, Poe, Duterte and Binay statistically led in the National Capital Region (30%, 29% and 23%, respectively). The NCR poll was conducted from March 8-13 on a sample size of 450 and an error margin of +/-4.6%. Poe took Luzon (34%) against Duterte (16%), Binay (26%) and Roxas (15%). The poll in balance Luzon had 1,800 respondents (925 from north/central Luzon from March 8-12, and 875 from southern Luzon from March 8-13). Roxas dominated Visayas (36%) with Binay (21%) Poe (20%) and Duterte (18%) close to each other. The poll in Visayas was from March 8-12 on a sample of 850 (western: 200 from March 8-12; central: 275 from March 8-12; eastern: 200 from March 8-11; and Negros island region: 175 from March 8-12). Duterte was the runaway winner in Mindanao (46%) followed by Roxas (20%), Poe (15%), and Binay (14%). Mindanao had a sample of 900 from March 8-12 (western: 400 from March 8-12; northern: 300 from March 8-12; and southern: 200 from March 8-11) Poe and Duterte statistically led the biggest voting bloc class D (26% and 25%) versus Binay (21%) and Roxas (20%). Duterte took class ABC (35%) versus Poe (22%), Binay (18%), and Roxas (17%). Class E was almost evenly divided between the top four candidates. The same poll also tested who would be voters' second choices if their first choice candidate for whatever reason does not pursue his or her candidacy. Poe also appeared to be the top second choice among all candidates. Binay (27%) and Roxas (25%) would get the bulk of those whose first choice was Poe. Poe (33%) would end up with the biggest chunk of those whose first choice was Duterte, with the next biggest chunk going to Binay (22%). Poe (45%) would also get the lion's share of those whose first choice is Binay. The senator would also benefit the most from those whose first choice is Roxas and Santiago. In a statement, Poe expressed her gratitude to the Filipino people who continue to support her presidential bid. 'We owe it to them for making their voices heard on their choice for the next president. With about a month and a half remaining in the campaign period, there will be no letup in our efforts to get to the people to explain what Gobyernong May Puso can do for them. We will be steadfast in connecting with the people and earning the highest mandate come May 9,' she said. The camp of Binay, likewise, said the Vice President will not stop working harder and will continue to fight poverty. 'Natutuwa kami sa talagang mainit na pagtanggap sa ating Vice President kahit saan siya magpunta. Hindi natitinag ang matibay na suporta at pagtitiwala ng mga core groups at supporters ni Vice President at lalo pang magpupursigi ang mga ito upang palakasin ang kandidatura ni Vice President,' said Atty. Rico Quicho. The camp of Roxas, for his part, noted that the presidential race remains to be tight. They are confident that the Roxas-Robredo tandem will eventually win come the May 9 polls. 'Kita naman natin noong debate na si Mar ang may pinakamalinaw na plano kung saan dadalhin ang Pilipinas, may pinakamalinis na intensyon, at pinakahanda para sa pagkapangulo. Naniniwala kami na lalabas sa susunod na survey ang positibong reaksyon ng sambayanan sa pamumuno ni Mar,' Cong. Barry Gutierrez said. In the vice-presidential race, Senators Bongbong Marcos and Francis Escudero were statistically tied at the top, 25% vs 24%, with Congresswoman Leni Robredo at 20%. Senator Alan Peter Cayetano is at 13% followed by Senator Antonio Trillanes IV at 6% and Senator Gregorio Honasan at 5%. Marcos and Escudero were already tied at the top spot in the March 1-6 ABS-CBN Pre-electoral National Survey also by Pulse Asia which had 2,600 respondents. In the latest survey, Marcos and Escudero also statistically led in the NCR (35% vs 30%) and balance Luzon (29% vs 28%). Robredo was the runaway winner in the Visayas (34%) while no one had a clear lead in Mindanao. Marcos took Class ABC (31) and statistically led Class D with Escudero (27% vs 25%). Respondents were also asked to pick a second choice in case their first choice couldn't continue running for president. A big chunk (30%) of Marcos' voters will go for Escudero if he is out of the game. Marcos, Robredo and Cayetano seem to benefit if Escudero is out of the game. A big chunk (34%) of Robredo's voters will go for Escudero if he is out of the game. Escudero benefits the most if Cayetano and Trillanes are out of the election. Marcos benefits the most if Honasan drops out of the race. 'We are happy that our message of unity resonates and is being accepted by the people. I think we are moving in the right direction. We remain focused on the campaign and we will continue to work harder to get our message across to more people,' said Marcos. Robredo said: 'Makikita sa huling Pulse Asia survey ang pagtaas-baba ng ating mga kalaban samantalang within striking distance tayo. Hindi pa nakakapagdesisyon ang ating mga kababayan kaya ipagpapatuloy pa rin natin ang sipag sa pag-iikot upang maiparating ang ating adhikain at platapormang mabigyan ng oportunidad na umasenso ang lahat ng ating kababayan... Tiwala tayo na sa huli, maaabot natin ang minimithing tagumpay sa Mayo at higit na mapagsilbihan ang ating mga kababayan bilang inyong pangalawang pangulo.' Meanwhile, Vicente Sotto, Franklin Drilon, Kiko Pangilinan, Ping Lacson, Migz Zubiri, Sergio Osmena, Risa Hontiveros, Ralph Recto, Leila de Lima, and Dick Gordon, Manny Pavcuiao, Sherwin Gatchalian and Joel Villanueva are in the likely winning circle. (UPDATED) For the first time, Davao City Mayor Rodrigo Duterte joined consistent frontrunner Senator Grace Poe at the top spot of the presidential race.

title: 190 Filipino trafficking victims return home from Cambodia
body: The repatriates landed via Philippine Airlines flight no. 2P9222 at the Ninoy Aquino International Airport (NAIA) Terminal 1 at around 4:20 a.m., about an hour ahead of their original schedule, according to the DMW. DMW Secretary Hans Leo Cacdac said the group was initially composed of 191 overseas Filipino workers, but one woman was left behind in Phnom Penh for medical treatment. 'Ambassador Flerida Ann Camille Mayo and our team remain on the ground to monitor her condition,' Cacdac said during a press conference. The repatriates consisted of 95 males and 95 females. The woman who was not cleared to travel was undergoing dialysis, with her condition reportedly aggravated while in Cambodia, DMW Assistant Secretary Venecio Legaspi said. 'She is not yet allowed to travel until her condition stabilizes,' Legaspi said. The Filipinos were rescued from at least six different areas in Cambodia, with the majority coming from Phnom Penh. Others were located in Pailin, Koh Kong, Mondulkiri, Svay Rieng, and Siem Reap. Cacdac said the latest batch brought to 877 the total number of Filipinos repatriated from Cambodia since 2022. 'They came in one hour early, and our action fund is ready to provide immediate financial assistance,' he said. Upon arrival, the repatriates will be transported by the Overseas Workers Welfare Administration (OWWA) to temporary accommodation facilities, where documentation and processing will begin. The government will also conduct screening to determine their provinces of origin. The DMW said the repatriates would get medical services from the Department of Health, psychosocial support from the Department of Social Welfare and Development, and legal assistance from the DMW and the Inter-Agency Council Against Trafficking. To help reintegrate the victims, the DMW said it was coordinating with the Department of Labor and Employment and other agencies to provide employment and livelihood opportunities. Partnerships with private business process outsourcing firms are also being explored, including the possible holding of job fairs. Justice officials said the victims were mostly recruited online and were often transported through both formal and informal channels before being forced to work in scam operations. 'Some individuals are already being considered for criminal charges now that the victims are back in the country,' Justice Undersecretary Nicholas Felix Ty said. Cacdac warned Filipinos seeking overseas work to be vigilant against illegal recruitment, urging them to verify recruiters through the DMW and to be wary of job offers conducted solely through social media platforms. A total of 190 Filipino victims of human trafficking arrived in the Philippines from Cambodia early Thursday morning, the Department of Migrant Workers (DMW) said. MANILA — A total of 190 Filipino victims of human trafficking arrived in the Philippines from Cambodia early Thursday morning, the Department of Migrant Workers (DMW) said.

title: DSWD extends help to family of hazing victim Ahldryn Bravante
body: Digna Esguerra-Cabrera, Ahldryn's grandmother, expressed her gratitude saying, 'Nagbigay po sila ng tulong para doon sa funeral service at sinagot na nila yung sa punerarya.' The DSWD also provided financial assistance of P10,000 to the family. Cabrera said the DSWD also offered educational assistance for Ahldryn's two siblings and his 6-year old daughter. 'Malaking tulog po sakanila yun sa gastusin. Hindi naman nila kakayanin yung gagastusin...talagang walang pagkukuhanan,' Cabrera said. Ahldryn's mother is expected to arrive in Manila Friday morning from Oman, where she works as a domestic helper, with the assistance of OWWA. 'Kami po ay talagang lubusang nagpapasalamat sa lahat po ng nagpapahatid ng tulong...di namin kakayanin ito kung walang tulong ng bawat isa,' Cabrera said. Cabrera also expressed hope that the remaining suspects will surrender soon. 'Sana magdirediretso na rin yung mga pagsuko nung mga ilan na pinangalanan kasi pansamantala lang yung pagkalaya nila sa pagtatago nila. But at the end of the day kailangan na nilang managot sa kung ano man yung ginawa nila,' she said. MANILA - The Department of Social Welfare and Development in Calabarzon provided assistance to the grieving family of Ahldryn Bravante, who lost his life during fraternity initiation rites.

title: Sandiganbayan upholds dismissal of graft, malversation raps vs ex-Mexico, Pampanga mayor
body: MANILA — The Sandiganbayan’s Second Division upheld its dismissal of graft and malversation charges against former Mexico, Pampanga Mayor Teddy Tumang and others over alleged irregularities in the procurement of construction materials in 2009 and 2010. In a six-page resolution penned by Presiding Justice Geraldine Econg, the anti-graft court rejected the prosecution’s bid to overturn a previous ruling that granted Tumang and his co-accused a demurrer to evidence for 'lack of merit.' The prosecution argued that the court committed a 'gross misapprehension of facts' and 'grave abuse of discretion' when it ruled that there was no sufficient evidence presented that proved the accused acted with gross inexcusable negligence in awarding procurement contracts to Buyu Trading and Construction. However, the Sandiganbayan said it 'stands firm on its findings' because the prosecution failed to add new substantive arguments and sufficient evidence to convince the court to reconsider. 'It (prosecution) failed to persuade the Court that accused had malicious and deliberate intent to bestow unwarranted partiality upon Buyu,' the resolution read. The charges, filed by former Ombudsman Samuel Martires in 2023, alleged that the accused officials bypassed mandatory public bidding rules in purchasing construction materials supposedly meant for road repairs but were never delivered to the recipient barangay. In granting the demurrer, the court cleared Tumang, Buyu Trading and Construction owner William Colis, and other municipal officials of 64 counts of graft and seven counts of malversation of public funds. The hold departure orders were also lifted against the accused. The Sandiganbayan’s Second Division upheld its dismissal of graft and malversation charges against former Mexico, Pampanga Mayor Teddy Tumang and others over alleged irregularities in procuring construction materials in 2009 and 2010. Mexico, Pampanga Mayor Teddy Tumang. Mexico, Pampanga's local government website

title: Fil-Canadian father from Surrey wins record-breaking $80-million lotto max jackpot
body: According to the British Columbia Lottery Corporation (BCLC), 35 year old Justin Simporios couldn’t believe his eyes when he saw the winning numbers. 'It was 10:30 p.m. I saw that someone won $80 million in Surrey. I was joking and told my wife ‘we’re millionaires!’ and she told me to stop making that joke,' Simporios said in a BCLC press conference. 'After, I manually checked each number before scanning [the ticket] using the BCLC Lotto! App. I cried and shouted, ‘we’re millionaires!’'< He immediately woke up his wife to share the life-changing news — despite their daughter not feeling well at the time. 'She was in complete disbelief and a bit mad at me because our daughter wasn’t feeling well,' he said, 'but she agreed this was a good reason to wake her.' Simporios, who works as a health and safety officer, has since resigned from his job. More than the luxury, he says the win means the chance to lift up his loved ones. 'I want to help my family and my wife’s family. I’ll pay off my sister’s medical school debt and help my mom retire early – just giving my family a head start in life.' Helping others is also part of his bigger plan. 'I’ve struggled before and needed help. Even if I can give an ounce of happiness, I want to help where we can,' he added. Simporios, who bought the lucky ticket at the Walmart Supercentre in Central City on King George Boulevard in Surrey, also plans to check a few items off his personal bucket list — such as watching an L.A. Lakers game in person before LeBron James retires, and returning to the Philippines for a family reunion. 'My wife and kid have never visited!' As for what truly matters now? 'The biggest change will be having more time with my wife and family. We want to live with a purpose – to help the community around us,' he said. A Filipino-Canadian father from Surrey has just made history as Canada’s biggest solo lottery winner, after claiming a staggering $80-million Lotto Max jackpot from the May 9, 2025 draw. SURREY, B.C. — A Filipino-Canadian father from Surrey has just made history as Canada’s biggest solo lottery winner, after claiming a staggering $80-million Lotto Max jackpot from the May 9, 2025 draw.

title: Arrest warrant out vs Garma, 4 others over 2020 assassination of PCSO exec
body: Based on the arrest warrant dated September 13, Garma, along with former National Police Commission (NAPOLCOM) commissioner Edilberto Leonardo, and police officials Jeremy Causapin, Santie Mendoza, and Nelson Mariano, are charged with murder and frustrated murder. Barayuga was shot dead inside a moving vehicle by an unidentified man on a motorcycle on July 30, 2020 in Mandaluyong City. His driver survived the attack. According to PNP spokesperson Brig. Gen. Randulf Tuaño, the Philippine National Police will fully enforce the warrant, regardless of the status or former positions of those involved. 'Ito po ay recommended na no bail... Hindi po tayo magke-create ng tracker group or special group sapagkat nanatili na meron tayong task group manhunt... kahit anuman kaso, malaki man o maliit, we will all implement the warrant of arrest,' he said. Of the five accused: Mendoza is currently in a floating status under the custody of the Personnel Holding and Accounting Unit (PHAU), Mariano has been dismissed from service, and Causapin reportedly resigned. Meanwhile, the Criminal Investigation and Detection Group (CIDG) is verifying information that Garma has left the country. 'Ang information po na nakuha ng CIDG, sila po ay umalis ng bansa pero madali lang naman pong i-verify. Kukuha po tayo ng certification coming from the Bureau of Immigration para ma-certain po natin ‘yung mga report na natanggap ng CIDG kaugnay ng kanyang presence sa Pilipinas o saan man,' said Tuaño. A Mandaluyong court has issued warrants of arrest for former PCSO general manager Royina Garma and 4 others over the assassination of PCSO board secretary Wesley Barayuga in 2020. MANILA (2nd UPDATE) — The Mandaluyong Regional Trial Court (RTC) branch 279 has issued a warrant of arrest against former Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) general manager Royina Garma and four others in relation to the killing of PCSO board secretary Wesley Barayuga in 2020.

title: De Lima: Don't blame DOJ for Mamasapano case delay
body: Speaking to ANC's Headstart Tuesday, de Lima claimed the justice department did its part by immediately investigating the incident and identifying 90 perpetrators in the attack against SAF troopers. 'I created a joint NPS or National Prosecution Service. NBI special investigation team located possible witnesses. We thought at first we could not locate material witnesses. We did, the one we codenamed as 'Marathon' who shed light on certain events in the Tukanalipao cornfield,' she said. De Lima, who is running for senator, said it was hard for them to advance the case because of challenges like finding more witnesses and getting the cooperation of the Moro Islamic Liberation Front (MILF), whose members, alongside the Bangsamoro Islamic Freedom Fighters (BIFF), were among those being linked to the killings. 'We did our job but there were a lot of challenges because one, finding really more witnesses and second, getting the cooperation also. We tried to get the side of the MILF. We had a dialogue with them but we were hoping that they could provide further witnesses especially in the case also of the allegations that there are civilian casualties, there are MILF casualties,' de Lima said. She added the case they filed against the 90 suspects underwent preliminary investigation. Most of the respondents did not appear and are nowhere to be found. De Lima also explained why no high ranking MILF official was charged for the killing of SAF troopers. 'Was it proven that there’s an order from anyone in the MILF hierarchy to continue firing and killing the SAF? There was none so how could we charge anyone from the MILF? We could only proceed from the evidence that cropped up,' she said. Meanwhile, de Lima said nothing new came out from the reopening of the Senate probe on the Mamasapano clash, noting Senator Juan Ponce Enrile's plan to pin down President Benigno Aquino III for his alleged active participation on the Mamasapano incident did not succeed. She said Enrile's points backfired and even showed how former SAF chief Getulio Napeñas should shoulder the blame for the bloody incident. 'We all expected, the public expected some testimonial of documentary bombshell that would pin down the President as desired by Senator Enrile, but there was no bombshell. Any such bombshell turned out to be actually Director Napeñas because he was the one pinned down in that investigation by the AFP and even by the PNP,' de Lima said. 'The reopening of the investigation did not prove those things that Senator Enrile intended to prove, nag-back fire pa nga [it even backfired], because in spite of the badgering of all the AFP commanders, the PNP officials categorical stated that there was no such order from the President to stamp down or not to rescue. The President is being given inaccurate and incomplete information about the true state of things on the ground by former Director-General Purisima and also by Napenas,' she added. Former justice chief Leila de Lima on Tuesday said the Department of Justice should not be blamed for the slow pace of the cases filed against suspects linked to the deaths of Special Action Force (SAF) troopers in Mamasapano, Maguindanao last year.

title: Attacks hit UAE after Iran blames it for strikes
body: Missiles and drones rained on the United Arab Emirates on Monday, killing one civilian, disrupting the world's busiest airport and sparking a fire at a crucial oil hub. Iran has kept up strikes on the UAE since war erupted in the Middle East, disrupting commercial air travel and targeting energy installations on both sides of the Strait of Hormuz. The attacks have piled economic pressure on the oil-rich country, which also serves as a hub for international travel. In the usually quiet northern emirate of Umm Al Quwain, a drone attack damaged an unspecified building but inflicted no casualties, authorities said. The slew of attacks came a day after Tehran's top diplomat, Abbas Araghchi, said that US bases in the Middle East had been used to launch air raids and that missiles had been fired from the UAE to strike Iran's Kharg Island. UAE officials have denied the claim. In the eastern emirate of Fujairah, a drone attack on oil infrastructure sparked a fire, authorities said, reporting no injuries and adding that 'efforts continue to bring it under control'. The blaze came days after an AFP journalist saw smoke rising from a major energy installation in Fujairah. A pipeline to the emirate allows a large proportion of the country's oil exports to bypass the Strait of Hormuz, a vital waterway shut by Iran in response to US-Israeli attacks that sparked the war. The UAE's state-owned oil giant ADNOC has halted the loading of oil into storage tanks at their Fujairah facility, a source with knowledge of the operations told AFP, following repeated strikes on the energy installation there. 'ADNOC oil loading at Fujairah is suspended,' the source told AFP. A Palestinian civilian was killed on the outskirts of the Emirati capital Abu Dhabi when a missile hit a car, the state media office said. The UAE has reported seven deaths since the war began, including five civilians and two military personnel, who died in a helicopter crash blamed on a technical malfunction. Dubai Airports said earlier that flights were gradually resuming at the world's busiest international hub, following a nearby fuel tank fire sparked by a 'drone-related incident'. The airport has been targeted by several attacks since Iran began its Gulf campaign. Two witnesses told AFP they saw a thick plume of black smoke rising from the direction of the airport around 10:00 am (0600 GMT), hours after the attacks. A witness at the airport said that passengers awaiting their flights had been evacuated to a lower floor for several hours after the attack. 'It has been a difficult few weeks hearing explosions regularly, but the Iranian attacks followed me in my last hours before I could fly back home,' the witness told AFP. Iran has fired more than 1,900 missiles and drones at the United Arab Emirates, more than any other country targeted by Tehran since the start of the Middle East war. The strikes have upended travel plans in the financial hub, despite its air defence intercepting the vast majority of projectiles. Tehran has taken aim at US assets in the Gulf countries, as well as civilian infrastructure, including landmarks, airports, ports and oil facilities. AFP journalists also heard explosions in the Qatari capital Doha, where foreign ministry spokesman Majed al-Ansari told reporters that talks with Iran were only possible if it stops its Gulf attacks. Neighbouring Saudi Arabia's defence ministry also reported intercepting more than 60 drones since midnight on Monday in the east of the country. Qatar said later that it was targeted by 14 missiles and a number of drones launched from Iran on Monday, adding that it intercepted all but one missile, which 'landed in an uninhabited area without causing any losses'. Meanwhile, Bahrain said it has intercepted 350 Iranian missiles and drones since the start of the war. Smoke rises from the direction of an energy installation in the Gulf emirate of Fujairah on March 14, 2026. AFP

title: European Parliament members meet Remulla
body: They arrived Thursday afternoon and had a closed-door meeting with Remulla and other officials of the justice department. In a statement, the DOJ said the visit of the European Parliament Subcommittee on Human Rights took place in the context of the open and regular engagement on human rights issues between the Philippines and the European Union. The DOJ also said they aim to further engage in a constructive dialogue and cooperation on human rights. The delegation of the European Union in the country visits different countries every year using their own budget as part of the European Parliament subcommittee’s core work, the DOJ added. Also present during the meeting are officials of the Department of Interior and Local Government and other agencies of the Philippine government. According to DOJ, representatives of various government agencies affirmed the importance of continued dialogue and cooperation with the European Union. The delegation arrived in the country Wednesday and will stay until Friday. The European Parliament is one of the European Union’s legislative bodies and seven institutions. MANILA - Members of the European Parliament on Thursday made a courtesy call on Philippine Justice Secretary Jesus Crispin 'Boying' Remulla at the office of the Department of Justice in Manila.

title: UP Diliman choral conductor faces sexual abuse accusations
body: Philippine Collegian, the university’s official student publication, reported March 6 that several alumni have spoken about the alleged sexual harassment by Edgardo Manguiat, the founder and conductor of the UP Singing Ambassadors. The publication reported that a viral post on the UP Freedom Wall Facebook page exposed 'a ’singing organization’ for an alleged tradition of abuse, hazing, manipulation, bullying, among others.' This, it said, prompted more victims to come forward, including two alumni who first met Manguiat in the 2010s when they were 17 and 19. Both were said to have fallen under Manguiat’s 'manipulation.' The victims also reportedly said he claimed to have erectile dysfunction and had been 'advised by doctors to ‘release’ often to avoid prostate cancer.' One victim said Manguiat 'asked [him] for help to ejaculate.' Another alleged that he was invited to sleep in the same room with Manguiat and made to touch the conductor's nipple. The Collegian reported that alumni reached out to the organization’s current officers to ensure members’ safety. They also attempted to file a case with the Office of Anti-Sexual Harassment, but were turned away because of limited jurisdiction over 'persons who are not employees, officials, faculty, staff, students, or otherwise members of the UP community.' In an online exchange with ABS-CBN News, Manguiat declined comment, saying he was waiting for advice from his lawyer. He said, however, that 'definitely, those are grave accusations that demand legal action.' UPSA's current officers said a formal legal investigation is underway, but alumni victims countered that no one contacted them about their cases, according to the Collegian. The report also said Manguiat was seen at a reception dinner for UP President Angelo Jimenez on January 27 despite going on complete leave by mid-January. The Collegian reported that Manguiat's 'persistent' presence at events despite the allegations 'underscored the urgent need to examine the university’s safeguards, or lack thereof, that leave students vulnerable and cases unresolved.' The student paper added that the lack of options to hold Manguiat accountable within the university system 'reveals a structural gap in the protection of university constituents.' It added: 'When authority figures operate within university spaces but fall outside formal employment, oversight becomes blurred.' Philippine Collegian, the university’s official student publication, reported March 6 that several alumni have spoken about the alleged sexual harassment by Edgardo Lumbera Manguiat, the founder and conductor of the UP Singing Ambassadors. MANILA — A choral conductor in a singing organization at the University of the Philippines - Diliman is facing accusations of sexual abuse from its alumni.

title: Over 7,000 remain in evacuation sites in Marikina
body: Despite the water level subsiding, Mayor Marcelino Teodoro advised residents living in low-lying areas not to return to their homes yet as torrential rains are expected to continue in the coming days. 'At 14.9 [meters above sea level], the alert level is still on and we strongly advise all residents in the low-lying areas to remain on safer grounds,' he said. Some 4,000 relief packs have set to be distributed to the evacuees across 11 centers today. The local government said donors should direct their donations to evacuation centers, preferably items such as hygiene kits, medicines, and infant formula. City workers have also arrived at the riverbanks to clean up the mud left at the ebb of the river. Authorities said that if the weather improves, the rest of the evacuees can return home. On Friday evening, Alert Level 1 was raised in the city after the river reached 15 meters above sea level, and all floodgates have been opened. Teodoro said, they are also monitoring the amount of rainfall in the mountain areas surrounding Marikina 'because that would greatly affect the level of the river.' ''Kapag malakas ang ulan sa taas, may isang oras hanggang dalawang oras because of the water retention capacity of the Marikina water shed,' he said. He added, class suspension announcements will be made within the day. More than 1,000 families or roughly 7,000 individuals are still in evacuation centers in Marikina City despite the water level of the Marikina River subsiding to 14.9 meters. MANILA - Around 1,472 families or 7,069 individuals are still in evacuation areas in Marikina City even after the water level at the Marikina River has eased.

title: 2 Chinese arrested over kidnapping of 3 foreigners in Batangas
body: Philippine National Police (PNP) spokesperson B/Gen. Jean Fajardo said the two male suspects, aged 27 and 29, were apprehended at a police checkpoint in Bacoor, Cavite, on Saturday, May 3. In a video obtained by ABS CBN News from a CCTV and amateur footage, police can be seen intercepting a pickup truck at an intersection in Barangay Bayanan. Officers were forced to shoot at the vehicle’s tires after the driver refused to stop and nearly rammed their vehicle behind. The driver attempted to flee on foot but was eventually caught by responding officers. 'Positively identified po sila ng mga kidnap victims natin,' Fajardo told reporters in a telephone conference. (They were positively identified by our kidnap victims.) According to Police Lieutenant Colonel John Paolo Carracedo, chief of Bacoor Component City Police Station, the alert came from the Philippine National Police Anti-Kidnapping Group (PNP-AKG), who informed them that the vehicle was driven by those involved in the abduction of two Chinese nationals and one Korean national earlier on May 2 in Nasugbu, Batangas. 'They evaded, hindi sila tumigil, tapos muntik pang banggain yung checkpoint natin. They alerted us tumawag sa TOC namin we tried to hanapin, hindi sa mismong checkpoint siya na-flag down... we tried harangan 'yung sasakyan then yun na nga nangyari,' said Carracedo. According to a news release, police recovered a .45 caliber pistol, a 9mm handgun, several rounds of ammunition, and items described as 'directly linked to the kidnapping incident' during the arrest. The suspects are facing charges of kidnapping, illegal possession of firearms, and other related offenses. They were presented before the Department of Justice on Tuesday for inquest proceedings. Immigration-related charges are also being prepared in coordination with the Bureau of Immigration. The PNP said the suspects were involved in the abduction of two Chinese nationals and one Korean national in Barangay Balaytigue, Nasugbu, Batangas, during the early morning hours of May 2. They were with two Filipino companions at the time. 'Papunta sana sila, sir, sa Punta Fuego diyan po sa may Nasugbu, Batangas for fishing, pero finlag down po sila allegedly ng more or less pitong armadong kalalakihan, at allegedly, ’yong tatlo po doon ay nakasuot ng police uniform,' Fajardo said. (They were on their way to Punta Fuego in Nasugbu, Batangas, for fishing when they were allegedly flagged down by around seven armed men, three of whom were reportedly wearing police uniforms.) The two Filipinos were later released separately in Tagaytay City and Naic town in Cavite. Fajardo said the victims’ families paid an undisclosed amount of ransom in cryptocurrency in exchange for their release. 'Lahat po itong negotiation ay without the knowledge ng ating mga pulis dahil dumirekta ng communication ’yong mga kidnappers sa pamilya ng mga biktima, gamit ang kanilang sariling cellphones,' the police official said. (All of the negotiations happened without the knowledge of our police because the kidnappers communicated directly with the victims’ families using the victims’ own cellphones.) The victims were found with bruises, indicating they were physically harmed during captivity. PNP Chief Police General Rommel Marbil confirmed that the incident is linked to Philippine Offshore Gaming Operator (POGO) activities. Authorities are still searching for other suspects, including the driver of the kidnapped group and six Filipino accomplices. 'More or less related po dun sa POGO 'yung reason ng kidnapping yun,' said Marbil. -- with a report from Jeff Caparas, ABS-CBN News MANILA — The 'resurgence' of kidnap-for-ransom cases in the Philippines could hurt the nation's economy, an anti-crime advocacy group warned Monday. The Philippine National Police in recent months recorded scores of kidnapping cases , some of which involved Chinese nationals as victims. Recently, the country was gripped by the kidnapping and murder of Filipino-Chinese businessman Anson Que, who was found dead in Rodriguez, Rizal. PAOCC to share info on suspected mastermind in abductions of foreigners While kidnappings are a 'personal tragedy' for the victims, they are also a 'direct blow [to] our economic future,' Arsenio Evangelista, president of Volunteers Against Crime and Corruption, told ANC. Evangelista said that 'when peace and order is down, there will be a capital flight amongst the businessmen.' Capital flight refers to the phenomenon in which large amounts of money are sent out of a country because of fears about its economy. He also pointed out that because the Philippines is a tourist-driven country, the Marcos administration should win 'back the trust and confidence' of foreigners. Early this month, the Chinese Embassy in the Philippines issued an advisory for Chinese citizens in the country over what it called 'unstable public security.' The embassy claimed that Chinese citizens and businesses have been 'frequently interrogated and harassed' by Philippine authorities, adding that security risks faced by Chinese nationals have also increased. Family says Anson Que had no business ties with POGOs DOJ looking into POGO links in Anson Que kidnap-slay case President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. last week ordered the police to conduct a thorough investigation into the kidnapping of steel magnate Que. Palace Press Officer Claire Castro in a Palace briefing vowed that the government will act on the case. Filipino-Chinese business leaders have expressed concern over the kidnappings and called for swift justice for Que and his driver. Head of PNP Anti-Kidnapping Group relieved pending investigation —with reports from Pia Gutierrez and Rose Carmelle Lacuata, ABS-CBN News The 'resurgence' of kidnap-for-ransom cases in the Philippines could hurt the nation's economy, an anti-crime advocacy group warned Monday. MANILA (UPDATE) — The Philippine National Police Anti- Kidnapping Group (PNP-AKG) has filed charges against 3 suspects involved in the kidnapping and killing of Anson Que and his driver, Armanie Pabillo. 'Nakakasira sa bayan': Fil-Chinese bizman wants death penalty for kidnappers Que, also known as Anson Tan, and Pabillo were first reported missing last March 29, after leaving Que's office in Valenzuela. A ransom money of $20 million was demanded by the kidnappers, and the family of Que paid, in multiple tranches, P200 million in crypto currency. The bodies of Que and Pabillo were eventually discovered in Rodriguez, Rizal last April 9. PNP spokesperson Police Brigadier General Jean Fajardo said in a press briefing that authorities first implemented a search warrant in a house in Meycauayan, Bulacan where Que and Pabillo were reportedly held and killed. 'The family of Anson Tan received a message demanding $20 million as ransom money... We discovered that this crime had been planned months in advance, starting January of this year. The suspects used a Chinese female contact to lure the businessman [in Bulacan] and the meeting initially planned last March 28, but was carried out a day after,' Fajardo said. 'Natunton po natin yung bahay po na yun, nung sinundan po natin yung mga cctv footage. Iba sa mga nakuha po nating cctv, around march 29. Si [suspect] na po ang nagda-drive ng Lexus,' she added. Backtracking of CCTV footage in the area resulted to the identification of two Filipino suspects who allegedly killed and kidnapped the victims. Authorities recovered some of the victims' belongings inside the said house, together with other items like rope, handcuffs, duct tape, and other items believed to be used in killing the victims. A follow up operation resulted in the arrest of the two Filipino suspects in Bgy. IV in Roxas Palawan, at around 3:30 am, April 18. The third suspect, who is a Chinese national, surrendered to authorities on Saturday morning. Initial investigation revealed that the Chinese suspect was previously arrested for Kidnapping in Angeles City last November 2009. He is also linked to five other kidnapping cases in other areas in Metro Manila in 2022 and 2024. Kidnap-for-ransom 'resurgence' could hurt economy, VACC warns The two Filipino suspects served as assistant and driver to the detained Chinese. 'Sa pagkakahuli po kay [suspect], mayroon pa po tayong lima pang kidnapping case na maso-solve po dahil involved din po si [suspect] doon sa mga insidente po na yan na kung saan, isa sa mga ginamit po niya ay yung Ford Everest po na ginamit sa pagtapon ng bangkay nila Ginoong Anson tan at Ginoong Pabillo. Yung isa pong sasakyan din diyan sa Bulacan ay isa rin po sa ginamit niya sa lima pa pong kidnapping case,' Fajardo said. Fajardo said that the suspects have undergone inquest proceedings and are facing two counts of kidnapping for ransom with homicide. When asked if the incident was POGO-related, Fajardo said, 'May tinitingnan po kaming anggulo na posibleng POGO-related ito, pero maliban pa po doon ay meron pa po kaming isa pa pong anggulo na we have very solid lead na tinitingnan. Na hindi lamang po kidnapping for ransom ang pakay ng grupo na ito noong kuhanin po nila si Anson Tan.' A follow-up operation is still ongoing for the two other Chinese nationals who are said to be the masterminds behind the kidnapping. 'Maliban po kay [Chinese suspect] ay may dalawa pa pong Chinese national na kasama sa lugar na pinuntahan po ng ating mga biktima,' Fajardo said. PNP disputes rumored kidnapping of 'high-profile' business personalities In a statement, the family of the slain businessman thanked law enforcers for the arrest of the suspects. 'The family of the deceased... would like to express its heartfelt gratitude and appreciation to President Ferdinand Romualdez Marcos Jr.... and to all men and women of the police force who tirelessly made their best efforts to arrest the perpetrators in the abduction and murder of Anson Tan or Anson Que,' the family's lawyer said. The Philippine National Police (PNP) on Saturday announced that three suspects involved in the kidnapping and killing of Anson Que and his driver are now under their custody. MANILA — The Department of Justice is looking into alleged Philippine offshore gaming operator (POGO) links behind the kidnapping and killing of steel magnate Anson Que and his driver. DOJ Secretary Jesus Crispin Remulla said this was brought up during his meeting with the Philippine National Police and the National Bureau of Investigation on Friday. There were reports alleging that POGO bosses were demanding a refund from Que. Remulla said they are looking into all possible motives behind the murder. Que and his driver were found dead in Rodriguez, Rizal, the Philippine National Police said Thursday, two weeks after the victims went missing. Palace: Gov't will not 'sleep on' kidnapping, killing of businessman Anson Que 'That is also at the range of our conversation earlier, but we cannot release everything that we talked about, it’s not executive privilege it’s just these are operational secrets, we cannot divulge right now,' said Remulla. 'It’s a different kind of kidnapping, hindi lang ito kidnapping as we see it. It’s more than that,' said PNP Chief P/Gen. Rommel Marbil. 'Sabi ko nga, may malaki kasing twist eh, so hanggang doon lang po, after we finished the investigation and filing of cases doon lamang po kami magsasalita what really transpired during the whole process of negotiation and sa kidnapping po.' 'We have theories and we have suspects, yung theory siyempre why the crime is committed by whom. Yung dalawang tanong na iyan, we are working on it already,' Remulla said. 'We know what happened, we know what’s happening but we need the help of the community para maka-act kami accordingly sa mga problemang darating.' The DOJ said they will create a task force that will be composed by the PNP and NBI. 'Ang aim nito is to have a higher visibility leadership that people can talk to pag mayroong problema. Kasi ang problema when this things happen, a lot of them don’t trust marami hong doubt about law enforcement and government ang maraming tao, they rather do it themselves,' said Remulla. 'With this TF that will be crated by our secretary, napakaganda na iyan. Makakapagtulungan kami, di na ako mahihiyang magtanong kay PNP Chief, di na rin siya mahihiyang magtanong sa akin,' said NBI Director Jaime Santiago. Santiago also said there is no need for the Chinese and business community be afraid. 'Don’t be alarmed, nasa taas pa rin kami ng situation, with our Secretary of Justice, huwag po kayong mag-alala sa mga kababayan natin sa Chinese community huwag po kayong mag-alala, gagawa po kami ng aksyon,' Santiago said. Dr. Cecilio Pedro, who represents the business community, said they are confident with law enforcement. 'We are confident that this issue be addressed very soon, sooner than soon, kasi natatakot yung business community dahil po sa kidnapping na ito. Kasi this is very unique eh, di lang kidnapping eh, binayaran na pinatay pa,' said Pedro. The DOJ said a hotline will be created to receive matters regarding kidnappings and will be activated next week. The DOJ also held a meeting with the Chinese and business community regarding kidnappings. 'There were some unreported incidents, many families chose not to cooperate with law enforcement, and we only found out kapag tapos na,' said Remulla. 'In the very end pag hindi tayo nag-cooperate, di natin mahuhuli yung gagawa, ipapain lang tayo lang tayo lahat sa kanilang gawain, kailangan talaga cooperation ng community at enforcement.' Remulla said a special court will also be created to handle kidnapping cases. 'Special courts to be drawn up just to hear cases of this nature kasi urgent concern ito sa ating bansa, it is a national secuity issue and economic issue and as issue as well being of Filipinos,' he said. The Department of Justice is looking into alleged POGO links behind the kidnapping and killing of businessman Anson Que and his driver. The family of steel magnate Anson Que on Saturday denied allegations that he had links to Philippine Offshore Gaming Operators (POGOs). Que and his driver were found dead in Rodriguez, Rizal, two weeks after the victims went missing. 'The family of the late Anson Tan (Anson Que) firmly disputes allegations that their father was involved in POGO transactions. They have no rental property in Bulacan to speak of,' the family's legal counsel Atty. Jose Christopher 'Kit' Belmonte said in a statement. 'We appeal to the public to remain critical of misleading news and thank everyone for their support. The family will continue to fully cooperate with the Philippine National Police even as they request that they be accorded privacy during this time of grief and mourning.' Que's legal name is Anson Tan, but he was known socially as Anson Que. 'Mr. Tan has been engaged in legitimate business for decades and is a stalwart member of the Filipino Chinese business community and is known for his charitable work. During his lifetime, he stayed away from shady dealings and only did business with people he knew and trusted,' said Belmonte. Justice Secretary Crispin Remulla earlier said the POGO link was brought up during his meeting with the Philippine National Police and the National Bureau of Investigation on Friday. Authorities suspect that an alleged POGO operator was behind the kidnapping and killing of Que after reports surfaced alleging that POGO bosses were demanding a 'refund' from the businessman. Remulla said they are looking into all possible motives behind the murder. MANILA — President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr. has vetoed a proposed law granting Filipino citizenship to controversial Chinese national Li Duan Wang, saying he cannot ignore alarming and revealing warnings raised by relevant national agencies. Palace Press Officer Claire Castro quoted Marcos Jr.’s veto message where he said that he could not set aside the red flags raised by Philippine authorities on Wang’s character and influence, which he found 'full of ominous and dire consequences, if not of a clear and present danger.' 'Ayon sa Pangulo, ang ipagsawalang-bahala ang babalang ito ay tila pagsuway sa ating tungkulin sa sambayanang Pilipino. Ang Filipino citizenship ay isang pribilehyo at hindi ipinamimigay nang basta-basta. Hindi din ito dapat gawing kasangkapan upang pagbigyan ang pagsulong ng mga kaduda-dudang interes,' Castro said in a press briefing. 'Sinabi rin ng Pangulo na kapag tayo ay nagbigay ng Filipino citizenship, higit pa sa mga legal na karapatan ang ating ibinibigay. Binubuksan natin ang buong karangalan ng ating kasaysayan, ang ating lahi, at ang ating pamana. Kaya dapat ang tatanggap nito ay sumasailalim din sa mga adhikain ng ating bayan,' she added. Senate grants Filipino citizenship to Chinese man despite 'red flags' In January, the Senate approved House Bill 8839, which seeks to grant Philippine citizenship to Li, on third and final reading, with a vote of 19-1. Sen. Risa Hontiveros opposed Li’s naturalization bid, citing information linking him to the illegal operation of POGOs in the country. But other senators such as Sherwin Gatchalian and JV Ejercito defended the naturalization bill, saying the Chinese man underwent stringent screening and vetting. Gatchalian says no proof Li Duan Wang has POGO links 'Nais ipaalam ng Pangulo na kung may mga kaduda-dudang interes, at hindi naman po bingi ang ating Pangulo para dinggin at pakinggan kung ano yung kanyang mga nakukuhang mga data or facts about supposed to be grantee,' Castro said. 'So kung nagkaroon man ng desisyon ang Kongreso na patawan o bigyan ng Philippine citizenship itong si Li Duan Wang, hindi po kumbinsido ang Pangulo. At yun lamang po, hindi natin ipamimigay ang Philippine citizenship kung may mga kaduda-dudang interes, yung tinatawag na grantee,' she added. President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr. has vetoed a proposed law granting Filipino citizenship to controversial Chinese national Li Duan Wang. The suspects allegedly attempted to evade arrest by fleeing toward a nearby residential area when flagged down, but were eventually caught after a brief chase. MANILA ( UPDATED ) – The Philippine National Police (PNP) said on Wednesday it has arrested two Chinese nationals allegedly involved in the kidnapping of three foreigners in Batangas province. The 'resurgence' of kidnap-for-ransom cases in the Philippines could hurt the nation's economy, an anti-crime advocacy group warned Monday.

title: Aquino pitches for successor who will continue CCT
body: In a speech, Aquino expressed optimism Filipinos will choose a leader who will continue the government's conditional cash transfer (CCT) program. The program has been praised by the Asian Development Bank (ADB), saying it has become a model for other developing nations hoping to help poor families. 'There is also a possibility that my successor will opt to put a stop to the CCT, leaving the fate of millions of our countrymen in limbo. If this were to happen, the Philippines would definitely lose its hard earned momentum,' Aquino said. 'It is my deepest hope that coming into the elections, our people have realized that they can demand for more good governance and I believe that they will choose the right leader. One who has integrity and experience who will sustain our gains and will definitely put the country above himself,' he added. ADB President Takehiko Nakao, meanwhile, pledged more support for the government's CCT program. 'Based on the positive outcome of the CCT program, I am now recommending through the ADB board, an additional 400 million dollars of financing which is expected to be approved by the board of ADB soon,' Nakao said. MANILA - President Benigno Aquino III has made another pitch for administration bet Mar Roxas for the upcoming polls without directly mentioning his name.

title: CHR 'extremely disappointed' in Ombudsman decision on 'secret jail' in Tondo
body: 'We are extremely disappointed but again we are not without hope that this will pan out and will result in better appreciation of the evidence that were presented and submitted with the Ombudsman,' CHR commissioner Karen Gomez-Dumpit told ANC. She considered the decision as a setback on CHR's advocacy in eliminating illegal, inhumane practices in detention facilities. 'It also contributes impunity to the country if we are not going to be able to correct this. If we are not able to exact accountability for these transgressions that have happened,' she added. The CHR has already filed a motion for reconsideration before the anti-graft body. 'It's not a lost cause. We have strong evidence, we believe that. We will fight this 'til the end. There are other avenues co-existing in exacting accountability, obtaining justice and access to remedies for the victims,' Dumpit said. The agency maintained the alleged secret detention facility was used as a 'cash cow' by policemen to extort money from drug suspects. The claim was also supported by the fact that the detainees were not registered, Dumpit added. In a resolution approved by Deputy Ombudsman for the Military and Other Law Enforcement, Officers Cyril Ramos, Police Supt. Robert Domingo, PO2 Dylan Verdan, PO1 Berly Apolonio and other John Does were cleared from complaints of arbitrary detention, grave threats, delay in the delivery of persons to the proper judicial authorities, grave coercion, grave misconduct and conduct prejudicial to the best interest of the service. The Ombudsman said there was not enough proof of 'bad faith' on the part of policemen in holding a dozen of people in the detention cell, which was described by CHR as 'cramp, dingy, fetid and dark.' On April 27, 2017, the CHR conducted a raid at the Raxabago Police Station 1 in Tondo, Manila to investigate allegations that policemen were illegally holding detainees to be released reportedly upon payment of ransom. The detainees were found inside a cramped detention cell hidden behind a bookshelf. The Commission on Human Rights said Wednesday it was 'extremely disappointed' in a decision by the Office of the Ombudsman for clearing Manila policemen of any liability over an alleged secret detention facility for drug suspects. MANILA - The Commission on Human Rights said Wednesday it was 'extremely disappointed' by the decision of the Office of the Ombudsman to clear Manila policemen of any liability over an alleged secret detention facility for drug suspects.
