title: PNoy on Duterte: I presume he's a patriot
body: In an interview with The New York Times' Javier Hernandez in Malacañang on Thursday, Aquino was asked about the differences between his and Duterte's policies in handling the Philippines' sea row with China. According to Aquino, the tough-talking Davao City mayor will be given facts which he shall review once he assumes office on June 30. 'I presume, for me, he's a patriot and he will do what is best for our country,' said Aquino. Outgoing Presidential Communications Secretary Sonny Coloma, likewise called on the incoming administration to get their facts straight regarding the West Philippine Sea. 'It's the prerogative of the incoming President to seek and obtain complete and accurate information on matters of national interest, especially on foreign policy,' Coloma said in a statement. 'It will be well to go beyond the realm of conjecture and focus on confirmed facts from credible and authoritative resource persons as basis for sound policy- and decision-making,' he added. This was in response to Duterte's remarks regarding the Scarborough Shoal or Bajo de Masinloc, which, he claimed, the Philippines lost to China in 2012. Duterte said he is interested to ask the Aquino administration why and how China was supposedly able to take control of the Scarborough Shoal after a tense standoff. The Philippines has a pending case against China before the Permanent Court of Arbitration in The Hague, Netherlands. But China, which claims sovereignty over almost the entire resource-rich sea, has boycotted the proceedings and rejected the court's authority in the case. A decision in the case is expected this year. During the third and last leg of the PiliPinas Debates on April 24, Duterte said if the arbitration court issues a favorable ruling to the Philippines and China refuses to honor its decision, he will ask the Philippine Navy to bring him to the boundary of Spratlys so he can 'ride a jet ski while bringing the Philippine flag.' His plan: go to the airport built by China on reclaimed land in the Spratlys and plant the Philippine flag there. Only then will he say: 'This is ours. Do what you want with me.' Aquino, meanwhile, defended Philippines' strong alliance with the United States amid rising tensions with China. The Philippines and US signed the Enhanced Defense Cooperation Agreement (EDCA) which grants the US increased military presence in the country. 'Too open? Well, if they look at it realistically, can we do it all by our self? Can we assert our rights all by our self?' quipped Aquino, adding that it is necessary to have the world public opinion on the Philippine side. Meanwhile, the outgoing president trumpeted the reforms his administration has started, which he will now pass on to the incoming government of Duterte. Aquino said 4.6 million households now live better lives and about 300,000 high school graduates are assured of a better future, all because of the government's conditional cash transfer (CCT) program. He also boasted of the high economic growth during his six years in office as well as the lower unemployment rate and increased membership with the health insurance program. He added that the worsening traffic situation in the metro only 'indicates the intense economic activity that is happening.' 'How do you actually move from a situation of poverty to that which you are able to take care of your basic needs? To us, it has been all of these investments in education, in the CCT, in the health insurance, in increasing infrastructure spending, and doing all of these things without increasing any tax except the sin tax,' he said. He also took a swipe at those who had supposedly taken it as an occupation to criticize his administration instead of just helping them. According to Aquino, he considers the Filipino people's change of mindset from being hopeless to 'now thinking that there are no limits to what our aspirations should be and can be' as his greatest achievement as president. Aquino said he is hoping that the people will demand more from the incoming administration, claiming that his government has already laid out the 'stepping stone to even greater heights.' 'Hopefully, our people will continue to be engaged and will demand of those who'll replace us even more than what we have produced,' he said. ''Cause those that succeed us will have less problems than what we have when we started.' MANILA - Outgoing President Benigno Aquino III believes presumptive President Rodrigo Duterte is a patriot and will do what is best for the country and the Filipino people.

title: COVID home care: What medicines to take for mild symptoms
body: The omicron variant presents milder symptoms compared with the delta variant as it 'spares the lungs,' according to Dr. Tony Leachon, former adviser of the National Task Force Against COVID-19. 'It might be the flu but there are fatigue and general body pains,' he told ANC's Headstart. 'Dito sa omicron, sa 400 (patients) na nakita ko this week, bihira ang merong loss of sense of smell and taste. Sa 2 araw lang, they would present the symptoms. Wala pa nga iba fever eh.' (In 400 patients I saw this week, presentations of loss of sense of smell and taste were rare. In just 2 days, they they would present the symptoms. Some don't even have fever.) It is important for patients to stay home for the first 5 to 7 days if they experience symptoms, according to Leachon. Adults may take 500 milligrams of paracetamol every 4 to 6 hours along with naproxen, a nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug, for 3 days, he said. Mucolytic and antitussive medicines can be taken with each other, he added. It is also important to hydrate with water and eat fruits and vegetables, Leachon said. Drinking warm water with lemon and honey will also help, he added. Two tablets of 6 milligrams melatonin, a natural supplement, can be taken to help with sleeping, according to the public health expert. Patients must consult their doctors first before taking Lianhua Qingwen, a Chinese traditional medicine approved by the Food and Drug Administration, Leachon said. Traditional herb lagundi and virgin coconut oil have a 'soothing effect' and can be a panacea for some patients, he added. 'I-try niyo, wala namang masama eh. Kaya nga sila over-the-counter kasi meron silang data na maaaring effective sila, kaya sila over-the-counter kasi safe po sila,' he said. (You can try it, there's no harm in it. That's why they're over-the-counter medicine because there's data they can be effective and that they're safe.) MANILA - As virus infections increase across the country, the public is urged to recognize its symptoms, isolate and consult a doctor, a health reform advocate said Friday.

title: DOJ: No leads on Davao Death Squad probe
body: In a news conference on Friday, outgoing Justice Acting Secretary Emmanuel Caparas said the lone witness in the probe initiated by the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) early last year is no longer with the Witness Protection Program (WPP). READ: 'Davao Death Squad' hitman now under witness protection program Caparas said the witness voluntarily left the program, adding that there is no way anymore to verify the witness' claims in his affidavits. 'There's really nothing there... there are affidavits but if you want to confirm the affidavits, ask the witness. He left the program voluntarily, so the exit was voluntary,' he said. The justice chief added that there has been no concrete evidence on the DDS case. 'Katulad ng sinabi ko naman dati tungkol sa DDS [issue], lumilitaw tapos nawawala tapos lumilitaw... to me it's a matter of facts; prove it, then we can proceed and do what's appropriate. 'I don't want to speculate; it's not fair to speculate,' Caparas said. In May of last year, then Justice Secretary Leila De Lima, a staunch critic of Duterte and who, during her stint as chairperson of the Commission on Human Rights (CHR), probed the DDS, confirmed that the NBI, an attached agency of the DOJ, is investigating the DDS. De Lima, however, refused to give details other than to say a witness has come forward and was admitted to the WPP. MANILA - The Department of Justice (DOJ) has hit a wall in its investigation into the so-called Davao Death Squad (DDS), a vigilante group that has repeatedly been linked to incoming president and Davao Mayor Rodrigo Duterte.

title: Escudero to Dutertes: Don't drag AFP into politics
body: This, after Vice President Sara Duterte said the military should explain why it allowed the Philippine National Police to be in control of Villamor Air Base when her father, former President Rodrigo Duterte was arrested. 'Alam mo, hindi ko rin ma-intindihan yung concern na yun ng ating Vice President. Maliban sa ito'y patuloy, pagpapatuloy ng tila mensahe na common sa kanila, na umaapila palagi sa sandatahang lakas, sa anumang level, anumang issue, anumang kaganapan,' Escudero said. 'Dahil hindi ko rin maunawaan kung ano yung apila niyang sinasabi kaugnay noon, na yung Presidential Security Command daw ay under ng AFP. Pero anong gusto niyang palabasin? Magbabangayan ang sundalo at ang pulis? Claro, law enforcement operation yun na nag-assist lamang ang AFP. At hindi pwedeng palagan (at) lumaban ang AFP sa kapulisan kaugnay sa law enforcement operation. Bakit ba natin pilit na pinag-aaway yung dalawa?' Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro earlier said that the defense department and the military are not primarily tasked with law enforcement. 'Our sole mandate is to give support to the Philippine National Police when it is asked for. And therefore, such ends our jurisdiction,' he said. He said the move to arrest Duterte was a law enforcement operation that the PNP could ask the military for help with. 'And that is the extent of the participation of my department and Armed Forces of the Philippines. And all matters in supporting the Philippine National Police are governed by national security.' 'Marahil bahagi lamang ito sa constant na apila ng kanilang panig kaugnay sa Armed Forces of the Philippines. Um, nakampihan marahil sila o magkaroon ng posisyon o paninindigan sa ilan sa mga issue na involved sila,' Escudero said. 'Well, ulitin ko, ang tila pilit nilang dinadamay ang sandatahang lakas sa iba't ibang isyu na hindi naman dapat talaga at wala namang isyu. Dahil facility man yan ng pulis, facility man yan ng sundalo, facility man yan ng executive branch o ng Kongreso, ang law enforcement operation ay law enforcement operation pa rin,' he explained. The Senate president maintained that the military should stay out of the political issues, adding that partisans should also stop dragging the AFP into any political disputes. 'Hindi dapat mangampi ang AFP kaninuman dapat tumayo sila sa likod ng chain of command at ng Saligang Batas sa ilalim ng commander-in-chief natin at hindi para sa kanila ang maglaro ng pulitika at pumabor sa isang kulay o kabilang kulay man.' Escudero said, 'At sana tigilan na rin ng magkabilang panig kaugnay sa panunuyo sa ating sandatahang lakas, dahil hindi natin ginugusto, hindi natin gusto na sila'y mang himasok, kada na lamang may hindi pagkakaunawan ang mga politiko sa ating bansa. Hindi nila trabaho maging referee sa anumang issue political sa ating bansa. ' Escudero, meantime, saw nothing wrong with Vice President Duterte's continued stay in the Netherlands, despite the appeal of the Palace for her to return to the country for work. 'Ang pangunahing trabaho para sa akin ng ikalawang pangulo ay maging handa kung sakasakaling sa panawagan ng panahon, ay kinakailangan niyang pumalit o humalinhin sa pangulo kung anumang kadahilanan. Yun para sa akin ang pangunahing trabaho niya,' he said. Senate President Francis Escudero on Monday urged the Duterte camp not to involve the Armed Forces of the Philippines in every issue involving the political family.

title: Trump shooting scare renews 'staged' conspiracy theory
body: Trump and his top administration officials were evacuated Saturday from a media gala in Washington after gunfire erupted outside the ballroom, marking the third assassination attempt targeting the Republican in two years. AFP's fact-checkers identified a series of social media posts from anti-Trump accounts circulating an unsubstantiated theory that the White House staged the shooting to distract attention from unfavorable news, including the unpopular US-Israeli war with Iran. Posts advancing the claim garnered 80 million views on Elon Musk's platform X alone within two days of the shooting, according to the disinformation watchdog NewsGuard. Many of the same accounts previously claimed that two assassination attempts on Trump in 2024 -- in Pennsylvania and Florida -- were also staged. The narrative largely emanated from a left-wing conspiracy movement that researchers call 'BlueAnon,' a play on the right-wing QAnon cult. 'Many of the anti-Trump accounts baselessly claiming that the WHCD (White House Correspondents' Dinner) shooting was staged made identical claims after the 2024 assassination attempts,' NewsGuard's Sofia Rubinson told AFP. 'Some viral posts we've seen explicitly cite those earlier incidents as 'evidence' that staging shootings is part of Trump's playbook -- to generate sympathy and distract from unfavorable coverage.' Fast-developing breaking news events such as shootings often trigger a frantic search for new information on social media, frequently allowing falsehoods to spread and intensifying online confusion. There is no evidence that the Trump administration orchestrated Saturday's attack. The White House on Monday blamed what it called a 'left-wing cult of hatred' for the shooting and a 31-year-old suspect, Cole Allen, faces a potential life sentence if convicted of attempting to kill Trump. Following Saturday's shooting, state media in US adversaries -- Russia and Iran -- amplified further conspiracy theories, including claims that the attacker had ties to the Israeli military, according to the London-based Institute for Strategic Dialogue. In recent weeks, a growing number of MAGA influencers -- shorthand for Trump's 'Make America Great Again' slogan -- have also promoted the conspiracy theory that the Republican had staged the 2024 assassination attempt at a Pennsylvania campaign rally. The trend underscores how Americans on both sides of the political divide are prone to outlandish conspiracy theories, as many turn to partisan influencers for information amid mistrust of mainstream media, researchers say. 'The staged assassination conspiracy theory has advocates on the left, particularly among liberal content creators. It's also starting to take off on the right as they lose faith in Trump,' Mike Rothschild, a researcher who studies conspiracy theories, told AFP. 'The theory casts him (Trump) as a master manipulator,' he said. 'And it uses as 'evidence' either misconceptions, other viral videos, or things that people have just made up.' The theory has gained traction as Trump faces backlash from Americans across the political spectrum -- including segments of his MAGA supporters -- over the war with Iran, which has driven up oil prices and raised concerns about American casualties. The military campaign has laid bare divisions within Trump's political base, with even longtime conservative supporters -- such as former Fox News host Tucker Carlson -- condemning the Republican's departure from his non-interventionist policy. The conspiracy theory continues to spread largely unchecked online, as many tech platforms have scaled back content moderation efforts that were once used to curb disinformation. Influencers are often incentivized to amplify sensational rumors, which can attract followers and increase earnings on revenue-sharing platforms like X. 'The more irresistible the claim the better in terms of the business of political commentary. The actual politics of a party is now secondary to the monetization of the political brand,' Walter Scheirer of the University of Notre Dame told AFP. 'In the long run, this likely does weaken Trump's base.' A flood of misinformation exploded online after US President Donald Trump escaped a weekend shooting scare, fueling renewed—and unfounded—claims that he has staged assassination attempts to boost his political fortunes. WASHINGTON, United States — A flood of misinformation exploded online after US President Donald Trump escaped a weekend shooting scare, fueling renewed—and unfounded—claims that he has staged assassination attempts to boost his political fortunes.

title: 'Malayo pa tayo roon': Marcos leaves Mary Jane Veloso clemency up to legal experts
body: The mother of two awaits a hoped-for pardon in a women's prison in Mandaluyong, where she was transferred on Wednesday following a repatriation deal between the Philippines and Indonesia that was over a decade in the making. 'Malayo pa tayo roon. We still have to habe a look really at what her status is and of course we are aware of the request for clemency from her representative, of course, her family,' Marcos told reporters. 'We will leave it to the judgement of our legal experts to determine whether the provision of clemency is appropriate,' he added. The 39-year-old Veloso was arrested and sentenced to death in 2010 after the suitcase she was carrying was found to be lined with 2.6 kilograms of heroin, in a case that sparked uproar in the Philippines. She flew home without handcuffs alongside Filipino correctional officials on an overnight commercial flight after a Jakarta ceremony marking 'the end of a harrowing chapter in Veloso's life', the corrections bureau said in a statement. In a Wednesday statement, Marcos thanked Indonesia for turning over custody, but made no mention of a pardon or clemency. Under the agreement, Veloso's life sentence now falls under the Philippines' purview, 'including the authority to grant clemency, remission, amnesty and similar measures'. 'Wala naman condition na ibinigay ang Indonesia. It's really up to us. But we're still at the very preliminary stage,' Marcos said Thursday. Justice Undersecretary Raul Vasquez earlier told reporters that Veloso's clemency bid was 'on the table' and would be 'seriously studied'. DOJ Undersecretary Jesse Andres noted that the Board of Pardons and Parole should first make the proper evaluation and submit a recommendationon Veloso's pardon. 'Matagal na proseso po yan, hindi natin pwede madaliin. Kasalukuyan pinapatupad lang natin ang panuntunan natin with the government of Indonesia for Mary Jane Veloso just to serve her prison sentence in the Philippines,' the official said. 'Yun lang po ang ginagawa natin sa ngayon. Hintayin po natin, may tamang panahon po na magsalita kami dyan pero may proseso din na kailangan sundin,' he added. Veloso will serve out her life sentence if not pardoned, Vasquez added. Indonesia's government has said it will respect any decision made by Manila. President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. on Thursday said the possible grant of clemency for Mary Jane Veloso, the Filipina who spent nearly 15 years on Indonesia's death row, rested on 'the judgement of our legal experts.' MANILA -- President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. on Thursday said the possible grant of clemency for Mary Jane Veloso, the Filipina who spent nearly 15 years on Indonesia's death row, rested on 'the judgement of our legal experts.' title: The ANC Brief: Calls for transparency
body: What’s in those deals signed during the recent visit of Chinese President Xi Jinping? So far, many important details of the deals have not been made public by the government. Senators and critics are calling for transparency on the deals. The Palace said the documents will be released when they are 'certified and available.' Philippine Ambassador to China Chito Sta. Romana told ANC that the oil and gas deal is 'essentially' Manila’s version. On the South China Sea, both sides agree to exercise self-restraint. The Palace also said President Duterte has accepted invitation to visit China again. The President will be at the inauguration of the Cavite Gateway Terminal in Tanza, Cavite at 4 p.m. He might talk about the recent visit of Xi, maybe even give more details. He could also tackle his favorite topics like drugs and corruption. ANC will be ready to air his speech live. Shabu shipment Speaking of illegal drugs, the Senate blue ribbon committee will continue its hearing on the P6.4 billion shabu shipment from China at 10 a.m. Let’s see if there are any new revelations. ANC will bring you these proceedings live. Nine years and counting The 9th anniversary of the massacre is coming up on Friday. The decision on the case could be out soon. Relatives of those who died are still calling out for justice. Floods from Samuel Tropical Depression Samuel brought heavy rains that caused flooding in Eastern Visayas. As of this writing, it threatens Cuyo in Palawan. ANC will be bringing you updates on the effects of the weather system. Lawmakers are asking for more transparency in the deals signed between the Philippines and China. Lawmakers are asking for more transparency in the deals signed between the Philippines and China. And the Senate continues its hearing of shabu shipments. These are the stories ANC will be covering today:

title: Will the 2016 elections be 'dirtier'? Analyst weighs in
body: Speaking to ANC's 'Top Story,' Casiple, an analyst of the Institute for Political and Electoral Reform (IPER), believes that despite all the issues relating to the upcoming elections, it will still not be as dirty as previous ones. 'Certainly, it cannot surpass the dirtiest election of 2004, 1992.' 'I remember the dagdag-bawas thing became a by-word precisely because the whole manual system of elections was just not that reliable,' Casiple said, adding that rampant cheating in the manual system eventually led to automation. For Casiple, it has yet to be proven whether the automated election system can be used for cheating. 'It has yet to be proved that the system, meaning the machine themselves, can be part of the cheating program. The cases that were filed in the 2010 and 2013 elections invariably led to the human intervention side, rather than the machine side,' he added. Among the issues hounding the 2016 elections is the delay in the printing of ballots. Commission on Elections (Comelec) Commissioner Christian Robert Lim announced Monday morning that the printing of ballots was moved to February 15, instead of February 8. Lim explained that the delay was due to a problem discovered in the program of the Consolidation Canvassing System or CCS, a major component of the automated elections. 'Ang problema, there is something in the CCS, gumagana lang ang transmission kapag nakakabit yung network, which hindi dapat kasi stand-alone yun eh,' Lim explained. MANILA - Political analyst Ramon Casiple gives his opinion on whether the upcoming elections will be dirtier than previous elections.

title: Imee rejects Zubiri's statement blaming her for RCEP delay
body: Sen. Imee Marcos rejected Senate President Juan Miguel Zubiri's assertion that she was to blame for the delayed ratification of RCEP. The 'late' passage of RCEP is part of what allegedly irked Malacañang with Zubiri, according to a news report. The report alleged that Zubiri was flagged for the Senate's 'low output' in terms of passing laws. To defend himself, Zubiri explained that 'the delay in the passage of RCEP was due to the lack of support it got from the main sponsor herself who is the chair of the Committee on Foreign Relations,' referring to Marcos. But according to Marcos, she had already conducted a public consultation regarding RCEP way before the Office of the President transmitted the RCEP ratification package to the Senate on November 29, 2022. 'The ratification package was referred to my committee only on December 6. In less than a week, on December 12, I conducted the first formal hearing. Again, the concerned departments were unresponsive,' Marcos said. 'I asked Senate President Zubiri's help to get these agencies to respond adequately and in concrete terms, but to no avail,' she added. Without assurance from government agencies that agriculture workers would be protected under RCEP, Marcos said she refused to sponsor the bill before the plenary. 'I immediately told the Senate leadership that I would not be able to sponsor the measure without adequate protection for farmers, fisherfolk, and MSME's, as well as aggressive measures against smuggling,' Marcos explained. On February 21, the Senate ratified RCEP, with 20 senators voting in its favor, Sen. Risa Hontiveros voting no, and Marcos abstaining. MANILA -- Sen. Imee Marcos on Friday rejected Senate President Juan Miguel Zubiri's assertion that she was to blame for the 'delayed' ratification of Regional Comprehensive Economic Partnership (RCEP), a priority measure of the Palace. 'The change in Executive Secretary may have resulted in the late transmittal of RCEP. But I can hardly be pointed out as the cause of delay in its passage,' Marcos said in a statement.

title: Marcos says gov't closely monitoring rice prices
body: Marcos, who is also the concurrent agriculture chief, said high farmgate prices and some imported farm inputs contributed to the price increase. 'Binabantayan namin nang mabuti ang pag-supply ng ating bigas at pagbantay sa tumataas na presyo ng bigas,' he said in a video message. 'Mayroon naman tayong balita na nagsimula na ang pag-aani sa Nueva Ecija, sa Isabela, at saka sa North Cotabato. Kaya’t ito’y magdadagdag sa supply natin,' he added. (We are closely monitoring the rice supply and its rising prices. We received news that the harvest has started in Nueva Ecija, Isabela, and North Cotabato. This will increase our supply.) The Philippines aims to harvest 11.5 million metric tons of rice in the second half of the year, said Dr. Leo Sebastian, undersecretary for rice industry development of the Department of Agriculture (DA). 'This would breach the 20-million MT total national palay production, making it a record, being the highest in the country’s history,' Sebastian said in a Palace news release. Rice demand from July to December this year is projected at 7.76 million metric tons, according to DA data. Based on the agency's monitoring as of Thursday, prices of imported commercial rice ranged from P46 to P65 per kilo, while local rice retailed at P40 to P62 per kilo in Metro Manila. Meanwhile, DA officials did market visits earlier in the day to check on price of rice. This is part of their regular price monitoring in 13 wet markets across Metro Manila. They said they saw large volumes of rice in stalls, and that on their level, they would say rice is still sufficient. 'Wala naman pong nakikitang shortage, sufficient ang supply base sa regular report, wala po kaming nakikitang dahilan para mangamba,' said Junibert De Sagun, director of DA's Agribusiness and Marketing Assistance Service. (We did not see any shortage. The supply is sufficient based on our regular report. We do not see any reason to worry.) 'As far as yung nakita namin kanina, marami pong nakalagay na bigas sa mga tindahan nila,' Sagun added. (A lot of rice is still in their stalls.) The Federation of Free Farmers, for its part, said it was important to consider the timing of imports and the projected harvests. 'Yes, we have enough stocks at the moment, but these stocks are in short supply and rapidly thinning out. This explains the spike in prices,' said Raul Montemayor, national manager of the Federation of Free Farmers. 'Some imports will reportedly come in soon. Some early harvests will occur in August and September. But will they come on time before stocks run out? At best, it will be a photo finish,' he added. MANILA ( UPDATED ) — President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr. on Friday said government is 'closely monitoring' rice prices, which topped P50 per kilo in some wet markets.

title: It's official: Bam Aquino is returning to Senate
body: Aquino was proclaimed as senator on Saturday by the Commission on Elections, officially securing his political comeback in the Senate. The senator-elect received a total of 20,971,899 votes based on official figures from the National Board of Canvassers (NBOC). He ranked in the second spot, behind topnotcher Sen. Christopher 'Bong' Go, a Duterte ally, who clinched a second term. Aquino acknowledged that he faced a tall order at the start of the campaign period, pointing out that he did not even break into the Top 20, much less the Top 12. 'Ito pong pagtakbo ito ay kakaiba talaga. Nagsimula po tayo, napakalayo. Wala po tayo sa Top 12. In fact, wala nga po tayo sa Top 20 noong una po tayong nagsabi na tatakbo tayo,' said Aquino, who thanked volunteers and the Filipino youth for propping up his campaign. 'Dahil po sa sipag at tiyaga ng mga napakaraming volunteers, napakaraming kabataan na nagsuporta ... Sa kabataang Pilipino, lubos-lubos po ang pasasalamat natin. Maraming, maraming salamat,' he added. Aquino ran on education and job platforms and was said to have benefited from the zeal of young voters and last-minute endorsements , including from the bloc-voting Iglesia Ni Cristo religious group. Aquino joined his ally Senator-elect Kiko Pangilinan, who also made an unexpected showing in this year's elections by placing 5th in a race of 12. 'Ang binigay niyo pong mandato, hindi po sasayangin. Ang binigay niyo pong mandato, iingatan po natin,' he said. Last year, Aquino announced that he had left the Liberal Party and assumed the chairmanship of Katipunan ng Nagkakaisang Pilipino , said to be the country's youngest political party. Aquino was a senator from 2013 to 2019. In the 2019 midterm polls, he failed to win his re-election bid under the 'Otso Diretso' slate, placing 14th overall with 14,144,923 votes. Aquino did not run for an elective position during the 2022 national elections and instead served as then-presidential candidate Leni Robredo's campaign manager. Robredo ran for Naga City mayor in Halalan 2025 and won . MANILA ( 2nd UPDATE ) – The Commission on Elections (Comelec) proclaimed the winning 12 senators at Manila Hotel Tent City on Saturday, five days after midterm elections and the earliest in the poll body's history. Of all the senators-elect, only 11 were able to attend as Kiko Pangilinan is currently with his family in the United States for his daughter's graduation program. During the proclamation, Comelec called the 12th-ranked senator first, while the top vote-getter was the last. Imee Marcos with 13,339,227 votes at 12th place was first to be proclaimed. Bong Go, who achieved 27,121,073 votes at 1st place, was called last. The other senators-elect with their acquired votes based on the National Certificate of Canvass (NCOC) are as follows: 2. Former senator Bam Aquino – 20,971,899 votes 3. Sen. Ronald dela Rosa – 20,773,946 votes 4. ACT-CIS party-list Rep. Erwin Tulfo – 17,118,881 votes 5. Former senator Francis Pangilinan – 15,343,229 6. SAGIP party-list Rep. Rodante Marcoleta – 15,250,723 7. Former senator Panfilo Lacson – 15,106,111 8. Former Senate president Vicente Sotto III – 14,832,996 10. Las Piñas City Rep. Camille Villar – 13,651,274 Proclamation of 'Magic 12' senators set on Saturday Of the 12 winners, five were reelectionists: Go, dela Rosa, Cayetano, Lapid, and Marcos. Meanwhile, four got fresh mandates: Aquino, Pangilinan, Lacson, and Sotto. Three incumbent congressmen will make the switch from the House of Representatives to the Senate: Tulfo, Marcoleta, and Villar. Former First Lady Imelda Marcos was present for daughter Imee's proclamation. Except for the incumbent senators who got a second term, the other winners will be formally called senators-elect till June 30, 2025, when their term of office begins. The poll body plans to proclaim the winning party-list groups on May 19. -- with a report from RG Cruz, ABS-CBN News The Commission on Elections (Comelec) on Saturday has began proclaming the winning 12 senators at Manila Hotel Tent City, five days after midterm elections and the earliest in the poll body's history. Supporters flock to the launching of the grassroots campaign 'Mga Kaibigan' led by senatorial and congressional aspirants, former senators Kiko Pangilinan, Bam Aquino, Atty. Leila Dr Lima, and Atty. Chel Diokno, in Quezon City on October 16, 2024. Mark Demayo, ABS-CBN News/File MANILA — Senator-elect Francis 'Kiko' Pangilinan will miss the senators’ proclamation by the Commission on Elections on Saturday, May 17. Pangilinan and his family are currently in the United States to attend his daughter Frankie’s college graduation, per a statement by his office. 'Para sa bawat magsasaka at mangingisda, bawat manggagawa, bawat solo parent, bawat inang nagtitipid at yung isusubo na lang niya ay ibibigay niya sa kaniyang mga anak, bawat ama o inang nahihiwalay sa kaniyang mga anak mapaaral lang sila, mapaangat lang ang kanilang buhay, para sa inyong lahat ang tagumpay na ito,' Kiko said through his sister, Maricel Pangilinan Arenas, who represented him in the proclamation. Pangilinan had claimed what he said was a 'miracle' win when he ranked fifth in the senatorial race with a total of 15,343,229 votes. Halalan 2025: Kiko Pangilinan calls Senate race top 5 spot a 'miracle' This was in contrast to the survey results before the election day, in which Pangilinan consistently ranked outside the ‘Magic 12.’ He first served as a senator from 2001 to 2013 and came back to the Senate in 2016. In between, Pangilinan served as the Presidential Assistant for Food Security and Agricultural Modernization from 2014 to 2015, under the administration of the late former president Benigno Aquino III. Pangilinan ran for vice president in the 2022 national elections, but was defeated by incumbent VP Sara Duterte. Pangilinan, who ran under the Liberal Party, carried the advocacies on agriculture and food prices in his campaign. He promised to lower food prices, establish food banks in every barangay through public-private partnerships and strengthen feeding programs to combat malnutrition. Pangilinan lobbies for the enactment of the Sagip Saka Act—which will mandate the government to make direct purchases of goods from farmers and fisherfolk, institutionalizing a Magna Carta for small farmers, increasing the budget for the agriculture sector, and investing in sustainable and climate-friendly farming. He also wants to incentivize the private entities’ investment in agri enterprises, and reinforce local production. Bam, Kiko wins 'positive', but dynasties dominated Halalan 2025 — Makabayan Senator-elect Francis 'Kiko' Pangilinan will miss the senators’ proclamation by the Commission on Elections on Saturday, May 17. The Makabayan bloc announces its senatorial slate for the 2025 midterm elections at Liwasang Bonifacio during the observance of National Heroes Day on August 26, 2024. Malabayan's candidates include (left to right) Mody Floranda, Jocelyn Andamo, Teddy Casiño, Arlene Brosas, Ronnel Arambulo, Liza Maza, Danilo Ramos, Jerome Adonis, France Castro, and Mimi Doringo. Mark Demayo, ABS-CBN News MANILA — The Makabayan coalition on Thursday expressed dismay over the partial and unofficial results of the 2025 midterm elections, where political dynasties and traditional politicians emerged victorious in most races. In a press conference, Makabayan president and senatorial bet Liza Maza lamented the outcome of the recent polls. Makabayan Senate bets vow to convict VP Sara if elected 'Kung makikita natin, nagpapatunay yung election 2025 na ang nananalo pa rin sa halalan ay yung mga reactionaryong politiko, mga traditional na pulitiko. Out of 12, yung 10 nasa either kadiliman o andun sa kasamaan,' she said. (The 2025 elections show that many of those who won are reactionaries, are traditional politicians. Out of 12, 10 are either from 'kadiliman' or 'kasamaan.) Apart from former Sens. Paolo Benigno 'Bam' Aquino IV and Francis 'Kiko' Pangilinan, the winning candidates in the 12-seat Senate race are from either the Marcos Jr. or Duterte factions of the former UniTeam. The former congresswoman said the fight already began even before the polls. 'Kahit wala pang kampanya ang namayani na yung pagbili ng loyalty ng tao tulad ng TUPAD at kung anu-ano pa... para magkaroon ng loyalty base ang mga pulitiko, which is patronage politics,' Maza said. (Even before the campaign season, buying people's loyalties through (Tulong Panghanapbuhay sa Ating Disadvantaged/Displaced Workers ) and other aid programs for politicians to have a loyalty base, which is patronage politics) However, the Makabayan president saw as positive the victory of Bam Aquino and Kiko Pangilinan, who are leading the Senate race. 'Although, we are very much viewing in a positive light yung pagkakapanalo ni Bam [Aquino] at Kiko [Pangilinan]. They have a lot of work to do especially impeachment and pagtaas ng sahod ng manggagawa,' Maza said. She likewise congratulated Akbayan Party-list first nominee Chel Diokno and Mamamayang Liberal Party-list first nominee Leila De Lima, who are expected to win in the party-list race. Akbayan Maza also congratulated ACT Teachers Party-list ranked 34th and Kabataan Party-list ranked 40th for getting one seat each in the 20th Congress. She said the fate of Gabriela Party-list remains hanging in the balance, since it is in the 55th spot based on the partial and unofficial count. Despite none of their senatorial candidates making it , the Makabayan coalition thanked all those who stood by them amid the red-tagging and harassment they said they faced especially during the campaign. Makabayan pushes for accountability of social media platforms 'Makabayan thanks all its supporters and campaigners for the 2025 elections. Amid sustained and coordinated attacks by state forces, relentless red-tagging and repression and a campaign of disinformation, we were able to reach out to millions and present an alternative vision for the Philippines,' Maza said. 'No other political party today had to endure the attacks that Makabayan and its party-list groups faced. The regime sought to wipe out Makabayan in the electoral arena but it failed. This is a testament to the people’s strength and defiance,' she shared. Maza also congratulated Akbayan Party-list's Chel Diokno and Mamamayang Liberal Party-list's Leila De Lima, who are expected to win in the party-list race. Akbayan The HAGUE - Who did overseas Filipino voters in The Netherlands pick in the 2025 Philippine midterm elections? According to the Commission on Elections dashboard , a total of 516 out of nearly 2,700 registered overseas Filipino voters in The Netherlands cast their votes in the May 12 elections. Clustered precinct ID 93170001 had the highest number of voters who participated in Monday’s polls at 501. Top vote-getters in the senatorial race in that clustered precinct include: The Special Board of Canvassers (SBOC) ended the canvassing of votes at 6.58 p.m. (Netherlands time) on Tuesday, May 13. Led by Chairperson and Ambassador Eduardo Malaya, the SBOC began the official canvassing of votes at around 5 p.m. The canvassing was delayed due to the late arrival of the Canvassing and Consolidation System from the Commission on Elections which was only received in the morning of May 13. Meanwhile, the Special Board of Election Inspectors (SBEI) successfully transmitted the election returns to the Manila servers in the afternoon of May 12, hours after the casting of ballots ended. Former president Rodrigo Duterte is currently detained at the International Criminal Court in The Hague on charges of murder as a crime against humanity. Duterte won the Davao mayoral contest on Monday, with eight times more votes than his nearest rival. Who did overseas Filipino voters in The Netherlands pick in the 2025 Philippine midterm elections? Members of the Dumagat-Remontado indigenous group cast their votes at the Mababang Paaralan ng Nayon in Sta. Inez in Tanay, Rizal on May 12, 2025. Jonathan Cellona, ABS-CBN News MANILA — Disillusionment with the government and with politics may have prompted some not to vote, but apathy over perceived lack of change after each election season helps make it harder for that change to happen, a political analyst said. Maria Elise Mendoza, a political analyst and assistant professor at the UP Diliman Department of Political Science, acknowledged that people have valid reasons to decide not to exercise their right to vote. 'I think one of the reasons would be the long-standing issues we have in politics. Like for example, yung notion na pare-parehas lang naman yung tumatakbo, wala namang nagbabago,' she said. (For example, the notion that all candidates are the same and that nothing really changes) But she argued, 'if we do not exert efforts to try to put the right people in power, then things actually just stay that way.' Skipping elections would allow traditional political dynasties to maintain their hold on power and creates room for bad leaders to thrive. 'Magkakaroon ng chance 'yung mga same old names na manatili in power kasi walang nagko-contest sa kanila. Walang nagta-try na mag-elect ng candidates who could potentially…. beat them in the elections,' Mendoza said. (The same old names will have a chance to stay in power because nobody is contesting them. Nobody will try to elect the candidates who could potentially beat them in elections) Philippines midterm elections: Youth want dynasties out And even a single vote can have an impact, especially on the local level, where races have been won and lost by a couple of votes. 'One or two votes or less than 10 votes, but still, it made a difference,' she said. Partial and unofficial results of the Monday elections show a surge of votes for former senators Bam Aquino and Francis 'Kiko' Pangilinan. Progressive party-list Akbayan is also seen to get three seats at the House as it tops the party-list elections. WRAP: Leftist candidates take on political dynasties in Halalan 2025 The Commission on Elections has yet to release data on voter turnout but Comelec chair George Garcia said the poll body saw an 'unexpected' number of voters heading to voting precincts on Monday despite the heat. 'Kudos to the Filipinos': PH closes midterm polls with 'unexpected' turnout 'Something is really happening because the people really wanted to say something, 'yun yung naramdaman namin kanina (that was the sense that we got earlier),' Garcia said. 'Ang haba ng pila kahit sa mga lugar na hindi humahaba ang pila kahit sa presidential elections,' he added. (There were long lines even in areas where we didn't see lines even during the presidential elections) 'If we do not exert efforts to try to put the right people in power, then things actually just stay that way,' UP Political Science assistant professor Maria Elise Mendoza said. Former Sen. Bam Aquino finished the 2025 midterm race with a stunning performance, surprising both analysts and the public by securing the No. 2 spot in the senatorial results despite being a laggard in surveys. MANILA — Former Sen. Bam Aquino finished the 2025 midterm race with a stunning performance, surprising both analysts and the public by securing the No. 2 spot in the senatorial results despite being a laggard in surveys by institutional polling firms . 'If we do not exert efforts to try to put the right people in power, then things actually just stay that way,' UP Political Science assistant professor Maria Elise Mendoza said.

title: Duterte inside Scheveningen detention center, ICC spokesperson confirms
body: This, after former Executive Secretary Salvador Medialdea said that they do not know where Duterte is, and was reportedly told he was not inside the facility. In a message to ABS-CBN News, ICC spokesperson Fadi El Abdallah said Duterte underwent medical check ups as part of the standard procedure. 'M. Duterte is under ICC custody at the ICC detention centre in Scheveningen. He was admitted today following all medical checks,' he said. 'When a suspect arrives in ICC custody, the Court as standard practice takes measures to protect the health and well-being of the suspect,' El Abdallah added. El Abdallah also said the court will announce the schedule for the initial appearance of Duterte. In an earlier interview with ABS-CBN News outside the detention center, Medialdea said they asked the ICC to bring Duterte to the hospital as soon as they landed due to his poor health. The ICC agreed, but Medialdea said they were not informed which hospital he was brought to. He was later told Duterte was not inside the detention facility when he tried to visit him. Duterte earlier arrived at the Scheveningen detention center in The Hague on Wednesday night (March 12) where he will be detained while facing murder charges at the International Criminal Court (ICC). He is expected to stay at the ICC Detention Centre, which is located within the prison facility. Duterte, who led the Philippines from 2016 to 2022, was arrested early on Tuesday in Manila, marking the biggest step yet in the ICC's probe into alleged crimes against humanity during an anti-drugs crackdown that killed thousands and drew condemnation around the world. Pro-Duterte supporters have gathered outside the prison, before his arrival, holding banners, chanting Duterte's name and waving Philippines flags. Duterte, 79, could become the first Asian former head of state to go on trial at the ICC in The Hague. The ICC's warrant for his arrest says that as president, Duterte created, funded and armed death squads that carried out murders of purported drug users and dealers. In coming days, he will be brought before a judge and the allegations will be read out in court. Prosecutors accuse him of crimes against humanity for systematic attacks that led to dozens of murders. - with reports from Reuters Former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte is under the custody of the International Criminal Court in its detention facility in Scheveningen, The Hague, the ICC spokersperson confirmed Thursday. MANILA — Former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte is under the custody of the International Criminal Court in its detention facility in Scheveningen, The Hague, the ICC spokersperson confirmed Thursday.

title: How many Filipinos were functionally literate in 2024? How many were illiterate?
body: 'Among individuals 5 years old and over, 6.9 percent—or about 7 out of 100—cannot read and write or [are] illiterate,' the PSA said in its 2024 Functional Literacy, Education, and Mass Media Survey (FLEMMS) dated June 30 but released to the media this week. Based on PSA data, there were 103.36 million Filipinos five years old and above. The PSA noted that the highest proportion of individuals who cannot read and write was recorded in age group 5 to 9 years at 20.1 percent. 'Among regions, BARMM had the highest proportion of individuals aged 5 years and over who cannot read and write at 14.4 percent,' it also said. Functional literacy is an individual’s ability to read and write with a higher level of comprehension skills, and compute, according to PSA. PSA’s 2024 FLEMMS showed that the functional literacy rate in 2024 was at 70.8 percent or about 7 out of 10 individuals aged 10 to 64, translating to around 60.17 million individuals who can read, write, compute, and comprehend out of the estimated 85 million individuals in the same age group. The Cordillera Administrative Region recorded the highest functional literacy rate of 81.2 percent while the Zamboanga Peninsula recorded the lowest with 59.3 percent. The rate of functional literacy is highest, with 74.1 percent, among individuals aged 20 to 24 years, while lowest for those aged 60 to 64 years at 57.8 percent. While the functional literacy rate is lower, PSA said the basic literacy rate in the country is at 90 percent for Filipinos aged five and over. This, however, is lower than 2019’s 94 percent for the same age group. Basic literacy is one’s ability to read and write a simple message in any language or dialect, to compute or perform basic mathematical operations, said PSA. The basic literacy rate is highest among individuals residing in the Central Luzon region at 92.8 percent, and lowest for the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao at 81 percent. PSA noted that the gap between basic literacy and functional literacy is the highest among individuals aged 60 to 64 years. To alleviate the issue of illiteracy, the Department of Social Work and Development (DSWD) on Friday said it aims to institutionalize its initiative 'Tara, Basa! Tutoring Program.' The program provides financial assistance for college students majoring in education and social work who render at least 20 days of tutoring sessions for elementary students who need to enhance their reading and comprehension skills. 'Ang programa pong ito ay may executive order na mula sa Pangulo para mas mapabilis ang implementation. Pero yes, tama kayo, para for the longer term, kailangan natin ng batas. Sa Tara, Basa!, nakahanap na kami ng sponsor para sa bill,' DSWD Undersecretary Ed Punay said. Since its pilot implementation in 2023, the DSWD program has already helped over 350,000 beneficiaries, including struggling young readers, their parents, and college student tutors. On the part of the Department of Education, the agency has revised its senior high school curriculum and implemented it in some public schools this school year 2025-2026. The revised SHS curriculum reduced the core subjects from 15 to 5, to allow students to focus on their specialized subjects and be prepared for higher education and employment after graduation. The core subjects will be taught across two semesters to promote better lesson retention and ease academic workload. The DepEd is also filling some 16,000 new teaching positions by the third quarter of this year to address the insufficient manpower in the education sector, especially in kindergarten, elementary, and subject-specific areas in junior and senior high school. MANILA — More than 700,000 Filipinos aged 5 and above could not read or write in 2024, the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA) said in a report.

title: Excitement as Pinoys prepare to watch final presidential debate
body: Security is also tight at the University of Pangasinan, where 2016 hopefuls Jejomar Binay, Miriam Defensor-Santiago, Rodrigo Duterte, Grace Poe, and Manuel Roxas will go head-to-head in the last Commission on Elections (Comelec) debate Sunday. LOOK: Security personnel deployed outside UPang for the #PiliPinasDebates2016 pic.twitter.com/Ma3tTg1rjP | via @KerOliva #Halalan2016 Groups from all over Pangasinan and nearby provinces are cheering for their respective bets while clad in their candidates' campaign colors. Large tarpaulins can be seen at the vicinity of the debate venue, while campaign jingles rang in every corner. Presidential candidates' supporters gather outside UPang for #PiliPinasDebates2016 #Halalan2016 | @ivymaepolintan pic.twitter.com/Nth4NkPqFm 'Youth for Miriam' arrives at UPang to support the senator for #PiliPinasDebates2016 #Halalan2016 | @ivymaepolintan pic.twitter.com/JwL0QXKSE1 Rallyists also took the chance to air their grievances. Online, netizens are also expressing excitement over the presidential debates. Some are expecting heated exchanges while others want to see who will win this round. Doing the household chores before 5:45pm. I'm excited. #PiliPinasDebates2016 Finale debate later. Sino ang lilitaw at sino ang lulubog sa kanilang LIMA. #PiliPinasDebates2016 Eto na, eto na. Final leg of the #PiliPinasDebates2016 . Candidates, try not to devour each other. I predict that all tirades will be on Duterte tonight at the #PiliPinasDebates2016 crabs will do anything to get on top Excited na ako sa bangayan ni Roxas at Paring Du30 mamaya! 😂😍 #PiliPinasDebates2016 For some, the debate will also help voters decide who to vote for. I think most people have already decided who to vote. Watching #PiliPinasDebates2016 is just to confirm their biases. The debate will carry a townhall format, where people will be allowed to ask the candidates about pressing issues. Candidates will bare platforms and stand on issues before a live audience, in a program aired on ABS-CBN Channel 2, simulcast on cable channel ANC and radio dzMM. Livestreaming is also available on NEWS.ABS-CBN.COM ( http://news.abs-cbn.com/livE ) and iWantTv. The debate is in partnership with Manila Bulletin and the KBP. PANGASINAN-- Excitement is palpable in Dagupan, Pangasinan, the venue of the final face-off of the five presidential candidates.

title: Vietnam gets new PM with tough task and big shoes to fill
body: Phuc rises from deputy prime minister to lead a government committed to overhauling its troubled state sector and broad reforms under a U.S.-led Trans-Pacific Partnership, a major trade pact covering four-tenths of the global economy. The 61-year-old completes the trio of core leaders of a youthful country hooked on social media, and growing in its awareness of politics and Vietnam's complex ties with China and the United States. Phuc has some big shoes to fill, taking over from Nguyen Tan Dung, a tough-talking reformist whose decisiveness won him broad support but, say experts, saw him sidelined by conservatives concerned he would become too powerful. Vietnam is officially ruled by consensus with key decisions made by the Communist Party's elite politburo. Dung had served a maximum two terms and is no longer a politburo member, although key policy makers from his government are among the new 19-member body. 'Phuc certainly will be lower key than the hard-charging Dung,' said Murray Hiebert, a Southeast Asia specialist at Washington's Center for Strategic and International Studies. 'We should expect him to operate within the consensus of the ruling politburo. He will have seen the impact on Dung of his more flamboyant, independent style.' Phuc was the only candidate chosen at the party's January congress. His appointment for a five-year term was approved by 475 of 480 lawmakers present. He is from the central province of Quang Nam and his expertise is management and economics. He held key posts in local politics and on legislative committees and was once head of planning and investment in Danang, Vietnam's third-biggest city. Phuc becomes part of a new triumvirate with party chief Nguyen Phu Trong and President Tran Dai Quang, who was endorsed last week. Addressing the assembly, Phuc pledged to achieve targets for the economy, which grew 6.7 percent in 2015, tackle graft, improve the investment climate and fight to protect Vietnam's sovereignty. Hiebert said Phuc would initially have a lower international profile than Dung, who stood up to China's assertiveness in the South China Sea and sought tighter U.S. ties. 'Phuc will recognize that there's broad consensus in Vietnam not to let Beijing push Hanoi around and hedge ties with China through closer relations with Washington,' he said. HANOI - Vietnam's parliament approved a new prime minister on Thursday, handing former bureaucrat and legislator Nguyen Xuan Phuc the challenge of maintaining the momentum of one of Asia's fastest-growing economies.

title: ITCZ in Mindanao, fair weather in Metro Manila for next 2-3 days: PAGASA
body: PAGASA weather forecaster Rhea Torres said the ITCZ will bring cloudy skies with scattered rainshowers and thunderstorms in Davao Region, Caraga, and Eastern Visayas, which could bring flash floods or landslides. Metro Manila and the rest of the country will have partly cloudy to cloudy skies with isolated rainshowers or thunderstorms. Courtesy of TeleRadyo Serbisyo An intertropical convergence zone will continue to affect Mindanao while fair weather conditions will prevail over Metro Manila and the rest of the country in the next 2-3 days, weather bureau PAGASA said Thursday.

title: ‘Dapat Isa Lang’ movement launches push for anti-political dynasty law through People’s Initiative
body: The coalition includes 1SAMBAYAN, Clergy for Good Governance, Simbahang Lingkod ng Bayan, Kontra Dinastiya, Makati Business Club, People’s Budget Coalition, and the Ateneo Human Rights Center. The proposed measure would limit political families to only one elected official in a national position and one in a local position. It also seeks to prohibit relatives up to the fourth degree of consanguinity or affinity — including cousins — from simultaneously holding office. Under the People’s Initiative process, proponents must gather signatures from at least 10 percent of registered voters nationwide, or around 6.9 million voters, with at least 3 percent from every legislative district. Once the signatures are collected, a petition will be filed before the Commission on Elections (COMELEC), which will verify the authenticity of signatures and determine whether signatories understood the proposal. The poll body would then hold a nationwide referendum where voters would decide whether to approve or reject the measure. The group said a People’s Initiative has become necessary because Congress has failed to pass an anti-political dynasty law nearly four decades after the 1987 Constitution mandated it. Lawyer Alex Lacson, founder of Kontra Dinastiya, criticized pending anti-dynasty bills in both chambers of Congress, saying these contain loopholes that would still allow political families to retain power. '‘Yung sa Senate version, although dalawa lang, isa sa national, isa sa local, pero hanggang the second degree,' Lacson said. 'Ang ibig sabihin, hindi covered ‘yung pamangkin, hindi covered ‘yung tita, tito, hindi covered ‘yung pinsan. Anong mangyayari doon? After the three terms, maximum terms, ipapasa nila sa pamangkin nila, sa tito, tita nila, at sa mga pinsan nila. ‘Yan ang nangyayari,' he added. Lacson also criticized House Bill 8389, principally authored by Isabela Rep. Faustino 'Bojie' Dy III, along with Ilocos Norte Rep. Sandro Marcos. 'It allows one family, one in the national but several congressmen basta magkaiba daw ng distrito, several governors basta magkaiba daw na probinsya, several mayors,' Lacson said. 'Is that really anti-dynasty? Huwag na tayong maglokohan pa,' he added. Dy has previously defended his bill, saying it would help Congress fulfill its constitutional mandate to prohibit political dynasties. Former Supreme Court Associate Justice Antonio Carpio, meanwhile, argued that political dynasties contribute to corruption and misuse of public funds. 'About 80 percent of the members of the House and 75 percent of the members of the Senate belong to political dynasties. And every year, the political dynasties in effect determine the budget,' Carpio stated. 'Over a trillion pesos have been lost to flood control projects, flood control lang ha. So this is how they perpetuate themselves in power, by getting money from the budget through flood control projects, through ayudas. And every year it grows and grows,' he added. Carpio said breaking political dynasties is necessary to stop families from controlling the national budget and political system. The movement said passing a stricter anti-dynasty law would open the political process to more qualified and deserving candidates. Attorney Carlo Africa, spokesperson of Dapat Isa Lang, said the coalition has begun pilot signature drives in Zamboanga and Naga City ahead of a nationwide rollout in the coming days. The group hopes to complete signature gathering before the start of the 2028 election cycle. Africa said online signature collection is also being explored, particularly for overseas Filipino voters, although discussions with COMELEC are still ongoing. 'Right now, we are talking to multiple schools and institutions to become collection sites,' Africa said. 'You can reach out to us and we will give you the proper training, we'll give you the proper process to make sure na we are fully compliant with the legal safeguards and standards,' Africa added. The movement also assured the public that safeguards would be in place to ensure the validity of signatures, including identity verification measures for online signatories. Africa said COMELEC initially estimated that around P16 billion may be needed to conduct a nationwide referendum. Former COMELEC commissioner Luie Guia, however, said the poll body already maintains contingency funds for exercises such as recall elections, referendums, and plebiscites. 'Mandato ng COMELEC na magpatakbo ng referendum,' Guia said. Despite the challenges, organizers expressed confidence they can secure the required number of signatures. 'Sa tingin ko ay handa naman po ang mga Pilipinong ipaglaban ang tunay na demokrasya na hindi na po tayo bihag ng mga iilang pamilya,' said Atty. Eirene Aguila of the People’s Initiative Coalition Against Dynasties. A coalition of civic, religious, and advocacy groups on Friday launched the 'Dapat Isa Lang' movement, seeking to pass what it calls a 'genuine' anti-political dynasty law through a People’s Initiative. QUEZON CITY — A coalition of civic, religious, and advocacy groups on Friday launched the 'Dapat Isa Lang' movement, seeking to pass what it calls a 'genuine' anti-political dynasty law through a People’s Initiative.

title: BuCor to get 'first taste' of Duterte's corruption crackdown
body: A series of raids on the National Bilibid Prisons since late 2014 have revealed convicted crime lords were living in air-conditioned villas with stripper poles and recording studios, raising allegations that their guards were on the take. 'I believe that I have the blessing of the president. My action in the Bureau of Correction will be very very drastic. They will not like it, but it will be effective,' Aguirre told reporters. 'BuCor shall be the first to taste that kind of administration from the Secretary of Justice,' he added. Aguirre said he would not tolerate corruption in the justice system. 'Ang unang pagkakaiba, you will have a very honest, not corrupt DOJ secretary. Corruption is zero, and that was my marching order from the president,' he said. Aguirre attended Duterte's proclamation as president-elect at the House of Representatives on Monday. Duterte skipped the event to attend to his duties as Davao City mayor. 'Tinatanggap naman ni Mayor Duterte, nagpapasalamat siya, nagpapaabot siya ng pasasalamat sa lahat ng 16.6 million na sumoporta sa kanya,' he told reporters. MANILA - Incoming Justice Secretary Vitaliano Aguirre said he would implement 'very drastic' changes in the country's prison system, which is hounded by allegations of corruption.

title: Manila court enters not guilty plea for Teves in another murder case
body: Teves’ lawyer Ferdinand Topacio said the Manila Regional Trial Court Branch 15 entered a not guilty plea, as required when an accused refuses to enter one. This particular case involves the 2019 murder of a Negros Oriental board member, which according to Topacio, was previously dismissed by the Department of Justice (DOJ). 'Na-dismiss na po ito ng DOJ after preliminary investigation, so binuhay lang naman po ito,' Topacio said. When asked why his client again refused to enter a plea, Topacio said Teves opposes the manner of his repatriation from Timor-Leste. 'He's still protesting the manner by which he was repatriated by the Philippine government. In fact, it was such a controversy sa Timor-Leste na iniimbestigahan ng parliament ng Timor-Leste,' he said. Teves was detained at a driving range in Dili last year, but a Timorese court blocked his extradition. In an abrupt turnaround, Timor-Leste late in May deported Teves, saying his continued presence represented a security risk. According to Topacio, the pre-trial has begun, but they requested that Teves be brought back to the Bureau of Jail Management and Penology (BJMP) Annex 2 facility where he is detained. 'Ni-request po namin sa hukuman na ibalik na po si Ginoong Teves sa kaniyang place of detention for security reasons. Most importantly, ayaw na rin nating nasi-strain 'yung resources ng ating gobyerno,' he said. Topacio said Teves was in good condition and his doctors can now visit him at the detention facility to avoid the need for transfers. 'Okay naman [siya]. Last week, may follow-up check up siya from his doctors, pero dun na po pumupunta sa Annex 2 para di na rin po maabala 'yung BJMP,' he said. 'Matindi po 'yung logistical requirement pag ginagalaw siya. As much as possible, ayaw na namin, to save on government resources,' he added. On Monday, Teves refused to enter a plea during his arraignment in a separate 2019 murder case under Manila RTC Branch 12. Teves is facing multiple murder charges, including one in connection with the assassination of Negros Oriental Governor Roel Degamo in 2023. Former Negros Oriental Representative Arnolfo 'Arnie' Teves Jr. again refused to enter a plea during his arraignment on Tuesday before a Manila court in one of his murder cases. MANILA — Former Negros Oriental Representative Arnolfo 'Arnie' Teves Jr. again refused to enter a plea during his arraignment on Tuesday before a Manila court in one of his murder cases.

title: More than 200 arrested in Manila protest riot; Isko tags alleged instigators
body: Of the 212 arrested, 89 are minors, with 24 of them aged 12 and below. The Manila city government and the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) have confirmed they are investigating certain groups or individuals suspected to be behind the chaos. Despite heavy rain, various organizations held a protest rally Monday afternoon in front of the MPD headquarters, calling for the release of those arrested during the violent protest. Families of some detainees waited outside the MPD gates. 'Sabi nila nahuli daw sa rally. E ang alam ko po pumunta lang sa Divisoria para bumili ng damit. Kagabi pa po kami nandito, hindi pa po namin nakikita kung nasaan na,' said Romeo Paneda, father of one of the rescued minors. 'Nagpaalam po siya samin na lalabas siya pero hindi niya sinabi kung saan siya pupunta. Hindi ko po papayagan. Di ko po ia-allow na ganon, 13 lang ‘yong anak ko, hindi ko siya pasasamahin sa ganon,' added Clarizza Babaran, the mother of another minor involved. According to MPD spokesperson Police Major Philipp Ines, the minors will be transferred to the custody of the Department of Social Welfare and Development while several criminal charges will be filed against the arrested individuals. 'May mga kaso po tayo ipag-haharap dito katulad po ng arson, ng direct assault, physical injuries, malicious mischief at lahat po ng mga nilabag nila, isasampa po natin laban dito sa mga taong ito,' Ines said. 'Unang-una po, palaging namin gustong sabihin sa inyo at pinanawagan na po namin, huwag niyo po kami itratong kaaway niyo. Kakampi niyo po kami. Kalaban po natin ang korupsyon. Nandito lamang po kami para bantayan po ang kaayusan,' he added. Authorities are still identifying those who orchestrated the attacks, but Manila Mayor Francisco 'Isko Moreno' Domagoso earlier confirmed that they received information of possible individuals who instigated it. 'Mga dayo eh. Taguig, nakita ko yung mga address eh. Taguig, Pasay, Paranaque, Caloocan, Quezon City. May nag-organize eh. Inaalam namin ngayon yung ano. May mga balita kami, mga dating politiko, Filipino-Chinese. May isa naman, lawyer. Ayan yung mga binabantayan namin ngayon,' said Domagoso. DILG Secretary Jonvic Remulla also expressed concerns over possible deliberate incitement to violence. 'Our biggest fear did not happen: That someone will incite violence in the People Power Rally. Our biggest fear was there would be instigation there,' he said. Remulla revealed that the DILG is now coordinating with the Department of Justice (DOJ) on the possibility of designating certain individuals or groups as terrorists, which he considered 'anarchists'. 'Alam na namin kung anong grupo pero hindi pa namin pwede sabihin kasi yung actual participants kinoconnect pa namin sa kanila. We will confirm after all the interviews are done,' Remulla added. Police, however, denied allegations of gunfire during the dispersal. 'Wala po tayong gano'ng impormasyon at alam naman po natin yung tinatawag nating standard operating procedure pagdating po sa mga ganito. Wala po tayong reported na ganyan,' Ines said. Meanwhile, 95 police officers were injured, including 17 who were admitted in different hospitals. MPD officers recounted violent encounters during the protest, particularly at Ayala Bridge, where tires and a motorcycle were set ablaze. '‘Yong isa nga po naming nakita, may hawak pang palakol na nasa bag niya. May nakita kaming bote na may lamang gas po. No’ng naka-shield po kami, naramdaman ko na parang may namanhid po sa kamay ko, pag tingin ko umaagos na yong dugo,' said Patrolman Louie Versoza of MPD’s Civil Disturbance Management. In a separate incident in Mendiola, Patrolman Emerson Macaslig suffered head injuries after being mobbed by protesters as seen in a viral video. 'Hinugot po kami nung nabuwag na po ‘yung barrier po namin. At doon na po nagsimula na pinagpapalo na din po kami. Tinulungan ko ‘yung senior ko kasi matanda na po.. Pero sa kasamaang palad po, ako po ‘yung napuruhan,' said Macaslig in an interview. The Commission on Human Rights (CHR) condemned the violence that unfolded during the protest. 'Para sa amin, hindi po kailangang sirain yung mga traffic lights, hindi po kailangang sunugin ‘yong mga motorsiklo, hindi po kailangang sunugin yung truck, and lalong-lalo na hindi po dapat pinagnakawan ‘yong isang hotel along Recto,' said CHR Commissioner Beda Epres in a phone interview. The CHR also vowed to assist victims and ensure due process for all, especially the minors. 'We will make it a point na magkaroon po ng proper investigation, magkaroon po ng due process doon sa investigation na isasagawa doon po sa mga nahuling protesters, especially the minors. If ever po kailangan nila ng legal assistance, magsabi lang po sila,' Epres added. Meanwhile, both the DILG and DOJ assured the public that the rights of all arrested minors will be respected as investigations continue. 'Mga dayo eh. Taguig, nakita ko yung mga address eh. Taguig, Pasay, Paranaque, Caloocan, Quezon City. May nag-organize eh. Inaalam namin ngayon yung ano. May mga balita kami, mga dating politiko, Filipino-Chinese. May isa naman, lawyer. Ayan yung mga binabantayan namin ngayon,' Domagoso said. MANILA - More than 200 individuals believed to be involved in a riot that erupted in Manila during anti-corruption protests on Sunday are now in police custody, according to the Manila Police District (MPD).

title: Senators, House Speaker hail Angara's appointment as next DepEd chief
body: Angara will begin leading the DepEd starting July 19 when his predecessor Vice President Sara Duterte officially steps down from the agency. Angara is on his second of 2 allowed consecutive terms in the Senate. Senate President Francis Escudero said that while Angara's task is 'indeed gargantuan,' he has 'full confidence in his ability to lead the DepEd with excellence and integrity.' 'His proven track record in legislative work, particularly in education reform, uniquely positions him to address the challenges and opportunities that lie ahead,' he said. The DepEd is the biggest bureaucracy in the government and receives the highest allocation in the annual national budget. Angara has a Master of Laws from Harvard University, a Bachelor of Laws from the University of the Philippines, and a Bachelor of Science in Economics from the London School of Economics. Senate Majority Leader Francis Tolentino said that while the upper chamber would 'lose another legal mind as a consequence,' Angara 'will do a good job as DepEd secretary with his EdCom background and familiarity with current issues.' For former Senate President Juan Miguel Zubiri, Angara is 'more than qualified' to head the country's 'most important agency, with the biggest share in the national budget no less.' House Speaker Martin Romualdez said Angara's background and legislative work in education policy equip him with the expertise needed to lead the DepEd. 'The House of Representatives fully supports Secretary Angara in this new chapter. We are eager to collaborate with him to realize our vision of ensuring every Filipino child has access to quality education,' Romualdez said. EdCom commissioner Rep. Jose Francisco 'Kiko' Benitez also welcomed Angara's appointment to the education agency, saying the sector needs a leader who 'intimately knows the ins and outs of our education system, and understands it from a finance and management perspective.' 'In this regard, Senator Angara is the right man for the job,' he said. The Coordinating Council of Private Educational Associations (COCOPEA) also lauded Angara's appointment. 'First of all, we congratulate the honorable Senator on his appointment and this is a welcome development, we look forward to his efforts as the new Education chief,' Atty. Kristine Carmina Manaog, COCOPEA’s legal counsel, told ABS-CBN News. Sen. Angara was one of the five names that COCOPEA endorsed to Pres. Marcos when it submitted a letter to his office recently. Atty. Manaog said they believe Angara is a good fit for the role given the number of legislative measures he advocated for the education sector. 'Senator Angara we believe has extensive experience in education, especially in his legislative efforts for education reforms and we also think and see that he has a deep seated commitment sa education. And we also saw in his career, he has consistently demonstrated a strong dedication to advancing and uplifting education here in the Philippines,' Atty. Manaog said. A teachers' group had called on Marcos to appoint an educator and not a politician to the top education post. Benjo Basas, Teachers' Dignity Coalition's national chairperson, pointed out in a release that 'politicians always have political considerations, whether for upcoming elections or the interests of their party and political clan.' The Philippine Business for Education (PBEd) welcomed Angara's appointment, saying in a release that his 'swift' appointment is 'crucial to ensure that there is no disruption' in Filipino children's education. PBEd said that while it acknowledges the significance of emphasizing competence over political considerations in the appointment of a new DepEd secretary, Angara 'is expected to use his political savvy to navigate legislative processes, build partnerships, and secure resources and support for learners and teachers.' Sen. Juan Edgardo 'Sonny' Angara's colleagues, including House Speaker Martin Romualdez, on Tuesday hailed his appointment to the Marcos Cabinet as the next secretary of the Department of Education. MANILA — Sen. Juan Edgardo 'Sonny' Angara's colleagues, including House Speaker Martin Romualdez, on Tuesday hailed his appointment to the Marcos Cabinet as the next secretary of the Department of Education, saying his background in championing reforms in education made him qualified for the job. Sen. Juan Edgardo 'Sonny' Angara's colleagues, including House Speaker Martin Romualdez, on Tuesday hailed his appointment to the Marcos Cabinet as the next secretary of the Department of Education.

title: S. Africa mom sentenced for trying to sell baby on Internet
body: The 20-year-old mother will not spend time in jail, but will live under house arrest for three years. The woman, who cannot be named to protect the identity of the child, put the baby up for sale for 5,000 rand in an advertisement on the Gumtree website last year. A member of the public alerted the police who arrested her in an undercover operation in October. 'She was given a wholly-suspended sentence of five years,' National Prosecuting Authority spokeswoman Natasha Ramkisson-Kara told AFP. She will 'undergo correctional supervision, which means basically house arrest for a period of three years,' Ramkisson-Kara added. The mother said she had tried selling her baby after her boyfriend stopped paying childcare following paternity tests that showed he was not the child's father. Human trafficking carries a maximum life jail term or a fine of 100 million rand ($6.7 million). But the court sitting in the eastern city of Pietermaritzburg on Wednesday handed down a lighter sentence after considering the woman's circumstances. 'The magistrate found that the woman did not have intention per se to traffick the child,' said Ramkisson-Kara. JOHANNESBURG, South Africa - A South African mother who tried to sell her 19-month-old baby boy on the Internet for $340 was given a five-year suspended sentence, a justice official said Thursday.

title: Court upholds de Lima’s dismissal in drug case
body: The Muntinlupa City RTC Branch 204 acquitted incoming De Lima in her drug case that was remanded by the Court of Appeals last April. Her co-accused and former driver and bodyguard Ronnie Dayan was likewise acquitted of violation of Republic Act No. 9165 or the Comprehensive Dangerous Drugs Act of 2002 in relation to illegal drug trading. 'Wherefore both accused Leila M. De Lima y Magistrado and Ronnie Palisoc Dayan are hereby acquitted of the crime charged on the ground of reasonable doubt,' said Presiding Judge Abraham Joseph Alcantara. In the April 30, 2025 decision of the CA, the decision was remanded to the RTC following the petition filed by the Office of the Solicitor General 'for failure' of Alcantara to substantiate his perception of the alleged insufficiency of the prosecution’s evidence convicting De Lima and Ronnie Dayan. Judge Alcantara however said in his new decision that he has already 'clearly and distinctly'stated the facts and law upon which his May 12, 2023 decision was based. Alcantara further stated that the original decision essentially explained that the recantation of the prosecution's main witness already created reasonable doubt. 'The totality of said recantation, by itself, was sufficient basis for the RTC to uphold the Constitutionally guaranteed presumption of innocence,' Alcantara said. 'Vindicated numerous times, and yet they are relentless in trying to silence me,' she said, stressing what she sees as a continuous effort to suppress her voice. 'But no matter how hard they try, they cannot erase the truth, nor undo what has already been revealed in the light of justice.' The former senator also thanked the judge for his firmness. She said, 'Ito lang yata ang kaso sa Pilipinas na kailangan ulitin ng huwes ang pag render o promulgate ng judgment of acquittal.' This legal maneuver highlights the persistent challenges her legal team has faced. De Lima expressed hope that the latest acquittal would be the final blow to what she has long maintained is a politically motivated case. 'I hope and pray that this is the last nail in the coffin of this most expensive frame-up in Philippine history,' she explained. De Lima, who was recently elected to the House of Representatives, also vowed to continue her work for justice and reform. 'Patuloy po tayong maninindigan para sa hustisya, reporma, at para sa mga kapakanan ng mga nasa laylayan,' she added. She also stressed that despite past attempts to stop her, her resolve remains unbroken. 'Hindi po nila tayo napigilan noon, hinding hindi po tayo magpapapigil ngayon. Nakatindig pa rin po tayo,' she asserted. She ended her statement with a call for unity and urged the public to work together for a more accountable government. 'Sama-sama po tayo para sa isang mas makatarungang Pilipinas na may pananagutan ang mga nasa kapangyarihan,' de Lima concluded. 'Vindicated numerous times, and yet they are relentless in trying to silence me,' she said.
