body: Police found the document on a laptop computer discovered on March 22, the day suicide bombers from the Islamic State group launched attacks at Brussels airport and on the Belgian capital's metro which killed 32 people. The computer was found at one of the hideouts used by the jihadist attackers, according to a Belgian federal police report from April, which AFP gained access to on Wednesday. Investigators found the Word document dated February 2, 2016 -- over a month before the Brussels attacks -- which they believe is a letter from Abrini to his mother, according to the report. The letter, written in capitals and littered with spelling errors, 'can be considered his last testament' police said. The author, who calls himself 'Abou Yahya', writes that he had travelled to Syria and become 'very interested in religion' following the death of his younger brother there. 'After some research, Islamic State (IS) appeared legitimate in his eyes,' the police report said. In March, Belgian police arrested Abrini, a key suspect in the Paris attacks last November in which 130 people were killed. He also turned out to be the so-called 'man in the hat' seen in CCTV footage with two bombers shortly before they staged the Brussels airport assault. The Brussels investigators' summary said that Abrini believed the Paris attacks were 'just reward from Muslims to France, especially given its participation in the coalition against the Islamic state.' He spoke in the letter of the 'heroes...who set off explosions to terrify' the infidels. 'The tone of the letter, as well as its contests, makes it clear the wish of the person to die as a martyr, probably during a future terrorist attack,' the investigator said. Abrini, 31, a Belgian national of Moroccan descent was arrested in Brussels on April 8. BRUSSELS, Belgium - Belgian investigators said they have discovered a 'last testament' attributed to Brussels and Paris attacks suspect Mohamed Abrini, suggesting that he wanted to die a martyr.

title: Binay trying to deflect corruption criticism: Poe
body: In an ambush interview after visiting Baclaran Church, Poe said Binay was just deflecting corruption criticisms against him by raising the issue of American citizenship in her family. Binay has been fending off accusations that he and his son pocketed millions of pesos in kickbacks from Makati City infrastructure projects. During Sunday's debate, Binay was asked if he can be a president capable of seeing his son, former Makati Mayor Junjun Binay, convicted if the latter is found guilty of graft. Binay answered in the affirmative and insisted that his government will uphold the rule of law. Poe, however, countered that if Binay is willing to advocate the rule of law, he must first be ready to obey the simple guidelines of the debate. She said Binay should have defended himself and his son from corruption allegations by attending the hearings of the Senate on the issue. Binay then went on the offensive and lambasted Poe for forsaking her Filipino roots when she became a citizen of the United States between 2001 and 2010. 'Lagi mong sinasabi na ikaw ay tunay na Pilipino. Paano ka magiging tunay na Pilipino e sumumpa ka para maging Amerikano... Ikinakahiya mo ang pinanggalingan mo,' he said. READ: Binay, Poe trade barbs over corruption, citizenship issues After Binay asked about the ongoing renunciation of Poe's husband and children of their American citizenship, Poe said she had already answered even before the campaign period that the process of renouncing their US citizenship is still ongoing. Poe also reiterated the points she made during the Cebu debate such as her pro-coconut farmers stand. Poe's senatorial candidates Isko Moreno, Lorna Kapunan and Roman Romulo joined her small campaign rally near Baclaran church with vendors, church-goers and their supporters. Aside from Baclaran in Paranaque, Poe and her team also went to Don Carlos Multi-Purpose Hall in Pasay City. Poe and her team will take a break from the campaign this Holy Week starting Tuesday. MANILA - A day after attending the second PiliPinas debate, independent presidential candidate Grace Poe resumed her campaign in Baclaran and defended herself from the tirades of Vice President Jejomar Binay.

title: AFP says ready to support Coast Guard in West PH Sea
body: AFP spokesperson Col. Francel Margareth Padilla on Monday said the military is ready to support the Philippine Coast Guard and other maritime law enforcement agencies in their operations. 'The AFP supports the maritime law enforcement operation of the PCG and other maritime law enforcement agencies. For our part, any challenges faced from foreign forces are addressed with utmost professionalism and we will practice maximum tolerance,' Padilla told reporters in a Viber message. The AFP’s statement followed the latest incident between Philippine and Chinese vessels in Bajo de Masinloc or Scarborough Shoal. Manila on Sunday accused the China Coast Guard of attempting to block the Bureau of Fisheries and Aquatic Resources’ BRP Datu Sanday, which was delivering supplies to fishermen near Bajo de Masinloc. The incident happened on February 22, according to the PCG. Padilla stressed that the military will remain committed to protecting the country’s people and territory. 'The AFP commits in fulfilling its mandate to protect its people and territory. We will assert our presence in the WPS to protect our sovereignty, sovereign rights, and national interests,' she said. MANILA — The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) said it is ready to support law enforcement in the West Philippine Sea even as it maintained that it will practice maximum tolerance in 'addressing challenges from foreign forces.'

title: Marcos won’t monitor Duterte’s ICC hearing on Feb. 23—Palace
body: 'Hindi po imo-monitor, pero siyempre kailangan pong maging updated ang Pangulo sa lahat ng nangyayari, dahil mga kapwa Pilipino natin ang nasasangkot dito,' Palace Press Officer Undersecretary Claire Castro said in a briefing Friday, February 20. The Palace official maintained that what the President is monitoring is the country’s interest and whatever will transpire from the hearing is 'personal' to the former President. 'Ang sabi nga natin, ang mino-monitor po ng Pangulo ay iyong interes po ng ating bansa. Kung mayroon pong mangyayari sa hearing, ito po ay personal na po kay dating Pangulong Duterte,' she said. 'At tatandaan po natin na ang mga nagsampa po ng kasong ito ay kapwa nating Pilipino. Pilipino ang nagsampa ng kaso, nirereklamo ay kapwa nating Pilipino,' Castro added. Further asked if the government will help the kin of drug war victims in going to the Netherlands, Castro said the Department of Justice will extend assistance but it remains unclear up to what extent. Castro also refuted the 'kidnapping' claim of Duterte, arguing that the former president himself said he is willing to get arrested if the ICC issues an arrest warrant. Duterte earlier informed the ICC that he wishes to waive his right to attend his confirmation of charges hearing next week, citing his continued refusal to recognize the chamber’s jurisdiction and alleging that his 'kidnapping' was 'facilitated by the Office of the incumbent President of the Philippines.' '[Ang] purpose lamang nito ay sirain at ibagsak ang imahe ng ating Pangulo dahil mismo si dating Pangulong Duterte ang nagsabi na siya ay willing magpaaresto kapag nandiyan na ang warrant of arrest,' she said. She showed a printout – which she often does when responding to Dutertes – of a Reuters article published March 10, 2025 and quoted Duterte who said in a rally in Hong Kong, 'If this is truly my fate in life, it's okay, I will accept it. They can arrest me, imprison me.' 'So, papaano nilang masasabing may kidnapping kung mismo ang dating Pangulong Duterte ay handang magpaaresto kapag ang warrant of arrest ng ICC ay nandiyan na?' Castro stated. The Palace maintained that what the President is monitoring is the country’s interest and whatever will transpire from the hearing is 'personal' to the former President. MANILA — There is no need for President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. to monitor the proceedings of the International Criminal Court as the confirmation of charges hearing of former President Rodrigo Duterte begins on Monday, February 23. The Palace maintained that what the President is monitoring is the country’s interest and whatever will transpire from the hearing is 'personal' to the former President.

title: Bayanihan E-Konsulta now offers mental health services
body: According to Dr. Keisha Mangalili, operations head of Bayanihan E-Konsulta, they have so far received 70 patient requests for mental health consultations since October 28. Patients will undergo a psychosocial assessment to determine an appropriate intervention them, she told ANC's 'Rundown.' The teleconsultation service has psychologists, psychiatrists, counselors, and psychometricians in its roster. Mangalili said they are also seeking partnerships with different institutions in order to serve people with mental health concerns. Among them is MLAC Institute for Psychosocial Services, which she said helps train volunteers in handling psychosocial cases and provide briefing sessions. Bayanihan E-Konsulta was launched by former Vice President Leni Robredo to help address the COVID-19 pandemic. Since it was revived on July 25 , the program has served nearly 4,000 patients who are undergoing general care, specialist care, and COVID-19 consults. At least 10 to 20 percent of patient requests they received are COVID-19-related, Mangalili said. 'Many cases that we see are being treated at home. We offer support through monitoring and sending of our COVID care kits,' she added. Amid the lingering pandemic and with the upcoming holiday season, Mangalili said they are 'wary' in the easing of pandemic protocols, such as the lifting of indoor mask mandate. 'We would still prefer to implement for at least indoors 'yung masking since not all facilities would have proper ventilation that would help reduce the risk of having COVID,' she said. MANILA — Three months after the relaunch of Bayanihan E-Konsulta, the telemedicine platform is now also offering mental health services.

title: Binay camp: LP using P100-B BUB fund for campaign
body: Speaking to reporters in Rizal, United Nationalist Alliance (UNA) spokesperson Mon Ilagan said the ruling party is legitimizing bribery through the BUB program which, according to the administration, allows the people at the barangay level to determine the projects they need. READ: PNoy, Roxas woo barangay leaders with BUB ''Iyong sinasabi nga natin, doon sa bribe your barangay, nagsisimula na, hindi ba? Parang sinasabi 'nong kalaban sa politika, bilangin ninyo ang numero ng inyong populasyon, bawat isa isang libo. Bibigyan natin ng isang libo iyan in a form of project. O hindi ba, sa barangay ibinibigay, anong klase iyon?' Ilagan said. The continuation and expansion of the BUB program is one of the promises that administration bet Mar Roxas has been making as he visits places in the country for the 90-day official campaign period. Under the program, Roxas said barangay chiefs will have access to some P100 billion in funds each year. The distribution of funds depends on the population of the barangay. Ideally, P1,000 is allotted for each person. Ilagan said while the concept of BUB program is good, for the administration to dangle the funds under it to barangay officials is questionable. ''The concept is good, pero I think 'yong timing ngayon is medyo questionable. For example, marami kaming mga local government officials na nakakausap sa iba't ibang lalawigan, they are now waiting for the budget na naipangako sa mga barangay, bawat munisipyo pero until now, wala pa. So again, questionable. Di ba parang this is a form of bribe na naman sa mga local officials,'' he said. The camp of Davao City Mayor Rodrigo Duterte , another Roxas opponent, said the BUB program is a way of the ruling party to buy votes. The Palace downplayed the allegation, saying the program will benefit any administration that will replace that of President Aquino. MANILA – The camp of Vice President Jejomar Binay criticized administration bet Mar Roxas for using government funds, through the so-called bottom-upb (BUB) program, supposedly to get the support of barangay officials.

title: Major Trump fundraiser the latest in big bucks battle with Biden
body: While spending huge sums of money on campaigning may not be something to brag about in some countries, it's often a source of pride for US political candidates. And this year, raising big bucks is as important as ever, with the November 5 presidential election expected to be the most expensive electoral cycle in US history. The Republican former president and the Democratic incumbent have been working to outraise each other for months, boasting about each new significant cash infusion. Biden, 81, raised the stakes at the end of March when he headlined a huge event in New York with former presidents Barack Obama and Bill Clinton at his side, raking in at least $25 million — a record, according to his team. But the 77-year-old Trump is hoping to raise nearly twice as much at the Florida event on Saturday evening — between $43 and $50 million, according to US media. The event will take place at the home of billionaire hedge fund manager John Paulson, not far from Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort. 'The response to our fundraising efforts has been overwhelming, and we've raised over $43 million so far. There is massive support amongst a broad spectrum of donors,' Paulson said in a statement sent to AFP. 'Biggest night in Fund Raising of ALL TIME!!! Will double up the Biden number of last week at Radio City,' Trump wrote on his Truth Social platform. The Palm Beach guest list will include businessman Robert Bigelow, who made a fortune in the hotel industry before launching an aerospace research company, and John Catsimatidis, owner of a major grocery store chain. Both are major Republican donors. Some of Trump's former Republican rivals will also be in attendance, including Senator Tim Scott, tech entrepreneur Vivek Ramaswamy and North Dakota Governor Doug Burgum. After suspending their presidential campaigns, the trio have fully endorsed Trump, perhaps angling for a job in his administration if he succeeds in returning to the White House. According to The Washington Post, a seat at Trump's table costs $814,600. Such astronomical sums are used to finance candidates' travel, pay their aides, commission opinion polls and, the costliest of all -- buy television ads. Biden, who portrays himself as a product and champion of the middle class, was quick to criticize Trump's fundraiser. 'While Trump is raising money from a bunch of hedge fund billionaires, our grassroots campaign raised $187M this quarter because of folks like you,' he said Saturday on X, formerly Twitter. At the moment, Biden's campaign war chest is bigger than Trump's, as the former president faces mounting legal fees stemming from multiple criminal indictments. Biden's campaign was all too happy to point that out, referring to Trump as 'Broke Don' in a recent statement about the billionaire, who himself is known for using mean nicknames. WASHINGTON — Major political donors will gather Saturday in Donald Trump's fiefdom of Palm Beach for a fundraising event that his campaign hopes will mark a new phase in a swaggering, high-stakes race for big money with Joe Biden.

title: US destroyer fires on, seizes Iran cargo ship trying to evade blockade
body: Trump posted on Truth Social that after the Iranian vessel, Touska, ignored warnings to stop, the guided missile destroyer USS Spruance 'stopped them right in their tracks by blowing a hole in the engineroom.' 'Right now, U.S. Marines have custody of the vessel,' the US president added, 'and are seeing what's on board!' The incident comes with tensions high in the Strait of Hormuz, a vital conduit for the world's oil and liquefied natural gas, which has been virtually closed since the start of the US-Israeli war with Iran seven weeks ago. Iran briefly reopened the strait on Friday in recognition of an Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire in Lebanon, but closed it again the following day in response to the United States maintaining its blockade on ships traveling to and from Iranian ports. US Central Command (CENTCOM) said Sunday that the Touska was attempting to sail toward the Iranian port of Bandar Abbas. The Spruance fired several rounds from a five-inch caliber gun to disable the Iranian ship's propulsion after directing it 'to evacuate its engine room,' CENTCOM said in a post on X accompanied by a short video showing rounds fired at a vessel in the distance after the warning. CENTCOM said US forces have directed 25 commercial vessels to turn around or return to an Iranian port since the US blockade began. Trump said the Touska is under US Treasury sanctions 'because of prior history of illegal activity.' The Touska is listed on the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control website as an Iranian-flagged container under US sanctions. According to the latest data available from the Marine Traffic website, about six hours before Trump's announcement the Touska was about 45 kilometers (28 miles) off the southern coast of Iran, near the city of Chabahar. Another ship monitoring site, Tanker Tracker, said the Touska had sailed from Malaysia. A US destroyer on Sunday fired on and seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman that tried to evade a US naval blockade, President Donald Trump and the US military said Sunday. WASHINGTON, United States — A US destroyer on Sunday fired on and seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman that tried to evade a US naval blockade, President Donald Trump and the US military said Sunday.

title: Surging billionaire wealth a political threat, Oxfam warns
body: US President Donald Trump's policies in particular spurred the fortunes of the ultra-rich, which jumped 16.2 percent in the first year of his second term to $18.3 trillion, the NGO said in a report released each year ahead of the Davos forum. 'Actions of the Trump presidency including the championing of deregulation and undermining agreements to increase corporate taxation have benefited the richest around the world,' Oxfam said. The world now has more than 3,000 billionaires for the first time, it added, with the top 12 -- led by Tesla and SpaceX chief Elon Musk -- having 'more wealth than the poorest half of humanity, or more than four billion people'. Increasingly this money is buying political power, Oxfam said, pointing in particular to tycoons' buying newspapers and other media, such as Musk's takeover of X or the purchase of The Washington Post by Amazon's Jeff Bezos. 'The widening gap between the rich and the rest is at the same time creating a political deficit that is highly dangerous and unsustainable,' Oxfam's executive director Amitabh Behar said. Trump will arrive with one of the biggest US delegations ever for the Davos conference, where he is expected to dominate an agenda officially billed as 'A Spirit of Dialogue'. Trump's participation galvanized around 300 protesters who arrived in Davos on Sunday, many wearing masks of Musk or US Vice President JD Vance and holding fistfuls of euros. Nathalie Ruoss of the Swiss Young Socialists told AFP the most powerful people in the world make decisions at Davos that impact everyone. 'And they do it with no democratic legitimacy,' she said, calling it 'unacceptable' that the WEF welcomed 'fascists like Donald Trump.' For Oxfam, Washington's decision to exempt US multinationals from an internationally agreed minimum tax rate of 15 percent was a stark example of ignoring growing inequality. 'In country after country, the super-rich have not only accumulated more wealth than could ever be spent, but have also used this wealth to secure the political power to shape the rules that define our economies and govern nations,' it said. 'Such power gives billionaires a grasp over all our futures, undermining political freedom and eroding the rights of the many.' The collective wealth of the planet's billionaires soared to a record level in 2025, charity Oxfam reported Monday, warning of 'highly dangerous' political consequences as the global elite gathers for the World Economic Forum. DAVOS, Switzerland — The collective wealth of the planet's billionaires soared to a record level in 2025, charity Oxfam reported Monday, warning of 'highly dangerous' political consequences as the global elite gathers for the World Economic Forum. The collective wealth of the planet's billionaires soared to a record level in 2025, charity Oxfam reported Monday, warning of 'highly dangerous' political consequences as the global elite gathers for the World Economic Forum.

title: PNP mobilizes full force to help quake-hit Cebu
body: Acting PNP chief Lt. Gen. Jose Melencio Nartatez Jr. said police units were instructed to immediately assist local government units in relief and security operations, particularly in areas hit hardest by the tremor. 'Our personnel on the ground were among the first responders and we assure the people of Cebu that we will extend all the necessary assistance— from securing affected establishments to attending to the needs of the local residents,' Nartatez said in a statement. The earthquake, which jolted parts of the Visayas, caused damage to historic churches and vital infrastructure, and left portions of Cebu province without electricity. Reports of casualties have been confirmed by local officials, although the full extent was still being assessed. The PNP activated its Disaster Incident Management Task Group in Region 7 and directed a Rapid Damage Assessment and Needs Analysis (RDANA) to guide emergency operations. Police were ordered to secure damaged buildings and critical facilities to prevent looting and ensure public safety, especially in densely populated and commercial areas. 'We are closely monitoring the development on the ground to maximize police assistance in areas where additional manpower, and your PNP is needed,' Nartatez added. Police stations in Cebu were directed to keep communication lines open for any urgent requests, including evacuation support and coordination with disaster response agencies. The PNP said additional resources from the national headquarters and nearby regions were on standby to reinforce local units if needed. Local authorities were closely working with the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (Phivolcs) to monitor developments, particularly on the likelihood of aftershocks following the powerful quake. The Philippine National Police (PNP) has deployed personnel and resources across Cebu and nearby provinces following the magnitude 6.9 earthquake that struck the region Tuesday night. MANILA — The Philippine National Police (PNP) has deployed personnel and resources across Cebu and nearby provinces following the magnitude 6.9 earthquake that struck the region Tuesday night.

title: Household transmission 'major fuel' in COVID-19 surge: ex-health chief
body: 'Right now, the major fuel of this surge is really household transmission. Instead of only 1 or 2 persons in the household getting COVID-19 as we've seen in the past year, it's now the entire household, like 10 or 12 people testing positive all the same time,' Dr. Paulyn Ubial told ANC. President Rodrigo Duterte has placed the capital region and its neighboring provinces under general community quarantine for 2 weeks amid spike in virus cases. Wearing of mask for the elderly and the vulnerable and for the rest of the household is strongly advised. With the lack of anti-coronavirus jabs, she called for massive testing to quash fresh coronavirus cases. 'If we will rely on vaccination, I don't think that will be forthcoming because really there's a global shortage. So, the best way to look at this is still to test massively. We do household per household. No longer individuals,' Ubial said. The Philippines launched its vaccination drive on March 1 and has so far inoculated over 293,000. Ubial, who leads the molecular laboratory at the Philippine Red Cross, also noted that the public and government became complacent in fight against COVID-19. For her, the public became weary of the pandemic due to lengthy restrictions on movement. 'People are really still being complacent. They are no longer afraid to go out and mingle,' she said. Some people, Ubial said, were also in denial if they tested positive for the virus. 'We've heard stories like that they wanted a repeat of the testing. They don't believe they are positive because they're asymptomatic. It can't be true. Maybe there's a problem in the laboratory. So, there's a lot of, shall we say, denial. How can I be affected by COVID-19 when I'm doing all the minimum health protocols?' she said. Ubial said the government also became complacent after battling the virus for over a year. She lamented the frequent policy changes by health authorities without reportedly vetting the process to the private sector. 'Like the one-stop-shop in the airport, we used to meet every week. Now it's once a month or every 2 weeks. Also, they changed the policy from testing at the airport to testing in the hotel. Now, there's a lot of operational issues,' she said. Ubial said testing was taking a longer time and overseas Filipino workers were staying for more days in quarantine facilities. 'It's really a nightmare right now what they did with the policy of testing in the quarantine hotels,' she said. On Saturday, the Philippines logged 7,999 coronavirus cases, its highest daily tally of infections since the pandemic began. More than 577,000 have recovered from the disease while nearly 13,000 have died. Active cases stood at 73,000. A former health secretary said Monday the public and government became complacent in fight against COVID-19, which was partly to blame for the recent surge of coronavirus cases in the country. MANILA - Household transmission is driving the surge of COVID-19 cases in the country, a former health secretary said Monday, as coronavirus infections topped 663,000 after a year into the pandemic.

title: AFP tells China: PH not expansionist, only protecting territory, maritime domain
body: AFP spokesperson Col. Francel Margareth Padilla said the Mid-Range Capability (MRC) missile system’s presence in the country was part of the long-standing defense alliance of between Manila and Washington. 'In terms of the AFP, the Typhon Mid-Range Capability missile system natin in the country is here, in the side of the AFP, for training purposes... It’s for us to enhance our operational readiness and our capability development as well,' Padilla told ABS-CBN News in a phone interview on Tuesday. In a press briefing in Beijing on Friday, China’s Ministry of National Defense spokesperson Senior Colonel Zhang Xiaogang 'required' the Philippines to 'recognize the high sensitivity and severe consequences of the deployment of the Typhon missile system and remove it as soon as possible to honor its previous open promises.' Zhang added that the missile system was an 'offensive weapon.' 'Such decision would only place the Philippines’ own security and national defense in the hands of others, and lead to geopolitical confrontation and risks of arms race in the region. It’s like giving an open invitation to the burglar and assisting the evildoer,' he said. 'By using the deployment of [the] Typhon as a bargaining chip on the South China issue, the Philippine side is selling out its own national security, putting the well-being of its people and regional peace and stability at grave risks. Such behavior is ridiculous and very dangerous,' he further claimed. Padilla stressed that the Philippines is not expanding but protecting its own territory and maritime domain. 'We’d like to emphasize that the AFP and the Philippines as a whole has never been an expansionist force. Our military capabilities are designed for defense. We deter aggression, we secure borders and we safeguard our maritime domain. So, ang presence nito [Typhon missile system], it’s something that would reinforce this defense posture of ours,' the military spokesperson said. Padilla also reiterated that the AFP did not make any commitment to remove the missile system from the country. The Typhon missile launchers are being used in the military’s training, which focuses on the orientation and familiarization of the capability. 'Ang missile na ito was introduced in 2024 as a critical component in enhancing our national defense capabilities for training purposes, upscaling our personnel, letting us to see if we are able to practice our logistical trains, how we will deploy such a capability,' Padilla noted. She added that the Philippines plans to acquire a similar system so training with it was important. The AFP last month confirmed that the MRC missile system has been relocated from Laoag City, Ilocos Norte to an undisclosed location. Beijing had called on Manila to pull out the missile launchers, but President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. offered China a deal, saying he is willing to pull out the weapon system if it will stop its acts of aggression in the West Philippine Sea. The AFP on Monday responded to China's remark on the deployment of US Typhon missile launcher in the Philppines. MANILA — The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) defended anew the deployment of the United States' Typhon missile launcher in the country following China's fresh remark on the issue.

title: Poe's biggest campaign contributors revealed
body: According to Poe's Schedule of Contributions Received submitted to the Commission on Elections, Poe's mother Jesusa Poe, more popularly known as Susan Roces, contributed a total of P25 million. Roces' P10 million contribution was received on February 9, and another P15 million was given on February 23. Poe's husband, Teodoro ''Neil'' Vera Llamanzares, meanwhile, contributed a total of P6 million – P5 million given on February 23 and P1 million on April 22. Someone named Teodoro Paraiso Llamanzares also contributed P4 million on February 29, documents show. But the biggest contributor is Oscar Astudillo Valera Jr., a resident of Greenmeadows in Quezon City. He shelled out P50 million for Poe's campaign. Valera is followed by Odilon Tiongson Sta. Teresa and Maria Delas Mercedes Urquijo Zobel who gave P20 million each to Poe's campaign. Marvin Mangonon Go shelled out P15 million, while Ramundo Martin Maslog Escalano, Mario Santos Tan, Bryan Uy Villanueva, gave P10 million each. Rowena Villafuerte Ramos and Marilyn Maestral Lacsa gave P7 million each. Poe, who ran as an independent, defended her high campaign spending, citing her lack of a political party and machinery. ''The apparently high amount of expenditures that I incurred for my campaign, as duly reflected in my SOCE (Statement of Contributions and Expenditures) and fully covered by campaign donations, reflects my challenging bid as an independent candidate, without the machinery and full backing of a political party, unlike other bets,'' Poe said in a text message. ''As such, the absence of a party and a nationwide political machinery constrained me as a matter of strategy to rely extensively on the more expensive media advertising that enabled me however to convey my platform of government to our electorate nationwide.' Poe explained that unlike her opponents, there was no political party to help defray her campaign expenses. Poe was the biggest campaign spender in the recently concluded presidential election, based on the SOCE. She spent a total of P510.84 million for her campaign. Poe received a total of P511.95 million in campaign contributions, all in cash. President-elect Rodrigo Duterte of PDP-Laban spent a total of P371.46 million for his campaign, four million less the total campaign contributions he received. Vice President Jejomar Binay spent a total of P463,375,216.37, while Senator Miriam Santiago spent P74.6 million for her candidacy. Second placer Mar Roxas of the Liberal Party has yet to submit his SOCE and has asked the Commission on Elections for an extension. – with a report from Adrian Ayalin, ABS-CBN News MANILA (UPDATED) – The mother and husband of defeated presidential aspirant, Senator Grace Poe, are among her top contributors in her campaign.

title: Summer has started, PAGASA says
body: Easterly winds associated with warm and dry conditions have started to prevail in most parts of the country, PAGASA said. According to the state weather bureau, the day-to-day weather will gradually become warmer and drier in the coming weeks, while humid conditions are expected until May. However, PAGASA said the parts of the country can expect isolated rainshowers and thunderstorms in the afternoon or evening particularly in the eastern Philippines, due to localized convection. PAGASA earlier warned that the strong El Niño will intensify the dry season this year. MANILA - The Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA) on Friday declared the official start of the dry season or summer in the country.

title: Chinese national linked to alleged telecom fraud arrested in Makati
body: Operatives from the Fugitive Search Unit (FSU), in coordination with the Intelligence Service of the Armed Forces of the Philippines (ISAFP), Philippine National Police Internal Affairs Service (PNP IG), and Makati City Police, conducted the operation along Mercedes Street, Barangay Bel-Air, Makati City, resulting in the arrest of the suspect. According to intelligence information and official confirmation from the Embassy of the People's Republic of China, the suspect is a fugitive wanted by authorities in Guangdong, China, for alleged involvement in telecom fraud. Three other Chinese nationals were also encountered during the operation. They failed to present valid passports or immigration documents and authorities are now verifying their identities and immigration status. According to investigators, the suspect was allegedly under the protection of armed security personnel, including an active police officer and a reservist. The matter is now under further investigation. Records from the Bureau of Immigration show that the suspect arrived in the Philippines in November 2019 as a temporary visitor but failed to extend or update his visa, making him an overstaying alien. He also has an existing blacklist order issued in 2021 for violation of visa conditions. Authorities have also pinpointed the suspect's possible connection to Baofu Corporation, which has been previously linked to Alice Guo, as part of a broader investigation into alleged illegal operations in the country. The suspect will be turned over to the Bureau of Immigration Warden Facility for booking and documentation. Further developments are expected as the investigation continues. Bamban, Tarlac Mayor Alice Guo testifies at the Senate hearing in Pasay City on May 22, 2024 as she faces questions on her identity and alleged ties to a raided POGO hub in her town. The Senate resumed its investigation on the alleged human trafficking and cyber fraud ops in Clark and Tarlac. Mark Demayo, ABS-CBN News MANILA — A former accountant of Bamban, Tarlac Mayor Alice Guo denied allegations that she and Guo have ties with the illegal Philippine Offshore Gaming Operators (POGOs) in the town. In an interview with ABS-CBN News, Nancy Jimenez Gamo admitted that she registered Hong Sheng Gaming Technology with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) but did not have any participation with the POGO in any other capacity. A staff of Guo supposedly requested Gamo to facilitate the registration. 'Sa Senate inquiry, ‘di ba sinabi niya (Guo) na nag-bi-bridge lang din siya ng ano, tinutulungan niya, being the lessor of that company. Ni-refer niya ‘yung services ko for that,' Gamo said. (During the Senate inquiry, Guo said she just acted as a bridge for Hong Sheng, she helped it being the lessor of that company. She referred my services for that.) READ: Ombudsman suspends Bamban Mayor Guo, 2 others over raps filed by DILG READ: OSG probe into Guo to proceed despite mayor's suspension -- Guevarra Guo was former incorporator of Baofu Land Development which owned the compound where Hong Sheng leased office space. Hong Sheng was later on shut down for illegal activities such as scamming. Guo divested from the corporation before running for the 2022 elections. Gamo insisted that all documents of Hong Sheng were legal: '‘Yung documentation niyan ay dumaan sa tamang proseso. Legal. Kaya, makikita mo yung pangalan ko doon. Dahil, kung alam ko na iligal, eh hindi ko gagamitin yung pangalan ko.' (The documentation went through the right process. Legal. That’s why you see my name there. Because if I knew that it was illegal, I wouldn’t have used my name for that.) When Guo was already mayor, a representative from Zun Yuan Gaming Technology allegedly reached out to Gamo and asked her to register the business with SEC, which she did. Asked if she worked for Zun Yuan, Gamo said, 'No. Definitely not.' As for her relation with Guo, Gamo said it was only on a per project basis. 'I have known Miss Alice way back 2012. Nag-freelance na ko that time. Tinap niya yung services ko for some work sa registration ng companies niya…Hindi ako regular accountant,' she said. (I have known Miss Alice way back 2012. I was already freelancing that time. She tapped my services for some work in registering her companies… I was not a regular accountant.) The reason why Guo allowed Hong Sheng and Zun Yuan to operate when she was Mayor, according to Gamo, was because they were compliant with the law. 'In-establish ‘yung company na ‘yun, may compliance siya sa PAGCOR. Merong, ah, PAGCOR officials monitoring the operation inside the premises. Fully compliant siya sa aspeto na ‘yun. So, tapos, bilang Mayor, wala namang report na may criminality na ganon,' Gamo said. (When the company was established it was compliant with PAGCOR. There were PAGCOR officials monitoring the operation inside the premises. It was fully compliant in that aspect. And as Mayor, there was no report of any criminality.) Gamo was absent in the May 22 Senate hearing. She said she received the invitation to attend only on May 21 and was not able to read the email immediately. During the hearing, Guo initially denied knowing her but later on acknowledged employing Gamo upon the prodding of Senator Risa Hontiveros. For Gamo, this was Guo’s way of protecting her. 'Noong narinig ko na sinabi niya na hindi niya ako kilala, ang, ang isip ko ‘nun is she wanted na hindi ako madamay, ma-drag doon sa mga usaping ‘yun. Siya ‘yung taong ano eh, mahal niya ang empleyado niya,' she said. (When I heard her say she did not know me, I thought she did not want to drag me into the issue. She is the kind of person who loves her employee.) Gamo committed to attend the next hearing if invited. Amid speculations about Guo’s biological mother, Gamo said Wen Yi Lin is only a business partner and girlfriend of Guo’s father, Angelito. Lin is a Chinese national who is an incorporator in several businesses of the Guos. READ: Former associate insists Lin Wen Yi not Guo's biological mother Senator Sherwin Gatchalian previously said she might be the mother of Guo since she was introduced to some residents of Valenzuela as such. Her being incorporator is also something that would only be usually seen among family members, according to Gatchalian. Gamo, on the other hand, believes differently based on what she has witnessed between Guo and Lin. 'In terms of affection, mother ako eh, I can sense kung ‘yung anak mo eh, will treat you really as a mother kung ikaw ‘yung mother niya. Pero, in the case of, ano, Miss Alice, hindi eh, hindi ko nakita ‘yun. Para siyang stranger lang,' she said. (In terms of affection, I am a mother, I can sense if your child will treat you really as a mother. But in the case of Miss Alice, I didn’t see that. Lin looked like a stranger.) As for Angelito and Lin, Gamo said, 'May terms of endearment yun, eh. Yung sa action, makikita mo na, na parang boyfriend-girlfriend.' (There are terms of endearment. You will see in their action that they are like boyfriend-girlfriend.) Gamo does not know who Guo’s real mother is, especially since she did not meet Amelia Leal, the supposed housekeeper of Angelito who is the mother of Guo. Gamo saw Shiela and Seimen Guo a couple of times but did not know they were siblings. She said she has not talked to the Guos since 2020. A former accountant of Bamban, Tarlac Mayor Alice Guo denied allegations that she and Guo had ties with the illegal Philippine Offshore Gaming Operators in the said town. Bamban, Tarlac Mayor Alice Guo in a Senate hearing in Pasay City on May 22, 2024. Mark Demayo, ABS-CBN News MANILA – Authorities filed qualified human trafficking complaints against suspended Bamban Mayor Alice Guo ang others before the Department of Justice on Friday. The information filed by the Presidential Anti-Organized Crime Commission and the Philippine National Police-Criminal Investigation and Detection Group referred to the mayor as ' Guo Hua Ping '. 'Lumabas ang mga ebidensya dito tulad ng fact na siya ang nag-apply dun sa dating POGO (Philippine Offshore Gaming Operations), lumabas din ang kanyang involvement doon sa lessor company, kasama na rin ang ibng ebidensya diyan tulad ng pangalan niya na lumulutang sa mga sabi-sabing dokumento na nahanap sa POGO compound,' said DOJ Undersecretary and Inter-Agency Council Against Trafficking Chairperson Nicholas Ty. 'In collaboration with PAOCC and other law enforcement agency, we are intensifying our fight against these illegal POGOs and the PNP is one with this fight, especially the PNP-CIDG,' PNP-CIDG Director Major General Leo Francisco said. Is Bamban Mayor Alice Guo's real name Hua Ping Guo? Alice Guo itinangging siya si Guo Hua Ping The Department of Justice said proceedings for the issuance of the International Lookout Bulletin (ILCO) against Guo were ongoing. 'Dahil na-file na ang kaso, pina-process na namin ang ILBO, dadaan lang ito sa internal processes ng DOJ at hopefully by next week mapirmahan na ni secretary ang ILBO,' Ty said. Hontiveros more confident cases vs Mayor Alice Guo are strong The complaint included 12 Chinese nationals who are officials of Zun Yuan Technology Inc., Hongsheng Gaming Technology Inc. and Baofu Corporation, as well as former Technology and Resource Center director general Dennis Cunanan. 'We discovered that the Zun Yuan operation actually started with one corporation which was Baofu, and then another corporation was created. All these three corporations, we believe that they are part of a grand conspiracy to commit labor trafficking,' said IACAT prosecutor Benjamin Samson. Guo asserted that 'h aving connections to companies or individuals, especially when vague, is not enough to link someone to a case, particularly one involving human trafficking,' She said this claim needed to be backed by substantial evidence. The mayor reiterated that she had no involvement with Zunn Yuan Technology, Inc. or any other POGO. 'Since the day of the raid on March 13 until now, there has been a concerted effort to unjustly link me to the case,' she said. 'If concrete evidence existed, charges would have been filed against me much earlier,' Guo said in a statement. 'The principle of 'innocent until proven guilty' should prevail, and it is crucial to respect human rights and justice in all aspects of any case,' she added. Guo writes Bersamin to deny POGO allegations The DOJ assured Guo and the respondents that due process would be followed as the complaints are still subject to preliminary investigation before a panel of prosecutors. 'Itong kaso na na-file complaint pa lang ito, dadaan pa ito sa proseso sa DOJ, dadaan pa ito sa preliminary investigation kasama na si Mayor Guo na bibigyan ng pagkakaton na sabihin ang kanilang panig, wala pa ito sa korte, dadaan pa ito sa proseso at ayaw naming pangunahan ang mga prosecutors na independently magko-conduct ng preliminary investigation,' Ty said. With the recent events involving POGO hubs, the PAOCC said other local government unit officials especially in Pampanga are coordinating with them. 'Yung ibang kaso na pwede pa nating isampa laban sa ibang respondents ay paguukulan namin ng panahon para makalikom kami ng sapat na ebidensya sa mga kaso,' said PAOCC Executive Director Gilbert Cruz said. The human trafficking raps are the second set of complaints filed so far against Guo. The Department of the Interior and Local Government had earlier filed administrative complaints against Guo and other municipal officials before the Office of the Ombusman. The administrative complaints p ending resolution at the Ombudsman include grave misconduct, serious dishonesty, gross neglect of duty and conduct prejudicial to the best interest of the service. Tarlac mayor Alice Guo's COC: 'I am a Filipino citizen' The Presidential Anti-Organized Crime Commission and the Philippine National Police-Criminal Investigation and Detection Group filed qualified human trafficking complaints against suspended Bamban Mayor Alice Guo ang others before the Department of Justice on Friday. Operatives of PNP Anti-Cybercrime Group process seized computers and cellphones used for alleged illegal Philippine Offshore Gaming operation in Almanza Uno in Las Pinas City last June 2023. ABS-CBN News/File MANILA — They were touted as a new source of revenue when the government started regulating them in 2016—but 8 years later, several sectors are now calling for the total ban on Philippine Offshore Gaming Operators (POGOs). • 'POGO-politics': Suspected China ties of Tarlac town mayor alarms solon Now called Internet Gaming Licensees (IGL), the business has been associated with illegal activities such as scams, torture, human trafficking, and possibly even espionage and killings. This situation was different from when former President Rodrigo Duterte endorsed POGOs in 2020. 'I will assure you under my oath of office as President of the republic, as elected by you, ‘yang POGO na ‘yan (that POGO)…malinis 'yan (that’s clean),' Duterte said during the General Assembly of the League of Municipalities of the Philippines. Phil-Asian Gaming Expo features offshore games targeting bettors outside the Philippines. Games can be played on desktop or mobile devices. Photos taken at the SMX Convention Center. July 12, 2019. Jessica Fenol, ABS-CBN News/File Fast forward to 2024, the Philippine Amusement and Gaming Corporation (PAGCOR) said they estimate illegal IGLs to be around 250, and only 46 have licenses. • Around 250 POGOs operating illegally, may be involved in crimes: Pagcor According to PAGCOR chairman and Chief Executive Officer Alejandro Tengco, what started as legal gaming platforms became hubs for illegal activities when syndicates came in. 'May mga nag-ooperate na criminal syndicates na talagang ‘yan ang hanapbuhay. Baka maaaring ‘yun, sapagkat nakita nilang medyo maluwag ang PAGCOR nung panahon nung nakaraan. Eh, napasok sila without PAGCOR knowing that they were already doing criminal activities instead of gaming operations,' Tengco said. (There are criminal syndicates who operate in that line of work. It could be that they saw PAGCOR to be a bit lenient in the past. They were able to come in without PAGCOR knowing that they were already doing criminal activities instead of gaming operations.) Presidential Anti-Organized Crime Commission (PAOCC) chief Undersecretary Gilbert Cruz and Senator Sherwin Gatchalian said syndicates ran to the Philippines after a crackdown in other countries such as China and Vietnam. 'Ang pakiramdam kasi nila dito sa Pilipinas, masakit man sabihin, pero parang napakaluwag natin dito eh. Parang lahat dito puwede mong kaibiganin para makuha mo ‘yung gusto mo. Lahat dito parang puwede mong suhulan,' Cruz stated. (They feel that here in the Philippines, although it is painful to say, it’s like we are very lenient here. It’s like you can befriend everyone here to get what you want. It’s like you can bribe almost anyone. They found a paradise here.) 'Itong mga organized crime na nagtakbuhan, naging legitimate because we are giving them license,' Gatchalian said. (These organized crime that ran, they became legitimate becase we are giving them licenses.) Crimes related to POGOs such as kidnapping were observed around the time of the pandemic, according to Gatchalian, but illegal activities increased in 2022. • Kidnapped POGO worker rescued in Cavite after 10 days When Tengco took the helm of PAGCOR, they restructured the rules and ordered POGOs to reapply. More than 250 did not. They assumed that these are illegally operating still after relocating and hiding from the law. PAGCOR also rebranded the name POGO to IGL to disassociate it with the negative reputation. The big question then is: How are illegal POGOs able to operate? According to PAOCC and PAGCOR, operators hide behind their licenses. 'Sasabihin nila na willing silang magpa-inspection sa PAGCOR para sabihin na legit sila but actually ang pinapa-inspect lang nila ‘yung lugar let’s say halimbawa sa isang building o kaya sa isang floor na kung saan makikita mo ‘yung legit na online gaming. But sa likod nun, meron silang ibang floors o kaya meron silang ibang rooms na nag-eengage naman dito sa scamming activities,' Cruz said. (They will say they are willing to be inspected by PAGCOR but what they open to inspection is a building or floor where legit online gaming happens. Behind that, there are other floors or rooms where scamming activities happen.) Tengco cited the case of Baofu Compound in Bamban, Tarlac where PAGCOR employees were assigned to monitor the POGOs. • Tarlac gov’t had no idea about POGO in Bamban: official Gatchalian previously criticized PAGCOR for not being able to monitor the illegal activities in Bamban, but Tengco said the coverage of the license they issued was limited to just one part of the area. 'Ang binabantayan lang nila ‘yung isang building which is how many hundreds of meters away from the illegal activities…‘Yung may lisensya doon ay nasa isang gilid lang. At doon sa kanan na lugar ng compound, ay gated doon…Wala na po kaming kapangyarihan makapasok doon,' Tengco said. (What they monitored was just one building which is how many hundreds of meters away from the illegal activities…The one with the license is just one part. The right side of the compound is gated. We do not have access to that.) 'We cannot differentiate anymore which are legal and illegal because the legal, in front, sasabihin nila na wala silang ginagawa pero sa likod, may mga ganitong nangyayari (will say they are not doing anything illegal),' Gatchalian said. Authorities are also suspicious of local government units saying they do not know how illegal POGOs in their communities were able to operate. In the case of Lucky South 99 in Porac, Pampanga, PAGCOR already denied its application for license in May 22 of this year but it was still operating on the day it was raided on June 4. • Another raid at Porac POGO hub: Cash, jewelry, suspected drugs seized 'Kung titignan mo kung gano'ng kalaki ang operasyon. Kahit papaano naman siguro sa barangay man lamang, malalaman mo na meron doon 46 na building at libong tao,' Tengco stated. (That big of an operation, the village officials could have at least known that there were 46 buildings and thousands of people.) Gatchalian believes money is involved, saying '‘Yan naman ‘yung lumabas sa executive session namin at conversations namin sa mga enforcement agencies na itong mga POGOs marunong rin gumamit ng pera para makakuha ng impluwensiya.' • Senate executive session on Mayor Guo set on June 5 (That is what was revealed in our executive session, conversations with enforcement agencies that POGOs know how to use money to get influence.) In a June 6 press conference, Gatchalian said they suspected the influence of POGOs to have already reached politicians, enforcement agencies, the judiciary, and local criminal syndicates. Senate Deputy Minority Leader Risa Hontiveros also points to corruption as the alleged reason why even after eight inquiries led by her committee, illegal activities of POGOs persist. 'Corruption of public officers is a problem. Mukhang may mga protektor sila sa gobyerno,' Hontiveros said in a statement. (It seems like POGOs have a protector in government.) 'Kung sa kaso lang ni Mayor Alice Guo ang pagbabatayan, nakikita na natin na may impluwensiya ang POGO sa mga opisyal, kasama na ang mga lokal na pamahalaan,' she added. (Just looking at the case of Mayor Alice Guo, we already see that POGOs have influence on officials, both in local and national government.) Gatchalian and Hontiveros have appealed to President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr. to ban POGOs. • Calls mount to ban POGOs; Marcos urged to address the issue Officials from various government agencies attend the senate Inquiry on money laundering, crimes, and other illegal activities associated with the operations of Philippine Offshore Gaming Operators (POGOs) in the country, held at the Senate building in Pasay City on March 5, 2020. Jonathan Cellona, ABS-CBN News Tengco is quick to say, however, that banning POGOs is not the way to go due to loss of revenue for the country. In 2023, PAGCOR’s revenues from POGO operations amounted to P5.3 billion from the 46 licensed operators; the agency expects P6.5 billion to P7 billion at the end of 2024. He said that in terms of Gross Domestic Product, POGOs contributed more than P20 billion last year. Part of the money collected by PAGCOR goes to the national treasury, PhilHealth, and donations, among others. POGOs also directly employ around 31,000 Filipinos which is slightly higher than the 29,000 to 30,000 foreign workers. Tengco said the employees, along with others such as janitorial services, will suffer from unemployment once POGOs are prohibited. He said legal POGOs may also go underground that will result in harder monitoring and foregone revenue. PAGCOR insisted that it has become stricter in regulating POGOs, adding that of the 46 legal gaming operators, three are currently suspended for not meeting requirements. Tengco said they also help other agencies in going after illegal POGOs such as in providing them with the necessary information. 'Napakahirap ng panahon ngayon,' the PAGCOR chief said. '‘Yung contributions natin to nation-building sayang din.' (The current times are very heard. Our contributions to nation building will go to waste.) Lawmakers argued, however, that peace and order must take precedence. • 'No redeeming value': Lawmakers urge Marcos to ban POGOs 'It is not enough. Walang katapat na pera ang seguridad at kaayusan ng ating lipunan,' Hontiveros stated. (No amount of money can equal the security and order in our society.) 'Chairman ako ng Ways and Means Committee. Ang trabaho ko, maghanap ng kita,' Gatchalian said. 'Willing ako tanggalin ‘yung kita sa POGO…basta tahimik ‘yung ating bansa.' • [EXCLUSIVE] Guo denies being a Chinese spy: 'I am a Filipino, I love my country' Hontiveros will file a bill repealing Republic Act 11590 or an Act Taxing Philippine Offshore Gaming Operations, the law that legitimizes the operation of POGOs. In May, Gatchalian filed Senate Bill 2689 which also seeks to revoke the same law. Other agencies such as the Department of Finance and National Economic and Development Authority have also supported calls to ban POGOs saying they do more harm than good. POGOs are associated with illegal activities such as scams, torture, human trafficking, and possibly even espionage and killings. Bamban, Tarlac Mayor Alice Guo testifies at the Senate hearing in Pasay City on May 22, 2024 as she faces questions on her identity and alleged ties to a raided POGO hub in her town. Mark Demayo, ABS-CBN News MANILA — Senator Risa Hontiveros is more convinced that there is basis to file criminal and administrative cases against Bamban, Tarlac Mayor Alice Guo after the Senate’s executive session with different government agencies on Wednesday where confidential information about illegal Philippine Offshore Gaming Operators (POGOs) and their possible connection with Guo were discussed. Some of the agencies present during the closed-door meeting were the Presidential Anti-Organized Crime Commission (PAOCC), National Security Council (NSC) and Anti-Money Laundering Council (AMLC). 'Walang nakabawas o nakabura doon sa namumuo talagang opinion ng komite na siguradong may kinalaman siya sa POGO hub diyan sa Bamban. Doon pa lang sa documentary evidence, tapos siyempre, ‘yung ilang mga inputs mula sa mga resource persons,' Hontiveros stated. (Nothing was able to diminish the Committee’s opinion that she certainly has ties with the POGO hub there in Bamban. There’s the documentary evidence and of course, the inputs from resource persons.) 'I'm more confident na talagang magiging mabigat at solido yung mga kasong inihain na at maaring ihain pa ng iba't ibang mga ahensiya ng executive ng ating gobyerno,' she added. (I’m more confident that cases filed and cases that will be filed against her by various agencies of government have weight and are solid.) Hontiveros reiterated that Guo’s business partners when she was still part of the Baofu Land Development, Inc. are alleged criminals with two being tagged in the 'largest' money laundering case in Singapore. 'Bakit may ganyang kalalaking mga pera na allegedly na money launder nung kanyang dalawang kasosyo? Maiisip tuloy, teka, ang laki din ang sinasabing halaga ng kabuuang Zun Yuan complex na ‘yan. Paano nakapasok at nakaikot sa ating ekonomiya kung walang malinaw na sources si Mayor Alice Guo at kanyang mga kasosyo?' the Senator said. (Why are there huge amounts of money allegedly laundered by her two business partners? You will think, wait a minute, the supposed cost of the entire Zun Yuan complex is also huge. How did that enter and circulate in our economy if Guo and her partners do not have clear sources?) The senator also maintained that there is no strong evidence showing Guo is a Filipino. The Mayor was not invited to the closed door meeting but her lawyer submitted a letter to Hontiveros’ office answering the allegations against her. According to Guo, Baofu was a legitimate business when it started. She did not know that her business partners were involved in criminal activities, and did not intend to represent the POGO Hong Sheng Gaming Technology in securing its letter of no objection from the local government of Bamban. After being elected Mayor, she gave a permit to the POGO Zun Yuan Technology because its documents were complete and was green lighted by the Philippine Amusement and Gaming Corporation (PAGCOR) to operate. Guo insisted that her father has nothing to do with money laundering. As for her personal life, she said her true mother is Filipino citizen Amelia Leal, her father’s former house help. READ: Guo's former accountant helped register Bamban POGOs, denies involvement in illegal activities 'May mga nagsasabing si Lin Wen Yi ang tunay kong ina. Siya ay kasama sa mga kumpanya ng aming pamilya, ngunit si Amelia Leal ang aking ina sa lahat ng dokumento. Hindi totoo ang paratang na si Amelia Leal ay non-existing person. Si Lin Wen Yi ay kinakasama at isang business partner ng aking ama,' Guo reasoned out. (Some say Lin Wen Yi is my real mother. She is a part of my family’s companies, but Amelia Leal is my mom based on all documents. The allegation that Amelia Leal is a non-existing person is not real. Lin Wen Yi is a live-in partner and a business partner of my father.) 'May mga kapatid ako na may iba’t ibang nasyonalidad. Hindi ko nais na madamay sila sa mga isyung ito. Lumaki kami nang magkakahiwalay at may iba-ibang ina,' Guo said. (I have siblings of different nationalities. I do not want them to be dragged into these issues. We grew up separately and with different mothers.) The letter ended with Guo swearing that she is not a protector of POGO, 'Hindi po ako protektor ng Pogo! Wala po akong kinalaman sa murder, prostitution, human trafficking, love scam, money laundering at iba pang cyber crimes! Hindi po ako nagnakaw!' (I am not a protector of Pogo! I am not involved in murder, prostitution, human trafficking, love scam, money laundering and other cyber crimes! I did not steal!) READ: PAOCC eyes tax evasion and other charges vs Guo Senator Risa Hontiveros holds a press conference in Pasay City on March 18, 2024, regarding the contempt case filed against Pastor Apollo Quiboloy. Mark Demayo, ABS-CBN News During the executive session, Hontiveros appealed to the NSC to brief President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. about the alleged national security threat that POGOs pose. Hontiveros could not give details because of the confidentiality of the matter, but said that the situation in the country is definitely alarming. 'Ipinanawagan ko na ilang beses kay Presidente na i-ban na ang POGO, patigilin na ito, palabasin, paalisin na sila sa ating bayan,' Hontiveros remarked. (I have asked the President a number of times to ban POGO, put an end to it and make them leave our country.) 'Kahit yung nagsisimulang legit or legal na POGO, or POGO na may lisensya nung una, nagmo-morph din eh. Nagmu-mutate na nagiging illegal.' (Even POGOs which started as legit or lega,l or POGO with a license in the beginning, morph. It mutates into something illegal.) The Senate will have another briefing with AMLC before it holds the next public hearing on POGOs and Guo. BASAHIN: POGO hub sa Pampanga sinalakay; higit 150 dayuhan natagpuan Senator Risa Hontiveros is more convinced that there is basis to file criminal and administrative cases against Bamban, Tarlac Mayor Alice Guo after the Senate’s executive session with different government agencies on Wednesday. Authorities on Wednesday arrested a Chinese national wanted for alleged telecom fraud during a coordinated enforcement operation in Makati. MANILA -- Authorities on Wednesday arrested a Chinese national wanted for alleged telecom fraud during a coordinated enforcement operation in Makati. 'In terms of affection, mother ako eh, I can sense kung ‘yung anak mo eh, will treat you really as a mother kung ikaw ‘yung mother niya. Pero, in the case of, ano, Miss Alice, hindi eh, hindi ko nakita ‘yun. Para siyang stranger lang,' she said. Guo asserted that 'h aving connections to companies or individuals, especially when vague, is not enough to link someone to a case, particularly one involving human trafficking,' 'Since the day of the raid on March 13 until now, there has been a concerted effort to unjustly link me to the case,' she said. 'If concrete evidence existed, charges would have been filed against me much earlier,' Guo said in a statement. 'The principle of 'innocent until proven guilty' should prevail, and it is crucial to respect human rights and justice in all aspects of any case,' she added. Guo writes Bersamin to deny POGO allegations

title: China's rocket launch forces temporary closure of Philippine navigation routes
body: Several navigation routes in the country were closed in preparation for possible debris drops from China's rocket launch. The agency closed several navigation routes in the country in preparation for possible debris drops from the rocket launch. 'Though the debris from the rocket launch will unlikely fall on inhabited land, it may still pose a danger to aircraft and seacraft,' CAAP said in its press release on Thursday. The issued NOTAM identified possible drop zones approximately 65 to 79 kilometers from Bajo de Masinloc or the Scarborough Shoal. 'There are no reported incidents from the expected debris drops last night,' CAAP spokesperson Eric Apolonio said. 'Negative sightings of debris so far,' Apolonio added. The Philippine Space Agency has been monitoring debris from the Long March 7 rocket confirmed to have launched at 9:22 p.m. on Wednesday from Wenchang Space Launch Center in Wenchang, Hainan, China. MANILA — The Civil Aviation Authority of the Philippines (CAAP) on Wednesday issued Notices to Airmen (NOTAM) following reports from the Philippine Space Agency that debris from China's rocket launch would reach the country's territory.

title: Duterte urged to be wary on China talks
body: Senator Leila de Lima issued the warning in the wake of the Philippines' victory in a case Manila lodged against Beijing over the maritime dispute. ''With this ruling, it is hoped that the present administration will be properly guided in its future actions, especially in its declared bias for bilateral talks which has only proven to be always one-sided in favor of the world power at the other end of the table,'' De Lima said in a statement. De Lima, a former justice secretary, was part of the Philippine delegation that former President Benigno Aquino III sent to The Hague to participate in the arbitration proceedings. In filing its arbitration case, the Aquino administration said it had exhausted all means to resolve the bitter sea dispute, including holding direct talks with China. The Aquino government said it saw no point in holding one-on-one talks with China as the latter would not back down on its claims. The Aquino administration preferred holding multilateral talks with China, wherein other claimants would be involved. The previous administration said through a multilateral discussion, the Philippines and other smaller sea claimants would be able to put more pressure on China. A stance unfriendly towards Beijing, however, may now change under the administration of the newly minted Duterte administration. Even before the the United Nations-backed tribunal came out with its ruling, Duterte had expressed willingness to hold bilateral talks with China. Appearing to soften Manila's stance, Duterte said in his first Cabinet meeting that he would like to reach a ''soft landing'' with China after the tribunal rules. Philippine foreign affairs chief Perfecto Yasay also called for 'restraint and sobriety' after the tribunal ruled in the Philippines' favor. In a landmark ruling, the Hague-based Permanent Court of Arbitration said that there is no legal basis to China's claims to rights across the South China Sea, a vast maritime body believed to contain a significant amount of untapped oil reserves and home to some of the world's most diverse fisheries. [ READ: Philippines beats China in international court ] The tribunal said that China's ''historic rights'' and ''nine-dash line'' claim to South China Sea had been extinguished by the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Seas (UNCLOS) to which both the Philippines and China are signatories. The tribunal also ruled that the artificial islands that China has been building in the Spratlys, located at the southern portion of the disputed sea, cannot generate a 200-nautical mile exclusive economic zone. Thus, the artificial islands built by China near the island of Palawan falls within the Philippines' exclusive economic zone. ''Accordingly, the Tribunal concluded that none of the Spratly Islands is capable of generating extended maritime zones. The Tribunal also held that the Spratly Islands cannot generate maritime zones collectively as a unit,'' the Tribunal said. ''Having found that none of the features claimed by China was capable of generating an exclusive economic zone, the Tribunal found that it could - without delimiting a boundary - declare that certain sea areas are within the exclusive economic zone of the Philippines, because those areas are not overlapped by any possible entitlement of China.' The case filed by Manila against Beijing was triggered by a 2012 naval standoff at the contested Scarborough Shoal in the South China Sea, a rocky outcrop west of Luzon considered a traditional fishing ground by Filipinos. Beijing currently controls Scarborough and has been preventing Filipino fishermen from accessing the resources-rich area. There have been fears that Beijing could also reclaim land on Scarborough in the wake of the unfavorable artbitration ruling. Beijing, which snubbed the arbitration proceedings, has said it will not follow the tribunal's ruling, even as it faces the risk of being labeled an international outlaw. While the tribunal is a victory for the Philippines, it may only be considered a moral one as the tribunal has no means of reinforcing its ruling. The ruling, nonetheless, is considered a blow to Beijing, which is seeking to project itself as a benign superpower. Several Filipino senators, meanwhile, welcomed the Philippines' victory and urged China to respect the tribunal's ruling. ''This is a victory not only for the Philippines but more importantly for the Rule of Law,' said neophyte senator Joel Villanueva. 'I call on China to respect the Tribunal's decision and for both parties to work together for our mutual benefit as all neighbors must.' Senator Franklin Drilon, meanwhile, said he hopes the ruling would cause the mending of ties between the two neighbors instead of fueling further tension. He added, the ruling should also expedite the creation of the formal code of conduct in the South China Sea, which will serve as a binding code for all sea claimants. ''We hope that the decision puts to rest any tension, mend strained relations, and restore goodwill among countries in the region,'' Drilon said. ''ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) and China can now move forward to finalize the Code of Conduct to ensure peace and stability in the South China Sea.' Senator Francis Pangilinan, meanwhile, signified no opposition to a possible direct Philippines-China talks. He said the installation of a new Philippine government will give the two countries an elbow room in improving ties. ''The decision of the Permanent Court of Arbitration can serve as a reboot in the stalled China-Philippines diplomatic relations. It gives the Philippines a more equal footing in the inevitable bilateral talks with China, who should not lose face,'' Pangilinan said. ''Together with having a new administration, the ruling allows both parties to come to the negotiating table with fresh perspectives. I suggest we remind China of the centuries' old people-to-people interactions between our nations, and of the similar historical indignities we faced as colonies of foreign powers. Of the latter, we can tell them that their occupation of the reefs in the West Philippine Sea resonates to that past.' For Senator Loren Legarda, China not following the ruling would only disrupt the international legal order. 'It serves no valid purpose to further exacerbate the dispute by refusing to heed the decision handed down by Tribunal, for in the end, such refusal to honor its decision will only undermine the primary objective of the UNCLOS - and that is to establish a legal order for the seas to promote its peaceful use,' Legarda said. Countering a Chinese envoy's statement that the ruling is 'nothing more than a piece of paper', Senator Grace Poe said: 'The Tribunal's ruling is no paper judgment. Our government should exhaust all avenues and encourage full compliance of the decision through peaceful and diplomatic means.' Senator Antonio Trillanes IV, meanwhile, lauded the Aquino administration's China policy for the victory. 'The landmark decision of the Arbitral Tribunal is a historic victory for our Country. Equally important is that, based on early reports, it is definitive, categorical and expectedly upheld the provisions of UNCLOS which could help the Duterte Administration in pursuing the next course of action. Lastly, the favorable decision is a vindication of the foreign policy direction of the Aquino administration,' he said. MANILA – Holding one-on-one talks with China over the West Philippine Sea would only prove futile, a senator warned the administration of President Rodrigo Duterte.

title: PNP identifies owner of barge linked to alleged illegal fuel transfer in Navotas
body: According to PNP-HPG spokesperson PLt. Nadame Malang, the owner is a Filipino businessman who has allegedly been engaged in profiteering activities for years. Authorities have withheld the suspect’s identity pending verification from the Maritime Industry Authority (MARINA) and the Philippine Coast Guard (PCG). 'Almost 2 years na niyang ginagawa ‘yung gantong kind of profiteering. And ‘yun din ‘yung hinihingi natin sa certification from the DOE,' Malang said. The police also confirmed that the registered owner of the tanker has been identified, based on vehicle registration records. The individual, a businesswoman, has been invited by authorities to explain whether she was aware that her vehicle was used in illegal trading activities. The PNP-HPG said it is not discounting the possibility that more individuals are involved in the alleged scheme. Nine suspects were initially arrested and cases have already been filed. They are facing violations of Presidential Decree 1865 in relation to BP 33, which governs regulations on the trading, handling, and profiteering from petroleum products. 'Hindi kami naniniwala na isang tao lang ‘yan. Napakihirap din na ipapasok at ipalabas ‘yung vessel na nakuha natin. So, marami pa tayong tinitingnan na anggulo at hindi naman natin isinasantabi ‘yung mga impormasyon na nakukuha natin,' Malang said. Meanwhile, authorities are also looking into where the petroleum products were being distributed, with initial information pointing to possible delivery within Metro Manila or to unauthorized trading areas. The HPG said the fuel involved may be unprocessed or non-compliant with standards, which could potentially damage vehicle engines if used. 'May mga nakaabang na rin tayong mga papeles para panagutin din ‘yung mismong area kung saan nahuli ng PNP-HPG ‘yung illegal trading or distribution ng petroleum products,' he said. 'And even nga ‘yung market kung may kakaharapin silang kaso. Based din sa pagkakaalam natin, unauthorized ‘yung area para magkaroon ng gano'ng uri ng distribution of petroleum products,' he added. The seized barge remains under the custody of the PNP-HPG as authorities conduct an ocular inspection and inventory of the estimated 65,000 liters of petroleum products found onboard. The HPG said coordination with the DOE, PCG, and MARINA is ongoing to complete documentation and verify ownership and other key details. Authorities continue to investigate the full scope of the operation, including possible accomplices and the broader distribution network behind the alleged illegal fuel transfer scheme. The Philippine National Police–Highway Patrol Group (PNP-HPG) has identified the owner of a barge allegedly involved in illegal fuel transfer at the Navotas Fish Port Complex. MANILA — The Philippine National Police–Highway Patrol Group (PNP-HPG) has identified the owner of a barge allegedly involved in illegal fuel transfer at the Navotas Fish Port Complex.

title: ICI grants Rep. Romulo's request for executive session
body: Romulo's counsel Erika Migriño explained that livestreaming of the hearing could put the life and safety of her client in danger. According to Migriño, livestream of the ICI proceedings could also be misused to malign the reputation of Romulo. 'The livestream of this proceedings could be misused by members of the public to malign the reputation of the good congressman and would create an environment that would not favor the impartial deliberation of the matter at hand,' she said. Romulo said he did not submit any letter to the ICI to request for an executive session and voluntarily submit himself to the Commission believing this is the proper forum to air their side. 'In fact, I did not submit any letter, I voluntarily appeared here and the reason for that actually—first, the House members with the Speaker decided that this is the best venue for us to be able to air our side,' Romulo said. According to Romulo he is willing to make his affidavit available to the public. 'With respect to the affidavit that will be required, I have no problem because that’s public documents,' said Romulo. Pasig Rep. Roman Romulo's counsel explained that livestreaming of the hearing could put the life and safety of the lawmaker in danger. MANILA — The Independent Commission for Infrastructure (ICI) on Wednesday granted the request for an executive session of Pasig City Rep. Roman Romulo during his appearance before the body.

title: Pope to plead for justice in Mexico visit: archbishop
body: Cardinal Norberto Rivera told AFP that while Francis may not meet privately with the parents of 43 missing students whose case caused international outrage, he will address the plight of all victims during his February 12-17 visit. The disappearance of the trainee teachers in 2014 was 'a tragic event for Mexico. What happened was terrible and the families are still suffering, but the problem is not limited to 43,' Rivera said in an interview in his office when asked why such a meeting was not planned. The archbishop noted that more than 26,000 people are reported missing in the country. 'I think that the (parents of the) 43 would have the right (for a private audience), but all of those who have loved ones missing also have the right for a message of hope, a message of justice,' the 73-year-old cardinal said. 'First of all, he will address those who are suffering from violence so that they don't lose hope, so that they can keep demanding justice and truth,' he said. 'I think it's very important that the pope come to ask that citizens and those who govern speak the truth.' Vidulfo Rosales, an attorney representing parents of the 43 students, told AFP that they have requested an audience but have not received an answer and that it will be 'very hard' to have a meeting. Families of hundreds of other missing people have requested an audience as well. But the secretary general of the Mexican Episcopal Conference, Eugenio Lira, said in December that it would be 'difficult' for Francis to find time in his busy agenda. But Lira said they could attend the pontiff's final mass in the border city of Ciudad Juarez, where he will address victims of violence and the plight of migrants. Turning to violence against priests, Rivera said that more than 50 have been killed in the past 25 years. 'Many priests continue to be affected by organized crime. Here in (Mexico City) at least 400 priests have suffered from extortion, threats, etcetera, because there are people who see priests as a symbol of values that they (the assailants) don't want to see disseminated,' Rivera said. In addition to Ciudad Juarez, Francis will visit Mexico City, the crime-plagued regions of Michoacan and Mexico State, and the impoverished southern state of Chiapas. Mexico launched a crackdown against illegal migration at the Chiapas-Guatemala border region in 2014 following a crisis of unaccompanied child migrants who flooded into the United States that year. Human rights groups say the operation has led to human rights abuses. 'With his moral quality, the pope can touch many doors, many hearts, many powers in this world so that migration can be more humane,' said Rivera, whose parents migrated to the United States when he was a child. 'What we ask of the north (the United States), we must also offer in the south in the case of migration. It wouldn't be wise for Mexico to demand respect and human rights for our compatriots and badly treat the migrants who come from the south,' he said.
