Comelec identifies 38 election areas of 'grave concern' Garcia said the list of areas under red category may still change as the election nears. 'May posibilidad na yung 38 na nasa BARMM ay bumaba... May posibilidad din na 'yung 38 ay madadagdagan dahil yung mga nasa orange category, yellow category o kahit 'yung green category areas ay biglang nagiging red,' he said. Garcia said that all the checkpoints would be under the supervision of the poll body. AFP runs background check on 2025 election bets 'Ang lahat ng checkpoint ay under supervision ng Comelec,' he said. 'Yung mga in-charge sa check point, PNP, AFP, sila ay limited lang sa plain view search,' Garcia added. The Comelec chief also said that checkpoints must be in well-lit areas. Garcia said that inspecting officers cannot order motorists to alight their vehicles unless there is prior information of possible illegal activity. 'Hindi pwedeng magpabukas ng kung-ano-ano sa sasakyan ninyo. Hindi pwedeng pababain maliban na lang kung may prior information sila patungkol dun sa ise-search na sasakyan o tao,' he said. '[Pero] walang masama kung ibababa ang bintana,' he added. Garcia said that the during checkpoints, the rights of the passengers should be upheld 'at all cost.' 'At all cost, yung karapatan ng mga mamamayan ang priority sa checkpoint,' he said. The Philippines will hold its midterm elections on May 12, 2025. Comelec chairman George Garcia said that all the checkpoints would be under the supervision of the poll body. 38 places in the Philippines are identified as areas of grave concern for the may mid-term elections. Most of these are in the Autonomous Muslim Region, which has long been troubled by election-related violence. -ANC, The World Tonight, January 9, 2025 38 places in the Philippines are identified as areas of grave concern for the may mid-term elections. The Comelec has exempted the distribution of 4Ps, AKAP, and AICS among other social welfare programs during the election ban but laid out the condition that no candidate or politician be present during the distribution. Comelec chairman George Garcia laid out the condition as the Comelec law department recommended the approval of the request for exemption submitted by DSWD Secretary Rex Gatchalian for 28 DSWD projects during the election ban. 'Insulated from politicking': DSWD outlines guardrails for AKAP implementation Comelec Resolution 11060 prohibits the release, disbursement or expenditure of public funds for social services from March 28 to May 11, or 45 days before the elections. On AICS, the Comelec reiterated that there should be no distribution of the said fund from May 2 to 12, 'except those which are normally given to qualified individuals such as, but not limited to basic needs in the form of food, transportation, medical, educational, burial, and other similar assistance.' DSWD resumes issuance of guarantee letters, temporarily suspends outright cash aid 'No candidates/politicians during the distribution of ayuda in whatever nature/form,' Garcia wrote alongside the recommendation of the Comelec law department. Garcia also said that the Comelec should be furnished with the guidelines 'as committed by the DSWD in public pronouncements' and that there should be 'faithful adherence' of the guidelines. The DSWD should also submit a periodic written report of the disbursements made. The Comelec also said that the issuance of a certificate of exemption 'shall not prevent the conduct if preliminary investigation in cases where the prohibited acts' as described in the Omnibus Election Code occur. The request for exemption of the DSWD covers the following projects with their amount of disbursement: 1. Pantawid Pamilyang Pilipino Program (4Ps), P38.4 billion 2. Sustainable Livelihood Program (SLP), P511 million 3. Services for Residential and Center-based Clients, P33.4 million 4. Supplementary Feeding Program (SFP), P96.9 million 5. Social Pension for Indigent Senior Citizens, P401.6 million 6. Implementation of RA No. 10868 or the Centenarians Act of 2016, P15.175 million 7. Protective Services for Individuals & Families in Especially Difficult Circumstances –COMBASED, P29.4 million 8. Protective Services for Individuals & Families in Especially Difficult Circumstances- PROPER, P2.089 million 9. Services for Displaced Persons (Deportees), P4.002 million 10. Recovery and Reintegration Program for Trafficked Persons, P628,200 11. Assistance for Persons with Disability & Senior Citizens, P380,580 12. National Household Targeting System for Poverty Reduction, P1,513,436.51 14. Philippines Multi-sectoral Nutrition Project, P44.35 million 15. Bangsamoro Umpungan sa Nutrisyon (BangUn), P123.26 million 16. PAMANA- Peace and Development, P47.298 million 17. PAMANA- DSWD/ LGU Led Livelihood, P534.289 million 19. Philippine FOOD STAMP Program/ Walang Gutom Program (WGP), P7.254 billion 20. Ayuda sa Kapos ang Kita Program (AKAP), P882.974 million 21. KALAHI CIDSS-National Community-Driven Development Program- Additional Financing (NCDDP-AF), P564,633.36 22. Beneficiary FIRST Project (BFIRST), P238.7 million 24. TARA BASA! TUTORING PROGRAM, P1.081 billion 25. Enhanced Partnership Against Hunger and Poverty (EPAHP)- National Program Management Office, P4.645 million 26. Social Technology Development and Enhancement, P32.5 million 27. Assistance to Individuals in Crisis Situations (AICS), P7.585 billion 28. Financial Assistance that are routinary and normally given by the Department to qualified individuals (May 2-12, 2025), P3.075 billion Ininspeksyon ng Commission on Elections ang mga automated counting machine na gagamitin sa Halalan 2025. Magsasagawa rin ng field test ang komisyon sa darating na Sabado. Nagpa-Patrol, Willard Cheng. TV Patrol, Huwebes, 14 Nobyembre 2024. Ininspeksyon ng Commission on Elections ang mga automated counting machine na gagamitin sa Halalan 2025. MANILA — On May 12, 2025, Filipinos will once again exercise their right to vote as the Philippines holds its midterm elections to elect new leaders in national and local positions.

title: DA confiscates 600 kgs of smuggled meat in Parañaque
body: NMIS on Thursday raided four shops in Parañaque City. During the operation, it was discovered that these shops possessed counterfeit meat inspection certificates, raising concerns about the safety and origin of the meat. The NMIS Central Office Enforcement revealed that the presented meat inspection certificate was fake and contained fraudulent information. This suggests a high likelihood of smuggling or illegal entry into the country. 'Mayroon din tayo na na-present satin na Meat Inspection Certificate. Ito po ay fake po. Na-verify natin sa San Simon ang (certificate), pero ang pangalan po ay nasa NCR. Yung meat establishment po ay hindi po dito sa NCR, at ito ay nasa Pampanga. Hindi rin natin ma-identify kung sino nag-issue (ng certificate).Technically po smuggled ang lalabas po sya ngayon dahil walang documents. Ang tawag sa kaniya ay hot meat so in violation sa meat inspection code of the Philippines,' said Dr. June Padasas of NMIS Central Office Enforcement. The NMIS emphasizes the significance of inspecting imported meat to prevent the introduction of diseases such as bird flu. Moreover, it strictly prohibits the importation of pork from countries affected by African Swine Fever (ASF), such as China. 'Ito yung reason bakit tayo nag-ban kasi may mga sakit. Example natin yung bird flu yung bird flu po ay disease siya na kakalat sa tao kung patuloy na papasok dito. Chances are pwede tayo tamaan. Yung pork eh yung ASF tuloy tuloy yung pag-ragasa satin. Malaki epekto sa ekonomiya at sa local industry,' Padasas said. The NMIS states that this recent raid on several stores in Parañaque City is similar to previous operations conducted in Bulacan, Rizal, and Pasay. The four stores selling the confiscated meat share common branding and can all be traced back to China. 'Na-trace natin na mayroon siyang pinangggalingan na isang grupo na nagpapa-takbo kasi pare-pareho yung mga brand yung sa Meycauayan. Nakikita natin sa Binangonan. Nakikita din natin sa Taytay. Itong brand ng black chicken ay same markings na nakuha natin last month sa Pasay naman at sa Cartimar,' Dennis Solomon, Department of Agriculture Inspectorate and Enforcement head, said. 'So yung tinatakbo ng investigation malapit na naming matumbok ang source ng lahat ng hot meat na ito at bawal na produkto,' he added. In order to prevent the sale and consumption of these illegal products, the DA will take appropriate measures such as destroying, burning, and burying the confiscated meat. MANILA — The Department of Agriculture's National Meat Inspection Service (NMIS) seized meat worth P250,000 or around 600 kilos of chicken , duck, and pork meat, suspected to be smuggled or illegally imported from China.

title: 2024 Bar Chair: Passing grade lowered because justices 'want more lawyers'
body: Lopez, Bar Examinations chair, said the adjustment meant 953 more examinees passed. A total of 3,962 bar-takers passed the exams, a passing rate of 37.84 percent. 'The justices would like to have more lawyers. Ayaw nilang 3,000 lang, they want more, and there are several reasons,' Lopez said. 'One reason, perhaps, is the scarcity of lawyers in some areas... the provinces may not have enough lawyers and, at this time, I wish lawyers will also decongest Metro Manila and serve the people there in the rural areas.' Lopez said the lowering of the passing grade was also the reason there was a delay in the announcement of the topnotchers and bar passers. He noted that the decision to lower the passing mark was a decision that went through the en banc. 'I was prevailed upon by the generosity of the justices, hence I cannot do otherwise,' Lopez said. He also explained that the advance information that some examinees received individually was due to a new technology that the Supreme Court used for the 2024 Bar Exams. 'We will know better. Of course, with this new technology, we can prevent this [from happening] again. But the integrity, I will assure you, of the Bar Examination was never diminished. There is total confidentiality and nobody had an access with the sensitive issue of the questions and answers from the bar examiners.' 'The justices would like to have more lawyers. Ayaw nilang 3,000 lang, they want more, and there are several reasons,' Lopez said. MANILA — The passing grade for the 2024 Bar Examinations was adjusted to 74 percent from 75 percent to allow more applicants to pass, Associate Justice Mario Lopez said.

title: 'Di ako critic': Imee denies rift with Marcos Jr., says they are a 'normal family'
body: According to Sen. Imee Marcos, she was merely expressing her opinions to improve the administration of her brother President Bongbong Marcos. 'Hindi ako critic. Gusto ko lang ipaliwanag... Ang ginagawa natin eh naghahanap ng solusyon,' Sen. Marcos said during a press conference. During the one year that her brother has been in office, the senator opposed some of his policies and proposed legislations, such as the ratification of Regional Comprehensive Economic Partnership and the housing of Afghans formerly employed by the United States. But according to Sen. Marcos, she was merely expressing her opinions to improve the administration of her brother. 'Hindi naman sa pagkokontra, we're trying to hammer out details... Makulit ako eh. Talagang gusto kong alamin yung detalye. Matagal akong executive... So I'm always concerned that a law should work... So kinukulit ko talaga sya,' she said. The senator said that she loves her brother but added that they go through conflicts just like any other 'normal family.' 'Hindi kami nag-uusap nang madalas.., Minsan may sasabihin si Sandro, minsan papaabot ni Bongbong. Ganon. Minsan si Sandro nagsusumbong. Okay lang, normal kaming pamilya,' she said. 'I'm solid admin, no ifs or buts. I'm only here to protect the President and the family name. We fought hard and long for this and we're deeply invested in making certain that the Marcos administration should work,' Sen. Marcos added. Thirty-six years ago, the Marcoses were deposed and forced into exile in the US following the February 1986 'People Power Revolution' led by opposition leader Corazon C. Aquino. Marcos Jr. took the presidency last year after a landslide victory in the May 9 general elections. MANILA -- Sen. Imee Marcos on Thursday denied having a rift with brother President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr. as she defended her criticisms on several policies of the current administration.

title: Zaldy Co’s allegations not admissible without oath – IBP
body: IBP National President Atty. Allan Panolong emphasized that unsworn statements are not admissible as evidence in court. 'Para ang statement ng isang tao ay mabigyan ng credence o halaga ng ating korte, kailangang panumpaan iyon. So, there is a need for that statement to be under oath,' Panolong said in an interview on the sidelines of a memorandum of agreement (MOA) signing between the IBP, National Police Commission (NAPOLCOM) and Philippine National Police (PNP) in Quezon City. He added that sworn statements ensure accountability. 'The essence of why a person’s statement should be under oath is sakali mang nagsisinungaling siya, pwede siyang kasuhan ng perjury. Kung totoo ang sinasabi niya, it has an evidential weight, lalo na kung yung statements are corroborated by pieces of evidence,' Panolong explained. The lawyer stressed that due process requires giving accused parties the opportunity to respond. 'It should be under oath because it will offer also, give an opportunity to the other party being accused to have their own day in court also,' he said. 'Unang-una po, dapat magpakita po siya para masuportahan niya ang kanyang mga sinasabi. Hindi po pwede kasing ang isang tao ay susulpot, biglang may ikukwento at wala man lang oportunidad doon sa kabilang party. 'Yan ang essence ng due process — kailangang may right to confront also the person who is actually accusing. It is very necessary that when someone is exposing something, dapat magpakita siya because it will be a sign of sincerity and good faith on his part,' he continued. Panolong also said that not all material shown online are automatically admissible. 'Madali lang mag-allege ng ebidensya. Yung pictures kailangang i-authenticate ‘yan. There must be someone who took the picture and may personal knowledge ano ang laman ng mga maleta. Hindi lahat ng bagay ay pwede maadmit as evidence in court,' he added. On Sunday, Co released the third part of his video statement, accusing Romualdez of threatening to ' shoot' him should he 'talk ,' saying he could also be killed should he return to the Philippines. Co, who left the country in July, alleged that he got the threat as early as March 2025 during a 'meeting.' The former lawmaker also said that he still had 'a lot to bring to light' which he would share in the next few days. The Integrated Bar of the Philippines (IBP) on Monday said that the allegations made by former Ako Bicol Representative Zaldy Co against President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. and former House Speaker Martin Romualdez hold no legal weight unless placed under oath and properly presented before authorities. MANILA — The Integrated Bar of the Philippines (IBP) on Monday said that the allegations made by former Ako Bicol Representative Zaldy Co against President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. and former House Speaker Martin Romualdez hold no legal weight unless placed under oath and properly presented before authorities.

title: Teodoro warns SEA nations vs relying on ‘mightier’ countries on territorial issues
body: Teodoro warns ASEAN veterans against ‘use of bigger, mightier countries of brute power’ to force themselves on others’ territory, sovereignty The defense secretary, a former legislator and mining exec, made the remarks before around 160 delegates of the Veterans Confederation of ASEAN Countries (VECONAC) at an assembly in a Pasay City hotel. The delegates come from the Philippines, Singapore, Thailand, Vietnam, Cambodia, Malaysia, Indonesia, Laos, Myanmar, and Brunei. 'We are beset in this country by the force of greater powers seeking to expand their reach and influence into our national sovereignty and territory, and thus, the Philippines struggles again,' Teodoro said in his speech. The Philippines has been navigating relations with treaty ally the US and with regional power China and has been pursuing a policy of being a friend to all and an enemy to none. 'We are facing the same threats again in a different guise. Different actors again threaten our security and peace, and if one member of ASEAN’s security is strengthened, then collectively, we open the door for further expansionism by others whom we do not want.' The top defense official also urged the veterans and their organizations to heed the 'signs of the times' and learn from the country's leaders' past mistakes. 'Why am I telling you this? Because if we disregard the signs of the times and we fail to act collectively, and you fail to exercise your influence on your current leadership in order to act as one, then your individual struggles may have been for naught, which we do not want to see,' Teodoro said. 'Then again, use our examples too of your individual struggles not to repeat the mistakes that our leaders made in the past.' Retired military and police officials in the Philippines retain influence over active officers and personnel. 'We seek the path of principled peace, sustainable peace, institution-building, and mechanisms of trust between our countries,' Teodoro said. 'We seek brotherhood, sisterhood and friendship between ASEAN countries because it is not just accident that we are here together. We have to work together to make this region the most prosperous and most peaceful in the world.' The general assembly of the VECONAC aims to fortify unity and collaboration among veterans' organizations, fostering regional solidarity through 'impactful initiatives' and formulation of clear action plans. It also aims to promote and defend regional peace and security and ensure the health and well-being of all veterans in the ASEAN. MANILA — Southeast Asian nations should be wary of relying on 'bigger and mightier countries of brute power' to advance their issues of national territory and sovereignty, Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. said on Tuesday.

title: Tension erupts as residents resist demolition attempt in Tondo, Manila
body: By early morning, residents had already padlocked the gate leading into Barangays 262 and 264 to block the entry of police and demolition team. The Mayhaligue Neighborhood Association said that they did not receive any formal notice regarding the demolition. 'Nagulat kami, bigla kaming gigibain, wala kaming kahit anong notice. Aksidente lang namin nalaman dahil no’ng pumunta sila ng May 19, dala ni sheriff ang maraming pulis din, magsusukat daw sila…. As early as May 19, 2023, meron na rin pong desisyon ang parehong korte, MTC Branch 24, na-dismiss outright due to lack of jurisdiction.' said Ruby Dela Cruz, president of the association. However, Sheriff Raymundo Rojas countered that a demolition order had been issued as early as October, 2024 by the Metropolitan Trial Court Branch 24, after four years of litigation involving the land which is said to be owned by a private corporation. 'May order of demolition na. Ang sabi ko kanina ang makakapagpigil ay TRO saka God’s act… Nag-usap sila after Christmas, pinagbigyan. Tapos after klase [at] eleksyon. Tapos na lahat mag-oopening na naman,' said Rojas. The tension intensified when the demolition team attempted to force open the gate around 11 a.m. The Manila Fire District sprayed water at residents who refused to move, but the latter held their ground as some used their bodies as shields. Residents fought back using fire extinguishers and throwing empty bottles, forcing police and demolition personnel to temporarily retreat. The confrontation led to several arrests. The personnel of the Presidential Commission for the Urban Poor (PCUP) were also at the site and offered financial assistance of P38,700 to each affected family should the demolition transpire. 'Ang concern lang kasi ng PCUP kung compliance naman po at merong writ of demolition at saka ‘yong mga document, ina-assure lang namin na kung walang relocation, at least financial assistance,' said Mary Gene Agoncillo, Development Management Officer of PCUP. Manila Vice Mayor Yul Servo and several city councilors also arrived at the scene, requesting the sheriff to hold the demolition. 'Kinakausap pa namin na mapagbigyan din ‘yong nirequest ng tao,' said Servo. Around 1 p.m., the demolition team and police dispersed, though no official reason was given. According to Manila LGU officials, the residents' association filed a request for a Temporary Restraining Order (TRO). 'Tomorrow po magkakaroon ng hearing pansamantala for our hearing for issuance of injunction and temporary restraining order sa bagong judge. 10 am bukas,' said Manila Citu Councilor Uno Lim. City officials assured its support to affected residents as the legal battle over the land continues. The private corporation said to own the disputed land has yet to release an official statement as of this writing. A standoff between residents and a demolition team erupted Monday morning, May 26, in Mayhaligue, Tondo, Manila, as the latter blocked a scheduled demolition of their homes, asserting that the operation was illegal. MANILA — A standoff between residents and a demolition team erupted Monday morning, May 26, in Mayhaligue, Tondo, Manila, after the residents blocked a scheduled demolition of their homes, asserting that the operation was illegal.

title: Death of witness inside Bilibid will not affect De Lima case: lawyer
body: The Department of Justice earlier confirmed Sebastian's death as it ordered an investigation into the sudden demise of high-profile inmates at the national penitentiary. Sebastian was a witness in 2 criminal cases filed against De Lima and an accused in a third complaint, according to one of her lawyers Boni Tacardon. Sebastian had claimed he collected money from drug lords in the New Bilibid Prison to fund De Lima's supposed senatorial run in 2013, Tacardon said. 'We believe that Mr. Sebastian will not in anyway bolster the prosecution's evidence against Sen. De Lima. We believe that all the evidence against the good senator are weak from the very beginning simply because they are mere fabricated stories devoid of any factual basis,' he told ANC. 'Whether the fabricator is dead or alive doesn’t change the fact that the stories are all lies and that the case against me are all fake,' Tacardon read from the senator's official statement. He added that De Lima's legal team has been 'exposing the fabricated nature of the testimonies' presented by prosecutors for the past 3 years. 'Any follower of the hearings can easily observe how incredible their stories are and how they actually contradicted each other. There was even 1 witness who intimated to the senator herself in open court that he will be killed if he did not testify,' he said. Tacardon identified the witness as Engelberto Durano, who testified on Oct. 25, 2019 in the third criminal case against the senator. 'In the course of his testimony, there was a short lull and after that, Senator De Lima was surprised seeing Durano, with his right hand, palm down, making a slashing motion across his neck. Sen. De Lima was taken aback and felt threatened,' he told ABS-CBN News. 'When asked if he indeed made that gesture, Durano denied that he was threatening Sen. De Lima. What he allegedly meant was that he would be killed if he will not testify. The panel of prosecutors tried to downplay the incident by assuring Sen. De Lima that she was not being threatened.' The senator last month filed a petition for bail in one of her cases due to 'hearsay' evidence. De Lima has rejected allegations against her, claiming she had been locked up to stop her from criticizing the administration's war on drugs that has seen thousands of suspected criminals killed. The death of high-profile inmate Jaybee Sebastian, one of the accusers of detained Sen. Leila de Lima, will not affect her drug-related cases, the senator's counsel said Tuesday. MANILA (UPDATE) - The death of high-profile inmate Jaybee Sebastian, one of the accusers of detained Sen. Leila de Lima, will not affect her drug-related cases, the senator's counsel said Tuesday.

title: Young bet for Las Piñas councilor says youth voice, issues should not be ignored
body: Among the youngest candidates in the National Capital Region and running under the Makabayan coalition, Castillo said youth welfare and development must be a government priority. 'Hindi dapat isinasantabi ang boses ng kabataan dahil tayo ang magilas na new generation na kayang mag-transform ng progressive ideas into concrete action. Ang pag-asa ng bayan ay ang kabataan, hindi ang mga political dynasty,' she said in a release. (The youth voice should not be sidelined because we are the new generation that will transform progressive ideas into concrete action. The country's hope is in the youth and not in political dynasties. She said that many incumbent officials are from political dynasties, are senior politicians or are backed by business interests. 'Kung may youth representation man sa gobyerno, madalas mga young politicians yan na nadala ng political grooming at blind obedience sa nakakatanda sa kanilang pulitiko. Ayan ang tinututulan ko, hindi ako baby crocodile and I want to inspire fellow youth to fight for genuine youth representation,' she added in an exchange with ABS-CBN News. (Even when there is youth representation in government, this is usually by young politicians who have been influenced by political grooming and offer blind obedience to older politicians. That is what I am opposing. I am not a baby crocodile and I want to inspire fellow youth to fight for genuine youth representation) 'As the youngest candidate, youth issues talaga yung front and center platform ko. Gusto ko magkaroon ng P1,000 monthly student allowance sa Las Piñas dahil ito na lang yung lungsod sa buong [National Capital Region] na walang student allowance,' she said. (As the youngest candidate, I put youth issues front and center. I want to oush for a P1,000 monthly student allowance in Las Piñas. We are the only city in NCR without a student allowance) She said she also wants to lead in addressing calamities like flooding, by opposing reclamation projects in Manila Bay and pushing for effective and scientific disaster risk reduction and management. 'Coastal city ang Las Piñas so nasa sharp end siya ng climate crisis (Las Piñas is a coastal city and is at the sharp end of the climate crisis,' she said. Castillo said she also wants to help provide more labor desks to help Las Piñas residents find sustainable jobs and to protect their labor rights and welfare. Makabayan senatorial candidate Liza Maza and Kabataan Party-list first nominee Renee Co were among Castillo's supporters on the first day for local electoral campaign. Although she acknowledged the lack of resources and political machinery will make campaigning a challenge, Castillo said she would prioritize the grass-roots approach of meeting and talking to her potential constituents. 'Hindi dapat isinasantabi ang boses ng kabataan dahil tayo ang magilas na new generation na kayang mag-transform ng progressive ideas into concrete action. Ang pag-asa ng bayan ay ang kabataan, hindi ang mga political dynasty,' candidate Jinsei Castillo said MANILA — Jinsei Castillo, a youth leader vying for the Las Piñas 1st District councilor seat, rolled out her campaign Friday at the Diocesan Shrine and Parish of Saint Joseph, a city landmark because it is home to the famous Bamboo Organ. 'Hindi dapat isinasantabi ang boses ng kabataan dahil tayo ang magilas na new generation na kayang mag-transform ng progressive ideas into concrete action. Ang pag-asa ng bayan ay ang kabataan, hindi ang mga political dynasty,' candidate Jinsei Castillo said

title: Lipa Archbishop Gilbert Garcera is new CBCP president
body: The CBCP made the announcement on July 5, 2025, at the beginning of their 130th plenary assembly in Bohol. The 66-year-old prelate will succeed Kalookan Bishop Pablo Virgilio Cardinal David, whose second and final term as the president of the bishops' collegial body will end in November. The CBCP president and other officials serve two-year terms and may be reelected once. Garcera has been serving as archbishop of Lipa since 2017, when he succeeded Archbishop Ramon Arguelles. The new CBCP president previously chaired the Commission on Mission and the Commission on Family and Life. Garcera also served as chairman of the Federation of Asian Bishops’ Conferences’ (FABC) Office on Laity and Family and as director of the Synodal Leadership for Asian Bishops Seminar. Before becoming Lipa archbishop, Garcera served as Daet bishop from 2007. Meanwhile, Zamboanga Archbishop Julius Tonel was elected as CBCP vice president. He will succeed Pasig Bishop Mylo Hubert Vergara, whose term will also end in November. The 68-year-old Tonel was ordained to the episcopate when he was appointed bishop of the Diocese of Ipil. In 2023, Pope Francis appointed him as archbishop of Zamboanga, succeeding the late Archbishop Romulo Dela Cruz. Tonel is the incumbent head of the CBCP Committee on Bishops’ Concerns and previously served as chairman of the CBCP Commission on Liturgy. The newly elected officials will officially begin their term on December 1, 2025. Filipino bishops have elected Lipa Archbishop Gilbert Garcera as the new president of the Catholic Bishops’ Conference of the Philippines (CBCP). MANILA - Filipino bishops have elected Lipa Archbishop Gilbert Garcera as the new president of the Catholic Bishops’ Conference of the Philippines (CBCP).

title: Sandigabayan junks Estrada motion for reconsideration in PDAF graft case
body: Estrada had sought to dismiss the remaining graft cases against him, saying the prosecution 'not only failed to prove the scienter (knowledge or intent) element but also failed to show that he actually caused the award of the PDAF projects.' Aside from this, the senator said there was no proof that he had knowledge about the PDAF scheme and that 'he actually met and conferred with any of the other accused regarding the PDAF projects.' He added there was no 'direct proof' of his acts, and that his endorsements that ultimately led to the issuance of PDAF funds 'were not directives but merely recommendations.' Estrada said he 'did not ask for or direct any violation of law but, rather, specifically directed that the relevant rules and regulations be complied with.' He added his signatures on project documents are not evidence of intentional participation in the scam, which involved putting discretionary funds into foundations that turned out to be fake. But, in a 22-page resolution, the anti-graft court’s Special Fifth Division said there was no reason to reverse its previous rejection of Estrada’s demurrer to evidence, a motion seeking to dismiss a case for lack of evidence. 'After a careful perusal of the arguments raised by accused Estrada in his motion for reconsideration, the Court finds no cogent reason to disturb its findings in the assailed resolution denying his demurrer to evidence,' the Sandiganbayan said. 'The findings and conclusions in the plunder case do not automatically warrant the dismissal of the present graft cases,' it added. The Sandiganbayan in April said the prosecutors were able to allege Estrada’s PDAF 'was systematically pocketed, was divided among the accused, and nothing went to the supposed beneficiaries of the PDAF-funded agricultural and livelihood programs.' It added prosecution was able to show that Estrada allegedly 'received kickbacks' from businesswoman Janet Lim Napoles. 'While it would be premature at this stage to delve into the probative value of the evidence adduced by the prosecution, the court notes that the prosecution has formally offered, to and the record contains the explicit testimony of witness Ruby Tuason,' the document read. 'Tuason testified that she met with accused Estrada, informed him of the scheme of accused Napoles as early as 2004, and personally delivered amounts to him on various occasions at the Senate and his house. There is likewise testimony on record from witness Benhur Luy that he had endorsement letters signed by accused Estrada at the Senate through accused Labayen,' it added. Estrada had argued that the daily disbursement reports (DDR) of PDAF whistleblower Benhur Luy were 'unreliable as they are mere print-outs and can nowadays be easily manufactured.' In 2024, the Sandiganbayan upheld its earlier decision to acquit Estrada of indirect bribery and direct bribery due to lack of evidence. The case stemmed from P183 million in alleged kickbacks from 'fake projects' in collusion with Napoles. The Sandiganbayan has denied Sen. Jinggoy Estrada’s motion for reconsideration seeking the dismissal of his graft cases in connection with the Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) scam. MANILA (UPDATED) — The Sandiganbayan has denied Sen. Jinggoy Estrada’s motion for reconsideration seeking the dismissal of his graft cases in connection with the Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) scam 'for lack of merit.'

title: 62 Chinese naval, coast guard ships spotted in West PH Sea in February
body: The ships from the People’s Liberation Army Navy (PLA-N) and China Coast Guard (CCG) were seen in the following features: Bajo de Masinloc – 23 vessels (11 PLA-N, 12 CCG) Ayungin Shoal – 17 vessels (2 PLA-N, 15 CCG) Escoda Shoal – 13 vessels (6 PLA-N, 7 CCG) Pag-asa Island – 9 vessels (5 PLA-N, 4 CCG) 'These figures reflect the continued illegal Chinese Communist Party (CCP) presence in areas wherein the Philippines exercises sovereignty, jurisdiction and sovereign rights,' AFP spokesperson for the West Philippine Sea Rear Admiral Roy Vincent Trinidad said on Tuesday. Meanwhile, across the country’s broader maritime domain, the Philippine Navy monitored a total of 18,360 vessels in February, reflecting sustained maritime activity within Philippine waters. Of the total, 15,327 were foreign vessels while 3,033 were domestic vessels. A total of 13,523 vessels responded to radio challenges while 4,837 did not respond. Monitoring efforts remained distributed across key maritime areas nationwide, with heightened activity recorded in Northern Luzon, the West Philippine Sea, Western Mindanao, and Eastern Seaboard areas, consistent with established maritime traffic patterns and ongoing operational monitoring. 'In line with national policy, the AFP remains committed to safeguarding national territory, sovereignty and sovereign rights, while upholding international law and contributing to regional peace and stability,' Trinidad said. Philippine Coast Guard spokesperson Commodore Jay Tarriela during a joint NTF-WPS press conference at the PCG Office in Manila on March 6, 2024. Jonathan Cellona, ABS-CBN News MANILA — The government’s approach to publicizing incidents in the West Philippine Sea remains guided by the principle of 'one message, many voices,' according to a Philippine Coast Guard official, who said there has been no directive to tone down messaging despite concerns over its impact on diplomatic space. 3 Filipino fishermen injured in Chinese ships' water cannon attack In an interview on the sidelines of the second founding anniversary of the Philippine Coast Guard - West Philippine Sea Transparency Group (PCG-WPSTG) on Thursday, Commodore Jay Tarriela stressed that transparency must be sustained as public awareness on maritime issues has significantly increased over the past three years. Tarriela said there has been no order for government officials to soften their rhetoric on the West Philippine Sea. He explained that while agencies share a unified message, they differ in delivery based on their mandates. Tarriela cited the roles of the spokespeople of the PCG, Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP), and Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA). 'All of us have different levels of messaging, and it represents the mandate of each agency that we represent,' Tarriela told reporters in Manila. In a separate interview during the event, National Security Adviser (NSA) Eduardo Año reiterated that the 'one message, many voices' approach reflects the varying levels of responsibility within government. 'We have tactical, operational, and strategic levels, so we cannot just talk about one thing,' Año noted. He added that transparency helps generate international support and puts pressure on China to stop its bullying and harassment in the area. 'So, let’s stay true to the truth,' the official said. PUBLIC BACKLASH SEEN IF TRANSPARENCY STOPS Tarriela warned that putting a stop to transparency efforts could trigger strong public backlash, pointing to survey results showing that 94 percent of Filipinos support the government’s transparency initiative on maritime incidents. 'It’s important to continue it because right now, the level of awareness of our fellow Filipinos is very high. For the past three years, they're already used to it. They know everything that's happening. They know what the government is doing,' the official said. He then continued, 'If ever, suddenly, we decide to put a stop to transparency, for me, our fellow Filipinos will have a pushback.' ‘TRANSPARENCY SHOULD BE INSTITUTIONALIZED’ Año emphasized that the transparency initiative must be institutionalized to ensure long-term protection of the country’s sovereignty and sovereign rights, regardless of changes in political leadership. 'We have to take care of our future. We want to institutionalize this so that we will be able to protect our sovereignty, our sovereign rights, and at the same time, ensure that what we have gained will be protected and will be continued by incoming leaders,' Año stressed. 'This is for the good of our generation… This is our territory and our sovereign rights. We cannot give this away,' he added. The official noted that the administration is backing proposed legislation to strengthen national security and protect the public. 'We have a lot of senators who are supporting us. So, the Anti-Espionage Law, we support that. The Foreign Interference and Malign Influence Act, we also support that. These are the laws that will give protection to our countrymen,' Año said. He went on, 'Now that we have the support of the people of the Philippines, we hope that our countrymen will continue to support us in our transparency and in protecting our sovereign rights.' Tarriela also said sustained transparency efforts could make the West Philippine Sea a major consideration for voters in the next elections. He noted that if the policy is maintained until 2028, public awareness and expectations would likely influence voters’ choices, with Filipinos seeking candidates who are willing to defend the country’s maritime rights. China info manipulation may expand closer to 2028 polls: officials 'Of course, they will be looking for a potential candidate—not just for president, but even for the Senate, or even for their district representatives in Congress—they will want to have a politician who's going to stand up for our rights in the West Philippine Sea,' Tarriela said. 'For me, eventually, the transparency efforts that we're doing right now will definitely make the West Philippine Sea as an election issue,' he added. The government’s approach to publicizing incidents in the West Philippine Sea remains guided by the principle of 'one message, many voices,' according to a Philippine Coast Guard official. Lawyer Levito 'Levi' Baligod, accompanied by his legal team, files a formal complaint before the Office of the Ombudsman on Thursday, February 26, following allegations from active and retired Marines that certain congresswomen received unlawfully obtained government funds. Maria Tan, ABS-CBN News MANILA — The Philippine Marine Corps (PMC) has branded as 'opportunistic' the group of individuals claiming to be 'bagmen' of former lawmaker Zaldy Co. In a statement on Thursday, PMC Commandant Major General Vicente Blanco III underscored that individuals who were dishonorably discharged from the service cannot be considered true Marines and have no moral authority to invoke the adage 'Once a Marine, Always a Marine.' PH Navy: 4 of 18 presented as ‘bagmen’ were never Marines Reiterating the Corps’ time-honored ethos and strong esprit de corps, Blanco stressed that the Marine identity is inseparable from honor, discipline, and integrity—values that must be upheld both in and out of uniform. 'It is unfortunate that in the recent press conference called by a private lawyer, three retired Marines involved themselves,' Blanco said. 'Barring use of force, threats, intimidation, and/or promise of rewards to them (3 retired Marines), based on the information available to us, we understand that their involvement was entirely of their own choosing, and that they did this on their own personal volition, and as such, we should always look with disdain against opportunistic groups or individuals trying to link their actions as being true Marines—they are unworthy of our respect,' he added. On Tuesday, lawyer Levito 'Levi' Baligod claimed that the 18 individuals served as 'bagmen' delivering P805 billion to President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. and former House Speaker Martin Romualdez. The group said they had worked as security personnel for Co and claimed they had transported cash to certain government officials. Lawyer refers affidavit of 'ex-Marines' vs lawmakers, gov't officials to Ombudsman The Philippine Navy earlier said it conducted due diligence in verifying the identities of the 18 individuals. Based on official records, the Navy said four were never members of the Philippine Navy nor the PMC, while the majority were dishonorably discharged. Others retired in good standing. Navy spokesperson Captain Marissa Arlene Martinez said the service could not disclose the names of those dishonorably discharged in order to 'protect their families and [preserve their] dignity.' The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) said the allegations should be addressed through proper legal forums, stressing that the military supports the release of the truth through lawful processes. AFP spokesperson Colonel Francel Margareth Padilla clarified that no military security detail was ever assigned to Co and emphasized that the individuals presented as alleged 'bagmen' were already retired or separated from the service at the time of the supposed incidents. She added that once personnel retire or are discharged, they act in their private capacity and no longer represent the AFP. Padilla also stressed that the military remains focused on its mandate, including territorial defense, particularly the security situation in the West Philippine Sea, and humanitarian assistance and disaster response (HADR) operations. The Philippine Marine Corps (PMC) has branded as 'opportunistic' the group of individuals claiming to be 'bagmen' of former lawmaker Zaldy Co. MANILA — A cadet of the Philippine Military Academy (PMA) has completed his cadetship and academic training at the Republic of Korea Air Force Academy (ROKAFA), marking another milestone in international military education and defense cooperation. Cadet Euniño Joaquin Manuel, 24, graduated from ROKAFA on Wednesday after completing a rigorous four-year cadetship in South Korea. A native of Cabuyao, Laguna, Manuel is the eldest son of Engineer Elrey Manuel and Mrs. Sheryl Manuel. He is a member of PMA 'Bagong Sinag' Class of 2024. In January 2022, Manuel was dispatched to ROKAFA under the Philippines–Republic of Korea defense cooperation framework, allowing selected PMA cadets to continue their military education and training in foreign academies. He completed his studies with a Bachelor of Science in Mechanical Engineering and, upon his return to the country, will be commissioned as a Second Lieutenant in the Philippine Air Force (PAF). Marcos Jr. hails PMA's first batch of West PH Sea-trained cadets as they graduate from academy During his stay at ROKAFA, Manuel distinguished himself through exceptional physical performance and military bearing. He earned the title 'Strongest Cadet' for six consecutive semesters and served as 2nd Battalion Athletic Staff Officer during the second semester of Academic Year 2025–2026. Reflecting on his journey, Manuel described his graduation as 'more than the completion of military training—it is the result of endurance, adaptation, and unwavering commitment.' He shared that the Korean phrase '피할 수 없으면 즐겨라' ('If you can’t avoid it, enjoy it') became a guiding mindset throughout the most demanding phases of his cadetship, teaching him to embrace hardship and face challenges with composure and gratitude. According to Manuel, representing the Philippines in a foreign military academy was both an honor and a responsibility, as he carried the national flag in every formation, evaluation, and exercise. For the PMA, Manuel’s graduation from ROKAFA underscores the institution’s commitment to broadening the perspectives of future Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) officers through international education and training. 'Such programs help future AFP officers gain exposure to allied doctrines, operational approaches, and military cultures, strengthening long-term interoperability and defense cooperation between the Philippines and the Republic of Korea,' the PMA said in a statement. The academy also views Manuel’s accomplishment as an inspiration to young Filipinos aspiring to serve the nation. 'It demonstrates that through discipline, faith, and hard work, Filipino cadets can excel in demanding international settings while carrying the honor of the PMA, the AFP, and the nation on the global stage,' it added. A cadet of the Philippine Military Academy has completed his cadetship and academic training at the Republic of Korea Air Force Academy. Sen. Rodante Marcoleta joins a plenary session, August 6, 2025. Joseph B. Vidal, OSP/File MANILA — Sen. Rodante Marcoleta on Wednesday sought to remove his 'give up KIG (Kalayaan Island Group)' remark from the minutes of the Commission on Appointments (CA) February 4 meeting, proposing corrections instead. The CA, in its February 25 meeting, opened the discussion on Resolution 099-016 of the Sangguniang Bayan of Kalayaan, Palawan, which seeks to expunge from its records the alleged 'defeatist sentiments' of Marcoleta. CA defers action on resolution of Kalayaan municipality over Marcoleta remark To recall, Marcoleta said in the February 4 meeting that four features in the Kalayaan Island Group (KIG) are not part of the Philippines’ exclusive economic zone (EEZ). Marcoleta has since clarified that he did not mean to remove the KIG from Philippine territory. Marcoleta: Spratlys not discovered by Tomas Cloma On Wednesday, he objected to the proposed minutes of the February 4 meeting and proposed corrections. 'I'd like to make the following corrections on the draft minutes. Delete the entire portion pertaining to my interpellation with Captain Jess B. Pilar,' he said. Pilar is one of the 35 officers of the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) whose ad interim appointments were considered on February 4. Marcoleta then read his proposed amendments to the minutes: 'Senator Rodante Marcoleta expressed his views on the issues surrounding the West Philippine Sea. The senator said that the recent controversy started with Commodore Jay Tristan Tarriela's public discussion on the subject, wherein he called the Chinese President Xi Jinping a bully and showed unappealing caricatures of the Chinese President which clearly offended the Chinese Embassy,' he said. 'Senator Marcoleta asked if there is a policy that heads of other countries be subjected to satire and be vilified through caricature to which Captain Pilar said that there is none.' Marcoleta then shared a portion of the Department of Foreign Affairs letter to Senator Risa Hontiveros, which stated that disputes between states are best addressed through diplomatic channels than in public, and that escalation even in public messaging should only be done when it advances clear strategic objectives and the national interest. He then asked Captain Pilar what we gain strategically from Commodore Tarriela's actions in terms of national interest, to which Captain Pilar replied that, being a member of the AFP, we were just voicing out that our seats, our rights, and what is legally ours, as is stated in our laws. Marcoleta replied that while what is legally ours must be defended, Commodore Tarriela should be transparent in explaining that the West Philippine Sea and the Exclusive Economic Zone are two different entities. Marcoleta then proceeded to explain technical details on the West Philippine Sea, stating that the archipelagic baselines have been defined under Republic Act No. 9552, and that from the archipelagic baseline we can already measure our EEZ, contiguous zone, and territorial sea. However, he highlighted the fact that the arbitral award only ruled on the EEZ. He emphasized that the four features of the Kalayan Island Group, Pag-asa, Parola, Kota, and Lankiam, currently being occupied by the Philippines, are located beyond our EEZ. Captain Pilar maintained that these features are within our EEZ and that they can also generate their own EEZs. Senator Marcoleta, however, reiterated his point that these four features are indeed beyond the country's EEZ and that as rocks, these features do not generate their own EEZs. He said that the problem lies with how people tend to use both the term EEZ and the West Philippine Sea interchangeably. Senator Marcoleta said that under Administrative Order Number 29 (2012), the National Mapping and Resource Information Authority, or NAMRIA, and the DFA are required to prepare an official map of the Philippines with the West Philippine Sea; to deposit it with the Office of the United Nations Secretary General; and to notify the appropriate international organizations. However, this was not complied with despite passage of several years precisely because of the legal complexities surrounding the KIG that lies outside our EEZ. He said that if a map is thrown appropriating the high seas, we will violate the UNCLOS itself. With the dilemma of plotting the West Philippine Sea that will include the waters of the KIG in the high seas, Senator Marcoleta used a hypothetical scenario of giving up the KIG if only to simplify the problem of delimiting the West Philippine Sea. He further said that within the South China Sea are the EEZs from different countries and that Vietnam, Malaysia and China were able to manage their differences and consequently had numerous oil and gas explorations within their areas. The Philippines has been left behind on this aspect as we have been unable to manage our disputes. Finally, Marcoleta mentioned that when he met with the Chinese Ambassador, the latter opened up his willingness to discuss with all concerned agencies of the Philippine government. Senator Marcoleta then suggested that we should begin to engage with them and to start with less contentious matters such as fishing rights, which both parties may benefit from.' Marcoleta emphasized that his proposed amendments do not intend to change the substance of the proceedings, but to ensure that the records reflect what occurred. Senate Deputy Majority Leader Risa Hontiveros asked if deliberation on the matter could be rescheduled since the CA still needed to confirm the ad interim appointments of 81 AFP general/flag officers and senior officers. CA Majority Floor Leader Camiguin Rep. Jurdin Jesus Romualdo agreed. 'I think because of lack of material time, Mr. Chair, I think we can reschedule this on the next hearing of the Committee on National Defense to tackle this issue,' Romualdo said. Sen. Rodante Marcoleta on Wednesday sought to remove his 'give up KIG (Kalayaan Island Group)' remark from the minutes of the Commission on Appointments (CA) February 4 meeting, proposing corrections instead. MANILA — Philippine Navy spokesperson for the West Philippine Sea Rear Adm. Roy Vincent Trinidad believes that China’s interference with military communication and navigation systems during maritime patrols are part of its ‘coercive and aggressive actions’ in the West Philippine Sea. Speaking on state-run media PTV Wednesday, February 25, Trinidad linked this to previous incidents involving laser pointers and radio challenges. 'Every now and then, ‘yung ating mga barko at eroplano ay nakaka-experience ng interference sa ating mga systems – navigation, communications and other sensors aboard ship,' he said. (Every now and then, our ships and aircraft experience interference in their systems — navigation, communications and other sensors) 'Walang jurisdiction ang China dito': Risa reasserts Philippines' claim to Pag-Asa island He said the inteference could only have come from Chinese ships or artificial bases. The Philippine Navy official also vowed that authorities are doing their best to continue asserting the country’s sovereign rights and sovereignty, emphasizing that the fight is ‘intergenerational’. He added China's actions have effects beyond remote parts of Palawan and Batanes. 'Existential threat ito sapagkat ang kinakaharap natin dito ang threat sa atin is tagpi-tagpiin ang ating bansa,' Trinidad underscored. (This is an existential threat... the threat to us is that the country will be broken up) He said the government does not want to pass on to the next generation a country that has fewer islands than it used to. 'So the AFP, the Department of Defense and I believe even other government agencies, we are doing every that we can with all that we have to ensure the integrity of the territory and to protect our sovereignty,' he added. PNP to coordinate with BI, AFP amid suspected espionage activities by foreign nationals Philippine Navy spokesperson for the West Philippine Sea Rear Admiral Roy Vincent Trinidad believes that China’s interference with Philippine communication and navigation systems during maritime patrols are part of its ‘coercive and aggressive actions’ in the West Philippine Sea. A total of 62 Chinese naval and coast guard vessels were monitored in the West Philippine Sea in February, according to the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP). MANILA -- A total of 62 Chinese naval and coast guard vessels were monitored in the West Philippine Sea in February, according to the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP). Sen. Rodante Marcoleta on Wednesday sought to remove his 'give up KIG (Kalayaan Island Group)' remark from the minutes of the Commission on Appointments (CA) February 4 meeting, proposing corrections instead.

title: Duterte poses big challenge, Poe admits
body: 'I think that every candidate nga may alas eh. There's no denying dahil siya yung frontrunner eh talagang malaki ang hamon sa amin,' Poe said. 'Pero hindi naman kami nawawalan ng loob kasi -- lalo na ako -- bakit ko ba ginagawa ito? Dahil gusto kong magpresenta ng alternatibo na mas maayos at mas mapagkakatiwalaan na pamumuno,' she added. Duterte remains the top choice for president of Filipino voters, according to the latest ABS-CBN survey conducted by Pulse Asia. The survey, conducted from April 26 to 29 among 4,000 respondents, showed Duterte keeping his 33% voter support, over 10 percentage points higher than second-placers Mar Roxas and Poe. Roxas obtained a 22% rating while Poe got 21%, followed by Vice President Jejomar Binay (17%) and Senator Miriam Defensor Santiago (2%). Poe stressed that surveys are not the end-all be-all of a candidate's campaign, citing the cases of Roxas and Binay in the 2010 vice-presidential race and her father, the late Fernando Poe Jr., in the 2004 presidential elections. 'Kung matatandaan ninyo, dati kay FPJ nung tumakbo siya, yung exit poll sa NCR (National Capital Region) talong-talo si FPJ. Pero nung lumabas ang resulta, panalong-panalo sa NCR. Kaya hindi naman infallible ang mga surveys,' she said. 'Alam naman natin na 10 days before the elections eh lamang pa rin si Secretary Mar kay Vice President Binay, pero nakita natin kung paano nagpalit,' Poe added. 'Gusto kong paniwalaan ang survey kung saan mababa ako para hindi ako maging kampante. Ang kabangga natin dito ay napakalakas na makinarya ng administrasyon; at pangalawa, siyempre, yung dissatisfaction rin ng ating mga kababayan sa administrasyon na ito. Kaya hindi ako nawawalan ng enerhiya, patuloy ang aking pag-iikot.' Poe campaigned in vote-rich Quezon today where more than a million votes are at stake. In the final week of the campaign period, almost all of Poe's campaign sorties are in vote-rich areas in Luzon. Meanwhile, Poe shared the sentiments of Senator Antonio Trillanes IV that a Duterte presidency will be vulnerable to a coup d'etat. 'I think it will... I think it is vulnerable to a lot of things. Unang-una, hindi pa nga nakakaupo meron nang malaking kwestyon tungkol sa kredibilidad ng kanyang ari-arian,' she said. 'Kaya nga para sa akin, unsolicited advice kahit na kanino, eh ipakita lang para ma-disprove kung hindi man totoo yon.' 'Tapos pangalawa, yung mga pronouncements din, nakakabahala kung anong gagawin. Pag ikaw ay kumontra, ipapakulong ka. Parang tama ba naman yon, ipapakulong ka. O kaya kung isang institusyon ay kumontra, ipapasara. Parang hindi naman yata demokratiko yung ganoong klaseng sistema.' When asked if she thinks her administration will be as vulnerable, Poe had this to say: 'Pag presidente ako, sa tingin ko, hindi magkaka-coup d'etat.' QUEZON - Senator Grace Poe on Wednesday said she cannot deny that Davao City Mayor Rodrigo Duterte is a threat to her presidential campaign.

title: Search and rescue ops underway for Filipino seafarers from ship attacked by Houthis
body: Migrant Workers Undersecretary Bernard Olalia said they continue to verify reports that three Filipino seafarers were killed during the attack, but they were able to rescue eight out of the 21 Filipinos aboard the vessel. These individuals were already in a safe place, Olalia added, not disclosing their exact location for security reasons. 'Sila ay kasalukuyan nating inaasikaso, nasa kamay sila ng rescuers at sila ay nakapaunta na sa isang safe port para sa eventual facilitated repatriation mula sa Red Sea papunta ng Pilipinas,' Olaloa told reporters in a Saturday news forum. '‘Yung karamihan at kasamahan nila ay kasalukuyan pa nating inaalam ang whereabouts. Ongoing pa ang ating search and rescue, kasama natin dito ang Department of Foreign Affairs at Department of National Defense para sa paghahanap sa kalagayan ng mga Filipino seafarers,' he added. Olalia said they would only be able to confirm the reports of casualties if they would be able to talk directly to the rescued Filipino seafarers. 'Mayroon tayong unconfirmed reports. Aalamin natin ito sa mga kababayan natin na na-rescue na 8 sa oras na makausap natin sila, tatlo ang sinasabi sa news na may kababayan tayong namatay sa MV Eternity C,' he said. 'Maliban sa walo at dalawa na security personnel, unaccounted pa ang the rest,' he said. Olalia said the initial information they received showed their vessel was supposed to go to Israel through the Red Sea. 'From Somalia, kung saan nagbaba sila ng kargamento, sila ay papunta ng Jeddah port para mag-gasolina… sila ay magkakarga ng bunker fuel at from there, sila daw ay pupunta papunta ng Mediterranean Sea—ang sabi ay sila ay tutungo ng Israel,' he said. 'Alam niyo sa Red Sea, na-identify kasi ng Yemeni at Houthis ang patungo na direksyon ng mga barko kaya nata-target ang barko. Kaya matagal na nating sinasabi sa shipowners na iwasan ang Red Sea,' he said. The official reiterated that Filipino seafarers could verbalize their right to refuse sail to shipowners and they should not be discriminated against for doing so. Under a DMW Department Order 1 issued in March 2024, shipowners and manning agencies are mandated to report planned passages through the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden and ensure Filipino crew members are fully informed of the risks involved. Filipino seafarers have the right to refuse to sail in these so-called warlike zones. Olalia said 11 Filipinos from another Houthi-attacked ship MV Magic Seas would arrive in the Philippines later tonight. Eternity C is the second ship that the Iran-aligned group has attacked this week after months of calm. The attacks revived a campaign by the Iran-aligned fighters, who had attacked more than 100 ships from November 2023 to December 2024 in what they said was solidarity with the Palestinians, before a lull in their campaign earlier this year. Search and rescue operations are underway for the 13 Filipinos in the ill-fated MV Eternity C, which sank off the coast of Yemen after being attacked by Houthi militants earlier this week. MANILA — Search and rescue operations are underway for the 13 Filipinos in the ill-fated MV Eternity C , which sank off the coast of Yemen after being attacked by Houthi militants earlier this week.

title: Iran says 'no negotiations' as US warns to accept 15-point deal
body: The ramped-up rhetoric dashed hopes of any imminent de-escalation as violence on the ground showed no sign of abating, with Iran, Israel, Lebanon, Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan and Saudi Arabia all coming under fire. Trump insisted later Wednesday that Iran was taking part in peace talks, but Tehran is denying it because their negotiators fear being killed by their own side. 'They are negotiating, by the way, and they want to make a deal so badly,' Trump told a dinner for Republican members of Congress. 'But they're afraid to say it, because they figure they'll be killed by their own people,' he said. 'They're also afraid they'll be killed by us.' Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi denied that Iran was negotiating with the United States, saying an exchange of messages through 'friendly countries' did not equate to talks with Washington. 'We do not intend to negotiate,' Araghchi told state TV. 'We seek an end to the war on our own terms.' In Pakistan, officials said Islamabad had conveyed to Tehran a 15-point American plan to stop the fighting that began on February 28. Iran' state-controlled Press TV cited an unidentified official as saying Tehran had 'responded negatively' to the plan and the war would end only on Tehran's terms, which include guarantees against future attacks. White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said talks had been 'productive' but declined to say whom the United States was dealing with in Tehran following the assassination of supreme leader Ali Khamenei. His son and successor Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen in public. 'If Iran fails to accept the reality of the current moment... Trump will ensure they are hit harder than they have ever been hit before,' Leavitt said. 'President Trump does not bluff and he is prepared to unleash hell.' With thousands more US troops reportedly headed to the Middle East, Iran also threatened to open a new front by targeting Red Sea shipping should the United States launch a ground invasion. Iran's military said cruise missiles fired at the USS Abraham Lincoln carrier group had 'forced it to change its position' and warned of 'powerful strikes' when the fleet comes into range. Admiral Brad Cooper, head of US Central Command, said the United States had hit two-thirds of Iran's production facilities for missiles and drones, and drone and missile launch rates were down by 90 percent. In a video on X, Cooper also estimated that 92 percent of the Iranian navy's largest vessels had been damaged or destroyed. 'They've now lost the ability to meaningfully project naval power and influence around the region and around the world,' he said. UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres said the war was 'out of control' and had 'gone too far.' According to The New York Times, the 15-point US plan touches on Iran's contested nuclear and missile programs as well as 'maritime routes.' Tehran has largely blocked the vital Strait of Hormuz oil route in retaliation for the US-Israeli attacks, pushing up global energy prices. The Iranian official quoted by Press TV said Tehran had put forward its own five conditions for hostilities to end. They include a robust mechanism guaranteeing that neither Israel nor the United States will resume the war, and compensation for war damages. Iran's conditions also include a cessation of hostilities on all regional fronts and against all 'resistance groups,' a reference to the Tehran-backed Lebanese group Hezbollah that has been under attack from Israel. Tehran also wants international recognition and guarantees of Iran's rights to exercise its sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz. In the event of a US ground invasion, Iran would also block the Bab al-Mandeb Strait, which connects the Indian Ocean to the Red Sea and the Suez Canal, an unidentified military official told local media. Iran supports and arms the Houthi rebel group in Yemen, which greatly reduced Red Sea traffic in October 2023 when it began attacking vessels in retaliation for Israel's bombardment of Gaza. While striking targets in Iran on Wednesday, Israel also kept up its campaign against Hezbollah in Lebanon, with planes pounding the southern suburbs of Beirut. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said his forces were 'expanding' a 'buffer zone' in Lebanon, while Hezbollah chief Naim Qassem said negotiating with Israel under fire would amount to 'surrender' for Lebanon. Lebanon was pulled into the war when Hezbollah began firing rockets into Israel on March 2 to avenge the killing of Ayatollah Khamenei. According to Lebanese authorities, more than 1,000 people have been killed and upwards of one million people displaced in over three weeks of Israeli strikes. With the war sending energy prices soaring , fueling fears of higher inflation and weaker global growth, markets remained focused on the Strait of Hormuz, through which one fifth of the world's oil usually passes. Araghchi said the strait was 'closed only to enemies,' adding that 'There is no reason to allow the ships of our enemies and their allies to pass.' Stock markets rallied and oil prices had tumbled on initial reports of potential negotiations, but on Wednesday the Brent crude benchmark crept back above $100 a barrel. US President Donald Trump is ready to 'unleash hell' if Iran does not accept a deal to end the nearly four-week Middle East war, the White House warned Wednesday, but a defiant Tehran said it did not intend to negotiate. US President Donald Trump is ready to 'unleash hell' if Iran does not accept a deal to end the nearly four-week Middle East war, the White House warned Wednesday, but a defiant Tehran said it did not intend to negotiate.

title: US woman rushed to hospital with shark stuck to arm
body: The small nurse shark, which was about two feet (61 centimeters) long, was killed by a beachgoer soon after the attack, the Palm Beach Post reported. The victim was an unidentified 23-year-old woman who was bitten while bathing at a beach in the coastal city of Boca Raton, city fire-rescue spokesman Bob Lemons told the newspaper. Lemons said the woman was rushed to the city's regional hospital with the shark still attached to her right forearm. 'I have never seen anything like it,' Ocean Rescue Captain Clint Tracy told the South Florida Sun-Sentinel. 'Never even heard of anything like this.' Nurse sharks, which can grow to up to 10 feet (three meters) in length, are common along Florida's Atlantic coastline, and are normally peaceful creatures. But one witness told the Sun-Sentinel that before the attack he saw swimmers bothering the shark and holding it by the tail. The victim was initially calm when she approached a lifeguard station at the Red Reef Park beach seeking help. A male companion was holding the shark and there was little blood, Tracy said. 'It was barely breathing but it wasn't letting go of her arm, like it was stuck to her or something,' witness Shlomo Jacob told the Sun-Sentinel. As time went by and a crowd gathered, the woman became agitated and when paramedics arrived they gave her oxygen. Then they used a board to support her arm and the shark, placed her on a stretcher and drove to the hospital, Tracy told the newspaper. According to the Shark Research Institute, nurse sharks are nocturnal creatures, and are often found lying motionless on the ocean floor in rocky coastal areas during the day. The sharks eat small fish and creatures like lobster, squid, and sea urchins -- not human flesh. Most grown nurse sharks are less than 9.8 feet (three meters) long, but are known to reach 14 feet (4.3 meters) in length. MIAMI - A woman in the US state of Florida was rushed to a hospital Sunday after being bitten by a shark -- with the whole animal still clutching on to her right arm, local media reported.

title: Group to pursue Christmas convoy to Ayungin shunned by security council
body: The Atin To coalition said it would go ahead with a Christmas civilian convoy mission for fisherfolk and frontliners in the West Philippine Sea. Around 40 boats will join the Dec. 5 civilian convoy mission from El Nido, Palawan to Ayungin Shoal, a flashpoint for maritime tensions between Manila and Beijing. The group will distribute in-kind provisions such as food, navigating tools, and other needs for fishermen. The coalition describes the mission as a 'bold but peaceful attempt' to assert sovereign rights in the face of harassment from Chinese aggressors. 'It's not an exaggeration when we say Filipinos are being harassed on a daily basis,' said Sen. Risa Hontiveros, who slammed China's continued aggression in the West Philippine Sea. 'Sa kabila ng diplomatikong protesta, patuloy na nagbibingi-bingihan ang Tsina. Hindi sya titigil hanggang di n'ya naaagaw ang teritoryo at katubigan na hindi kanila,' Hontiveros added. Akbayan president Rafaela David said that the Philippines should normalize civilian missions in the West Philippine Sea since it is part of its territory anyway. The coalition said it was coordinating with the Philippine Coast Guard and the National Security Agency for the mission. 'We should normalize our voyages to the West Philippine Sea, treating them with the same routine as travels to various regions in the country,' Akbayan Party president Rafaela David said. National Security Council Assistant Director Jonathan Malaya recently disapproved the proposal, as it could increase tension in the area. Atin Ito said that while it was 'taking note' of the official's statement, it was committed to the well-being of fisherfolk and frontliners. 'It is with optimism and confidence that we express our belief in the support of the government,' the coalition said in a statement. Meanwhile, the group will also stage a Christmas musical event at the UP Bahay ng Alumni on November 29, which aims to collate donations for the fishermen's needs that will be given through the civilian mission. The concert will feature volunteer artists such as Noel Cabangon, Ebe Dancel, Autotelic, and 6Cyclemind, among others. MANILA — The 'Atin To' coalition said Monday it would go ahead with a Christmas civilian convoy mission for fisherfolk and frontliners in the West Philippine Sea, despite discouragement from the National Security Council.

title: Philippines summons Chinese envoy over sea confrontations
body: The Philippines has summoned China's envoy, the foreign ministry said Monday, following the most tense confrontations between the countries' vessels in years at flashpoint reefs in the West Philippine Sea. Videos released by the Philippine Coast Guard showed Chinese ships blasting water cannon at Philippine boats during two separate resupply missions to fishermen at Scarborough Shoal and a tiny garrison at Second Thomas Shoal on Saturday and Sunday. There was also a collision between Philippine and Chinese boats at Second Thomas Shoal, where a handful of Filipino troops are stationed on a grounded warship, with both countries trading blame. Diplomatic protests had been filed and 'the Chinese ambassador has also been summoned', foreign ministry spokeswoman Teresita Daza told a news conference on Monday. Daza said declaring China's ambassador Huang Xilian as 'persona non grata' in the Philippines was also 'something that has to be seriously considered'. China's ramming and water cannoning of Filipino boats as well as the use of a long-range acoustic device was a 'serious escalation' of their tactics, Jonathan Malaya, assistant director general of the National Security Council, told reporters. The China Coast Guard, however, said one of the Philippine supply boats deliberately hit its vessel after 'disregarding our multiple stern warnings'. China claims almost the entire South China Sea, including waters and islands near the shores of its neighbors, and has ignored an international tribunal ruling that its assertions have no legal basis. It deploys boats to patrol the busy waterway and has built artificial islands that it has militarized to reinforce its claims. On Sunday, the US State Department called on China to halt its 'dangerous and destabilizing' actions in the sea, while foreign diplomats in Manila also criticized China's behavior. The confrontations at Scarborough Shoal and Second Thomas Shoal were the most intense between Philippine and Chinese vessels in years, analysts said, as the countries seek to assert their competing maritime territorial claims. 'I expect that this will become even more frequent and persistent,' said Jay Batongbacal, director of the University of the Philippines' Institute for Maritime Affairs and Law of the Sea. 'We should use the opportunity to build up our alliances and partnerships, and to prepare for the worst as it is clear that they will continue to deprive us of access to our rights and resources as a country,' Batongbacal told AFP. Song Zhongping, an analyst and former officer in the Chinese military, said US support for the Philippines was making Manila feel 'emboldened to provoke China', which was exacerbating tensions. 'If the Philippines clings to its course and feels that it can provoke China with the support of countries outside the region... then conflict or some other contingency could erupt at Ren'ai Reef or Huangyan Island,' Song said, using the Chinese names for Second Thomas Shoal and Scarborough Shoal. China seized Scarborough Shoal from the Philippines in 2012 following a tense standoff, while the Philippine Navy deliberately grounded a World War II-era navy ship on Second Thomas Shoal in 1999 to check China's advance in the waters. Relations between Manila and Beijing have deteriorated under President Ferdinand Marcos, who has sought to improve ties with traditional ally Washington and deepen defense ties in the region, while also pushing back against Chinese actions in the South China Sea. In a statement late Sunday, Marcos said the Philippines remained 'undeterred' following the latest incidents. 'No one but the Philippines has a legitimate right or legal basis to operate anywhere in the West Philippine Sea,' Marcos said, using Manila's term for the South China Sea waters to the immediate west of the Philippines. MANILA ( UPDATED ) -- The Philippines said it had summoned China's envoy on Monday and flagged the possibility of expelling him following the most tense confrontations between the countries' vessels in years at flashpoint reefs in the disputed South China Sea.

title: Harapan 2025: What led Vic Rodriguez to vie for a Senate seat in 2025
body: Speaking at Harapan 2025, Rodriguez said former President Rodrigo Duterte encouraged him to try his luck to enter the Senate this 2025 elections. 'Walang pinangko si Pangulong Duterte kung hindi ay susuportahan ako. At kakausapin niya ang kanyang mga supporters, 'yung kanyang core group na tulungan ako sa pangangampanya,' he shared. When he filed his certificate of candidacy in October 2024, the former Executive Secretary said the former President endorsed his senatorial bid. Rodriguez said he is running to fight what he claimed as 'very bad' corruption in government. He shared he spoke with the elder Duterte several times before the period of filing of certificate of candidacy. 'Initially sabi niya 'Vic tumakbo kang Senador'. (Sagot ko) 'Ako hindi ko hilig ito Mister President, mas magaling ako sa likod tutulong nalang ako'. I was expecting that he will be the one to run for Senator and I would go all the way for him,' Rodriguez said. 'Ako ang tumugon sa hamon na pamunuan ang tunay at maisug na opposition,' he added. Reports of the elder Duterte's possible senatorial bid spread during the COC filing period. The former President filed his COC for Davao City mayor with his son Sebastian 'Baste' Duterte as his runningmate. Rodriguez was a known ally of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr., even serving as his Spokesperson during the 2022 campaign and Executive Secretary early in the presidency. However, he stepped down from his post and exited the cabinet, resulting in a falling out with Marcos Jr. Rodriguez lamented what happened between him and the President, pointing to the individuals who entered the picture after the elections. 'Maraming sumama nalang noong panalo na kami sa kampanya. Gusto nila na business as usual… This is not the kind of leadership and government we agreed to have 'pag siya ay pinalad at tangkiliking maging Pangulo,' he said. Rodriguez said the fallout with Marcos Jr. was a painful experience. 'Masakit talaga 'yun dahil tinuri mong kaibigan eh. Inakay mo, magkasama kayo doon sa isang bahagi ng buhay niyang napakadilim. Hanggang saan sa dahan-dahan nakita namin pasikat na yung araw, kami magkasama. At nung dumating sa puntong mababang-mababa ang aking buhay, nanay ko 'yun eh. Ni ho ni ha wala kang narinig, masakit yun eh,' Rodriguez said. 'Hindi ko hahangarin na mangyari sa kanya o kung kanino man 'yung eksperyensya ko na 'yun o yung nangyari na 'yun,' he added. Senatorial aspirant Vic Rodriguez shared the backstory of what led him to run in the 2025 national and local elections. MANILA – Senatorial aspirant Vic Rodriguez shared the backstory of what led him to run in the 2025 national and local elections.

title: AFP chief tells Army troops: Be prepared to secure Metro Manila
body: General Romeo Brawner Jr. said the AFP is counting on the 2ID to support the seat of government and maintain the country’s stability. 'One of the most important tasks of the 2ID is to support the seat of government—Metro Manila... Thus, your spirit of unity and professionalism is what we at the GHQs are counting on,' Brawner said during the 2ID's 49th anniversary celebration at Camp General Mateo Capinpin in Tanay. Brawner explained this was because of the division's proximity to Metro Manila. 'Because of the proximity of the 2ID to Metro Manila, ito ang isa sa divisions na kapag nagkaroon ng giyera, for example, or destabilization, itong 2ID ang isa sa mga tututok o reresponde sa threats na ito. Nakapalibot ito sa Metro Manila,' Brawner said in a chance interview. Brawner also emphasized that the military remains united and professional, and focused on the challenges the country is facing. 'It’s not just a reminder, pero ‘yan ang panawagan natin sa lahat ng mga sundalo na dapat tayo ay united and we are always professional.
