But government forces withdrew from their positions with no major resistance in some areas, according to the Observatory. The rebels swiftly took control of several strategic cities within days, starting from second city Aleppo, then central Hama and Homs before marching on to the capital. Thirteen years of civil war have exhausted Syria's military equipment and army, which has lost around half of its personnel, estimated at 300,000 before 2011. In recent days, analysts have emphasised the military's weakness on the battlefield, without effective Russian or Iranian support. A source close to Lebanon's Hezbollah told AFP the Iran-backed group had withdrawn from positions in the vicinity of Damascus and in a border area with Lebanon, where it had bases and depots. READ: 'Waiting a long time for this': Damascus wakes up in rebel hands Syrian Prime Minister Mohammed al-Jalali, who took up the post in September, said in a message broadcast on his Facebook page that he was ready to 'cooperate' with the leadership chosen by the Syrian people and for any handover procedures. The rebel factions announced a 'new era' in Syria. The leader of Syrian rebel group Hayat Tahrir al-Sham, Abu Mohammed al-Jolani, ordered forces Sunday not to approach official institutions in Damascus, saying they would remain under the prime minister until they are officially handed over. In the statement on Telegram, Jolani used his real name Ahmed al-Sharaa instead of his more well-known nom de guerre. With the army's collapse in areas it controlled, including around critical facilities, the rebels and other armed groups who control swathes of the country face major challenges. Mohanad Hage Ali from the Carnegie Middle East Center said on X, that 'the main challenge today lies in re-building the Syrian state and shifting from the chaos and fragmentation phase.' 'Until now, the factions have shown awareness in dealing with minorities and prisoners,' he said, amid hope this could help translate 'into the reconstruction of state institutions'. MANILA -- The Department of Foreign Affairs issued Sunday a statement regarding the ongoing conflict in Syria. The DFA addressed the involved parties to 'exercise restraint' so as to avoid additional deaths in the fighting. 'The Philippines calls on all concerned parties to exercise restraint and refrain from further violence, to avert further casualties and deaths of civilians,' DFA said. The agency also called on the Filipinos in Syria and advised them to 'take the necessary precautions and stay in contact with the Philippine Embassy in Damascus.' So far, there have been no reports of Filipinos caught in the crossfire or efforts of repatriation, as of Sunday (December 8, 2024). The chaos in Syria ensued around two weeks ago when the Islamist Hayat Tahrir al-Sham (HTS) group launched its campaign challenging the more than five decades of rule by the Assad family. Earlier, the HTS also declared Damascus 'free' as it reportedly drove away 'tyrant' President Bashar al-Assad. Rebels on Syrian state TV say toppled 'tyrant' Bashar al-Assad The Department of Foreign Affairs issued Sunday a statement regarding the ongoing disorder in Syria. Lebanese people celebrate the taking over of the capital Damascus by Syrian rebel fighters in the northern Lebanese city of Tripoli on December 8, 2024. Fathi Al-Masri, AFP DAMASCUS, Syria -- Syria's capital woke up Sunday to chanting, cheering and gunfire in celebration of the fall of President Bashar al-Assad, after rebels said they entered the city and toppled the longtime ruler. 'I can't believe I'm living this moment,' tearful Damascus resident Amer Batha told AFP by phone from the capital's Ummayad Square, where witnesses said dozens of people had gathered to celebrate. 'We've been waiting a long time for this day,' said Batha, as Islamist-led rebels and a war monitor declared the end of decades of Assad family rule amid 13 years of grinding civil war. 'We are starting a new history for Syria,' Batha added. At the dawn call to prayer, some mosques were broadcasting religious chants usually reserved for festive occasions, while also urging residents to stay home with the city engulfed in uncertainty just hours into the rebels' takeover. Rebel group Hayat Tahrir al-Sham and allied factions have pressed a lightning offensive since November 27, sweeping swathes of the country from government control, including major cities Aleppo, Hama and Homs and entering the capital Damascus early Sunday. In extraordinary images, rebel fighters announced on state television that they had toppled 'tyrant' Assad, who war monitor the Syrian Observatory for Human Rights said had 'fled'. From the garden of a shopping centre, dozens of Damascenes were celebrating, chanting 'Allahu akbar', or God is greatest, and standing on a smashed statue of Assad's father Hafez that they had torn down, AFP footage showed. Gunmen spread out through some Damascus streets, shooting into the air and chanting, 'Syria is ours and not the Assad family's.' Local residents told AFP that dozens of soldiers, from the Assad government's forces, had hastily taken off their military uniforms and left the Ummayad Square headquarters. State television and radio buildings were empty, a former employee said. Five strong explosions were heard in the capital early Sunday, with a fleeing soldier, requesting anonymity, saying it was probably artillery or blasts from a munitions depot. 'Our direct superior told us to leave and go home, so we knew it was over,' he told AFP. In Damascus's picturesque Old City, home to a small Christian community, young people in the streets were chanting 'Syrian people are one', a message of reassurance to minority groups in the multi-confessional country. Elsewhere, in the Shaghur neighbourhood, women ululated from balconies, some throwing rice on passing fighters who were shooting into the air. Ilham Basatina, 50, said she couldn't believe that 'after today', she would not longer have to 'be afraid'. 'There is huge happiness today, and it won't be complete until the criminal has been held to account,' she said from her balcony, referring to Assad. READ: Syrian government loses Aleppo after lightning rebel offensive: monitor In the street, fighters in fatigues were kissing the ground, praying or taking photos as gunfire rang out. Many Syrian media workers, government employees and members of parliament quickly changed their profile pictures on social media, replacing them with the opposition flag. Waddah Abd Rabbo, editor in chief of pro-government online daily Al-Watan, wrote on social media: 'Syrian media and media workers are not guilty. They, and we with them, were only carrying out instructions and publishing the news they sent us.' Assad's Baath party ruled with an iron fist, heavily curtailing freedoms including freedom of press, with only state media or outlets close to the government permitted to operate. Famous Syrian actor Ayman Zeidan, who hadn't publicly criticised the Assad rule before, wrote on Facebook: 'How delusional I was. Perhaps we were prisoners of a culture of fear, or maybe we feared change because we imagined it would lead to blood and chaos.' 'But here we are, entering a new phase with men who impressed us with their nobility... and the desire to restore the unity of the Syrian people,' he added. Syria's capital woke up Sunday to chanting, cheering and gunfire in celebration of the fall of President Bashar al-Assad, after rebels said they entered the city and toppled the longtime ruler. The UN special envoy for Syria called Tuesday on Israel to halt its military movements and bombardments in Syria, after a war monitor reported 300 air strikes since the fall of president Bashar al-Assad. DAMASCUS, Syria-- The UN special envoy for Syria called Tuesday on Israel to halt its military movements and bombardments in Syria, after a war monitor reported 300 air strikes since the fall of president Bashar al-Assad.

title: U.S. Indo-Pacific strategy centered on partnerships, not militarization — experts
body: The forum, part of the group’s 'The New West Philippine Sea CATCH' series, brought together security analysts to discuss evolving geopolitical dynamics and the role of cooperation in maintaining regional stability. According to Dr. Celia Lamkin, the initiative aims to engage the youth and the public in deeper conversations about the West Philippine Sea and broader Indo-Pacific issues. Maritime security expert Dr. Benjamin Blandin said the U.S. 'pivot' to Asia has often been misinterpreted as a military buildup. 'A lot of people misunderstand what the pivot was about. It was never simply a military comeback. It was about empowering partners, supporting their own strategic autonomy, and helping them manage their own affairs,' Blandin said. He explained that the U.S. has, in fact, reduced its permanent troop presence in parts of Asia since the Cold War, shifting instead to rotational deployments, infrastructure access, and joint cooperation with allies. The discussion also highlighted the Enhanced Defense Cooperation Agreement (EDCA), which allows U.S. forces rotational access to selected Philippine military facilities but does not permit permanent bases. Retired U.S. Navy officer Capt. Carl O. Schuster said the Indo-Pacific pivot was largely about reallocating resources. 'We weren’t moving new forces in. The reality was a pivot in resources and strategic focus, not simply a repositioning of troops,' he said. He added that perceived increases in U.S. presence were partly due to reductions in other regions. Meanwhile, Dr. Bhushan Dewan said the strategy counters the notion that the U.S. is trying to encircle its rivals. 'It is a wrong impression… Instead of direct presence, America would like to empower partners to take on responsibilities with U.S. support,' Dewan said. He warned that without such an approach, the United States could face strategic overstretch in addressing global security challenges. For his part, Col. Nalin Herath emphasized the need to balance defense posture with economic growth and trade engagement, especially amid China’s growing influence in the region. The panel concluded that the Indo-Pacific strategy reflects a long-term, partnership-driven approach aimed at strengthening regional resilience, rather than direct militarization. Organizers said the forum is part of continuing efforts to raise awareness among Filipino youth on key issues affecting the West Philippine Sea and regional security. The United States’ strategy in the Indo-Pacific is focused on strengthening alliances and empowering regional partners, rather than expanding military presence, experts said during a youth forum organized by the National Youth Movement for the West Philippine Sea.

title: Isko declares garbage crisis in Manila
body: Domagoso noted that the capital produces over 2,000 metric tons of garbage daily. He said delays in collection over recent days have led to mounting waste in streets, waterways, and residential areas. 'Masakit sa mata, masakit sa ilong, mapanakit sa kalusugan ang ating sitwasyon patungkol sa basura,' he said on Facebook Live. 'Pumanatag kayo, may gobyerno na po sa Maynila,' he told residents. 'We will not point fingers. We will act on the matter. We will confront this problem head-on.' He said he had tapped Manila's previous waste collector Leonel Waste Management Corp. to return and begin hauling garbage across Manila without charge. The mayor also ordered the city's Department of Public Services (DPS), Department of Engineering and Public Works (DEPW), and Manila Traffic and Parking Bureau (MTPB) to join the effort. He told the city's over 800 barangay officials to lead cleanup operations in their communities. 'Talikuran niyo na ang pulitika. Panahon na para tugunan niyo ang sinumpaan niyong tungkulin sa mamamayan, hindi sa pulitiko,' he said. The returning mayor asked residents to wait for the streets to be cleaned up before dumping their waste. 'Kung kinakailangan ipa-delay nang konti... iloob niyo muna sa inyong tahanan ang inyong basura habang kinokolekta ang basura sa kalsada, until we normalize the collection of garbage,' he said. Leonel halted operations in 2024 due to the previous administration's alleged failure to pay eight months worth of services amounting to P560 million. In January 2025, then Manila mayor Honey Lacuna said the city was already processing Leonel’s payment but this could be jeopardized 'as it abandoned its duty to collect the city’s waste.' Leonel denied the allegations. To replace Leonel, the city contracted MetroWaste Solid Waste Management Corporation and Phil. Ecology Systems Corp. But Domagoso said both companies also struggled to keep operations running after experiencing severe delays in payment, receiving compensation only in January 2025. Lacuna has yet to respond to ABS-CBN News' request for comment. On his first day back as Manila's mayor, Francisco 'Isko' Moreno Domagoso declared on Monday a garbage crisis. MANILA — On his first day back as Manila's mayor, Francisco 'Isko' Moreno Domagoso declared on Monday a garbage crisis due to alleged issues on payment for waste collectors. On his first day back as Manila's mayor, Francisco 'Isko' Moreno Domagoso declared on Monday a garbage crisis.

title: Sara Duterte refuses to confirm if she will answer impeachment cases
body: In a chance interview in Digos City, Davao del Sur, Duterte was asked of her plans to file an answer on or before the Monday deadline. 'Antayin nalang natin kung mag-submit ako o hindi. Ngayon ang deadline sa pagkakaalam ko,' Duterte said. Duterte was given 10 days to answer the impeachment cases against her. Bicol Saro Party-List Rep. Terry Ridon, a member of the justice panel and among the vice president's vocal critics, said failure to answer could speed up proceedings. Those proceedings will include a hearing for probable cause to determine if the complaints will be sent to the House floor for voting. At least a third of the House must vote in favor of impeachment to send the case to the Senate for trial. Meanwhile, Duterte said some members of the House of Representatives were abusing their power. This comes after Bicol Saro Representative Terry Ridon's statement they may issue a subpoena for Duterte's tax and business records. 'Kausapin muna nila ng maayos 'yung mga abogado nila dahil sa nakikita natin ngayon ay 'yung mga iilan sa mga members of House of Representatives ay inaabuso ‘yung kanilang posisyon at kapangyarihan,' Duterte said. 'More than that, ay lumalabas na para silang mga law benders,' she added. President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. and Vice President Sara Duterte. PCO/Maria Tan, ABS-CBN News MANILA ( UPDATED ) — Less than half of Filipino adults expressed appreciation for the work done by President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. in the past three months, while approval is the predominant sentiment regarding Vice President Sara Duterte’s quarterly performance, according to a Pulse Asia survey released Monday. Out of 1,200 adults polled by Pulse Asia from Feb. 27 to March 2, 36 percent approved of Marcos’ work, while 55 percent appreciated Duterte’s performance. 'Across geographic and socioeconomic subgroupings, the only majority approval score of the President is recorded in the rest of Luzon (54%). In contrast, disapproval for the latter’s work prevails in the Visayas (61%), Mindanao (73%), and Class E (60%),' the pollster noted. 'Almost half of those belonging to Class D (47%) also have a negative assessment of presidential performance. While the same approval and disapproval ratings are posted by the President in Metro Manila (both at 42%), a three-way divide may be observed in Class ABC (41% approval, 29% indecision, and 30% disapproval),' it added. Meanwhile, Duterte 'enjoys small to big majority approval ratings in the Visayas (72%), Mindanao (95%), Class D (57%), and Class E (81%),' Pulse Asia said. It said half of Metro Manilans are appreciative of Duterte’s work while a big plurality of those in the rest of Luzon disapproved of her performance. 'Practically the same approval and disapproval figures are recorded in Class ABC (36% versus 44%),' it said. 'Lagi akong nagpapasalamat sa ating mga kababayan sa patuloy nila na suporta at pagtitiwala sa akin, sa aking mga kasamahan sa Office of the Vice President,' Duterte said of her approval ratings. 'Wala kasi akong macomment sa mga survey dahil hindi ko alam kung paano siya ginawa. Sino ang nagpakumisyon noong survey,' Duterte also said. 'Pero ako, may survey man o wala, nagpapasalamat dahil ‘yung pagtitiwala at paniniwala ng taumbayan sa akin ay nandyan pa rin,' she added. 'At lagi ko sinasabi tulad kanina, sabi ko, ‘yung power ko kasi o ang gahum nako ay galing iyon sa tao.' Forty-four percent of respondents said they distrusted Marcos, while 35 percent said they trusted him and 21 percent were unable to express their sentiment. In Duterte’s case, most adults or 54 percent trusted her, while 26 percent distrusted her and 20 percent were ambivalent. The need to control inflation continued to be the leading urgent national concern of Filipinos at 59 percent, Pulse Asia said. Inflation is followed by graft and corruption in government at 47 percent and workers’ pay 36 percent. Corruption is the leading third-mentioned response at 19 percent. The national administration scored its only majority approval rating for its work in protecting the welfare of overseas Filipino workers at 53 percent. In contrast, small to big majorities were critical of administration efforts to reduce poverty at 53 percent; fight corruption in government, 67 percent; deal with the problem of illegal drugs, 68 percent; and control inflation, 73 percent. Around the time that the survey was conducted, the House of Representatives found two impeachment complaints against the Vice President 'sufficient in substance', while her father, former president Rodrigo Duterte faced a confirmation of charges hearing at the International Criminal Court. Other developments around the time of the survey included anti-corruption protests and the surge in oil prices due to the war in the Middle East. The survey had a ± 2.8 percent error margin at the 95-percent confidence level. -- with a report from Jervis Manahan, ABS-CBN News DAVAO CITY - Vice President Sara Duterte told the House of Representatives to wait for her response or whether she would answer the two impeachment complaints filed against her, which is expected to be submitted by Monday. 'Malapit naman ang Monday no? Hintayin natin kung magfa-file ako o hindi. Hintay na lang sila na mga taga-House of Representatives,' she said in a media interview in Davao City. She also said it was her first time hearing that a respondent’s choice of lawyers was questioned. House Committee on Justice member and Bicol Saro Party-list Representative Terry Ridon said that in the next hearing of the House Committee on Justice scheduled for Wednesday, March 18, they will discuss a potential conflict of interest involving two of Duterte’s lawyers—former Education Assistant Secretary for Procurement Reynold Munsayac and former Department of Education (DepEd) spokesperson Michael Poa. House panel: Sara Duterte impeachment complaints ‘sufficient in substance’ Duterte did not directly answer the issue on the possible conflict of interest involving her lawyers. 'Ngayon ko lang narinig yung respondent ay hindi pinapayagan kung sino ang kanyang lawyers. Ngayon lang ako nakarinig ng circumstance na hindi pinapayagan ang respondent kung sino ang pinipili niya na lawyers. Hintayin na lang natin kung ano ang mangyayari,' she said. When asked whether the impeachment issue may involve another 'maleta,' which was first referred to as luggage allegedly containing money used as bribes as claimed by 18 former Marines against some politicians, Duterte said to wait and see if new photos would surface. 'Antayin natin tapos kunan natin ng pictures tapos ilabas na naman natin ang pictures tapos ilabas na naman natin yung pictures kung mayroong bagong maletas na lalabas,' she said. VP Duterte's response to impeachment complaints expected by Monday, says Ridon Amid the hurdles she has faced since the start of her vice presidency, Duterte said she would not bear a grudge or take the issues personally but would not spare their illegal acts committed against the country, such as corruption and abuse. 'Yung ginawa nila sa akin, personal yan sa akin. So bilang isang politician, natural na ganoon ang gagawin sa iyo. So wala lang yan sa akin. Pero ang ginawa nila sa bayan, yung korapsyon at yung abuso nila, hindi yan personal sa akin. So hindi natin yan palalampasin,' she said. Meanwhile, more than two years before the 2028 national elections, Duterte said the potential running mate she earlier revealed she had recruited has yet to decide whether to run. 'Meron akong ni-recruit last year, but hindi pa siya nag-decide kung tatakbo siya for vice president or hindi. Hintayin na lang natin yung desisyon niya. Huwag na lang natin siyang guluhin. Huwag na natin siyang pangalanan baka atakehin siya, sisirain yung pangalan niya. So pabayaan natin siyang magdesisyon,' she said. Duterte also said there are about four people who intend to run for senator in the next polls who have asked to be included in her lineup. Meanwhile, Duterte and former President and Pampanga Representative Gloria Macapagal-Arroyo graced the signing of a coffee table book marking the 90th anniversary of the Office of the Vice President (OVP) in Davao City on Saturday afternoon. Sara Duterte slams ‘political’ impeachment bid, says she’s ready to clear name The two also toured a pop-up exhibit featuring all the vice presidents of the Philippines, highlighting their personal lives, key programs, and achievements. Duterte and Arroyo also personally signed copies of the OVP’s coffee table book for over 100 participants at the event. In her speech, Duterte said Arroyo’s attendance was not merely ceremonial but a testament to her legacy, noting that Arroyo and her father, former President Diosdado Macapagal, are the only father-and-daughter pair who both served as vice president and president. 'Former President Arroyo is here who is also a former vice president and she is the only person in this room who has held both posts. And I learned from the pop-up exhibit, your father and you are the only two individuals in our country that held both the VP and president positions,' she said. Duterte also joked that the book and exhibit feature permanent records of the country’s 14 vice presidents, excluding herself. '14 lang kasi hindi pa tapos yung pinaka-15. Hindi natin alam kung ma-impeach siya o hindi. Hindi pa tapos ang kwento niya,' she said. Arroyo, meanwhile, spoke about the hurdles that leaders face, including impeachment, noting that she herself went through four impeachment proceedings. 'In a democracy governed by the rule of law, such mechanisms should neither be personal nor political theater. It is important that those in charge conduct the proceedings with sobriety, fairness, and fidelity to the law, free from prejudice, and mindful that institutions must be stronger than momentary passions,' she said in her speech. Speaking about the impeachment cases against Duterte, Arroyo relayed that House Speaker Faustino 'Bojie' Dy said the hearings would be handled fairly, as she addressed her colleagues in Congress. 'The eyes of the Filipino nation are upon us in Congress. Thus, as we start our floor debates, I urge my fellow congressmen to do so in a spirit described by Speaker Bojie Dy,' she said. Vice President Sara Duterte has told the House of Representatives to wait for her response or whether she would answer the two impeachment complaints filed against her, which is expected to be submitted by Monday. Vice President Sara Duterte speaks to the media, November 25, 2024. Maria Tan, ABS-CBN News/File MANILA — The 10-day period that the House Committee on Justice gave Vice President Sara Duterte to answer the impeachment complaints against her ends on Monday with lawmakers and the public so far left to guess whether she will file one. Her legal team — 16 lawyers, majority of whom are from the law firm of Fortun, Narvasa and Salazar — has already submitted their Appearance Ad Cautelam (with caution), a precautionary measure that does not necessarily acknowledge the panel's jurisdiction. 'Malapit naman ang Monday 'no? Hintayin natin kung magfa-file ako o hindi. Hintay na lang sila,' she said of the House in an interview in Davao City on Saturday. (It's almost Monday. Let's just wait to see if I will one or not. They can just wait.) Sara Duterte tells House to wait for response to impeachment complaints The committee, which served her notice on March 5, said if she does not file a verified answer, she will have waived her right to do so 'and shall be considered to have interposed a general denial to the allegations contained in the Impeachment Complaints.' Bicol Saro Party-List Rep. Terry Ridon, a member of the justice panel and among the vice president's vocal critics, said failure to answer could speed up proceedings . Those proceedings will include a hearing form probable cause to determine if the complaints will be sent to the House floor for voting. At least a third of the House must vote in favor of impeachment to send the case to the Senate for trial. WHAT ARE THE ALLEGATIONS AGAINST DUTERTE? The committee found two of three complaints against Duterte — one filed by Fr. Joel Saballa and other members of the clergy, and another filed by lawyer Nathaniel Cabrera — sufficient in substance. That means the allegations, if true, constitute the impeachable offenses of culpable violation of the Constitution, betrayal of public trust, graft and corruption, and other high crimes. Among the grounds cited are the alleged misuse of confidential funds, alleged bribery of officials from the Department of Education, alleged unexplained wealth and material non-disclosure in her Statement of Assets, Liabilities and Net Worth, and her allegedly contracting someone to kill President Ferdinand Marcos Jr., First Lady Liza Araneta Marcos, and Leyte Rep. Martin Romualdez — the former House speaker. The allegations are the same that led to Duterte's impeachment in 2025. The Supreme Court has since ruled that those proceedings were unconstitutional since complaints filed earlier were already deemed initiated. Relief from House 'abuse' or court 'overreach?' SC closes case on 2025 VP Sara impeachment Another complaint, filed by activists and endorsed by lawmakers of the Makabayan bloc, was dismissed after the committee decided it was filed before February 6 — when the one-year ban on fresh filing complaints against Duterte lapsed. Duterte has long denied the allegations and has tied them to the 2028 elections, when, she said in February, she will run for president. VP Duterte says 4 politicians have asked to run for senator in her 2028 slate In preparation for the potential impeachment proceedings, Akbayan Party-List Rep. Chel Diokno for the House to take custody of Ramil Madriaga , an alleged kidnapper in detention in Taguig City and who claims to have been Duterte's bagman. Diokno has also asked the justice committee to issue subpoenas for documents, including Duterte's SALNs, that he said will be needed in the hearings. Duterte has called the request for the House to take custody of Madriaga special treatment for an alleged kidnapper and ' a grave insult to the minor victim who was traumatized by his criminal acts .' She also said that the move to subpoena her records shows the complaints are baseless. She said the subpoenas would be an attempt to 'search for evidence to support accusations already made in the impeachment complaints.' Diokno, in response, said his request is meant to preserve documentary evidence and to make sure Madriaga is safe 'so the Committee on Justice can fulfill its duty of assessing the evidence.' Ridon also rejected the allegation of a 'fishing expedition', saying the committee can proceed even if it does not issue subpoenas for the documents. 'It is essentially complete,' he said of the case. Duterte this week also questioned the propriety of holding the proceedings while the country faces a fuel crisis brought by the US and Israel's war against Iran that has seen pump prices surge. She called it a misplaced priority and 'another insult' to migrant Filipino workers, the poor, and to families 'who may soon be pushed deeper into hardship as a result of war and its global consequences.' Ridon countered that the House can address those concerns — approval of proposed legislation for emergency powers to suspend the excise tax on fuel is also expected on Monday — alongside impeachment. — from reports by Harlene Delgado, Vivenne Gulla, Jervis Manahan, Job Manalo, Jamaine Punzalan, Hernel Tocmo, ABS-CBN News The 10-day period that the House Committee on Justice gave Vice President Sara Duterte to answer the impeachment complaints against her ends on Monday with lawmakers and the public so far left to guess whether she will file one. Vice President Sara Duterte announces her intention to run for president during a press conference at the Office of the Vice President (OVP) headquarters in Mandaluyong City on Wednesday, February 18, 2026. Maria Tan, ABS-CBN News MANILA ( UPDATED ) — The House justice committee voted Wednesday that two impeachment complaints against Vice President Sara Duterte were 'sufficient in substance' to move forward. The panel ordered the issuance of a notice for Duterte to file her answer to the complaints within 10 days. Duterte, who recently announced her candidacy for the 2028 presidential election , was impeached by the House of Representatives last year only to see the Supreme Court toss the case out over procedural issues. VP Duterte's planned presidential run 'irrelevant' in impeachment proceedings: Ridon The revived impeachment bid against her leans heavily on allegations that she misused public funds while in office. Duterte also stands accused of making a death threat against her former ally and current President Ferdinand Marcos, with whom she is engaged in an explosive political feud. Fifty-four House panel members voted to declare the complaints 'sufficient in substance'. Only Quezon City Fourth District Representative Bong Suntay voted against it , and no one abstained. 'Our vote today is not a verdict of guilt nor an act of condemnation. It's simply a decision on whether the constitutional process should move forward,' Rep. Ferdinand Hernandez said minutes before the vote. One complaint was filed by members of the clergy and endorsed by Rep. Leila De Lima, while the second was filed by lawyer Nathaniel Cabrera and endorsed by Reps. Paolo Ortega and Bienvenido Abante Jr. At least one-third of the House must vote in favor of impeachment for the case to move to the Senate. A guilty verdict in the Senate trial would result in Duterte being barred from politics and sidelined from the 2028 presidential race. 'If she doesn’t file an answer, that is considered as a denial of all the allegations contained in the complaint. The Justice Committee will simply proceed to hearing the complaints,' House Committee on Justice Chairperson Gerville Luistro said. If Duterte files an answer, the complainants will be given a opportunity to file a reply within three days from receipt of the answer. The House panel tentatively scheduled its next hearing on April 8th to determine whether based on the pleadings, there is sufficient ground to proceed to the hearing proper. Analysts have warned that Duterte's presidential announcement will weigh heavily on lawmakers forced to gauge the repercussions of a vote against someone who may yet hold the country's highest office. President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. will not interfere in the impeachment proceedings against Duterte, Malacañang said. 'Hindi po makikialam ang Pangulo kung ano po ang mangyayari at magaganap sa hearing sa impeachment complaint laban kay Bise Presidente,' Palace Press Officer Undersecretary Claire Castro said. 'Ang tanging nais lamang po niya ay masunod ang proseso, masunod ang Konstitusyon at makita kung anuman ang ebidensiya at kung may dapat na managot, managot – hanggang doon lamang po,' she said in a press briefing. The Palace official noted that she and Marcos Jr. have yet to talk about the impeachment, saying that they were focused on issues concerning the growing Middle East conflict. The same committee hearing the case against Duterte last month tossed out a pair of impeachment complaints against Marcos, ruling that allegations of corruption over a scandal involving bogus flood control projects lacked substance. DUTERTE TEAM TO 'CAREFULLY REVIEW' PROCEEDINGS Michael Wesley Poa, spokesman for Duterte's defense team, said they 'have been informed of the vote of the House of Representatives finding the 3rd and 4th impeachment complaints sufficient in form and substance.' 'At this stage, the Vice President’s legal team will carefully review the actions taken by the House and the Committee, as well as the records of the proceedings,' he said in a statement. Duterte earlier in the day said she had not been monitoring the proceedings , which she said were not a priority of her office. 'For now, we will refrain from discussing the substance of the case in the media and will instead address these matters through the proper constitutional processes,' Poa said. Duterte refused to answer questions if she was willing to attend the proceedings if invited by the House. In a statement, Davao City 1st District Representative Paolo Duterte said the decision of the House panel on the sufficiency of the two complaints against his sister did not come as a surprise, adding it shows 'certain forces in Congress are willing to weaponize impeachment for political ends rather than uphold fairness and justice.' 'To the 54 others who voted in favor of pushing these complaints forward, remember this: political power is temporary, but accountability to the Filipino people is permanent. You may have the numbers today, but history has a way of judging those who abuse their authority,' he said. 'There will be a time when the Filipino people will render their verdict. And when that day comes, everyone should be ready to stand by their actions and answer for it,' Rep. Duterte added. Before the voting on sufficiency in substance, House members debated on the statement facts to support the allegation that the Vice President amassed unexplained wealth. Bicol Saro Party List Representative Terry Ridon showed Duterte's supposed SALNs, which showed that her net worth increased from more than P18.4 million in 2008 to P77.5 million in 2023 and P88.5 million in 2024. 'We leave it to the Justice Committee as to whether or not we would consider it as a case of unexplained wealth,' Ridon said. 'Has that been authenticated by the Ombudsman? We cannot inject anything not found in the complaint,' Cagayan de Oro City 2nd District Representative Rufus Rodriguez asked. 'It is public record, Madam Chair. I am speaking as a House member and participating in the House committee on Justice. The point is not on point,' Ridon replied. 'That might not be introduced as evidence yet, but that might be introduced as part of the manifestation of the honorable Ridon… All your manifestations… will become the basis of the judgment of the justice members in determining the sufficiency in substance,' Luistro said. Some lawmakers also questioned the credibility of Ramil Madriaga, who claimed to be Duterte's former bagman. READ: Sara Duterte files perjury complaint vs Madriaga 'Nasaan ba si Madriaga? 'Di ba nakakulong yata? So we are relying on an affidavit of an individual currently in prison? Sino ba si Madriaga? Mayroon ba siyang appointment kay VP Sara to prove that he is an employee?' Suntay asked. 'Are we talking now here about the credibility of a prospective witness in these proceedings? We're not yet at the evidentiary stage. We're not yet supposed to judge whether or not Mr. Madriaga is credible. It is premature and it tries to divert the matter now,' De Lima replied. ''Yung confidential agents, which is basically I think who Madriaga is… are treated actually as employees of offices that implement confidential funds? I don't think so,' Ridon added. In an interview after the meeting, De Lima questioned the timing of the Vice President's filing of a perjury complaint against Madriaga on Wednesday. 'Bakit dati, hindi nila ginagawa 'yan? Noong November pa lumabas 'yung sworn affidavit ni Ramil Madriaga, and then bakit ngayon lang? Tina-timing na nila. Alam na nila na magbobotohan na ngayon. So maybe, they are hoping na more or less ma-dissuade o maiimpluwensiyahan, mag-aalinlangan bang bumoto in favor of the declaration of the sufficiency in substance ngayong araw na nangyari na nga, buti na lang walang pumansin,' De Lima said. The House justice committee voted Wednesday that two impeachment complaints against Vice President Sara Duterte were 'sufficient in substance' to move forward. MANILA - Vice President Sara Duterte’s statement criticizing a motion for the House Committee on Justice to take custody of Ramil Madriaga 'suggests fear' for his testimony, according to House Committee on Justice member and Manila 3rd District Rep. Joel Chua. He issued the statement after Duterte tagged the request as giving Madriaga 'special treatment' and 'a grave insult to the minor victim who was traumatized by his criminal acts'. 'So, takot pala talaga siya sa mga nalalaman ni Madriaga. Such statements may ultimately aid the House when it performs its constitutional duty of presenting the Articles of Impeachment before the Senate,' Chua said. 'The tone and substance of the Vice President’s statement suggest fear rather than truth. It appears directed primarily at her political base in an effort to shape public opinion through misleading narratives,' he added. Madriaga claimed to be Duterte’s alleged former bagman, but the Vice President has denied this, calling him 'an alleged kidnapper who fabricated an entire story'. Chua challenged Duterte to engage the impeachment proceedings against her 'with clarity and respect rather than attempt to discredit it' if she truly believes in due process. 'The House will not be deterred from performing its constitutional duty,' the lawmaker said. Akbayan Party List Representative Chel Diokno, who requested the House panel to transfer Madriaga to its custody, meanwhile, stands by his motion to preserve documentary evidence and secure the witness in the impeachment proceedings against Duterte. 'I respect the Vice President’s opinion about the proceedings. However, my motion is clear: to preserve the documentary evidence and secure a witness so the Committee on Justice can fulfill its duty of assessing the evidence,' he said in a statement. 'The Vice President has every opportunity to refute the allegations against her by submitting her answer and evidence. Sa huli, ang ebidensya ang magsasabi kung dapat may pananagutan. At ang impeachment proceeding ang tamang lugar para lumabas ang katotohanan,' Diokno added. For Bicol Saro Party List Representative Terry Ridon, Duterte’s statement appears to be 'conflicting' with the statement of her lawyer, Atty. Paolo Panelo. On Thursday, Panelo reiterated that their camp wants Madriaga 'alive and well' so he can testify, and if transferring his custody to the House of Representatives ensures that, they 'welcome the move'. 'Parang conflicting ho ‘yung pahayag ng ating pangalawang Pangulo doon po sa pahayag ng kanya pong abogado doon po sa perjury case against Mr. Madriaga… Tingin ko po ay nagbabangga po ito,' he said. Ridon and House Committee on Justice Chairperson Gerville Luistro sees Diokno’s point in seeking protection for Madriaga, but they said the decision on the matter is up to the court handling his pending criminal case. 'It will ultimately depend on the court.. if it will agree. Pero… dapat po masecure talaga ‘yung person ni Mr. Madriaga kung totoo ‘yung sinasabi pong threats against this life. It doesn't have to be in the House detention facility sa totoo lang, basta I think BJMP should really secure Mr. Madriaga,' Ridon said. 'I don't think that that is special treatment, because I have seen the significance of the affidavit of Madriaga… If something wrong will happen to Madriaga, how will the complainant substantiate the allegations in their complaint?' 'I perfectly understand the position of Congressman Chel Diokno in requesting for a transfer of custody… of course, you will be concerned about addressing the safety, especially their allegations of threat already. Because if we lose Madriaga, then there will be very much difficulty on their part on how they're going to prove their allegations in the complaint,' Luistro added. Luistro is 'inclined' to transmit Diokno’s request to the court through the Office of the Clerk of Court, if the House panel agrees. 'We can only request… at the end of the day, it will be the decision of the court… In the case of Madriaga, he has a pending case and that's why strictly the jurisdiction over his person belongs to the court,' she explained. Diokno’s motion is expected to be tackled by the House Committee on Justice when it reconvenes on March 18th, Wednesday. The panel is also expected to discuss the appearance ad cautelam filed by Duterte’s lawyers. Ridon has raised a possible 'conflict of interest' in the inclusion of Atty. Michael Poa and Atty. Reynold Munsayac in Duterte’s legal team for the impeachment proceedings against. Luistro said if an objection to the appearance ad cautelam is raised, the panel will rule on it. 'They were people who are associated to the VP during the period when the alleged (confidential fund) misuse took place. So there is a very big possibility ipe-present sila ng prosecution as witnesses, perhaps as hostile witnesses. If that happens, and we allow their entry of appearance as lawyers of VP, pwedeng ma-raise ‘yung rule ng privileged communication between a lawyer and a client, which means na lahat ng pinag-usapan ng abogado at ng kliyente within the definition of the attorney-client relationship cannot be divulged in court because it is protected by the rule on privileged communication,' Luistro said. 'So there is where conflict of interest arise. But of course, this will be addressed in the Justice Committee if Cong. Ridon will formalize his objection to the entry of appealance, there's a possibility, pagbotohan ito ng justice members whether or not to allow the entry of appearance of Atty. Munsayac and Atty. Poa,' she added. Asked whether Ridon will make a motion to reject the entry of appearance of Munsayac and Poa, he said, it is 'on the table'. 'Pagka po finormalize ito pong entry of appearance noon pong mga defense panel. I think that is on the table… I think that can be formalized into a motion,' Ridon said. On Diokno’s motion to subpoena Duterte’s bank records, Luistro said it will be tackled by the House panel if it finds sufficient ground to proceed to a hearing proper. Luistro and Ridon rejected Duterte’s claim that conducting an investigation to 'search for evidence to support accusations already made in the impeachment complaints' would amount to a 'fishing expedition' by the lower chamber. 'There can be no fishing expedition kung mayroon na po already available documentation na papasok doon sa mga impeachable grounds against the Vice President. Pwede lang magka-fishing expedition kung absolutely zero documentation ‘yun pong impeachment processes as we speak. Hindi po ‘yun totoo… Even without the subpoenas, complete po ‘yung documentation for all the other grounds. So, ang binabanggit ho natin dito, we would want to get additional information, additional documentation in the course of the proceeding. And, more importantly, kasama naman po ito doon sa aktwal na kapangyarihan at trabaho ng House Committee on Justice serving as a part of the impeachment proceedings. So, hindi po totoo that there is another fishing expedition against the Vice President' Ridon added. The House Committee on Justice Chairperson also denied Duterte’s claim that the impeachment proceeding against her is a 'political attack designed to protect certain individuals and advance personal and foreign interests'. 'We do not look on the political landscape. We do not look on the 2028 election. We do not look on the issues, political issues surrounding the Philippine politics right now. We simply focus on the impeachment complaint and the rules that we are supposed to follow as we move forward to our impeachment proceeding… We are rule-based. We are following strictly the rules because we understand the importance of the impeachment process.' 'We cannot please everyone. If to her, there's a question about the integrity of the justice members, well, for us, we are doing it properly because we understand the solemnity of our duty under the Constitution,' she said. As of Friday, Duterte has yet to file a verified answer to the two impeachment complaints against her, which the House panel found sufficient in form and substance. She has until Monday to do so. Vice President Sara Duterte’s statement criticizing a motion for the House Committee on Justice to take custody of Ramil Madriaga 'suggests fear' for his testimony, according to House Committee on Justice member and Manila 3rd District Rep. Joel Chua. Vice President Sara Duterte on Monday kept mum on whether she would answer the impeachment complaints against her before the House of Representatives. MANILA ( UPDATED ) — Vice President Sara Duterte on Monday kept mum on whether she would answer the impeachment complaints against her before the House of Representatives.

title: Suspek sa pagpatay sa isang online seller tiklo
body: Isinilbi ang warrant of arrest sa kasong murder alas-10 ng umaga, August 26 sa bahay ng kanyang kinakasama sa Tanza, Cavite kung saan siya nagtago. 'Itong suspek natin ay nagtatago Antipolo hanggang ma pinpoint natin dito sa may Tanza, Cavite,' ani Police Lt. Ryan Valenzuela, assistant chief ng DPIO Unit ng Manila Police District. Ayon sa PNP, nangyari ang pamamaril nito lang June 10 sa Barangay 775, Dagonoy Street, Corner Radium, San Andres, Manila, pasado alas singko ng hapon. Nakunan pa sa CCTV ang insidente kung saan makikita ang lalaking biktima na nakatungkod at naghihintay umano ng buyer sa kanyang tinitindang t-shirt online. Ilang saglit pa, dumaan ang gunman na may suot na helmet at pinaulanan ng bala ang biktima. Ayon sa PNP, una na nilang nahuli ang driver ng motorsiklo na sinakyan ng gunman sa pagtakas matapos ang pamamaril. Nagpapasalamat ang ina ng biktima sa pagkakahuli sa itinuturong gunman pero naniniwala siya na inutusan lamang ang lalaki at may iba pang tao sa likod ng pagpaslang sa kanyang anak. May nakarating na impormasyon kasi sa kanya na may nakaalitan na maimpluwensyang taong nasa kulungan pa ang kanyang anak na nakulong noon dahil sa ilegal na droga. 'Sa loob pa lang pinagplanuhan na at maraming inalok para patayin 'yung anak ko worth 1 million daw ang binayad nung nagpautos,' dagdag ng ina ng biktima. Dagdag pa ng ina ng biktima, mismong ang nahuling driver ng motorsiklo na kasama ng gunman ang umamin na may nag-utos para barilin ang kanyang anak. 'Kasi ang kwento ng driver, 'yung gunman may kausap na babae, which is 'yun ang ginamit para makipag-meet up sa anak ko. And then ang sabi ng driver according to him sabi niya sabi daw ng babae kailangan makita ko na mamatay and then after non sabi ng driver na na makipagkita pa sa kanila ang babae. Binigay nung gunman ang baril atsaka 'yung cellphone ng anak kasi yun daw ang kabilin-bilinan na once natapos na, kailangang damputin 'yung cellphone,' ayon sa nanay ng biktima. Wala naman umanong record sa barangay ang biktima kaya laking gulat nila sa sinapit ng biktima ayon kay kagawad Demetrio Bohol. 'Pero hindi siya pala labas sa aming lugar. Hindi siya katulad nung ibang laging nasa labas. So far, wala pong record sa aming barangay yung batang yun,' aniya. Nasa custodial facility na ng Manila Police District ang suspek na dati na ring nakulong sa mga kasong paglabag sa RA 9165 o Comprehensive Dangerous Drugs Act of 2002, RA 10591, o Comprehensive Firearms and Ammunition Regulation Act, Frustrated Murder at Murder. Patuloy naman ang imbestigasyon ng PNP para matukoy ang nasa likod ng pamamaslang. Nagpapasalamat ang ina ng biktima na may iba pang tao sa likod ng pagpaslang sa kanyang anak. Nahuli ng mga operatiba ng District Police Intelligence and Operations Unit ng Manila Police District ang suspek sa pagpatay sa isang 27-anyos na online seller.

title: In ICI report, ex-DPWH engineer details scheme involving Joel Villanueva
body: The ICI on Wednesday released its interim report and recommendation to the Office of the Ombudsman, outlining schemes allegedly involving several incumbent and former members of the House of Representatives and the Senate in obtaining bribes of unwarranted monetary benefits from contractors of flood control projects. Former Bulacan DPWH 1st District Engineer Henry Alcantara appeared before the Commission during its September 24 hearing and provided copies of his sworn affidavits. On September 29, former Assistant District Engineer Brice Hernandez and former construction chief Jaypee Mendoza reappeared before the ICI. During those hearings, ICI said, the three former engineers of DPWH testified, among others, about the process by which government funds allocated for flood control projects were pocketed by various government officials. On October 8, 2025, ICIC said Hernandez and Mendoza executed a supplemental affidavit at the Department of Justice detailing the alleged involvement of public officials in different DPWH infrastructure projects. According to ICI, Alcantara and Hernandez voluntarily answered its questions and provided details of their dealings with several lawmakers. Alcantara testified that in 2022, Villanueva asked for a multipurpose building amounting to P1.5 billion, which was allowed but only for P600 million. Former DPWH Usec. Roberto Bernardo and Alcantara were allegedly forced to make a way to appease Villanueva. Alcantara told ICI that Villanueva was given projects under the 2023 unprogrammed allocations in the amount of P600 million, which were all flood control projects. Twenty-five percent of the amount was agreed to be given to the senator. P125 million was brought personally by Alcantara to a rest house in Bgy. Igulot, Bocaue, Bulacan and was left with a certain 'Peng' who allegedly works for Villanueva, with the instructions to hand the money to the senator. Villanueva has repeatedly denied all allegations. In late September, he said he was 'fully prepared to be investigated.' 'I have nothing to hide and I welcome any inquiry that will bring out the truth,' he said. During the anniversary celebration of Jesus Is Lord Church Worldwide earlier this month, Villanueva called the allegations a 'set-up.' 'Kaya ko pong tingnan ang bawat isa sa inyo dito mga kapwa ko JIL. Mata sa mata, wala tayong flood control. Hindi tayo sangkot sa katiwalian,' he said. According to the ICI, Alcantara's statement was corroborated by Engr. Brice Hernandez. Hernandez said Alcantara and Villanueva frequently communicate through messaging apps that feature disappearing messages. The ICI report stated that Hernandez would often take pictures of the messages between Alcantara and Villanueva because the former was worried that he would not be able to read them. 'One particular exchange would reveal Sen. Villanueva asking for funds from the DPWH. Further, it would appear that Sen. Villanueva was mad that he was not initially given flood control projects. Conversations between Engr. Alcantara and Sen. Villanueva would likewise confirm the Six Hundred Million Pesos (PhP600,000,000.00) worth of flood control projects with Sen. Villanueva as the proponent,' ICI's interim report said. Alcantara also testified that sometime in 2024, he and Usec. Bernardo attended the budget hearing at the Senate. At the time, Alcantara said Bernardo asked him if he wanted more funding since a certain 'SJE' still had an excess of P355 million. Hernandez corroborated Alcantara's statement and said that sometime in 2025, Sen. Jinggoy Estrada coordinated with Alcantara and informed him that he has P355 million worth of funds for allocation to fund flood control projects. According to the ICI report, former Ako Bicol party-list representative Zaldy Co allegedly had suitcases of money delivered to a house he owned in a gated subdivision in Pasig City that served only as storage for cash. Aside from Co, Villanueva, and Estrada, among those implicated are former congresswoman Mitch Cajayon-Uy, ex-DPWH Usec. Robert Bernardo, and COA Commissioner Mario Lipana. The ICI has referred its interim report and recommendations to the Office of the Ombudsman. MANILA ( UPDATED ) — A freeze order has been slapped on the assets of Senators Joel Villanueva, Jinggoy Estrada, and AKO BICOL Party List Rep. Zaldy Co, Justice Secretary Jesus Crispin Remulla said Tuesday. Remulla made the announcement during Tuesday’s hearing of the Senate Blue Ribbon Committee, after evaluating the testimony of former DPWH Bulacan district engineer Henry Alcantara for possible inclusion in the Witness Protection Program. Remulla said the Anti-Money Laundering Council (AMLC) has secured a Court of Appeals Order that also freezes the assets of former Caloocan Rep. Mitch Cajayon-Uy, and former Public Works and Highways officials Roberto Bernardo and Henry Alcantara. 'We treated this as already a complaint with the NBI as the endorsing agency,' Remulla said. 'I believe that freeze orders have been issued already by the AMLC.' 'The recommendation of the NBI for prosecution and probably this is what AMLC followed — for Mr. Alcantara, for Senator Joel Villanueva, Senator Jinggoy Ejercito Estrada, Congressman Elizaldy Co, former Undersecretary Roberto Bernardo, and former Congresswoman Mitch Cajayon,' he added. Remulla said cases of indirect bribery, malversation of public funds, and violation of the Anti-Graft and Corrupt practices act will be filed against the mentioned personalities. The DOJ is still looking into the case of Commission on Audit Commissioner Mario Lipana and his wife, Marilou, who is a contractor. According to Alcantara, Commissioner Lipana texted and personally visited him in 2022 to ask for a listing of flood control projects. He said Lipana asked for P1.4 billion from 2023 to 2025. 'At the right time we should perhaps refer the matter of Commissioner Lipana’s involvement based on the testimony of Engineer Alcantara to the Committee on Justice because this looks like an impeachable offense,' Senator Francis 'Kiko' Pangilinan said. Asked about the freeze order, AMLC Executive Director Matthew David said, however, 'We’ve received the referral from the DOJ and are actively working on it. The investigation is being fast-tracked, and we’re doing everything we can to move things forward as quickly and thoroughly as possible.' The Securities and Exchange Commission meanwhile, said it supports the AMLC's freeze order on the assets of individuals and companies allegedly involved in anomalous flood control projects. The SEC noted that the CA has also already issued a freeze order on 135 bank accounts and 27 insurance policies linked to persons and entities, allegedly involved in anomalous flood control projects. 'The issuance of the freeze order is a step towards the right direction in the government’s efforts to address the widespread corruption that hound flood control projects, and to exact accountability from everyone involved,' SEC Chairperson Francis Lim said. The assets of Senators Joel Villanueva, Jinggoy Estrada, and AKO BICOL Party List Rep. Zaldy Co have been frozen, Justice Secretary Jesus Crispin Remulla said Tuesday. MANILA - Senator Joel Villanueva on Saturday lashed out at his critics amid controversies surrounding his alleged involvement in spurious flood control projects. He described the allegations against him as a 'set-up.' 'Hindi po ba ito set-up mga magulang at kapatid? Sa mga kinaaway at nilabanan natin sa mga nakaraang panahon lalo na sa sindikato ng flood control, meron kayang nagagalit at pilit ginugulo ang storya ang totoong pangyayari para siguruhin na i set-up si Joel Villanueva,' he said. In a speech during the anniversary celebration of the Jesus Is Lord Church Worldwide, Villanueva firmly denied any involvement in the accusations against at him. Villanueva said, 'Kaya ko pong tingnan ang bawat isa sa inyo dito mga kapwa ko JIL. Mata sa mata, wala tayong flood control. Hindi tayo sangkot sa katiwalian.' His father, JIL founder Brother Eddie Villanueva, also spoke during the event and mentioned Senator Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa, expressing gratitude for defending his son. 'Sabihin mo, kung humingi sayo ng flood control project, mag reresign ako bilang senador. Sino may sabi? Born again ba? Hindi. Evangelical ba? Hindi. Ilang taon lang nakasama ni Joel sa Senado si Senator Ronald Bato tumayo, nanindigan. Hindi pa ako nakakapag pasalamat sa kanya.' said Bro. Eddie He likewise thanked Senator Robin Padilla for his support. Bro. Eddie said, 'Si Senador Robin Padilla na isang Muslim nung makilala niya si Joel, yung pagkatao ni Joel, sinabi niya handang-handa siyang ipagtanggol kahit kanino dito sa mga false accusations kay Joel.' The Villanueva patriarch expressed confidence that his son Joel would overcome the allegations being hurled against him. Senator Joel Villanueva on Saturday lashed out at his critics amid controversies surrounding his alleged involvement in spurious flood control projects. Justice Secretary Jesus Crispin 'Boying' Remulla takes his oath as the new Ombudsman before Supreme Court Associate Justice Marvic Leonen in Manila on Wednesday, October 9, 2025. Maria Tan, ABS-CBN News MANILA -- Ombudsman Jesus Crispin Remulla said they are not yet done with the case of Senator Joel Villanueva regarding his 2016 dismissal order that was apparently reversed in 'secret' by former Ombudsman Samuel Martires. In an interview on Luchi Cruz-Valdez' Youtube channel 'URL,' Remulla admitted his frustration upon learning that his predecessor has reversed the ruling over Villanueva's alleged misuse of his 2008 Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) when he was still a congressman. 'Umaga yung kinausap ko si Karen Davila. When I got to the office ibinigay sa akin yung decision ni Ombudsman Martires na nireverse niya ang ruling ni Conchita [Carpio-Morales] na hindi natin nakikita. Walang nakakita noon e. It's never been made public, he released a statement hinanap niya 'yung decision,' said Remulla. 'It sucks 'yung bagay na 'yun, ang sama e.' The Ombudsman said Villanueva is now trying to make it look as though he has been politically harassed regarding the case. Remulla: Pharmally case backlog a 'symbol of impunity' Remulla said he felt something wrong about the effort to 'cover it up.' 'The whole time hindi sila nagsasalita, ngayon si Joel Villanueva who's been quiet about it, ngayon sinasabi niya he's being harassed. Pero ba't ayaw niyang sabihin that thing has been reversed, ba't di niya sinabi?' said the Ombudsman. 'Tama ba yon? Ba't hindi sinabi sa 'ting lahat, may interes ang taong bayan, binoto natin ang tao tapos ganoon? Itatago mo sa amin ang katotohanan?' Remulla was supposed to request Senate President Vicente 'Tito' Sotto III to enforce the ruling issued by former Ombudsman Conchita Carpio-Morales when Villanueva 'revealed' the clearance he received from Martires. 'We were upended by the fact that Ombudman Martires already released a decision in 2019 that nobody knew about except him and maybe Joel Villanueva,' he said. Remulla drops bid to enforce Villanueva dismissal after 'secret' reversal 'Silang dalawa lang ang may alam. Hindi pa kami tapos... pag aaralan namin. We might have to refile the case, wala namang double jeopardy 'yun. For now aral muna... aral ulit.' In the same interview, Remulla revealed he was diagnosed with leukemia back in 2023. It was during the time when he underwent a quintuple heart bypass. 'Noong pagaling na ko ay nadiagnose naman ako ng cancer, leukemia... cancer ng dugo,' he said. 'Dito'y ginamot din ako... ako'y nagpasaialim ng cancer treatment sa duktor dito sa St. Luke's BCG... chemoteraphy, two cycles. Total body irradiation at 'yung tinatawag na bone marrow transplant.' Martires defends 'secrecy' of Villanueva dismissal case reversal: I protect a person's dignity 'Siguro meron lang ano sa akin marami pa kong gagawin sa mundo. Marami pa ring hamon, pagkatapos ng treatment may mga ilang problema so tuloy tuloy pa rin,' he said. 'Ang tingin ko lang tomorrow's another day... mapalipas mo lang ang isang gabi pagkagising mo sa umaga, isang araw na naman yun... I just live by the day.' Ombudsman Jesus Crispin Remulla said they are not yet done with the case of Senator Joel Villanueva regarding his 2016 dismissal order that was reversed in 'secret' by former Ombudsman Samuel Martires. Senator Joel Villanuez interpellates during the Senate Blue Ribbon Committee hearing on anomalous flood control projects on Thursday, September 18, 2025. Jonathan Cellona, ABS-CBN News MANILA — Sen. Joel Villanueva slammed the allegations made by former Bulacan District Engineer Henry Alcantara that he received kickbacks from flood control projects, saying he and his office are ready to be investigated to 'bring out the truth.' 'I am fully prepared to be investigated in these allegations. I have nothing to hide and I welcome any inquiry that will bring out the truth,' said Villanueva in a Senate session Tuesday, Sept. 23. This after former Department of Public Works and Highways Bulacan 1st District engineer Henry Alcantara alleged that Villanueva received a P150 million share for a P600-million multi-purpose building project in the province. Alcantara said the money was given to Villanueva's supposed aide, 'Peng.' Lacson: Estrada, Villanueva not yet cleared in budget insertion issue The engineer said the senator did not request any flood control project and was unaware that the money came from flood control allocations. 'If we carefully review what transpired this morning, it was clear from Mr. Alcantara’s own statements that hindi ako nag-request ng kahit anong flood control project. Wala akong alam, at hindi rin ako kailanman sinabihan tungkol sa mga proyektong ’yan—ang request po ay multi-purpose buildings para sa aking mga kababayan sa Bulacan, hindi flood control,' he said. Villanueva, whose allegations of involvement in the flood control mess started with an alleged text conversation with Alcantara, decried the mention of his name in the corruption issue. 'Napakadaling mag-doktor': Villanueva casts doubt on photos linking him to corruption 'Ano po ba ito? Masabi lang po yung pangalan natin at idadawit po tayo sa flood control issue kahit ang msimong testigo na ang nagsabi po na wala po akong alam. Mema lang?' he said. Villanueva underscored he is ready to be questioned in any forum to 'prove the allegations wrong.' 'I am ready, Mr. President, and my office is ready to be investigated that the truth may come out. At katulad po ng pagdalo ko kanina sa ICI (Independent Commission for Infrastructure), nakahanda rin po akong bigyang linaw ang mga bagay na ito sa anumang forum o lugar at patunayan na mali, malisyoso at pawang kasinungalingan lamang ang ipinupukol po sa ating pangalan,' he said. MANILA — Former Bulacan district engineer Henry Alcantara’s sworn affidavit submitted on Tuesday confirmed the earlier claim of his ex-deputy Brice Hernandez that millions of pesos in kickbacks were delivered to Senators Jinggoy Estrada and Joel Villanueva. But Alcanta’s 49-page affidavit contained more details, including the involvement of former Public Works Undersecretary Roberto Bernardo, Ako Bicol Rep. Zaldy Co, former Rep. Mitch Cajayon-Uy, and Commission on Audit Commissioner Mario Lipana. Both Alcantara and Hernandez alleged that Villanueva downloaded P600-million worth of projects in 2023. Hernandez claimed that the Bulakenyo senator received 30 percent in kickbacks, but Alcantara said that the lawmaker’s cut was only 25 percent. Alcantara admitted to personally delivering P150 million to Villanueva’s rest house in Barangay Igulot in Bocaue, and left it with a staff named 'Peng.' 'Sinabi ko kay Peng na pakibigay na lang kay Boss, tulong lamang iyon para sa future na plano niya pero hindi po niya alam na doon galing iyon sa flood control,' the former district engineer’s affidavit read. 'Matapos po noon ay hindi na kami nag-usap.' While Hernandez’s allegations against Villanueva stopped at the amount of projects he downloaded to the DPWH Bulacan’s First District Engineering Office, Alcantara gave more details about how Villanueva pitched for a P1.5-billion multi-purpose building in 2022. 'Sa halagang P1.5 billion ay P600 million lamang ang napagbigyan na pondo… Hindi ito ikinatuwa ni Sen. Joel kaya napilitan kami gumawa ng paraan ni Usec. Bernardo,' Alcantara said. 'Hindi humingi ng partikular na proyekto o porsiyento si Sen. Joel pero inutos ni Usec. Bernardo na bigyan na lamang ng proyekto na may katumbas na P150 million na proponent itong si Sen. Joel,' he said. 'Hindi alam ni Sen. Joel na mga flood control ang mga proyektong inilaan sa kaniya dahil sa aking pagkakaalam ay ayaw ni Sen. Joel ng mga proyektong flood control.' Villanueva has denied the allegations, and asserted that he is 'fully prepared to be investigated on these allegations.' 'I have nothing to hide, and I welcome any inquiry that will bring out the truth,' he said in a manifestation hours after Alcantara submitted the affidavit to the Senate Blue Ribbon Committee. The two former DPWH officials’ statements about alleged Estrada’s involvement in the flood control scandal also matched in terms of the amount of projects, but differed in several details. Both Alcantara and Hernandez claimed that Estrada downloaded P355-million worth of projects to their district under the 2025 national budget. 'Nasa 2024 budget hearing kami sa Senado… Habang kami ay naghihintay, tinanong ako ni Usec. Bernardo kung mayroon pa akong gustong lagyan at mayroon pang available na P355M si ‘SJE,’' Alcantara said. 'Agad-agad po akong nagpagawa ng listahan sa staff ko at sa loob ng 10-15 minutes ay ipinasa ko na po ang listahan kay Usec. Bernardo,' he said. 'Ang proponent po nito ay 25 percent [kickback]… Wala po akong direktang transaksyon o direktang pakikipag-ugnayan kay Sen. Jinggoy,' he said. This statement is contrary to Hernandez’s claim that Alcantara is the 'chief implementor' of the scheme as he allegedly is in charge of negotiating 'with all involved politicians.' Meantime, other government officials that were mentioned in Alcantara’s affidavit that were not included in Hernandez’s are as follows: - allegedly received 20 percent in kickback for every project - kickbacks usually delivered to a hotel in Manila - downloaded P350-M for 6 projects in 2022 - downloaded P710-M worth of projects in 2023 - downloaded P3.3-B worth of projects in 2024 - profitted from 426 projects worth P35.02-B - charged 20 percent in kickbacks in 2022 - increased his rate to 25 percent since 2023 - payoffs usually delivered to his Pasig City home or dropped off in Taguig hotel - asked for a list of flood control projects in Bulacan in 2022 - inserted projects amounting to P500M in 2023, P400M in 2024, P500M in 2025 FORMER CALOOCAN REP. MARY MITZI CAJAYON-UY - kickbacks delivered to a restaurant in Commonwealth Avenue, Quezon City - inserted P411-M worth of projects in the 2022 budget Alcantara also mentioned a certain Ferdstar Builders that sought for a list of projects in Bulacan. He did not give further details as to how the construction firm may be involved in the scheme. Magbubukid Party-List Rep. Ferdinand Beltran used to own Ferdstar Builders. Last week, Beltran denied involvement in anomalous flood control projects in Bulacan and dismissed allegations of conflict of interest. Former Bulacan district engineer Henry Alcantara’s sworn affidavit submitted on Tuesday confirmed the earlier allegation of his ex-deputy Brice Hernandez that millions of pesos in kickbacks were delivered to Senators Jinggoy Estrada and Joel Villanueva. A former DPWH engineer claims to have personally brought P125-million meant for Senator Joel Villanueva to a rest house in Bocaue, Bulacan, according to a report by the Independent Commission for Infrastructure. MANILA -- A former Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) engineer claims to have personally brought P125-million meant for Senator Joel Villanueva to a rest house in Bocaue, Bulacan, according to a report by the Independent Commission for Infrastructure (ICI).

title: DOJ assures VP Sara Duterte of fair probe despite skipping NBI hearing
body: DOJ Undersecretary Jesse Andres said it was 'unfortunate' that the Vice President did not show up at the NBI on Wednesday. 'Due process is being afforded her. I do not understand why she does not want to be part of the process which could possibly exculpate her or get her out of criminal liability,' Andres said. He said the Vice President could expect fairness even if a case is filed by the NBI at the DOJ and a preliminary investigation is called by prosecutors. But Andres cautioned that sending a letter, like the Vice President did at the NBI, was not enough as notarized affidavits are required during preliminary investigations. 'Kapag siya po ay pumasok sa preliminary investigation, kailangan po na ang kanyang mga salaysay ay sinumpaang salaysay so that the statements will be accountable because any statement given under oath which is false is punishable also for perjury and other criminal offenses,' Andres said. Whether at the NBI or DOJ stage of investigation, Andres assured the Vice President that the rule of law is always upheld. 'Tinitiyak po namin sa Vice President at sa lahat po ng mamamayan na ang Department of Justice po ay magiging patas at matuwid sa kanilang evaluation kung kailangan po mag-file ng kaso o hindi, tingan niyo po ang reporma ng Department of Justice sa loob ng dalawang taon, lahat po ay nakasentro sa karapatang pantao, primacy of human rights, and observance of the rule of law,' Andres said. Andres reiterated that the investigation of the NBI was also for the security of the Vice President as she also claimed that there were threats to her life, the reason why she made her supposed counter threats against the President, First Lady Liza Araneta-Marcos, and House Speaker Martin Romualdez. 'Tungkulin po ng ating pamahalaan na pangalagaan din ang kaligtasan ng Bise Presidente at kaya po ang NBI ay nagsusumikap na kunin ang statement niya kung ano po yung mga sitwasyon,' Andres said. Duterte said her statement was taken out of context. She said the 'revenge' plot had a specific condition before it could be put into motion: that she had to die first. She added that her remark 'does not constitute to an active threat.' The Department of Justice said that even if Vice President Sara Duterte skipped a hearing of the National Bureau of Investigation, she could expect a fair evaluation on the supposed threats she made against President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr. MANILA — The Department of Justice said that even if Vice President Sara Duterte skipped a hearing of the National Bureau of Investigation, she could expect a fair evaluation on the supposed threats she made against President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr.

title: Obama calls for efforts to counter Islamic State in Libya
body: Islamic State militants have taken advantage of chaos in Libya to establish themselves in the city of Sirte, and they have carried out several attacks on oil installations this month. 'The President directed his national security team to continue efforts to strengthen governance and support ongoing counterterrorism efforts in Libya and other countries where ISIL has sought to establish a presence,' the White House statement said, using an acronym for Islamic State. Earlier on Thursday, Defense Secretary Ash Carter told a news conference that Islamic State was establishing training sites in Libya and welcoming foreign fighters the way it had done in Iraq and Syria in years past. 'And we don't want to be on a glide slope to a situation like Syria and Iraq. That's the reason why we're watching it that closely. That's the reason why we develop options for what we might do in the future.' On Wednesday, Pentagon spokesman Peter Cook said the United States had already sent 'a small number of military personnel' into Libya to try to 'to engage in conversations with local forces to get a clearer picture of exactly what's happening there.' 'We're looking at military options,' Cook said, according to a transcript of his news conference on the Department of Defense website. The political chaos in Libya has slowed the international community's ability to partner with the loose alliances of armed brigades of rebels who once fought veteran leader Muammar Gaddafi, who was overthrown in 2011. WASHINGTON - U.S. President Barack Obama directed his national security advisers on Thursday to counter efforts by Islamic State to expand into Libya and other countries, the White House said.

title: Marcos Jr denounces terror attacks in Israel amid deaths of 2 Pinoys
body: 'My heart is heavy upon hearing confirmation of the deaths of two Filipinos in Israel. The Philippines condemns these killings and stands firmly against the ongoing terror and violence,' Marcos, Jr. said in a statement. Israeli Ambassador Ilan Fluss condoled with the families of the victims. He also thanked the overseas Filipino workers staying in Israel. 'I would like to take this opportunity to express my condolences to the families but also to express our gratitude to those 6 Filipinos and generally to the OFWs in Israel who are there staying with our families, with the elderly people,' Fluss said in an interview on ANC. 'Even in the time of this emergency, the Filipinos are not requesting to be evacuated from Israel,' he added. The envoy said the Hamas militants continue to enter Israel mostly in the southern part of the country to 'kill Israelis,' noting that this was war. He also blasted the terror organization for its actions and for using civilians as 'human shields.' 'The border with Gaza has been bombed... It is a war situation. There is no discussion with Hamas which is holding 150 plus captives. People have been kidnapped from Israel... No humanitarian gestures,' he said. In a statement, the Presidential Communications Office (PCO) said Marcos met Fluss at Malacañang to talk about the situation in Israel. Marcos, PCO said, was concerned about the 3 Filipinos still unaccounted for. The Philippine Embassy in Israel earlier said that another Pinoy was feared dead because of the armed conflict. Authorities were still waiting on the result of the DNA test. 'The Ambassador assured the President that Israel is doing all it can to ensure the safety of our nationals,' the PCO said. 'The President assured Amb. Fluss that the Philippines will always stand with Israel in this war against the inhuman terrorist attacks by Hamas,' it added. The DFA and Department of Migrant Workers (DMW), for its part, said it would not identify the victims as per their families' request. It vowed to work closely with the Philippine Embassy in Tel-Aviv, Amman, Jordan, and the Overseas Workers Welfare Administration (OWWA) to repatriate the victims' remains. 'Handa rin ang inyong Tahanan ng OFW na tulungan ang mga naiwang kamag-anak sa pagproseso at pagtanggap ng anumang tulong at benepisyo na nararapat sa kanila,' the DMW said. There are some 30,000 Filipinos in Israel, and 137 others in the Gaza strip. Malacañang said the Philippine government would continue in its pursuit for 'lasting peace in line with the United Nations resolutions and international laws.' The government will also give support and aid to overseas Filipino workers (OFWs) and the Filipino community in Israel affected by the armed conflict. The death toll in Israel has surged above 900 from the worst attack in the country's 75-year history, while Gaza officials reported 900 people killed so far, and Israel's army said the bodies of roughly 1,500 militants had been found. International NGOs issued a stark warning over the health and humanitarian situation in the Gaza Strip. MANILA (UPDATE) — President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr. condemned Wednesday the terror attacks in Israel following the deaths of 2 Filipinos there amid the ongoing armed conflict between the country and Hamas militants.

title: DPWH inspects Valenzuela drainage project to ease flooding linked to rail construction
body: The project is located along T. Santiago Street near MacArthur Highway, where what used to be a grassy, unpaved area has been converted into a road. DPWH Secretary Vince Dizon said flooding in the area has reached severe levels, with water sometimes rising to chest-deep during heavy rains. He added that the construction of the North–South Commuter Railway (NSCR) was among the factors that contributed to the problem. 'Nangalahati 'yung waterway at the same time, bumabaw din because of siltation and daming basurang na-accumulate bumabaw nang bumabaw,' Dizon said. 'So ang nangyayari, lahat ng tubig nag-o-overflow diyan, tumatapon ngayon. Lahat ng tubig napupunta sa MacArthur tumitigil diyan,' he added. The drainage system will have an outfall that leads to Longos Creek near the Malanday area. The current project is being implemented as a short-term solution aimed at reducing flooding before the rainy season begins in June. Construction started in February and was expected to be completed by the end of March. 'Malaking bagay 'to kasi kung mapapansin niyo dati 'yung tubig daw di tumutuloy, dumidiretso dito, umiikot pa. So ngayon, mas mabilis na 'yung magiging flow,' he added. He said the DPWH would coordinate with the Department of Transportation (DOTr) to identify a long-term solution to the flooding problem. Valenzuela City Mayor Wes Gatchalian said around four to six barangays were affected by flooding in the area and pointed out that flooding in the area was not as severe years ago. 'If you come here maybe 10 years ago or 8 years ago, maluwang po 'yan. May mga structures po dito pero di po kami ganun binabaha,' Gatchalian said. He added that the situation also affects commuters traveling along MacArthur Highway. ''Yung commuters po doon sa MacArthur ang talagang kawawa. Hindi makabalik sa trabaho, ambulansya, hindi makapasok, kasi talaga ho 'yung tubig mataas po diyan,' Gatchalian said. 'Pag baha rin, di rin makapasok ang commuter para sumakay sa future train po natin,' he added. The Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) and the Valenzuela City government inspected Tuesday the ongoing construction of a drainage and road improvement project in Barangay Veinte Reales. MANILA — The Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) and the Valenzuela City government inspected Tuesday the ongoing construction of a drainage and road improvement project in Barangay Veinte Reales.

title: MRT resumes full operations after passenger jumps onto tracks
body: The MRT-3 operations was temporarily disrupted on Wednesday. Train services from Taft to North Avenue stations resumed as of 1:20 p.m., the MRT management announced on Twitter, after the incident forced the railway to limit its services.
