title: Trump warns Gaza 'you are dead' if hostages not freed
body: The United States meanwhile confirmed unprecedented indirect talks with Hamas, which it proscribes as a 'terrorist' group, focused on American hostages in Gaza. Strongly backing Israel as the ceasefire teeters, Trump said he was 'sending Israel everything it needs to finish the job' as his administration expedites billions of dollars in weapons. 'Release all of the Hostages now, not later, and immediately return all of the dead bodies of the people you murdered, or it is OVER for you,' he wrote on his Truth Social platform after meeting freed hostages. 'This is your last warning! For the leadership, now is the time to leave Gaza, while you still have a chance.' Trump also warned of repercussions for Gaza as a whole, where virtually the entire population has been displaced by Israel's relentless military campaign in response to Hamas's October 7, 2023 attack. 'To the People of Gaza: A beautiful Future awaits, but not if you hold Hostages. If you do, you are DEAD!' His comments follow Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's warning of 'consequences that you cannot imagine' if Hamas does not hand over the remaining hostages seized in the October 7 attack. The first phase of a ceasefire ended over the weekend after six weeks of relative calm that included exchanges of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners held in Israeli jails. While Israel has said it wants to extend the first phase until mid-April, Hamas has insisted on a transition to the second phase, which should lead to a lasting ceasefire. Israel has ramped up pressure not just with threats but also by halting the flow of goods and supplies into Gaza. 'Hamas has indeed suffered a severe blow, but it has not yet been defeated. The mission is not yet accomplished,' Israel's new military chief Eyal Zamir warned Wednesday. Also on Wednesday, France, Britain and Germany jointly called the humanitarian situation in Gaza 'catastrophic,' and urged Israel to ensure the 'unhindered' delivery of aid. South Africa said Israel's restriction of aid into Gaza amounted to using starvation as a weapon of war. 'We lack drinking water. People are complaining of lack of water in general. We cannot get rid of the waste, which affects the groundwater,' Abu Hammam al-Hasanat, a 34-year-old who lives in the Gaza Strip, told AFP. Trump's hawkish language came after the United States confirmed unprecedented direct talks with Hamas, with the US envoy on hostage affairs, Adam Boehler, discussing American hostages. 'Look, dialogue and talking to people around the world to do what's in the best interest of the American people is something that the president' believes is right, White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said. The United States had refused direct contact with the Palestinian militants since banning them as a terrorist organisation in 1997. But Leavitt said that the hostage envoy 'has the authority to talk to anyone'. Both the White House and Netanyahu's office confirmed Israel was consulted in advance. Five Americans are believed to remain among the hostages -- four have been confirmed dead and one, Edan Alexander, is believed to be alive. The attack resulted in the deaths of 1,218 people, most of them civilians, while Israel's military retaliation in Gaza has killed at least 48,440 people, also mostly civilians, data from both sides show. Of the 251 captives taken during Hamas's attack, 58 remain in Gaza, including 34 the Israeli military has confirmed are dead. In an interview on Wednesday night, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio urged Hamas to take seriously Trump's threats of retaliation. 'He doesn’t say these things and not mean it, as folks are finding out around the world. If he says he's going to do something, he'll do it,' Rubio said. Trump has floated a proposal to take over the Gaza Strip and displace its people, an idea that has drawn wide condemnation around the world. Arab leaders have sought support for an alternative plan that would finance Gaza's reconstruction through a trust fund. The Arab summit also called for unified representation under the Palestine Liberation Organization to sideline Islamist Hamas. Hugh Lovatt at the European Council on Foreign Relations said the Arab leaders' plan was 'far more realistic than what the Trump administration is proposing.' But Ghassan Khatib, a Palestinian political analyst and former Palestinian Authority minister, was skeptical. 'It doesn't make sense to expect Israel to drop the plan of Trump and to adopt the plan of the Arabs. There's no chance.' Speaking after a UN Security Council meeting on Gaza's future, French diplomat Jay Dharmadhikari said the final plan should neither allow Hamas to continue governing nor eject Palestinians. 'We are clear that any plan must have no role for Hamas, must ensure Israel's security, must not displace Palestinians from Gaza,' he said. 'To the People of Gaza: A beautiful Future awaits, but not if you hold Hostages. If you do, you are DEAD!' US President Donald Trump on Wednesday threatened further destruction of Gaza if all remaining hostages are not released, and issued an ultimatum to Hamas leaders to flee.

title: Duterte's daughter, ex-wife lead 'Byaheng Du30'
body: Mula Davao City, bumyahe ang Byaheng Du30 bus papuntang Tagum City, Davao del Norte at dumaan ng Compostela, Compostela Valley. Didiretso ito sa San Francisco at Bayugan City sa Agusan del Sur. Pupunta rin ito sa Surigao City, Leyte, Tacloban at Samar. Ayon kay Sara Duterte, tatlumpung araw ang kanilang byahe sa Visayas at Mindanao para mangalap ng suporta sa kandidatura ng kanyang ama. Sabi naman ni Elizabeth Zimmerman, ang dating asawa ng alkalde, kahit matagal na silang hiwalay ni Duterte ay nananatili silang magkaibigan at sinusuportahan niya ito sa kaniyang kampanya. Si Elizabeth ay may sakit na kanser. Kahit sumasailalim ito sa radiation therapy, pinayagan ito ng kanyang doktor na mangampanya. Ayon sa kanya, naka 18 sessions na siya sa therapy. DAVAO CITY - Umarangkada na ang ''Byaheng Du30'' (Duterte) ngayong araw na pinangungunahan ng pangawalang anak ni Davao City Mayor Rodrigo Duterte na si Inday Sara Duterte at inang si Elizabeth Zimmerman.

title: Akbayan: DOTr should consult commuters before closing door on extending MRT, LRT hours
body: The transportation department said while the appeal may ' provide greater convenience to commuters, it may compromise the maintenance time of the rail systems '. DOTr said this may put more passengers at risk since maintenance work is done during the trains' downtimes. Akbayan Rep. Perci Cendaña said DOTr should hold dialogues with commuters before dismissing the call. 'Makinig at makisimpatiya naman ang DOTr sa ating mga commuters imbes na agarang magsara ng pinto sa panukalang i-extend ang MRT-LRT operations,' he stressed in a statement. (DOTr should listen to and sympathize with commuters instead of automatically shutting the door to the call to extend MRT and LRt operations) 'Every day, commuters, especially [Business Process Outsourcing] workers and night-shift employees, face immense hardships. Extending train hours by even one or two hours can significantly alleviate their struggles, providing them with safe, efficient, and reliable transportation options after long hours of work,' Cendaña said. He also pointed out that Metro Manila’s rail systems shouldfollow the train services standards in other Asian capitals like Bangkok, Taipei, Kuala Lumpur, Jakarta, and Singapore, where Cendaña said trains do operate until midnight. The party-list hopes DOTr will hold discussions with stakeholders on extending the operating hours of railway systems and other reforms that the commuters need. 'Halimbawa, puwede namang i-extend pa rin ang operations pero tuwing MWF lang or kung anong days of the week mataas ang ridership,' Cendaña said. (For example, how about extended hours on certain days, or on the days of the week when ridership is high) 'Maraming options. Kung gusto, may paraan. Kung ayaw, may dahilan,' he added. (There are many options. They will find a way if they really want to, or find an excuse if they really don't) Akbayan kicked off a signature campaign on Thursday and trooped to the MRT-3 Quezon Avenue station to collect support for extending the train station hours. The group said it is vital to alleviate the 'worsening transport crisis' in Metro Manila. 'The DOTr must listen to the voices of commuters before shutting down their appeals. Public transportation is a public service—it must serve the people,' Cendaña added. Akbayan Party-list on Friday urged the Department of Transportation (DOTr) to reconsider its appeal to extend the operating hours of Metro Manila's three main railways to midnight, after the agency rejected the proposal. MANILA — Akbayan Party-list on Friday urged the Department of Transportation (DOTr) to reconsider its appeal to extend the operating hours of Metro Manila's three railways to midnight, after the agency rejected the proposal as unfeasible.

title: Comelec begins crackdown on illegal campaign materials
body: The simultaneous operation started at 4 a.m., with different local Comelec offices and partner agencies spearheading their own operation. In Quezon City, around 300 personnel fro city hall were deployed in six districts of the city, supervised by six Comelec election officers. 'Ang gagawin natin for today ay tatanggalin natin mula sa mga lansangan ang mga campaign posters ng mga kandidato, national candidates as well as party-list candidates. Ngayon kasi start na ng campaign period meaning to say, bawal na silang magpost ng mga campaign materials sa mga public places. Doon lang dapat sila sa designated areas,' said Atty. John Fajardo, Comelec Election Officer for District 5. 'Ang sizes na required [for posters] is 2x3 [feet] lang however, regardless of the size, pagmali ang pinaglagyan natin ay bawal po ito lalo na kapag nilagay sa mga puno, sa kawad ng kuryente, or places kung saan sinabi ng Comelec na bawal,' he added. Personnel from the Quezon City Department of Public Order and Safety (DPOS), Traffic and Transport Management Department and other agencies dismantled campaign posters of different partly-list candidates, illegally posted along Araneta Avenue, Quezon Avenue, E. Rodriguez and other areas. 'So ang effort namin ay concentrated sa anim na distrito, historically ang districts 1, 4 and 2 ang mga mararami [illegal campaign materials] Kaya minabuti namin na marami ang binuong grupo na magbabaklas ng mga illegal campaign materials. Designated 2 trucks per district, atleast 50 personnel per district,' said Dexter Cardenas, Chief of Quezon City's Traffic and Transport Management Department. 'i-coconsolidate namin sa isang lugar, at hindi na ibabalik yun kasi ilegal sila,' he added. MANILA - The Philippine National Police (PNP) said Monday it is ready for the upcoming campaign period for national candidates and party lists, which will officially start on February 11, 2025. In a press briefing, PNP Spokesperson P/BGen. Jean Fajardo emphasized that their top priority includes securing the entire electoral process, stressing that they will implement strategic security measures to ensure a safe and orderly election season leading up to May 2025 in coordination with the Commission on Elections (Comelec). 'Asahan po natin na ‘yong security coverage po natin doon sa inaasahang influx ng mga national candidates po natin from the National Capital Region down to Visayas and Mindanao po ay naka-ready po ‘yan. Bilang paghahanda ay magdadagdag tayo ng mga tauhan natin doon sa mga engagement areas particularly,' said Fajardo. She added that the PNP is also working closely with national candidates to coordinate for their campaign schedules. 'Ine-expect po natin na paglapag po nila doon ay may mga nakalatag pong security. And, of course, doon sa mga closed in security na ididikit po natin sa ating mga kandidato para po masiguro ‘yong kanilang kaligtasan habang sila ay nagsasagawa po ng kanilang campaign sorties,' she added. Meanwhile, the PNP has committed full support for Comelec’s committee on 'Kontra Bigay' to prevent vote buying. Fajardo added that the 'money ban' policy, which was previously implemented in past elections, will be strictly observed. 'May mga indicators po, ‘yong mga red flags po na tinatawag natin na kung sakaling may mare-receive po tayong report na may ganitong kahalagang pera, may nakalagay sa indibidwal na mga sobre, may mga pangalan at may mga intended na recipient, ‘yon po ay isa sa mga red flags na babantayan natin,' she explained. The PNP will also tighten security checkpoints across the country. 'We don’t want to delay longer than necessary ‘yong mga supporters at mga kandidato po na dadaan sa ating mga checkpoints but we would also like to appeal to them na respetuhin po ‘yong umiiral na checkpoints,' she added. Morevover, Fajardo noted that the PNP is now at the final stage of polishing guidelines for Oplan Katok, which involves police personnel making house visits to remind gun owners with expired licenses to apply for renewal and encourage them to surrender unlicensed firearms for safekeeping. '[It] should be conducted within office hours, between 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. Dapat ang mga pulis natin ay unipormado ‘pag pumupunta po doon sa mga intended subject po ng kanilang revitalized 'Katok.' Dapat po ay nakikipag-ugnayan sila sa mga barangay officials para samahan po sila doon sa intended subject… Siguraduhin na maging courteous at ipaliwanag kung ano ba ‘yong sadya nila.' To date, the PNP registered over 1 million expired firearm licenses across the country. The PNP, along with other law enforcement agencies, will also monitor Private Armed Groups (PAGs) which will help to ensuring a 'peaceful, secure and fair' 2025 elections. The Philippine National Police (PNP) said Monday it is ready for the upcoming campaign period for national candidates and party lists, which will officially start on February 11, 2025. Simula February 11, babaklasin na ng Comelec ang campaign materials na nakapaskil sa mga hindi tamang lugar. Kasabay ito ng pagsisimula ng 90 araw na kampanya para sa halalan ng mga kandidato sa pagka-senador at party-list. TV Patrol, Sabado, 8 Pebrero 2025. The Commission on Elections (Comelec) officially launched its 'Oplan Baklas' or the removal of illegal campaign materials on Tuesday, the official start of the campaign period for senators and party-list candidates.

title: 'The tip of the iceberg': Expert warns of nat'l security threat amid alleged Chinese spies arrests
body: Prof. Renato De Castro of the De La Salle University’s Department of International Studies said that espionage goes beyond ordinary information gathering. 'Nag-ga-gather sila ng information na kumbaga ang tingin nila ay sensitibo at dapat itago. Doon pumapasok ang term na espionage… Pumunta ka sa isang bansa, nagbasa ka ng diyaryo pero kapag kumukuha ka ng impormasyon na gustong itago no’ng bansa na ‘yon doon na pumapasok ‘yong espionage,' said De Castro. The arrested individuals were reportedly gathering information related to military camps and Enhanced Defense Cooperation Agreement (EDCA) sites in the country. According to De Castro, these locations play a crucial role as far as the national security is concerned. 'Ito ‘yong tinatawag mo na tactical information na maaari mong gamitin sa isang military operation. Bakit mo aalamin ‘yong kampo? Hindi lang ‘yong kampo, ‘yong lokasyon. Kumukuha pa sila ng 3D images… Gagamitin nila ‘yong impormasyon para gamitin sa drone warfare. Nakikita natin na nangyayari sa Ukraine, or impormasyon na maaari ring gamitin sa pag-launch ng missile attack. ‘Yon ang nakakabahala,' De Castro added. Meanwhile, De Castro emphasized that China’s interest in Philippine military installation can be linked to its objective, particularly on their ambitions concerning Taiwan. 'Ito ‘yong sinasabi ng ating kaibigan mula sa Hukbong Dagat natin, this is just a tip of the iceberg… Posible ‘yan na gamitin sa isang military operation kasi naghahanda rin ang China ng isang malaking military operation na maaari nilang isagawa a couple of years from now. Gagawin nila ito sa bansang napakalapit sa atin, ang Taiwan,' he added. De Castro also believes that it is due time for an update to the country’s laws on espionage, citing provisions in the Commonwealth Act of 1941. 'I think it’s 1941, a commonwealth act, dapat nang baguhin ‘yan. Dapat nang i-revise. Kasi unang-una, hindi na tayo Commonwealth na isang teritoryo ng America. Isa na tayong malayang bansa. Of course, iba na ‘yong nature ng pagkukuha ng impormasyon o espionage in the 21st century kung ikukumpara mo in early 1940s,' he said. De Castro also emphasized that some Filipinos may be aiding foreign intelligence agencies, following the arrest of Deng Yuanqing and his two alleged Filipino cohorts on January 17 for alleged espionage. 'Unang-una diyan ay pera. Pangalawa ay naniniwala ka kumbaga sa sinasabi ng bansa na ‘yon. Isang elemento rin na kapag nilapitan ka no’ng mga intelligence agencies na ‘yon, ibig sabihin importante ka. Kumbaga pinagpapalit mo ‘yong iyong pagiging Pilipino para magsilbi sa isang banyagang bansa,' he added. China, however, said that the Philippines should stop 'chasing shadows' and 'peddling' the narrative of espionage and 'earnestly protect the lawful rights and interests of Chinese nationals' in the country instead. MANILA (2nd UPDATE) — Some of the five Chinese nationals arrested for suspected espionage in Palawan and in Manila had posed as tourists, a local official said Thursday. The suspected spies posed as Taiwanese tourists and set up CCTV cameras facing the sea in Palawan, Provincial Board member Ryan Maminta said. 'Yun po ang impormasyon na nakarating sa amin: na may mga aktibidad sila katulad ng pagkuha ng videos at hindi lang yun, paglalagay ng CCTV camera sa mga lugar. Tapos ang kanilang mga posing ay mga turista — hindi nga Chinese tourists — kundi Taiwanese tourist dito sa aming lalawigan,' he told TeleRadyo Serbisyo. NBI arrests 5 Chinese nationals suspected of espionage In a separate statement, the National Bureau of Investigation said the group of Chinese nationals was monitored in Barangay Buenavista in Puerto Princesa City, posing as buyers of marine products and roaming around the city. The Chinese nationals were identified as members of the Qiaoxing Volunteer Group of the Philippines, as well as the Philippine China Association of Promotion of Peace and Friendship, Inc. One of the suspects was also recorded taking a video of Philippine Coast Guard ships, the NBI said Wednesday. Upon their arrest, officials found photos of Coast Guard station, vessels, small navy crafts and assets, as well as fooatges of PCG vessels BRP Teresa Magbanua and BRP Gabriela Silang, in their mobile devices. National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) director Jaime Santiago told a news conference the men used a drone to document goings-on at a naval detachment, coast guard ships, an air base, a naval base, and a dockyard in Palawan province, the closest major land mass to the Spratlys. 'We consider them very dangerous to national security because of course, if this falls into other hands, this could be very dangerous for our personnel in the base and also those on board our ships,' Philippine military chief General Romeo Brawner told the news conference. Two other Chinese men were arrested elsewhere in Manila and another in the central city of Dumaguete last week, Santiago said. NBI cybercrime unit chief Jeremy Lotoc said Thursday an unnamed 'foreign national' was giving orders to Chinese software engineer Deng Yuanqing, who was detained earlier this month. Some of the suspects have been living in the Philippines since 2002 and did not have any criminal records, immigration bureau spokeswoman Dana Sandoval told the news conference. The arrests come as maritime confrontations between the Philippines and China, over contested reefs and waters in the West Philippine Sea, have escalated in recent months. PCG confronts another Chinese ship off Zambales coast China claims most of the strategic waterway despite an international ruling that its assertion has no legal basis. Philippines to train with US Typhon missile system in February Assistant Director General Jonathan Malaya of the National Security Council said authorities were looking into whether the suspects were linked to a Chinese software engineer who was arrested this month for allegedly spying on Filipino military and police camps. The Chinese software engineer, identified as Deng Yuanqing, and his two Filipino drivers were arrested last week as part of counter-espionage operations that began last month, National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) chief Jaime Santiago told a news conference. He alleged that Deng was affiliated with a Chinese university controlled by the People's Liberation Army and was part of a team sent to 'conduct espionage in our territory'. Wife of arrested Chinese national denies he is a spy AFP backs findings on alleged Chinese spy despite wife's denial The Chinese embassy in Manila did not respond immediately to requests for comment on the arrest and allegations. The NBI's cybercrime chief Jeremy Lotoc said Deng had made frequent visits to 'critical infrastructure, specifically military camps, local government offices, power plants, police camps, stations, even shopping malls'. 'They were basically collecting data and they have this remote application which transmits outside the country in real time data that they collected in our country,' Lotoc said. Lotoc said it was 'alarming' because the information being transmitted could include geographical coordinates and topography. Philippine military chief General Romeo Brawner said it was possible the information 'could be used for military targeting purposes'. AFP says ‘foreign power’ mapping out Philippines Lotoc said Deng had been in the Philippines for five years and was part of a group that also included hardware engineers and a financier who were still 'at large'. The group received funding of P1.5 million a week via 'shell companies', he said. Some of the five Chinese nationals arrested for suspected espionage in Palawan and in Manila had posed as tourists, a local official said Thursday. Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) building in Pasay City on September 5, 2022. Jonathan Cellona, ABS-CBN News/File. MANILA — The Department of Foreign Affairs said it is ready to support agencies investigating several Chinese nationals arrested for alleged espionage activities Foreign Affairs Secretary Enrique Manalo said the department is ready to help the Department of Justice and the Armed Forces of the Philippines 'in whatever way we can'. Alleged Chinese spies posed as tourists in Palawan: official He said the DFA is among the agencies concerned with national security nd that suspected espionage in the country is an issue it has to take seriously. 'But at the same time, I know that the Department of Justice saka ‘yung NBI saka ‘yung AFP are looking into this.' The National Bureau of Investigation arrested five Chinese nationals in Palawan and Manila this week for alleged espionage activities. Authorities noted that they are looking into whether the nabbed Chinese nationals are linked to Deng Yuanqing who was earlier arrested with two other Filipinos also for suspected espionage. Yuanqing's Filipina wife has denied the accusations. China's foreign ministry has played down Philippine concerns on Chinese spies as 'chasing shadows' and to ' earnestly protect the lawful rights and interests of Chinese nationals ' in the country. Foreign Affairs Secretary Enrique Manalo said the department is ready to help the Department of Justice and the Armed Forces of the Philippines 'in whatever way we can'. The Philippines says it will root out more Chinese spies in the country. The statement was made by top security officials following the arrest of a Chinese national who had surveyed sensitive facilities. - ANC, The World Tonight, January 29, 2025 The Philippines says it will root out more Chinese spies in the country. The statement was made by top security officials following the arrest of a Chinese national who had surveyed sensitive facilities. MANILA — A Chinese man allegedly involved in espionage at military sites covered by the Enhanced Defense Cooperation Agreement with the US faces deportation proceedings, the Bureau of Immigaration said. But deportation may come only after he is cleared of any criminal cases that might be filed against him. In a statement, BI said it had obtained details about the unnamed Chinese national who allegedly conducted espionage activities at the EDCA sites. Chinese national, 2 Filipino 'cohorts' arrested for alleged espionage BI Commissioner Joel Anthony Viado said that details about the foreign national were obtained by the agency, such as his travels in and out of the Philippines since 2015. 'The BI Chief said that they will be initiating deportation proceedings against him, to add to the other cases that will be filed against the man locally,' the BI said in the statement. The National of Bureau of Investigation, the Department of Justice and the Armed Forces of the Philippines presented the alleged spy in a press conference Monday and identified the individual as Deng Yuanqing. Suspected Chinese spy, 2 Filipino accomplices arrested The BI also said the alleged spy is supposedly married to a Filipina. 'We will ensure that we will identify any foreign cohorts that might be in the country assisting him,' said Viado. Viado also assured that alleged spies in the country will face serious punishment for their crimes under the administration of President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr. The alleged Chinese spy involved in espionage activities in the country faces deportation proceedings, but only after he is cleared of possible criminal cases that might be filed against him, according to the Bureau of Immigration. MANILA (UPDATE) — The Philippine military and law enforcers on Monday announced the arrest of a Chinese national and 2 Filipino 'cohorts' suspected of conducting espionage activities in the country. Beijing denies 'Chinese sleeper cells' in PH 'Yung nahuli natin, isang Chinese national na based on our open source intelligence, with assistance from our Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP), is a graduate [of a university] controlled and operated by the People's Liberation Army (PLA),' NBI Cybercrime Division chief Jeremy Lotoc said in a press conference. PLA is the military of the Chinese Communist Party and the People's Republic of China. Lotoc said the Chinese 'spy' was a specialist in 'control engineering.' He was arrested while inside a vehicle driven by the 2 Filipino 'cohorts' last January 17 in Makati City. 'Meron na po silang mapa kung saan iikot. In fact, ang plano po nila pagkatapos nilang maikot yung buong Luzon, yung next target nila buong Visayas at Mindanao,' Lotoc added. Chinese na naaresto dahil sa baril at hacking devices, na-inquest na Authorities found in the vehicle some high-tech equipment allegedly used for spying. 'We found out that they have a laser sensor... May GPS tracker, nakakakuha ng frequency sa critical structures tulad ng military bases, police camps. Nagko-collate sila ng data, they have a remote equipment na tina-transfer in real time sa labas ng ating bansa. Alarming in a sense that it's producing coordinates pero puwedeng gamitin to control. Malalaman yung structure natin lalo na yung may interest dito,' Lotoc said. NBI director Jaime Santiago said they received the information about the 'Chinese crew' in December last year. Lotoc called the foreign national a 'sleeper' agent. 'Tinawag namin syang sleeper kasi throughout our monitoring, yung galawan niya talagang nasa normal lang, kaya niyang mag-blend in sa iba't ibang grupo. Hindi po sya pansinin,' he said. The NBI said the Chinese man has been in the Philippines for more or less 5 years. According to AFP chief-of-Staff Gen. Romeo Brawner, Jr., the group led by the alleged Chinese spy had visited areas where Enhanced Defense Cooperation Agreement (EDCA) sites are located. 'A monster': Hontiveros bares POGO ties to China 'espionage, disinformation' ops 'When we looked at the areas that this group went to, nakita namin na mga EDCA sites yung iba, so it's very possible that the coordinates and the topography could be used for military purposes, military targeting purposes,' he said. Brawner believes Chinese espionage began earlier than last year when they arrested a Chinese national for possessing an equipment supposedly used for spying. Cellphone calls can be hacked with device, NTC confirms 'We believe na nagsimula yung espionage ng China even prior to the apprehension of the equipment last year. But nakita po natin just recently yung paggamit po nila ng mga equipment na ito,' he said. Underwater drone found off Masbate may have ‘military applications’: PH Navy The AFP said it was also investigating the possible connection between the arrested Chinese 'spies' to the underwater drones found in different parts of the country. —With reports from Zyann Ambrosio, ABS-CBN News A national security expert has raised concerns over the recent arrests of alleged Chinese spies in the country, emphasizing the potential threat posed by foreign intelligence operations targeting sensitive military information. MANILA -- A national security expert has raised concerns over the recent arrests of alleged Chinese spies in the country, emphasizing the potential threat posed by foreign intelligence operations targeting sensitive military information.

title: Duterte to revive death penalty debates, says close ally
body: Reviving the death penalty in the conservatively Catholic nation 'will not be smooth sailing,' but it it should be placed on legislators' agenda, said Senator Aquilino Pimentel, president of Duterte's PDP Laban party. 'I'm sure dito sa Duterte administration, babalik tayo sa debate sa re-imposition ng death penalty. Babalik tayo doon. In the meantime, meron tayong existing na batas, i-enforce na lang yun,' he told DZMM. 'Wala na kasi natatakot. Under a President Duterte, matakot na sila,' he said. Former president Gloria Arroyo signed a law repealing capital punishment in 2006, just before she flew to the Vatican for an audience with Pope Benedict XVI and as she grappled with waning public support due to an election fraud scandal. 'Hindi po yan smooth sailing,' Pimentel said of the possible death penalty discussions. 'Kasi kung magsu-survey ka sa mga senador diyan, hati-hati ang opinyon, wala pong unity diyan sa death penalty. At least, ma-agenda, mapapagusapan,' he added. Duterte has vowed a ' bloody war ' against criminals, particularly drug dealers, as he sought to bring to the entire country the anti-crime drive that he said, made Davao attractive to investors. The tough-talking mayor will also seek a review of juvenile delinquency laws and push for the death penalty for drug-related crimes, Pimentel said. Duterte will crack the whip on high-profile detainees who have turned their jail cells into extravagant dwellings, the senator said. 'Ang nangayri sa atin, the jailer has lost control of his or her jail,' he said. Successive raids on the national penitentiary since late 2014 have revealed that inmates have built bars and recording studios in jail. Some were even found to have been keeping inflatable sex dolls. MANILA - Presumptive president Rodrigo Duterte will revive debates on the re-imposition of capital punishment to deliver on his campaign promise to fight crime relentlessly, a close ally said Saturday.

title: ‘Pagod na rin ang mga tao’: Iglesia ends anti-corruption rally after 2 days
body: INC spokesperson Edwil Zabala made the announcement Monday night, saying the members who attended the rally at the Quirino Grandstand in Manila are already tired. He also said they do not need to finish the planned three-day rally for them to relay their call for truth, justice, and accountability. The second day of the rally drew at least 500,000 INC members, according to the Manila Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office. As of 9:52 p.m., several groups that joined the rally were seen leaving the venue. Those who camped overnight were also seen packing their belongings and preparing to go home. The manpower team of the production was also dismantling the stage and other technical equipment. The Manila LGU had begun collecting trash from the venue, and cleanup was still ongoing. The rally, which began Sunday, November 16, was originally scheduled to conclude Tuesday, November 18. Scores of construction firm owners, government officials and lawmakers have been accused of pocketing funds for substandard or so-called ghost infrastructure projects. The crowd estimate at INC's rally on Sunday peaked to more than 600,000, according to police. The religious sect is a church that has historically been a powerful voting bloc with ties to the Duterte political dynasty. Religious group Iglesia ni Cristo (INC) on Monday announced it will be ending its anti-corruption rally a day early. MANILA — Religious group Iglesia ni Cristo (INC) on Monday announced it will end its rally a day early.

title: Like the 'Excelsior Pass': Philippines eyes digital proof of COVID-19 vaccination
body: Nograles said the Department of Information and Communications Technology is working on a 'common vaccination digital ID with QR code' for Filipinos. 'What we want really is a QR code, digital...The digital vaccination ID card will be very important,' he told ANC's Headstart. Currently, local government units in the Philippines issue cards to individuals who get inoculated with COVID-19 vaccines. The card contains information on the brand of vaccine used, how many doses the person has received and when it was given. The cards vary per city and municipality. The Philippines is among several countries that asked the World Health Organization to come up with a standard protocol for a digital 'common vaccine passport.' Nograles said the interagency task force on COVID-19 is discussing 'what advantages or what sort of incentives or new protocols' will be observed for fully vaccinated individuals. Among the incentives being floated is the waiving of RT-PCR tests for tourist trips, he said. The task force is also monitoring how other countries are doing vaccine passports. A small group headed by the Department of Foreign Affairs has also been tapped to look into ways how the government can verify the vaccination of those coming in from abroad, he said. They are expected to present their proposal within the week. The Philippines still bans travelers from India, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, Nepal, United Arab Emirates, and Oman this month as it aims to keep out the Delta variant of COVID-19. In a separate interview, Philippine ambassador to the US Jose Manuel Romualdez said the Philippines could adapt a system similar to New York state's Excelsior Pass, introduced as the first and only government-issued vaccine passport in the US that assigns a QR code for every individual vaccinated. He noted that the electronic record would be difficult to duplicate since each QR code would show when and how many COVID-19 vaccine doses that individual has received. 'They said they will get this done in the next couple of weeks because a lot of people are complaining...We all need vaccination passports,' he said. He said that in New York, some restaurants refuse entry to people who have not been vaccinated or do not have vaccine passports. 'It is very convenient. Almost everybody has a cellphone, it is easy and very efficient,' he said. As of Sunday, the Philippines has a total of 1,359,015 coronavirus infections, with 57,679 still active. There have also been 23,621 reported deaths and 1,277,715 recoveries. The country is also rolling out vaccination, with 14 million doses already arriving. Some 7.5 million doses have been administered. According to a monitoring by the ABS-CBN Investigative and Research Group, about 2 million individuals have received their second dose The Philippine government is working on digitalizing a common ID that would prove an individual's vaccination against COVID-19, Cabinet Sec. Karlo Nograles said Monday. MANILA - The Philippine government is working on a common digital ID that would serve as proof of an individual's vaccination against COVID-19, Cabinet Secretary Karlo Nograles said Monday.

title: The ANC Brief: Oct. 2, 2018
body: Rising fuel prices A new round of fuel price increases is sure to put more pressure on prices of goods. LPG prices will increase by P2.35 per kilo. Prices of the socially sensitive diesel will rise by P1.35, with gasoline going up by P1. How much more can we take? Dubai crude breached the P80 level for the first time since 2014. Meanwhile, the peso weakened to P54.11 despite a recent rate hike of 50 basis points. In the PSEi, traders are cautiously waiting for Friday’s latest inflation data. The BSP already said last week that inflation could quicken to 6.8 percent, with a range of 6.3 to 7.1 so the market could already be factoring in these numbers in this week’s trading. Clearly, all this caution and hesitation is weighing on the market which is still stuck in the 7100 to 7200 level with no possible positive triggers in the horizon. Analysts said many issues are already in attractive levels, but high inflation is still dampening appetites in the market. Budget Sec. Ben Diokno will be on ANC’s Headstart. He had said the Development Budget Coordinating Committee will be meeting to discuss possible changes to the government’s forecasts for economic growth and inflation. Fiscal regime The House of Representative will hold a hearing 9 a.m. on establishing a fiscal regime for the mining industry. In the last hearing, government and the mining industry were deadlocked on a bill for the local extractive sector. Developments in this front are likely to impact listed mining issues. ANC will monitor developments on this sensitive industry. Arrogance and comeuppance ACTS-OFW partylist Rep. Aniceto Bertiz looks like he’s in a bad place right now. First, he could be facing an ethics case in Congress. Next, he stirred a hornet’s nest again when in a press conference where he apologized for confronting an airport security screener, he said his 'monthly period' was causing him stress and affecting his temper. Gabriela was quick to lash out at Bertiz, saying he was making an excuse for his arrogance by stereotyping women having their monthly period. Special Assistant to the President Bong Go, whose name Bertiz had dropped, looks like said abusive officials would not be tolerated. An OFW group also recounted its members' bad experience with Bertiz in Hong Kong last year. Migrante chair Arman Hernando will be on ANC’s Early Edition to tell us what happened. New player The DICT has a 2 p.m. event and local companies vying to be selected as the new major telco player could be revealed, including their foreign partners. There has been a lot of interest on this in the PSEi so expect the event to affect the stock market. Presidential appearance Fresh from very good survey numbers, President Duterte will appear at a 3 p.m. event in Catarman, Northern Samar with former rebels. ANC will be prepared to air his with probable topics such as the peace talks with the CPP-NPA, an explanation for his 'only sin' statement, and others. Fighting back Sen. Antonio Trillanes is scheduled to give a privilege speech at 3 p.m. where he might come out with guns blazing against the administration. ANC will be prepared to air this speech as he is expected to talk about the nullification of his amnesty. Another probable issue is his promise to summon government officials to the Senate on alleged anomalies. More troubles are in the horizon for party-list Rep. Aniceto Bertiz. More troubles are in the horizon for party-list Rep. Aniceto Bertiz. Here are the stories ANC will be covering today:

title: UN special rapporteur Singhateh to meet with DOJ chief Remulla: spox
body: DOJ spokesperson Asec. Mico Clavano said Singhateh will visit their office on Wednesday, Dec. 7, following Remulla's invitation. 'The purpose of the meeting is for Secretary Remulla to express his gratitude to the UN representative for visiting the Philippines and to answer any questions she may still have after her visit to the different local governments around the country,' Clavano said in a statement. The Justice spokesperson added that Remulla wants to give Singhateh 'a more macro perspective' of the online sexual abuse and exploitation of children situation. Remulla will also explain the role of the national government in putting an end to criminal activities involving minors, said Clavano. 'We are looking forward to the UN Special Rapporteur’s visit to DOJ. Even more, we believe that this visit will provide more momentum in our war against OSAEC (Online Sexual Abuse and Exploitation of Children),' Clavano said. Singhateh's visit is the first by a UN special rapporteur under the administration of President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos, Jr. and the first in the country in seven years. In 2016, former President Rodrigo Duterte canceled a scheduled 2017 visit by then-UN special rapporteur on extra-judicial, summary or arbitrary executions Agnes Callamard after a public word war over thousands of killings in connection with his bloody drug war. MANILA — UN special rapporteur on the sale and sexual exploitation of children Mama Fatima Singhateh is set meet with Department of Justice Secretary Jesus Crispin 'Boying' Remulla next week, an official confirmed Saturday.

title: 'Less in life, more in law': PNP chief urges cops to find balance in quarantine rule implementation
body: Philippine National Police (PNP) chief Gen. Archie Gamboa made the remark after Mang Dodong, a fisherman from Caloocan, was detained for nearly 2 weeks because he could not produce a quarantine pass for Navotas, where he went to fish to make a living during the strict lockdown. 'I will find the details of Mang Dodong and we will issue a directive to balance--we don’t really know the circumstances sa baba--we will encourage our policemen to balance between those who have less in life, sabi nga nila, must have more in law,' he told ANC. (I will find the details of Mang Dodong and we will issue a directive to balance--we don’t really know the circumstances on the ground--we will encourage our policemen to balance between those who have less in life, as they say, must have more in law.) 'Generally ang guidelines namin sa kapulisan, you have to balance sometimes yung humanitarian aspect as against kailan mo iimpose ang law.' (Generally our guideline to policemen is you have to balance sometimes the job's humanitarian aspect as against when you'll impose the law.) Gamboa said the PNP updates everyday its list of authorized persons allowed outside their residence, which includes frontliners. The PNP also previously gave warnings to quarantine violators as lockdown measures were imposed in some areas, he said. Policemen are urged to 'balance' the implementation of law on the poor during the COVID-19 pandemic, the country's top cop said Monday. MANILA - Policemen are urged to 'balance' the implementation of law on the poor during the COVID-19 pandemic, the country's top cop said on Monday. Policemen are urged to 'balance' the implementation of law on the poor during the COVID-19 pandemic, the country's top cop said Monday.

title: Philhealth to abide by SC decision on controversial transfer of unused funds to government
body: 'Sa issue po noong 89.7 billion ho na binanggit ninyo, sa kasalukuyan po ay mayroon na pong proseso na nangyayari sa Supreme Court. We respect the process that the Supreme Court is undergoing and we will abide by whatever ruling the Supreme Court will have,' Dr. Edwin Mercado told Palace reporters in a Malacanang briefing Wednesday. Mercado’s statement comes as the Supreme Court begins oral arguments on the transfer of unused Philhealth funds which petitioners argued is unconstitutional. The new Philhealth chief also vowed to 'heal' the various 'illnesses' plaguing the state health insurer, including issues of inefficiency and wastage. 'Ako bilang manggagamot, parang tinitingnan ko rin po ang PhilHealth na may sakit at inaalam ko rin po, ano iyong lunas. So, iyon po iyong aking unang kailangang gawin – aralin pong mabuti kung ano po iyong mga prosesong kailangang baguhin para po iyong directive po ng ating Presidente, mahal na Presidente na tuluy-tuloy ang serbisyo at palalawigin pa natin iyong benepisyo,' Mercado, a US-trained physician, medical researcher, and longtime hospital administrator said in the same briefing. Mercado was sworn in by President Marcos Jr amid various controversies faced by Philhealth, including its supposed inefficiency in delivering healthcare benefits to members. 'Ang objective po natin ay mapalawig iyong benepisyo at hindi ma-deter o hindi ho maapektuhan iyong pagbibigay ng ating serbisyo,' he said. 'So, ang una pong kailangan gawin nga po is the financial reporting para po mas malinis, mas standard – iyon po, medyo mas madali po iyon dahil parang chart of accounts ang idi-define. Kapag mayroon na ho tayong datos iyon na po iyong pagbabasehan natin – apple to apple po tayo moving forward.' The official says he will look into addressing the billions of pesos on unpaid claims which Philhealth currently owes hospitals, by improving the claims process and prioritizing digitization. 'Una po naming tututukan ay ano ho ba iyong pinaka-reason bakit nari-return to hospital at ano ho iyong reason bakit bumabagal iyong claims processing,' he said. 'Hopefully nga po ay makapaglabas kami ng mga panibagong procedure para iyong mga causes ay ma-identify at iyong mga minor lang naman ay maituloy kaagad. So, iyon po ay hindi lang prospectively kung hindi also retroactive – so, iyong mga datihang denied claims ay aaralin po namin; kung ano po naman iyong puwede nang mabayaran kung ang basehan po ay mai-set namin na in compliance din po siyempre sa COA rules,' he continued. 'Iyon pong digitization ay talaga pong ipu-push namin kasi iyon po ang long-term solution natin for claims adjudication, at saka iyong data para malaman po natin magkano po talaga ang kailangan nating pondo na pagbabasehan natin ng pagba-budget.' The new President and CEO of the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation says they will abide by the Supreme Court decision on the controversial transfer of Philhealth’s excess funds to the National Treasury. MANILA -- The new President and CEO of the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation says they will abide by the Supreme Court decision on the controversial transfer of Philhealth’s excess funds to the National Treasury.

title: Barrier at viral Mt. Fuji photo spot to be replaced after holes found
body: The new screen will be made of stronger material and possibly changed to blue or green, as black 'has a negative image,' Mayor Hideyuki Watanabe of Fujikawaguchiko, Yamanashi Prefecture, said at a press conference. After setting up the screen on May 21 to prevent visitors from flocking to the site in front of a Lawson convenience store in the town, local authorities confirmed the first hole the following day, with the number increasing since. 'It is disappointing to see a lack of morals' among those making the holes, Watanabe said. On Tuesday, the town put up a sign in English requesting that visitors not touch the screen and mentions that it will be repaired as necessary. The area has become a popular photo spot for foreign tourists after the sight of Japan's highest mountain towering over the convenience store went viral online. However, the surge in visitors drew complaints from residents due to an uptick in people jaywalking and illegally parking vehicles in the area. The town decided to install the screen after stationing security guards and posting warnings in English to manage the crowds did little to improve the situation. A black screen set up by a central Japan town to block a scenic view of Mt. Fuji following troublesome behavior by tourists will be replaced after multiple holes were found in it, the town's mayor said Thursday. KOFU, Japan - A black screen set up by a central Japan town to block a scenic view of Mt. Fuji following troublesome behavior by tourists will be replaced after multiple holes were found in it, the town's mayor said Thursday.

title: MARINA suspends passenger ship safety certificate of ill-fated ferry
body: MARINA has suspended the passenger ship safety certificate (PSSC) of a ferry that caught fire off Bohol over the weekend. The MV Esperanza Star caught fire off Doljo Point in the Bohol Sea early Sunday morning. In a statement, MARINA said the incident has put in question the ship's seaworthiness. 'The suspension... shall remain effective and may be restored/ reinstated after a satisfactory result of MARINA survey and/or evaluation of all available data surrounding the incident,' the statement added. At least 127 people were rescued after the ship caught fire as it traveled from Iligan City to Tagbilaran in Bohol. Authorities are still looking into the cause of the fire. MANILA -- The Maritime Industry Authority (MARINA) has suspended the passenger ship safety certificate (PSSC) of a ferry that caught fire off Bohol over the weekend.

title: US government shutdown becomes longest in history
body: Federal agencies have been grinding to a halt since Congress failed to approve funding past September 30, and the pain has been mounting as welfare programs -- including aid that helps millions of Americans afford groceries -- hang in limbo. There were in recent days the first fragile signs of progress in Congress in the search for an off-ramp, although -- for now -- 1.4 million federal workers, from air traffic controllers to park wardens, remain on enforced leave or working without pay. Hours before the shutdown record toppled at midnight, the Trump administration sounded the alarm over turmoil at airports nationwide if the crisis drags beyond a sixth week, with worsening staff shortages snarling airports and closing down sections of airspace. 'So if you bring us to a week from today, Democrats, you will see mass chaos...you will see mass flight delays,' Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy told a news conference in Philadelphia. 'You'll see mass cancellations, and you may see us close certain parts of the airspace, because we just cannot manage it because we don't have the air traffic controllers.' Thanksgiving air travel is expected to set a new record this year, the American Automobile Association (AAA) projected -- with 5.8 million people set to fly domestically over the November 27 holiday. More than 60,000 air traffic controllers and Transportation Security Administration (TSA) officers are working without pay, and the White House has warned that increased absenteeism could mean chaos at check-in lines. Airport workers calling in sick rather than working without pay -- leading to significant delays -- was a major factor in Trump bringing an end to the 2019 shutdown. Both Democrats and Republicans remain unwavering however over the main sticking point in the current stoppage -- health care spending. Democrats say they will only provide votes to end the funding lapse after a deal has been struck to extend expiring insurance subsidies that make health care affordable for millions of Americans. But Republicans insist they will only address health care once Democrats have voted to switch the lights back on in Washington. While both sides' leadership have shown little appetite for compromise, there have been signs of life on the back benches, with a handful of moderate Democrats working to find an escape hatch. A separate bipartisan group of four centrist House members unveiled a compromise framework Monday for lowering health insurance costs. Democrats believe that millions of Americans seeing skyrocketing premiums as they enroll into health insurance programs for next year will pressure Republicans into seeking compromise. But Trump has held firm on refusing to negotiate, telling CBS News in an interview broadcast Sunday that he would 'not be extorted.' The president has sought to apply his own pressure to force Democrats to cave by threatening mass layoffs of federal workers and using the shutdown to target progressive priorities. Trump on Tuesday repeated his administration's threat to cut off a vital aid program that helps 42 million Americans pay for groceries for the first time in its more than 60-year history, even though the move was blocked by two courts. The White House later clarified, however, that it was 'fully complying' with its legal obligations and was working to get partial SNAP payments 'out the door as much as we can and as quickly as we can.' The US government shutdown became the longest ever Wednesday, topping the 35-day record set during Donald Trump's first term, as his administration warned of holiday air travel chaos and threatened Americans' benefits in a bid to force a resolution.

title: Half of local roads nationwide unpaved, says lawmaker
body: During the budget briefing of the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH), Deputy Minority Leader Bernadette Herrera noted that according to the agency's website, all national roads were already while 54.7 percent of local roads were still unpaved '[Around] 95,127 kilometers pa ang badly in need of paving. When you allocated [funds] per region, did you take this into consideration?' Herrera said. DPWH Secretary Manuel Bonoan noted that his agency was responsible for national roads while local governments oversee local roads. 'At this point in time what we can provide, support, is actually through the convergence programs and of course yung allocations under the legislative districts for the local projects,' he said. 'But we cannot put in our core program the paving of local roads because...under Executive Order 138, these are already devolved to the LGU.' Earlier in the briefing, Bonoan reported to the House Appropriations Committee that the agency's budget will go down 8 percent in 2024 from this year's budget. The DPWH has P822.2 billion in the 2024 National Expenditure Program, down from P894.2 billion in the 2023 General Appropriations Act. Capital outlays in particular will be cut by 11 percent to P778.5 billion in 2024. MANILA — A minority lawmaker on Thursday raised concern over official data that half of local roads remained unpaved.

title: 'Dahil sa yosi break': Lalaking muntik maiwan ng high-speed train sumabit
body: Ayon kay railways spokesman Herbert Hofer, papunta ng Vienna ang railjet train mula Zurich, Switzerland nang huminto ito sa Sankt Poelten para sa isang scheduled stop. Dito na bumaba ang lalaki sa station para manigarilyo. Sa ulat ng Austrian tabloid Heute, tumalon ang lalaki sa gitna ng 2 carriages ng tren nang bigla itong mag-umpisa ulit sa biyahe. Dito na siya kumatok nang ilang beses sa mga bintana upang makaakit ng atensyon na nagresulta sa pag-activate ng emergency brake bago isinakay ng mga tripulante ng tren ang lalaki. 'It is irresponsible, this kind of thing usually ends up with someone dying. And you're not just putting yourself in danger, if you end up under the train there's rescuers, there's police, fire service that come,' ani Hoffer. Pinagalitan ng konduktor ang lalaki na isang 24-anyos na Algerian. Kinuha din ng pulis ang lalaki pagdating ng tren sa Meidling station sa Vienna para sa masusing imbestigasyon. Noong Enero, isang 40-anyos na Hungarian na lalaki ang nakaligtas sa pagkapit sa isang German high-speed na tren sa loob ng 32 kilometro pagkatapos din nitong umalis bago niya maubos ang kanyang sigarilyo. Buhay ang isang lalaki na sumabit sa labas ng isang Austrian high-speed train matapos umanong muntik maiwan ng tren dahil nagyosi pa siya. VIENNA, Austria — Himalang nakaligtas ang isang lalaki na sumabit sa labas ng isang Austrian high-speed train matapos umanong muntik maiwan ng tren dahil nagyosi pa siya.

title: Mga deboto patuloy na dumarami sa Quezon Boulevard
body: Kanya-kanyang paraan ng panata ang ilan sa mga nakausap nating deboto. May grupo ng mga health care workers gaya ni Kathleen de Leon na namigay ng libreng burger, tubig at mga candies para sa mga deboto. Ayon kay De Leon, isang dekada na nila ito ginagawa dahil hindi nila kaya ang sumampa ng andas. Si Juan Victor naman, 15 taon nang deboto. Mula sa Quirino Grandstand ay naglakad nang nakayapak papunta sa Quiapo. Una na niyang nasilayan ang andas sa Ayala Bridge kanina, pero nais pa niyang makalapit at makitang makauwi sa Minor Basilica ang Hesus Nazareno bilang bahagi ng kanyang panata. Una niyang idinasal dati na gumaling sana ang ama na may cancer pero binawian rin ito ng buhay. Gayunpaman, mas lalo lang ang lumakas ang kanyang pananampalataya at panata kay Hesus Nazareno, para naman ngayon sa mga natitira pa niyang mahal sa buhay 'Nakasama namin ng matagal pero ayun nga, nawala rin sya... Inaalay ko na ito ngayon sa mga natitira ko pang mahal sa buhay.' Si De Leon naman, 10 taong nang deboto ng Nazareno. 'Kami pong mga high school friends ito, through the years mas dumarami ang nagdodonate po sa amin, may nagbibigay din po sa amin from abroad tapos pinapamigay namin. Ito po yung panata namin, na makatulong sa iba pang mga namanata na napagod.' May ilang mga dayuhan rin gaya ng Amerikano na si Doug Casper mula Atlanta ang pumunta pa sa Pilipinas, para masaksihan itong taunang traslacion. Ito na aniya ang pangalawa niyang beses na nakilahok sa Pista ng Nazareno. Sa bawat metro na nakakalapit ang andas dito sa Minor Basilica of Jesus Nazareno ay lalong ramdam ang emosyon at debosyon ng mga deboto. Hindi nila alintana ang puyat, init at pagod, at tila ay mas lalong nagkakalakas pa, sa bawat lapit ng andas dito. Handa anila silang hintayin na makabalik dito sa simbahan ang andas. Iba-iba man ang kwento, kanya-kanyang panata, pero iisa lang ang sigaw ngayon ng ating mga kapamilya sa lugar: Viva Poong Hesus Nazareno Habang palubog ang araw ay parami nang parami ang mga deboto ngayon na nakapuwesto dito sa kahabaan ng Quezon Boulevard, sabik na masilayan at malapitan ang andas sakay ang Poong Hesus Nazareno. Bitbit nila dito sa lugar ang mga dasal ng pasasalamat at mga hiling para sa pamilya. MAYNILA — Habang palubog ang araw ay parami nang parami ang mga deboto ngayon na nakapuwesto dito sa kahabaan ng Quezon Boulevard, sabik na masilayan at malapitan ang andas sakay ang Poong Hesus Nazareno. Bitbit nila dito sa lugar ang mga dasal ng pasasalamat at mga hiling para sa pamilya.

title: After second upset, Bayan Muna says fight will continue beyond polls
body: Under the party-list system, party-lists that fail to get at least two percent of votes in two consecutive elections are delisted. Groups may register again or register under a different name. 'We extend our deepest gratitude to the thousands of Filipinos who voted for and stood with Bayan Muna in the recent elections. In the face of overwhelming odds, your votes are not just numbers—they are declarations of resistance, solidarity, and hope for genuine change,' the party-list said in a Facebook post Tuesday, May 13. Bayan Muna had been representing ordinary Filipinos at the House since 2001, often as a member of the minority bloc and of the political opposition. The party-list consistently won seats until the 2022 elections, where they received only 0.60% of total votes. An attempt to return to the lower chamber in 2025, saw Bayan Muna ranking 76th with 0.39% of votes, according to partial, unofficial results based on 97.28% of election results. 'Our commitment to serve the Filipino people continues—inside or outside Congress. The struggle for land, wages, rights, and justice cannot be confined within parliamentary walls,' Bayan Muna said. 'Hindi natatapos sa halalan ang laban. Ang tunay na pagbabago ay makakamit sa pagkakaisa at kolektibong pagkilos ng mamamayan.' (The struggle does not end with the elections. We will get real change through unity and the collective action of the people) Bayan Muna, in their post, highlighted the red-tagging and disinformation attacks toward the party-list and its nominees. 'The vilification, red-tagging, harassment, fake news, and disinformation campaigns targeted against us only exposed the desperation of those in power to silence the voice of dissent and suppress the will of the marginalized,' the post read. On Sunday, the Commission on Elections had to issue a clarification to address false claims that the party-list had been disqualified. 'Mas tumindi ang panunupil para patahimikin ang progresibong tinig—pero mas lalong tumibay ang ating paninindigan,' Bayan Muna added. (The repression of progressive voices intensified — but our resolve has also hardened) Bayan Muna on Tuesday questioned reported irregularities in the automated counting machines (ACMs), which they said 'raise serious concerns about the credibility and integrity of the electoral process.' It also pointed out that the country’s party-list system, which used to be a representation of the marginalized, has become 'thoroughly corrupted' by the domination of political dynasties, billionaires, and bureaucrats. Election watchdog Kontra Daya in February this year said that more than half of the party-lists that ran in the 2025 elections are either related to political dynasties, associated with big business, related to the police or military, with dubious advocacies, or have insufficient information. Bayan Muna challenged the newly elected party-lists to be 'genuine representatives of the people'—to stand against corruption, militarization, and to uphold the rights of the marginalized. The party-list vowed to continue representing the Filipino masses, outside Congress. 'Bayan Muna will continue to be a voice for the voiceless, a defender of the oppressed, and a staunch advocate of genuine social change,' it said. It is the end of the two-decades-long representation for the Bayan Muna party-list after it failed to grab a House seat in the 2025 midterm elections. MANILA — Bayan Muna party-list will need to register again if it wants to participate in the next elections after being projected to not get a seat in the 20th Congress, the second time for the pioneering party-list since the 2022 polls.

title: Slow chamber? 'Quality not quantity,' says Koko
body: 'Let us not judge lawmaking in terms of the number of laws passed but in terms of how the laws we pass improve the quality of life on earth in general and the quality of life of Filipinos in particular,' the Senate leader said. 'Again, I say, change mindset. Quality not quantity,' he said in a statement. Pimentel made the comment in response to his ally House Speaker Pantaleon Alvarez's recent remarks calling the Senate 'mabagal na kapulungan' (slow chamber). 'Siguro kailangang bilis-bilisan para 'yung gustong gawin ng ating Pangulo ay mas mailatag na. 'Yung institusyon mabagal na talaga,' Alvarez told ANC in an interview Wednesday. For House Speaker Pantaleon Alvarez ( @speakeralvarez ), Senate as an institution is moving slowly. #ANCRecap #ANCHeadstart pic.twitter.com/DQRUp1EJVh The Senate President, however, said the Senate is the 'nag-aaral na kapulungan' or the chamber that studies matters well. He also noted that more bills would naturally come from the House of Representatives since the chamber handles local measures. 'All bills- renaming and or merging schools, increasing hospital beds, etc.- must and do come from the House. But we must all change our mindset—from quantity to quality,' Pimentel said. President Rodrigo Duterte has signed into law more than 10 Senate bills, including the tax reform law , the free higher education act , and laws extending the validity of the passport to 10 years and the driver's license to 5 years. Pimentel's comments come after House Speaker Pantaleon Alvarez called the Senate 'mabagal na kapulungan' or slow chamber. MANILA - Senate President Aquilino Pimentel III on Wednesday defended his chamber against criticism of its 'slow' pace, saying legislative performance should be measured in terms of the quality and not the quantity of laws passed. Pimentel's comments come after House Speaker Pantaleon Alvarez called the Senate 'mabagal na kapulungan' or slow chamber.

title: De Lima says inmates hurt in riot forced to testify against her
body: 'All of these na mga nasaktan at namatay nung alleged riot na yun, yung incidente na yun, recent incident, these are those na hindi pa nila napipilit to also testify against me,' she said on ANC's Headstart. For this, she said, she understands why Sebastian is pursuing a case against her, and supposes that it is because he 'also feels the pressure now because his life is under threat.' 'As you know, matagal na siyang pinipilit, kinukumbinsi nila Secretary [Vitaliano] Aguirre and his operators to also testify against me, like Colanggo, but hindi siya napipilit. I also had info about that, very credible info, merong text pa nga. Hindi siya napipilit, hindi siya nakukumbinsi,' she said. [As you know, Secretary Aguirre and his operators have long been forcing him, convincing him to testify against me, but they couldn't coerce him. I also had information on that, a very credible information with text messages. He couldn't be forced, he couldn't be convinced.] 'And then may nangyaring ganun, muntik mamatay; may namatay pa,' she said. [And then something like that happens where he almost dies; where someone has died.] According to Sebastian, he was stabbed by a Tomas Doniña, alias Tom, between 7:00 to 8:00 in morning in the mess hall of Building 14 of NBP last September 28. He added that he was watching TV when he was stabbed from behind with an ice pick. Sebastian also said in his statement that he talked with Doniña and a certain Clarence Dongail before the stabbing incident. De Lima said, 'only they know' what truly happened there, but 'they must have been instructed to do that because clearly, there was an attempt on the life of these Bilibid 19 inmates who still refuse to testify against me.' She said, perhaps the concession for Sebastian not being fatally wounded was to sue her. Otherwise, she said, he should have pursued the case a long time ago. 'He should have done that the moment I agreed with the proposal of Director General Rainier Cruz to also transfer him to Bilibid 19 because it’s getting to be an issue daw. It was no longer defensible na naka-hiwalay din si Sebastian,' she said. 'Alam na nila dati yan na yung information could have been…could have come from, some of the information emanated from Jaybee Sebastian. So why sue me only now? Sila Colanggo [Herbert Colanggo] and others previously sued me for transferring them to NBI Detention Facility from Bilibid,' she added. Sebastian's legal counsel Eduardo Arriba said that 'because of De Lima's revelation that Sebastian was an asset, inmates in Building 14 [of the New Bilibid Prison] were enraged, thus his purported killing on September 28.' They are set to file a complaint against De Lima before the Office of the Ombudsman for 'gross inexcusable negligence' and violations of the anti-torture law. But De Lima clarified that Sebastian was not his personal asset as was reported. 'First, there was a report, he was my personal asset. I could not have any personal asset because I’m not into intelligence gathering. I was just a recipient of intelligence info,' she said. She said, former Bureau of Corrections (BuCor) chief Franklin Bucayu and Presidential Anti-Organized Crime Commission Executive Director Reginald Villasanta were the ones giving her information. 'They told me, these two former officials, told me that one of the sources of their information was Sebastian. So, in a sense, he was an asset, but he was not my personal asset. He was never close to me,' she said. 'All these trash, all this crap about that story about me frequenting his kubol, staying there for three hours, making it appear as if I was a sex worker visiting the kubol of high-profile convicts is total trash,' she said. She maintained, she never went to Sebastian's kubol inside the New Bilibid Prison alone, but had 'I passed, about once or twice' in the course of her term as Justice Secretary, when she conducted surprise inspections. 'Before and after that major raid on December 15, 2014, I had several of those surprise inspections. So necessarily, I would be going into certain places, including those so-called kubols, which were dismantled after December 15,' she said, adding that she was with BuCor and Bilibid personnel and her security detail when she went. Senator Leila de Lima claimed Thursday that the convicts who figured in the fatal stabbing incident in the national penitentiary last month were the ones who refused to testify against her.

title: Comelec begins destruction of 6.7M unused ballots
body: Commisisoner Rey Bulay, commissioner-in-charge of the Committee on Ballot Printing, said this is in compliance with the law that says all unused ballots and other accountable forms must be disposed properly. 'Wala po kaming itinagong proseso sa inyo….hanggang i-deploy ang mga balota - ngayon po hanggang sunugin natin yung mga hindi valid na ballots para po hindi magamit kahit kailan na eleksyon,' Bulay told reporters. According to Bulay, this process of ballot destruction should be done within 10 days. 'Saan nga dadalhin? Sa tunawan sa Pampanga. 'Yun po yung last step,' Bulay explained. Comelec data showed a total of 6,730,969 unused official ballots and 32,680 accountable forms slated for disposal and destruction. Bulay explained the poll body did not spend a single centavo for the unused ballots and other accountable forms. 'We only pay for ballots that are valid, the good ones. Pero itong mga ito hindi binabayaran,' Bulay said. 'It will be regarded as losses ng production, chargeable to the provider — ganon yung contract namin with the provider. We pay only for the good ones,' Bulay added. Meantime, Commissioner Bulay confirmed he has already written the resolution on the pending case against the Duterte Youth party-list and it only needs the signatures of other commissioners of the second division. Bulay clarified the case had been pending even before the previous commission and the present composition of the poll body has no intention to delay its decision. 'Nag-commit naman si Chairman na before July 1, dapat matapos namin majority ng mga issues. Kaya po sa tingin ko, kung natatagalan po yun, hindi naman po yun intentional. Madami ding kaso na nasa amin, nakabinbin,' Bulay added. The Commission on Elections began the process of disposal and destruction of more than 6.7 million unused official ballots for the 2025 midterm polls. MANILA — The Commission on Elections began the process of disposal and destruction of more than 6.7 million unused official ballots for the 2025 midterm polls.

title: Doc Willie Ong formally withdraws from Senate race
body: Ong, who is battling cancer, earlier said he was backing out of the Senate race to focus on his health. 'Kaya po nagwi-withdraw si Doc Willie, kasi may ilan pa po kaming mga sessions so gusto niya kasi hindi ma-short change ang sambayanan, so sabi niya sige magwi-withdraw muna ako kasi may few treatments pa,' Dra. Ong said. According to Dra. Ong, Doc Willie can no longer do the campaigning because of his medical condition. 'Una kasi kaya niya aabot yung treatment namin bago magsimula yung 100 days ng kampanya, pero nagsimula na po eh hindi pa pala kami tapos so napag-isipan niya, matagal din niyang pinag-isipan na magwi-withdraw siya,' Dra. Ong explained. 'Ang schedule kasi namin, 10 days here in Manila ‘pag wala kaming treatment and 10 days in Singapore para naman po hindi lugi yung tao na hindi kami nakakaikot,' Dra. Ong added. She said Doc Willie’s condition improved after completing his chemotherapy. 'May good news po ako sa inyo, tapos na po ng chemotherapy si Doc, immuno therapy na lang tayo, tsaka targeted therapy. Yung dati pong 100 percent na sarcoma niya, from our last PET scan, 10 to 15 percent na lang yung natitira and magpe-pet scan po kami….2 weeks again so hopefully mag-zero na po yun,' Dra. Ong told the media. She also thanked all those who prayed for Doc Willie’s speedy recovery. 'Sa mga supporters, kundi dahil sa prayers nyo hindi gagaling si Doc Willie eh. Don ko nakita ang powerful pala ng dasal kasi considered miracle na po yung nakuha namin,' Dra. Ong said. Dra. Ong was greeted by Comelec commissioner Ernesto Maceda Jr at the Palacio del Gobernador in Intramuros, Manila. Maceda said Doc Willie was his classmate since their preparatory school days. He acknowledged the gesture of Dra. Ong as a sign of respect to the country’s electoral process. 'It is a manifestation of his respect to the electoral process, his respect for the position and most of all, his respect to the Filipino people. So we from the Comelec send our wishes and prayers for his speedy recovery,' Maceda said. Like other senatorial candidates who withdrew their candidacy, Maceda said Doc Willie’s name would remain in the official ballot for the May polls. 'Yes his name will remain in the ballot, in the case of Dr. Ong, if through a special power, Dr. Liza is here then we are going to accept their withdrawal because nobody is compelled to run up to the last day you can still withdraw,' according to Maceda. Maceda said candidates who withdrew from the race still had to file their Statement of Contribution and Expenditures (SOCE). 'Kailangan pa rin naming malaman yun. Because they are engaged with the public, because if there are donations made and they’re expenditures done just to see if they have stayed within the limits or if those who have contributed to them have themselves filed in their own returns their donations,' he explained. Failure to file SOCE is an election offense, the official added. 'May election offense yan and may possible disqualification from running or from suffrage if you do it 2 consecutive times,' Maceda said. Senatorial candidate Doc Willie Ong formally filed his withdrawal from the 2025 midterm polls Friday through his representatives. MANILA — Senatorial candidate Doc Willie Ong formally filed his withdrawal from the 2025 midterm polls Friday through his representatives.

title: PHOTO: Families grieve as 23 confirmed dead in Thai school bus fire
body: Authorities confirmed that rescuers pulled children's bodies from the charred wreckage of the vehicle in believed to be Thailand’s deadliest road accident. A relative (2nd right) of a student who died in a bus fire covers the eyes of a young relative who was travelling on a separate bus on the same school trip as they walk past the burnt bus wreckage on the outskirts of Bangkok on Wednesday. The head of police forensics confirmed that rescuers pulled children's bodies from the charred wreckage of the vehicle in believed to be Thailand’s deadliest road accident.

title: Pulse Asia: Marcos, Duterte machineries struggled in 2025 elections
body: The research body said in its latest press release that the votes for the Marcos camp notably dropped in Central Luzon, Calabarzon, and its strongholds in Ilocos Region and Eastern Visayas, showing that its machinery failed to mobilize voter support. Meanwhile, the Duterte-aligned 'opposition' saw a diminished performance in Mindanao. Pulse Asia said that the survey results reflected the winning possibility of all the election winners. Eight of the winners were already expected as they topped Pulse Asia's May survey. Meanwhile, the survey body found that several candidates—mostly independents and those affiliated with progressive groups—received higher actual votes than their pre-election survey standings. Candidates like Bam Aquino, who ranked second in the actual election results, performed similarly to non-winners in the surveys. In Pulse Asia's May survey, for instance, Aquino was favored by 32.8 percent of the respondents, closely similar to Bong Revilla with 32.3 percent. 'These small differences are even more pronounced in the case of the lower-ranked candidates,' said Pulse Asia. But it also said that a comparison of the vote shares from the survey results in April and May, and the actual election results revealed that voter preferences shifted markedly over time, particularly as election day approached. Aside from Aquino, other unexpected election winners—Kiko Pangilinan, Rodante Marcoleta, Erwin Tulfo and Lito Lapid—were already showing a positive trajectory in the pre-election surveys. The Pulse Asia data also showed a region's preference over time, compared to the actual election results. 'From these trends, it appears that the political machinery of both the Marcos administration and the Duterte opposition struggled to sustain or expand support for their respective candidates,' it said. Pulse Asia Research said that both the Marcos and Duterte political machineries struggled to impact public opinion in #Halalan 2025. MANILA — Pulse Asia Research pointed out that both the Marcos and Duterte political machineries struggled to impact public opinion in #Halalan 2025.

title: Mary Jane Veloso spends Christmas Day with family for first time in nearly 15 years
body: According to Celia Veloso, Mary Jane's mother, a total of 23 family members celebrated Christmas on Wednesday with Mary Jane. They include her two sons, Danielle and Darren Candelaria, as well as Mary Jane's three sisters, grandparents, aunts, nieces and nephews. 'Masayang masaya kaming lahat. Sa loob ng 15 taon, makakasama na namin siya ng Pasko. Mga kapatid nya, tita at tito, mga pamangkin niya, 'yun 'yung mga kasama,' Celia said. 'Masaya po nagkakita-kita po kami saka nayakap po namin,' Veloso's aunt Apolonia Piyesta said. 'Halo-halo na po may tuwa, may lungkot. Syempre, ngayon ko lang po nakita kaya sobrang saya po,' said another aunt, Imelda Magday. Veloso is still adjusting to her new surroundings and was unable to sleep for the first 3 days. But her mood was immediately lifted when she saw her family again, especially her children, her parents said. 'Ngayon ko lang po nakasama ng Pasko yung nanay ko kaya masayang-masaya po ako. Tapos po lahat po kami nakasama, kaya sobrang saya po ng aming pamilya,' added Veloso's 16-year-old son Darren. Veloso expressed concern upon learning that her eldest son did not finish school, her family said. 'Yakapan po, tapos kiniss naming magkapatid. Tapos yun po, mag-aral daw po ako ng college, kami pong magkapatid… pero sabi ko naman sa kanya babalik ako,' said Veloso's 22-year-old son Mark Danniel. Her family also noticed that Veloso was having a hard time speaking Filipino. 'Nung pong kakain na kami, magpe-pray siya po nag-lead ng prayers. Yung prayers niya nung una Tagalog tapos biglang Bahasa na. Pero hinayaan lang namin… nagulat kami, nag-aadjust pa siya,' said eldest sister Leah. But unlike their first meeting, the family had more time to catch up with each other this time. Veloso recounted what she went through in the last 15 years, especially when she narrowly escaped death by firing squad in April 2015. 'Maraming tao na hindi naniniwala na wala akong kasalanan, sabi daw niya. Pero ikaw lang yung nakakaalam papa God, ikaw lang nakakaalam na wala akong kasalanan… ngayon niya lang kinwento yun, yung pangyayari na yon. Kaya iyakan kami,' her sister Maritess said. Veloso hopes to speak or just see President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. to either make a personal plea or just pray for her clemency, her family said.
