'Gustong-gusto raw niya makita si President, kahit malayo. Gusto niyang matanaw, gusto niyang ipagdasal na Lord hipuin mo si Presidente, ipaisip mo sa kanya, sabihin mo sa kanya na bigyan ako ng clemency,' Maritess added. Still, the family is thankful that Veloso is now safe from death and that they can now visit her easily compared to when she was incarcerated in Indonesia. 'Wala raw pera kaya sabi niya pwede na yung minsan sa isang buwan. Kung ako, kung maaari lang, linggo-linggo eh, kung may pera,' said her father Cesar. Veloso's mother Celia said they would visit Mary Jane again on her 40th birthday on January 10. 'Ang mahalaga sana ay makalaya na siya. Ang plano namin sa birthday niya mga (January) 9 babalik kami dito. January 10 birthday niya eh,' she said. For the first time in almost 15 years, the Veloso family spent their first Christmas together with drug convict Mary Jane Veloso here in the Philippines. This was the family's second visit inside the Correctional Institution for Women in Mandaluyong, where Veloso was detained since her arrival in the country last Wednesday.

title: WRAP: Anger over 'ghost' flood control projects overflows onto the streets
body: Probes into the projects had dominated headlines, with lawmakers grilling public works officials and Pacifico and Sarah Discaya, owners of St. Gerrard and who are linked to eight more contracting firms. On Sept. 4, activist groups, including Kalikasan and People Surge, stormed the St. Gerrard office, threw mud at the gate and spray painted it with slogans. Activists also protested at the Department of Public Works and Highways office in Manila and pelted it with rotten vegetables and water balloons. Members of Youth Against Kurakot and Akbayan Youth trooped to the St. Gerrard office on Sept. 5, with some of them dressed as ghosts — symbolic of the 'ghost' projects, they said — and others beating drums to 'exorcise' corruption. 'Hindi na pwedeng investigations lang dapat may managot. Kailangan ngayon may mapakulong, may mapanagot na mga public officials, mga pulitiko,' Khylla Meneses, secretary-general of Akbayan Youth, said. (Investigations are no longer enough. People should go to jail, public officials, politicians should be held liable.) Groups with Bagong Alyansang Makabayan meanwhile marched to the House of Representatives, where there was a clash with police guarding the Batasang Pambansa compound. Within hours of the St. Gerrard protest, Pasig City Mayor Vico Sotto, the Discayas' political rival, called for calm and advised against '[resorting] to violence or acts that could potentially lead to violence or injuries.' He said the protest would only put St. Gerrard's staff and the activists themselves at risk. He added that the city government will help with holding contractors linked to botched projects accountable and is already coordinating with national government agencies. 'Nararamdaman din namin na bumibilis ang kilos nila ngayon,' he said. (We do feel that they are acting more quickly now.) Mayor Francisco 'Isko Moreno' Domagoso of Manila, where the DPWH headquarters is, said activists could protest in designated areas but that police have been instructed to maintain order and protect government property. He said only the courts can declare anyone's guilt and that he would not 'tolerate mob rule in the city.' Palace Press Officer Claire Castro said President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. does not want investigations into corruption to lead to chaos. 'Sinusunod natin ang due process (We follow due process),' she said of a panel that has yet to be created. The Philippine Contractors Accreditation Board, itself facing allegations of corruption, has cancelled the licenses of firms linked to the Discayas , but no charges have been filed against the couple. The National Capital Region Police Office is meanwhile considering filing complaints over the Pasig protest. The Quezon City Police District has also promised 'appropriate legal actions' against activists for protesting without a permit and 'obstructing traffic and causing inconvenience to the public' as well as injuries to officers in a scuffle after protesters threw paint balloons at a replica project billboard they had brought with them. Akbayan Youth had planned to picket the House hearing on the DPWH budget but, noting it might be postponed, announced the indignation rally would instead be held at the St. Gerrard office because 'our outrage cannot wait.' It was a sentiment that had been floating on social media since protests against government excesses began in Indonesia , with some users wondering aloud when something similar would happen in the Philippines. When protests did start, they were met with concerns for due process and against a supposed descent to mob rule. Kalikasan spokesperson Jonila Castro said in front of the Discayas' office that people cannot be blamed for being angry at the corruption in government and for expressing it. 'Mas salaula naman po 'yung ginawa nilang pagnanakaw sa mga mamamayan,' she also said. (What they have done — stealing from the people — is worse.) She acknowledged ongoing probes in Congress but pointed out lawmakers are potentially investigating each other. People Surge, an organization of disaster survivors, has also called the protests justified, saying 'hollow words' are useless 'while we drown...in decades of systemic corruption, incompetence, and the boundless greed of those in power.' Bayan, in a statement, said the threat of criminal complaints reflects a bias in Philippine laws and institutions for the wealthy and for those in power. 'Authorities are quick to find fault with those who are expressing anger against corruption, but slow to act or have done nothing to pursue accountability against those who stole billions of pesos from public funds,' the group said Saturday. Asked about comparisons to protests in Jakarta and elsewhere, Bayan Secretary General Mong Palatino told ABS-CBN News that it would be a compliment: 'It acknowledges the braveness, militancy, and determination of our activists.' He said, however, that activists in the Philippines are not merely copying those in Indonesia, as 'we are pursuing our own struggle for democracy based on the actual collective actions of our communities.' He said local protests '[recall] our own experience in denouncing corruption and tyranny in society', including the 'Million People March' against pork barrel funds in 2013. 'We are aware of the continuing legacy and relevance of EDSA People Power in 1986 and 2001,' he also said. Not all protests have been confrontational — more than 1,000 joining a protest run in UP Diliman on Sunday — and calls for systemic change have not been made exclusively by activists. 'We express our outrage at government officials, politicontractors, private sector parties–and their family and relatives — who unabashedly flaunt their lavish lifestyles as they steal our hard-earned taxpayer money,' a joint statement signed by more than 100 organizations and institutions, including the Integrated Bar of the Philippines and Makati Business Club, reads. They also demanded that 'this corrupt system be replaced with a system of transparency, accountability, and people’s participation.' The Catholic Bishops' Conference of the Philippines has also called for the return of funds lost to corruption, saying many officials and contractors found guilty would still be rich even if that happened 'yet the nation remains poor if the funds are not restored.' It also made an appeal to the youth as the saga of corruption in flood control projects lurches forward: 'Use your platforms not only for outrage, but for vigilance. Expose injustice, share facts, demand reforms. Make corruption shameful again. ' — From reporting by Harlene Delgado, Ched Rick Gatchalian, Izzy Lee, ABS-CBN News Kalikasan spokesperson Jonila Castro said in front of the Discayas' office that people cannot be blamed for being angry at the corruption in government and for expressing it. 'Mas salaula naman po 'yung ginawa nilang pagnanakaw sa mga mamamayan,' she also said. MANILA — The paint and mud on the gate of the St. Gerrard construction firm's office in Pasig City had barely dried before officials warned against 'violent' protests over alleged corruption in flood control projects found to have been built of substandard materials or not built at all except on paper. Kalikasan spokesperson Jonila Castro said in front of the Discayas' office that people cannot be blamed for being angry at the corruption in government and for expressing it. 'Mas salaula naman po 'yung ginawa nilang pagnanakaw sa mga mamamayan,' she also said.

title: Youth dance in protest to urge Senate to proceed with Sara Duterte impeachment trial
body: Led by the College Editors Guild of the Philippines (CEGP), the demonstrators assembled in front of Vinzons Hall and danced to the beat of SB19’s viral hit 'Dungka!'—a song whose title echoes the phrase 'doon ka'—where participants demanded that the Senate fulfill its constitutional duty as the impeachment court. The protest followed a student-led forum where speakers underscored the importance of public participation and transparency in the impeachment process. In February 2025, the House of Representatives impeached Duterte on multiple charges, including culpable violation of the Constitution, corruption, misuse of confidential funds, and an alleged assassination plot targeting government officials. However, in June, the Senate remanded the Articles of Impeachment back to the House—a move that has drawn criticism from legal experts and youth advocates alike. Protesters vowed to continue their actions and keep public pressure on the Senate until it acts on the impeachment and brings the case to trial Youth and student groups gathered at the University of the Philippines Diliman on Friday afternoon, staging a vibrant dance protest to call on the Senate to proceed with the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte. Quezon City, Philippines — Youth and student groups gathered at the University of the Philippines Diliman on Friday afternoon, staging a vibrant dance protest to call on the Senate to proceed with the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte.

title: 'Solidarity purchase' of onions to help farmers, buyers amid rising prices
body: Groups Rice Up and Bayanihang Marikenyo at Marikenya have banded together to address the rising prices of onions by forming a 'solidarity purchase.' LOOK: 'Solidarity purchase' of Bayanihang Marikenyo at Marikenya of 1,700 kilos of white onions from Bayambang, Pangaasinan. Through the group Rice Up, onions were bought at P375 per kilo from farmers and sold at P400 to sure buyers. pic.twitter.com/IaGIi9Zell Around 1,700 kilograms of white onions were delivered by Rice Up, headed by its founder Elvin Austria Laceda, at the designated 'bagsakan,' at the Immaculate Heart of Mary Parish Church in Quezon City Tuesday morning. Upon delivery, the Bayanihang Marikenyo at Marikenya, led by co-founder Zena Bernardo, immediately re-packed the sacks of onions for the sure buyers who pre-ordered and pre-paid the goods. 'Ang kagandahan po kasi sa ganitong organisasyon, we make sure that everyone wins, 'yung consumer at the same time 'yung farmer, tulad ngayon napakamahal ng retail price pero hindi naman nakikinabang 'yung mga farmers natin so talagang may disconnect po, may gap between doon sa supply chain,' Austria Laceda said. The initiative started with a social media post made by Bernardo on the rising prices of onions which got the attention of Austria Laceda. The two then coordinated for the transport of onions, bought at P375 per kilo, literally from farm to consumer. 'Actually hindi ko nga sasabihing binebenta namin eh, parang nagsabay-sabay kaming bilhin sa farmers at the price ng 400, so parang binreak namin, binridge namin directly 'yung farmers sa consumers pero magagawa mo lang ito if you have a community working together as a team, so ito 'yung tinatawag nating solidarity purchase,' Bernardo said. Austria Laceda also raised concerns on the importation of onions that might affect the income of local farmers. 'Sa side naming mga magsasaka, kung pwede sana hintayin muna 'yung harvest hanggang February, kasi may peak po tayo sa local harvest po hanggang February and then hopefully after ng peak na lang ng local onions doon... magpatupad ng importation,' Laceda said. Laceda and Bernardo also admit that their initiative is only a temporary solution to the onion problem and long-term solutions must be put in place to address both the needs of farmers as well as consumers. 'Hindi ito ang solusyon eh, the solution is in the system, bakit ba nangyayari 'yun, so bakit may cartel, bakit nandyan 'yan, alam niyo na pala bakit nandyan pa rin 'yan,' Bernardo said. The groups Rice Up and Bayanihang Marikenyo at Marikenya have banded together to address the rising prices of onions by buying from farmers at a higher rate and selling them at a relatively lower rate compared to prevailing market prices to consumers.

title: China coast guard drives away Filipino fishing boat near Ayungin Shoal
body: The maritime incident, a video of which was shared on the PCG Facebook account, happened on Jan. 9, days after President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr. visited China. The boat captain of FFB KEN-KEN reported their experience to authorities on Friday, based on the PCG statement. 'Accordingly, [the China Coast Guard vessel] maneuvered towards FFB KEN-KEN at a distance of approximately 800 yards and deployed a rigid hull inflatable boat,' the PCG said. 'The CCG personnel onboard the RHIB gestured for the Filipino fishermen to leave the area. Consequently, FFB KEN-KEN altered her course and departed Ayungin Shoal. However, CCGV 5204 continued to shadow FFB KEN-KEN while underway to Boxall Reef.' A man aboard the fishing boat could be heard on the video saying, 'Tinataboy kami, pinapaalis kami dito sa bahura sa Second Thomas. Kawawa ang mga Pinoy na mangingisda.' Boxall Reef is the international name of Rajah Soliman Reef, which, as with the Ayungin Shoal, is within the Philippines' 200 nautical mile exclusive economic zone. The PCG said it is currently investigating the reported incident and Task Force Pag-asa was immediately deployed in the area to establish the country's presence there. 'This is to protect the interest and ensure the safety of Filipino fishermen who conduct fishing operations in the said vicinity waters as their source of livelihood,' it said. The Department of Foreign Affairs said it is 'awaiting official reports and will take the appropriate diplomatic action after verification and assessment of the incident.' The incident happened after Manila and Beijing agreed to establish 'direct communication line' in case of maritime incidents like this in the West Philippine Sea. Fisherfolk group Pamalakaya urged new National Security Adviser Eduardo Año to protest the harassment against Filipino fishermen, saying this is against the law. Pamalakaya said the National Task Force on West Philippine Sea (NTF-WPS) should craft a 'strong protest' against China for driving away Filipino fishermen in the country's territory. 'Sapat na ang video na nakuhanan ng mga mangingisda para mapatunayang laganap pa rin ang presensya at intimidasyon ng Chinese Coast Guard sa mga Pilipino sa sarili nating karagatan,' said Pamalakaya national chair Fernando Hicap. 'Malakas na batayan na ito para maghain ng diplomatikong protesta ang Pilipinas sa gobyerno ng China,' he added. The group reminded Año that it is part of his mandate to make sure that the country's sovereignty is upheld. Forming a strategic policy based on the International Tribunal on the Law of the Sea (ITLOS) must also happen to ensure maritime security, Pamalakaya said. A fishermen in Zambales also recently said their fishing activity in the Scarborough Shoal remains limited. China and the Philippines are at odds over the South China Sea, with Beijing claiming sovereignty over almost the entire area despite an international court ruling that it has no legal basis. The Philippines, Vietnam, Malaysia and Brunei have overlapping claims to parts of the sea, and Manila ordered its military last month to boost its presence after a report that China had started reclaiming several unoccupied land features around the Spratly Islands. Taiwan also lays claim to parts of the sea. After his return from China, Marcos said they agreed that Beijing would not stop Filipinos from fishing in the country's waters, clarifying former NSA Clarita Carlos' statement that Beijing wants to partner with the local fisherfolk. Marcos this week said the maritime dispute is something that keeps him up at night . There is no statement yet from the Chinese government regarding the latest incident. MANILA (UPDATE)— The Chinese Coast Guard has reportedly driven away a Filipino fishing boat in Ayungin Shoal (Second Thomas Shoal) in the West Philippine Sea and continued to shadow them until they left the area early this month, the Philippine Coast Guard said Saturday.

title: Lalaki arestado matapos umanong gahasain ang menor de edad na kinakasama ng kaibigan
body: Ayon sa Baseco police, nakikipag-inuman ang suspek at kinakasama ng 15-anyos na biktima kasama ang ilang kaanak at kaibigan nang mangyari ang insidente, madaling-araw ng Enero 16. 'In the course of their drinking, ito raw suspek, pumasok sa kwarto ng biktima at yung biktima… nagising nga po siya dahil somebody was kissing her,' pahayag ni Police Lt. Col. Emmanuel Gomez, Baseco Police Station Commander. Dagdag ni Gomez, tinawag ang suspek ng kanyang mga kainuman kaya lumabas muna siya ng kwarto. Habang nasa baba ang suspek, binarahan ng biktima ang pinto. Gayunpaman, malakas itong tinulak ng suspek kaya muli siyang nakapasok. 'Second time around umakyat uli siya. Dun na niya pwinersa ang sitwasyon… pinilit lumaban o humingi ng tulong ang biktima ngunit di na niya nagawa. Nangyari po yung karumal-dumal na intensyon dito sa biktima,' sabi ni Gomez. Nagsumbong ang biktima sa kanyang magulang at kinakasama, kaya naalerto ang pulisya at agad na inaresto ang suspek, na itinanggi ang krimen. 'Hindi po ‘yun totoo… masaya lang naman kami nag-iinuman. Tapos paggising ko, sabi ganun na raw ginawa,' sabi ng suspek. Dagdag ng suspek, kasama pa niya ang kanyang asawa habang nakikipag-inuman. Umakyat umano siya sa kwarto para gisingin at yayain ulit uminom ang kinakasama ng biktima. Sinabi niya na nakita niyang magkatabi ang biktima, ang kinakasama niya, at ang kanilang isang taong gulang na anak habang natutulog. Lumayo umano siya matapos tawagin ng mga kainuman para bumalik sa unang palapag ng bahay. 'Pag-akyat ko, pagsabi ng pinsan niya (biktima) na bumaba na ako. Bumaba na agad ako nung hindi siya nagising,' sabi ng suspek. Nasa Baseco Police Station na ang suspek na nahaharap sa kasong statutory rape. 'Kahit mga ka-close natin o para nating kamag-anak o kaibigan, pag nag-i-inuman po sa loob ng bahay, sana po i-secure natin ng maagi ang mga babae, mga asawa, anak. Ilagay sila sa safe na lugar. Kasi nga katulad ng nangyari, natutulog na yung bata, dalawang beses niya binalikan,' ani Gomez. Inaresto ng pulisya ang isang 24-anyos na lalaki matapos umano niyang gahasain ang menor de edad na babaeng kinakasama ng kanyang kaibigan noong nakaraang linggo sa Baseco, Maynila. MAYNILA — Inaresto ng pulisya ang isang 24-anyos na lalaki matapos umano niyang gahasain ang menor de edad na babaeng kinakasama ng kanyang kaibigan noong nakaraang linggo sa Baseco, Maynila.

title: Earthquake hits Quezon, felt in Metro Manila
body: The tectonic tremor hit 24 kilometers northwest of General Nakar. It had a depth of 10 kilometers, the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology said. Phivolcs initially reported the quake's magnitude at 5.6. At the Senate, a hearing of the Committee on Public Services chaired by Sen. Raffy Tulfo was suspended abruptly because of the quake and subsequent evacuation. The quake prompted evacuations at other buildings in Metro Manila, including the Palace, Senate, House of Representatives, National Bureau of Investigation, Commission on Elections, and Department of Migrant Workers premises. 'Umuga ‘yung upuan namin. Para kaming itinulak… Hindi naman ganoon kalakas,' said Rommel Asuncion, who was in an office at the third floor of the House of Representatives main building during the earthquake. Cheng Cruz, another House employee, said she thought she was just having a vertigo attack when the earthquake happened. The LRT-1, LRT-2, and MRT-3 briefly paused their services due to the quake. 'For the moment, we are expecting damage and expecting aftershocks,' Phivolcs Director Teresito Bacolcol told TeleRadyo Serbisyo. Phivolcs said the quake was felt at the following intensities. Intensity 4 - Makati, Manila, Marikina in NCR; San Pedro, Laguna; Tanay, Rizal Intensity 3 - Navotas, Quezon City, Pasay, San Juan, Taguig; Guiguinto and Malolos in Bulacan; Palayan, Nueva Ecija; Mabalacat City, Pampanga; Angeles City; Biñan, Laguna Intensity 2 - Caloocan, Mandaluyong, Paranaque, Valenzuela City; Obando, Bulacan; Cabiao, Nueva Ecija Quakes are a daily occurrence in the Philippines, which sits along the Pacific 'Ring of Fire', an arc of intense seismic and volcanic activity that stretches from Japan through Southeast Asia and across the Pacific basin. Most are too weak to be felt by humans, but strong and destructive quakes come at random with no technology available to predict when and where they will happen. A magnitude 4.6 earthquake struck Quezon province on Tuesday noon and was felt in parts of Metro Manila, the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology said. MANILA -- A magnitude 4.6 earthquake struck Quezon province on Tuesday noon and was felt in parts of Metro Manila and nearby areas, where people scurried out of some government buildings.

title: Sen. Tulfo to take closer look at lotto jackpot winners
body: Sen. Raffy Tulfo says he will investigate the legitimacy of jackpot winners in the lotto games of the PCSO. In a release, Tulfo noted that a recent P43-million lotto jackpot winner’s edited viral photo 'raised doubts and suspicions from the public.' PCSO General Manager Melquiades Robles has said that the photo was heavily altered to protect the bettor’s identity. In a recent Senate hearing he presided over, Tulfo asked for the names of the jackpot winners in a closed-door executive session, saying he would tap investigators to look into their identities. He raised concerns that the winners were not legitimate bettors. In a January 19 release, PCSO said that it had 'managed to produce two multi-millionaires in succession resulting in the charity body giving away some P1.4-B in prizes in just two days.' Tulfo added that he would also look into prohibiting the PCSO from increasing jackpot money for its lotto games through the prize fund reserve. He cited as an example Robles' admission that the jackpot money of all PCSO lotto games got bigger in December. The senator said that the PCSO added P100 million each on 6/42 and 6/45, while half a billion pesos was added each on 6/49, 6/55, and 6/58 from their prize fund reserve. As a result, he stressed that a total of P1.7 billion had been transferred from the prize fund reserve to jackpot money for the said lottery games. 'Ang P1.7 billion ay savings na sana ito ng PCSO na puwede nang gamitin para sa mga charity project nila,' Tulfo said, adding that the scheme was 'suspicious.' At the Senate hearing, Robles confirmed that P1.7 billion in prizes were won in one month. He however said the PCSO is increasing the jackpot prizes to make the draws more exciting. MANILA — Sen. Raffy Tulfo on Tuesday said he would investigate the legitimacy of jackpot winners in the lotto games of the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO), as the Senate resumes its inquiry into the agency this week.

title: DOH to craft new guidelines for medical assistance
body: This, following President Ferdinand Marcos Jr’s orders to ensure that Filipinos will have easy access to health care. In a statement, Department of Health spokesperson Albert Domingo said that will 'lay down the guidelines for MAIFIP so that it can be availed by Filipino patients easily, with dignity, and without political interference.' This is in response to Senator Risa Hontiveros’ calls to ensure that patronage politics will be eliminated from the new administrative order as mandated by provisions in the 2026 General Appropriations Act. 'Klaro ang naging desisyon namin sa Senado at House: Hindi na dapat mag-door-to-door pa sa mga opisina ng pulitiko para manlimos ng guarantee letters para lang makapagpagamot,' Hontiveros said. The DOH has repeatedly emphasized that guarantee letters issued by elected officials are not a requirement to receive medical attention in government hospitals. While the health department issues its own guarantee letters, they are subject to certain requirements. The lawmaker said she is also ready to begin the period of interpellations on Senate Bill 1593 or Universal Health Care Medical Assistance Program (UHC MAP). 'Tuldukan na natin ang panlilimos para sa GL. Ang tulong na kailangan ng ating mga kababayan para sa pagpapagamot ay dapat ibigay diretso sa kanila,' Hontiveros said. 'Lahat ng nangangailangan, dapat mabigyan ng tulong. Walang kapalit. Walang kondisyon. Walang palakasan.' DOH Secretary Ted Herbosa earlier said the department will release the new guidelines for MAIFIP 'by the end of the month or by mid-February.' The Department of Health is set to release updated guidelines on the for Medical Assistance to to Indigent and Financially Incapacitated Patients (MAIFIP). MANILA — The Department of Health is set to release updated guidelines on the for Medical Assistance to to Indigent and Financially Incapacitated Patients (MAIFIP).

title: Robredo gains momentum in final weeks of campaign
body: MANILA - Camarines Sur Rep. Leni Robredo has gained momentum in the final weeks of the vice-presidential campaign based on the latest ABS-CBN survey by Pulse Asia. Nationwide, 29 percent picked Senator Bongbong Marcos, 23 percent picked Robredo, 20 percent picked Senator Chiz Escudero, 16 percent picked Senator Alan Peter Cayetano, 4 percent picked Senator Gringo Honasan, while 3 percent picked Antonio Trillanes. Marcos led in the National Capital Region (NCR) and Balance Luzon by wide margins (43 percent and 36 percent), Robredo continued to dominate Visayas (35 percent) while Cayetano, Davao City Mayor Rodrigo Duterte’s running mate, dominated Mindanao (32 percent). Marcos ruled classes A,B,C and the biggest voting bloc D at 39 percent and 31 percent, while Robredo took Class E at 26 percent Escudero was the top second choice for vice president. Compared to the previous survey, Marcos and Robredo saw slight increases in their scores while Escudero and Cayetano shed some points. Honasan and Trillanes kept their previous scores. Marcos continues to do well despite continuing criticism over Martial law. Marcos and Honasan skipped the second vice-presidential debate last April 17. For University of the Philippines Professor Ranjit Rye, the survey results show that the vice-presidential race is still a four-way race. 'I think they're going to change. The vice-presidential contest is very competitive. Some people say it's a three-way contest, I feel it's s four-way contest,' he said, adding that Duterte, in the last debate, attempted to recognize Cayetano. 'There is still that sense that this is not a two-way fight but a four-way, one largely because of Duterte, but the frontrunner and the number 2 contender there is a big fight,' Rye also said. Rye said he is expecting survey rankings to change due to Robredo's 'very strong campaign.' 'Congresswoman Leni Robredo is running a very strong campaign and expect these numbers will change over the next week or so,' he said. 'I think you're seeing in Leni's vote improvements not just in greater Luzon but also in Visayas and Mindanao. Her hold in NCR will also improve class-wise. I think she's done very well all throughout, she's gained a lot of momentum all those weeks. She's going to improve in Luzon, she's going to improve in NCR. There's some improvement in Mindanao and then per class. I think she's going to get hold of A, B, C and probably hold D,' Rye added. Meanwhile, Pulse Asia Research Director Ana Maria Tabunda explained that gender bias, as well as urban and rural bias between Robredo and Marcos, is almost even. 'Sa ngayon pantay na, more or less [in terms of] voter preference. Voter preference ng babae/lalake kay Senator Marcos. Medyo marami pang babaeng voters boboto kay Congresswoman Robredo… pumapantay na rin sila across urban/rural, pumapantay na rin silang dalawa,' she said. Robredo and Marcos are already tied in the latest SWS survey for Businessworld. Robredo got 26 percent, Marcos got 25 percent, Escudero got 18 percent, Cayetano got 16 percent, Trillanes got 5 percent and Honasan got 2 percent. Five percent of those who joined the survey were undecided or were voting for other candidates. The April 2016 Pre-Election Survey was conducted from April 18-20, 2016 using face-to-face interviews of 1,800 validated voters, 300 each in Metro Manila, North Luzon, South Luzon, Visayas, North Mindanao and South Mindanao (sampling error margins of ±2% for national percentages, ±6% each for Metro Manila, North Luzon, South Luzon, Visayas, North Mindanao and South Mindanao). [Admin] 'Proud of my president.' — Leni Robredo #BATMAR #RoxasRobredo pic.twitter.com/h3v7ozDMGT

title: 'All eyes on Rafah' AI image shared 44 million times online
body: The image depicts densely packed rows of tents stretching endlessly across a desert landscape overshadowed by mountains, alluding to the hundreds of thousands of Palestinians who fled there during Israel's military campaign against Hamas. Chilean-US actor Pedro Pascal, top models Bella and Gigi Hadid, who are of Palestinian descent, and French football star Ousmane Dembele are among the celebrities to have shared it on Instagram. The slogan 'All eyes on Rafah' has also been widely shared in other publications and social networks, especially X, where the hashtag #alleyesonrafah has attracted almost one million hits, according to online monitor Visibrain. The platform, formerly Twitter, has also seen 27.5 million messages published in three days about the attack on the southern Gazan city bordering Egypt that generated international outrage. Gaza's Hamas-run health ministry said an Israeli strike on the camp that sparked a fire on Sunday killed 45 people and injured 249. Israel's military said it had targeted and killed two senior Hamas militants, with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu speaking of a 'tragic accident' that his government was investigating. The deadliest Gaza war was sparked by Hamas's unprecedented October 7 attack on southern Israel which resulted in the deaths of 1,189 people, mostly civilians, according to an AFP tally based on Israeli official figures. Militants also took 252 hostages, 121 of whom remain in Gaza, including 37 the Israeli army says are dead. Israel's retaliatory offensive has killed at least 36,171 people in Gaza, mostly civilians, according to the Hamas-run territory's health ministry. An AI-generated image bearing the words 'All eyes on Rafah' has been shared by more than 44 million Instagram accounts since Monday after a deadly Israeli strike at a camp for displaced Palestinians in the Gazan city. PARIS, France- An AI-generated image bearing the words 'All eyes on Rafah' has been shared by more than 44 million Instagram accounts since Monday after a deadly Israeli strike at a camp for displaced Palestinians in the Gazan city. An AI-generated image bearing the words 'All eyes on Rafah' has been shared by more than 44 million Instagram accounts since Monday after a deadly Israeli strike at a camp for displaced Palestinians in the Gazan city.

title: Gaza truce to take effect 0630 GMT Sunday: mediator Qatar
body: 'As coordinated by the parties to the agreement and the mediators, the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip will begin at 8:30 am on Sunday, January 19, local time in Gaza,' Qatar's foreign ministry spokesman Majed al-Ansari said on X. 'We advise the inhabitants to take precaution, exercise the utmost caution, and wait for directions from official sources.' The exact time of the ceasefire's start had been unclear, though Israel, whose cabinet earlier on Saturday approved the hostage and prisoner exchange deal, had said no prisoners would be freed before 1400 GMT. A ceasefire between Israel and Hamas in the Gaza Strip will take effect at 0630 GMT on Sunday morning, Qatar, which helped mediate the deal, said on Saturday.

title: The ANC Brief: Cam's 'bombshell'
body: Controversial Cam Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) board member Sandra Cam said she is ready to drop a bombshell on corruption in the PCSO’s small-town lottery (STL) system. Earlier, she said several ranking officials including retired military generals are allegedly involved in corrupt practices. This time, Cam said she would release 'shocking' documents to back her allegations. She is scheduled to appear at the House of Representatives for a briefing on the PCSO. Will she drop her bombshell there? On the economy Economic managers led by Central Bank Gov. Ben Diokno will be at a 9 a.m. forum to talk about the impact of economic policies of the Duterte administration. At 10 a.m., officials of the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) will hold a press briefing. Heading for Luzon A low pressure area (LPA) spotted east of the country has developed into a tropical depression. The weather system, named 'Jenny,' is threatening Luzon and 15 areas have been placed under Tropical Cyclone Wind Signal no. 1. Indisposed President Duterte skipped Monday morning’s events in celebration of National Heroes Day. His former man Friday Sen. Bong Go said the president was 'indisposed.' But Go admitted he was with the president at 4 a.m. On Tuesday, the President has another event, this time at 5:40 p.m., to celebrate the anniversary of the Comprehensive Agrarian Reform Program (CARP). True or false There is a surge of deep fake images and videos online, bringing with them ethical dilemmas and opportunities to commit crime. ANCX discovers, for example, that a deep fake face might be able to fool a phone’s facial scan. Or in the case of the Leila De Lima video, lots of gullible netizens. Controversial PCSO board member Sandra Cam is threatening to blow the lid on alleged corruption in the agency. Here are the big stories making the headlines on ANC today:

title: US or Iran: Who will win the Hormuz endurance game?
body: Now approaching its fourth month, the crisis off Iran's coast is marked by mutual blockades. Tehran has been charging ships up to $2 million for safe passage through the strait, while the United States has imposed a naval embargo, turning back vessels carrying Iranian oil exports. These competing blockades have failed to deliver decisive results. Some Iranian ships continue to slip through, while several Asian shipping firms have agreed to pay tolls, despite such fees violating international maritime law. Fragile negotiations between the US and Iran to reopen Hormuz have, meanwhile, stalled several times, sparking the risk of escalation into a wider regional conflict. Despite Pakistan-led mediation efforts and a proposed one-page memorandum aimed at ending hostilities and reopening Hormuz, neither side appears ready to blink first. Dania Thafer, executive director of the Washington-based think tank Gulf International Forum (GIF), believes US President Donald Trump's on-off military threats — intended to increase its leverage over Iran — may have backfired. 'The Iranian response suggests the opposite,' she told DW. 'They interpret it as the US lacking the will to escalate the war.' Trump faces increasing pressure both at home and abroad to avoid further military action, with Gulf allies such as Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates and Qatar urging restraint. Surging oil prices and rising domestic inflation are adding political heat ahead of the US midterm elections in November. Iran, meanwhile, is losing some $435 million per day in trade, nearly two-thirds of which comes from exports of mainly crude oil, Miad Maleki, a senior fellow at the conservative Washington-based Foundation for Defense of Democracies (FDD), estimated in April. This means that with the US blockade stretching to 39 days on Friday, Iran's public finances have already suffered an estimated $17 billion loss. According to Maleki, this is in addition to around $144 billion in economic damage caused by US-Israeli strikes in the first weeks of the war. Burcu Ozcelik, a senior research fellow at the London-based Royal United Services Institute (RUSI), thinks Iran may have gained 'outsized leverage' through its missile attacks on shipping and Gulf neighbors, but it is now being 'hit hard' by the disruption to its own oil exports. 'Despite Tehran's bluster about regime resilience, its economy is not blockade-proof,' Ozcelik told DW. Experts describe the standoff as a dangerous waiting game. Both the US and Iran believe they have time on their side. However, the Gulf states are far more risk-averse and economically exposed. Their frustration with the deadlock has hardened into coordinated pressure for a diplomatic breakthrough. Various Gulf states have urged Trump to shelve plans for further strikes and give negotiations more time. In private, they've warned that a frozen conflict will jeopardize plans to transition their economies away from fossil fuels. The Gulf states have been spending hundreds of billions of dollars on ambitious industrial and tourism projects. They strongly support the Pakistan‑mediated talks and a joint US–UN initiative to reopen the Strait without Iranian tolls or control claims. As a power that sees itself reshaping the Middle East region, Iran is also using the war to push for long‑term gains. GIF's Thafer thinks Iran's ambitions go well beyond war victory and it will try to seek to 'flip the regional order in its favor' in the long term. It wants 'the Gulf states to expel the US and bring the region under an Iranian security framework,' she told DW, adding that this approach was not in the interest of Gulf states, despite their frustrations with Washington. While remaining cautiously optimistic about a breakthrough in talks, Washington is insisting on a full reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, an end to all Iranian nuclear enrichment activity and no sanctions relief without major concessions. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio told NATO foreign ministers gathered in Sweden on Friday that 'we have to have a Plan B' if Iran does not compromise. 'The challenge is that there is no magic target that the US can hit that will immediately translate into regime surrender,' Ozcelik told DW. 'If civilian infrastructure is targeted, this may trigger Tehran to execute a harsher retaliation against Gulf states.' Tehran maintains it will stand firm, despite the growing hardship facing ordinary Iranians, which is unlikely to ease soon. 'Tehran's various proposals to extract fees for transiting the Strait of Hormuz or charging for undersea cables is indicative of the realization by some pragmatic voices in Tehran that the Iranian economy and its people are in for a protracted period of hardship, even if sanction relief is somehow agreed,' Ozcelik said. Annual inflation in Iran has surged above 54%, with prices for some food products more than doubling. A nationwide internet blackout has lasted more than 80 days, further isolating citizens and crippling daily life. 'While Trump sees this [winning the war] as part of his presidential legacy, the Iranians view it as a matter of regime survival and the future of their country,' Thafer underlined. Tehran is betting that its sanctions‑hardened economy can outlast the Strait of Hormuz crisis. Washington, meanwhile, is contending with elevated oil prices, renewed inflation pressure and rising global recession risks Deadlocked, dysfunctional and dangerous. That is how the Strait of Hormuz standoff is increasingly being described.

title: 'Peace be with you': Pope Leo XIV ushers in new era with powerful first blessing
body: Before thousands gathered at St. Peter’s Square, the new pontiff delivered his first 'Urbi et Orbi' (to the city and the world) blessing, signaling the beginning of a new chapter for the Catholic Church marked by faith, peace, and unity. 'La pace sia con tutti voi!' ('Peace be with you all!') Pope Leo XIV proclaimed in Italian, echoing the words of the risen Christ. In his first message as the 267th successor of St. Peter, Pope Leo XIV spoke of a peace that is 'humble and disarming,' rooted in the unconditional love of God. He referenced the voice of his predecessor, Pope Francis, whose Easter Sunday message reminded the world that 'God loves you. Evil will not prevail.' Amid loud cheers and emotional applause, Pope Leo XIV extended a heartfelt call for dialogue, reconciliation, and unity among nations and peoples. 'Let us walk hand in hand with God and with one another,' he declared, urging the faithful to be bridge builders in a divided world. The pontiff also thanked the college of cardinals for their trust, vowing to serve as a 'bishop for the people' and a 'servant of unity.' Quoting St. Augustine, he said, 'With you, I am a Christian. For you, I am a bishop.' Cardinal Lorenzo Baldisseri described the new pope as 'buonissimo, mite,' 'bravo'—(very good, gentle, kind)—sentiments echoed throughout the vast square that pulsed with joy and anticipation. 'He’s a good choice,' Baldisseri said, smiling amid the emotional crowd. Jonathan Gonzales, a tourist from Melbourne, stood in awe among the thousands gathered. 'We never in a million years thought that an American would be elected as pope,' he shared. The newly elected pontiff’s nationality and message struck a chord with many, including Filipino clergy present at the Vatican. 'Inaasahan natin na magiging kaisa siya sa pagpe-pursue ng kapayapaan,' said Father Derick James Vergara of the Diocese of Malolos. 'I think he will be the pope of social reconciliation and peace,' added Father Randy Boquiren of Opus Dei, noting the global unrest facing the Church and the world today. As evening fell over Rome, the energy in St. Peter’s Square remained electric. The global Catholic community stood united in a vigil of faith, embracing a new spiritual chapter led by a pope with an open heart and a call for peace. The Vatican later confirmed that Pope Leo XIV will celebrate Mass for the cardinals at the Sistine Chapel on Friday at 11 a.m. (0900 GMT), marking his first liturgical act as pontiff. He is also scheduled to meet with members of the world’s media on Monday morning. In Pope Leo XIV, many see not just a new leader but a shepherd willing to guide the flock through uncertain times—with compassion, humility, and a voice that offers peace.' In a moment etched in history, newly elected Pope Leo XIV stepped out onto the Benediction Loggia of St. Peter’s Basilica, radiating humility and strength, to deliver his first apostolic blessing to a sea of pilgrims and millions watching around the world. VATICAN CITY — In a moment etched in history, newly elected Pope Leo XIV stepped out onto the Benediction Loggia of St. Peter’s Basilica, radiating humility and strength, to deliver his first apostolic blessing to a sea of pilgrims and millions watching around the world.

title: Prosecutors junk obstruction of justice rap vs father of fratman tagged in hazing case
body: DOJ prosecutors junked the obstruction of justice complaint against the father of a fratman tagged in the alleged hazing of an engineering student. BREAKING: DOJ prosecutors junk the obstruction of justice complaint vs father of a person of interest in Salilig hazing case, DOJ spox Asec Mico Clavano says. Reason: Lack of probable cause with reasonable certainty of conviction. Father to be released soon. pic.twitter.com/Emp0eydkt9 'The resolution on the obstruction of justice against the father of the respondents has been dismissed due to lack of probable cause with reasonable certainty of conviction,' he told reporters, citing information from the DOJ panel of prosecutors. There were no additional details as to the reason for the dismissal. 'I haven’t read the resolution myself… All I know is that the resolution was for dismissal,' Clavano explained. Biñan, Laguna police on Thursday filed the obstruction of justice complaint against the father of a person of interest in the hazing case because he supposedly tried to stop officers from serving a search warrant intended to secure a blue SUV allegedly used during the initiation rites which led to the death of 24-year-old John Matthew Salilig. The father was arrested on Wednesday, March 1. With the junking of the obstruction of the justice complaint, the father 'will be released immediately,' according to Clavano, although he is not yet certain if the Biñan police have already been informed of the resolution. Meanwhile, 7 Tau Gamma Phi fraternity members are currently under police custody over the killing of Salilig. Six were arrested on Wednesday night while a 7th fraternity member, Daniel de los Reyes Perry, who is said to the be master initiator, surrendered to authorities on Thursday. 'He will also go through the same inquest proceeding which is afforded to the previous 6 respondents and that will take place either today or tomorrow,' Clavano said Friday. A total of 15 frat members took part in the initiation rites of 4 individuals, according to the DOJ spokesperson. Salilig, a chemical engineering student, was reported missing by his brother on Feb. 20. He had told his brother on the night of Feb. 17 that he would attend the fraternity’s initiation rites. His body was found on Tuesday, Feb. 28, in a remote site in Cavite, after a fraternity member brought the police to the location. Salilig was allegedly hit at least 70 times and was no longer responsive while on board a vehicle. A medico-legal report from the PNP said Salilig suffered severe blunt force trauma to the lower extremities, which was the cause of his death. On Thursday, Salilig’s brother and a fraternity neophyte filed 2 complaints for violation of the Anti-Hazing law against 6 suspects who were invited to the Biñan police station and arrested after they were identified by a complainant on Wednesday night. The preliminary investigation is set on March 10 where the respondents are expected to submit their counter-affidavits. Police considered the case 'solved' even if around 10 persons of interest have yet to show up. Clavano called on schools, school officials and authorities to help in looking for the remaining frat members involved in the alleged hazing incident. 'We encourage the school authorities to help our law enforcement agencies if ever there are requests for any information sa school to please cooperate that we can get to the bottom of this as soon as possible,' he said. MANILA — Department of Justice prosecutors have junked the obstruction of justice complaint against the father of one of the fratmen tagged in the alleged hazing of an Adamson University student, DOJ spokesperson Assistant Secretary Mico Clavano said Friday.

title: Halalan 2025: Francis Zamora re-elected San Juan mayor
body: He was proclaimed mayor anew at the FilOil EcoOil Centre past 1 a.m. on May 13. Based on partial, unofficial results from Comelec data as of 10:14 p.m. on Monday, Zamora garnered 50,152 votes over his political rival and former PBA player Philip Cezar who recived 7,231 votes. Zamora's running mate, Angelo Agcoili, was also ahead of his rival, independent candidate Candy Crisologo. Agcoili received 41,823 votes, while Crisologo got 11,806. It will be Zamora's third and final term as San Juan mayor. He first won the city’s top seat in 2019 by defeating Ejercito-Estrada scion Janella Estrada. Filipinos worldwide can also stay updated on election-related news and results via the ' Halalan ' page on ABS-CBN.com. Where to view partial, unofficial 2025 election results. Incumbent Mayor Francis Zamora has been reelected for a third term in the city of San Juan. MANILA (UPDATED) -- Incumbent Mayor Francis Zamora has been reelected for a third term in the city of San Juan.

title: Harapan 2025: Senatorial bet Marc Gamboa promises to make Filipinos rich
body: Gamboa was interviewed on ABS-CBN’s Harapan 2025 on Friday. 'Medyo apprehensive ako na pagdating po sa censorship,' he said. 'Naniniwala ako na ano e, na there is a way naman para i-police, to mag-deterrent na pagkalat ng fake news without censoring yun pong free speech. 'Kung sasabihin nung isang vlogger na, hindi ko gusto si ganitong politiko, opinion niya yun. Hindi yun dapat i-label as fake news.' Gamboa has been a political vlogger for six years and is known for his channel Models of Manila TV. In laying out his credentials, Gamboa said he will do a better job than others who are running for senator. 'You would get a senator po na nakikinig sa inyo na ipaglalaban kayo. At ito po yung tagline ko, ‘Payamanin ang Pilipino’. Araw-araw po ang gagawin ko sa Senado, maghanap ng paraan kung paano po dumami yung pera ninyo.' One of his suggestions is to create a website where the unemployed can register so that the government can help them find a job. 'Kailangan pong turuan natin yung mga kababayan natin na maghawak ng pera at kung paano po mapadami yung pera na yun sa school pa lang and eventually hanggang sa pagtanda,' Gamboa explained. Asked about his personal view about the impeachment of Vice President Sara Duterte, Gamboa said, 'Kailangan pong dinggin ng Senado. Ang dami kong naririnig na mga, kung tumatakbong senador na iniiwasan, sinasabi waste of time lang yan. Pero bottom line po dito, ayaw po nilang mawala yung pong mga Duterte supporters. Ayaw siguro at kinakabahan din sila sa INC (Iglesia ni Cristo).' 'Ako po, I greatly respect INC. Talagang sana, ma-endorse din ako. Pero, ang bottom line po dito, na-impeach na, kailangan po dinggin,' he added. Gamboa said he is often in the company of former senator Antonio Trillanes IV, a known critic of the Dutertes, but will maintain fairness if he becomes a senator-judge. 'We will be unbiased, we will be fair. Pero kapag ang, kapag lumabas yung mga bank accounts, kapag totoo yung sinasabi ni Trillanes regarding dun sa mga bank accounts nila VP Sara, I think that would be enough para po maconvict si VP Sara diyan po sa kanyang impeachment case,' Gamboa said. He also supports the call for former President Rodrigo Duterte to face the International Criminal Court (ICC) which is investigating Duterte's war on drugs. 'Madalas kong kasama sa election, sa campaign, si former senator and soon to be mayor, Sonny Trillanes ng Caloocan. At ayun, ano eh nakita ko yung detalye nung kaso…yung mga ebidensya is enough to warrant ICC na para i-try po siya doon,' Gamboa said. He approves of the government’s pivot to the United States in relation to the West Philippine Sea. 'I fully support yung foreign policy ni Pangulong Bongbong Marcos. Naininiwala ako na dapat i-update yung mga espionage laws po natin na medyo makaluma na,' he said. 'Yan ang biggest threat po ngayon, yung pong ginagawa ng China sa ating bansa. Yung mga spies, yung mga pakikialam nila, yung sa West Philippine Sea, sa mga mangingisda natin, and yung pong ginagawa nilang pag-influence at yung pagpasok ng pera nila dito para i-influence yung ating gobyerno, idagdag mo pa yung POGO.' Gamboa clarified, however, that befriending China and other countries such as Japan, Australia, Korea, and Russia will be beneficial for the Philippines. The senatorial candidate also suggested a solution to the rising prices of commodities. 'Gusto ko ang goal natin eventually tanggalin na yung middleman. Yan po ang nagpapahirap, nagpapatas ng presyo, nagmamanipulate,' Gamboa said. 'I believe meron pong technology na para yung pong ating pagbili ng palay, ng bigas, diretso from farmers papunta po sa mga vendors or sa consumers.' Senatorial hopeful and political vlogger Marc Gamboa does not believe social media should be regulated despite the spread of fake news.

title: DOH expands modular hospitals as COVID-19 cases surge
body: 'We're coping this up in terms of expanding the number of infrastructures by the setup that we did since last year of a modular field hospital here in Metro Manila,' treatment czar and Health Undersecretary Leopoldo Vega told ANC. The bed capacity of modular health facilities in the Lung Center of the Philippines in Quezon City and Dr. Jose N. Rodriguez Memorial Hospital in Tala, Caloocan City have been increased, he added. A similar facility in Quezon Institute, which is managed by the Jose R. Reyes Memorial Medical Center, has also been operationalized. 'This is something we are really trying to expand because the number of, especially the allocated beds here for COVID, is not enough to cover the surge that we have, which is roughly more than we saw in August of 2020,' Vega said. Building modular hospitals, which are intended to treat patients with moderate and severe COVID-19, takes about 45 days. They are designed by the Department of Public Works and Highways. The Philippines is facing surging coronavirus infections, reaching for the first time more than 10,000 cases on Monday. To date, more than 740,000 have been infected by the virus, of which over 603,000 recovered while 13,000 died. The tally also includes more than 124,000 active cases, the highest among Southeast Asian nations. In the interview, Vega bared that the One Hospital Command Center, a referral network linked with service providers such as hospitals, quarantine facilities and medical transportation, has also been overwhelmed by the spike in coronavirus infections. 'Because of the surge, the One Hospital Command [Center] is also experiencing a lot of strain and a number of calls. With respect to the number of calls last month, we had an average of 68 to 70 calls per day but now we are receiving almost 400 calls per day,' he said. To decongest hospitals from COVID-19 admission, mild and asymptomatic cases are brought to temporary treatment or isolation centers. Some COVID-19 patients are also being referred to hospitals in other regions. 'If you're admitted at a COVID ward or isolation, [it takes] a minimum of 10 days but the ICU (intensive care unit) can extend up to more than 30 days. In other words, there's a longer turnaround time,' Vega said. The health official also said they had gathered some 40 doctors from different provinces, in which they would be deployed to various health institutions. Mass hiring for other health care professionals is also ongoing, he added. Vega admitted that hospitals were caught off-guard by the sharp increase in COVID-19 cases due to the presence of more contagious coronavirus variants. 'Most of the institutions were not ready for this because some of the hospitals at this time were already converting their COVID beds towards non-COVID because there were a number of non-COVID patients who also needed admission and also needed the required treatment. So, everybody had to readjust in terms of allocation of COVID beds,' he said. The Department of Health is expanding modular health facilities in Metro Manila to cope with the COVID-19 surge that is putting a strain on hospitals in the capital region, a health official said Wednesday. MANILA - The Department of Health is expanding modular health facilities in Metro Manila to cope with the COVID-19 surge which has been straining hospitals in the capital region, a health official said Wednesday.

title: Rural popularity secures Pacquiao's ratings
body: The latest ABS-CBN survey by Pulse Asia showed Pacquiao placing fourth. Others included in the top 12 are Senator Vicente Sotto, Senate President Franklin Drilon, former senator Francis Pangilinan, former senator Panfilo Lacson, former senator Miguel Zubiri, Pacquiao, former PhilHealth Board Director Risa Hontiveros, Senator Serge Osmeña, former Technical Education and Skills Development Authority (TESDA) Director General Joel Villanueva, Senator Ralph Recto, former senator Richard Gordon and former Justice Secretary Leila de Lima. However, the rankings of Hontiveros, Recto, De Lima and Gordon have been fluctuating. According to Tabunda, the numbers often differ in urban and rural areas, except in the case of Pacquiao, who is popular even in far-flung places. 'Ang problem namin sa senatorial race, itong survey na ito, 4000 [respondents] na kahit itong mga rural areas na ang liit ng munisipyo, sila yung mas mababa yung shineshade nila kaya very volatile, akyat baba talaga 'yung senatorial candidates sa positions 9 to 12, very volatile. 'Compared to nung time na monthly [surveys lang] kami, 1,800 [respondents] lang,...ang laki ng diperensiya ng urban vote nila sa rural vote nila sa lahat ng kandidato, pwera kay Manny Pacquiao,' she said. 'Numerically, mas mataas [ang suporta sa] urban kaysa rural, except Manny Pacquiao. Ibig sabihin, si Manny Pacquiao kilala kahit sa libliban, they know him. Mas malaki, pero may kandidato na malaki ang agwat na ang ranking nila sa urban much better than ranking sa rural dahil ultimately, kahit ano pa 'yung numerong 'yan, importante 12 ka,' Tabunda added. MANILA - Pulse Asia Research Director Ana Maria Tabunda said Sarangani Representative Manny Pacquiao's popularity even in rural areas has helped him maintain his standing in pre-election surveys, unlike some candidates whose ratings have been fluctuating.

title: Lawmaker apologizes after vehicle caught using EDSA busway for 2nd time
body: In a statement released by the office of his father, Sen. Raffy Tulfo, the younger Tulfo admitted that his vehicle used the EDSA busway. He also clarified that they did not use their position to try to evade the penalty. He also said they have already paid the fines and will undergo the necessary seminars as part of the penalty. 'Nais ko lamang pong linawin na WALANG naganap na pagbanggit o paggamit ng pangalan ng sinumang nasa posisyon. WALA rin pong nangyaring anumang pang-aabuso sa awtoridad para makalusot o mapawalang-sala sa insidenteng ito. Sa halip, tinanggap po namin ang tiket at ang aming pagkakamali nang walang pag-aalinlangan,' he explained. (I just want to clarify that there was no mention or use of the names of anyone holding office. There was also no abuse of authority to evade the penalty. Instead, we accepted the ticket and our mistake without any questions.) He also promised to do everything to avoid a similar incident in the future. 'Muli, taos-puso po akong humihingi ng inyong paumanhin at pang-unawa. Nangangako po akong gagawin ko ang lahat ng hakbang upang maiwasan ang ganitong uri ng pagkakamali sa hinaharap,' Tulfo said. (I am sincerely asking for your forgiveness and understanding. I promise to do everything to avoid this kind of mistake in the future.) Operatives from the Special Action and Intelligence Committee for Transportation (SAICT) have apprehended Tulfo’s driver for using the EDSA Carousel. His vehicle was first seen using the EDSA busway on Sept. 26, 2024, and again last Jan. 23, 2025 - allegedly violating Republic Act 4136, also known as the Land Transportation and Traffic Code. Sen. Tulfo earlier admitted that a vehicle carrying his son was caught using the EDSA busway, adding that he has already reprimanded the latter. The Metro Manila Development Authority (MMDA) previously said that passing through the Busway is only allowed for authorized buses, emergency vehicles, the President, Vice President, Senate President, Speaker of the House of Representatives, and Supreme Court Chief Justice. Quezon City 2nd District Rep. Ralph Tulfo on Tuesday apologized after his vehicles was caught using the EDSA busway for the second time. MANILA — Quezon City 2nd District Rep. Ralph Tulfo on Tuesday apologized after his vehicles was caught using the EDSA busway for the second time.

title: PACC probes Faeldon over freed heinous crime convicts, may summon Bato
body: The anti-corruption body may also question Senator Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa, who previously headed the BuCor, PACC Commissioner Greco Belgica said. 'We can ask Sen. Dela Rosa to give clarification, to give clarity to the issues that may come up. Hindi naman masama 'yun eh (There's nothing wrong with that),' Belgica told ANC. Faeldon on Monday faced a Senate inquiry over the alleged questionable application of the good conduct time allowance law. Under the law, a convict's sentence may be reduced if he or she exhibited good conduct while in prison. Data from the BuCor showed that a total of 1,914 prisoners convicted of heinous crimes have been granted early release since 2014, a year after the amendment of the good conduct time allowance (GCTA) law. In his testimony, Faeldon said no convict is excluded in the granting of GCTA based on the law's implementing rules and regulations. 'That’s why in their computation… all convicted of any crime when they behave well in jail, they were granted GCTA,' Faeldon said. He said based on the law’s IRR, a prisoner’s GCTA can only be suspended during the month that an infraction was committed. 'In the succeeding month that they behaved well, the granting of GCTA resumes regardless of the gravity of these offenses,' he said. The releases followed the enactment of a 2013 law expanding the computation of GCTA to detention time. The Supreme Court upheld its retroactive application. 'We want to know if there are prisoners who are convicted of heinous crimes that were released. If that was the case then that is a clear violation of the law,' Belgica said. The PACC will also summon other BuCor officials to explain the implementation of the GCTA law, Belgica said. 'We want to see the documents and hear his (Faeldon) side and explanation on what transpired with the GCTA and the freeing of these 2000 criminals,' he said. The Presidential Anti-Corruption Commission (PACC) said Monday it is investigating Bureau of Corrections director-general Nicanor Faeldon as reports surfaced that he signed release orders for convicts of heinous crimes. MANILA - The Presidential Anti-Corruption Commission (PACC) said Monday it is investigating Bureau of Corrections director-general Nicanor Faeldon as reports surfaced that he signed release orders for convicts of heinous crimes.

title: VP Sara Duterte pays respects to former House Speaker de Venecia in Dagupan
body: She visited the wake at the Simbaan Ya Bato (Annunciation of the Lord Parish) in Barangay Bonuan Gueset, where De Venecia’s remains lie in state until early Monday morning. The Office of the Vice President said Duterte offered prayers and extended her condolences to the family and loved ones of the late former Speaker. On February 14, large crowds filled the streets as a solemn procession brought his remains to the City Plaza, followed by a memorial program and the presentation of a city resolution recognizing his life, legacy, and publi c service. Among the other prominent figures who visited his wake in Dagupan was former President and current Pampanga Representative Gloria Macapagal-Arroyo. It was Arroyo’s second visit, after paying her respects to De Venecia at Heritage Memorial Park in Taguig last Wednesday. A memorial Mass will be offered for the former House Speaker at the Minor Basilica of Our Lady of the Rosary of Manaoag at 5 a.m. tomorrow. His remains will then be transferred to the House of Representatives of the Philippines inside the Batasang Pambansa Complex in Quezon City. A necrological service will be held at the Plenary Hall on February 16 at 10 a.m. The program will include an invocation, eulogies from House leaders and colleagues, the offering of legislative memorabilia, a video presentation highlighting De Venecia’s decades of public service, and a response from a member of his family. De Venecia, who served as Speaker from 1992 to 2008 across five terms, died on February 10, 2026, at the age of 89. He will be laid to rest on February 18 at the Libingan ng mga Bayani in Taguig City. -- with a report from Harlene Delgado, ABS-CBN News Vice President Sara Duterte paid her respects on Sunday to former five-time House Speaker Jose de Venecia Jr. Former speaker of the House Jose De Venecia in attendance at the Rotary Club of Manila event held at the New World Hotel in Makati on July 18, 2019. George Calvelo, ABS-CBN News/File MANILA (2nd UPDATE) — Former House Speaker Jose de Venecia, Jr. has passed away, his family confirmed to ABS-CBN News on Tuesday. He was was 89 years old. 'With heavy hearts, our family announces the passing of our beloved husband, father, grandfather, and patriarch, former Speaker of the House Jose C. de Venecia Jr.,' his family said in a statement. Senate honors former speaker Jose De Venecia De Venecia, also known as 'JDV,' was longtime lawmaker and political leader. Born in Dagupan City, Pangasinan, De Venecia was elected representative of Pangasinan seven times. The House of Representatives said that De Venecia was the only member of the House who served as Speaker for five terms, particularly during the 9th to 10th, and 12th to 14th Congresses. Sen. Loren Legarda called it 'an unprecedented feat in postwar Philippine Congress.' He was a principal author of the Dollar Remittance Program, the Bases Conversion and Development Act, the Build-Operate-Transfer Law, the Philippine Economic Zone Act, and the New Central Bank Act, among others. 'He championed landmark legislation that transformed the Philippine economy, strengthened democratic institutions, and uplifted millions of Filipino families, including overseas workers,' the De Venecia family said. 'A tireless peacemaker, Speaker de Venecia pursued dialogue at moments when peace efforts were difficult and often unpopular, contributing to historic agreements with RAM-AFP-YOU in 1995 and the Moro National Liberation Front in 1996,' they added. In 2024, both the House of Representatives and the Senate honored De Venecia in separate resolutions recognizing his decades-long career in public service. 'He helped elevate Asia’s collective voice and placed the Philippines at the center of global conversations on peace and diplomacy,' the De Venecia family said. 'As we mourn his passing, we give thanks for a life that helped shape the Philippine Republic — and for a legacy that endures in our laws, our institutions, and our continuing pursuit of peace,' they noted. Arrangements for De Venecia’s wake and interment will be announced later by his family. House speaker Jose de Venecia delivers a speech at the House of Representatives in Quezon City late night February 4, 2008. De Venecia ended his 12-year leadership at dawn 05 February 2008 as he was ousted by Davao City Representative Prospero Nograles. AFP/File In a Facebook post, Dagupan City Mayor Belen Fernandez said 'Dagupan mourns' the passing of De Venecia. 'With deep sorrow and profound gratitude, we bid farewell to former Speaker Jose de Venecia Jr. — a five-time Speaker of the House, a statesman of national stature, and above all, the foremost son of Dagupan,' Fernandez said. Fernandez recalled that during the 'devastating 1990 earthquake' in Dagupan, it was De Venecia who stood firmly for the city. 'Through his leadership and authorship of the necessary national funding, he became the principal architect of our rehabilitation, recovery, and renewed rise. Because of him, Dagupan did not simply rebuild — we rose stronger,' she said. Former House Speaker Martin Romualdez in a statement also paid tribute to the veteran lawmaker, whom he called 'one of the greatest pillars of Philippine democracy.' JDV pushes joint dev't: South China Sea given to us by God 'Today, we mourn the passing of one of the greatest pillars of Philippine democracy and the political father of Lakas-CMD, former Speaker Jose de Venecia Jr.—a man whose brilliance, courage, and compassion helped shape the soul of our nation,' Romualdez said. 'On a personal level, I will forever be grateful for his kindness, guidance, and quiet encouragement over the years. He was generous with his wisdom, gracious in victory, and dignified in all seasons of public life. To me and to many in our party, he was a mentor, a statesman, and a father figure. His passing is deeply personal,' he added. Sen. Juan Miguel Zubiri said he considers JDV as one of his 'politiko idols.' 'Sa kanya ko talaga natutunan kung paano maging consensus builder. Yung leadership style ko, malaking bahagi niyan ay galing sa kanya. Madami akong nakuha sa kanyang brand of consultative leadership, yung style niya na listening first before deciding, and then finding a path that benefits everyone,' Zubiri said. For Sen. Chiz Escudero, De Venecia was a 'towering figure in Philippine politics,' who 'guided younger legislators with patience and wisdom.' 'I recall my early years as a neophyte in the House of Representatives, when Speaker de Venecia was fatherly to me. He guided younger legislators with patience and wisdom, offering counsel that was both practical and inspiring. His mentorship reflected his belief that leadership meant nurturing others to serve with integrity and vision,' Escudero said. Former House Speaker Jose de Venecia, Jr., also known as 'JDV,' has passed away. MANILA - Prominent political figures paid tribute to former House Speaker Jose de Venecia Jr., remembering him not only as a seasoned statesman but also as a mentor, friend, and family figure whose influence shaped generations of leaders. Friends, family members, and political figures continued to arrive at Heritage Memorial Park in Taguig on the third night of JDV's wake. Senator Bam Aquino highlighted De Venecia’s legislative accomplishments and his quiet efforts to nurture young public servants. 'Marami pong napasang batas si Speaker De Venecia, marami na rin po siyang nagawa para sa bayan, and yung isang bagay na hindi siguro alam ng mga tao talagang nagbibigay siya ng pansin sa mas batang lider at lehislador. In my case he was very kind, always willing to give advice and talagang meron po siyang puso para sa mga mas batang congressman at senador. And all this years, he was extremely kind and helpful sa amin, we will miss Speaker De Venecia and our country will miss him.' Aquino emphasized that beyond De Venecia’s legislative record, his mentorship left a lasting mark on younger lawmakers who benefited from his guidance and generosity. Former Representative Mikey Arroyo described De Venecia as more than a political ally, calling him family. 'Ninong namin siya sa kasal ni Angela, more than politics, I look at him really as a family. Even his son si Toph he is close to me, he is close to my daughter. He mentored me and he mentored others during my time so from him I learned how to listen to people and how to build consensus.' Arroyo credited De Venecia for teaching him the importance of listening and consensus-building — skills he said he carried throughout his time in public service. Senator Loren Legarda recalled how De Venecia played a pivotal role in her political career. 'He was the one responsible for bringing me into Lakas in 1998, thats why I became a number one senator in 1998, I’m very grateful for all the help that he has given me and since then he and Gina had been my friends, since his last breath. Gina asked me for a video to say goodbye to Manong, and I sent him that morning maybe 10 am, and he passed away shortly after that, according to Gina, in 10 mins. Sabi niya parang hinintay ka mag-goodbye.' Legarda described De Venecia as a 'global Filipino' and a visionary leader who was ahead of his time. As tributes continue to pour in, colleagues and family members remember the former Speaker not only for his decades of public service but also for the personal relationships he cultivated across political lines. Leaders say his legacy will endure through the many laws he helped pass and the countless public servants he mentored throughout his career. De Venecia’s remains are scheduled to be brought to the Annunciation of the Lord Parish in Dagupan City, Pangasinan, tomorrow until Sunday, February 15. A memorial and necrological service is scheduled at the House of Representatives in Batasang Pambansa, Quezon City, on Monday, February 16, from 10 a.m. to 2:30 p.m. After the ceremony, his remains will be returned to Heritage Memorial Park in Taguig. According to the family, De Venecia will be laid to rest at the Libingan ng mga Bayani on Wednesday, February 18, at 11 a.m. Prominent political figures paid tribute to former House Speaker Jose de Venecia Jr., remembering him not only as a seasoned statesman but also as a mentor, friend, and family figure whose influence shaped generations of leaders. MANILA -- Government officials, colleagues, family members and friends gathered on Wednesday for the first night of the wake of former House Speaker Jose de Venecia Jr. at the Heritage Memorial Park in Taguig. Tributes also poured in from political leaders, colleagues as they remembered de Venecia as a statesman, peacemaker and devoted family man. De Venecia, a five-time Speaker of the House and veteran lawmaker, passed away Tuesday at the age of 89. According to his family, he died due to health complications brought about by old age. No further details were disclosed. Among those who paid their respects were former House Speaker Martin Romualdez; Senators Sherwin Gatchalian, Migz Zubiri, Loren Legarda, Mark Villar and Kiko Pangilinan; Commission on Elections Chairman George Garcia; former Vice President Jejomar Binay; former Senators Cynthia Villar and Mar Roxas; actress Helen Gamboa and her daughter, MTRCB Chairperson Lala Sotto; former Interior Secretary Benhur Abalos; and former Congressman Mikey Arroyo. Former House Speaker Jose de Venecia, Jr. passes away His wife, Pangasinan 4th District Representative Gina de Venecia, expressed heartfelt gratitude to supporters and constituents who have extended their condolences to their family. 'Una sa lahat, nais ko pong magpasalamat sa lahat ng mga kaibigan natin. Kahit mga hindi natin nakikita mga constituents sa amin sa Pangasinan, and all over the Philippines, nakikiramay sa aming mahal na asawa na si Former Speaker Jose De Venecia,' she said. She reflected on her husband’s decades-long political career, noting his historic tenure as a five-time Speaker of the House of Representatives spanning nearly 15 years. 'I think he has touched many lives, because he was a five-time speaker of the House, almost 15 years and he was a peacemaker. Pinagsama-sama niya ang mga fragmented political parties during the time of President (Fidel) Ramos and he was called the rainbow man, the rainbow coalition,' she said. De Venecia was instrumental in uniting fragmented political parties during the Ramos administration, forming what became known as the 'Rainbow Coalition.' Under his leadership, key pieces of legislation were passed benefiting overseas Filipino workers and members of the Armed Forces of the Philippines, to name a few. He also championed landmark measures such as the Central Bank Act, the Foreign Service Act, and the Bases Conversion and Development Act (BCDA), which paved the way for the transformation of Fort Bonifacio into what is now Bonifacio Global City. 'Marami siyang nagawa because he was a great man, a global Filipino,' Rep. De Venecia added. Beyond politics, she described her husband as a loving and faithful partner who always made her feel valued despite the demands of high office. 'Napakabait niyang asawa, he was gave me priority, and kahit busy siya, talagang pinakita niya sa akin at pinadama ang kanyang pagmamahal… nakasulat doon, sinusulat niya he cannot live without me, he was so happy, sabi niya I gave him new beginnings and mahal na mahal niya ako,' she shared emotionally. 'He was the wind beneath my wings, he allowed me to grow.' Comelec Chairman Garcia, who served as legal counsel to the De Venecia family early in his career, also paid tribute. 'I have a lot of fond memories with them. He is a builder of peace, of nation and he is a total politician,' Garcia said. Former presidential spokesperson Salvador Panelo described De Venecia as a towering figure in Philippine politics. 'Legendary yan, magaling, mabait, caring, generous na tao. Alam mo sa politika, magkakasama kahit magkakalaban kaya kahit minsan hindi kami magkapartido eh magkakasama pa rin kami,' Panelo said. Singer and businessman Jose Mari Chan, a longtime acquaintance, recalled first meeting De Venecia in 1974 when both were honored among the Ten Outstanding Young Men of the Philippines. House, Senate honor legacy of former Speaker Jose de Venecia 'His award was for government service, my award was for the field of arts,' Chan said. 'I feel sad that he is gone, but he lived a good age. God must have loved him and I know he is resting in peace. He was warm, friendly, and most importantly, honest gentleman.' The remains of De Venecia will lie at state at the Heritage Memorial Park in Taguig until February 13. On February 14 and 15, his remains will be brought to the Annunciation of the Lord Parish in Dagupan City, Pangasinan, where he was born and raised. The House of Representatives is also set to hold a memorial and necrological service at the House of Representatives on Monday, February 16, from 10 a.m. to 2:30 p.m. After the ceremony, his remains will be returned to Heritage Memorial Park in Taguig. Both the House and the Senate have passed resolutions expressing condolences to the De Venecia family and honoring his decades of service to the nation. For those wishing to pay their respects, the wake is open to the public from 1 p.m. to 10 p.m. Government officials, colleagues, family members and friends gathered on Wednesday for the first night of the wake of former House Speaker Jose de Venecia Jr. at the Heritage Memorial Park in Taguig. MANILA ( 2nd UPDATE ) -- The House of Representatives and the Senate on Wednesday expressed its condolences and formally paid tribute to the late former Speaker Jose de Venecia Jr. The House adopted two resolutions honoring his life, public service, and lasting legacy during the chamber’s regular plenary session. Meanwhile, the Senate adopted proposed Senate Resolution 297, also honoring the late Speaker. De Venecia passed away February 10, 2026 at the age of 89. READ: Former House Speaker Jose de Venecia, Jr. passes away The measures, House Resolution No. 763 and House Resolution No. 764, expressed the House’s collective grief over the passing of one of the most influential figures in Philippine legislative history, while extending condolences to his family and recognizing his decades-long contribution to nation-building. 'House Resolution 763, commemorating the life, service, and legacy of former Speaker of the House, Jose de Venecia Jr., and expressing the profound sympathy and sincere condolences of the House on his passing,' read during the plenary session, sponsored by Rep. Eduardo Villanueva and referred to the Committee on Rules. A second measure, House Resolution No. 764, filed by Representative Agatha Cruz, echoed similar sentiments, highlighting De Venecia’s stature as a distinguished statesman.
